reviews.author: Selena Goldman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2026
reviews.content:
I bought this AOC portable monitor during a sale and it turned out to be a great deal. Being a well-known brand, the build quality definitely shows, the screen feels sturdy, slim, and very easy to carry around. Setup was truly plug-and-play with USB-C, no drivers needed, and it worked instantly with my Windows laptop or MacBook.

The 1080p IPS display looks sharp and bright, it could be cooler if it's 2k or 4k, but for this price I get it. Perfect for work and watching videos, and play Switch Game, yes, Switch only support 1080p. I was pleasantly surprised by the built-in speakers, they’re actually quite decent for a portable monitor and good enough for game or viode without headphones. Overall, for the discounted price, this monitor is excellent value. Lightweight, reliable, and very convenient for travel or a dual-screen setup. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: L Emir Samonte
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026

reviews.author: Andy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2026
reviews.content:
Monitor works great. Really nice to have such a light weight monitor.
2 complaints
1. the included cord is like six inches too short. The usb-c on my laptop is on the left side and the plugs on this monitor are on the right side. So I have to run the cord the full length of both the monitor and the laptop. The supplied cord is just barely long enough to do this and is tight and putting pressure on the ports. Yes I know I could just put this on the other side but that’s just not how the desk is and not how I want it.
2. The stand is not what I want it be. I wish it had 3 grooves for viewing angle, because one is near straight up and the other is too played back. I ended up using a different type stand.
All told I like it just little bit picks.

reviews.author: Mark Ortaliz
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.content: The monitor itself is really good. It has bright and crisp pictures with goodn colors. However, the magnetic connection with its cover is weak. The cover won't stay on.

reviews.author: Srinivas MD
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 25, 2026
reviews.content:
I got this to use as a second screen when working away from my desk and it's been great. The display is sharp and bright — 1080p IPS at 15.6 inches looks really good and the colors are accurate. Setup was dead simple, just one USB-C cable from my laptop and it was good to go. No drivers, no fuss.

The smart cover doubles as a stand which is a nice touch. It holds the monitor at a comfortable angle and feels reasonably sturdy. The built-in speakers are there if you need them, nothing amazing but functional. Mini HDMI is a bonus for connecting to other devices like a gaming console or a Raspberry Pi.

Fits easily in a laptop bag alongside my laptop. At this price it's excellent value for a portable monitor. Would buy again without hesitation.

reviews.author: Norman Boles
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 1, 2026
reviews.content: Excellent DEX Monitor for using your Samsung PHONE DEX OS using USB C cable and external USB 3.0 Charger to power monitor and offers pass thru usb C charging for simple setup even without a usb hub. Video quality is outstanding and really nice to have for playing videos in the kitchen and garage while working listening to music etc I connect a bluetooth speaker with audio cord for a easy transportable PC mobile surf setup. High Quality monitor for mobile use etc and I have used it with power bank for a really cool off grid setup.

reviews.author: Daniel Alzate
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content: Nice , light and really convenient for working as a second screen. Also good price!

reviews.author: Walter
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.content: Good for the price and goob colors. The quality is good and easy of setup.

reviews.author: Jonni
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026
reviews.content: I had a MSI MAG 255XF 300hz 1080p IPS monitor that broke (due to accidentally hitting it) which led me to get this oled monitor. Honestly, I get what people are saying now. I don't think I can go back to using non-oled. I saw a lot of people complain about text fringing on 1440p, but if you aren't sitting inches away from the screen, you can barely notice it. Either that or I'm already used to it and can't tell it's there. The price for these monitors have been going down and I think the value is good especially compared to other brands. 240hz is a downgrade from my old monitor, but I don't think I can tell too much of a difference even with a lower refresh rate (I play competitive FPS games). The color is great, HDR seems to be working fine, and the monitor stand is really nice and has a lot more movement than other cheaper MAG monitors. I was thinking of getting another proper stand along with it but this one is perfect for my needs. I am a little worried about burn-in but it seems the 3 year warranty covers it so I'm not too worried. If you're on the edge of getting an oled and have the funds, it's definitely worth the upgrade!

reviews.author: Huy Vu
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.content:
I upgraded from the Dell S2721DGF to the MSI MAG 272QP QD-OLED X24, and the difference is incredible. The QD-OLED panel is the standout feature. The deep blacks, vibrant colors, and improved motion clarity completely changed my gaming and media experience. Everything looks sharper, smoother, and more immersive compared to my old IPS display.

I wanted a true upgrade, not just a small improvement, but also to get value for your money too. The QD-OLED technology and gaming-focused features made this feel like a next-gen jump over traditional IPS monitors.

Coming from the Dell S2721DGF, this feels like a massive upgrade, and I’m extremely happy with it.

reviews.author: TheCrimsonRaccoon
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.content: 100% worth the money. The oled looks absolutely amazing only been using it for an hour and I already can’t believe I ever gamed without it. The refresh rate is also a very nice 240 and you can easily tell when playing it. I was also easily able to match the height my other monitor due to the stand which was a nice help. Overall just a wonderful monitor. Oled on the left but you can guess that from the quality difference

reviews.author: DumisoSIW
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2026
reviews.content:
Received it today, and I'm blown away. This replaces my old Gigabyte M27QP IPS monitor, and the difference is literally night and day! The colors are so vibrant, and watching movies on this thing is just pure bliss! Even my wife, who's not into computers, was able to see the difference.

For $429, I doubt you'll find an OLED monitor that comes anywhere near this. The monitor is well-built, and everything about it feels high quality. The 240Hz panel is buttery smooth when playing games. The difference is very noticeable coming from a 165Hz IPS panel. I highly recommend this to anyone looking to upgrade their monitor.

reviews.author: Scott
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content: 1st Oled and it did not disappoint, huge upgrade from my LG 32GN600-B monitor. Everything looks amazing and the vibrant colors just blew me away. The image and overall quality are phenomenal, the 240hz has made gaming very smooth and immersive. It has definitely changed my gaming experience compared to the standard IPS monitors. Worth the price for what it has to offer, I recommend it to anyone who is interested in buying an Oled. You won't regret it

reviews.author: Ben Dover
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content: Insane price for a glossy oled. Everything is perfect with the exception of brightness. Wish the monitor was a bit brighter (coming from a mini led), but is the the nature of the beast. I gave up brightness for faster response times, deeper blacks, and richer colors. Beat value OLED, period.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content:
sharp visuals and 240Hz refresh rate. The display features the blacks look great and my
Gaming experience is enhanced. I love my new screen so far.

reviews.author: Keith
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content:
The monitor was shipped well and fast. My only complaint is that while this monitor CAN have firmware updates this on does NOT use MSI's apps to install the firmware updates but instead you have to somehow manually do it yet they DO NOT include the cable USB-A to USB-B that they say you need to update it so no app to update and no cable included to update it Also the settings are most likely going to need to be changed when you first use it as the default settings make the screen look very dark and some small things look kinda blurry its also standard set to Eco preset I recommend setting to Racing or FPS or just changing each setting yourself i had to max the brightness and crontrast. OVERALL the monitor is really great and im happy with it.

Pros: 240hz with great image (once fine tuned in settings)
AI stuff is kinda cool

Cons: Heavy Monitor with a Heavy Base
Default settings Suck
No Cable included for updates
Info Page only shows how to assemble but doesn't show how most of the features work or what some of the settings do.
Doesn't seem to work with MSI apps like OSD or Gaming Intelligence

reviews.author: A Hopkins
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 5, 2026
reviews.content: Great monitor, good price. Vivid colors. Monitor is as described.

reviews.author: Mark Kachalla
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 5, 2026
reviews.content: Very easy to use , great picture and simple set up

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 8, 2025
reviews.content: This monitor provides incredible value for the price. It supports 2 HDMI ports and 1 VGA port so you connect up to 3 computers to it. I use it to manage three servers. The VGA port is nice since not all servers provide HDMI. It comes with one HDMI cable and a sturdy stand, though you can also wall mount it. The pattern is 75 mm x 75 mm and the 4 M4 10 mm screws are included. The materials, construction, packaging and documentation are all excellent.

reviews.author: Ryan D
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 23, 2025
reviews.content: Solid budget monitor. Nothing spectacular, useful in a home office but certainly wouldn’t work for modern gaming.

reviews.author: Wood stuff guy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 23, 2025
reviews.content: I have had a 20 Scepter monitor for 3 years and now I am adding a 2nd one to replace my old 2nd one the graphics are awesome just a great addition to my system.

reviews.author: Robby
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 18, 2025
reviews.content:
It’s the right size you want for a desk monitor,has a nice HD picture but it’s more along the lines of 720 not 1080,it’s not crisp but it’s better than a tv. It’s not that heavy so if you need something portable this is nice but you need speakers,the speaker that’s with the monitor the volume is low at 100% and sounds muffled and youre straining to hear your movie or video game.It did pause and skip when I was playing black ops 6 which my tv has never done but im not 100% sure if it was the monitor or if someone I was playing with had really bad connection 😆

Would I buy it again,probably not,but for the price I’ll keep it and use it.You can honestly probably find better if you keep looking some more so I’d suggest that

reviews.author: sarah stacy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.content: Love it, use it every day. Good monitor for the price

reviews.author: georgina
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content: Does what it says and great visual

reviews.author: p0
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content:
I tried two other portable monitors from competitors in a similar price range, and this ASUS ZenScreen is hands down the best one. The stand is excellent - it allows for different viewing angles and is very versatile for various setups. The brightness is decent and works well for my needs.

I use it daily with my MacBook Pro and honestly don't feel much difference from the built-in display - the picture quality is great. My only wish is that it came in 4K, but I understand that would likely increase the price significantly.

I really appreciate that it comes with a protective cover - great for portability. Though I wish the cover was built-in rather than separate, it still does the job well.

Overall, highly recommended for anyone looking for a reliable portable monitor!

reviews.author: Topcat
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 21, 2025
reviews.content:
About a year ago, a customer support rep from a company I work with was raving about the "portable monitors" her team had acquired. I remember being intrigued by the concept but I'd yet to encounter an application for such a thing -- both my home and on-site offices were already equipped with full-sized, dual screens. However, since then, my wife and I acquired a travel trailer, out of which I plan to work remotely on occasion. I started thinking about using the trailer's onboard HD TV as a second monitor but it's really too far away from my workspace to serve as a 2nd monitor. I then thought of hauling and extra, full-sized monitor along but the work to set it up, tear it down and find storage space seemed to outweigh the benefit. Then I remembered that conversation last year about portable monitors ....

The ZenScreen seemed to garner good reviews, and I've been happy with other Asus products I've purchased in the past. Mine arrived yesterday and I gotta' say that I'm rightly impressed.

I'm not sure why you'd want to consider the larger models as those seem to fly in the face of portability. But the 16" model (15.6" viewable) is lightweight and small enough to coexist with a laptop in most computer bags. Setup is fast and easy – a quick-connect kickstand allows you to place the display right next to your laptop at the perfect angle. Power and video are simultaneously delivered via a single USB C connection. And brightness, contrast and image quality really exceeded my expectations.

My local Target store has a similar Asus 16" ZenScreen for twice the price. The performance specs seem identical to this one. The only difference seems to be that it has an integrated folding stand verses the screw-on "kickstand." While the integrated stand seemed a bit more convenient, it didn't look as sturdy (especially when propping up the display in portrait mode) and I couldn't justify twice the price.

Obviously, I have not yet had enough time to speak to the long-term reliability of this product. I'll be sure to edit this review in the future if I encounter any issues. However, this Asus backs the ZenScreen with a 3-year warranty and, as I said, the company has a good reputation for quality.

So far, I enthusiastically recommend the ZenDesk portable display for those who travel with a laptop and have the need for dual displays.

reviews.author: CrackBerry Kevin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 27, 2017
reviews.content:
Happened to be in Taipei during the Computex Electronics show and saw this new ZenScreen on the show floor at the ASUS booth.

Was so immediately blown away by the road warrior practicality of it that I ordered one from Amazon right then on the show floor. Then later when I got back to the hotel room I ordered another one (why stop at two? My MacBook Pro has 4 USB C ports!).

Have had the ZenScreens for a couple days now and I love them. Can't wait to hit the road again so I can put them to work in hotel rooms, coffee shops, etc. The work flow I've developed works best on a 3 monitor setup, which I have in my home office, but I always lose that when I travel and am confined to laptop. So being able to take my workflow with me now and have all my dashboards setup on additional displays the way I need to is a godsend.

Overall, I find this one of the most oddly practical and magical "accessories" to come out for a long time. Blows my mind that the ZenScreen gets both power and video from the same cable. That you don't have to find additional power outlets to plug it in makes it so much more practical. Also find it amazing that a phone can power the display (plugged it into a Galaxy S8 and the screen powered on and got video - doesn't work with all phones though). Also love that it just gets detected as a display, so you can set it up how you want to - landscape or portrait.

The screens are big and I think deceptively lightweight for just how big they are. I like that you can avoid lugging around the covers/stands if you want to save some weight and just rely on a pen (included!) to prop up the displays. Since getting them I've already had half a dozen friends and work colleagues pick one up.

reviews.author: Jean-Etienne LaVallee
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 24, 2019
reviews.content:
I'm a software engineer who travels about 25-50% of the time for work. Mainly, I code apps and websites and as such, I almost always prefer to work with multiple screens. My primary work laptop is a 2018 MacBook Pro 15 and my sidecar "hobby" machine is a 12.5" Asus Chromebook C302 that I absolutely adore (seriously, see my review of it). At home and work I have a plethora of displays setup on my workstations to service both of these machines and over the years I've become used to being able to "spread" my work out... Communications and productivity apps on one screen, code editor and output browser/simulators on another and one just dedicated to debug terminals and consoles. It really is workflow bliss...

And then I got the new gig... Traveling 1-2weeks out of the month, living in hotels... trains, planes and automobiles... pop-up meetings in restaurants/coffeeshops/conferences. Suddenly my workflow bliss started feeling like a single-desktop pile-on. It's not that I need to drag several 30" displays on the road with me... just some more breathing room.

And then a friend told me about this line of ASUS monitors that sounded way too good to be true... So, I picked one up just to see if it could give me the breathing room I needed w/o the inconvenience of hauling around an external display, power brick and connection cables.

Happy to say, this device hits all 5-stars at a price that's comparable to a low-end desktop monitor. It has the same footprint as my 15" MBP and slips in right next to it in my bag w/o a fuss. Yeah, it adds a little extra weight to my bag, but no more than a hefty USB battery brick or extra Apple MBP wall charger. For me, the big-win is that it does everything over a single USB-C cable, data, image, and power... just magic.

Pros:
- Light weight and low-overhead, just need the device and a USB-C cable that's DisplayPort-over-USB certified
- Same footprint as a 15" MacBook Pro
- Great resolution and color
- Power and image over USB-C, no need for a power-brick
- Easy to use adjustment settings directly on the device
- Sturdy and low-profile magnetic cover very similar to the covers you can get for Android tablets and iPads
- Solid metal/plastic construction

Cons:
- Doesn't have the ability to auto-rotate on MacOS
- No USB-C pass-thru... it eats a whole port, so have a port-expander dongle with power pass-thru for your other port
- Doesn't support HDMI input on the device (I've heard you can get an HDMI-to-USB-C DisplayPort adaptor, but I've yet to source one/try it, would love to have for on-the-road console gaming)
- Hard to find replacement USB-C data cables that support DisplayPort over USB-C

I've been using this for about 2 months now and it's been a worthwhile travel companion... I even crack it out at the home office on occasion to mirror my screen for small 2-3 person group demos/presentations. I haven't tried gaming on it, but video playback and color matching to my MBP screen are great.

reviews.author: Sai
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 13, 2025
reviews.content: The screen quality is crisp and bright, and the portability makes it perfect for travel or dual-screen work on the go. Setup with USB-C is simple and reliable. However, the build feels a bit delicate, so you need to handle it carefully. Overall, a useful portable monitor with a slightly fragile feel.

reviews.author: S
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 31, 2026
reviews.content:
As someone who travels frequently for work and values a dual monitor setup, this ASUS ZenScreen has become an indispensable tool. The ability to have a second screen anywhere transforms laptop productivity from cramped and limiting to spacious and efficient.

The 16-inch size is the perfect balance between portability and usability. Large enough to actually work on comfortably, small enough to slip into a laptop bag without adding significant bulk or weight. The slim profile and light weight make it genuinely portable, not just technically movable like some "portable" monitors.

The IPS display quality is excellent. Colors are accurate and vibrant, viewing angles are wide, and the Full HD resolution provides crisp text and clear images. This is not a compromise display you tolerate for portability. This is a legitimate quality monitor that happens to be portable. Reading documents, editing spreadsheets, and viewing presentations all look great.

The USB-C connectivity is brilliantly simple. One cable provides both power and video signal. Plug it into your laptop and it just works. No separate power adapter to carry, no multiple cables to manage. The simplicity cannot be overstated. For travel, reducing cable clutter is a massive benefit.

The dual USB-C ports provide flexibility. You can use either port for connectivity, and the extra port can charge your phone or connect other devices. The inclusion of Mini-HDMI means you can also use this with devices that do not support USB-C video output. Versatility matters for real-world use.

Setup is literally plug and play. No drivers to install, no configuration needed. Connect the cable and the monitor works immediately. Windows recognizes it instantly and you can start using the extended display right away. The simplicity makes it accessible even for less technical users.

The auto-rotate feature and portrait mode work well. For reading documents or coding, portrait orientation is fantastic. The monitor detects orientation and adjusts automatically. Small touches like this show thoughtful design.

Eye care features including flicker-free technology and blue light filter make extended use more comfortable. When you are working on the road all day, reducing eye strain matters. These features actually make a difference during long work sessions.

Build quality feels premium. The materials are solid, the stand works smoothly, and everything feels well-engineered. This is not a flimsy gadget. It is a serious productivity tool built to withstand regular travel and daily use.

For remote workers, digital nomads, consultants, or anyone who works from different locations regularly, this monitor is transformative. Turn any coffee shop, hotel room, or client site into a productive dual-monitor workstation. The productivity gains easily justify the investment. Highly recommend for mobile professionals.

reviews.author: Gina
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 14, 2024
reviews.content: Perfect screen that is easy to move from my home office to any other room in the house. Sturdy handle, lightweight and comes with a setup to either hang from a door or perch on the end of a table. I wouldn't recommend this size for travel as it is larger than a traditional laptop screen and doesn't have a protective cover. But, if you are wanting something to move locally, or room to room that is large enough to see everything on, this is the monitor you want! I am truly pleased with this. I work from home and often move from my office to the kitchen for a change of scenery or to let the dogs lay out on the patio while I'm in the kitchen. It would be a pain to move my other larger monitors...but this one is a breeze! Super easy to take on road trips too for a quick setup in a hotel (wouldn't fly with it) Resolution is great! Buy it!! You won't regret it.

reviews.author: Kyrsten Assiduous
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 5, 2025
reviews.content: The external monitor was very nice for the first 60 days I had it. And then it stopped connecting to all my devices. One fine morning, as I am starting to work at the hotel, I plugged in the monitor and it did not connect. I thought maybe it was a fluke, rebooted the laptop and all, still nothing. Since I'm more than 1000 miles from home, I went and bought a new cable, thinking the cable had an issue. I plugged in with the new cable and again, nothing. At this point I can't do much other than work on one screen for 3 more days. So I get home, and thought maybe it was my laptop, so I plugged it into my PC, and again nothing. To be clear, the monitor is on, the brand name ASUS displays, and then the monitor says no connection and shuts down. I tried plugging into my personal laptop, my phone, even my television screen and nothing. I called Asus more times than I can remember and they offered to send me a new one. So I sent back the original, and never got the new one. It was a decent screen for remote work and travel. But it doesn't work.

reviews.author: Robert
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026
reviews.content: Works as it should nice not having to use mouse all the time

reviews.author: Johaan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 9, 2025
reviews.content:
The display works out of the box with Windows 11. The image was a little fuzzy at first, but this was easily fixed by changing display settings - namely, maximizing the Sharpness. Additionally, the image quality is noticeably worse over VGA compared to HDMI, so use the latter if possible.

Within Windows, the screen required no configuration - I was swiping around within apps and games without issue. I ended up using it at 150% scale to match the pixel size of my traditional desktop displays.

I have the display mounted on a light-weight monitor arm - you'll be pleased to know that the "stud" for the monitor stand can be completely removed with a few screws for a clean, mounted appearance. When moving the monitor around with the arm, it's obvious the shell is a thinner variety with some flex, but the display is so light that you rarely meet enough resistance to flex the display during arm adjustment.

For an IPS display, viewing angles are not amazing - if you were thinking of mounting this in a camper, expect to sit close together for everyone to see clearly. The colors and image response are otherwise sufficient for what it is.

If there's anything I dislike about the display, it's a lack of speakers. Just something basic to hear system sounds.

reviews.author: Kenny Rucker
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 16, 2026
reviews.content:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Great Baby Monitor + Excellent Customer Service!

We’ve been using the HelloBaby monitor for 3 years now. This is our second and overall it’s been a great experience. The video quality is clear with a big display, the connection is always stable, and it gives us peace of mind being able to check our little one easily. Setup was simple, and the parent unit is very user-friendly! Grandma is a pro!

What really stood out was HelloBaby’s customer service. When we reached out with a compatibility question, their team responded quickly, explained everything clearly, and made sure we were well taken care of. It’s refreshing to see a company that actually stands behind their product and warranty.
Highly recommend HelloBaby for parents and grandparents looking for a reliable monitor and responsive support. Don’t second guess it!

reviews.author: Liaday Alvarez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026
reviews.content:
I bought this baby monitor to keep an eye on my baby while sleeping and it has been a great purchase. The screen has a clear image and the sound is very easy to hear, even when the baby makes small noises.

I really like that it does not require WiFi, so it connects directly and feels more secure. The camera moves smoothly and allows me to see the whole room without any issues. The night vision works well in the dark.
The battery lasts for several hours and it was very easy to set up right out of the box. The size is convenient to carry from room to room.
I recommend this monitor for parents who want a simple, reliable, and easy-to-use baby monitor.

reviews.author: Jiovanni
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2026
reviews.content: This baby monitor is outstanding. The 6” IPS screen is incredibly clear, the battery lasts forever, and the no‑WiFi setup feels truly secure. The pan‑tilt camera, night vision, and VOX mode make it easy to keep an eye on everything, and the 2‑way talk is crisp. Perfect for parents, caregivers, or even pets—reliable and effortless to use.

reviews.author: Ernesto
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 26, 2025
reviews.content:
I am extremely happy with this baby monitor and would absolutely recommend it to other parents. The 6-inch IPS screen is large, bright, and very clear, making it easy to see my baby both during the day and at night. The night vision works surprisingly well and provides a clear image without being grainy.

One of my favorite features is that it does not require WiFi, which gives me peace of mind about security and reliability. The connection has been stable with no lag or dropped signal, even when moving around the house. The pan and tilt function allows me to adjust the camera remotely and see the entire room without having to physically reposition it.

The battery life is excellent and lasts through the night with no issues. The two-way talk feature is also very helpful for calming my baby without immediately going into the room. Setup was quick and straightforward, and the controls are easy to use.

Overall, this monitor offers excellent quality, strong features, and great value for the price. I am very satisfied with this purchase and would buy it again.

reviews.author: John B.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 4, 2026
reviews.content: Nice camera. We have owned or tried several camera systems over the years. This camera and display unit are one of our top picks for stand alone units. The pan and tilt of this is very nice and we have had no issues. Night vision is very good and we can see and hear everything we need. This model with the bigger display is really. I do wish the volume was separate buttons to more easily adjust the volume but once you get used to it adjusting the volume is no big deal. The only reason for 4 stars is the lack of separate volume buttons and the video clarity could be a bit clearer to capture details (this is just being a little picky). But don’t get me wrong for the price point the video quality is very good and we can see all we need. Would highly recommend this

reviews.author: Carli
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 11, 2026
reviews.content: Excellent baby monitor! Easy to use and provides quality image! The camera is really good, with the ability to pan, tilt, and zoom! The sound quality is excellent! Audio stream is consistent and not triggered by mic gate. If you're needing a baby monitor that provides constant sound, unlike most new versions that only provide audio triggered by mic gate, this is the monitor you want! Exceptional product at a low cost!

reviews.author: Marisol Ann Ciovacco
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2026
reviews.content: I love this product! I wanted a baby monitor that is not connected to WiFi because I’ve heard horror stories of people hacking accounts through WiFi and watching and talking to babies. No thank you!! This product is WONDERFUL. If I have the camera in my room at the back of my house, I can walk down my driveway to my sidewalk and still have signal on the monitor. You can talk to baby through the monitor and you can still connect multiple cameras if you more than one camera in different rooms. The image quality is really good for what it is. The night vision is really good, so no complaints there. This camera does everything a camera with WiFi does, you just can’t be too far away, which isn’t a bother for me. The only thing I will say is DONT DROP THE MONITOR ON THE FLOOR! My toddler dropped mine and the screen image is all jumbled and more than half the screen shows a black image now, so just be careful. But customer support has been great about it!! I can’t recommend this brand enough!

reviews.author: Meghan Garcia
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.content: This is the best baby monitor EVER! It’s easy to step up and use. The quality is amazing and you don’t need wifi to use it, which is incredible! We mounted the camera on the wall and it holds great and when you move the camera it doesn’t make any noise. The volume is so good that I usually have it on the lowest volume and can still hear baby!

reviews.author: Ömer Barış
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 4, 2025
reviews.content: I don't often write reviews on products, but this one is fantastic. The image and sound quality are excellent. It's 100 times better than the baby monitor I bought for $250 before. I love that it works while charging. The retractable camera is very useful. The image is very clear. I haven't had any connection issues even at the farthest corners of the house. It's very easy to use, and the screen is very large. The color quality is excellent. Another feature I like is that it measures the room temperature. It's very useful to know where my baby sleeps and adjust the room temperature accordingly. The quality never decreases when using the zoom feature. I bought the HQ Screen 7, and although I've paid high prices for other cameras before, I realized they were worse than this one. It's both affordable and very high quality and useful. I definitely recommend it.

reviews.author: Eduardo Cazita
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 29, 2025
reviews.content: This camera is actually really good and budget-friendly. As a mom, I need something that makes me feel safe and confident while my baby sleeps. The baby monitor lets me keep track of movement, temperature, and sounds. And since it doesn’t require Wi-Fi, I feel more secure knowing only our family has access.

reviews.author: Renee
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 20, 2025
reviews.content: Excellent quality easy set up. The picture quality is wonderful. This was a great gift it connects easy. The battery life is good. My son and daughter-in-law really appreciated it.

reviews.author: Jessica M
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 15, 2025
reviews.content:
I bought this monitor as my biofun monitor was giving me more of a headache that help so I felt like it was time to go with Vtech since it's a well known, reliable brand,

First impressions, the camera is nice and the monitor is huge. It's 7 inches and to be honest I think it's a bit too big. It's clear and you can see your baby well but I guess from going to a small portable monitor, this seems like an ipad. The camera has great rotational ability and I like how the plug into the camera from the wall is secure in the bottom of the camera. my previous camera utilized a usb c and it was not secure and kept falling out. This does not have that feeling at all.

Monitor - Like i mentioned before the monitor size is a large 7 inches. It says the battery life is about 12 hours. I was surprised that it really was about that time when it was dying in the morning. When it was low battery, it makes this obnoxious beeping sound that warns you it will die every 10 seconds. I would recommend keeping the plug near your bedside which was a rookie mistake on my part. I love the temperature setting. My child's room doesn't have air conditioning at the moment so the temperature accuracy is really important to us. We keep the monitor on silent but it seems like good sound quality when I had the volume on. you can also use the talk feature from the monitor the camera.

The camera and monitor were very easy to connect to eachother.
The one con I find is that the charger for the monitor is not a regular usb C and it's own plug which can be annoying as you can't use any charger for that. That would be the one feedback I give as it's 2025 and it's a new upgrade that the charger should be a standard charger.

We will see how the monitor goes. For the ease of use and the quality, I think it's worth the price but no more.

reviews.author: Steve Skaggs
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2026
reviews.content: Got it to watch over my Dad, who is 95 years old and cannot move very well. I can keep an eye on him in his room with the monitor. It is a great help and brings me peace.

reviews.author: TC
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 10, 2025
reviews.content: Big IPS screen so you can see how terrible the artifact filled video is. Absolutely terrible. Go buy 2 of the HB brand monitors whose power connector goes after a year, but enjoy a monitor where you can actually see your child. The zoom is great... For seeing even bigger pixelated blur children. Joke of a kickstand for a big screen that folds if you move the screen. I really can't recommend more elastically that you go buy a better monitor for far less money.

reviews.author: Katlyn Lewis
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content: I should start and say this thing is huge, screen quality is almost perfect I love v tech

reviews.author: Sierra Fuhrmann
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 3, 2025
reviews.content: This camera has good clarity in day or night vision and the monitor has lots of features. Our most favorite is the shushing sound that can be played from the camera. The pan distance is pretty generous as well. The connectivity distance is impressive— I’ve been able to still have good signal even from outside my house. The monitor battery lasts a few hours which is pretty common, but wish it would last longer especially when the screen is off. The biggest flaw is the thermometer feature is useless. There is at least a 4° difference between the temperature shown on the monitor and the actual room temp and there is no way to adjust it.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.content: Love this monitor great value for the cost, has night vision and works well and good security as it doesnt use wifi . Very good picture quality and works with other moniditors and videos purchased with same brand. Recommend this 100% cant beat it.

reviews.author: Trinity Sorensen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.content: This monitor is a great purchase! I’m happy with its abilities. It’s not over fancy. It’s simple to use. Good quality. I like that it doesn’t have Wi-Fi. The night mode actually works really well. And I appreciate that you are able to talk through the camera. The sound is crystal clear and the picture quality is good. It also has the ability to link more than one camera.

reviews.author: Dakota
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.content: I wasn't going to get a camera monitor, but I'm glad I did! The range is great, we have a big back yard on the opposite end of the house from her room (smallish house though) and it reaches out while doing yard work. There's a pretty wide range to see her. The video quality isn't phenomenal, but you can definitely see your baby and their breathing. It's easy to use with a lot of settings that aren't hard to find. The night vision does great. It's honestly worth the money! I'm so glad I got this. It's also easy enough to move fron room to room if you don't mount it. The only down side is having to unplug the camera to charge the parent portion. Not worth taking away a star to me, as I don't use it constantly.

reviews.author: Jennifer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 6, 2026
reviews.content: Easy to set up, easy to use, and excellent quality! As a parent of two, I love having access to multiple cameras for different rooms on a single parent unit. The picture quality is fantastic, including clear and reliable night vision. Customer support was outstanding—they responded immediately when my parent unit stopped charging and sent a replacement, upgraded model with a split-screen feature. The price is very reasonable, and it’s well worth it.

reviews.author: A. jones
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.content:
Here are the benefits of this monitor:
-The set-up is easy. You just PLUG both the monitor and the camera in! This also means there’s no need to worry about a third-party hacking into your stream (I think…!).
-The sound quality is good; I wouldn’t say it’s great, but it’s good.
-There is zero lag.
-It’s cheap! Considering the price, the product itself is great!

Con:
-The camera quality is good; I wouldn’t say it’s great, but it’s good. I am using this for a toddler, so I’m not listening for crying. I don’t need the sound. I just need to see, and this works great for that.

All in all, I’d buy this again for my toddler. If you’re wanting to check really closely on an infant, like checking for breathing, pacifiers, etc., it might be a bit too grainy for that.

reviews.author: klmart08
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.content:
This two‑camera baby monitor works just the way it should. Both cameras connect easily, the picture is clear, and switching between rooms is quick and smooth. The monitor stays connected without dropping the signal, and the audio picks up what I need to hear without a lot of background noise.

It’s straightforward, reliable, and does exactly what I bought it for.

reviews.author: Kaitlyn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026
reviews.content:
We’ve been using the Hello Baby HB6550 camera and monitor for a while now and it’s been excellent. It pairs easily with multiple cameras, and the zoom is just right so you can see the entire crib clearly without constantly adjusting it. There’s no lag at all, which is huge when you want to check on your baby quickly. The battery life is impressive too. It lasts for days and charges really fast.

The range is another big win. I can be outside while my baby is sleeping inside and the signal is still strong and reliable. The sound range is great. It’s sensitive enough to pick up quiet noises but can get very loud if needed. The picture quality is clear, and the night vision works extremely well. Even in a dark room at night, I can see everything clearly.

What really sets this monitor apart is the customer service. After more than a year of heavy use, our monitor broke and they immediately sent a replacement with no hassle. For that reason alone I would recommend this product, but combined with the performance and reliability, it’s an easy choice. Highly recommend, especially for the peace of mind and outstanding customer service.

reviews.author: Lynn Brockwell
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026
reviews.content: This monitor is awesome!!! I got it to monitor my older dog at night and so I can see him and hear him!! The camera is clear and can be moved around and I can hear when my dog gets up and whines to go out!! It came timely and was well packaged and in great condition!! The price was great!! The setup was easy and the dog is on one side of the house and my room is on the other side and I get good reception!!! No issues-good buy!!!

reviews.author: Red Hulk
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 6, 2025
reviews.content:
The HUANUO Dual Monitor Stand is easily one of the best upgrades I've made to my home office. This stand is an absolute game-changer and deserves every one of these five stars.
​First off, the setup was incredibly easy and hassle-free. They provide all the necessary tools and hardware right in the box, which meant I didn't have to hunt through my toolbox to get started. Once clamped down, my two monitors (both 27 inches) are held firmly and securely—no wobble, even when adjusting the arms.
​The ergonomic benefits are immediately noticeable. Being able to independently adjust the height, tilt, and swivel of each monitor means I can achieve the perfect sightlines, which has drastically reduced my neck and shoulder strain during long work sessions.
​Finally, the amount of desk space I recovered is incredible. The sleek, floating design gets the monitor bases off the desk, creating a clean, professional, and clutter-free workspace. If you run a dual monitor setup, stop looking and buy this HUANUO stand. It’s durable, highly adjustable, and completely essential!

reviews.author: Shayz3hQU33N
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026
reviews.content: My husband is an avid pc gamer and also uses his set up for college courses. He has been begging for dual monitor arms for awhile now. We finally ordered them and he said it is a huge quality of life upgrade. They were easy to install and are very sturdy. They move easily for any changes he needs to make, had all the hardware needed (besides brackets for the back, we ordered those separately. That's just due to his curved scepter monitors), overall amazing purchase!

reviews.author: Bailey Kinder
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 23, 2026
reviews.content: great option for mounting dual monitors as one piece (with full adjustability and functionality) without mounting to the wall. the clamp to attach it to the desk was fairly easy to assemble and worked with my samsung curved monitors! it is very versatile and adjustable to multiple heights and angles and they are tiltable. they are also adjusted individually so they don’t have to both be in the same position. it is a good weight where it is hefty enough to fully support the monitors without being too heavy or putting too much weight on the desk. I have a fairly thin, affordable standing desk that fully supports the clamp without issues (at least so far). I feel like the price is a little higher than necessary but the adjustability and ergonomic options this mount provides makes it worth it for me since I use it everyday. also, love that it comes with cord management!! makes it very streamlined.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2026
reviews.content: Very sturdy and easy to assemble, the clamp it comes with isn't going anywhere. Cord management works well. Very adjustable (I have one monitor vertical). Like most monitor stands I do wish it went a bit taller. I have an adjustable desk but for good body mechanics the distance from desk to monitor could be a bit higher for taller people at max height.

reviews.author: GMoney
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 27, 2026
reviews.content: Great Product, quality and easy to assemble. I bought this for my workstation and it works perfectly for 2 32 inch monitors. I would make sure the product is going to fit your desk before purchasing, mine worked great.

reviews.author: Adalynnn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.content: This dual monitor stand feels very solid and well built. Installation was straightforward, and once mounted, the arms hold both monitors securely without sagging. The adjustment range is excellent—you can easily change height, tilt, and rotation to get the perfect setup. It really helps free up desk space and makes the workstation look cleaner and more organized. Great option for work, gaming, or anyone running a dual-monitor setup.

reviews.author: Aaron T.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content:
This dual monitor mount works as advertised and when it says two 34 inch monitors it means 16:9 sized. I knew that when I bought it and even still it holds both my 21:9 ratio monitors great. One is a 34 inch Sceptre C34 and the other is a 27 inch discontinued LGb ultrawide. I have to rotate the LG into portrait orientation for them both to fit and I'll admit things are a little tight but I am still able to move the monitors for better viewing somewhat.

These monitor mount arms are solid and of high quality materials. I recommend following the instructions and not making adjustments until the monitors are installed as they can whip upwards under the tension they have if you don't have the counterweight of the monitor. The adjustments that can be made to how hard or easy it is to move each part of the arm are a great feature. Except where the arms attach at the base where I haven't found any adjustment screw to be tightened. These arms don't move for me unless I move them but I wouldn't use them for mounting in a trailer or other mobile scenario as the resistance isn't very great.

The whole thing did take me about 45 minutes to put together. This is mostly because I had to pull the monitors off the desk, remove the base stands and then wanted to run the cables through the cable guides on the arms. The guide on the upper arm uses only one screw and clips in on one side. It honestly feels a little flimsy but ultimately holds both the thick three prong PC power cable and the DisplayPort cable without bulging or warping.

All in all this is a great option if you're looking to free up some desk space from the monitor stands or you need/want to be able to adjust your monitor orientation.

reviews.author: CaT
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026
reviews.content:
Easy to set up, and still mounted perfect on my Secret Lab Magnus Pro which is notorious for being finicky with monitor arms. Really cleans up the look of the desk and helps save space over clunky monitor feet.

Holds up my LG 32” OLED without breaking a sweat. Easy to move around if I need to mess with cables or anything too which is nice.

Only downside is I’m not personally a huge fan of the overall style of the arms, but they stay hidden behind my monitors so I can complain too much.

Overall very worth the price and does exactly what I need it to.

reviews.author: Stephanie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.content: Purchased this monitor for my 14 year old nephew. He wants a gaming set up. For starters, this monitor is awesome . Its exactly what he was looking for that is good quality and reasonably priced. I recommend this is you're looking for something with good quality, curved screen, stability and affordable.

reviews.author: Tylor S Dolan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 7, 2025
reviews.content: It was delivered super fast and had no damage and the quality of the monitor for the price is insanely good. If you cannot afford an expensive gaming monitor then this is the next best option that I highly recommend and honestly will buy this again if something happens to mine or even as a gift for a friend.

reviews.author: Mike Michalczik
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 2, 2025
reviews.content: Hook it up for my son he played on his new monitor for 3 hrs looks amazing For MSI brand would deff get one for me NXT my opinion very good monitor

reviews.author: Lauren
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 16, 2024
reviews.content: This has held two monitors for the past 4 years and is still in perfect condition. It’s exactly what I needed and still to this day couldn’t be more happy with it. It’s very sturdy, but if your monitors are positioned more forward, like anything that requires a bit of balance, it’ll become forward heavy and want to tilt over. So just make sure you position them properly. The quality is great in my opinion. It’s solid metal and hold my monitors very well. I’ve never had an issue with that. It’s a simple, black design that goes well with the rest of my set up so I really like that as well. You can also tilt your monitors to or away from you, adjust the height you want them (they’ll both be at the desired height), and it comes with cable clips so you can do some cable management.

reviews.author: Nathan Smith
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 10, 2023
reviews.content:
Full disclosure: I ended up taking this back, but it was totally a case of "It's not you, it's me." This stand is FANTASTIC and you should totally buy it.

The reason I got it originally was because I found a 32" smart TV locally that I couldn't pass up; at the time I had a 24" TV and a 20" monitor, so I decided to try doing the 32" TV as a regular TV with my 24" TV as a "monitor" for my computer. My vision isn't the greatest, so the 24" TV wasn't doing great for me as a monitor, and the 20" monitor I have (an older Acer) doesn't have a VESA mount. So it was either buy a different monitor, or get a different stand, and I opted to go back to my old setup for my monitor but buy a VIVO Single Monitor stand for my 32" TV.

Back to this stand's information: They give you all the parts you need, and although there are several steps and different types of screws & wrenches involved, I was able to put things together without much trouble. My only complaint is that I wish they had arranged things in the bags better during packaging so I didn't have to figure out what size screws and Allen wrenches things went to. Other than that, once I figured out what size of things went to what, it was totally fine thanks to their instructions. I loved being able to adjust the tightness of the arms and tilt angles, etc with the Allen wrenches; very awesome, and I wish the VIVO single monitor stand was that way instead of using an actual wrench with a nut/bolt situation. Once I got the TVs situated the way I wanted to, everything was steady with no danger of tipping over. I would totally trust this for the setup I had again if I had the right monitor to match the VESA mount.

All that to say, this is an awesome monitor stand, and you should totally get this if you're looking into a dual-monitor setup that meets the size & weight requirements for your monitors. Thanks for reading!

reviews.author: napapapa
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 25, 2019
reviews.content:
Im one to never review things unless they are extremely good or extremely bad. I am pleased to say in this case that it is because it is extremely good! The stand is extremely sturdy without mounting it and using it with just the weighted base. It was really easy to put together, only had to put in 3 screws for the base and 4 screws each for the monitor mounts (i actually used the ones provided with my monitor instead of the ones they sent, but the ones they send looked nice enough.)

The monitors slid into place easily and there is one nut to secure it on the stand so you dont unhook it when you adjust it. Once you make your adjustments you can tighten the bolts with the included allen wrenches to lock the stand in place. You can then even store the allen wrenches in the included wire clips for future use!

I am also just amazed at how much space it freed up on my desk.

The ONE WORD OF CAUTION i have is that when attaching the monitors, after putting the first one on it tends to tip over. So between putting the first and second monitors on, either have a buddy hold it steady, or in my case, help weigh the base down more with a ream of paper or a heavy book. Once the second monitor is on, the stand is way more steady then i would imagine.

Overall this is a really good stand for the money and i would recommend it to anybody who needs one!

reviews.author: Everyday Reviews
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 26, 2025
reviews.content:
A Solid and Space-Saving Solution for Dual Monitors

This VIVO freestanding monitor stand has significantly improved my workspace organization. The assembly process was straightforward with clear instructions and all necessary tools included. Within about 30 minutes, I had both my 24-inch monitors mounted securely. The build quality feels substantial with mostly metal construction that inspires confidence in its durability.

The adjustable arms provide excellent flexibility for positioning monitors at the perfect height and angle, eliminating neck strain from looking down at screens. I particularly appreciate the cable management clips that keep wires organized and out of sight. The large base provides decent stability for everyday use, though I did notice some slight forward tipping when extending the monitors too far forward from the base.

For the price, this stand offers excellent value compared to more expensive clamp-mounted alternatives. It freed up valuable desk space that was previously occupied by monitor bases, creating a much cleaner work environment. The range of motion allows for comfortable viewing positions whether I'm working, gaming, or watching content.

Bottom line: A reliable, well-built monitor stand that delivers great functionality and ergonomic benefits at an affordable price point. Recommended for anyone looking to organize their dual monitor setup without permanent desk modifications.

reviews.author: Dfowler1776
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 28, 2025
reviews.content: This was the least expensive & "sponsored" item. So I thought it would be kind of bad,cheap or a flat-out POS. It turns out it works really good and above all else is very stable.

reviews.author: Mercedes Zelaya
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 4, 2025
reviews.content: The VIVO STAND-V002F is a solid and reliable dual monitor stand. It was easy to assemble, and it holds both of my monitors securely without any wobble. The adjustable arms make it simple to get the perfect angle and height, which really helped improve my workspace and posture. Great build quality and totally worth the price!

reviews.author: Jenn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 18, 2017
reviews.content:
just got done setting this up and have some initial thoughts.

things i like:
- cord management and hex caddy are a plus.
- i love that adding and removing monitors is so easy via the plate that attached with just one screw. helped with setting up.
- very sturdy cinstruction
- once set up micro adjusting monitor height was simple.

things i did not like:

- WARNING: this design is unstable with 27" monitors using only the free standing base. it is simply not wide enough to take advantage of the full range of motion this stand has to offer. had i known this i probably wouldn't have bought it. i have a large glass top desk and can't make use of the various stabilizing features it has that require going straight through the desk. i will look into other options to make my base more sturdy (perhaps more weight or a padded clamp.) i wish i could attach it to my old monoprice base this stand was replacing. it was very wide for a single panel stand but low profile. it would be perfect for this but is not compatible.

- set up was a pain because of the balancing issues. i am still slowly tweeking the positions but because of the balancing issues i mentioned it's slow going and tenuous to maintain stability. it was particularly difficult getting both monitors on the stand because of these balancing issues. if one is added or removed, it's thrown off kilter.

- the joints in the arm are impossible to loosen. i don't know if this is by design (the ends of the screws appear painted over and really stuck in place.) it makes for strong and reliable holding power, but again because of the balancing issues it makes adjusting everything without toppling my displays a nightmare.

i think once everything is as it should be it will work out fine, but the instability of the free standing base is a big disappointment. i would recommend folks with full sized monitors+glass top desks look for other solutions.

reviews.author: BFW
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content: Very nice product. Professional appearance.

reviews.author: Donna Marie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviews.content:
My son loves this gaming monitor. He wanted one to play his games on the Xbox. It looks great. He even says it might be too big as he isn't too far away from it. It is perfect for his gaming need. It is bright and shows all his games on the screen quick, refreshing amazing.
We got this on sale at an amazing price, This was an excellent buy. If we need another monitor I will look at this company again.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 5, 2026
reviews.content: I spent a lot of time searching for an affordable gaming monitor and finally chose this one. This monitor boasts an excellent 240Hz refresh rate and 2K resolution. Trust me, once you use it, you'll never go back; forget 1080p. There are some drawbacks, but they're acceptable. For example, the screen brightness isn't very high, but I live in a basement, so it doesn't bother me. The included stand isn't height-adjustable, but you can buy a stand mount for $30. Can you believe a monitor at this price has speakers? The sound quality is average, and the volume isn't loud, but for someone who eats while watching a monitor, this is definitely a plus; I don't want to wear headphones while eating. This is currently the cheapest monitor with the highest refresh rate you can buy. The monitor is very light, and the casing is cheap plastic, but that's not important to me. Remember, you're buying resolution and refresh rate; don't waste money on expensive packaging and LED lights. They don't help with gaming.

reviews.author: FloridaTech
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 5, 2026
reviews.content: I am not a video connoisseur. I got this to replace an old 1080P with a better 1440P display. It was hard finding one that spec, good color rating, adaptive synch, 24", etc. and still be reasonably priced. I guess they had to skimp somewhere as this may be the lightest, cheapest feeling monitor I have ever handled. Something to consider if you need to move it around a lot. Me? I stuck it on its shelf and that is not a factor for me at all. The display is rich and sharp and no bad pixels after a few months. I haven't noticed any ghosting or other issues either. It does take several clicks to move between HDMI and DP but is comparable to other monitors. I am very satisfied with this purchase.

reviews.author: Thomas Rankel
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 28, 2025
reviews.content: Broke within a month. It stopped working, the screen will light up as ifs its connecting but then turns off, idles and the power button turns red. Ive tried multiple hdmi/display port cables, each input on the monitor and different sources and the same thing happens. Also I cant access the menu, the same thing happens. When I push a button screen lights up as if it's about to do something, but the menu never appears on screen and eventually the screen turns off and idles again. This leads me to belueve its some kind of software issue. Regardless, it is actually a piece of junk and id be hesistant to try again with a new one. I heard aoc is making a similar monitor (24.5", 2k but 300hz refresh rate). I have no interest in putting my trust in this company again after it broke so close to the 30 day return window.

reviews.author: Rye bread
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 30, 2025
reviews.content: First off, this panel looks great. I wasn't expecting a monitor this cheap with that good of spec's. In all the games I play, mostly FPS shooters etc it's very responsive and feels like 1ms response time haha. The only down fall is the build quality the frame is very cheap feeling plastic not the worse out there but most certainly not the best. Overall I recommend this monitor only when it's on sale for regular MSRP it's not worth it based off the build quality.

reviews.author: Rashid M.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026
reviews.content: I'm using this for FPS games on PC , picture quality is spot on. Product quality is fine. for the price I definately recommend this monitor.

reviews.author: Rayce
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 3, 2025
reviews.content:
Highly recommend.

I've had this monitor for about 7months now and here are some pros and cons to consider.

Pros:
-Stand is sturdy
-Picture quality is fantastic
-240hz refresh rate is insane and you won't find anything close at this price point.
-Adaptive sync feature (coupled with that 240hz rate)makes for buttery smooth gameplay on everything I've played

Cons:

-Size is a little small. 24.5" isn't the worst but I do wish they had at least a 27" option
-Unadjustable stand. It sits a little low and is fixed at a slight downward angle but you get used to it. And you can get a different stand if it bothers you that much.

For the price point, it is top of the line. 1440p, 240hz, IPS, $140 (at the time of purchase)? Absolute steal. Even now, it's running for $170 and if I was still in the market for a new monitor, I would grab this one again.

reviews.author: Emiliano
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 16, 2025
reviews.content: I got this monitor for my Xbox and it runs perfectly and the colors are super good. The brightness is good as well along with its screen size. I play game like Fortnite and it’s way better now with a consistent 120 fps with little to no drops.

reviews.author: Aaron
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content: Wow, the colors on this Acer are incredible! Switched from a VA monitor and it's night-and-day better—especially with HDR enabled. Old one on the left looks dull and flat next to the right side (Acer). HDR really brings it to life. Game-changer!

reviews.author: Stacey Seman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.content: Works well. Packaging was a little weird I did the discrete box and there was barely any packaging securing it so box was damaged but got lucky wasn’t broken. Monitor works well

reviews.author: Anjan Humagai
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content: The monitor is amazing, some stuff the post doesn’t tell you is that it has a feature where u can clean the screen so there is no burn in, of course it takes longer depending on when u last did it, that is why there is no coverage for burn ins on the warranty, I am a professional gamer and I approve of this monitor

reviews.author: Hallie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 5, 2026
reviews.content: Works wonderfully. Very sturdy, extremely bright

reviews.author: Dipendra
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 20, 2025
reviews.content:
First off, let me start off with the price of this. I got this for only $109 and it’s a 180hz FULL HD monitor?! It was a steal.
Let me list some pros and cons:
Pros:
- Great quality
- Lots of modes
- Low blue light mode for dim areas
- Great brightness
- Great functionality
- Tilt adjustable
- Easy to set up
- GREAT price
Cons:
- The stand color is a little weird
- Stand is a little big for smaller desks

Overall this monitor is a great budget option for people who want to play competitive games with some work on the side. The low blue light modes are also great if you sit at a screen in a dim area!

reviews.author: Braxton
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 12, 2025
reviews.content: These were great. The quality on the display is amazing. Especially for the cost of the product. I ended up buying two. The only negative is how wide the base is on the bottom. Just an awkward shape.

reviews.author: Cody
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 5, 2025
reviews.content:
Monitor was looking really solid with good refresh rate and smooth for gaming.

However after only 2 months the monitor screen became blurry and was unreadable. Definitely not worth for 3 months and the monitor goes bad. Maybe i got a dud, or they shipped me a bad product, but otherwise i'd be warned.

reviews.author: Joseph zumwalt
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 9, 2026
reviews.content: For what it is it's decent I would say I did a lot of research and disappeared to be in my opinion one of the best budget friendly monitors you could find I game on it sound quality is garbage you absolutely will need speakers. I think the internal speakers even cracked the first time I tried using it LOL but it's a budget monitor so you can get a pair of cheap speakers to go with it or good ones no complaints

reviews.author: MAD
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 10, 2026
reviews.content: Excellent monitor with one issue hence the 4 stars. I cannot stand the location of the power button. It's on the back of the monitor and since it's mounted on a multi-monitor stand, it's difficult to access it. Otherwise I love the quality and performance of the monitor.

reviews.author: Kadi B.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 22, 2025
reviews.content:
To who ever buys this I have bought 2 monitors and have had them for a year. BUY ANY OTHER MONITOR OUT THERE! Both my monitors have broke. Idk if it’s because of shipping or manufacturing but both screens have these white lines all over the screen and this white haze, making it hard to navigate anything. Mind you I rarely use these monitors. (I started to get busy and use my pc less) It started out with me just casually using it like normal for YouTube and such and then a white flash and bang the white lines appeared.

Recently brought to place that fixes PCs and they said they could replace the screen because it is broken that’s why the lines are showing up but it’ll cost the same as the price I bought it for plus extra for labor fees. The guy said it would be best just to buy a whole new monitor.
- I plugged an old monitor I had and it is def the monitor only NOT my tower.

reviews.author: Shaunque stanback
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 15, 2025
reviews.content: I've been using the Acer Nitro KG241Y M3 in this past 2 week with my Xbox Series S, and I'm very impressed. The 165Hz refresh rate makes a noticeable difference in games like Aimlab and Marvel Rivals, providing incredibly smooth and responsive gameplay. The 23.8-inch screen is a great size for Competitive., and the colors are vibrant and accurate. The monitor handles fast-paced action without any noticeable motion blur. However, this monitor does not include built-in speakers. It does offer a 2.1 audio output, which means you'll need external speakers or a headset to enjoy sound. While this wasn't a problem for me, it's something to consider if you prefer an all-in-one solution. If you're looking for a high-refresh-rate monitor that's perfect for the Xbox Series S, the Acer Nitro KG241Y M3 is an excellent choice."

reviews.author: Natasja Beaty
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 21, 2025
reviews.content: have had these for almost a year!! bought one originally but ended up buying a second one. truly beautiful picture. setup was beyond easy!!! these don’t have built in speakers, so keep that in mind. there are two HDMI ports, but if you don’t have a second HDMI port in your pc, a display port will also work, since the monitor also has that. no flickering unless you are turning on your pc. definitely worth my money, especially if you want something affordable.

reviews.author: Charles
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 10, 2025
reviews.content: First off: great value. Second: great monitor. Had to mess with windows setting a bunch for a few hours to get it to look right. Id recommend "scenery mode" then disable HDR in windows. Do some goofy stuff with Nvidia settings too to get the colors right. Gsync works. The colors are beautiful, very rich and crisp. Fast response time, good size. Only issue is the stand cant adjust height so i bought a 10 dollar little laptop stand which works flawlessly and allows me to run cables under the monitor. Upgrading from a 1080p 24inch im happy with it. Can recommend. Just be sure your PC can handle it, 1080p to 1440p is 77% more pixels, your PC may not appreciate that.

reviews.author: yahaira zuniga
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 12, 2025

reviews.author: Caitlin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026
reviews.content: Amazing monitor exactly what I needed for my PS5 it does everything it says it does, no refresh rate delay or anything, the colors are bright and exactly what they need to be.

reviews.author: Madi J
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content: Great monitor, haven’t had any problems and it’s durable. A great purchase

reviews.author: Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 7, 2025
reviews.content: I like this monitor a lot and think it's definitely worth the money. The picture quality is excellent but there aren't as many fine tune adjustments available as the AOC monitors, and the height is not adjustable. This is the only reason I gave it 4 out of 5. Overall a great gaming monitor.

reviews.author: Scott
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content: Working great, im very pleased with Asus hardware.

reviews.author: Jeremy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 19, 2025
reviews.content: I absolutely love this monitor! I got the 27 inch 4K Monitor. I use this for my Xbox Series X as I saw it has HDMI 2.1. Everything works great for me. The monitor set up was very quick and easy as they give you the tools necessary in a little bag. The instructions are very clear with pictures. Make sure to use an HDMI 2.1 cable and then enable 4K, variable refresh rate and 120hz if you’re using console in your settings so that you’re getting the best quality. Playing Black Ops 7 on this monitor has totally enhanced my gameplay. I’m noticing it’s easier to track moving targets and I’m seeing enemies from a farther distance than before. Also, going through menus I noticed the text is crystal clear now. I was in the market for a new monitor and this was a huge upgrade for me. I recommended this monitor to all of my friends who game. Definitely try to play around in the settings with brightness contrast and saturation. They also have plenty of preset color modes to choose from as well. I don’t use the preset modes as I prefer to set my own.

reviews.author: DJ Edmonston
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2026
reviews.content: I have always been very impressed with Asus monitors. Not so with this one. I don't know if I have just gotten a bad one or not. After spending 2-3 days going through what should be a basic plug and play setup, I am still having issues and am finally sending it back for a replacement. Monitor would blink on and off. Checked all the HDMI and D cords, all to no avail. After looking at reviews online, many people are having similar issues. blinking on and off. This is my 6th Asus monitor. Will try a new replacement and see how that goes.

reviews.author: D. Rude
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 4, 2024
reviews.content:
This monitor is really simple to use, just plug it in and setup as an extended display. It maintains the same look and feel as the HP Z-Book I’ve paired it with. It’s thin and comes with a basic protective sleeve that will fit in your laptop bag.

The included cable is pretty stiff and prevents the screen from Being as close to my laptop as I would like, but I am getting used to it a couple of inches away. I doubt others are any different.

reviews.author: Dana
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.content: Made me cancel buying a laptop. Connect this to my iPad. So clear and functional and ultra light

reviews.author: Tandi Kritsiotis
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 9, 2025
reviews.content: Light and clear screen. Just uses a lot of battery power from the laptop.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 8, 2025
reviews.content: Perfect for my thinkpad.

reviews.author: Phillip Wickline
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 8, 2025
reviews.content: absolutely light weight and comes in handy to have a second screen to use with my laptop at work during meeting, training, or presenting slides.

reviews.author: wowbear
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 12, 2021
reviews.content:
I bought this to use it as a second monitor for my Alienware M15 R4 for browser, Discord and so on. It's sooooo good that I totally love it!

Pros:
1. Very slim design, solid built.
2. Single cable connection. I connect it to the gaming laptop with just one USB-C cable, versus 2 cables needed for my other GeChic monitor.
3. Gorgeous display performance. No bleed at all. Very even back-lid.
4. Nice sleeve, elegant and soft.
Cons:
The sleeve that comes with the monitor is too soft that it gives zero protection to the monitor in a crowded backpack. If you're gonna throw the monitor into a backpack with a bunch of other stuffs, remember put monitor and laptop together, facing the display side to your laptop's A side, away from cable.

reviews.author: Tiki
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 20, 2024
reviews.content: After using it regularly at my desk I decided to transport it to school. I properly transported it and the screen is now messed up. Be careful with this screen as it is sensitive.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 4, 2025
reviews.content: This is a light weight monitor that has a beautiful display but be aware that you cannot use a C to USB converter to connect to your HP laptop it will only display if the C port is plugged into the outlet for the power cord so you will need to get a round male to C converter to use the other power outlet on the laptop.

reviews.author: Rob P
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 2, 2025
reviews.content: I travel a lot and relying on just a laptop screen is really difficult for me, with spreadsheets, code and financial charts open at the same time. The Asus duo monitor are incredibly lightweight, I was surprised how light when I got it. It's also made of aluminum which feels high quality.
