The flip stand hinge is really solid and holds the monitor in place without any problems, but I am purchasing the 1/4" mount to raise the monitors higher off the desk. The quality of the screens is excellent, I use a MacBook Air M4 and I can have each monitor show independent screens as long as I connect both USB-C cables. It mentions that you. must connect the power cord to the monitor as well, but I have found that my laptop seems to power the monitors without the need for the power cable, but I would recommend plugging the laptop into power so that there is some pass-through. The monitor comes with a really nice zip-up carry bag that has a sleeve on the front to hold the USB-C cables which is very useful. You can easily pack the monitor in a backpack with your laptop without much extra weight/bulk. The only downside is the price, as it is a bit more expensive than many other solutions. However, this is built from aluminum rather than plastic, the bezels around the screens are very small, the hinges are good quality, the back kickstand hinges are also good quality, and the resolution of the monitor is excellent, so I suppose you get what you pay for. It also comes with a 3 year warranty if you register it with Asus, so that is a big plus in my book.

reviews.author: Linda E
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviews.content:
This is a great monitor! I use it when I travel. It's really thin so it slides easily into the front compartmemt of my carryon and it's super light. The screen resolution is great and it is ao clear and the colors are fantastic.

I have 2 others as well and this is my favorite.

reviews.author: Derian Watson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 16, 2025
reviews.content:
Screen: The screen quality is great. Colors look amazing and can be adjusted via an app downloaded from the ASUS website. The screen size makes viewing content very comfortable.

Sound: The sound quality from the built-in speaker is horrendous. Be in a very quiet environment if you want to hear anything playing from it. I recommend connecting speakers to the headphone jack or diverting the sound to play from the connected device (i.e. laptop or computer, etc).

Functionality: Overall, it functions as intended. There are built-in settings that can be adjusted to your liking, as well as the app to further adjust the display. Be aware that it connects to other devices via micro-HDMI. There are, however, adapters if your device doesn't support that, though. The monitor also won't power on unless there is an HDMI signal being received, and for the sound to play through the monitor, you MUST power on the connected device BEFORE powering on the monitor.

Durability: I haven't really dealt with durability issues that others have. The only issue I've has was the headphone jack not working on the monitor I purchased, but the 3- year warrant had that covered. Using the included screen guard as a stand doesn't work so well. It doesn't stand up well with the weight of the monitor, so I ended up purchasing as sturdier stand.

All in all, it's a great monitor. Everything works great and functions well minus the poor sound quality. My main reason for purchasing this was portability and name, but there are cheaper and better alternatives on the market.

reviews.author: thomas sesin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 2, 2025
reviews.content:
Wow. This monitor was so light I thought I had made a mistake when I felt the weight of it, I wanted a quality portable monitor and it did not feel like it judging by the weight. I was very incorrect when I fully took it out and fired it up.

Picture quality is crisp, it’s bright, and was very easy to set up. No need to plug it in, it is powered directly through the USB-C computer connection. I use this for extra space for document parsing, and use it for extra space to watch stock charts. This thing is perfect, it is exactly what I wanted. Well worth it and highly recommended. It even came with a nice Asus carrying case!

reviews.author: The Hansome Family
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 23, 2021
reviews.format: Style: 17" IPS 240Hz w/ Tripod, Carry bag, Cover
reviews.content:
Fell forward on the desk because of the poorly designed magnet base, and completely busted.

The is the worst product from ASUS I've ever owed, and I've been buying ASUS computers for 8 years. Never again will I buy a monitor.

The monitor doesn't come on with power, input, & HDMI connection
Since purchase, the monitor 50% of the time or more, refuses to come on. It doesn't detect the HDMI connection with the brand new cord. I will buy more cords, because maybe ASUS sent a defective cord, but after reading other reviews I believe this to be a manufacturing defect in the monitor. I love ASUS, but this is an expensive monitor to not work. This monitor is more trouble than the money is worth.

reviews.author: TWISM
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 23, 2023
reviews.format: Style: 17" IPS 240Hz w/ Tripod, Carry bag, Cover
reviews.content:
As a gamer who's always on the go, I've been looking for a portable monitor that can keep up with my fast-paced gameplay. And the ROG Strix XG17AHP definitely exceeded my expectations!

This stunning 17.3” Full HD IPS portable monitor is built specifically for gaming, with a 3ms response time, 240Hz refresh rate, and adaptive sync that ensure your game runs with speed and clarity. The colors are vibrant, the contrast is sharp, and the overall image quality is outstanding. I was truly impressed with how smooth and fluid my games ran on this monitor.

But what really sets the ROG Strix XG17AHP apart is its ultra-thin design and built-in 7800mAh powerful battery. I was able to take it anywhere I wanted to go, whether I was gaming at a friend's house or traveling for work. And with the included custom ROG Tripod and Smart Case, it was easy to set up and use on any surface.

Overall, I'm extremely happy with my purchase of the ROG Strix XG17AHP portable gaming monitor. It's the ultimate combination of speed, clarity, portability, and style. Whether you're a hardcore gamer or just someone who wants a high-quality monitor that you can take on the go, this is the one to get!

reviews.author: Blank
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.format: Style: 17" IPS 240Hz w/ Tripod, Carry bag, Cover
reviews.content: The image on the screen is great looks nice and clean. The buttons on the side suck I had such a hard time getting it to turn on. The battery life is almost non existent and doesn’t charge while in use. In fact it will turn off if the battery drains even while plugged in. The stand was a good idea but the kept collapsing on its own causing me to have to catch it before falling face first several times. The mini hdmi cable that came with it was of poor quality for the price and even slightly bent. The carrying case seemed a little small for the screen.

reviews.author: Mauro S.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 20, 2025
reviews.format: Style: 17" IPS 240Hz w/ Tripod, Carry bag, Cover
reviews.content:
This is an amazing monitor and I am so happy with it. I bought this because I do work at home, at an office and sometimes I go to my gf house and stay there. I am used to double monitor setup so this pairs excellent with my 17" laptop.

Image quality is really good. I love how easy is to setup and just use, just pull out the tripod and with a snap you are all set. Also the included bag has enough space to carry a laptop in there.

I know this is a gaming monitor but I haven't played many games on it. I tried CS2 and the 240 Hz at it looked really fluid. Sorry I can't comment in gaming capabilities, I bought this as an utility monitor to carry around and I choose this because I was getting way more specs for a few more dollars than the competition.

I really recommend this monitor for someone who doesn't have a fixed space to work/game and is constantly moving around. This monitor is lightweight, easy to assemble and has excellent image quality.

reviews.author: Just a Reviewer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 4, 2026
reviews.content: I have a fenix 7x that is hard to sleep in. I am trying to monitor and improve my sleep. I wanted to stay in the garmin ecosystem and this product enables just that. The spo2 measurements are much more accurate than my fenix due to what I understand is an improved ppg sensor. The band is extremely comfortable and you do not even feel it on your arm.

reviews.author: Nicholas
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2026
reviews.content: While this band is much more comfortable than sleeping with your Garmin wrist device overnight, it fails to live up to the core promise Garmin makes: it does not contribute to the physiological model your watch creates, so you end up having your watch's model and the sleep band's model that are disconnected. Body Battery, HRV Status, Training Status, and V02 Max had to completely reset when I started wearing this, and it wasn't until I dug deeper that I learned that the watch and sleep band don't work together to create a model - they create separate models. Until Garmin creates a better device ecosystem where their devices work together to create the physiological model, I'll be considering this sleep band a waste of money and would not recommend that anyone relying on Garmin for data on training progress. If you're casually tracking your body data and are not relying on the model for training, it could work just fine but I would advise leaving the watch on at night and putting it on in the morning while you still have the sleep band on so they both sync with the Connect app before removing the sleep band. Garmin - please fix your device ecosystem or give more clear information that the devices work to SEPARATELY track physiological data rather than working TOGETHER to create a comprehensive model.

reviews.author: DPerl126
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 8, 2025
reviews.content:
First, I'm a sleep nerd. At least my wife calls me that. I wear a lot of sleep trackers to bed, including the Wellue 02 ring, Oura, and Whoop. Now I've added this to my repertoire, although it's overkill.

What it's accurate on: HRV, and SP02 (although it only monitors your oxygen levels every minute - which is significantly better than only Oura, which does a snapshot like every 15 minutes and therefore will miss any potential breathing events.

Also - I find the breathing variations to be accurate and in-line with my Wellue 02 ring.

Comfort - a major plus, especially on nights I forget to wear my 02 ring. It will let me know with very good accuracy on nights that I have some breathing disturbances and signs of mild sleep apnea (which, are usually nights I drink alcohol).

Last positive: battery life is truly 6-7 days.

Now for the negatives: Sleep stages I would not rely on. It does ok with deep sleep but overestimates in most situations. I am also wearing an Oura and Whoop and they both are usually in sync and this is not in sync most of the time with those two - which in my opinion are probably the ones to go off of (but I am asleep so therefore it's truly hard to say).

Also - syncing is a major issue. In fact, there are nights it simply doesn't even record my sleep. Or if it does, it ends up showing up much later in the day. This continues to be an issue about once per week.

I do not wear a Garmin watch. The app is still useful. I wear the Apple but take it off at night in favor of the Whoop. For most people, this will be a more than suitable sleep tracker and will give you reliable HRV, and more importantly, give you insights into potential breathing disturbances you may be unaware that you have. For me, this is a secondary device that ends up as a 4th tracker at night, and the majority of people use one / two trackers at the very most, not four like me. That's why I'm the sleep nerd :)

reviews.author: katy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 9, 2025
reviews.content: Works great other than continually failing to sync sleep data. Hopefully this is some sort of software bug, otherwise really like the interface and setup.

reviews.author: Chris D.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2026
reviews.content:
I really wanted this to work, but after more than a month of troubleshooting, I can’t recommend this product to most Garmin users.

The only real positive: it is genuinely comfortable. If you hate sleeping with a watch on, this is well designed and easy to forget you’re wearing it.

The major problem: functionality is extremely limited unless you own Garmin’s very latest watches (for example Fenix 8 and a small handful of newly released models). If you own anything older, including premium models like the Epix Pro Gen 2, the Index Sleep Monitor does not reliably integrate with core training metrics like Training Readiness and stress history.

Sleep and HRV data may record, but critical components needed for Training Readiness frequently fail to sync or are ignored entirely. Garmin’s own forums are full of reports of missing sleep, missing recent stress, or incomplete readiness scores when using this device with anything other than their newest watches.

What makes this especially frustrating is that Garmin does not clearly disclose these limitations anywhere in the product description. The marketing implies broad compatibility, but the reality is very different. You only discover this after weeks of trial and error, following complex sync rituals and workarounds provided by Garmin support. By the time it becomes clear that your watch is effectively unsupported, the return window has passed.

This feels like a buyer beware situation. If you do not already own one of Garmin’s newest watch models with explicit, updated firmware support for the Index Sleep Monitor, this device is largely pointless. Comfort alone does not justify the price when the core promise of training integration does not work as advertised.

Bottom line: comfortable, but functionally unreliable and poorly disclosed compatibility. Do not waste your money unless you own Garmin’s very latest watch models.

reviews.author: Ccmrn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 22, 2025
reviews.content: Worth it. I forget it is on. Very comfortable to sleep with. I don't want to sleep with my watch. Very acurate data and instantly uploads when I get up and take it off.

reviews.author: Rodrigo S. Loureiro
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 13, 2025
reviews.content:
I love this product. I’ve always had trouble wearing my Garmin watch in bed — it’s uncomfortable and often disrupts my sleep — and this completely solves that problem.

The Garmin Index Sleep Monitor is lightweight, unobtrusive, and easy to forget you’re even wearing it. Despite that, it still delivers accurate and detailed sleep tracking that integrates seamlessly with the Garmin ecosystem. Setup was quick, syncing is reliable, and the data quality matches what I expect from Garmin.

If you want high-quality sleep metrics without having to wear a watch overnight, this is an excellent solution. Highly recommended for anyone serious about sleep tracking but sensitive to wrist-based devices.

reviews.author: Austin TX
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 31, 2025
reviews.content:
I'm a huge Garmin fan--but this is a dud sadly. Perhaps I got a faulty device, but the experience has been bad so don't want to try another one.

I tried for 2 days to troubleshoot--turned bluetooth on/off several times, turned phone off/on several times, "forgot device" in app and on phone & re-added multiple times, made sure app was running in background, confirmed it was connected to both phone and app itself before bed, tapped the back of the pod firmly and quickly up to 15 times per the troubleshooting recs, etc etc etc. But (1) it never synced with the app a single time at night despite all of the above and (2) most annoyingly, it would vibrate on my arm randomly throughout the night and wake me up (yes, I checked all of the recommended settings to prevent this happening). So, turns out, the device that is supposed to give you stats on your sleep quality actually ruins it in the process.

The one upside for anyone wanting to try it: it's very comfortable to wear at night. I can't sleep with any 'extras' on my body--I bought this because wearing my beloved Garmin watch is too bulky and uncomfortable since I sleep on my side. Garmin's done a great job at making it comfortable--you can barely feel it on your arm.

Garmin is a great company so I'll most likely try this again in a few iterations, but this one's going back.

reviews.author: haley
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content: purchased this because my other monitor white-screened. It arrived in it's original box, within an Amazon box. it was easy to mount and set up. No complaints so far.

reviews.author: Tsab
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.content: Nice Case! It's very sturdy. well made with good quality. fits my Lenovo Yoga 16inch perfectly

reviews.author: Rezz
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.content: Perfect size

reviews.author: Nora Samuel A
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.content: I use this portable monitor case inside my backpack alongside my laptop. The size fIt so the monitor doesn’t shift around. Function wise, it serves as protective layer against friction from chargers, cables, and other hard items in my bag. The exterior has a clean, minimal look, and the structure helps to reduce minor bending pressure. The magnetic closure is simple, which helps when packing in a hurry. Stitching and edges appear consistent with no loose threads. Overall performance meets expectations for daily commuting use.

reviews.author: Ellis
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026
reviews.content:
This case fits a 15.6-inch portable monitor nicely and feels well made from the first touch. The material is sturdy and has a soft lining inside, which helps protect the screen from scratches and dust. The zipper moves smoothly and doesn’t feel flimsy, so opening and closing it is easy. I like that the case is slim but still offers enough padding to keep the monitor safe while carrying it in a backpack or laptop bag. It doesn’t add much extra bulk, which is great for travel or commuting.

The inside space is designed well, with room for cables or small accessories without pressing against the screen. The handle feels comfortable and strong, making it easy to carry by hand. The overall design is clean and simple, matching well with most tech gear. It works well for both work and travel use, especially if you often move your portable monitor between home, office, or coffee shops.

reviews.author: Gonzales
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.content:
Carrying a portable monitor always made me a little nervous, especially when slipping it into a tote or backpack. This case immediately eased that concern. The monitor fits perfectly inside without extra space, so it doesn’t slide around. The padding feels substantial, especially around the edges, which are usually the most vulnerable parts.
The interior lining is soft and smooth, and it feels gentle against the screen. I like that it doesn’t leave lint or marks behind when I take the monitor out. The zipper moves easily and doesn’t snag, which matters more than people realize when you’re opening and closing it often.
What I appreciate most is that it doesn’t feel bulky. It adds protection without adding weight, and it slides easily into a larger bag. It’s now something I automatically reach for whenever I move my monitor from room to room or take it with me.

reviews.author: Jason
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content:
I purchased this protective case specifically for my 15.6-inch portable laptop monitor, which I use for work and travel. The monitor fits perfectly inside the case without being too tight, and the internal padding is thick enough to feel very secure. The outer material is durable and resistant to wear and tear, which is very practical when carrying it in a backpack or travel bag. The zipper is smooth and doesn't snag.
What I like most is the internal padding. It provides reliable shock protection. The case's structure is sturdy enough to prevent the monitor from bending under pressure, which gives me peace of mind during transport.

reviews.author: Frank Rocha
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.content: Sleek, Minimal, and Perfectly Protective Laptop Sleeve

reviews.author: cici
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026
reviews.content:
The material is thick and sturdy, not flimsy. I throw my monitor in my backpack with my laptop and charger, and I don’t worry about scratches or bumps anymore. The padding is just right.

Love how slim it is. My old case was bulky and annoying. This one adds almost no extra weight or size, so it’s super easy to just grab and go. Fits perfectly in my tote bag. The fit is exact. My monitor slides in snugly—no sliding around. The rounded corners are well-padded, which feels secure.

I got caught in a drizzle walking to the library, and my bag’s outer pocket got a little damp. The sleeve did its job—monitor was completely dry inside. Also nice for peace of mind around coffee cups. Does exactly what I needed: keeps my monitor safe, looks clean, and is hassle-free to carry.

reviews.author: The Wanderer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 15, 2026
reviews.content: This monitor arm is extremely well built and easily handles a large, heavy ultrawide monitor without sagging or drifting. The materials feel premium, the joints are tight but smooth, and once positioned it stays exactly where you set it. Installation was straightforward, and the cable management is well thought out, keeping the desk looking clean. Height, tilt, and rotation adjustments are smooth and precise, making it easy to dial in an ergonomic setup. The RGB lighting is subtle and tasteful rather than over-the-top, which is a nice touch. If you’re running a larger or higher-end monitor and want a sturdy, premium arm, this is absolutely worth it.

reviews.author: Tim Cook
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026
reviews.content:
I’ve been using the MSI MPG monitor mount, and it has been a solid upgrade to my desk setup. Installation was straightforward, and the mount feels very sturdy once installed. It holds my monitor securely with no sag or wobble.

The adjustability is excellent and makes it easy to fine tune height, tilt, and rotation for a comfortable viewing position. Cable management is also well thought out and helps keep the desk looking clean.

Overall, it is well built, functional, and does exactly what it is supposed to do. If you want a reliable and clean looking monitor mount, this one is a great choice.

reviews.author: dom
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.content: Great mount unfortunately the monitor I ordered for it was dead on arrival:(

reviews.author: Travis Sperling
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2026
reviews.content: Bought a brand new arm and it arrived used. All parts were out of bag a loose inside the box and the C-Clamp severely damaged, photo is how I opened the box.
