reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 21, 2025
reviews.content: It's great! I plan to get a 27 inch, too!

reviews.author: Samuray
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 19, 2026
reviews.content: easy to install, very sturdy, flexible bracket design

reviews.author: Maruan elneser
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 24, 2025
reviews.content: Excelente producto

reviews.author: SAPPRO - Phil
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content: I like the stand I just probably expected it to work better. I have a Samsung 49¨ which is a heavy monitor based on the way the monitor is shown I expected it to be able to quickly tilt rotate rise lowered by moving it but the reality is different, you need to tighten the nuts so hard to keep it in place that if you decide you need to move it you need to go get the hex wrench and loosen it which suddenly drops the monitor, move it to the position you want and then tighten the hex while trying to hold it in place. I guess I expected to be able to tilt and move easily though the monitor weighs 30lbs

reviews.author: Jeff Harris
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.content: Very easy to install. Easy to use. Super sturdy. Cannot recommend enough. Will hide cords. Excellent purchase.

reviews.author: Alex M.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 26, 2025
reviews.content:
Very sturdy and solid. Fits three 27 inch LG ultra gear monitors with no problem!

Takes a bit to set up but as a whole one person who reads the instruction should have no problem getting it going.

My biggest pro for this product is how functional it is as far as how well it can fit on various desks sizes. My desk has an awkward metal rim around it that makes any type of product struggle to fit properly onto it, this stand offers the ability to expand the clamp to get it over bigger desks.

reviews.author: Taylor
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 29, 2025
reviews.content:
Overall I love this item and unlike my last purchase, which I had to return, I plan to keep this one.

PROS:
Has two extension arms, meaning you have the middle part that connects to each side, plus an arm AND an additional arm, most would only have the middle part and one extension arm. This means for guys like me with 3 32" monitors you have the option of having them sit horizontally without having to sacrifice, IE one monitor is partially blocked by the other. As I noted I had to return a different brand and that was because of this.

Weight - My monitors are roughly 15 1/2 pounds each and I didn't have any issues moving the arms around with the monitors connected. Says a lot about the stability that I'm talking about this product being able to hold a 15 lb monitor on a second arm extension, as opposed to one.

Install time - About 10-15 minutes, 20 if you factor that I'm a moron who didn't check placement before setting everything up, then forced to disassemble and reassemble in the proper area. Bad joke aside was straight-forward setup but use your own screwdriver, the one that comes with this is limited.

Range - Similar to what I've used in the past. Easy to lift upwards, downwards, tilt the monitor, rotate to portrait. Standard stuff

CONS:

Only one. The middle pole is a decent size, but not tall enough for me. If you're like me you want your monitors higher to reduce strain on your eyes (I'm 6' so I prefer not having to look downwards so much). In my situation even when I had the middle area at it's highest my middle monitor was too low. I ended up using a stand for my middle monitor instead of connecting. Checked with the manufacturer and unfortunately there wasn't an option to buy a taller stand or add an extension, so while I give this 5 stars IMO it's really 4.5 and only because of this limitation.

TLDR - Great product, no regrets purchasing it. Can hold 3 horizontal 32" monitors but middle might be lower than you prefer.

reviews.author: Mike S.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 17, 2024
reviews.content:
I decided to go with a 3 monitor setup rather than my usual 4 monitor setup (2x2) as I wanted to reduce having to look up at the 2 monitors above. My monitors are 27" and when trying to do a horizontal setup, there is just too big of a space in between monitors. Meaning, if I put the third section of the outer arms in, they are just too spread apart.

I could adjust this, but the arms would then hit the wall so I would have to pull my new desk out further, which I don't want to do. For now, I had to rotate the two outer monitors to vertical, which dims the screens a bit and messes with the resolution.

I also wanted to make sure that the mounting bracket to the desk doesn't damage the desk. The box does not come with sufficient protective pads, so I use the round rubber peel and stick rubber feet from a switch and that seems to work fine. No slip, but over time this might leave a mark so I'm looking for something better for that.

My only other options are to get larger or smaller monitors which I'm considering doing as I need move toward 4k monitors for my content creation work.

Final thoughts:
The mount is nice and the cable management easy.
Installation was easy.
Works well on my L-shape Autonomous desk.
I will need to change my monitors to larger or smaller.
Smaller than 27" would use two sections on arms for horizontal layout.
Larger than 27" would use three sections on arms for horizontal layout.
They need to provide better padding for the desk mounting bracket.
Price is okay.

reviews.author: Andy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 25, 2022

reviews.author: Shelton Needham
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 2, 2024
reviews.content:
I've been using the HUANUO Triple Monitor Mount for a while now, and it's been a fantastic addition to my workspace. Here’s my experience with it:

The first thing I noticed is how sturdy and well-built this mount is. Made from aerospace-grade aluminum, it feels solid and secure, even with three monitors attached. Each arm can hold monitors up to 32 inches and 17.6 lbs, which is perfect for my setup. The gas spring adjustment is smooth and allows for easy height, tilt, and swivel adjustments, making it simple to find the perfect viewing angle.

One of the best features is the space-saving design. By elevating my monitors off the desk, it has freed up a lot of valuable workspace, making my desk look much more organized and less cluttered. The ability to rotate the monitors to portrait or landscape mode is also a big plus, especially for multitasking and coding.

The installation process was straightforward, with clear instructions and all necessary hardware included. I opted for the C-clamp mount, which fits securely on my desk without any wobbling. There’s also a grommet mount option if you prefer that.

On the downside, the initial setup took a bit of time, especially when adjusting the gas spring tension to balance the monitors correctly. It’s important to get this right to ensure the monitors stay in place. Additionally, this mount is best suited for wooden desks, as it might not be as stable on glass or particle board surfaces.

Overall, the HUANUO Triple Monitor Mount has significantly improved my workspace ergonomics and efficiency. It’s sturdy, flexible, and offers great value for anyone looking to enhance their multi-monitor setup.

reviews.author: RD
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 5, 2023
reviews.content:
Yes I am giving this item one star to catch your attention of the monumental task your about to place onto yourself. Do not buy this type of triple monitor mount if you are faint of heart. This thing will test you to your very core and bring you to your knees, but it works great once you make it right for you if you can master it and show it whose boss. Yes I realize I may not be using it it in the perfect way, but this thing is an akward nightmare that is almost sentient. I have made it work and am using it to max capacity, but it has been quite the experiment to get it to a stable enough point where I can justify this is better than using the stands my monitors came with. It's been lots of trial and error, multiple busted monitors, lots of experimentation, lots of reinforcement, and lots of fine tuning.

I work from home and have attached 3 32 inch monitors to this thing all horizontal. Whitch I do not recommend because while the description says it can support that, it really limits the range of motion of the 2 adjustable swing arms and you have to be careful not to bang the monitors into each other when adjusting or careful not to bust the screen of your middle monitor with the corner of the side monitor or just destroy the inner monitor corners all together. With 3 32 inch monitors this takes up the whole with and then some of 6 feet. And I can not pull the side monitors as far inward as I would like to get more of a half circle effect.

Like other reviewers have pointed out, the C clamp really puts a lot of pressure into a very small area of your mounting surface. You can drill a hole in your desk but the hardware seems a bit thin to do this. Hindsight, I would have given more consideration to wall mount options. Because I like lots of surface space, no drawers, and simple solid wood tables are pricey and a dining table as the desk I would need would be ridiculous; l opted for a 6ft plastic folding table as my desk. I made sure to buy some metal C clamp reinforcement plates before setting this up for the first time. There was so much downward force from the C clamp, this contraption would have caved in the table and twisted it in half if I had let go completely. I did a little bit of wood working on a 2x6 scrap for the bottom of the clamp and used a thick plastic cutting board for the top of the clamp. And then added in the metal C clamp reinforcement plates for good measure. Trust me, it actually looks decent and not ugly like it sounds. I also mounted a clamp to the wall to grab the middle pole to keep this from ever falling forward (later on, I made it months ago but never mounted it to the wall until after the events below like an idiot).

After using this set up for a few months, I noticed the C clamp was slipping away from the table. Like a moron, I did not remove any of the monitors before readjusting. I had my wife keeping things stable while I was under the desk to readjust the C clamp but the whole assembly fell forward and while she caught it, 2 of my 3 LG 32 inch gaming monitors got busted. I had to purchase
replacement monitors but made sure to take a metal handle and run it across the backside of the C clamp and secure it to the 2×6. This way the C clamp cannot back away from my desk without taking the whole 2×6 with it. Then I added my security clamp/mount to the wall as well.

This thing comes with plenty of spare hardware and parts to fine tune it to suit your exact needs for what you mount to it. Assembling it is a nightmare and the the directions would do a better job as bird cage lining. Pay attention to each arm segment. While all 4 looked the same, they are not and the directions will have you a bit off kilter. I had to unassemble and reassemble several times to get this thing right. Carrying this thing is about as easy as carrying a lifeless metal robot with heavy metal arms swinging every where ready to destroy anything in your path. Trying to add or remove one of the side monitors is rough as the spring loaded arm will fight you and it about takes 2 people to add or remove a monitor. You mount a versa plate with a plastic block attached to it that slides into the mechanical arm, but the arm moves up and down when trying to get it to lock into place. It's especially fun to remove a monitor as the spring loaded arm will keep popping up and relocking the monitor in place. Hence why I said you need 4 hands. You can adjust the tension on so many diffrent places that it's great for fine tuning.

To wrap this up, this thing works well if you have the determination of a warrior but at times it can be like arm wrestling an octopus. I have 2 other mounts made by this brand and they have been fairly easy to work with since I am not pushing them to the make like this hefty boy. It is nice being able to grab a monitor and hoist it up in to the air to demonstrate things on the screen to a bystander. This is also a good option for a standing desk as you would just need an elevated surface for your mouse and key board because the monitors can lift pretty high with a baby's touch. So think long and hard about purchasing this, but it's a good unit and made well. I'm only giving it one star because it's been one **** of a fight making this thing work the way I want it too and using it with 3 32 inch monitors and a plastic table is a fool's errand but I was determined to win this fight.

reviews.author: Joel S.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2026
reviews.content: Great product. It was easy to install. I had a question and customer service went above and beyond to ensure I was all set. Would highly recommend.

reviews.author: Ted Pavlic
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 2, 2025
reviews.format: Size: Single Arm - up to 27 inchStyle: Basic Stand
reviews.content:
Amazing value for this stand. I needed a stand with more degrees of freedom than the original one that came with my monitor, and this stand (which was inexpensive relative to the cost of the monitor) was a great purchase. The base of the stand is a little bulky/high, and the soft material on the bottom of it can rub off very easily if you let the stand catch the back edge of your desk. Otherwise though, the stand is extremely easy to install, and it provides far more degrees of freedom for rotation and translation than the original stand for my monitor. Now my monitor is sitting at a comfortable viewing angle closer to my desk tilted slightly upwards toward me.

Just keep in mind that you have to tighten down the height and tilt adjustments (the things that would be affected by gravity). So, if you want to adjust those frequently, this stand might not be a great fit for you. That said, it does provide a hidden but easy-to-access spot to store the keys needed to loosen it up to make those adjustments. So, if you need to make those adjustments, you'll always have quick access to the necessary tools.

Highly recommended for its price in particular.

reviews.author: Kid Mars
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 28, 2025
reviews.format: Size: Single Arm - up to 27 inchStyle: Basic Stand
reviews.content: This is an extremely sturdy monitor stand. Easily assembled in less than 20 minutes. Instructions were simple. The hex tools came with the stand, but you just need a Nr 2 Phillips Screwdriver for the bolts mounting the VESA plate to the monitor. Take note that this stand has a heavy base to hold larger/heavier monitors up to 22 lbs, but does not take a lot of space. I chose this variant so that it could handle my heavy 24-inch Planar monitor. The included cable management clip was a nice touch. Rotation and tilt feature moves easily and has decent range. I'm very happy with my purchase and it was available for overnight shipment in my region.

reviews.author: Everyday Reviews
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 26, 2025
reviews.format: Size: Dual Arms - up to 27 inchStyle: Basic Stand
reviews.content:
WALI Dual Monitor Stand: A Solid, Space-Saving Base Camp

I needed to reclaim some real estate on my crowded desk, and this WALI freestanding mount did the trick. It's a straightforward, no-frills solution that holds my two 24-inch monitors securely and opens up a ton of space underneath.

Assembly is intuitive, though having a second pair of hands is helpful when you're attaching the monitors to avoid any tipping. The all-metal construction feels sturdy and reliable. The adjustments are basic but effective—you can raise or lower both monitors together on the central pole and tilt or swivel them individually to get the angles just right. The included cable management clips are a nice touch for keeping things tidy.

The key thing to understand with any freestanding mount is balance. If you extend the arms all the way out toward you, the whole unit can get top-heavy and want to tip forward. I keep my monitors positioned more centered over the heavy base, and it's perfectly stable. It's not meant for dramatic, extended poses.

For the price, it's fantastic value. It doesn't have the infinite, fluid adjustability of high-end gas-spring arms, but it doesn't need to. It lifts your monitors, frees up your desk, and does its job without any fuss.

Bottom Line: An excellent, budget-friendly way to lift two monitors and dramatically increase your usable desk space. Just be mindful of the center of gravity and don't expect ultra-flexible, independent positioning.

reviews.author: Chopper
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 15, 2025
reviews.format: Size: Dual Arms - up to 27 inchStyle: Basic Stand
reviews.content: This dual monitor stand is great overall — very sturdy and easy to set up. It fits two 27" monitors perfectly with smooth tilt, swivel, and height adjustments. The only minor downside is that my monitors weigh about 10.7 lbs each, which seems to be pushing the limit a bit, so they can sag slightly if not tightened well. Other than that, it’s an excellent stand for the price and keeps the setup clean and organized.

reviews.author: Harold Rasco
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 6, 2026
reviews.format: Size: Triple Arms - up to 27 inchStyle: Basic Stand
reviews.content: Great stand! It works perfectly fine for my desk. Office looks cleaner and it increases my efficiency. Pous same dat delivery saved my day! Very satisfied

reviews.author: Shanara
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 21, 2025
reviews.format: Size: Dual Arms - up to 27 inchStyle: Basic Stand
reviews.content:
Let’s get one thing straight: if your stand is falling over, it’s not the stand’s fault. It’s basic physics, gravity, and your poor setup skills teaming up against you. Balance it properly.

Now, onto the good stuff: this was super easy to assemble! Took me about 30 minutes, and that includes taking apart my old setup. Did it all solo.
The price? Totally fair in my opinion. I wouldn't pay a dime more for another one, and thankfully, I didn’t have to.

All in all, I’m really happy with this purchase. It’s sturdy, functional, and doesn’t randomly collapse (unless you set it up like a Jenga tower).

reviews.author: dani
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 19, 2026
reviews.format: Size: Single Arm - up to 27 inchStyle: Basic Stand
reviews.content: This is a great stand I bought it because it was not visible and i needed it to go Low . this will go all the way down to the base and its heavy duty.

reviews.author: Helen L.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 9, 2023
reviews.format: Size: Dual Arms - up to 27 inchStyle: Basic Stand
reviews.content: I have two 21" monitors (heavier Lenovo and much lighter Acer) so I wanted this dual-monitor stand to consolidate their footprints to regain more space on my desktop. ASSEMBLY: After the first 3 steps, you would definitely need a helper to slide in the monitor and hold it while you screw on the nut and continue holding it (or the stand will topple over) while you attach the second monitor. Since my two installed monitors are of different weights, the stand initially toppled over. It was only when I adjust the arms to a "W" shape that the stand was able to support both different weight monitors without toppling over. (For about 10-15 min. until we reached this zen configuration, I was worried that we would need to disassemble the entire stand to return it.) You may not have this issue if you have equal weight twin monitors. Note, some screw holes on the stand did not allow for easy screwing. I needed to use more force to get the screws to screw in all the way. EASE OF USE: This budget stand is definitely rough around the edges. It only allows you to tilt the monitors without a tool. If you want to adjust the height, you would need a helper to hold both monitors while you loosen the bolt with the provided wrench to adjust the height and finally tighten the bolt. OVERALL: Once fully assembled and adjusted to my liking, this budget stand can hold 2 unequally weighted monitors and it is quite sturdy. For successful assembly, you must have a helper and be prepared for the initial hassles that I outlined.

reviews.author: Amber E. Stoltzfus
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 26, 2025

reviews.author: Jeff
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026
reviews.content: So far this has worked great. Straight out of the box, plugged in camera and monitor and they synced automatically. I haven’t installed the app on my phone yet so I speak to that. Also to early to speak of the battery life, but as long as you have both units are plugged in this shouldn’t be a problem. Picture quality is good, the camera is PTZ ( pan, tilt, zoom) and the audio quality is great. I bought this for elderly care so I don’t really care about the music option it has. I hope the app works well on my phone, even without knowing that I’m rating it a five star. This is probably the best baby/elderly monitor out at this time.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 5, 2025
reviews.content:
DO NOT BUY VTECH BABY MONITORS - Here's why:

My first VTech monitor (VM901) lasted about 1.5 years before the battery completely failed. The monitor would only work when plugged in, making it essentially useless as a portable monitor. I tried replacing the battery myself but found it was nearly impossible to access and replace. Eventually the monitor stopped working even when plugged in. Of course, this failure happened just after the 1-year warranty expired, leaving me without a functional monitor.

Hoping it was just bad luck, I purchased a second VTech monitor (VM901HD) for $129.95. This one completely failed within just one month of purchase. The monitor stopped working entirely, leaving me without any way to monitor my toddler during sleep and naps.

After the second monitor failed, I contacted VTech support and went through their troubleshooting process, but nothing worked. They directed me to fill out a warranty replacement form on their website. I submitted the form on Tuesday, May 27th, and their auto-response promised a reply within 1-2 business days.

Six days passed with no response. As a parent without a functioning baby monitor for nearly a week, I was dealing with constant anxiety about my child's safety during sleep times. I finally called on Monday, June 2nd to follow up, and only then did they escalate my case.

When VTech finally responded, their "solution" was insulting. Instead of replacing my $129.95 VM901HD with the same model, they offered to send me an RM5764HD—a different model that retails for only $119.95. So not only did I lose a week without a monitor, but they wanted to downgrade me to a cheaper model with no compensation for the inconvenience or safety concerns.

The pattern here is clear:
1. Poor build quality with non-replaceable batteries that fail quickly
2. Customer service that's slow, unresponsive, and doesn't prioritize safety concerns
3. A replacement policy that downgrades customers to cheaper products

My Strong Recommendation: Choose ANY Other Brand.

After two failed monitors and terrible customer service, I cannot recommend VTech baby monitors to any parent. Your child's safety is too important to risk on unreliable products backed by a company that doesn't stand behind what they sell.

I have attached images of all 3 monitors.

reviews.author: David Ferguson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 29, 2025
reviews.content:
Outstanding Baby Monitor With Clear Video and Reliable Alerts

The VTech VM901 has exceeded my expectations in almost every way. The 1080p camera delivers a crisp, clear picture both day and night, and the 5" IPS display is bright, sharp, and easy to see even from across the room. The remote pan‑tilt‑zoom is incredibly smooth, and being able to adjust the view without entering the nursery is a huge plus.

The WiFi connection and free app work surprisingly well. I love that I can check in from my phone when I’m away from home, and the motion and sound detection alerts are fast and accurate without being overly sensitive. The motion video clips are a great feature too—they make it easy to see exactly what triggered the alert.

Setup was quick and painless, and the monitor has stayed connected without any random dropouts. The range on the parent unit is solid, and the audio is clear with no static. It feels like VTech really thought through the features parents actually need.

Overall, this is a fantastic baby monitor that combines excellent video quality, reliable notifications, and user‑friendly controls. It gives real peace of mind, and for the price, it’s an incredible value. Highly recommended for any parent looking for a dependable, modern monitor.

reviews.author: Cara Ellen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2026
reviews.content: Got this after trying a couple other monitors. This one is a clear picture, has great sound and settings. Lots of options for music and multiple sound settings. You can zoom in and out. I love the mobile app!! I can see her when I leave the house.

reviews.author: Mrs. Fulmer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 25, 2025
reviews.content: Purchased this in Feb 2022. Here it is 3 years later and I still think this is the best camera we've ever purchased. It does all the things. Only compliant is sometimes it drops wifi connection so the monitor will beep until you reset everything and other times it fixes itself eventually. After 3 years of constant 24/7 use the battery life on the parent monitor is less than ideal but as long as you leave it plugged in it still works perfectly. The app makes it so you dont absolutely need the parent monitor. I recommend this to every new parent. Its been a lifesaver for years literally. Plus you can use multiple cameras in different rooms and view them all at once or individually on the app too.

reviews.author: Tully87
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 23, 2025
reviews.content: We have two of these and they're great. The first one is about 3.5 years old and the second almost 2 years old. Both are working fantastic still. I love all of the settings including the motion detection and temperature notification. You can set it so if the babies room gets too hot or to cold it will alert you. The option to connect with our phones is also a great feature. If we are gone and are missing the kids we can just check in on them when we want. You can control everything from your phone, or the monitor itself. The battery life for the monitors does decrease after a while, but with the option to use our phones that doesn't bother us much. The picture quality and sound quality are awesome. I also like to be able to talk to them from my phone or monitor. If I'm away and I see them getting ready for bed I can talk to them and tell them goodnight.

reviews.author: Edward Son
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 14, 2022
reviews.content:
So i've been in the market for a baby monitor and wanted one that had a standalone monitor AND Wifi capabilities to stream on an App/Phone. I tried the Hubble Nursery Pal Crib Edition which i liked because it satisified my first 2 requirements and also included a nice crib mount boom arm that centerd above the crib, however i returned it due to poor connection issues and the fact you had to pay to have more than two app devices connected to it, no thank you.

The Vtech vm901 seemed much than the hubble at first. However there were a several issues that made me give up on it. First the app connects ok to the camera however the only way to stream to multiple devices is that you share your account username and password to all users. That may not be an issue to some but when i'm sharing the camera with my parents or in-laws who arent exactly tech savy i'd like to share the camera but limit their access rights to prevent them from accidentally moving the camera or make changes to my user settings (ibaby cam app has this feature). Not a deal breaker but the issues go on. Battery life on the monitor is quite poor even with the screen turned off and the unit on standby it won't last more than 3-4hrs without a power source, this was the case with the wifi turned on or off (radio frequency instead of wifi being used). Also the volume setting on the monitor is too loud in the lowest setting and often found myself just turning off sound all together. Another issue i had was with the App, there is a feature where short motion videos can be recorded. The first few days it would only record upto 2 videos and then overwrite them as new motions were detected. Then after about a week, there would be no recordings at all, even though the setting was turned ON, i tried deleting and re-pairing the camera, resetting the camera, reinstalling the app, nothing worked. I emailed customer service, and received zero help. I receive an automated email stating that service volume was high, and never heard back thereafter. So i gave up on the VM901, really wanted to like it but sadly it was dissappointing like the rest.

I resorted to using the iBaby M8 2k camera, there is no standalone monitor, but i just dedicated an old tablet i had to stream from the camera. Connectivity is great, motion capture videos are on point, and i can share the camera with limited access with no subscription fees.

reviews.author: Nathan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2025
reviews.format: Color: GraySize: supports 17 to 45 inch monitors
reviews.content:
I couldn't be more pleased with this dual computer monitor stand. From the moment I unboxed it, the fit and finish stood out as top-notch. The materials feel premium, and every component reflects careful craftsmanship. This stand not only looks sleek but also feels incredibly robust and sturdy, giving me complete confidence in its ability to support my monitors securely.

The construction is solid and well-engineered. Each joint and connection feels durable, ensuring that my monitors stay exactly where I position them. The installation directions were another highlight. They were thorough, clear, and easy to follow, making the setup process smooth and hassle-free.

One aspect worth mentioning is the tilt adjustment. While it is a bit tedious to adjust the tilt, this actually proves to be a benefit. Once the monitors are set, they stay firmly in place and won’t shift unintentionally. This stiffness does not extend to other adjustments, however. Moving the monitors around when needed is still very manageable. Adjusting the height or rotating the orientation is surprisingly easy, allowing me to switch between landscape and portrait modes effortlessly.

Additionally, the integrated cable management is a nice touch, keeping cords organized and out of sight, which enhances the overall aesthetic and functionality of the setup. Overall, this dual monitor stand is an excellent investment. Its premium build quality, sturdy construction, and user-friendly adjustments make it a standout product. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enhance their workstation with a reliable and well-designed monitor stand.

reviews.author: Ryan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 3, 2024
reviews.format: Color: GraySize: supports 17 to 45 inch monitors
reviews.content: This monitor mount is great. I had the popular Huanuo dual monitor mount before and that one was good for the price but this one is on another level. It feels so sturdy and there is a really long range of adjustment compared to my last monitor mount. Also, it is really nice having the quick release. Even if I'm not moving my monitor around much it just makes installation that much simpler. The toolless cable management is also really convenient, and overall it looks sleek. It truly feels heavy duty and premium with well thought out features.

reviews.author: Trevan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2025
reviews.format: Color: GraySize: supports 17 to 45 inch monitors
reviews.content:
This monitor stand is the real deal. I was looking for something strong enough to hold my two monitors (dual 32") without any play, and MSI delivered. Putting it together was super easy. The instructions were good, and it came with all the tools I needed. Took me like 15-20 minutes, tops. Attaching the monitors was easy too. Dialing in the balance and movement wasn't hard and was pretty intuitive.

I've had this for about 6 months now, and it's still rock solid. No sagging, no shaking. Cable management is clean and easy to use, plus they look really good. Pay the extra and don't get the cheap Chinese crap.

reviews.author: Travis
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 30, 2025
reviews.format: Color: GraySize: supports 17 to 45 inch monitors
reviews.content:
This is a solid, high-quality mount for large, heavy monitors. I needed something sturdy for my LG 45" monitor, and this fit the bill. The description is accurate, assembly was simple, and the desk-clamp option worked well on my Secretlab desk. Because of the size of my 45" monitor, my secondary 34" can’t be stacked due to the arms not having enough height extension, but side-by-side works fine. Removing both monitor stands freed up a lot of desk space. The arms are stable with only minor wiggle if the desk is bumped, and once adjusted, they hold the monitors firmly. Overall quality: 4.5/5.

I deducted stars because a part arrived without screws. MSI support said they’d send the missing piece on 10/17/25, but as of 11/30/25 it hasn’t arrived. I ended up buying M6 nuts and bolts at my local hardware store to finish assembly. MSI support could be better.

reviews.author: Alejo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 5, 2025
reviews.format: Color: GraySize: supports 17 to 45 inch monitors
reviews.content: This is heavy and super sturdy but wouldn't recommend it at all. If your desk is flushed against a wall, the monitors will be in your face as the lower arm needs 12" clearance. If you need the monitors higher, you only have 5" to shift. This is like buying a Lexus with cloth seats and no power anything, a waste of money. Very limited product by a solid manufacturer.

reviews.author: Jennifer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 28, 2025
reviews.format: Color: GraySize: supports 17 to 45 inch monitors
reviews.content: My husband was able to set this up with ease. It attached with pressure screws to the desk and save so much space on his desk. There is a little wire manner on the arms to help make it look better. It is a little heavy so I would make sure to have a sturdy desk, but holds 2 27in screens with ease. He moves his monitors depending on if he is playing his PC or Xbox. He loves it and it looks great from a woman’s point of views.

reviews.author: Benjamin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.format: Color: GraySize: supports 17 to 45 inch monitors
reviews.content: Great build quality, easy to assemble and adequate built-in cable hiders

reviews.author: May Brooks
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 3, 2025
reviews.format: Color: GraySize: supports 17 to 45 inch monitors
reviews.content: I was a bit hesitant to get a pricier monitor arm, but it's honestly really worth it. I have a 32 inch monitor and an ultrawide, and this stand is sturdy enough to hold both with minimal wiggle. The range of motion and positioning function is also great - I have my monitors set up kinda weird to where both arms are off to one side, and it holds up great. The footprint is relatively small as well.

reviews.author: My2Cents
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 19, 2025
reviews.content:
This item is so good, I ended buying two of them. I loved the first one so much that when I needed another one, I chose this one. First for functionality - it either mounts with the included clamps or you can use an access hole to secure it, which is what I needed to do with this one and my desk was too thick for the standard clamp. The price of this is superior. It is a great value for the price and under $30 really hits the spot. It is easy to assemble and the instructions are super easy to follow. I have 2 27" curved monitors I am using with it and it is VERY STURDY - NO SHAKING. 5 STARS!!!
Disclaimer: My rating system: EVERYTHING starts at a 5, and goes from there. I thoroughly read the descriptions and if I don't like the description, I simply don't order the item. If the product shows up as advertised they get a 5. If there are unadvertised flaws, they are duly noted and stars removed accordingly. I clearly specify why any stars were removed, since what bothered me might not mean anything to another purchaser.

reviews.author: Quen10k
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 20, 2020
reviews.content:
This turned out to be a great solution for my dual-monitor set up, giving me some more height for my monitors and space back on my desk. I'll focus on the two primary questions I had going into my purchase for any of those who have similar questions as they're considering these:

1. This mount uses a single bracket for the height of BOTH monitors, how will it work with slightly different monitor models? - There is a height adjustment screw on each monitor bracket to allow you to adjust them up, but it only goes about 1/4". For me, this wasn't tall enough to properly line up my monitors, but adding a washer to the adjustment screw got me close enough to the height I needed. Additional washers for height is not possible or else your monitor will not be properly fastened in place. If you need more than 1/2" of vertical adjustment between your left/right monitors, this is not the mount for you.

2. How close far back will this mount allow me to put my monitors on my desk, against a wall? With my setup (mount offset so a main monitor can be centered, 24" monitors) my centered monitor is about 4" from my back wall, and my desk about 3" from the back wall as well. Due to the single mount and angles of movement for the arms, you're going to have to have your desk a few inches from your wall in order to get your mounts close to the back edge of your desk. You can see in my photos how the arms of this mount naturally angle BACK from the mount in order to get 24" monitors next to one another. You may be able to get things closer with larger monitors.

3. Is this thing really as sturdy as everyone says? Yes. This mount is incredibly sturdy and doesn't move in the slightest under normal desk use. The ONLY issue with this mount is the point where the VESA brackets attach to the arms. Due to how this is designed, there is about 1/8-1/4" of play for rotating the monitors, so it can be very easy to accidentally knock them off of being level horizontally/vertically. This turned out to not be as much of an issue as I initially thought, but it's definitely my biggest complaint about this mounting solution.

4. How easily can you move a monitor forward and backward? When I'm using my office for work I like to have as much desk space as possible and my monitors back on my desk. When gaming, I prefer to have my monitors much closer to my keyboard. For some reason it's very easy to move my center monitor forward and backward, but my offset one is significantly more difficult (i actually have to reach around to the back and move the arm as I need - i think this is because of the angles I have the arms at. I can live with that and it's not a major complaint.

In short, this is an excellent mount for the price. My minor gripes are easily overlooked by the overall quality and price of this dual monitor mount. I recommend for anyone looking for a cheap and effective mounting solution, but have caution if you use different models of monitors and want to have them be perfectly level.

reviews.author: chris pollard
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content: solid as a rock and easy to setup. holds my two 27inch monitors without any over the top wobble from regular gaming and work on my standing desk. comes with short and long vesa mount screws and brackets with standard mounting holes and smaller mounting holes if needed. arrived on time and in good condition. also includes some nice cable management clips to mount on the arms and the base of the mount. all in all i would say this is a solid pick for a clean and solid dual monitor setup.

reviews.author: Dean
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 8, 2025
reviews.content:
I bought these to tidy up my workspace and mount two 27inch monitors side by side. Overall, it’s a great product, especially for the price.

The stand is sturdy, well-made, and feels secure once everything is tightened. Installation is straightforward, and the base provides good stability even with larger monitors.

The arms offer a wide range of motion—tilt, swivel, and height adjustments—so getting the monitors roughly where you want them is easy. The only challenge is aligning the two monitors perfectly. It takes a bit of patience and fine-tuning, and once you get them aligned exactly how you want, don’t touch them, because even a small adjustment can throw the alignment off again.

It frees up a lot of space compared to using two individual monitor stands, which is a huge bonus.

For the price, it’s honestly excellent. You get a solid, functional dual-monitor setup without spending a fortune, and once it’s dialed in, it works really well.

reviews.author: Christian Neuville
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 22, 2026
reviews.content: Great monitor stand overall. Super clear instructions on how to put it together and it comes with a couple of different screw options to mount each monitor. I went with the thumb screw style and had no issues, but they also come with longer screws and spacers in case you need that specific support. Everything is super solid, sturdy, and quality metal. I was able to tighten everything down to where it doesn't move, I just haven't figured out how to keep them leveled out, but at the end of the day its great being able to shift them around if need be. All in all, the metal is a strong/full build and the weight and rigidity supports two 27 inch samsung curved monitors without issue.

reviews.author: Nishimwe Felix
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 11, 2025
reviews.content:
This VIVO dual monitor mount made a huge difference in my setup. As soon as I mounted both my 27” monitors, it instantly freed up a ton of desk space and made everything look cleaner. The arms hold steady once tightened, and adjustment is smooth — height, angle, and rotation were easy to dial in just right. Installation was simple with the C-clamp, and it feels secure on my desk. For the price, the build quality is solid, and the cable clips help keep everything organized.

Frees a lot of desk space

Strong and stable arms

Easy installation

Great adjustment range

Affordable

reviews.author: K. Cart
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 31, 2017
reviews.content:
VIVO Dual LCD LED Monitor Desk Mount Stand

There is a reason this has a #1 Best Seller flag on it. Its extremely good for what you are paying for it.
I wanted to get my desk cleared off and was seriously thinking about getting a second monitor to use. I came across this on amazon and decided to give it a try. I had read through a lot of the reviews. Most of them were good to excellent and some were not. I was hoping my experience was going to be better than some of theirs.
The first thing I noticed when i received this mount was just how heavy the boxes containing it were. I was expecting some heft. I mean this has to support two monitor up to 27 inches each, but it was heavier than I was anticipating.
I found most of the parts inside wrapped in plastic and in good condition. I followed the instructions and did not have any problem assembling this item. You attach the clamp to the post first, then mount that part on your desk where you want it. The clamp seems plenty strong enough to hold it. Just make sure your desk is strong enough. I have a all steel tanker desk so it was not problem for me. If you have a thin top you might want to think about placing a piece of wood under the desk in the clamp to help distribute the weight of the monitors. I would not mount this too glass at all. That might be a common sense thing, but just a warning.
After mounting the clamp/pole to the desk, you will want to place the vesa mounts on the monitors. This kit came with handy thumbscrews just for this, and they worked great. I did not need any special adapters for my monitors, both were 100x100 vesa mount compatible and the plates fit it perfectly. The mounts attached to the monitors will slide onto the plate on the post. This also worked great for me. No issue. Once you get the monitors on the arms, there is a tiny screw cap for the back to make sure the monitors stay on the arms. I will not be rotating my so this isn't an issue, but I put them there anyways.
The post does have height adjustment, do not do this with the monitors on the arms. Take them off then adjust the post. Put them back on and repeat as necessary until where you want them.
I saw some reviewers talking about how they could not keep the monitors level. I am going to guess that they have faulty kits. I have no problem here. The joint that allows the monitor to rotate seem nice and tight, but also easily movable if I wanted them too. The tilt of the monitor can be adjust up or down and then tighten with the included allen wrench. I am sporting a 24 inch monitor on the left and a 27 inch monitor on the right with absolutely no physical problems.
The multiple points of articulation in the arms made moving my monitors around a breeze. They stay where I put them too.
There are hooks for cable management which do help a lot in keeping a clean look. I could only use some of it because of a short power cord and dvi cable.
You can see from the photos that the post is leaning forward ever so slightly. It does not seem to be going any further and it is extremely stable.
One thing I can highly recommend is an Aukey LED gooseneck clamp light. It will clamp right to one of the arms and reaches over the monitors. It provides good light and runs off USB power. It looks like it belongs with the mount. I could not be happier with how that turned out.
I am completely satisfied with this mount. I love it. I recommend it to anybody who needs to get their monitors off the desk and more at eye level.

AUKEY LED Desk Lamp, Clip-on Table Lamp with Flexible Neck, USB Powered with 2 Brightness Adjustment for reading

reviews.author: Nicodemus
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 8, 2024
reviews.content:
VIVO Dual Monitor Desk Mount:

If someone had told me two days ago that you could get a reasonable quality dual monitor mount for anywhere near this price, I would have immediately discounted everything else they said as questionable. I would have been wrong. I'm rarely compelled to immediately write a review on something, but this thing is good under normal circumstances and amazing for the price. I've been working in IT for almost 30 years. I've come across many monitor stands, mounts, and armatures. For what it was designed for, it does quite well.

The construction seems solid. The first thing I did was mount a slightly older Benq 3-D gaming display. It's pretty heavy for a 24" display. Even with only the single monitor swung far to one side, there are no issues with the stand. The height adjustment held firm and there's no noticeable lean, etc.

The tension on the joints is appropriate. It holds the monitor in place in all axis, yet is still easy to adjust.
- Height adjustment requires using an allen wrench (supplied) to loosen the compression on the center post. This may be a bit fiddly once two monitors are mounted as you'll have to hold their weight during adjustment. The monitor heights cannot be adjusted independently.
- Without tools the monitors can be adjusted laterally as well as pivoted and rotated to support landscape and portrait orientations.

The assembly is easy and the necessary tools are supplied. Basic cable management is supplied.

reviews.author: M. Naeem Choudhry
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026
reviews.content:
I’m very happy with the HUANUO TitanLift Heavy Duty Monitor Arms. I use two of these arms for my dual-monitor setup, and they’ve made my life much easier. Adjusting height, angle, and position is smooth and simple, which really helps with comfort and desk space.

The arms feel very solid and well built. They hold my monitors securely without any sagging or shaking, even with larger screens. Installation was straightforward, and once set up, everything stays firmly in place. Overall, these monitor arms are strong, reliable, and a great upgrade for a clean and flexible workspace.

reviews.author: Aurostin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026
reviews.content:
The HUANUO TitanLift heavy-duty monitor arm is very well built and feels extremely solid. The aluminum alloy construction is sturdy and high quality. After installation, the monitor stays firmly in place with no sagging or wobbling, even with a large screen.

The weight capacity is impressive. I’m using it with a bigger monitor, and it handles the weight easily. Height adjustment and tilt are smooth, and the tension holds well without drifting over time. The movement feels controlled and stable rather than loose.

Installation was straightforward. The instructions were clear, and all necessary hardware was included. Overall, this is a reliable and professional-grade monitor mount, especially for large or heavy monitors. Highly recommended if stability is your top priority.

reviews.author: Dana Smith
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 9, 2025

reviews.author: Mike
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 16, 2025
reviews.content:
TL;DR: if you need a monitor arm for a large monitor, buy this. You won't regret it.

Full Review

I use Ergotron monitor arms at work every day, and honestly the difference in quality between the Huanuo arm and the Ergotrons is negligible for home use.

Is the Ergotron better? Yes, of course.

Is the Ergotron 200 extra dollars better? Not for 99% of people.

Pros:
-The arm is packaged very well with closed cell foam. I love seeing closed cell foam when i open a box. It's reusable if you ship a lot of stuff and it doesn't make a giant mess when you cut it like polystyrene.
-2 mounting options to choose from. You can use the dual clamps to clamp the arm to your desk, or you can feed the mounting bolt through a wire grommet hole and put the clamp on the bottom. That's how I did mine and it's perfectly solid. You can also drill a hole through your desk if there's no grommet and then apply the clamp; really that's probably the most secure way to do it (and technically makes it a third mounting option).
-Range of motion is crazy good. It goes up high enough that I can vertically mount my 34" ultrawide monitor. You can also tilt and rotate your monitor pretty easily once you get the tensions dialed in.
-They include a rubber dipped T-handle hex wrench for tension adjustments and clamp mounting. It's little things like this that stand out. Everyone includes some garbage allen wrench with their products but this actually feels nice, and it's big enough that I won't lose it. It's easy to keep in the drawer so if I need to adjust anything it's right at hand.
-It's very well built, everything that needs to be metal is made out of aluminum. All the non critical plastic parts still feel nice as well.
-The value is insane with these arms. I got it for nearly half off (sale plus coupon), but even at full price it would 100% be worth it. And they are often on sale.

At the end of the day there's really no reason to spend hundreds of dollars more on similar arms, this one will work perfectly fine for nearly everyone. In a professional/industrial setting where you're constantly moving and rotating monitors 24/7, yeah maybe go with the more expensive brands. But for home use the Huanuo is perfect.

reviews.author: Brandon
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 29, 2025
reviews.content: Tested the arm before mounting the actual monitor with 40lbs of weight. It adjust just fine even with all the weight, easy to put together, good size and works fine with my 49" ultrawide monitor. My only concern is i see pit holes in the metal due to low qc or cheap manufacturing (seen in other reviews) but other than that it seems to hold and looks good compared to regular monitor arms. I definitely recommend testing before putting anything expensive on it.

reviews.author: SDG
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 14, 2025
reviews.content: I bought this to hold a 55" TV which I use as a monitor in my office. The TV weight is roughly 30 lb and this mount claims to be able to handle up to about 40 lb. I was dubious but it was on sale, it was the cheapest option so I thought I would try it. I am glad I did. The mount is very sturdy, nothing wobbles, desk mount uses double clamp which works like a charm and has enough room for just about any thickness of desk top. The clamp has two screws below the desk and also the bottom part of the clamp can be easily set at different offsets from the top. My 1.5" thick desk top works with this clamp at the lowest offset and my estimate is that the desk top would have to be thicker than about 4.5" before these is an issue for the clamp to grab it. The mount comes in three big pieces (plus some screws and such) and they go together easily. And most importantly, the mount does hold my 30 lb with what seems to be plenty capacity for heavier stuff. It has enough room to move the TV above the clutter on my table (like cups and laptop) and can lower it down to the table level. It rotates all the way around so I can lift the TV up and show something to anyone in the room. In summary, the mount is as advertised and if anything I would say it underpromises and overdilvers. Just be sure to get the VESA pattern adapter if you use a large TV like me. The mount has rather limited hole selections and nothing that would work for a large TV (mine is 400x200 VESA pattern and I needed an adapter).

reviews.author: Job
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 27, 2025
reviews.content:
Over the years, I've owned several top-flight, pricey, gas arm mounts. When I purchase a new monitor, I also get a new mount, and gift the old monitor and mount to someone who can't afford it and needs it. While looking to purchase a monitor and mount for a small room, I searched and read every review. Because of its reviews, I chose HUANUO.

EVALUATION

Assembly
Assembly is so straightforward, it can be done with eyes closed. Seriously. Assembled, the build quality is very good.

Looks
Not bad. The lower arm crossover on the plate post is novel to me. I like it. And the same for the upper arm crossover on the lower arm pivot mount.

Base
Smart. Given its area, it should provide good support and diminished stress on the thinner composite, bamboo, and glass tops. Thumbs up for the double clamps.

Flexibility
Here is where I nearly sent it back. I could not make any adjustments, and I have never encountered this problem before. After 20 minutes of frustration, stubborn persistence was waning. I picked up the instructions and found there were no troubleshooting hints or recommendations. I was ready to send the mount back. I sped through the reviews: no one cited the problem I was having. After returning from lunch, I tried again. After a wee bit, I was able to move the upper arm, and eventually any part of the mount in any direction.

A fluke, I'm sure. Add a troubleshooting section or URL in the directions.

Functionality
It does what I want to do. With a little effort, it works for a five-year-old and a granny. Thumbs up!

Recommendation
Design factors should enhance ease of use and aesthetics (whatever that means). For instance, the monitor mount is slick.

What makes a good-looking mount look ugly is how wires can be managed. The upper arm has a split plastic channel cover for wires. The eternal question is, "Why doesn't the bottom arm have one that works as well?" The bottom channel is smaller in diameter and is not split to push wires into the channel. WHY? Only the designer can explain this mystery. And there are no instructions on how to accommodate a wire with a "big end" on it (in this case, the plug end to the monitor).

I took a short, strong wire, inserted it into an open end, and forced one side of the cover to open enough to place the wire inside and draw it up to the other end. There was a risk in breaking the plastic channel cover. Whatever the solution (if HUANUO can provide it), change the lower arm channel. Wires hanging from the upper arm look crude.

BTW
I believe HUANUO sells a plate for mounting on glass top tables, desks, etc. Smart.

RECOMMENDATION

Notwithstanding two difficulties -- that other Amazon buyers have not encountered -- and solving them, its functionality, flexibility, and price point are very good. I recommend this mount. Sincerely.

reviews.author: Cang
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 25, 2026
reviews.content: First time using monitor arm for my monitor since I just upgrade my old 27’ monitor to 34 ultrgear curved one and this arm work very well, easy to install and great quality. It is easy to move monitor up and down and close or far while playing game. I really like it. I highly recommend this monitor arm if you are looking for one but not too expensive.

reviews.author: Tiffany K.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 20, 2023
reviews.content:
2 screens are better than 1 and 3 screens are better than 2! I can now face forward on my teams calls and have more room on my desk. I was trying to walk before and my monitors were angled and throwing off my balance. Now I can work more effectively and walk and have camera straight center.

Cons- the laptop isn’t adjustable to push back! I want to try and find if they sell a shorter arm. If my desk was deeper this wouldn’t be such a problem.

The installation was a whole thing. Took a few hours. The arms on the side monitors were tricky to push down and loosen the tension- why do they start soooo tense?!?! Pushing down with force, on a step ladder, while trying to unscrew this tightness was not ideal.

I do like the spring loaded side arms- if someone in living room talks to me, I can raise a monitor to talk then lower back down easily.

Another pro I’d like to mention is the quality. Very strong. I’m not worried about the thing falling and I have a very thin desk top (under 1 inch).

reviews.author: Tyler Swartz
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 10, 2025
reviews.content: I honestly don't know how I lived without this product. I am able to multitask and get things done because the monitors are raised and separated making organization easier by having multiple browsers open and jumping from one monitor to the next not trying to find the right tab.

reviews.author: Susan Lynn Adams
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 2, 2024

reviews.author: Spindoc
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2021
reviews.content:
I was using this for a laptop (with doc) and two 23 inch monitors. Everything fit and held up just fine. The assembly instructions are very well put together they even include some extra parts if needed. The product does everything it says and deserves high marks (although I'm not really sure how to rate "sheerness" as it does not seem to apply to this type of hardware.

The only problem for me is the height of my desk. The pictures look great, and the concept seems great, but for me once it was all assembled it just doesn't fit my desk and space. I need to sit on a stool to keep from looking up so high all the time. Regrettably, I need to return the product and look for another option.

reviews.author: Mani
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 2, 2024
reviews.content: I didnt need the laptop stand, so I can't speak to that. But the rest of the unit seems sturdy. Included Packages, bags and Hardware clearly marked and instructions were clear. I had been looking for a while for a stand that stood taller than my 49 inch wide monitor (at least 17.5 in of a 27 monitor at its lowest point). I was pleasantly surprised by the clearance of this unit. I was able to set up both of my monitors exactly how I wanted. I hope it can withstand the test of time and only time will tell but, for now, it gets 5 stars. Price could be a bit better but, not a lot of options for my needs. Overall, I am happy with my purchase.

reviews.author: Summer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 11, 2026

I really wanted to love this but it is not convenient. The laptop sits so far ahead that it is impossible to work with a keyboard while using all screens. I guess you need a giant desk ( wide ) to make it work . Easy to assemble ( woman - not very handy and it took me 1hr approx)
But returning it. Sad

reviews.author: Salvatore C.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 26, 2024
reviews.content: I have another brand with two arms but not the monitor stand. The other one is better but was more expensive. I wanted two arms for my monitors and a stand for my work laptop to be used with a wireless keyboard and mouse. I couldn’t find anything so I rotated the monitor arm straight back and installed the laptop stand backwards. It sits a little higher than I wanted but I am getting use to it.

reviews.author: Axel
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.content: works if you want your monitors in one position only or viewing angle. Not as much range of motion as you would like. very easy to install. very sturdy, monitors will not move or fall. perfect size for my medium sized desk

reviews.author: Timothy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 5, 2026
reviews.content: Amazing monitor for the value purchased, and was very easy to setup. A huge step-up from my IPS monitor and the arm holds the monitor well. It has good height adjustment features because of the arm and fits my desk very well. The 27" proportions are great as well for the price

reviews.author: Russell England
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviews.content: These are fairly easy to put together and are very sturdy. I also like that they come with wire management and a way to run and hide your wires. It attached to the desk and is doing a great job keeping my monitors where I want them. Easily moveable and you can position your monitors however you please. Great addition to my at home office.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2026
reviews.content:
Got this stand to replace the single monitor stand that I had. It’s easy to assemble. Comes with a little screw driver and tool to put everything together. The instructions are easy to follow as well. It’s not difficult to move the arms around, and it stays in place. You can connect it to your desk in a few different ways as well.

The only thing that I didn’t realize, which is my fault, is that the arms don’t go up as high as I would like them to for the monitors to sit above my laptop screen. Probably another inch and a half up would be good.

With that being said, for the price, it doesn’t bother me.

reviews.author: Alex
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 21, 2025
reviews.content:
This is an amazing monitor arm for the cost. I checked a lot of longevity and quality focused online forums for recommendations on monitor arms that didn't suck, and I'll say that these don't disappoint, at least so far.

They don't sag or otherwise move in a way you don't want them to after you've positioned them, they weren't terribly hard to assemble either, and they've got a great range of movement in all directions, whether you need them to just be higher up, or way further back from your face.

The base takes up about 2 inches of space forward into the desk, and doesn't feel obtrusive. It's holding my relatively heavy monitors, with extra weighty cables, and can take a bit of downward or upward pressure from my hand on top before it begins to move, so it's definitely very sturdy.

One thing you MUST be aware of though, is that if you have your desk close to the wall, this will have to push your monitors closer to you, since the arms extend backwards if you push the monitors away from you. Think of a "W" shape, where the two points at the bottom of the W are the back of the arms. Pushing the monitors further away makes it more of a point, and pushes that point further back, meaning it could bump into your wall if your desk is too close. Give your wall and your desk some room where possible.

reviews.author: Testeverything
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 3, 2025
reviews.content:
In my initial setup, I sought to optimize my desktop space by utilizing an arm for my 30-inch 4K Asus display. This display, weighing 18 pounds, is manageable for the arm to hold it without any issues.

When considering the arm’s placement, it is crucial to evaluate the proximity of your arm to the wall. If there is sufficient space on your desk to position the clamp centrally, this configuration offers the most comprehensive functionality. Conversely, if your desk is adjacent to the wall, you will require additional space to accommodate the clamp on your desk. In such cases, the clamp will need to be positioned to the left or right of the center, rendering certain features ineffective.

1. Potential challenges include cable routing and display movement. Cable routing necessitates ensuring that cables have adequate reach. Additionally, as you move the display towards you, the arm may not maintain its level, necessitating adjustments at each step. If the arm is positioned to the right of the center, it will begin to tilt to the right, and the same issue occurs on the left side.

The arm’s design presents certain inconveniences. Tightening the arms to secure the display can hinder adjustments, as loosening them results in sagging. Furthermore, if the arm is only positioned vertically, it will maintain its level but begin to move to the right. Moving it back to the center requires repositioning it downward. The same principle applies when moving the arm downward, causing it to tilt to the left.

To mitigate these issues, it is advisable to adjust the arm to its most frequently used position.

In my setup, I installed speakers on both sides and centered mine at the appropriate height.

Lastly, cable management is a critical aspect to consider. The arm itself was relatively straightforward to assemble, but concealing the cables proved to be the most challenging task. It is essential to plan the routing of cables through the arms. I utilized a power cable, two HDMI cables, and cables originating from both the right and left sides. Due to the arm’s placement, I had to procure a longer power cable.

reviews.author: JeffRey A. Rinker
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content: High quality monitor arm that works well for my 24” TV (as a computer monitor) … easy assembly and adjustment of tension.

reviews.author: Nipdawg
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 9, 2025
reviews.content:
This is a good dual monitor stand for the price. While I was assembling the monitor stand with the two monitors, I found it easy to adjust the tension on the screws to put the monitor where I wanted them. The two monitors are off the desk, providing me more desk space, and at the level of my eyes where I am not bending my neck down. This should help me avoid neck pain problems or stiffness. Adjusting these monitors after installation is easy, the motion range for more custom adjustments is good. The monitor stand seems sturdy and stable where the monitors are not moving from vibrations.

My rating would be 4.5 out of 5.0. There were a few screws missing from the package and the Large Pad missing. I found something to substitute the missing large pad to avoid scratching my desk.

reviews.author: Jack Dang
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content:
I’ve been using the CIDETTY 14” Laptop Screen Extender for portable work, and it works well for my setup.

I chose not to mount the screens directly on my laptop lid because my laptop lid is not strong enough to support the extra weight. Instead, I use the attached stand and place the screens on the desk surface. This setup feels more stable for me.

The two side screens give a great extension of monitor space. It really improves productivity when working with multiple windows and documents. The 1080P IPS display looks clear and sharp.

Connection is simple with HDMI, and setup does not take long. When folded up, it is thin enough to fit into my rolling backpack, which is very convenient for travel.

It works very well in cafés and indoor environments. However, under strong sunlight, the screen brightness is not strong enough, so outdoor use in direct sun may be difficult.

Normally, I prefer working with 15” monitors, but for portable work, the 14” size is good enough. Overall, it’s a solid option if you need extra screen space for indoor use on the go.

reviews.author: Jamie Bender
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 10, 2026
reviews.content:
I didn’t realize how much I needed a laptop screen extender until I started using the CIDETTY 14" setup. It completely changed the way I work. Having the extra screen space makes multitasking feel effortless—no more constantly switching tabs or windows.

The displays are crisp and bright, with clear text and vibrant visuals that work equally well for spreadsheets, emails, video calls, and even streaming content. Setup was refreshingly simple: plug it in, follow the instructions, and it was up and running within minutes. No complicated software or tech headaches.

I was also impressed with the overall build quality. The screens feel solid and well-made, and everything you need comes neatly packaged, including cables and a carrying case. While it has a bit of weight to it, it feels sturdy and reliable—more like a serious productivity tool than a flimsy accessory.

Whether you work from home, occasionally travel, or just want to boost efficiency without investing in a full desk setup, this screen extender delivers excellent value for the price. If you’re on the fence, this is one of those purchases you’ll be glad you made once you start using it. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Brooke Marshall
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2026
reviews.content:
I use this portable monitor with my laptop when I need extra screen space, and it works really well. The 14” FHD display is clear and bright, and the IPS screen looks great from different angles.

Setup is super easy just plug in with HDMI or USB-C and it works. It’s thin and lightweight, so I can take it with me when I travel for work. Definitely makes working on the go much easier.

reviews.author: Yoan Gonzalez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content: Excellent product and price. Incredibly easy to install and use. I set it up in 2 minutes and it was just plug and play using the provided usbc cables. I’m using a 2021 MacBook M1 Pro 14 inch. Great picture quality at 1080p. The speakers on them are horrible. Otherwise no complaints. I like that they save a lot of space and are portable. It comes with a bag. Make sure you check compatibility with your laptop specs.

reviews.author: Kindle Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content:
This screen extender is an excellent all-in-one solution for anyone looking to improve their workflow and manage multiple tasks simultaneously. The combination of the 14’’ FHD 1080P IPS display and the Plug-and-Play USB-C connectivity works perfectly to help eliminate the hassle of switching tabs and maintain steady focus throughout the day without any lag.

The dual-screen effect is sleek yet sturdy, acting like a real "digital workspace expansion" for your laptop. It has helped significantly with organizing my spreadsheets and keeps my creative process feeling fluid. It is a high-quality hardware formula that fits easily into a travel bag. Highly recommended for a professional efficiency and workflow boost!

reviews.author: Anu
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content:
I’ve been using the CIDETTY 14" screen extender for a few days, and it’s been great for productivity. The 1080P IPS display is clear and bright, and the anti glare screen makes it comfortable for long hours.

Setup was simple with USB C, and extended mode works smoothly. It’s slim, lightweight, and easy to carry with the case. Overall, a very convenient and practical portable dual screen.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 5, 2026
reviews.content: Item is heavy causing my laptop screen to slant forward. Color on screen looked good. Did not work for me because my Mac can only have two screen maximum. Only gave it 4 stars due to heaviness of product.

reviews.author: Randy Schrager
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content:
This laptop screen extender delivers where it matters most: portability and picture quality. The panels are lightweight and fold neatly, making them practical for travel or moving between workspaces. Setup is straightforward with USB-C or HDMI, and connectivity has been stable in daily use.

The 1080p IPS screens look sharp for spreadsheets, browsing, and multitasking. Colors are decent and brightness is sufficient for indoor environments. The frame feels sturdy enough once attached, though it’s important to position it properly for balance.

Value for money feels reasonable considering you’re getting a triple-screen setup in one compact system. It’s not meant for professional color grading, but for productivity and convenience, it performs well.

reviews.author: Wolford2129
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 23, 2020
reviews.content:
I was recently diagnosed with Atrial Fibrillation (Afib). This works great for tracking my condition while sleeping. I was worried that it wouldn't fit, since it looked big in the product pictures, and I have skinny fingers/thumbs. It fits nice though. Not too big or small, and it's comfortable. I was also worried because some reviews said that it was not accurate, but mine seems to be really accurate. The heart rate matches my Garmin 405CX (w/chest strap) and my Emay portable ECG. The oxygen levels match the small pulse oximeter I have within 1%-2%.

It's super easy to use. Just put it on your thumb and it turns on and starts recording. So far I can use it all night, sometimes 9 hours or so, and it still has a little under half battery left. If you have it on over 10 hours it will start a new recording as each one is limited to 10 hours, but it does it automatically so you don't loose any data. The screen goes off after about 30 seconds and you can turn it on (for another 30 seconds) by tapping the round circle on the screen. This lets me easily check if I wake up in the middle of the night.

I especially like the graphs that their software produces. The oxygen level and heart rate are vertically lined up so it's easy to see what each was doing at a particular time. I wish the lines/bars on the "movement/activity" graph were a little larger so you could see movement better, but they are good enough. One graph shows all night on a single line with blocks of 1 hour (up to 10 hours) so you get a big picture view. Then it's broken into another graph that spans over 4 pages or so and are in 1-minute blocks displayed at 4-second intervals. For me, I can easily see when I go into and out of Afib. The device uploads data to PC quickly and I especially like that I can convert an entire graph/report to PDF with one click. This makes it super easy to keep permanent records. With my PDF program I can have two windows (with different nights) showing to compare.

I also have Sleep Apnea, so this allows me to monitor my oxygen levels, something I've wanted to do for a while. This lets me know that my CPAP is working.

reviews.author: Lucky Bones
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 28, 2024
reviews.content:
I will absolutely recommend this product! This has helped me have a greater piece of mind and has really impacted how I sleep and live my life. I also suggest listening to my review to find out why!

"Welcome to Spitefully Yours, your go-to podcast for candid conversations, heartfelt anecdotes, and now cutting-edge health tech reviews! 🎙️ In this episode, we're diving deep into the wellness world with a review that'll leave you breathless! 💨 Get ready to embark on a journey of discovery as we explore the incredible features of the Wellue O2 Ring Pulse Oximeter.
