But that's not all – we'll also delve into how this innovative gadget revolutionizes our approach of self-care and health monitoring. 🚀 With its intuitive app and seamless integration, staying on top of your wellness game has never been easier – or more stylish! 💼

So, whether you're a fitness fanatic, a wellness warrior, or simply curious about the latest in health tech, this episode is a must-listen! 🎧 Tune in to Spitefully Yours and discover why the Wellue O2 Ring Pulse Oximeter is the ultimate game-changer for your health journey. #Wellness #HealthTech #SpitefullyYours #WellueO2Ring #PodcastReview 🌟"

reviews.author: JazzFan97
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 30, 2025
reviews.content: This is a useful item that appears to generate reliable (or at least sensible) oxygen data while you sleep. I found it comfortable enough to sleep in even though I have very small hands, and though the app wasn’t incredibly intuitive at first, I did figure it out. Downside, at least with my use, is that it needs to be charged pretty much every day, and if you don’t remember to do that, it quits overnight without capturing O2 in the wee hours if the morning, which for me is the vulnerable spot.

reviews.author: Jeff
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 23, 2020
reviews.content: I purchased this oxymeter in hopes that I could wear it when walking. The ring certainly fit snugly on my index finger or thumb and even my baby finger, which lead me to believe that it would stay in place on one of my fingers to enable an ongoing oxygen absorption reading when I walk. I was incorrect. The reading when sitting perfectly still was within 1 point of 2 of other oxymeters I own, one by the same manufacturer. However, when I walked with it on my finger (and I tried three different fingers over the period I tested it) were totally inaccurate. Up to 8-9 points off from when I wore one or the other oxymeters in my possession on my other hand, rendering it worthless for the purpose I wanted it for, which was an accurate measurement of my oxygen when walking. It should be noted that I was walking in the house with it, in a controlled weather environment.. I exercise walking two consecutive miles (not on a treadmill) within my house on a daily basis and was interested to see how my oxygen absorption was while walking, besides the daily measurement I take when sitting still. I presently have to hold one of my other oxymeters in place on my fingertip when I walk to accomplish this and with the wearability of this particular device, was hopefull that I could just put it on my finger and not have to hold it like I do with the other ones, when I walked, to get a good reading. It failed. As a matter of experience, although it snugly fit on my finger it did not provide a good reading, even when I attempted to hold it in place on my finger like I do with my other oxymeters. I can't speak how this oxymeter works during sleep, but it certainly did not provide usable readings when walking. When I compared it to the other oxymeter on my other hand, held in place with my thumb as it clipped over my fingertip, the ring oxymeter was up to 8-9 points off a great deal of the time. Again, I can't speak for how it works during sleeping, but suggest not purchasing it as an oxymeter to use when walking, thus my low rating. I subsequently returned it.

reviews.author: Michael M. Clarke
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2021
reviews.content:
I bought this to monitor my O2 overnight because I have breathing issues.

I like the product and the information it has provided has been VERY helpful to me and my peace of mind. The product seems well built and has worked perfectly. The documentation does not explain the readings very well, but I found a bunch of information online that explained in detail all of the parts of this piece of equipment and what the information means. However, there are some tricks to getting the most reliable readings.

Finger position. Before you use this over night, take a few minutes to test the finger position of the device. Put it on a finger and turn on the App, and go to the Dashboard. This will show you O2 and heart rate, PLUS the strength of the O2 pulse signal (green bar graph on the right). I found that putting the device on different fingers, and on different parts of each finger, made a HUGE difference in consistent readings. The device, which uses a rubber band type attachment around the finger, can be uncomfortable on larger fingers and for extended periods of time. The device works best on the meaty part of the finger and I got the best signal on my left index finger. So, play with it. Put it on, give it a minute to catch up, and see which finger gets you the most green on the graph. YOU MUST HAVE A FIRM FIT AND GOOD PI TO GET CONSISTENT RELIABLE READINGS.

This is important because if the device is too loose on your finger, your graph in the morning will be all over the place. Too loose and it slips around and does not get enough signal. Move off the meaty part of the finger, for comfort, and you get poor signal. This can give you extremely unreliable information. Once I found my right spot, and left it there, and got used to the device over night, my results were consistent and reliable.

This has helped me find the best sleeping position to get the best O2 overnight. It has also alleviate my fears of sleep apnea. Its been well worth the purchase and I recommend the product!

reviews.author: ManReview
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2025
reviews.content: Be aware that this O2 monitor is not accurate at times. I had an overnight study and my clinical grade O2 was higher and this ring showed drops of up to 10% lower a few random times compared to medical grade O2. My suspicion is it becomes loose or shifted while worn. I feel this has caused the readings to be off or alarming below preset threshold at times. The data recorded by app for the O2 ring has been getting broken in parts of up to 4 time segments over night. This is the same as taking it off and putting it on. A connectivity issue of some sort as no data from sensor causes a new reading to start and old saved. When sleeping over night i get false readings below preset alarm which can not be trusted and they can be a nuisance waking me. The HR portion is correct as compared to my HR monitor. The other issue is design as the button on top is on the wrong side as you need your other hand to swipe it to view display. If you wake at night this is too much movement needing your other hand. If button was on opposite side of display you could reach with your pointer finger if wearing on thumb or use thumb to reach button if wearing on pointer finger all with same hand. Battery life has slowly decreased where i charge it daily for over night use. This device is good for self monitoring in the event you need to monitor and confirm using other device. My plan was to use this for camping as i could not bring my cpap machine. I have changed my mind using this as an alternative. Overall good O2 monitor considering mentioned.

reviews.author: William B. Otto III
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2026
reviews.content: As advertised, works well and appears accurate.

reviews.author: Ed B.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 10, 2026
reviews.content:
I’ve been using this ring for a while at night and it’s been helpful in letting me know when my O2 is low or pulse is erratic. The ring works with a nice app that provides 24/7 reporting of your results. The app is easy to maneuver through and you can add notes at the bottom of the reporting page. I mainly use this at night, to monitor my O2 levels.
.

reviews.author: gr2
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.content: This monitor arm works great with my 24" computer monitor. Assembly was very simple, just follow the instructions. I had no problems. I used the included C clamp and it is super sturdy. It also supports grommet base mounting. It has a VESA mount that works with most computer monitors up to 22 pounds. I like that you can dial in the position with ease. You adjust the tension on the gas spring mechanism to keep it nice and steady. Including raising, lowering, tilting, rotating, panning, etc. It has built in wire management capability. It is budget friendly. All things told, this is a great monitor arm and I would recommend it!

reviews.author: Houls
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.content:
The ErGear Dual Monitor Arm completely upgraded my desk setup! I was a little intimidated at first, but installation was honestly fast and straightforward. The tool-free VESA mounts made attaching my two 27” monitors simple, and the instructions were clear and easy to follow.
Once installed, the arms feel incredibly sturdy and secure. There’s no sagging, even with larger screens, and the adjustment range is fantastic. I can tilt, swivel, rotate, and adjust height effortlessly—perfect for switching between work mode and streaming mode. The cable management system keeps everything neat and clutter-free, which instantly made my workspace look cleaner and more professional.
I also love how much desk space this freed up. It feels more organized and ergonomic, and I’ve already noticed less neck and shoulder strain.
If you're looking for an affordable, heavy-duty dual monitor mount that’s easy to install and fully adjustable, this is a fantastic choice. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Booby D
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content: This thing work amazingly!!! Super easy to install, its heavy duty and strong. Its an awesome deal for the money. It sets the monitors at the perfect height and position around your main screen. Im very happy with it! 2 mounting options, both work great.

reviews.author: YAAU
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 22, 2026
reviews.content:
Assembly of the actual stand is easy, mounting to a monitor is not because of industrial design fluff. Gotta make the side you DON'T see look "sleek" and "smooth" and "organic" and "integrated" because...reasons. While this is on the manufacturer(s) of the monitor itself, what semi-modern monitor uses 100x100 VESA anyway?

The included long screws are too long. Even using the plastic spacers they'll bottom out with a 2mm gap; add washers and it's a lever arm and not solid or confidence-inspiring. I used a die grinder to remove the excess material closer to 75x75 and used the included short screws, perfect. So either include 25mm screws instead of the 30mm (but then the spacers would be too long), or just update the mount plate to 75x75 only, and throw 100x100 "compatibility" into the dustbin of history where absolutely no one will miss it.

Arm does move/adjust smoothly and is holding a 34" widescreen where I left it 2 days ago, so it IS a great product once it's properly fitted.

reviews.author: SKOSNAS
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.content: Great price for a very adjustable monitor arm! I love I can move the monitor closer to me or further away and every other direction I want to move it. I know some reviewers said the monitor tilted forward, I would advise to keep in mind that at every point of articulation, there is a hex nut that can be tightened with the tool included in the box with minute arm. I adjusted mine twice and then tightened the hex nuts at the tilt point and connector point and have had no issues with the monitor dropping forward and not holing after a week of daily use and moving forward and backward. I use this arm for a 34” ultra wide curved monitor and it performs flawlessly.

reviews.author: Justin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 26, 2025
reviews.content:
This thing is built like a tank. Don’t worry about your monitors, fear for your desk! The box it comes in shipped at 20 pounds. Everything is top notch solid and fits snugly.

Has a max height of 22” and width of just over 3 feet. Did I mention it is sturdy? Holds my 32” (which is 24 pounds) at max height and length like it’s a feather. Not fiddly in the least, this allows for infinite on the fly adjustments without tools. Just position at will. I’m absolutely thrilled with the performance of this product.

Note on assembly: I hadn’t contemplated exactly how I was going to mount two monitors onto these arms. Here is my recommendation.

Mount the desk bracket and the bottom half of the arms to the desk. Then attach the VESA plate with the other half of the arms to the monitors. That way you only have to deal with half of the monitor arm and it just inserts onto the rest of the bracket firmly. This will make sense when you see how it assembles.

Comes with all the hardware and tools. This is top notch. In the picture, that is a yardstick laying atop the arms. This is holding a 32” and a 27”.

reviews.author: WatermarkSD
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.content:
Best bang-for-the-buck monitor stand that I've found. Instructions that you can actually follow (I know!), a decently made product with all the screws clearly labeled (I didn't use them as I had my own, but nice to have). I installed it in 5 minutes, and then immediately bought another one :)

Now that I think about it, "bang for the buck" makes it sound like "well, for the money, it's not bad". That is not the case: the value is EXCELLENT. Unless I had a REALLY heavy monitor, I think spending more would be a total waste.

Great job on a well made monitor arm, ErGear!

reviews.author: Travis1210
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 29, 2023
reviews.content:
So I purchased two of these after I purchased the first one and received it I mainly ordered a second.

OK let’s get a few things out-of-the-way. There are a ton of monitor arms out there. What sets this one apart first off it actually works great. A lot of cheaper arms will not hold the weight they advertise. I love the fact that this has a built-in USB 3.0 connection. This is extremely handy for hooking up an external hard drive, or hooking up a dongle for a mouse or keyboard, or for just hooking up a flash drive, or even charging a phone. It is very well hidden underneath the side of the stand and once it is bolted and installed, N is extremely secure. Next to the bill quality of the arm is great. It has a nice set screws that allow you to set the tension of the swivels and to set the tension of the tilt and rotate function. The gas arm is adjustable for different weights of monitors, which I will speak about below. The cable routing mechanism is wonderful and definitely makes the install looks super clean. The attachment points are very secure and actually have adjustments so this could be used on a thin table as well as a very thick countertop style table. I’m using it on a almost 2 inch thick butcher block countertop, and I moved it down to the second mounting location and works absolutely great and fits wonderfully. It has nice feet on the bottom so that it doesn’t try to slide around and if you desire you can permanently install it by drilling a hole and using the supplied hardware to build it to your table.

Now to the meat and potatoes of this on my two separate arms, I am running a 32 inch TV and a 32 inch gaming monitor. On the first arm is a Samsung 32 inch television it’s not super heavy, but was able to adjust the arm to work perfectly for that weight. On the second arm I am running a LG 32 inch gaming monitor Which is relatively heavy. It holds this weight no problem and is super secure. This monitor was quite expensive and I definitely wanted a good arm to keep it secure and I couldn’t be happier.

The mount works extremely well especially on the gaming monitor. I am using an adapter on my 32 inch TV to bring it down to the standard 100 X 100 mount. It does push it out from the wall a little bit but not much and is perfectly acceptable, and works wonderfully.

reviews.author: Rob B.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content: I wasn't sure I wanted to trust my $1000 ultrawide monitor to a monitor arm in this price range, but I was pleasantly surprised how well made it is and how adjustable it is, after only minor adjustments at set up. The quick release vesa plate makes it easy for one person to mount the monitor without assistance. The grommet mount is rock solid.

reviews.author: Sarah
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.content: Extremely durable and well made. It even has a rotation limiting feature that prevents the bottom part of the arm from going behind your desk and hitting the wall. This is the only arm that I know of at this price point with this feature and it’s not even advertised!

reviews.author: Peone
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2026
reviews.content: I am very impressed with this support arm. The installation instructions are very clear, and the supplied hardware is first quality. Installation is straightforward, you don't need any handyman skills at all. If you can't clamp to the back of your desk/table AND your desk/table does not have a grommet hole in the top you will need to drill one hole, but otherwise this installs easily with only the supplied hardware and tools. I'm only hanging a 40-incher on it, so I can't say for sure how well it functions with a full 49 on it, but I'm having absolutely no trouble adjusting all the tensions to hold my monitor exactly where I want it and yet it still allows me to change monitor position easily at will. I'd be surprised if there was a problem with a bigger, heavier monitor, but I can't say from personal experience. There's a solid, quality feel to the entire unit. If I had to hang another monitor I would buy another one of these in heartbeat.

reviews.author: Spencer N.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 25, 2025
reviews.content: I am using this with a 34in 24 pound monitor. Overall it does what I need, I bought this as my old one could not support a level tilt and this one can. However, I would have doubts about it holding up over time with an Odyssey. I am ABSOLUTELY not a fan of the clamp. It is sturdy I guess I can give them that, but it is a pain in the butt to install and feels very hacked together. Range of motion left to right is pretty limited unless you want the monitor right in your face or have a very deep desk. I have not been using it long enough to comment much on stability, but at a glance it feels adequate. It shakes when I type (I am a fairly heavy typer) but so does my 27 inch monitor mounted beside it. For what its worth, I am going to keep it and try it out, but I will be curious to see how it holds up long term.

reviews.author: Arthur Azeredo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
reviews.content: best monitor arm i’ve had!! very easy to assemble, and also to use!

reviews.author: Ry
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 8, 2025
reviews.content:
I bought this VIVO monitor stand specifically for my 49-inch Samsung monitor, and it handles the size and weight perfectly! It feels incredibly stable and secure, which is a huge relief with such a large screen.
I paired it with my VIVO desk, and the combination is seamless—it looks like they were made to go together. Best of all, it has completely cleared up my working space, giving me so much more usable desk area.
The installation was a bit more difficult and time-consuming than I anticipated, but the final result is well worth the effort. Given the stability, functionality, and space it created, this is an easy 5-star product!

reviews.author: JT
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 22, 2025
reviews.content:
A main reason people get an arm is to adjust the height and depth. As someone who stands and works half the time, I needed something that I can easily adjust up and down. Sadly, you have to crank an allen wrench tool 20 times to loosen the arm to adjust the height. After installing I just found this out and I’m furious. What a piece of junk. Even when it moves it’s a measly 5” at most.

This is my second VIVO product and the keyboard tray was poorly designed and bang my knees so that’s also going back with this piece of junk.

Another frustrating thing is the arm and base is so bulky that if you are trying to save space on your desk the base is really thick and the arm goes right against any shelves or computer. So I wanted to move it 14” to the left but the arm doesn’t go 14” to center the monitor. So it has to go in the middle.

False advertising on the image which is what I was hoping for. Do not buy this unless you want a FIXED and CENTERED stand with at least 10” of spade behind the stand arm and wall.

Industrial designs that fail basic requirements should be on the Amazon naughty list.

(The hydraulics are supposed to let you move the display but even when tightened properly it will move with time and gravity)

reviews.author: Jason A. Carnes
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content: Works, I like it more than I expected. The cable management was bonus.

reviews.author: Malcolm Moussignac
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 22, 2026
reviews.content:
I recently picked up the WALI Single Monitor Mount for my desk setup, and I’m thoroughly impressed with the quality at this price point. It’s a massive upgrade over the stock stand, both in terms of ergonomics and reclaimed desk real estate.

What stands out:

Smooth Gas Spring Adjustment: The tension is easy to tune using the included hex key. Once dialed in, moving the monitor up, down, or tilting it is effortless, and it stays exactly where I put it without sagging.

Sturdy Build: I’m using this with a larger screen near the upper end of the size range, and the mount feels incredibly stable. There’s no wobbling or "bouncing" when I’m typing or gaming.

Easy Installation: The choice between the C-clamp and grommet base is great. I went with the C-clamp, and it took less than 15 minutes to get everything mounted and cable-managed.

Cable Management: The integrated channels do a great job of hiding the power and DisplayPort cables, keeping the "floating" look clean and professional.

Bottom Line: If you're looking for a gas spring arm that can handle 34-inch ultrawides without breaking the bank, this is it. It feels premium, looks sleek, and functions perfectly.

reviews.author: Ramesh
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.content: Great. Easy set up. Might be easier with two people. Well packed and labeled for setup. Almost zero building required. Is holding up a 32 inch curved lg monitor which is about 19 pounds. Works perfectly for my desk, now I have space for books and reading. Would buy again and recommend.

reviews.author: Z.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.content:
I finally bought something I actually needed. This was far better quality than expected. Its fit and finish is amazing. Installation was minimal effort. The price was far lower than I expected to pay for this level of quality. I honestly expected something that would simply work and look horrible and have poor quality. I was pleasantly surprised when I opened the box. This should probably sell for double what they are charging.

Height adjustment is easy and moving the monitors around requires minimal effort. Really a great product that I didnt think I would be this excited. If you have been on the fence about getting something like this like I was just do it and get this one. Again its much higher quality than I expected and isnt your run of the mill amazon product.

reviews.author: Werking reviews
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 23, 2025
reviews.content:
The WALI Single Monitor Mount performs about the same as my VIVO single monitor arm, which was a pleasant surprise given the lower price point. Setup was straightforward, and the gas spring adjustment works well once dialed in. It holds my monitor securely and offers the full range of motion I expected—tilt, swivel, rotation, and height adjustments all function smoothly.

That said, the materials and overall build quality do feel a bit cheaper compared to higher‑end arms. It’s not flimsy, but you can tell where costs were saved. If you’re willing to spend roughly 60–70% more, you can definitely get a noticeably sturdier and more refined arm. Still, for a budget option, this one delivers exactly what it promises.

Pros
- Works just as well as the VIVO arm in terms of functionality
- Easy to install with both clamp and grommet options
- Gas spring provides good adjustability once set
- Excellent value for an entry‑level monitor arm

Cons
- Materials feel cheaper than premium alternatives
- Build quality isn’t as high as more expensive arms

Overall
As an introductory monitor arm, this is an excellent choice. It does everything the VIVO arm can, just with slightly lower‑end materials. If you’re on a budget or setting up a simple workstation, it’s a great fit. If you want something more robust for long‑term heavy use, spending a bit more will get you a significantly more refined Monitor arm.

reviews.author: Juan David
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 9, 2026
reviews.content:
I love this dual monitor stand. The materials feel very solid and well built, and the overall quality exceeded my expectations. The arms have great mobility, making it easy to adjust the monitors to different positions, and the height adjustment works smoothly.

Everything feels sturdy once installed, and the installation process itself is super easy and straightforward. Honestly, it’s exactly what I was looking for.

I would 100% recommend this product to anyone looking for a reliable and high-quality dual monitor mount.

reviews.author: B M
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 17, 2026
reviews.content:
Okay, so I am not going to start writing reviews to hopefully help others decipher what is JUNK and what is a decent item. First I want to say this item is getting 5 starts because of the VALUE for the money. Is it the best quality? NO. Is it on the level of popular name brands? NO. However, for the price point, it is a GOOD VALUE. While the metal is not as thick of some of my other monitor arm mounts, nor the springs as high quality, you get something that is USEFUL, ADJUSTABLE, and just plain works for the $$. You can buy this item knowing that it will mount your monitor arm and look good.

If you are looking for something high end, or super high quality, buy a more expesive product.

reviews.author: SANKSQUATCH
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 18, 2025
reviews.content:
Compared to arms I bought in the past, this one is very good. It’s sturdy and the clamp is reliable.
Overall great design.
The only two things you should be wary of, is to not over-tighten anything on the arm OR the screws that go into your monitor.
The arm comes with a pack of assorted little screws to help mount it.
The screws are universal so measure them carefully before you insert them.
I had to get creative with the screws to make it work for my ASUS ROG 1080p 24 inch curved monitor.
In terms of functionality and sturdiness, this product is very good. I would certainly recommend this to a friend or even purchase it again in the future.

reviews.author: Troy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content:
I purchased this mount to go with my MSI 32in curved monitor. Installation took me about 30 minutes to complete. I've been using it for a few months now and it has held up well. I have it attached to the edge of my desk and and I find it to be both durable and sturdy.

It can be adjusted to the left, right, up, and down as well as tilted back and forth! One thing i didn't realize when i bought it is that it can also be rotated to make the monitor vertical if you would prefer it that way for viewing! I thought this was a huge plus since I didn't even know it was a feature.

After installation you can adjust tension points to make movement of the arm bar more fluid. After many adjustments, it is my opinion that it does not have the best fluidity of movement. I found it to be rather tight and a little hard to move around even at the lowest weight setting I could put it on before the weight of the monitor caused it to sink. I have to use both hands to move it around without feeling like I might break my monitor. If you are buying this as someone who intends to move the monitor around frequently, maybe this is not the right mount for you.

If you need an adjustable monitor mount, and you are going to leave the monitor in one comfortable spot after installation, you can make this purchase with confidence.

Overall, I am very happy with the purchase. It's safe to say that I use it daily and, between me and my wife, it gets adjusted frequently. It's held up, kept my monitor safe, and made our time at the computer so much more comfortable. Having the perfect angle, height, and distance to view from can make all the difference at the end of the day.

reviews.author: Matthew Pilar
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 13, 2026
reviews.content:
Fantastic heart rate monitor for under $100. Setup was fast and easy with my Garmin Edge 840, Apple Watch, and Zwift.

Connections are stable, readings are accurate, and it stays comfortable even on long rides. Battery life has been solid too.

If you want a reliable chest strap without spending big money, this is a great buy.

reviews.author: j conger
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 29, 2025
reviews.content: Good, comfortable strap and unit that functions well. Haven't had the problems I did with my other monitor that started telling me my heart rate was 170 while I was walking to the mailbox. Consistent reporting so far.

reviews.author: S and K
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 30, 2025
reviews.content: This heart rate monitor is a solid performer. It is comfortable, syncs quickly, and appears accurate. Would buy again.

reviews.author: rebecca moriarty
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 20, 2025
reviews.content: Purchased for my runner son. He loves the fit and accuracy. Using for a few weeks and it’s durable and works well for his long runs. Great gift for the runner in your life.

reviews.author: CVP
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 30, 2025
reviews.content:
Updating original review after realizing this HRM does not work properly in cold temperatures. I noticed this when temps dropped below 50 degrees and the HRM took almost 5 minutes to start working. This may seem like a small issue but it impacts the metrics for the entire activity. When temps are closer to 30 it does not work at all. Very frustrating.

Great for the first few months, but after the battery was replaced it has not functioned properly. It does not connect automatically. After it does connect it fails to read properly for the first five or so minutes.

reviews.author: Tony B
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 20, 2025
reviews.content: An upgrade to their former HRM with unnecessary capabilities. Pros: works and syncs well with my Garmin bicycle computer and the data and corresponding software update gives more meaningful HR data. Cons: I didn’t want the new encrypted data feature which increased the unit cost.

reviews.author: Mr. Magnum
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 20, 2025
reviews.content: Finally Garmin has figured it out. Yes, this is a great HR monitor. The belt feels premium. The sensor is detachable from the belt (once again). The sensor actually has an activation button and light to confirm it's active - no guessing before it connects to a device. And yes, it's very sensitive. Again, I think they finally have figured out how to produce "the" premium HR monitor.

reviews.author: Tony
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 12, 2025
reviews.content: I have this HRM connected to my Garmin forerunner 165. I've used it about 8 times since purchasing it and it does give a reading. And even my slow easy runs put my HR at high Tempo to Threshold. But I will continue to use it and see how my progress gets monitored as time goes on. Over its comfortable and practical. Yes I would recommend!

reviews.author: Diego
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 2, 2026
reviews.content:
This is my first time with an OLED monitor, I was very hesitant weather to go for the upgrade or not but this monitor had everything I was looking for and the price was great.
The high refresh rate along with the adaptive-sync function provide a super smooth gaming experience at high fps and I don't notice any visual tearing, the quality is algo great; 1440p is in my opinion is the perfect resolution to experience competitive fps in multiplayer titles and high fps and crisp quality in single player titles.
The monitor controls to adjust picture quality, colors and all of that good stuff is quite simple and user-friendly, and since this is an OLED monitor the colors and contrast are simply next level compared to my old IPS monitor.

Overall I am content with my purchase but it may not be perfect for everyone so I will list some of my pros and cons about this monitor.

PROS
-Great visual fidelity, 1440p is the sweet spot for both competetive multiplayer and single player games.
-Buttery smooth motion with 240hz refresh rate and adaptive-sync.
-Superb color management and contrast overall.
-User-friendly menu and easily updatable firmware.

CONS
-This model in particular (unlike the 271QP and 272QP) does not have an HDR certification and I could not get it to look any good with HDR turned on.
-Really sensible to scratches glossy coating; I've had this monitor for a little over a week and I can already see some small scratches in the monitor when it's turned off and pointing a flashlight against it, however this is completely unnoticeable while the monitor is turned on and without any light directly hitting it (also from what I have read the sensible coating is a thing in almost every OLED monitor out there.)
-The top brightness is still not as bright as my old IPS monitor (like the previous point, this is also the case with most OLED monitors in the market right now except for the really expensive brand new ones.)

reviews.author: Leslie Jefferson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.content:
I recently purchased the MSI MAG 274QP QD-OLED X24 and after spending time gaming, and working on it, I can confidently say this monitor delivers an impressive experience for the price.

The first thing that stands out is the QD-OLED panel. The picture quality is simply stunning. Blacks are truly deep and inky, and colors look vibrant without appearing oversaturated. Games with dark scenes look incredible because there’s no backlight bleed, and bright highlights pop nicely against the contrast. The 2560 x 1440 resolution on a 27-inch screen feels like a perfect balance between sharpness and performance. Text looks crisp, and games run smoothly without requiring an extreme GPU to push high frame rates.

Speaking of smoothness, the 240Hz refresh rate combined with the ultra-fast 0.03ms response time makes a noticeable difference, especially in fast-paced shooters and competitive games. Motion looks clean and fluid, and I haven’t experienced ghosting or smearing. Adaptive-Sync support also helps eliminate screen tearing, which makes gameplay feel even more responsive and polished.

Overall, I’m extremely satisfied with this monitor. If you’re looking for a 27-inch QHD gaming display with incredible contrast, smooth 240Hz performance, and premium OLED image quality, this is an excellent choice. It feels like a big upgrade over traditional IPS panels and makes both competitive and cinematic games look amazing.

reviews.author: Carlos
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content:
Absolutely amazing monitor. The QD-OLED panel is stunning colors are incredibly vibrant, blacks are true black, and the contrast is on another level compared to IPS. The 240Hz refresh rate paired with the 0.03ms response time makes gameplay insanely smooth, especially in fast-paced shooters.

1440p at 27 inches is the perfect sweet spot for clarity without being too demanding on the GPU. Adaptive Sync works flawlessly with no screen tearing, and input lag feels nonexistent. Build quality is solid, the stand is sturdy with good height and tilt adjustment, and setup was easy.

This monitor is perfect for competitive gaming and also looks fantastic for movies and general use. If you’re considering OLED for gaming, this one is 100% worth it.

reviews.author: Jalat Khan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.content: Absolutely beautiful OLED monitor! The 1440p resolution is so crisp its fantastic! It has multiple HDMI ports, a display port, easily mountable, and the stand can be adjusted as well! The response time is also extremely fast! The 240 hertz display also makes games look as smooth as butter. But what strikes me, is the insane clarity of the OLED pixels. The contrast is so vibrant, the dark colors look so dark, and there is no blur whatsoever. The 27” screen size is amazing as well! One of my best purchases from MSI, an absolutely great company and even better products.

reviews.author: Danica Huizar
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
reviews.content: So far, this thing rocks. The glossy display is my preference and the color saturation is fantastic. Going from the HP Omen 27i IPS to this is such a huge upgrade. This monitor is so thin and light, but not scary thin which is great. This has made gaming so much more immersive and definitely gives an edge in some gameplay. I can see so much better in dark games like Outlast Trials and Arc Raiders. Definitely benefits from some setting adjustments, there’s a few videos online for what some good base settings are for this panel. Overall very happy I went with this product over the others on the market, great value.

reviews.author: Von
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.content:
Absolutely amazing monitor! This is my first OLED display and I’m honestly blown away. The colors are incredibly vibrant, the brightness is powerful yet comfortable, and the high refresh rate makes everything feel unbelievably smooth.

Gaming on this monitor is a whole new experience titles like Kingdom Come: Deliverance, Silent Hill f, and Counter-Strike: Global Offensive look and feel fantastic. The clarity, contrast, and responsiveness really elevate gameplay.

I couldn’t be happier with this purchase and would absolutely buy another one without hesitation. Highly recommended for anyone considering an upgrade!

reviews.author: Matthew Nguyen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content: 240hz at 1440p, a good resolution sweet spot with infinite contrast. superb monitor at this price point. colors look vibrant, blacks are black, and highlights are bright enough for my taste. i strongly recommend this monitor for anyone with a budget

reviews.author: Farris
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content: Outstanding options to adjust color and brightness to my liking. 240hz at an affordable price!

reviews.author: Ronnie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026
reviews.content: Great monitor I run it off a hp.Omen sixteen. 5060gpu 32gb ram 1tbssd. Runs mint. I do, however wish it angled down more.It angles up a lot, but not down much

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 11, 2026
reviews.content: great monitor. Just the right size without being massive. I use it as a work pc, and some gaming. Highly recommended. I tried to go back to a flat monitor, it just wasn't the same and decided to buy this one.

reviews.author: Jimmie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 10, 2025
reviews.content: Great!!!

reviews.author: Wade Reese
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2026
reviews.content: It works perfectly with my hp desktop, recommend this for anyone with any hp computer model with any gpu.

reviews.author: Christopher Jarrabet
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 25, 2025
reviews.content:
This is an Awesome gaming monitor. You will not be disappointed.
I would purchase it again if needed too!

reviews.author: Warmbread
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 16, 2025
reviews.content: First ever OMEN monitor I've owned, and it's fantastic, has great color for this type of monitor, all while being what people would consider not the ideal size for a 1440p display. I would recommend this for anyone looking for a 32'' curved 1440p monitor, Some gripes are a worse stand compared to my previous monitors, and a 1-year warranty when others offer 3 years.

reviews.author: Nate
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 11, 2025
reviews.content:
2 months of constant use. I purchased these monitors for work, and I use an HP ZBook. I'm not sure if I'd use them for gaming, but the monitors display a clear picture, look good, and have adjustable features. Thank goodness it has adjustable features because these monitors are bright out of the box.

The reason I purchased these monitors is that my past HP and Apple laptops would lose connection to other name-brand screens. I'm unsure if it's the laptop or the monitors. It might be the monitor, because I still lose connection from time to time.

reviews.author: Kev Quaye
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 20, 2025
reviews.content: Anti lag is the best!!

reviews.author: Plant Based Celiac
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 16, 2023
reviews.content:
Holds my 32” ultrawide monitor at correct angle w/o any issue. I’ve had other mounts that couldn’t hold the weight of the monitor so eventually they would angle downward. This one actually supports the weight. Easy to adjust angles & stays in place!
Super easy to install & will be a breeze to uninstall. Works so well with dell monitors too because it just clips in super easily and you’re good to go. The vesa adapter works very well too. Just remember to use the thumb screws so it stays secure and so you can adjust vertical angle.
I highly recommend this over other brands because of how easy it is to set up and adjust. 100% worth the extra money. Also was able to get it at about half the msrp price so double win!

reviews.author: geekdom
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 16, 2025
reviews.content: I own two of these, they are outstanding for two my 27" Dells. Super easy to assemble, adjust, and mount the panel. Stand is very solid and high quality.

reviews.author: Sarah Diaz
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 29, 2025
reviews.content: Perfect, looks organized and brings so much more space to the desk. 10/10

reviews.author: Erik Davis
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 2, 2025
reviews.content: This is exactly what we needed to adjust the computer monitor. Multiple options and heights, very easy to adjust. Feels very sturdy when attached to the desk!

reviews.author: Kevin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2025
reviews.content: This monitor arm is heavy! Love it! I bought it for my Alienware 32" 4KQDOLEDMonitor AW3225QF. Does what it is meant to do. Love the OEM stand that the monitor came with but it takes up SOO much space on my desk and this arm frees up all that space. It's the same mount as the OEM stand so no need for adapters. I have not tighten the tension on the back of arms and it holds up my monitor very well, no sag or drooping over time! I can also use the monitor in portrait as you can see in the pictures. I do wish it were a bit longer but not a deal breaker for this price!!! Cable management is great as well!!!! Amazing arm!

reviews.author: Noel
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 14, 2024
reviews.content:
This product is actually really amazing for the price, however it will not fit just any monitor. Even if you buy an adapter, some monitors just don’t have enough space for the vesa adapter.

I own an ergotron lx arm and this dell arm is on par with the quality. It has a much better cable management system. The movement isn’t as smooth as the ergotron but it’s literally half the price. The base is so much better on the dell. Only sad part is this just won’t fit every monitor like the ergotron. I would recommend this to everyone over the ergotron if it had a standard vesa mount.

reviews.author: Kyle Nguyen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 6, 2024
reviews.content: It fits well with my AW3423DWF monitor. Easy to install. The monitor just snaps into place. It has height and angle adjustments. It's got built in cable management to hide the monitor cables. If I need to swap it to another table I just have to press a single button to remove the monitor from the stand and untighten the knob stand. It's so simple anybody can do it. Just remember it's only compatible with certain Dell monitors so make sure you check before buying.

reviews.author: Cdod1
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 1, 2023
reviews.content:
I bought 2 of these to mount 2 Dell monitors via the quick attach plate. The U3223qe and the S2721qs both fit flawlessly, even though it's not advertised as quick attach for the S2721qs. It's a simple snap together design and I was able to attach both monitors in just a few minutes each once I figured out where on my desk to mount them.

My tip for multiple monitor installs:
Measure from the middle of your monitors VESA mount in the back to the edge of your monitors, put the mounts on your desk, then slide them sideways until they're the right distance apart so your monitors won't hit or have too much of a gap.

For comparison, I also purchased another mount brand, Mountup, for an old, cheaper monitor just to have a third display. It took me 4x as long to assemble and adjust. It's just not as clean as the Dell. When I upgrade that monitor later this year, I will buy a third Dell msa20.

You will not regret this purchase!

reviews.author: Tina Ton
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 6, 2024
reviews.content: I'm pretty new to all computer stuff, but I was recommended to get this for my setup. It was very easy to put together and makes my setup look so good. Worth it!

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 9, 2024
reviews.content: It matched a monitor I already had for my desktop. Would buy it again.

reviews.author: RT
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 18, 2023
reviews.content: This is a utilitarian monitor. I think the color is pretty good for the price. You would be fine with casual gaming. The main thing to me is there are no built in speakers. If you don't have some laying around the house, then it may bump up the price to the point that a 4K monitor is in the same price range.

reviews.author: PT
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 27, 2024
reviews.content: For the price it could not be better, very good image clear and quality is decent. I loved it I install with my PC and is working amazing.

reviews.author: Doug Brown
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 22, 2022
reviews.content: Provided an inexpensive and top performing monitor to replace one that died. Very happy with the quality. Not using it for gaming so I don’t know how it works for that, but for computer monitor it is excellent

reviews.author: mags
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 29, 2023
reviews.content: Just received them today (purchased two)! They are fantastic! The picture quality is fantastic. It is different between type of cords you plug them into your computer with… as expected.

reviews.author: Rob
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 15, 2023
reviews.content: 24” monitor! Do I need to say more. Okay, yes I love this monitor. $100 was a great value.

reviews.author: David A. Thomas
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 23, 2023
reviews.content: Great monitor liked the first one I bought, I bought another one.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 18, 2025
reviews.content:
Great picture.
Great colors.
Great price.

reviews.author: KEVIN C. FLANAGAN
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2026
reviews.content: Great gaming screen! My grandson loved this for Christmas !

reviews.content: At first I thought it was going back, my son got this for Christmas and didn’t like the way that screen attached to the base, it leans forward a little. After playing on it awhile he said the graphics were awesome and he loves it. It’s 24 inches a good size not too big or small. It was a great price.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2026
reviews.content: Clear monitor, light weight. Easy set up

reviews.content: It is one of the best monitors I've ever had. It well rounded with color, right HZ for the price, and the base hold the monitor in place. I also liked that you can tilt the monitor

reviews.content: Salió tal cual lo esperado
