reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2026
reviews.content: Very good for the price but the colors sort of look just grey and it doesn't get very bright

reviews.author: Susan Q
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026
reviews.content: My gran s on says. Its great.

reviews.author: James Helisek
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 16, 2025
reviews.content: This is a great monitor! I've used it many times on the go, as well as at home as a secondary screen. I'd highly recommend this to anyone in need of an easily portable and reliable monitor. It's built very sturdy, and super easy to use - just plug and play.

reviews.author: Anonymous
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 5, 2025
reviews.content:
I picked this up on sale and I’ve been using it for a few days now. Really impressed so far. I bought the 16” 2K (2560×1600) 120Hz version, but when I hook it up to my laptop it actually refreshes up to 144Hz. I use two USB-C to USB-C cables: one connects the monitor to my laptop for display, and the second goes to a power brick. With that setup, everything works perfectly.

I’m pairing it with a Lenovo LOQ gaming laptop (Ryzen 7, RTX 4050), and the picture is smooth, bright, and has no flickering or ghosting. Colors look good, the screen wakes right up when plugged in, and it’s been great for gaming and general multitasking.

No complaints so far. If anything changes, I’ll update later, but right now it feels like one of the better portable monitors for the price, especially if you catch it on sale.

reviews.author: Chris
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 22, 2026
reviews.content:
I was originally comparing this monitor to the InnoView 14 inch OLED portable monitor since on paper they look nearly identical. After reading through a lot of reviews, it became clear that Upperizon addressed many of the complaints people had with the InnoView model. After using this monitor, I can confidently say those improvements make a real difference.

The first thing that stands out is how thin this monitor is. The entire body is aluminum, including the kickstand, and it feels very premium in hand. There are rubber feet along the edges where it contacts the table, which is a nice detail. Setup could not be easier. I plugged it into my MacBook Pro using a single USB C cable and it immediately detected the input and selected the correct resolution. No settings, no fiddling. Even more impressive is that it runs at full brightness using just that one cable. Most portable monitors I looked at require a second cable for extra power to reach full brightness, but not this one.

The display quality is excellent. The OLED panel paired with 4K resolution looks incredible. I do a lot of productivity work with small text and everything is crystal clear. The resolution matches my MacBook Pro perfectly, which makes side by side work feel seamless. Colors look great and contrast is excellent.
It also comes with a very nice carrying case, much nicer than I expected. You will want to use it because the monitor is shockingly thin and lightweight. I also appreciate that it comes with a two year warranty, which adds some peace of mind.

One small limitation is the stand when using portrait mode. It technically works, but the screen sits too upright. I ended up using a small wedge to tilt it back slightly, which worked fine given how light the monitor is. I included photos showing both landscape and portrait orientation for reference. I am running this with a 2024 14 inch MacBook Pro and the proportions feel just right.

Overall, for the price, this is easily the portable monitor to get if you want something thin, lightweight, and high quality that pairs well with a modern laptop. Compared to the InnoView version, the small price difference is well worth it, especially considering the better experience and the included case. I have only been using it for about a week so far, but if anything changes I will update this review.

reviews.author: Jim C.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 12, 2025
reviews.content: Took on vacation for a second monitor. Large clear, bright screen. You need to be directly in front of the screen for full brightness. I like that it can be run by one usb cable for power and video when connected to a laptop that can send video over the usb cable. It has a case with screen cover when folded and a built in stand. It also comes with an extra padded soft case for extra protection.

reviews.author: Maggie B.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 16, 2025
reviews.content: This has been working GREAT! I really appreciate the instruction sent and that there is a phone number in case you have any issues. I registered mine via their QR code and they offered me a free gift. I chose the folding monitor stand and it was a nice surprise. This monitor was easy to set up, connects well to my Asus laptop, and is lightweight. Great picture quality, and excellent customer service. I'd buy this again if I need another portable monitor. :)

reviews.author: Kaladin Stormblessed
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 13, 2025
reviews.content:
Not bad & I do enjoy the increased viewing size vs my 16.5" ASUS ZenScreen. What I'm less than thrilled about tho is the combo case/stand, which is not only flimsy, but severely limited in positioning. As a result of the case/stand limitations, the monitor has an aggressive angle/lean, that tilts too far back for my taste. I've had to get creative with positioning + sandwiching items underneath so that the monitor doesn't lean towards the ceiling. I gave up after one use & picked up the smallest collapsible magnetic stand I could find (NLIEOPDA), that's heavy/sturdy enough to support an 18.5" monitor. So far, so good, but the stand put me out another $40 (vs the AMAZING built-in stand of the ZenScreen). Screen itself isn't bad, but also not as clear (almost a Cybill Shepherd-esque Moonlighting filter) vs the brightness/clarity of the ZenScreen. In addition, though the monitor does power/pass-through via the connected device w/ a single USB-C, the brightness is unusable in standard lighting conditions (so again, comparing w/ the ZenScreen), I now have to use 2 cables (1 for power). Attempting to increase brightness without the additional cable results in the screen bouncing/powering off.

Regardless, it's MUCH larger (actually barely fitting in my backpack) & very reasonably priced, so I'll take the size over the smaller screen any day (though flexibility with USB Type-C inputs on both sides would've been a nice feature, but maybe a future model?). I've had it less than a week, so will see how durable this thing is (ZenScreen for example has been going strong w/ daily use for nearly ~2 years).

reviews.author: Kindle Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026
reviews.content: Easy to set up, great picture and travels well if you need to take it with you.

reviews.author: Hoon
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 13, 2025
reviews.content:
Ease of Use: Just received the monitor and the description and specs were promising. The monitor turned on quickly when connected with my MacBook Pro, and is quite bright. Very easy setup...actually no real setup needed.
Durability: Seems sturdy enough for its light weight.
Quality: However, upon opening the stand, looking at the back and laying the monitor on my desk getting ready to slide it into my backpack, I noticed 3 things.
First: The cover is dirty and smudges are visible.
Two: The wholes in the back are poorly drilled
Three: The whole monitor is BENT.

So IF the product is indeed new, as the packaging was fully wrapped and looked new, the product is defective and poor.
IF the product is a used product which the company sent to me, that is HIGHLY UNETHICAL.

Moral of the story: I'm staying away from this company.--
BUT HANG ON!!!!!!!!
EDIT: Customer Service promptly emailed me once they read my review.
They explained that Amazon may have sent me a returned product, and apologized.
I am changing my review to a full Five Stars for the following reasons: the company promptly sent me a new monitor, which I am using now for this review edit. Brand New, no dirt, clean. The first monitor I received was clearly used. The quality is great but what really sets this company apart is the CUSTOMER SERVICE.
Seldom do you find a company that values its customers and promptly addresses issues as they have done. Well done.
So ultimately, if you want a monitor that works great, is easy to use and has a top notch customer service behind it, look no further. You WILL be pleased with their service and monitors.

reviews.author: Dan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 23, 2025
reviews.format: Style: 27" OLED QHD 0.03ms 240Hz ROG Monitor Arm
reviews.content: Absolutely stunning! I have a nice Bravia main OLED tv and I thought that screen was great, but the picture quality on the ROG Swift PG27AQDM-R is bar non the most stunning color rich panel I've ever used. My friend has a QD OLED panel and yes it's quite nice but it doesn't have a true 10bit panel. This one does, and it shows! Just played some BF6 and man it's an entirely different experience when you're dealing with unlimited contrast with 10bit color. Came with a color calibration certificate signed by the technician who tested it not twice but 3 times! I have downloaded the most recent firmware MC110 and everything is working great. I will say that HDR is kinda wack like washed out but honestly SDR looks like the HDR on my old mini-led monitor. Extremely bright but not over saturated like with other less premium panel technologies. DCI-P3 is what you want to use. Shows every bit of that 10bit color! I wouldn't have bought this monitor because of price but I couldn't resist the massive Amazon sale right now. Yes this is a WOLED, not QD OLED but you just wouldn't know because the design of this panel is supreme. Everything including the picture quality is perfect. Using 7500K gamma which brightens it up more. The brightness has got to be hitting over 1k knits but I don't have a way of measuring that so you'll just have to take my word for it. The panel materials are fantastic. The screen is glass, the anti glare coating is not noticeable to me and the arm the master kit comes with is 100% steel. Asus has won a new and longtime customer. You can just feel the quality. If I were you I'd get the screen and monitor arm master package. It's cheaper than just getting the screen itself lol.

reviews.author: scott
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 11, 2025
reviews.format: Style: 27" Fast IPS QHD 180Hz G-SYNC USB-C

reviews.author: Alok G
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 31, 2025
reviews.content:
The monitor has good picture quality and build. I do not think anything lesser than an OLED monitor would surpass.

A few things I would like to see improved:

* If it does not find the signal, it shuts off in like one second. Damned, need to leave some time for the user to figure what is going on. I get it energy saving is important, but should stay on for at least some ten seconds?! This is really annoying, all the more so because HDMI seems to have issues with waking up when the laptop is put to sleep.

* Between day time and night time, monitor brightness needs adjusting. There should have been some easier means to do this, ideally even automated with an ambient light sensor. However, various monitors do not seem to support this. This monitor does allow having two setting profiles and you can customize the button to switching between these with just two clicks (I have set up upward click on its button to open setting 1 with larger brightness and downward click to open setting 2 with lower brightness.

* For some reason, monitors do not allow lowering the brightness below a certain level, which itself I find too high for night environments. I wish they allow letting the brightness scale do all the way to zero.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2019
reviews.content:
This is my experience with the Asus VG279Q. First off I want to clarify that prior to this monitor I have only ever used 1080p 60Hz TN panels. So this was my first experience with anything over 60HZ, my first experience with an IPS (IHVA) panel, and also my first experience with Variable refresh rate (Freesync/Gsync).

The first thing I noticed was the limited viewing angle from above, something I'm still struggling to get used to. I know that for my TN panels I get the best viewing angle when my eyes are about level with the top of the monitor. And if I stand up the monitor still looks fine. My TN only looked it's worst when viewed from the floor looking upwards. Since I never sit on the floor and look up at it the TN viewing angles were adequate. For this IPS panel I get the best viewing angles when my eyes are about level with the bottom of the monitor or looking at it from below. And it has the worst viewing angle when looking down at it from any position higher than perfectly eye level. So raising it up was my first step to reduce the washed out foggy angle.

The next step was to lower the brightness from the default 60 or so, down to around 23 *(I'm in a dark room). That helped to get a calm neutral picture, especially for white file explorer windows or web pages. This being my first IPS, if I had to describe the IPS glow, I think mine is only a bit on the bottom left corner, fairly subtle. But the viewing angle from above turns the image into a silver grey fog when viewing at anything but eye level. If you're eye level or below, then you can have a good image even when viewing off center for the side. However, you really notice an issue when the monitor falls below your eye level if you stand up. I knew with my TN panel that the picture would get bad when viewed at the wrong angles, but this is more extreme because the monitor turns so foggy from above as seen in the pics. So it's really bothering me to the point that I kind of wished I had gone with a TN that had these other features. However, now that I know it's limitations I just avoid the problem angle and it's all good.

The settings can be dialed in more with certain modes. For example I use Racing Mode because it allows me to use the Blue Light filter option whereas the FPS mode does not for some reason. The blue light filter isn't needed, except when it's a really dark room then it can come in handy so I like having the option. If I know I won't be using the blue light filter I made a profile for FPS mode because it allows me to adjust the saturation, color temp and skin tone options whereas racing doesn't for some reason. It's just weird that they make you pick and choose. The sRGB mode doesn't allow you to change anything, I guess they figure it doesn't need it. So I was thinking of choosing it - but I like to tinker more than the average guy. The SDR of contrast is genuinely full range and not limited range SDR like my old Asus VE258Q. The gamma is about .08 too dark, but using either Nvidia's gamma slider, or the built-in OSD shadow boost on level 1 take care of that. The dreaded pixel density issue everyone seemed to worry about in a 27" FHD monitor is thankfully not an issue whatsoever. I don't notice even a hint of pixel density issue, honestly. So that's a relief.

My VG279Q was stuck in 60HZ at first under the Nvidia display options heading for "native 1920x1080" But I was able to scroll down and choose a 2nd 1920x1080 option in the drop down that enabled all of the refresh rates. I immediately wanted to put it on 144HZ and enable the Gsync feature in the Nvidia control panel. I had to download the latest Nvidia drivers in order to get the Gsync feature to show up. After that I turned it on and went into a fast paced shooter. I put the graphics at a level to achieve a constant 144 fps, and I used the monitor's built in frame rate display counter as well as Nvidia's frame rate counter. The smoothness was outstanding. Everything was overall less blurry. Also no tearing ever. I loaded up Ace Combat 7 and flew around at 144fps locked and no tearing - an outstanding experience. I tried other things like running MAME and emulating old Mortal Kombat 1 with it's weird 55HZ native refresh rate - and the Gsync allowed me to run at a it's 55HZ perfectly with no tearing. So I'm really happy with Gsync, and really happy with having more than 60HZ for the first time. These features are so cool, that I definitely wouldn't want to go back to 60HZ or no Gsync.

As far as movies, 27" is superior to common 24" - 25" sized gaming monitors obviously. Watching movies benefits from the larger screen. The IPS's improved rich, vibrant colors are satisfying. Just gotta watch out for your viewing angle of course. Web browsing, writing this comment, it's nice and clear at 144HZ. It's cool to see the mouse move around in more focus because the monitor can keep up with it better. This comes in handy when I tested drawing in Gimp with my Wacom tablet and stylus. As I make little gestures in my shading and drawing lines, etc - the higher refresh rate keeps up with everything and feels less laggy in the strokes. As far as colors go - they're good.

Again, it's just that darn viewing angle issue that makes blacks look silverish and foggy from angles. If it wasn't for that then I would give it 5 stars. No dead pixels, no backlight bleed. Just some IPS glow (which is something all IPS panels have). So I figure I'll just get used to that. And a lot of great features. It's around 3 to 4 ms response time normally and around 1ms response time in the ELMB strobing mode. But that mode is greyed out in the OSD unless you disable Freesync and go into your PC's display settings and select a lower refresh rate like 120HZ or lower. I tried the ELMB mode, and it works fine. I can notice a sharper image in motion blur tests with ELMB enabled, but there is some ghosting. With ELMB disabled there's no ghosting but the object is blurrier. I'm not sensitive enough to notice the difference between 1ms and 5ms response time in game though. However, I am very sensitive to screen tearing that I get when Freesync/Gsync is disabled. Also once I got used to 144HZ, going down to 120HZ looked laggier - so I'll just stick with the 144HZ and Freesync/Gsync.

All in all, I'm happy. I'm not returning this monitor. I think it was well worth the price. I learned that TN isn't as bad as I thought as it turns out. People always said IPS has better viewing angles, but it's not the case. So my recommendation would be to get a monitor that has all these features but TN if you require a more versatile viewing angle when seated or standing. Or to get this monitor if you can ensure you'll only use it at eye level or lower. My bottom left corner is a bit foggy where it should be black. Not extremely mind you, but noticeable to me when I'm looking for it.

My settings that I prefer: Racing Mode, Brightness: 23, Contrast: 70, Saturation: 50, Color Temp: User mode (red:93,green:92,blue:100), Skin Tone: Natural, Smart View OFF, Sharpness: 50, Trace Free: 60, Vivid Pixel: 0, ASCR: OFF, Freesync: ON, ELMB: OFF, Shadow Boost: OFF (bumped up gamma in Nvidia control panel by .08). Other than bad viewing angles from above - everything looks beautiful with these settings. In the included pictures I show a movie still image at a good and bad angle, a black screen at a good and bad angle in the light, and then good and bad angles of solid black, red, green, and blue screens under ambient lighting.

reviews.author: Ben Carlson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 24, 2025
reviews.format: Style: 32" Fast VA Curved 280Hz QHD HDR400
reviews.content:
I’d argue this is one of the best gaming monitors money can buy and for the price it’s an absolute steal.

This is currently the highest refresh rate monitor that is larger than 28 inches and it’s a worthwhile difference compared to 240hz. At 32 inches game elements are much bigger compared to 24 or 27inch monitors which makes it easier to aim and hit headshots in games like cs2, and overall allows for a more immersive experience no matter the game, especially with the curved panel.

It’s also 1440p rather than 4k which still looks great on 32 inches. You are actually able to hit the max refresh rate of the monitor on many games without the need to lower your settings or have a 5090 which makes this a great monitor for native res players.

Historically VA panels have been avoided for gaming due to the slow pixel response times but this monitor is very fast for a VA panel and is equivalent or even better than the gaming IPS panels that currently flood the market. However with this monitor you also get amazing contrast compared to an IPS so it’s easier to spot enemies in game and overall the monitor looks so much nicer compared to an ips or tn. Highly recommend this monitor!

reviews.author: Patrizia
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 19, 2025
reviews.format: Style: 27" Fast IPS QHD 180Hz G-SYNC USB-C White
reviews.content: Had it for nearly half a year now. My last monitor was nearly a decade old so I have no frame of reference for quality etc, but I really like how it looks. I kinda wish the led on the back would detect when the computer was off and adjust accordingly, but it feels like too minor an issue to subtract a star. My phone is a samsung galaxy s23+ with a thick defender case and it fits snugly in the space provided in the base, and the usb-c connection can kinda charge it up. I haven't had any issues thus far, and it performs as advertised. It arrived very well packaged, and was pretty easy to assemble. I did get this monitor on sale, but I was strongly debating a purchase even at full price, and it feels worth it.

reviews.author: Jordan H.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 18, 2019
reviews.content: I received this monitor with the box in pristine condition, got everything hooked up and immediately noticed the IPS difference. Make sure you use the display port cable and change the monitor settings to 144hz before you do anything else! The smoothness and speed of this 144hz monitor has blown me away and until you see it and try it for yourself you won’t know what I’m talking about. I honestly never noticed screen tearing or ghosting on my other monitor until I started using the VG279Q. Although the Xbox One x only and PS4 pro can only play games at 60hz (FPS) the input lag is extremely low as confirmed from rtings.com which has an insanely in depth review on this monitor. Some people will say the pixel density is too high and for some it may be, but where I sit and game is far enough away I don’t notice it. The response time is not 1ms as advertised unless you use the ELMB setting but at that point you lose FreeSync / G sync and can only do 120hz. But let me explain, don’t get input lag and response time confused, input lag is from the time you aim and fire from your controller / mouse to when the monitor receives it and this monitor is the fastest out there. The response time is the grey to grey which you can read about somewhere else but it’s about 4ms here which unless you are a pro esport gamer you will never notice. Bottom line is this, what you get here is a well built monitor that is g sync compatible, 144hz refresh rate, low response time, INCREDIBLY low input lag, and features an IPS display which makes colors pop and doesn’t limit your viewing angle. I have not noticed any IPS glow on my panel and I may not even know what to look for but I am so happy with this purchase I wanted to leave a good review. If you are on the fence try this out, just try it. Amazon has the best return policy out there if you don’t like it you can get something else. I can’t wait to get home and really put some hours into Apex!

reviews.author: Aeee
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2019
reviews.content:
I've been a gamer for many years, but I don't play competitive PC games as much as I used to. This IPS panel meets all of my requirements: Great picture, high refresh rate, a good native resolution for high FPS without needing an absolute beast PC. The speakers are the only weakness, but I would only ever consider using built-in speakers as a temporary backup. The frame doesn't feel too plasticky and it doesn't look bad. The physical buttons seemed a little awkward at first, but I got used to them fairly quickly. The options on the OSD menus provide more than enough settings for me. I doubt I'll ever need the "GamePlus" features.

The only complaint I have is small: I prefer to have the monitor completely unpowered when my computer is off. I can do this using my UPS set to "green" mode and then plugging the monitor into the "green" outIet. Here's the problem: The monitor does not "wake-up" out of low-power mode until the computer reaches the OS login screen. My older monitors don't have this problem. I can live with this and it doesn't change my opinion of the monitor, but it is enough of a hassle (such as when I need to boot into the BIOS settings) that I no longer use this monitor in the "green" outlet.

Quality control: The first monitor I received had an issue of horizontal artifacts appearing randomly. This reminded me of the screen noise you might see when a power tool or vacuum cleaner is plugged into the same circuit; at least on older televisions. Amazon support was fast and professional with the handling of the return and refund.

Other: When I wanted to reorder this monitor (I liked what I saw, defect aside), it was only available from 3rd-party sellers. The monitor wasn't shipped in an additional shipping box like the first order which was sold and fulfilled by Amazon. The second monitor didn't survive whatever nightmare FedEx put it through. The box had holes in it and the panel was smashed. I returned it immediately for a refund. Maybe an additional shipping box would have given the monitor enough added protection. My *third* attempt to order this monitor through the Amazon site (direct from Amazon or 3rd party seller) was fruitless. it was unavailable directly from Amazon, the net cost had gone up by at least $60 from 3rd party sellers, and none of the sellers I contacted said they provide additional shipping protection. My third attempt to buy this monitor ended up being from my local Frys which cost the same as my first order. I ordered it for in-store pickup the next day. There have been no problems with this one and I'm very happy with it!

After a few days, ended up using the following settings for day-to-day work/gaming:: [First reset to defaults] GameVisual: FPS Mode, Brightness 23, Contrast: 80, Saturation: 38, Color Temp: User Mode: R94, G93, B100.

reviews.author: Ol' Hesty
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 19, 2026
reviews.content:
My son wanted a better monitor to play his 1st person shooter games. The monitor is used with both PC and PS5, and works flawlessly (per my son). It doesn’t overheat, no lag, and refresh rate is great. I asked my boy to rip it apart and give me his brutal thoughts. Honestly, he couldn’t find any issues. Now it’s a matter of longevity, time will tell, and I update as warranted.
Solid monitor, no frills. If this is in your price range, I’d recommend it.

reviews.author: Starlord
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026
reviews.format: Size: 28 inchStyle: UHD4K 60Hz White Flat Gaming
reviews.content:
I wanted an additional monitor for my work setup and debated for a bit before narrowing down on this model. I wasn’t looking for anything really expensive nor dirt cheap with nil features. This one fit the budget and features I needed perfectly.

I recvd it today and got to set it up immediately. It was a very simple to put it together and connected to my Mac via the included DP cable. Yes, I have a docking station with DP port.

The quality is great, very thin bezel and color/brightness are awesome. The screen size was one of the main factors of choosing this one and it didn’t disappoint me at all in reality.

Durability is not something I am worried about as I’m not expecting to abuse it beyond its capabilities. Functionality wise, it’s exactly serving my purpose and I’m very satisfied with my purchase overall. Just waiting for monitor mount to arrive to make it even more versatile.

Overall best budget 4K monitor out there in this screen size and best suited for work setup in my humble opinion.

reviews.author: Shane Arcaro
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 11, 2025
reviews.format: Size: 34 inchStyle: UWFHD 120Hz White Flat Gaming
reviews.content:
Looks like a good monitor, the set up was easy and the colors look great. The refresh rate is as advertised and the build quality over all is pretty good.

The only problem I have with the monitor is that it isn't 100% VESA compatible. I have a VESA mount that doesn't fit with this circular design, it cuts off the area of the mount.

I needed to use longer screws and a couple of extra washers to get the monitor to mount properly to the VESA bracket I have.

Monitor is pretty good just annoying to mount.

reviews.author: Fd Robinson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.content: Nice monitor big curved and great quality graphics truly impressed on how big it is perfect for the price. Extremley happy!

reviews.author: Cheikhtelly Diawara
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.content: Great looking monitor the fact that they also giving you a display port cable is 10/10 the refresh rate is here and the overall quality is really good I recommend 👍

reviews.author: Dixie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 31, 2025
reviews.content:
When my 7-year-old monitor bit the big one, we went monitor shopping. Since my husband's was the same age, we wanted to replace both at the same time.

This one was a great value - it's just as big as our last ones, slightly better specs, has the same curvature and has a great picture. We've found no dead pixels and the color is great, better than our last monitors for sure.

Setup was easy; you plugged it any and everything basically just worked.

Probably the biggest annoyance about this one is the button placement for adjusting the brightness, contrast, etc. We don't have a lot of extra space to reach around the back as it's positioned in a hutch and the monitor takes up all the available room, so it's very hard to get at those buttons and of course, you cannot see what any are.

We ended up installing the free "monitorian' program so we could adjust the brightness and stuff without having to mess wit the poorly placed buttons.

The other thing that was bothersome was the fact the stand was not adjustable at all in terms of height. Because we have them in in a small space, this was a problem. We ended up buying new stands so we could set the height to work with our space.

Overall, these were a great value for size and functioning and we'd absolutely get them again. Please note the picture is NOT the original stand, it's the replacement one we got. But as you can still see from the back, these are big montiors.

reviews.author: Michael
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 1, 2026

reviews.author: Alexis Zane Alexander
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 25, 2025
reviews.content: I rarely write product reviews, but just had to because of the cost to quality for this item is something else. This is one of the most affordable portable 1080p monitors that I found, and as soon as I got it, it was a super easy plug-and-play essentially to get it working - no need for an external power supply if you’re using it with the USB-C port either. I use multiple monitors at work, so this was perfect for me when I need to work on the go. The thickness is basically the size of a thin laptop or large tablet, and the case it comes with is pretty decent quality too. Definitely helps to protect the screen and makes it even easier to transport with my laptop. I also have no problem fitting it in the same laptop sleeve with my laptop. The screen seems to be a matte finish, which helps to be anti-glare. It’s also pretty cool that it’s easily compatible with other devices too, like phones or tablets, as an extra options if you want. All in all, highly recommend for the price, and am considering getting a second one just to have a triple monitor setup on the go.

reviews.author: Gage Soto
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 10, 2025
reviews.content:
Bought this monitor to be used as a second screen with my HP laptop to help with productivity. I must say this screen is impressive out the box. It comes with two plugs. I used the c video port and it connected and turned on seamlessly and was ready for use. The screen size is 15.6 inches. It comes with a foldable cover that helps it convert to a stand. It’s super light weight and an easily be placed in a backpack. The screen is bright and vivid and is true to color for spreadsheets and web browsing.

I recommend this to anyone who needs an extra screen to be able to monitor accounts/notifications without having to go back and forth on a single laptop screen. It’s simple to set up and is portable.

reviews.content: I am an administrative assistant, and I often have a lot of work I do at home in the evenings. Having this extra screen has been wonderful. It does perfectly fine.It’s easy to use and I can take it anywhere with me. I had a question about the screen and sent an email to the company and they answered me right back. It’s a great company!

That said, the process is quite reasonable.

reviews.author: Queen Nikki
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 6, 2026
reviews.content:
This portable laptop screen has been incredibly useful for working on the go. It folds up in a way that the screen is fully enclosed and protected, making it durable and I don’t need a separate case. I can just slide it into my backpack and know it’s safe during travel or my daily commute.

No matter where I’m working, having a second screen makes a huge difference in productivity. The 15.6-inch size feels large and comfortable to use, similar to a standard laptop screen, so it doesn’t feel cramped.

One of my favorite features is that it doesn’t require a separate stand. The foldable cover doubles as a stand, similar to how some tablet cases prop up a screen. It’s quick to set up and keeps everything compact and simple. It is easy to connect using a type c cord and USB adapter, everything you need is included. As soon as you connect, seconds later, you see your second screen on. You can adjust the volume or brightness.

For the price, the convenience, portability, and built-in protection make this a great value. I’d recommend it to students, remote workers, or anyone who travels and needs extra screen space without carrying bulky equipment.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.content:
I've had this portable monitor on my desk for about a week now. It's a very good, space-saving design. I have it sitting on a book holder to the side.

It did start flickering on me because I've been keeping it plugged into a power outlet and the included cable is rather thin. I now use my spare Dell 45w USB-C power adapter. Absolutely no issues. I have other 240w USB-C cables that I can use when traveling with this monitor to ensure stable operation. Probably not an issue when using it for true travel purposes, but I'd rather eliminate the possible issues. The included mini-HDMI to HDMI cable works perfectly and I just keep it plugged into my docking station. Light weight yet solid.

reviews.author: Patricio O'Gorman

reviews.content:

Good sound quality and color accuracy, make this monitor a great value for money. Took me a long time to find it (I needed specifically a white monitor) and I believe this is the most complete one in Amazon - with all the functionality I needed.

reviews.author: movie lover
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 8, 2025
reviews.content:
The monitor is great. I have this one wall mounted opposite an exercise bike in a spare bedroom and I can use the wifi in the house to get a good signal for watching cable TV using an app on the TV or Netflix as I have a subscription. I also can watch Disney + on it if I choose. It takes a little time to set up and make sure it returns to the previous station when started. The apps can take time to load, but there are buttons on the remote for certain streaming services like Netflix. I signed in once when I set up the monitor, and on start up now I need only press the Netflix button on the remote and can quickly bring up programs to watch from my list.

I have 2 Samsung smart monitors. The other I use in kitchen on the countertop.

AVOID using the Samsung channel as it is a pain to get rid of again. The remote doesn't have any numbers to put a specific channel in. So I have to use the guide for the app, as I have Charter Sprectrum for my cable provider. The Charter App takes some time to load on the monitor as it checks the subscription each time. A pain. I like streaming programs from my cable channels or from Netflix.

The remote volume control is also the mute button. Pushing it straight down mutes the sound. Toggling it up or down changes the volume level.

reviews.author: amazon customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 1, 2025
reviews.content:

This monitor gets the job done, is easy to connect to, and the smart features mean I can use it as a tv or to play games even when a computer isn’t connected. I like the sleek white look and I have it wall mounted in my kitchen where I can loop slideshows or art wall paper when it’s not in use, and it looks great.

The reason I cant give this a full 5 stars is that the resolution isn’t great compared to other Samsung monitors. This m5 only displays up to a max 1920x1080p resolution whereas the m8 is 4K UHD (3,840 x 2,160 pixels.)

however, because I am not using this as my primary monitor, this is not a major deterrent for me.

reviews.author: Awaken
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 21, 2025
reviews.content:
. The tv arrived in September 2025 with a black block and yellow line on the right side. After spending nearly 2 hours on a chat with a tech rep, trying various tricks to fix the problem, the tech told us to sent it in for repair . Samsung sent us a sturdy box and packing materials and we carefully packed the tv and sent it to the company with the label they provided. We were notified by Samsung when the tv arrived. 2 days later we received a message that parts were unavailable and they were returning the tv to us unrepaired. We called customer service and that representative was very oppositional but said he would bump our issue to a higher power and to expect a call within 2 business days. That call never came. Meanwhile we received notice that the tv had suffered impact damage. Then another message that the repaired tv was in route.
When the tv arrived it was in much worse shape then when we sent it. We called again. The next customer service guy said the warranty was void due to impact damage. So, basically, tough luck.
In this day and age of customer service, this experience was outrageously bad. I will never purchase another Samsung product. On the up side, we contacted Amazon to see if they could put some pressure on from their side. By this time it was out of Amazon’s return window. They offered us a gift card which will enable us to buy a new tv.

reviews.author: Boyd Mckee Kamer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2026
reviews.content:
Review:
I purchased two of the Koorui 34‑inch curved ultrawide monitors for a dual‑monitor setup, and so far I’m very pleased with them. The picture quality is sharp, the brightness is solid, and the “Normal” color preset looks natural without any adjustments. For the price, these 34‑inch panels deliver very good performance.

That said, there are a few important details the product listing and reviews don’t mention — and knowing them ahead of time would have saved me some stress during installation.

1. YES, the 34‑inch model DOES include stand‑offs (spacers)
This is not mentioned anywhere in the listing, and even Amazon’s Rufus incorrectly says they are *not* included. They actually come in the small accessory packet with the manual.

If you’re mounting these monitors, you will need those stand‑offs because of the recessed VESA pattern. They are already in the box — just not advertised. I rushed to order a separate spacer kit because the listing didn’t mention it, so I’m putting this here to help the next person avoid that.

2. One of my monitors flashed briefly until I disabled FreeSync
On one of the two monitors, I noticed a quick bright “flash” when moving the mouse. Turning off Adaptive Sync / FreeSync in the monitor’s menu fixed it instantly. The other monitor didn’t have this issue, so it seems to vary by panel.
Not a defect — just something to be aware of if you see flicker or brightness pops.

3. Color and brightness match well once set manually
After setting both monitors to the same brightness, contrast, and color mode, they match very well side‑by‑side. The “Normal” color preset looks the most accurate on these VA panels.

4. Build quality and packaging are solid
Both monitors arrived well‑protected, and the included cables work fine. The stand is basic, but I’m using monitor arms, so that wasn’t a factor for me.

5. Too early to comment on long‑term support or durability
I’ve only had these monitors set up for a few hours, so I can’t speak yet to long‑term reliability or how responsive Koorui’s support is. I’ll update this review if anything changes over time.

Bottom line:
These 34‑inch Koorui ultrawides are excellent for the price, especially for gaming or a dual‑monitor setup. Just be aware that:

- Stand‑offs ARE included (despite the listing not saying so)
- FreeSync may need to be turned off on some units
- Picture quality is great once you match settings manually
- Long‑term support is still unknown

If the listing had mentioned the included spacers, it would have saved me a last‑minute order — so I hope this review helps the next person.

reviews.author: Conner M.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 10, 2025
reviews.content: So I've owned this monitor for about 3 years now and here's what I have to say about this monitor. The picture quality is actually really good. I got the 1080p version and I mostly use it with my Xbox series x and occasionally will use it for some lighter gaming on my gaming PC both work effortlessly well. The screen size is okay I think next time if it's in the budget I'll buy there 34 inch curved monitor. But for right now I'm sticking with this monitor until it dies and so far it's running strong 3 years later. Right now this monitor cost $80 which is an absolute steal at that price. I paid $130 for it and would say it's absolutely worth getting. There's no flickering. The setup process went smoothly. It's compatible with both my console and gaming PC! I absolutely love this monitor! I don't own the 27 inch version but I'd recommend going with either that size or bigger if you can afford it.

reviews.author: Andy B
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 13, 2023
reviews.content:
Today I set up my dual KOORUI 27" model N07 monitors. I was somewhat worried by multiple reviewers reporting that this model isn't really a 4K screen. So while I don't usually write reviews, I'm here to assure you the N07 is in fact a 4K screen with resolution of 3840x2160 and refresh rate of 60hz.

And I know why some people got this wrong. When I unplugged my old 1920x1080 monitors and tried to use the same HDMI cables, Windows identified the monitor as having a 1920x1080 native resolution. But my cables were very old. I plugged both my new monitors in using DisplayPort cables and they were instantly reporting 3840x2160. My monitors did not come with DP cables, just new HDMI cables. But my office had extra DP cables laying around.

If you're still having trouble getting 4K on one of these, be wary of adapters, such as DP-to-HDMI, DVI-to-HDMI, etc. Also try shorter or newer cables. You CAN get 4K out of HDMI cables, but remember that HDMI has been around since 2002 and that standard has gone through many versions with new capabilities over the years.

As for reviewing the other aspect of these monitors... they look great to me! Neither of mine had dead or stuck pixels. the colors look good and image is sharp. I'm not a professional monitor reviewer, but these seem like perfectly good office monitors for a fantastic price compared to big brand names. I took a chance on some of their smaller screens several months back and was blown away at the quality and low price. I'm glad this higher end model hasn't let me down!

UPDATE: My original review was written 10/13/2023 and now it's 5/16/2025. I have never had a single issue with these monitors in the 1 year 7 months I've been using them. In fact I stumbled across my own review after looking to purchase a 3rd matching one... because a triple-monitor setup with these bad boys would be awesome! By the way, I use them on a dual-monitor desk mount. I love that they have VESA mounting holes so they can be used on wall or desk arms/mounts.

I work from home and needed a larger monitor in order to have all the information up that I needed. This is a wonderful monitor that works perfectly for me. I can watch movies videos do all my work and I’ve had no problems whatsoever. I don’t do any intense gaming as I’m sure there’s much better monitors for that. But if you need a monitor for your home that actually works for most everything this works well.

reviews.author: ౨ৎ ^ivysprout^ﾉ#!
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 7, 2023
reviews.content:
i have used this monitor with my new pc setup for about two weeks. the monitor remained in the box for a while as i worked through technical difficulties with my new build. it was long enough for the return window to pass, unfortunately, and now that i've started using the monitor these lines have begun to develop on the screen. to be clear, this is not the result of burning onto the monitor. i don't leave my computer running while i'm away, i put it to sleep every night and the display turns off automatically after a few minutes, and i have been sure to turn the pc entirely off once a week to preserve it. despite my efforts these lines have showed up completely out of nowhere, and are only getting worse. the first photo is yesterday and the second photo is today. the different color is irrelevant, don't mind that, but you see what i mean. more lines have appeared. there are some in the center of the screen not pictured that there was one of yesterday and now there are two of today, and i can assume this will continue until the entire display has essentially become a sheet of paper. save your money for something better, because i am now out of a lot of money because of the seller's complete lack of quality control.

if you're stubborn and still think you should buy it, allow me to give you a few more reasons not to;
the strange shape of the monitor's outline prevents you from putting any kind of cover around it in a consistent way.
it has no brightness controls on it's own. enjoy your eyes being constantly bombarded with blue light at max brightness!
like i mentioned before, the display automatically turns off after a few minutes of inactivity. this would be a good feature if not for the fact it also forcibly disconnects random devices from your pc. i hear the little disconnect-reconnect sound with every mouse shake to revive it.
in addition to that, it takes way too long to come back awake when that happens, and the pc is fully responsive too, leading you to click random stuff. which you will do, because it doesn't wake up from mouse movement. it has to be a click.

for those of you this did work for, all the power to you. i just can't personally recover from the fact i am losing money for something that is entirely the fault of the seller.

UPDATE 10/19/23
i have contacted customer support for the company and they proceeded to ignore me after giving one reply. the lines shown in the photos have consumed my entire screen are impossible to ignore. do not buy this, they have no quality control and will not take responsibility for their flaws.

UPDATE 8/9/24
i thought you'd all like to know this is still the case. the entire screen is covered in these lines like a crt tv. customer service has been useless and does not care. they did not offer a replacement or product support of any kind, and merely suggested that it was some kind of video setting built into the monitor. obviously, the nature of their appearance makes it clear this is not the case. i strongly advise that you do not buy this monitor. it is simply not worth it.
