For performance, it is equally impressive. The high refresh rate (e.g., 144Hz) makes motion silky smooth, whether I'm scrolling a long document or playing a fast-paced game. There's zero noticeable ghosting or lag, making for a truly immersive and responsive experience.

Finally, the design is sleek and practical. The thin bezels make it perfect for dual-monitor setups, and the fully adjustable ergonomic stand (tilt, swivel, and height) ensures I can find the perfect viewing angle for long sessions.

If you are serious about visual quality, productivity, or gaming, you need this monitor. It's perfection wrapped in a beautiful display, and worth every penny!

reviews.author: leonel sanchez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.format: Style: Tilt Stand, 100Hz refresh rateSize: 24 inch
reviews.content: I love this monitor the brightness is a bit dim but it's alr I wish there could be a remote to it to make it much faster to change hdmis, the cable connectivity is really good and real quick to my monitor and my Xbox, it's extremely easy to set up and the powers ourselves is ok ish it's a good screen size and color accuracy was a bit off but I was able to adjust it to my liking, the power source is ok I just wish it wasn't a huge block

reviews.author: Carter
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 25, 2025
reviews.format: Style: Tilt/Height Stand, 100Hz refresh rateSize: 24 inch
reviews.content: This computer is a great entry computer monitor. Works great and has good color, easy to set up and has tilting ability for the perfect angle. Overall great screen to work with and is a good size as well! Good price for what you get as well.

reviews.author: CT Crose
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.content: For the value it is incredibly stable. I've used other arm mounts in the past, and the stability of this one really can't be matched for this value. It currently supports a 32 inch pc monitor, and has supported it with zero issue. Hardware included, and was put together in maybe 5 minutes.

reviews.author: CyberTazer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 26, 2020
reviews.content:
TLDR: Experienced installer, and I chose this mount for my personal office for its ease of installation and its sturdiness. For any monitor mounting operation, careful thought and planning is required, and this mount solves more headaches than is causes.

I read over several reviews of this product prior to purchase. I've seen many that offer criticism of their sturdiness and various other features, but I feel most of them are unfounded.

My first purchase was for work... about 20 of the shorter dual monitor mount by the same company. When we installed them, they easily held the 32 and 27 inch monitor pairs we were attaching to them. Mounting any monitor of this size can be difficult, but with a little patience, all 40 monitors were mounted, aligned and ready to go. After installation, they were quite well fixed in place, and we have had no problems in the year since we bought them. No signs of joints getting weaker, or mounts leaning over or any of that in all that time.

Of course, when it came time to by for my home setup, I looked for the same company. I bought 2 of these mounts, which are higher, so that I could elevate 2 heavier 24 inch monitors above an ultra-wide monitor which will reside below. Even at their highest setting, which provides the most stress to the mount, I found them to be quite firm in their support. When I wiggle my desk (within reason) I get no more than 1/4th to 1/2 of an inch in deflection; nothing that appears dangerous. And trust me, there is no way I would mount 2 cheap monitors over my quite expensive ultra wide monitor if I did not feel they were sturdy enough.

A couple tips on setting them up.

Do NOT place anything in the clamp other than the surface of your desk. If you are worried about marring your desk, you likely should be looking for a floor or wall mount. The rubber grommet included for inside the clamp is not meant to protect your desk, but rather to act as a shock absorber to the system. It will provide some protection to the top surface, but do you really want to be clamping something to a really expensive desk? If you install it, and your mount is leaning, you either did not tighten it enough (this one will squeeze to nothing if adjusted right) or your desk is not sturdy enough to hold the mount. When properly adjusted, a gentle shake of the desk should cause a slight rocking, but nothing should be out of place when they stop.

Attach the monitor to the mounting plate, but do not attach the mounting plate to the extension arm until after the extension arm is attached to the vertical support. It says the opposite in the instructions, but if you lock the extension arm in place firmly, it is safer and easier to do it my way.

I did knock off one star on the range of movement. Each joint allows only a 90 degree bend. This somewhat limits some of your options, but should not preclude any use case you might have in mind. It just means you might have to think about it a bit as you install it. I did not feel this affects the use of the mount enough to have it affect the overall rating. In fact, it likely is a trade off which contributes to the sturdiness of the mount. This mount does provide a reasonable throw, which is the length of the extension arm in this case. If you need more than this one provides, you likely need to look at a spring loaded mount. Throw in this case is the horizontal distance a mount can place the center of the monitor from the mounting location.

Besides sturdiness, the ease of installation is of note. I've mounted hundreds of monitors and this one is quite strait forward. You have enough factors going for an installation, and extra doodads that provide little function are not appreciated. This one fits the bill nicely. This extends to the cable control devices, which are critical if you don't want to slice a cable in half.

All in all, a good, but simple mount with reasonable throw and good sturdy construction. A++

reviews.author: Ben Lords
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 14, 2026
reviews.content:
It took me a bit to get this working with my desk specifically, but once it was mounted properly it has been very solid.

It holds my 27-inch monitor securely and stays exactly where I place it. If your desk setup works with it, it does the job well.

The hardware is included, its easy to adjust, and the height feels great. Its Vesa mount so like, it'll work with pretty much everything. I also think it looks nice and doesn't have one of those crazy thick Z arms some of these have.

reviews.author: Rich H.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 20, 2025
reviews.content:
For the past few years I've been on a quest to find the perfect mount for my preferred triple-27-inch-monitor setup. The first was a cheap unit from a fly-by-night drop shipper, 'nuff said about that. The second carried the monitors on a massive cross-beam that worked well in and of itself, but the outboard monitors could not be slanted inward enough to avoid straining my unfortunately aging eyes. (The setup is fully six feet wide with all monitors flat to the wall.) The third had gas-charged arms, and was recommended by several tech sites. I have no idea what people see in gas-charged mounts. This one (also a VIVO) was finicky as heck to get positioned, you still have to tighten a bunch of screws HARD to get everything to stay in place, and the slightest bump to the desk makes it wobble to an almost dangerous degree and knocks the outboard monitors way out of alignment.

So I was browsing the other day looking for ideas for a way out of my dilemma; a stand that (1) lets the left and right monitors tilt inward about 20 degrees so I'm not constantly craning my neck back and forth to see everything, and (2) would allow you to sneeze without one or more monitors dropping and/or tilting. I was growing discouraged. Then, in the middle of a thread on reddit, it was like a heavenly beacon of light sprang up to highlight a comment reading, simply, "The best triple-monitor mount is three single mounts." And so here we are.

Vivo's mounts are nothing if not complete. You have your choice of clamp-on or through-hole mounts. There are plenty of fasteners, cable clips, and such to do just about anything you'd want to do. Instructions are simple and clear.
The units are also quite sturdy; heavy-gauge steel with serious hex bolts at all the pivot points. It takes some doing to get the arm set the way you want it, but once in place and tight it does not move. A smack on the edge of my desk that made the previous gas-charged mount nearly bounce the outboard monitors' edges off the desktop just makes these wiggle about 1/8 inch for a couple seconds. The only thing I would change is to beef up the desk clamp, maybe with two side-by-side screws instead of the one, to prevent what little side-to-side motion they have. (This was the issue with the gas mount as well - it was simply too heavy for a single clamp.)

Assembly was straightforward. As noted above, I used the edge clamps instead of through-hole, of which my desk has none.

Getting the three monitors to sit side-by-side on their three stands was, as you might expect, rather more involved than positioning gas-sprung arms. I had a real problem at first with all three, owning to the limited range of motion of that jointed arm. The arm will not fold flat on itself, or even close to flat. No matter how I positioned it, I could not get the outboard monitors to sit up against the center one at the right angle and tilt. The center monitor also sat too far out over my desk for comfort. But, Vivo's apparent preference for using a limited number of robust parts came to my rescue. It turned out that I was able to remove the part of that jointed arm closest to the vertical tube on all three. It takes some muscle, but it can be done without breaking or stripping anything. With that limited center joint out of the picture, I was able to get everything positioned where I needed it to be. As a bonus, the center monitor sits even closer to the wall than it did with the gas-spring mount, putting it at just the right distance for me.

Bottom line: Whomever posted that comment on reddit is a genius. This setup Just Works, due in no small part to Vivo's quality and modular design. And, as icing on the proverbial cake, the three stands together cost a little less than that gas-sprung octopus. Five stars.

reviews.author: Evictus
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 29, 2025
reviews.content:
Picked up the extra tall mount so that I could mount another 32" monitor above my primary 32" and it works flawlessly.

I also have two other VIVO mounts for a 3rd and 4th monitor for this system, as well as 3 other single VIVO mounts and a dual VIVO mount for other systems. I've completely fallen in love with the ease of setup and quality of VIVO mounts, across them all I've not had any sort of issues or complaints. They are easy to assemble, easy to mount, super stable, and easy to adjust.

reviews.author: Nathan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 27, 2025
reviews.content:
For the money I think this is worth 5 stars. I used the clamp mount on a desk (with a lip), and so far so good. The option to mount via grommet is nice, and would probably be the better option for my desk based on the lips that run
underneath. However the clamp is strong, and works even with a lip - feels sturdy.

The adjustability and range of motion of the the arm is decent. Horizontal movement of a monitor mounted on this works well, and is easy to adjust. Vertical movement of the monitor is more difficult as you actually have to loosen a screw that allows the arm to travel up or down on the pole. So ideally you'd mount this at the height you want and rarely adjust the height. I see this as obvious based on the design, but I thought it was worth mentioning.

If anything goes wrong I will update my review.

reviews.author: Chris
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 30, 2025
reviews.content:
I’m extremely happy with the VIVO Extra Tall Single Monitor Desk Mount. The build quality is outstanding. Very sturdy and stable, even with a larger ultrawide monitor. The 39-inch pole gives plenty of height adjustment, which is perfect for my setup, and the mount supports a wide range of screen sizes, especially my 27" screen.

Installation was straightforward with clear instructions, and the mount feels solid once set up. I love that it allows for tilt, swivel, and rotation, making it easy to adjust the monitor for comfort and ergonomics. The cable management system is a nice touch, keeping my desk neat and organized.

reviews.author: M. Crisp
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 28, 2021
reviews.content: I purchased two of these, and so far I have installed one of them. I decided to move, so I'm waiting until I am in my new place before I use the second monitor stand. The first one was easy to attach to the VESA mounts on my monitor, but I didn't want to move my desk out from the wall because it would require that I unplug almost everything on the desk, so I stretched and reached and wiggled my way under the desk to place the stand with the monitor already attached in the correct place and then tighten the stand so that it was firmly attached to the desk. In retrospect, if you will be reaching across a desk like I did, you should definitely wait to attach the monitor until after you have the stand clamped to the desk. Now that it is attached, it seems to be doing its job fairly well. The monitor is off my desk, so I can use the space for other things, and I feel fairly confident that the stand will remain attached and my monitor will remain safe. This stand does not adjust easily. I don't remember it claiming to do so, but just in case you think it will, I can tell you that it will not. It will adjust the height and angle of the monitor, just not super easily. You might actually have to use the included hex wrenches to loosen the stand arm in order to adjust the height, which would mean reaching across the desk again, so I probably won't try to adjust the height until I have to reinstall it at my new place. The construction quality seems fine. The stand seems strong enough to do the job it claims it can. The price was good. So, overall, amazon basics offers a decent quality item at a good price, unlike the backpack that I purchased in 2017 that is just about a joke. It has so much padding around the "laptop" sections that I was only able to carry two textbooks at a time to class. It had a special place for my cell phone with a small port that you could feed your headphones through, which seems neat, but is worthless because the idea of putting your phone in the backpack where you can't easily get to it is just dumb. I did not do anything good for myself when I bought the amazon basics backpack, but these monitor stands are pretty good, so I can recommend them to anyone looking for cheap, decent, not particularly impressive stands, but that do what they are supposed to, to anyone looking for stands that attach to a desk with a screw down clamp. If that is you, then buy them...just don't buy the backpack, cause it is not good.

reviews.author: Gil
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 16, 2023
reviews.content: I'm only a day into this monitor and can't speak for its longevity or how long before I get tired of it but so far Im satisfied for what it is, a basic no frills monitor. Screen is bright and I've been slowly reducing the brightness and contrast to adjust for my eye comfort. Fonts are sharp for a 27" 1080p, there is a sharpness adjustment that works well. I think a 27 inch is the biggest I would go with this type of resolution. Colors are nice and bright. I've read reviews where there is lots of movement on the panel and it will move (sort of springy) if touched but when placed on a solid or semi solid surface there's nothing to worry about. It is not frame-less but I don't even notice the frame when looking at the screen. In a world of PC's and monitors that most seem to be "game" oriented, this is a very nice basic monitor and for the money, even better.

reviews.author: Ephraim
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 18, 2025
reviews.content: Coming from a 22-inch HP 1080p monitor which has served me for 11 years, the reason why this 27-inch screen is $97.00 becomes apparently clear. This is a 27-inch 1080p monitor so the resolution compared to a 22-inch 1080p monitor is night and day. While clarity, colors and contrast levels are more accurate on the 22-inch monitor, this 27-inch monitor highlights weaknesses as a result of physics. Pixels are more dense on a 22-inch 1080p panel than on a 27-inch panel. With this monitor (27-inch), picture quality and colors are washed out, hard to reset, contrast setting isn't an option and pixelation is quite apparent compared to the 22-inch 1080p panel i currently have. For me, the monitor is used for work... writing and reading and watching videos primarily for information gathering and not video clarity or picture quality. The monitor is well built, has a slow refresh rate, and is of adequate size for the office. If you need a monitor for work or school... this one will do. If you are a gamer, artist, content creator who wants to utilize the full potential of your graphics card, this monitor is not for you. Value for money? well... if you need a monitor for office and school? This one will do. Gamer? ...look elsewhere, get at least a 1440p or better panel because this one will make you vomit especially if you are well adapted to higher refresh rates, accurate color representation and good picture quality. Why 5-stars? For my purpose, this monitor meets what i need for work and secondly, Amazon delivered a 27-inch monitor without breaking it... a rarity for Amazon.

reviews.author: Dr. K
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 23, 2021
reviews.content:
This monitor replaced an Asus monitor that constantly cut out intermittently. It is very bright and the volume can be easily adjusted to a loud setting. This does not cut out or even flicker. The sound is not stereo or high quality, but it is easily heard. There are numerous settings that can be adjust for everything--pic, sound, color, etc. And for the price ($150), I am very happy with this.
A main critique from other users is the flimsy stand. It is flimsy and wobbles each time you touch the monitor or access the menu, but it does not move during normal use. And by no means does it appear that it will fall apart. But the stand does have a great tilt feature that only adds to its appeal.
So great monitor, much better than my previous Asus.

reviews.author: DF
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 25, 2025
reviews.content: Is it perfect? No. Would I recommend it for gaming? No. But for the price I paid (Used - Like New), this is a great work desk monitor. The screen is crisp and bright, build quality seems solid. It does not come with VESA screws, so you'll need to pick some up if you're mounting it. Oddly the manual says maximum 14 mm M4 screws for the VESA mount holes, but the actual maximum depth seems to be around 10 mm. I picked up some 4mm-0.70 x 10mm Phillips Truss Machine Screws from the hardware store and they are holding it just fine. As others have mentioned, the built-in speakers are pretty bad, so you'll want to get your audio output from something else. There is an AUX output on the back if needed.

reviews.author: Bryce Mahns
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026
reviews.content: If you need a cheap secondary monitor screen works great to have music and web pages on separate screen.

reviews.author: Ricky Valley
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 17, 2022
reviews.content: I'm currently got my Chromebook hooked up to it and you do have to learn how to doggle through the unmarked buttons. Right to left I believe they go one through six or one through five we have to toggle or toggle between HDMI and vga. Once you figure that out, if you play audio on the TV by itself, it doesn't sound so good. If you play the audio from a computer or Chromebook hooked up to the tv, I can't explain how but it sounds incredible. It's like I added a 27-inch screen to my chromebook. It's not completely plug and play cuz you still have to press buttons on the Chromebook and that's alternate plus one of the top buttons usually the fifth button over from the left on top you got to press those two together. As far as the TV the button on the far right in is the power button. That only needs to be pressed once because once it receives a signal it turns itself on. And that is a plus in the long run. Hooking up a Playstation 2 you got to jump through a few more hoops but it can be done as well if you use the VGA serial connector. But, to use that, you must first hook up something with HDMI so you can rotate the screen over to vga. you better have the PlayStation on when you do this because it will revert to HDMI within 5 seconds if it does not receive a signal from the VGA. You got to have that PlayStation 2 up and running/ updatede PS2 fix! Get the PS2 HDMI converter with the power for USB... works better than the RCA cables :)

reviews.author: jack olson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2025
reviews.content: it is a pretty good monitor and worth its price but the base of the stand is kind of large but works well overall. it has 2 different hdmi ports so that is good. and i bought another because its permormance was valid and trustworthy. it also has very good colors.

reviews.author: wujanni
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 26, 2025
reviews.content: nice product, but the speaker could be better

reviews.author: Leah Mazdra
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 7, 2026
reviews.content: The monitor itself feels very high-quality and has a solid, premium weight to it. It’s definitely not a cheap-feeling plastic build. My screen arrived damaged, which was a huge letdown, but the physical quality of the frame and stand is excellent. Giving 4 stars because of the arrival condition, but the monitor itself is top-tier.

reviews.author: Mark
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026

reviews.author: Ramon Hernandez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2026
reviews.content: Bought for grandson’s computer. Works just fine. He has 3 monitors, all curved.

reviews.author: Joseph Poirier
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 16, 2020
reviews.content:
Samsung 27-inch FHD 1080p display had a Vesa mount, but it was too high on monitor to be positioned above a small laptop from a standard desk mount.

It did not have speakers, and for $10 more on a Walmart sale the following week, I could get one that did, so I traded it in for the 32-inch with speakers. That said, the 27-inch had a vesa mount and the 32 did not. Here are my thoughts.

27-inch or so, is an ideal size for a monitor with a QHD or WQHD 1440p display sitting a foot or so behind your laptop, but it does not provide any increase in real desk space for a software developer, graphic designer or film editor when it only has an FHD 1080p display, like this one. I tried the 32, thinking it would provide the more space and really only got bigger fonts. The end user really needs some extra lines of resolution in that desk monitor. I believe around 80 pixels / inch in a laptop and 50 pixels / inch in a desktop display is about ideal for most users.

In addition, 60 Hz is the native refresh rate. 4 ms response time. anything below 4 ms, will be basically un-noticeable to the human eye, but 120 Hz would be much better for gaming, graphics or video editing.

reviews.author: JR
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 3, 2024
reviews.content:
The Wali Single Monitor Desk Mount works better than I expected.

First, the basics. The mount comes well packed and the instructions are very simple to understand. There is so little assembly required that the instructions are almost not needed. Assembly and installation can be done in less than ten minutes (not including cable management if you choose to use that feature). Adjusting everything is easy. Overall quality of the parts and the assembled product are quite good.

I have two monitors that I wanted to mount on a flat desktop. I needed to be able to get the monitors right next to each other and still have the desk flat against the wall. Most double monitor stands will not allow for this unless your monitors are the perfect size for the spread of the arms. The movable arms will usually flex toward the wall if you want the monitors to be edge-to-edge with each other. The result is the desk having to be offset from the wall to accommodate this. By using two single monitor stands, I am able to move the posts to positions that allow the arms to be extended in such a way that everything is flat with the wall.

The other frequently stated issue with most monitor stands is that the arms have hydraulic pistons and/or springs to deal with the weight of the attached monitors so they are easy to move up and down. These pistons and springs are often of poor quality and either fail quickly or don't work as expected from the get-go.

This is not a problem with the Wali Single Monitor Desk Mount. The arm is solidly attached to the 16" pole and only moves up and down by loosening the arm mounting and sliding the arm up or down on the pole. The arm, once set to the desired height, can be move forward, backward, side-to-side and tilt. This makes for a much sturdier setup with no pistons or springs. This system works very well, since I (and I think most people) have little need to move a monitor up or down after the initial installation.

I leave the included Allen wrench stuck in one of the vertical facing screws behind a monitor so that it is always handy but out of sight. If I do need to move the monitors up or down, the tool is right there.

I will buy two more of these for my wife's desk.

reviews.author: J. Masotti
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviews.content: This is the monitor mount I should have had all along. The extra tall pole allowed me to mount my 32" monitor at the correct height (something I couldn't do with the spring-mount I previously had. It came with good directions, everything needed for install except for a Phillips head screwdriver and a 10mm wrench for one cap nut. The arm that holds the monitor is highly adjustable, and once everything is tightened down, very sturdy. I recommend this one without reservations.

reviews.author: Kirk Tinsley
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 31, 2025
reviews.content:
I bought the 31.5 model to mount a 27" monitor above a 32" monitor on my desk. This is the first monitor mount I have ever purchased and I am thrilled with the entire mount! The installation instructions were simple to follow. All hardware was clearly notated and packaged by part number. The mount holds the 27" monitor (which isn't all that light) perfectly above my desk monitor and is very stable. Adjustment is easy and effective and It doesn't wobble one bit.
The cable routing clips work well and are literally a snap to install. I used the clamp mount but a screw-down desk mount is included.

reviews.author: Cory
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 21, 2025
reviews.content: Its definitely sturdy and works well but it kind of feels very rigid in positionality. It does stay where you position it but I feel like it should be easier to position it.

reviews.author: Richard Neuman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 10, 2023
reviews.content: I recently purchased an LG 32UN500 monitor weighing ~17 lbs. The included monitor stand was not great according to the reviews so I opted to purchase another stand. The WALI would take the load and it appeared solid. Assembly was straightforward. I opted to put a hole through the desktop for the stand. The monitor came with its own screws for the VESA mount so I used those. Once assembled I adjusted the arm height and tightened the arm to the stand so it would not slide. I then placed the monitor with the attached VESA mount in the arm mount and attached the nut to secure it all in place. Adjustments are easy with the supplied Allen keys. The stand allows me to tilt, swivel, and raise/lower the monitor height. Solid product that does the job it was designed to do at a reasonable price. Adjusting the height does require loosening the fastener so if that is required frequently you might consider another stand. One last point, the attachment to the desk requires so little space that I have regained all the space I lost to the stand for the previous monitor.

reviews.author: A. M. White
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 12, 2020
reviews.content:
I have two PCs running six monitors on my desk. I run a complex GIS workstation and often need multiple windows open at once. To accomodate a new 32" monitor, I had to move one mo my 27" ASUS PB278Q monitors to an elevated location. Not having wall real estate in the immediate location, this monitor had to be supported on a pole-style mount 18" above the table top.

I was impressed with the ruggedness of this rig as soon as I opened the box. Nothing flimsy about it. Every bit is overengineered to ensure nothing fails. Even the parts are separately bagged and labeled and all tools are included. Assembly was easy and the Asus monitor was up and running in about 15 minutes. Note that that 27" monitor is hanging off of the side of the pole as an outrigger, completely beyond the pole's vertical position.

I used the clamp option for mounting as I saw no need to drill a big hole in my desktop. Just make sure that you really tighten the heck out of the mounting clamp to ensure that the pole stands vertically. If your desktop thickness is marginal, add a piece of wood to spread out the load. Very simple.

It's rare to see a product like this - well made and worth every penny - come out of China. Too bad it's not made in the USA. That's the only downside of this. Maybe someday, we will wake up.

reviews.author: M. Jeh
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 27, 2017
reviews.content:
Overall, this is a great product considering the $27 price tag. Looks nice, seems sturdy, and necessary tools are included.

Pros:
- Lightweight, easy to assemble and adjust
- Appears sturdy based on initial use
- Aesthetically pleasing
- Plastic cable hooks are useful for cable management
- Significantly reduces monitor wobble when typing (vs. factory monitor stand sitting on desk)

Neutral:
- C-clamp base is massive at 3" x 3". This is almost certainly necessary for stability / safety when mounting heavier 27" monitors, but still unexpectedly bulky. The width is mentioned in the product pictures, but both width / depth could and probably should be explicitly mentioned in the product description. My PC desk has a 2" overhang, with a slightly beveled edge, so I used a dense piece of cardboard underneath the overhang to ensure that the clamp was flush and level.
- Arm cannot be folded flat against wall due to inherent design limitation (180 degree joints), so vertical post must be placed off-center if desk is against the wall. So if you plan to move the monitor forward / back and want to keep it centered on your desk in any position, expect to do some fiddling with the mounting location of the vertical post. This is not really a drawback when you consider the price tag, and if you have space behind your desk this is obviously a non-issue.

Cons:
- One cable hook snapped as I was attaching it to the monitor arm, but I was able to make a flimsy repair with packaging tape.

Note to anyone considering this mount for use with a TV instead of a monitor... some manufacturers (Samsung in my case) use larger or non-standard VESA mounting sizes such as 200 x 100mm. I purchased the following Mount World adapter plate along with four M4-.7mm x 10mm flathead screws / nuts from Home Depot. The adapter included a bag of various nuts / bolts / washers, but the provided flathead screws were unfortunately too large to fit the original VESA plate included with the WALI mount.

Mount World 1056 VESA 200 x 200 Universal Adapter for Medium Mounts

reviews.author: The one lonely buyer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.content: Good monitor stand perfectly fits my purpose.

reviews.author: Paulc253
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.content: I can't believe this monitor is such high-quality for this price. Seriously. I spent HOURS looking for a second monitor for my home office. I was looking at price points up to $400 and frankly, was getting overwhelmed with the choices and reviews. Then this little guy showed up, 13.5 inches, 2K FHD 60HZ for $79??? So, I took a chance and wow, right out of the box hooked up the USB C to my Microsoft Surface laptop and immediate, incredible results. High quality, impressive brightness in a little monitor, and it came with all the cables you could possibly need and even a cover / stand (never used it). I bought a $9 iPad stand and everything, I mean EVERYTHING came out exactly how I wanted my home office / workstation to look and feel. If you're looking for a plug-and-play monitor, this is the one to get.

reviews.author: Mikey94025
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 8, 2025
reviews.content:
I wanted a portable monitor to pair with my 13" Windows laptop for extra workspace, and this product delivers beautifully. It stands out from competing options thanks to its smaller size, higher resolution, and impressive brightness—all powered by a single USB-C cable. Overall, this portable display is bright, sharp, and compact—an excellent companion for a 13" laptop when you need more screen space without sacrificing clarity.

𝗩𝗲𝗿𝘆 𝗯𝗿𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 - The brightness is excellent once adjusted. By default, the screen starts at only 30%, but pressing the knob on the right upward quickly boosts it to 100%. I estimate the brightness to be at least 300–350 nits, which is noticeably better than many portable monitors capped at 250–300 nits. Compared to my laptop’s 400-nit display, this monitor looks nearly as bright. One important detail is that the top USB-C port (C2) remembers your last brightness setting, while the bottom port (C1) always resets to 30%, which I find far too dim.

𝗛𝗶𝗴𝗵𝗲𝗿 𝗿𝗲𝘀𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 - Resolution is another highlight. At 2256x1504 (2K), it offers sharper clarity than the typical 1920x1080 (1080P) found in most portable monitors. Text and visuals look crisp, and the higher resolution makes a real difference in everyday use.

𝗦𝗺𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗿 𝘀𝗶𝘇𝗲 - The size is also ideal. At 13.3", it pairs perfectly with my 13" laptop. I prefer this smaller footprint over bulkier 14" or 15" models, since it maintains clarity and works seamlessly with my Windows scaling and accessibility font size settings. For me, higher resolution is more valuable than a larger screen.

𝗡𝗼𝘁 𝗢𝗟𝗘𝗗 - It’s worth noting that this monitor uses IPS technology rather than OLED. OLED panels, like those found in high-end iPhones and iPads, offer deeper contrast and richer colors, but they also come at a higher price. While some 13" OLED portable monitors exist (like this one), they are more expensive, so this IPS option strikes a good balance of performance and affordability.

𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲 - One of the other reviewers said the product wouldn't stand reliably for them. Make sure you're using the back panel bent at the top (flush with the monitor), so only the lower part of the panel bends back like an easel. My product is very stable like this.

reviews.author: Mason
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2026
reviews.content: Works great, high quality little monitor, light weight, easy set up, great picture. The foldable cover works pretty well to hold it up on the go but I recommend getting a little stand for your main desk or something.

reviews.author: Drew
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 8, 2025
reviews.content:
I work from home where I have a pretty sweet setup. When I travel to my office in another state, I don't have a dedicated space and needed something portable that I could use as a second monitor. I tried several different brands of portable monitors before settling on the this one.

PROS:
Lightweight
Easy to setup
Can be used with a stand sold separately by another seller
Cover is nice
Decent display for the price
Comes with all cables needed
Can run standalone power and powered by USB from my Dell.
Fits a standard travel case.

CONS:
Although the cover is nice, it is also used as it's stand. The cover will eventually lose its firmness and no longer be effective.
The switch on the side is used for brightness and other settings. It does not retain your settings, so you have to adjust every time you turn it on.
I wish it would be just a little brighter, not a deal breaker still plenty effective, but something I noticed.
Barely fits in my laptop bag. I had to purchase a slightly larger bag for flying and protection. (Yes, I know that is what you get with a 17.3" monitor)
Did not come with a travel case.

In the end, it is a nice inexpensive monitor. I do wish it had a case, and I do not like the built-in stand/cover, but it does the job. I bought a standard case and a monitor stand that is spring loaded. It only sits 4 inches or so off of a desk but that it all that I needed. I can also set in vertical mode with the stand. Minus one star as the cover as a stand could be problematic in the future.

reviews.author: Chewy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 11, 2025
reviews.content:
Edit: it supports 144Hz after all. The Steam Deck had to be manually set to the resolution and desired frame rate. However, FreeSync still didn’t work.

Pros:
• Solid build quality
• Supports HDR
• USB-C OTG port for connecting peripherals (mouse/keyboard)
• Two USB-C ports (one for power, one for video), plus a mini HDMI port
• Includes all necessary cables (USB-C video, USB-C power, and HDMI)
• Comes with a basic power adapter that claims to be UL listed (unverified—buyer beware)

Cons:
• No warranty beyond Amazon’s 30-day return window
• FreeSync and 144Hz do not work with the Steam Deck (despite enabling them in the OSD); untested on other systems
• Weak built-in speakers, though expected for a portable monitor
• Power adapter is rated at 15W and emits a loud buzzing under load—potential sign of poor quality or a safety issue
• Mediocre stand
• High price considering the limited features and lack of warranty
• Poor customer support—took multiple inquiries to get a response

According to the seller, there’s no warranty beyond the 30-day return policy. The monitor doesn’t support FreeSync or refresh rates above 60Hz with the Steam Deck. VRR is greyed out, and enabling FreeSync in the OSD menu (even with HDR disabled) didn’t resolve the issue—features that work fine with other portable monitors.

It’s unfortunate, because this is the only portable monitor I found with USB-C OTG port. I took a chance on this monitor, but due to its failure to meet advertised capabilities and the lack of warranty and support, I’m returning it.

Bottom line: I can’t recommend this monitor

reviews.author: Lanh V.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2026
reviews.content: Good quality, I like it. The brightness at its max is too bright for nighttime, so 50% is awesome. However not good for daylight, and I don't plan to use it outside, bright coffeeshops, or whatever. I'm happy with it, and I can confirm 30w maximum passthrough charging via USB-C for my steamdeck. For my Samsung Galaxy s21 ultra charging rate was surprisingly only 12w while I was using Dex. I use with my OLED steam deck and even though IPS, it's a good IPS so at the correct distance for the right scenario it's perfect. 60hz max, but I've never been spoiled by anything higher so I'm good with it. The USBC cable is included and works. Pretty simple to plug and play. Very light for my tablet clamp/stand. Speakers for emergency use only

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 22, 2024
reviews.content:
I have used a Geekom mini PC as my desktop for two years, and it drives the two large monitors on my desk and even the 55-inch TV on the wall, and I have gotten used to that amount of workspace, especially for programming and other data analytic work. So we are traveling, and my old laptop does not cut it any longer and has only one screen. I like a two+ monitor setup and I like the two monitors to be the same size (call it a symmetry issue). I decided that my Mini IT11 Geekom would do the traveling, but it has no screen at all. After searching all portable monitors on Amazon, I centered on the ALOLG 17.3 inch monitors and bought TWO of them. I was attracted by the features, looks, and price.

I am thrilled so far. They work out of the box (one connected USB-C and the other connected by HDMI. The Geekom powered the USB-C connection, but not the HDMI. No matter, I have both connected to the USB-A to USB-C power supply brick that came in the box. All the connections I needed are in the box and I was glad there was both a 'C' and 'HDMI' cable since my older Geekom (can drive 4 monitors, but all connected differently) - (newer Geekoms have dual HDMI and ... well go look that up yourself.

Not a negative for me, but HDR did not work on both monitors at the same time, but I don't care about that. Also, I was able to up one and then both refresh rates to 144Mhz, although not until I plugged them into the power supply.

Speaking of which, some description of the power consumption of the included brick and whether it is safe to use a higher wattage would have been nice for me since I am not conversant with that kind of tech detail.

I still distrust the covers (which are nice) to hold the monitor steady, but so far so good. The angle is perfect for me. I do like that the cover is not one of those wrap-around and under covers that take up valuable space. They have a very clean appearance, as seen in the picture.

BTW, I am working just as I would on my home setup; my travel productivity just increased.

i now know I can use it with my TAB (in Dex), my phone, and even my ancient laptop. I have high confidence that they will work simply because of how smooth this beginning is. If not, I will come back and modify this review.

My only issue is that I wish that ALOLG had discussed the durability of the screen more in their advertisement. Exactly how resistant are they to accidental scratches? My worry is that they come with a relatively thick screen protector fixed to the top edge. Do I need to leave it in place because the screen surface is delicate? Or should I remove it and take my chances? I wish they told more about this. BTW I can lift up the clear sheet covering the screen and the matte finish on the screens are excellent. Again, I am just wondering how important it is to affix a screen protector, either the one included or an aftermarket tablet screen protector.

Finally, I can get the Geekom, my Logi wireless keyboard and mouse, and both monitors in my travel case. They are so thin and protected by the cover that they take up minimal room in my bag. No longer will I have to haul my "monitor box" to have a larger and second monitor when I travel.

By the way, the footprint of my setup up as you can see includes the d the monitors measures a eekom and wires that are behind it measure 32" x 18" so this set up would comfortable fit on any small hotel desk (or MIL's dining table).

Bottom line, I am thrilled.

reviews.author: David
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 2, 2025
reviews.content:
This monitor is awesome! Used it for my Steam Deck and my phone (Samsung DeX desktop thing) and it's basically plug and play. The included magnetic cover is the same as a premium one for an iPad.

The picture is exactly as described, it looks great. Sure it's not OLED but I didn't notice any backlight bleed through, it's a great LCD picture.

The screen is light enough that I put a magnetic ring on the back and used a phone stand / connector from Mechanism to "mount" this screen to my Steam Deck, making a monstrosity of a psuedo-Nintendo DS. It's wonderful.

Going to try this with a raspberry pi next. Can't wait.

reviews.author: Kylee Y.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 28, 2025
reviews.content:
FANTASTIC, VIBRANT, BEAUTIFUL MONITOR.
This is my response to most negative reviews:
Listen, I have owned MANY different monitors over the last 10 or so years, from VA to IPS all the way to OLED. And this monitor my friend, is gorgeous.

BEZELS: Now, I see a LOT of people complaining about the black "bezels" around the monitor, which "make it appear smaller". This is nothing but placebo, yes, there are 'gaps' between the bezels of the chassis of the monitor and the actual panel itself (See pictures). So it LOOKS like it might be smaller than a traditional IPS 27" screen. But I've done measurements and the screen itself is about 26.6 inches. Whereas my old 27" Samsung IPS monitor screen is about ~26.7 inches. THIS IS MINIMAL AND NOT NOTICEABLE. Good luck trying to find an OLED without it.. ALSO TURN OFF THE LIGHTS IN YOUR ROOM IF THIS REALLY BOTHERS YOU THAT MUCH

COLOR FRINGING WITH HIGH-CONTRAST TEXT(See pictures): Color fringing in QD-OLED monitors occurs due to their unique triangular subpixel layout, unlike the traditional RGB stripe pattern found in IPS displays. This layout improves brightness and color accuracy but can cause text clarity issues since most text rendering algorithms are optimized for RGB stripes. As a result, fine text may appear with colored edges or slight blurring, especially at smaller sizes or high-contrast settings. And because you are buying a 1440p monitor, this is close to unavoidable. And like what a lot of reviewers said, its really a personal thing whether is bothers you are not. For me, this was something I noticed ever so slightly at first, and then after using this monitor for a few days, I had to actively look for it to notice it. (If you plan to just use this as a gaming monitor, then you will never notice this in games) don't forget you're supposed to be 2ft away from your screen. And if you can't afford the 4k version that's okay, don't leave negative reviews about color fringing when you're talking about a 1440p qd-oled.

Display: Oh. My. Gosh. Does this monitor not look absolutely stunning. The vibrant popping colors, the inky deep blacks, the responsiveness, the refresh rate. This display feels almost too good to own. You can go to the review for the G60SD on Rtings.com and get the ICC profile (color calibration file) for this panel. And once you get your HDR and colors right, this monitor just pops out at you. High-contrast scenes in video games/ movies pop out at you in a realistic way. I noticed this especially with flashlights in video games, it almost tricks your brain more into looking like real life. It's SO hard to describe without seeing it in person. I TRULY believe most people who have complained about this have not calibrated correctly, or are just blind to a certain degree, which is okay too.. (Also if you are an editor, I don't care what people say, this is a great monitor for you too...) INSANELY accurate colors, I do some color-grading and this next to my studio monitor looks close to identical color-wise, but, of course, the vibrancy isn't comparable to QD-OLED.

Don't let the negative reviews fool you, most times its either snobby or ignorance.
You will most likely be blown away by this screen and love it.

Enjoy!

reviews.author: Jennifer Reed
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content:
The Samsung 27” Odyssey OLED G6 (G60SD) is an outstanding gaming monitor. The screen quality is absolutely amazing. The QD-OLED panel delivers vibrant, accurate colors with deep blacks and incredible contrast. Everything looks sharp and smooth in QHD, and the 360Hz refresh rate with the 0.03ms response time makes gameplay feel incredibly fluid and responsive.

One of the biggest surprises for me is how comfortable it is for long gaming sessions. I can play for hours and my eyes don’t feel strained or fatigued. The anti-glare coating really helps, and the overall brightness and color balance are easy on the eyes while still looking vivid and immersive.

The color scheme and overall design are sleek and modern. It looks great on the desk and feels premium. The built-in cooling system is a nice bonus for long-term performance, and the 3 year warranty adds peace of mind.

For everything this monitor offers OLED quality, 360Hz performance, FreeSync Premium Pro support, and solid build quality, this is a great price. If you’re serious about gaming and want top-tier visuals and performance, this monitor is perfect!

reviews.author: Chris H.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 19, 2025
reviews.content: I waited over a year to write this review and honestly it's going to be a short one because this monitor has been flawless! I mostly play competitive FPS games, COD, counter strike, Apex, etc. Let me say this monitor improved my gameplay more than upgrading my whole computer to the current generation of top tier gaming hardware. I also own a similar LG monitor and LG 65" OLED TV but this stays as my main display. Build quality is excellent, colors, clarity are insane. Every game I played looked incredible and the movements felt so much better!

reviews.author: Wesley H.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 2, 2026
reviews.content: I’ve been using this Samsung monitor for roughly 8 months now and it has been an amazing change from my old 1080P 144 Hz monitor it’s definitely a premium monitor that I’ve had no issues with. It looks amazing very sharp image plays very well for story games and competitive games and you cannot beat that 360 Hz refresh rate. The response time is unreal. It is a very sturdy and heavy monitor with a nice metal base I would recommend it to anyone looking for a very premium OLED monitor for gaming.

reviews.author: Byron
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 14, 2025
reviews.content: Overall, quite pleased with this monitor. The colors are vibrant and rich. The refresh rate is the highest I have ever had before. It comes with a really nice stand, too. You can get the monitor right out of the box without getting greasy fingers on it because it clicks right into the stand. Easy setup. I get this random flicker sometimes on the screen and I can’t figure out if it’s an issue or if that’s a protective feature to keep the LEDs from burning in. Either way it’s so minimal I barely notice it. I would highly recommend this monitor. Especially for gaming. Games look gorgeous in OLED.

reviews.author: AP
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 19, 2026
reviews.content: Great product. I got 360hz OLED, I only wish the menu had a bit more of customization but it’s still really a good monitor. I was worried about it being anti glare instead of glossy, I used to own a glossy. However I don’t notice a difference at all, apparently it’s only a difference when there’s a lot of light, which I don’t have. The blacks are truly amazing too, and the input delay and smoothness are amazing. For 600 dollars a really good option, I also went from 240hz to 360hz and the difference was still very noticeable!

reviews.author: Nicky Little
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 6, 2026
reviews.content: The Samsung Odyssey OLED G6 has completely blown me away. The picture quality is on another level — deep blacks, vibrant colors, and buttery‑smooth gameplay thanks to the high refresh rate. Every game looks sharper and more immersive, and even everyday use feels upgraded. Once I dialed in my settings, it became hands‑down the best monitor I’ve ever used. If you want a premium OLED experience in a 27‑inch size, this thing delivers in every way.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content: It is an excellent monitor.!!! I give it 6 out of 5 stars.:) It provides a lot of proverbial real estate. It is a fantastic product for Architects, Video Producers, Podcasters. Specially those who work on a Mac. For the same money Apple provides 28", while LG is 40"! LG did it really well. The screen resolution is fantastic. The only one thing is kind of "chep" looking a plastic envelope the monitor is built into. No negative feelings about plastic but it could have had a better the feel of the surface. I got used to it since I do not look at the back that much anyway.

reviews.author: DRESS JUNGLE
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 22, 2026
reviews.content: I should have gotten one of these a long time ago it helps my eyes a lot staring at the screen all day. The only issue I have is that it I haven’t figured out how to turn off the light if it doesn’t go off automatically with timer which is a problem for sleeping in the same room. There’s hopefully a fix but it’s not intuitive.

reviews.author: Yevheniy Shestopalko
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 6, 2026
reviews.content:
I came from an 800R and 1000R monitor. This one is 2500R which means it's anywhere from 2.5-4.5x less curved than my previous displays. I didn't think it would be as much of an issue but I really miss how uniformly sized everything was on the more curved displays. The edges of this monitor have noticably smaller text due to the big increase in distance from my eyes to the pixels.

Otherwise it works great with the Mac. Single USB-C cable for power & display. 120hz, P3 display mode. No concerns. I do wish the bezels were smaller though, I bought an LG ultra wide 8 year ago that had the same exact chonky bezels and would have expected things to improve since then.

The stand is okay, but the area where it connects the arm to the bottom stand-plate looks a bit odd, you can see the pressure points where the screws holding it together warp the metal a little bit - looks a bit cheap even though it's solid metal.

The speakers are hilariously bad, but if you're spending this much on a monitor you've likely got your own audio solution.

reviews.author: Anam
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 15, 2025
reviews.content: I bought this monitor to save money instead of getting the Apple Pro Display XDR, and it was a smart choice. The Thunderbolt 5 feature is great—it perfectly charges my MacBook Pro M4 Max with one cable, which is key for my work setup. I did not like the included stand but attaching with an external VESA arm was easy. I appreciate the included white cables, but the power supply looks too big and heavy. My main issue is brightness. Rated at 450-600 Nits, it feels lacking compared to my LG G5 4K TV, which, at a similar price, is dramatically brighter. In a nutshell: A highly functional monitor with pro connectivity, but not flagship class brightness.

reviews.author: Mike Trent
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content:
I ordered this monitor to replace a LG 27MD5KL-B Ultrafine 27" IPS LCD 5K. The specs are excellent: bigger screen, more PPI, 5 Thunderbolt / USB-C ports instead of 4. I use a lot of USB ports for audio production: audio interfaces, digital keyboards, a wired Keychron keyboard, things like that.

I unboxed the display and integrated it into my setup. The display looked great, much larger than I was used to, very cool. The connection to my audio interface would not work over USB-C, it would only work over Thunderbolt even though it was a USB-C device; and even then the interface suffered from static and drop-out. A USB-C ground lift made the problems worse, not better. After a few minutes, the display decided to stop working with my Keychron USB keyboard. My Arturia music keyboard never worked at all.

This is a poor value for the money. You can easily spend less money on an excellent 32" screen with lousy connectivity options. If you're looking for a reliable Thunderbolt workhorse like the LG 27MD5KL-B Ultrafine 27" 5K, this model is not it.

reviews.author: Bob Woods
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 22, 2025
reviews.content: Hands down great monitor. Great color and sharpness. I’m a photographer and video editor and this is the best I’ve used. 80+% of the Apple Pro Display XDR at 40% of the price.

reviews.author: Steve H.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 7, 2025
reviews.content: I'm a huge LG fan. Every display in my house is LG. I have the LG Ultragear for my gaming PC and that is way better than this one. I think they have the best OLED technology, HOWEVER, this monitor isn't it. I'm so disappointed after waiting for this release. Having watched countless videos and have read many review articles, it almost seems like everyone was paid by LG for their positive reviews. I have two Apple Studio displays and the goal was to consolidate into one 32" inch display, without sacrificing quality. The size is perfect. I even like the design aesthetic, although some critics think the plastic is cheap. It's built well, and it looks great, so not sure what the fuss is about there. The display on the other hand is just as good as a few hundred dollar IPS display. This screen is not even close to the quality you deserve for $2000. I even tried tweaking the display settings (which wasn't easy), and still nothing good came from it. The overall display was very grainy, distorted in some areas, and the icons in the Mac OS Dock were just horrible looking. Going from a very crisp Apple display to this monitor was a huge let down. I would not recommend this product, and I would wait until better tech comes along to compete against Apple's extremely expensive displays, but you gotta give it to Apple - they create a superior product, and they charge an insane premium for it.

reviews.author: Dylan M Cunningham
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content: Beautiful resolution and color

reviews.author: David Zamrzla
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 13, 2025
reviews.content: I recently purchased this monitor and am very satisfied with it. I'm no geek but I did a lot of research and feel I chose the right brand and style. It was good value and easy to set up. The main thing I would share with others is that this 27" curved screen is great for whatever kind of computer use you have. I'm not a gamer or a graphic artist, just an older user with not the greatest eyesight and this monitor spoils me! Besides the size, the resolution and refresh rate are a noticeable improvement. The curve brings everything closer plus it makes for a much smaller footprint than a flat screen.

reviews.author: JONATHAN P.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 5, 2026
reviews.content: Great wide screen with vibrant colors. Does everything I need but I'm not a gamer looking for maximum performance. I couldn't say either way if it is. Great for surfing the internet and watching movies. Has tilt feature and seems stable on its base.

reviews.author: Stephanie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2026
reviews.content: Bought two of these for our office setup - very nice! The size and curved screen is easy on the eyes, the monitor quality is crystal clear, functionality is great as well! I may have to look into turning down the brightness (if possible). Sound quality is not as good as I expected, but I’m really not using it for sound so it works well for what we need.

reviews.author: Autumn Turner
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 25, 2025
reviews.content:
The monitor is fantastic. The only reason it is not a five-star product is the built-in speakers. They are barely audible at the highest setting. I did not buy this monitor for the speakers, though it would be nice if they worked decently.

A great value for the money, particularly considering the curved screen size. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Anthony G.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 3, 2025
reviews.content: Easy setup, quick response, good color, low price, includes hdmi cable

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 25, 2025
reviews.content: Graphics are awesome! Refresh rate is good. No issues with gaming.

reviews.author: Nicole & Jason
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 30, 2025
reviews.content:
Perfect for any home office setup!

Refresh rate of 75hz is perfect for routine office work, but isn’t ideal for gaming (as advertised) - great size to multitask - good value for the price - sound quality is decent for internal speakers - 1080p video is crisp & clear with its pixel density

reviews.author: Kevin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 7, 2026
reviews.content: Good equality, Sound Is good, and the image is pretty good for the price!!.

reviews.author: DOGAN UCAR
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content:
Very happy with this purchase. The screen is clear and sharp and the size works perfectly with my laptop. Setup was super easy, just plug and play. I’ve used it with USB C and HDMI, and both worked without any issues. Makes multitasking much easier, especially when working or travelling.

Honestly, I bought another two-screen setup before, and it was very heavy and complicated to set up. It looked fancy but wasn’t practical. This one is simple, easy to use, quick to get up and running, compact, and easy to travel with. You only need one cable, which makes a big difference.

great value for money, so far no issues with it but If I find something I'll update it here

reviews.author: Altidor jeangardy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026

reviews.author: T. Coll
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 2, 2024
reviews.content:
First, out of the box, it's a very easy setup IF you have a fully powered USB-C port on your laptop. Most newer ones do but be sure you have such a port (usually indicated by a small lightening symbol by it); if you don't, even though they send you USB-C to USB-A and USB-C to HDMI cables, they will NOT power the monitor enough. I imagine you'd have to buy the separate power adapter for the monitor they mention in the ad (NOT included) to make such a setup work. So be aware of that. I suppose perhaps they don't work just on my particular laptop, but I doubt that's the case.

With the supplied USB-C to USB-C cable it works perfectly. Well, I won't say "perfectly" (and this is the reason I gave only 4 over all stars) because as you can see from the first picture, when compared to my native monitor, the colors are different. It doesn't matter what setting I use or adjust on the S1 monitor. I can never get it to be like the native monitor of my laptop (ROG STRIX). I suspect the reason for this is because one cannot utilize "HDR" on this monitor. Note the picture of the HDR setting. It's off by default and for good reason; if you turn it on, the screen goes dark and will not return until you turn HDR off. I suspect it can't be turned on because, maybe, you'd need the power adapter they mention to enable that feature. (the power from just the USB-C port, even a full powered one isn't enough). This is my suspicion but I can't confirm it as I don't have the power adapter they mention (I don't even know what it is to be honest, as it's rather vague their description of the "power adapter").

Regardless, HDR will not function on this monitor with just the equipment they provide. Now, with that said, it's a *perfectly fine* monitor for what I wanted, which was just a second monitor to turn my laptop into a mini-desktop setup. So I could play games on my laptop (in full screen) while sitting in my favorite chair and still have the ability to check email (or other websites) on the other screen. It's perfect for this, and really will be perfect for many situations that don't entail gaming.

As you can see from the refresh rate tests attached, the refresh rate is approximately 50Hz (even though it says "calculating" the test never got to a state where it would be "finished" as in the test for my native monitor at 240Hz). But the test is accurate as it accurately reports the refresh rate for my native monitor. So, I'm quite confident it's accurate when testing this monitor. I can't tell you what website I went to test it, as my first review was rejected, I THINK because the screenshots had the testing website URL in it. So, I edited that out. Hopefully THIS review goes through. But you can search for any refresh test online it's quite easy and straightforward. The point is, that this is NOT a gaming monitor by any stretch of the imagination, but it's perfectly fine for non-gaming applications.

The first picture of the entire setup is NOT edited in any way, that's how the color difference is. At first it was a little annoying (disappointing really) but I got used to it and really don't notice the difference anymore, but it is there in an objective sense. The colors don't seem as "rich" (i.e., they're washed out) on the S1 monitor compared to my laptop's own monitor. Again, not sure why but I suspect it's the HDR issue above. But again, really doesn't matter for doing stuff that's not gaming.

As you can see, I don't use the kickstand that comes with it (on the back of the monitor). I'm not sure why it was built into the design it doesn't seem necessary. I was a bit concerned that the hinge may become worn and eventually not support the weight of the monitor but as I write this now it's well over a month since I've had this monitor and still the hinge/support system (without the kickstand) supports its weight perfectly fine without showing any signs of wear on the hinge. So again, not sure what the kickstand is for. I wouldn't worry about the weight of it in other words, it's more a question of whether or not you are expecting a decent refresh rate for gaming and/or if you don't have a USB-C connection. Don't buy this for gaming; don't buy it if you don't have a USB-C port. DO buy it though if you just want a mini-desktop (like me) and don't care too much about the (somewhat slight) color differences. You actually might not have a difference if you don't have a gaming laptop actually.

As a side note, the weight of it is not too heavy to be supported by lighter laptops like an HP Elitebook, which I also have for work. Again, you don't need the kickstand even for a light laptop like that. It holds up fine on its own. I hope you found this review useful.

Edit: A few days after posting this review, the seller reached out to me to try some color adjustments on the monitor, to see if that would work to get the S1 to be the same as my own laptop's monitor. While sadly they didn't work, it did make the difference less noticeable, (not pictured). For this reason (since they reached out to me) I'm revising my review to five stars. It was good customer service I'd say.

reviews.author: Vikas
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content: This portable triple monitor is a very useful product for work and travel. The screens are clear, bright, and easy to use, and the 1080P display looks great for meetings, movies, and daily tasks. I like that it supports different modes and rotates, so I can set it up the way I want. It is plug and play, which saves time and makes setup simple. The detachable design is also convenient. The packaging is great, and the carry on case is great too, making it easy to protect and carry anywhere. Overall, it helps me work faster and better.

reviews.author: David
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.content:
It is an incredible tool for those who need to work with multiple screens. The image quality is very good, with vivid colors and Full HD resolution that offers a sharp and professional visual experience.

It is very easy to install, which makes it super versatile. In addition, its 235° swivel and detachable design allows you to adjust it according to your needs, ideal for work, study or even entertainment.

The structure is firm and portable, perfect to take on trips or use in the office. Without a doubt, an excellent investment if you are looking for comfort and greater efficiency in your day to day.

reviews.author: Neil Larson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content: I'm very happy with my purchase. Setting up this unit was extremely easy and its fit snugly around my laptop. The screens are stable, large, of high quality, and rotate smoothly. The carrying case makes transporting this to and from my office a breeze. No drivers needed.

reviews.author: Knittermam
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content: This product is great. Fairly easy to set up. I had trouble with display and contacted support and they walked me through a little wheel adjustment on this screen that greatly improved the display. I didn’t even see the wheel at first. However, why is the kickstand on the laptop side, not on the monitor side? I find that the weight or perhaps just gravity will occasionally cause the screen to fall off the laptop connection. I have a book propped under this monitor now which by ridiculous. The kickstand on the monitor side would fix this. I cannot find any device to use to prop this up and keep it fully sturdy. An adjustable kickstand on the monitor would totally fix this! Why is the kickstand on the monitor side anyway?

reviews.author: Jyo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.content:
The KEFEYA 15.6" Laptop Screen Extender is a portable triple monitor setup praised for boosting productivity and ease of use. It features plug-and-play USB-C connectivity, Full HD 1080P IPS displays, and a flexible, detachable design. It simplifies multitasking on the go.
Overall, it's a solid investment for mobile workers seeking an efficient, expanded workspace.

reviews.author: Ana Kyle Spinelli
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2026
reviews.content: Really like this screen. I added this one for work and now want to replace my other one with this one. Nice picture, good size and easy to setup. I use it for work and not gaming but for designing it has nice colors. Would recommend

reviews.author: RALPH MANN
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 13, 2026
reviews.content: Good quality. Very happy.

reviews.author: Michael Evans
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 29, 2024
reviews.content: Purchased for wfh setup - display is very nice! Only con that I would list is that there are only 2 screw holes in the back, so if you plan on mounting them to an arm, make sure that the arm can accommodate this.

reviews.author: Whitney
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 28, 2026
reviews.content: No issues!

reviews.author: Dan K
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.content: A good work from home monitor. Size could be a bit larger for my needs but it gets the job done. Overall, I would recommend this monitor. No issues with connectivity. The picture/color accuracy are right for the price.

reviews.author: Bo Gibbs
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026
reviews.content: Very impressed

reviews.author: Dave
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 25, 2024
reviews.content:
The monitors are actually great and I ended up grabbing two when they were on sale for $69.

I was slightly triggered that there were only 2 holes instead of 4 for the VESA plate but if you stack a bunch of washers it will get tight enough. It get's the job done despite being a little shady. My monitor don't sag and my wife likes it so that's a win in my book.

reviews.author: Darien
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 13, 2024
reviews.content:
I love this monitor. I needed a second one for my wfh days. I got a 27in Acer monitor around Christmas from my stepdad, so I figured this one would work just as well and it does. The color and picture are great.

I got this one on prime day and I’m glad I did. It was pretty much the only good sale I saw.

I do wish you could move these monitors up or down so they are both the same height. To get over that I put some small books under the monitor.

reviews.author: J&J
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.format: Size: Monitor with One Parent Unit
reviews.content: Finally an old fashioned baby monitor. Sound is good and doesn't get too loud. There are 5 sound levels. Every time you turn it on, it has a robot sounding voice saying they care about your infant and a reminder to keep the monitors at least 3 feet apart. That's the only con. It's annoying to us. It works for our house. Very easy to just plug in and use. One way sound in baby's room goes to parents room (or living room) with volume control . Both have a power button. Only used it one night so far, but we are happy with it right now.

reviews.author: Night Owl Photography
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026
reviews.format: Size: Monitor with One Parent Unit
reviews.content: Recently divorced and so I wanted a way to monitor what's going on around the outside of my house at night. I bought 3 of these. When you first turn them on they sync with each other and don't overlap. So I have them in separate places and they do not pick up each others' signals. The one is pretty far away from the receiver and has to go through a lot of walls and really does just fine. I'm thinking of getting a few more because the price is very fair for what it does.

reviews.author: Solidmeelieboo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.format: Size: Monitor with One Parent Unit
reviews.content: I bought this baby monitor in advance for my grandson-to-be and so far am pleased with it. The set up was simple and straightforward,and the sound quality is clear witg no annoying static.I like that it has a decent range and pick up sound well ,even from another room. The unit feel well made and easy to use,which is important for new parents who dont want anything complicated .I also love the adjustable volume and the compact design . Overall it seems like a realiable and practical baby monitor ,feel confident gifting it to my family. I would recommend it to anyone looking for a basic audio that does the job well .

reviews.author: Clancy Thost
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 5, 2025
reviews.content:
Works fine. We bought this as an experiment instead of buying the roar monitors for 3x the cost. This works well enough for what we use it for. The range is decent. About 200ft through the walls of the house. Not sure the direct line of sight range. I can go anywhere in the house and most of the property. If I have the sound machine going, I can hear when it starts to cuts out. It will beep and flash when out of range as well. We use it mostly for getting chores done around the house during nap time or napping in the stroller. Often, the little one will fall asleep in the stroller on my runs. When I get home, I can leave him in the stroller in the front yard and make some food or get a few things done. We also use it when we first put him down at night and want to hang out in a different part of the house. The idea is also to be able to take it camping with us. I haven't done a full battery test so no idea how long the battery actually lasts for.

Some stupid design issues. First off, the chargers for the two different units are different and not interchangeable. This is pretty annoying and just more stuff to keep track of. It would be great if I could plug one in near his bed and one on the counter and charge either unit from either charger. The on/off switch on the baby unit also has a third sound only setting so it's east to think you turned it off but the batter is still draining. Also, there is not light or anything else to indicate that the unit is on.

Overall, iit works fine for

reviews.author: Monica Paul
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.format: Size: Monitor with One Parent Unit
reviews.content: We love it! It gets loud but I try not to keep the volume all the way up because it can pick up the sound machine in the kiddos room.

reviews.author: Casey
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 18, 2025
reviews.format: Size: Monitor with One Parent Unit
reviews.content:
We take care of our granddaughter at least two days a week, and this upgraded VTech audio baby monitor has become an essential tool for making those days easier. Initially, we chose an audio-only model intentionally—wanting to resist the temptation of continuously checking a video screen—and this VTech model has absolutely exceeded our expectations.

First and foremost, the sound quality is exceptional. We can clearly hear every little coo, giggle, or fuss from our granddaughter's nursery without any annoying static or interference. The clarity truly is impressive; it's reminiscent of traditional baby monitors but vastly improved with modern technology. There's never that distracting hiss or hum that you sometimes get with older models. Instead, it's quiet when our granddaughter sleeps and crystal clear when she wakes up or needs us.

One feature we've especially appreciated is the rechargeable parent unit. We're not tethered to an outlet; we simply carry it around wherever we are—doing chores, cooking in the kitchen, or even stepping outside briefly—and we've never had an issue with the battery running out during the day. At night or during longer stretches, we just plug it in by the bedside and it's ready again in the morning.

The monitor's impressive range is another huge plus. We haven't tested the extreme limits, but it easily reaches anywhere inside our house, into the yard, and even out to the mailbox or garage without losing the connection. Setup was incredibly easy: we just plugged the nursery unit in, turned on both units, and they instantly connected.

We also appreciate the visual sound indicator lights on the parent unit. Sometimes, if we're watching TV or busy with tasks, we keep the monitor volume on low. The lights visually alert us to any sounds coming from our granddaughter’s room, ensuring we never miss a moment when she needs attention.

After several months of use, it has consistently delivered. If you’re grandparents like us who frequently care for your grandchildren, having a dependable, portable, and clear baby monitor like this one from VTech makes life significantly easier. It truly provides invaluable peace of mind. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: saundra
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.format: Size: Monitor with One Parent Unit
