reviews.content: Cheap audio monitor that I purchased for my 13 month old. Works well! Large range that extends to be able to hear my baby while I am outside or in the garage.

reviews.author: Regina Tatro
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 14, 2025
reviews.format: Size: Monitor with One Parent Unit
reviews.content: Well this product is super easy to use. Just plug in and go and the parents part has batteries for easy moving around, however the baby one for power outages no way to use batteries. Also even when turned all the way up and having it close by the sound is very very quiet cant have music or tv on or any other noises in background (and I have good hearing) got it so when hubby is the one listening I can hear her without the monitor, but the other kids can not hear her well. Very soft so will be using it as a back for when my sound one goes out instead of all the time. Sadly ill need to get a different one. Got it cause no wifi needed which is our preferred method. It is hard to find those these days! ...

reviews.author: Albert
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 30, 2025
reviews.content: works perfectly for macOS

reviews.author: AmazonCustomer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2026
reviews.content:
I got this to replace another samsung monitor, which worked fine, but was just too small. OOB, the resolution has an inch of black on each side of the image. After a lot of work, I figured out that I just won't be able to change the resolution directly. The drop-down for that is grayed out. All I can do is select various refresh rates and see what the resulting resolutions happen to be. But that isn't even consistent--for some reason I am now stuck with 1260x1024, which isn't even the right shape, even when using the same refresh rate that gave me a reasonable resolution five minutes ago.

This is horrible and unusable--I'm going to have to return it unless it fixes itself magically, but I prefer not to rely on magic. I definitely suggest buying something else.

P.S. The very limited instructions that were included for setup are terrible--Ikea can write assembly instructions without words and still be clear, but Samsung apparently cannot.

reviews.author: D. J. Brogan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 14, 2025
reviews.content:
I got the version that has DisplayPort and HDMI. It's a great-looking, sharptastic monitor, but something serious is a bit off:

1. When I registered the product on Panasonic's website, it came up as not having any warranty or support, even though clearly the serial number was accepted added to my account. And trying to find firmware updates (for the issues #2 below) brought up nothing. Both confirmed with Panasonic Support (which I had to contact outside of the product support page).

2. I was experience sleep-related issues with one of my computers, where before another monitor worked fine:

(2a) Via DisplayPort, the laptop would wake up the video output and believe that it's connected via DisplayPort, and send windows to the monitor accordingly -- but the monitor would stay asleep, and even if manually woken up by power button, would timeout again and go back to sleep. Not every time, but within ~5 sleep cycles (and thereafter since) unless/until the monitor is turned off and back on, which is a hassle to do 3 times a workday.

(2b) Via HDMI, while the laptop is sleeping the video output, the monitor would "wake up" every few minutes to then say that it has no HDMI video and return back to sleep. Again, and again, and again...

Like I said earlier, I tried but couldn't find firmware (neither upgrade not even *current* firmware) from Panasonic's website. Switching laptop docks didn't change anything. Changing every Windows setting under the sun didn't change anything. Even tried a DisplayPort-to-HDMI cable, as well as going through a KVM switch.

I finally decided to give up on this monitor, just 1 day past the return window closed. Panasonic Support, for their part, say they're willing to replace under warranty, except I don't want the same model.

reviews.author: cs
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 15, 2024
reviews.content: This is a nice monitor for what I use it for, no gaming, just ordinary tasks, email, Word, Excel. Resolution is good. There are 3 USB ports on the back, not the handiest to get to. Would like a couple more USB's.

reviews.author: Nathan T.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 25, 2024
reviews.content: Monitor Vignetting. I did not know this was a thing until this monitor. Around the edges roughly 2 inches from the side bezels it gets very dark. To a point where it is not usable. I returned the same day i recieved it. And also for a 1440 P monitor it didn't look that great compared to other 1080 P monitors i have. Save the money go with a 1080P monitor.

reviews.author: Richard S
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content: Very nice monitor, clear colors, fast responses. Very useful that it rotate s

reviews.author: Monica
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 16, 2025
reviews.content: I’ve been using this monitor for about a year now, and I must say, I’m impressed. The quality is amazing, and I’ve had absolutely no issues with how things are displayed. I use it as my vertical monitor, and the connectivity is simple and easy to manage. Amazing!

reviews.author: Katie K
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 25, 2025
reviews.content: Unfortunately, this monitor broke in less than a year. I purchased it in November 2024 and it broke October 2025. There are lines all over the display and I can’t see the display. This product was great while it lasted. However, I would expect it to last longer than a year, never mind less than one year.

reviews.author: Brandi Yatchmenoff
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 5, 2026
reviews.content: Very cool, perfect size, color is amazing, sounds awesome, qaulity is on parr, gaming performance is on point and just cool.

reviews.author: Aidan C.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 15, 2025
reviews.content:
It’s a budget monitor that functions as intended. It’s a great secondary monitor that offers good picture and color quality as well as good refresh rates and response times for the price range.

The good:
The color is consistent with my more expensive monitor. The refresh rate is good for the price. The borders are small and great for using with other monitors. The settings are easy to change and navigate.

The bad:
The stand sucks. It mounts to the monitor in a not great way that allows it to fall out easily. It only rotates in one direction, and at least for me it will not connect to the stand fully when rotated, so it’s kind of just hanging there. More ports would be nice, but hey, it’s a budget monitor.

reviews.author: Shelliro
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 3, 2025
reviews.content:
Updated: The problem has been resolved. The seller sent me a new power cord. Now the monitor is working again. Thank you for the great customer service.

Original: I purchased this monitor less than a year ago, and I woke up to a black screen 3 days ago. I have contacted the company, sent them a copy of my original order showing my order date, and a video of the monitor. As of today they are not offering any type of replacement for the monitor. Their response was for me to purchase a replacement power cord to see if that helped. I let them know that I do not think I should have to do that on a monitor that is less than 1 year old. I will update this review if they end up replacing the monitor as that would be the only resolution that I am happy with.

reviews.author: Cassandra
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 19, 2024
reviews.content: This is and awesome monitor. The quality of it is great for the price. I needed and extra monitor for work, and have limited space. The ability to rotate screen is a great feature.

reviews.author: Kenneth W.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 2, 2025
reviews.content: Pleasantly surprised with this monitor. Wanted a vertical monitor to use with my other monitors since my desk does not have enough space for 2 horizontal monitors. This one is very bright with vivid colors, actually a lot more color accurate than my Dell! Still working long term as well! Highly Recommend.

reviews.author: Pete Craig
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 11, 2021
reviews.content:
This monitor is awesome! Very clear high contrast picture. I have Windows 10, I plugged in the monitor and it automatically went to 1920x1080 resolution at 60Hz. 75Hz at 1920x1080 resolution is available in the display settings and worked for me.

It comes with HDMI and VGA cables. Since my docking station has mini DisplayPort I bought a separate Mini DisplayPort to DisplayPort cable.

Unlike other reviewers, I don't notice any backlight bleed, even in a dark room with black background. My normal desktop background is very dark, and the first thing I noticed when plugging in this monitor is how much darker the black is than my previous monitor.

I tested various HD videos and I am impressed with the picture quality.

reviews.author: Mook Hayes
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 12, 2025
reviews.content:
4 3/4 stars, just wish it were taller. I finally had to get a display to go with my Macbook Pro and I had ordered another brand that would not work right so that one was returned and I bought this one. I love it! Super light, super easy to assemble, and has a great resolution. I mostly do research and a little bit of data at work so this is perfect for me.

The only thing is that I wish it were taller. I had to put it on three reams of paper so that I can see it above my laptop screen. Amazing for the price, and so relieved since I really didn't want to spend a lot of money.

reviews.author: Kelly
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 7, 2017
reviews.content:
This monitor provides a crystal-clear image, once you figure out how to use it. There are six buttons along the bottom-right-front. From left to right, they are: Image selection mode - button 1, 2, 3, 4 - power. At first I made the mistake of using the image selection mode to switch to a pre-programmed "gaming" setting. This was a big mistake, as the Windows taskbar and window borders became blurry and pixelated, and text was also blurry. After hours of searching, driver updates, and tech support, we couldn't figure out what was wrong. But as luck would have it, I finally figured it out!

Instead of pressing the left-most button, press any of the buttons 1-4. This will bring up more detailed menus and settings. Go to Main Menu, then View Mode, and select either Standard or Web. You can then customize the brightness and contrast to suit your needs. Both of these settings provide a much clearer image than the "Game" modes. I maintain the standard setting (with customized brightness) even while gaming, and the picture is beautiful.

The monitor seems very bright to me on a high brightness setting, but that's likely because I keep the lights off in my office. I've turned the brightness on the monitor down quite a bit, and it works well for me. If you work in a brightly-lit office, you will be able to turn up the brightness so the screen is still readily visible. However in a darkened office, the brightness definitely needs to be turned down, or otherwise it will hurt your eyes.

The monitor itself is very light weight, and very thin for most of its height. The bottom two inches or so are a bit thicker, to include the electronics, ports, etc. But even this thicker part is still thin compared to most other monitors (even smaller ones). The screen can be tilted back and forth for a better viewing angle, so you can place it directly on the desk or on a stand, whatever works best for you.

Overall I'm very happy with this monitor. I purchased a second one for my husband, and he likes it a lot as well.

reviews.author: Abhik Roy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2024
reviews.content:
I'm using this with a Mac Mini.
Good price. Does what it needs to do. Nothing fancy.
The stand is not that great and takes a little work before fitting it in, thus the 4 stars.
The monitor itself is beautiful and works perfectly with my Mac.

reviews.author: william10
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 29, 2024
reviews.content: To be perfectly honest, I was shocked at the price. I have one in my office same size although curved screen that is the reason I was happy to use the same company. I couldn’t be more happy. Perfect for my home office.

reviews.author: Glenn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 29, 2024
reviews.content: This monitor exceeded my expectations for general purpose usage. Even has a VGA port which was surprising. Came with all the cables including HDMI. Color and clarity is pretty good. Not sure how continued hour after hour use will be but for the times I’ve used it…about 45 minutes each time I did NOT experience eye fatigue.

reviews.author: Dane Shawson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 29, 2017
reviews.content: I'm not super into gaming so don't take my advice based off of a gamer's perspective. This monitor is amazing, honestly. 60 Hz refresh rate is plenty for this cat, plus the ultra thin design is very chic. It doesn't bleed light in the corners very much if even at all, only time you would notice is maybe at night time. Well worth the money and I would recommend it to anyone.

reviews.author: Ryan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 10, 2024
reviews.content: Use these on every desk at work. Great monitor for the $

reviews.author: Christopher
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 15, 2025
reviews.content:
The construction and included cover are great. This is probably not for gaming but for my use it's perfect. I'm using this as a small TV for my dining room table. Plugged in a Chromecast using an HDMI adapter. Picture is clear and sharp and brightness is good for my well lit room with abundant sunlight from windows. Not the best contrast but it's fine for watching TV. I recommend this for anyone using as a second monitor for productivity or just watching videos.
It included an HDMI cable to go from the mini to full size, you'll need an adapter to plug a Chromecast or other device with a built in HDMI. Also came with a really short USB-C power cord, I used my own and it works fine.

reviews.author: Rich
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 18, 2024
reviews.content:
Purchased this monitor for my wife. She works in finance and when at off-site meetings with other associates, needs more screen area than a 16" laptop provides. This helps her immensely.

Cabling up this monitor is also nice due to the multiple options available. She is also able to power the monitor through her laptop, so she need not worry about also packing the power adapter.

Quality of the image is also very good. Color hue is a bit different from her laptop, but this can be expected, and is not a problem. Sharpness and resolution is also very good.

I have used other Viewsonic monitors in the past. Their quality and reliability have always been very good.

reviews.author: K Lehn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 27, 2025
reviews.content:
The monitor has it's own case, and is easy to move from the laptop bag to the table. It's great to have the battery and you can charge the monitor using your laptop which is a plus.

I would say that I was a little disappointed with the lag between your laptop communicating with the portable monitor, but this only happens if you aren't plugged in using the type c connector. It does come with it's own power brick, but I didn't see a reason for it for my case use, which I just use it at work when I need to move to another workstation.

It's a good construction, easy to use for the for the cost, it works as advertised.

reviews.author: Gil
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 8, 2024
reviews.content:
The VG1656N Portable Monitor is a beautiful product that allows you to connect wirelessly or via a USB-C cable. Both options require that your laptop or other source device have a free USB-C port. In the case of a wireless connection, the wireless dongle must be plugged into the USB-C port on your source device. In the case of a cable connection, the USB-C cable must be plugged into the USB-C port on your source device as well. I could not use a USB-C Hub. Since I only have 1 USB-C port on my laptop, and I have a couple of USB-C devices, I had to return the monitor because it would not work when connected to the hub. I used a Belkin Connect USB-C to 4-Port USB-C Hub. IF you have a free USB-C port on your device that can be dedicated to the monitor, then I would highly recommend this monitor.

If anyone knows of a USB-C Hub that will support this product, please post a response to the review. I would buy the new hub and repurchase this monitor.

reviews.author: Chris Lukas
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 17, 2025
reviews.content:
This monitor pairs VERY nicely with small/compact laptops. It can connect via USB-C or HDMI and comes with its own power brick. I use this with my last-gen 11" MacBook Air when I have down-time and I want to watch YouTube/Netflix. The image quality and brightness is far better than what I was expecting. My personal loadout: this monitor and its cables, one compact surge protector (to which I connect this monitor's power supply and my laptop's charger) my laptop, and its charger. This monitor, it's cables, and my compact surge protector fit very comfortably into my laptop bag.

If you travel with a compact laptop, this monitor should be at the top of your list of accessories.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026
reviews.content: Worked great then stopped working. Amazon said to reach out to seller. Told nothing can be done by the seller, except to reach out to the manufacturer, a 3rd contact, outside of amazon and the seller, I think. Not sure how to do so.

reviews.author: Jen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 22, 2024
reviews.content: I trust this brand and wasn’t disappointed in this portable screen. It’s really lightweight and thin. So easy to use while laid up after a surgery. It would also be easy to travel with. I tested it with a Logitech laptop and my work MacBook - seamless for both. Picture looks good. Only complaint is the the cord for the power supply is laughably short and not usable for my purposes without an extension.

reviews.author: Gopher
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2021
reviews.content:
Working from home, and wanting to be able to work in more than one place at home, has caused me to contend with some unique challenges. It's hard to get a lot of what I need to do done with just one screen, but unless I'm at my desk it's a bit tricky to have multiple displays.

So I finally decided it was time to get one of these displays and there were a few reasons I was drawn to the ViewSonic.

One, I'm a fan of their products. ViewSonic has been around ages and I've used their monitors off and on throughout the years, so I expected something with fairly good build and image quality. On these points, the display did not disappoint.

The kickstand is thoughtful, allowing the monitor to be used horizontally or vertically. The overall build of the monitor feels solid, and the slim bezels are nice. Fortunately, as is standard for these, the thicker bezel is at the bottom which puts the display at a similar height to my laptop's screen. Additionally, one of the things that I preferred about this about a number of competing products is the subdued brand labeling on the front. Bright, shiny, or chrome logos are just obnoxious in my opinion (coincidentally, the ViewSonic monitors I have at my WFH station have a dark matte gray logo on a black bezel *chefs kiss*).

The controls are straightforward and the interface is not too dissimilar from my other ViewSonic displays. Along with the sturdy cover, this is easy to pack and protect. I wouldn't subject it to any abuse as it is still just a fairly exposed screen, even with the cover on it. The cover seems to have traded a softer or friendlier feel for being sturdier, however it does also serve nicely as a mat to place under the monitor when in use to keep it in place and prevent the housing from scuffing up whatever table you're using it on.

The scuff or scratch risk is worth calling out here. The metal kickstand feels like it'd pretty easily gouge a table or other surface, so using the cover as a base is recommended if you want to ensure that doesn't happen.

You are paying a premium for the ViewSonic brand, but you also get a 3 year warranty and hopefully the reliability I'd usually expect from ViewSonic.

The display includes everything you need: a collection of USB cables, microfiber cloth, and a power adapter that conveniently allows it to provide up to 60W of power to whatever device you connect to it.

Picture quality is perfectly fine for the application. It matches a decent IPS laptop screen but can't keep up with the best in terms of color and doesn't offer 4K (which I've grown to find unnecessary on displays this size anyway). I wouldn't have expected anything more from it given the price, so there's no disappointment there.

reviews.author: T.KirkWesson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 16, 2025
reviews.content:
Using this 32" curved monitor at work and it's been fantastic! The curved design is perfect for productivity - makes it easy to have multiple windows open and reduces neck strain during long work days. The 1800R curve feels natural and immersive.
The FHD 1080P resolution is crisp for documents, spreadsheets, and presentations. The VA screen provides excellent color accuracy which is great for design work. Both HDMI and DP connections make it easy to connect to work laptops.
The 32" size gives me plenty of screen real estate without taking up too much desk space. Much better than working on a small laptop screen! The refresh rate makes everything feel smooth and responsive.
Great value for a professional-quality monitor that works perfectly in an office environment. Has really improved my work efficiency and comfort!
Verified Purchase ✓

reviews.author: OROZŪK
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 12, 2025
reviews.content:
I landed on this monitor because of its combination of being 4K, operable on 12 VDC, its weight, its size and its price. I expected to check a few of those boxes but not all of them. This monitor is great! I run it as a second monitor at the desktop and take it with me for wilderness applications. It's part of a mobile setup featuring a Mac Mini M4, Starlink and stored 12 VDC , solar power.

The image is sharp and clear and integrates nicely to meet my desktop and off-grid requirements.

reviews.author: Leslie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content: Great monitor for 1080p gaming! Super easy to set up, looks great on a gaming setup. Made all of my games look better

reviews.author: JB
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 16, 2025
reviews.content:
Wow, this is a decent monitor so far! It's better than decent even. I love that it has dual HDMI and Dual display port connections! I also love how clean the front of the monitor looks in terms of buttons and bezel, of which there are none of the former, and a slight bezel frame.

The buttons of the monitor are on the back right-hand side if you are facing the display. They are easily reached and easy to press. The overall aesthetic is sleek and modern.

The display's quality is excellent! There is zero ghosting, and the semi-matte finish reduces glare to virtually non-existant. The resolution, color depth & reproduction, sharpness, and contrast ratio are all absolutely excellent. It supports HDR on my Windows 11 Pro machine and looks absolutely stunning when compared to my old Dell flat panel monitor that I bought back in 2009. This monitor is like going from an old brick-style Nokia cellphone to an iPhone 17 or the newest Samsung.

All I can really say is that this monitor looks much better than the price-point might suggest. Although I don't do much gaming other than GTA V, I can say that this monitor may not have every little feature available, but for the price, it has all of your basic gaming needs covered.

reviews.author: Theory Georgiou
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 4, 2025
reviews.content:
I thought it was a good deal for a decent monitor.
The up and down buttons for navigating the menu are backwards but not like I am using them all the time so no big deal. the auto detecting of source does not work reliably and must be done manually.

The reason for the 1 star review is that all the buttons but the power button stopped working, and now I can't change the input type thus bricking the monitor for my use case I have to constantly unplug and plug in cables to try and get it to detect and work.

Also of note, the stand is metal and the tips are very sharp so be careful not to scratch what surface you place it on when placing it.

Update: just noticed a dead pixel as well.

reviews.author: erika
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 20, 2025
reviews.content: Not bad for a basic 4k gaming monitor graphics look ok , depending on the game you might have to turn off hdr but other than that it’s a cheap basic monitor 8/10 .. the volume doesn’t work so you might have to get some speakers

reviews.author: JD
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.content:
Absolutely a winner! I purchased two of these 27" monitors and use them in a dual-monitor setup at 3840×2160 with 150% scaling. The image quality is crisp, clear, and bright—exactly what I was hoping for.

One important note: there are no built-in speakers. The monitor provides an audio-out jack, which works perfectly for connecting headphones or external speakers. Audio is carried through the DisplayPort cable from my desktop to the monitor, and the sound quality through my headphones is excellent.

Overall, these monitors are a fantastic value for the money. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Anonymous
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 20, 2026
reviews.content: Such a good monitor and especially for the price. I use it for school and light gaming. Great image quality and great performance. 10/10 highly recommend.

reviews.author: Katie B
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 3, 2026
reviews.content:
The JBL Tune 510BT headphones are fantastic for the price. The sound is clear, balanced, and punchy, with deep bass that really makes music enjoyable. Bluetooth pairing is quick and reliable, and the 40-hour battery life means I can go days without charging. The built-in microphone works well for calls too.
The only minor downside is that after long listening sessions, they can leave a slight dent on your head. It’s not painful—just something to be aware of. Overall, they’re comfortable, high-quality, and a great value for anyone looking for wireless headphones.

reviews.author: Landyn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026
reviews.content: love these. the headphones have a amazing battery life, they are worth a little less than $50. the sound quality is amazing, it’s very lightweight, the bluetooth is amazing and it’s very easy to use and immediately connect to my phone as soon as they turn on

reviews.author: Pamela
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026
reviews.content: These are great Bluetooth headphones. I use them at my gym, and they pick up the signal from my phone in its locker at the far end of the gym. Excellent sound quality and they do a good job of blocking outside noise. I'm not technically savvy, but I found them easy to sync with my phone. The battery lasts a long time. They're a very good value.

reviews.author: Brandie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 4, 2026
reviews.content:
Pros: The sound quality is great, they block out a decent amount of outside sound, the battery life surpasses my expectations and needs, they're pretty comfortable over all.

Cons: They started out too tight then within a couple of months became too loose. Then after less than 6 months of use, they broke. The sound still worked, the battery life was still amazing but the left headphone broke and was just dangling there. They didn't hold up as long as I had hoped. I do use headphones daily but I'm not rough with them and I was the only user so it was disappointing for them to break so quickly.

Overall I would still recommend these and use these. I especially recommend these to people who use headphones daily as the battery life is incredible! In fact I replaced them with a similar JBL model because I love them so much. I would just caution you to be gentle with them.

reviews.author: John B
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
reviews.content: I purchased these instead of less expensive headphones because I like JBL products. First timing using these and just had to write a review. EXCELLENT quality sound and comfort. I'm hearing some of my tunes better than ever!

reviews.author: Brittany McCue
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 31, 2026
reviews.content:
I bought the JBL Tune 510BT headphones as an affordable, everyday pair for music, videos, and calls, and they’ve been a really solid purchase.

Setup was super easy—paired quickly with my phone and reconnects fast after that. The sound is what you expect from JBL: punchy bass, clear enough vocals, and plenty of volume. I’m not an audiophile, but for the price these sound way better than I expected.

They’re also lightweight and comfortable for longer wear. The on-ear style doesn’t feel heavy on my head, and the buttons are simple to use for skipping tracks and adjusting volume. Battery life has been excellent too—I’m not constantly charging them, which is a huge plus.

Only minor downside: because they’re on-ear, if you’re very sensitive to pressure you might need a quick break after a long session. But overall, they’re durable, convenient, and a great value.

If you want dependable Bluetooth headphones with good sound and battery life without spending a lot, the JBL Tune 510BT is an easy recommendation.

reviews.author: Jen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content: Wow, these really give you the most bang for your buck!! The sound quality is great, and they block out almost too much outside noise. My daughter got these for Christmas and hasn’t put them down since. I almost wish I hadn’t bought them — she loves them that much! Haha.

reviews.author: Asiyah
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2026
reviews.content:
The JBL Tune 510BT Bluetooth Headphones are exactly the kind of wireless audio you actually use — comfortable, reliable, and sounding way better than the price suggests.

Sound Quality: These deliver that classic JBL audio vibe — punchy bass, clear mids, and crisp highs. Music sounds fun and lively, not flat or tinny like some budget Bluetooth cans. Whether I’m jamming playlists, podcasts, or calls, they stay detailed and smooth.

Bluetooth Performance: Pairing is quick and painless. Once connected, they stay locked in without constant dropouts or that annoying “reconnect already” issue. Great for commuting, walking, or chilling at the house.

Comfort: Lightweight with cushy ear pads that don’t turn your ears into a pressure cooker. I can wear them for hours without the “why is my head tight” feeling. The adjustable headband actually fits my giant human head and doesn’t pinch.

Battery Life: Long enough to forget you charged them. They last through multiple days of use without begging for a plug, which is always delightful.

Build & Controls: Simple buttons that actually work without mystery combos or accidental presses. They feel sturdy, not like they’re going to break if you look at them wrong.

Overall this is a well-rounded, user-friendly pair of headphones that hits all the essential notes: good sound, comfy fit, reliable Bluetooth, and long battery life. Five stars because they make listening easy and enjoyable.

reviews.author: Josephine Carrillo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 25, 2026
reviews.content:
Firstly, the color is soon cute, and exactly as pictured. I am so in love with the aesthetic of them. They also came fully charged, which was awesome so I got to use them immediately.

Secondly, the quality is super good so far. Whether listening to podcasts, rap, or metal music, the sound quality has been perfect. They are compatible with both my switch 2 and my phone which is to be expected, but still happy there were no surprises or difficulties there.

They aren't exactly noise canceling though. When they're on with no sound playing, other sounds are just dulled ever so slightly. When sound is playing, you can still hear people talking and other sounds like fans, but outside sounds aren't obtrusive and are blocked fairly well.

I also really like how big the ear holes in the pads are, I wasn't expecting that. My whole ear fits inside them, which makes them super comfortable since they aren't smooshing the tops of my ears into the arms of my glasses. It also makes them really comfortable to wear while laying down, again because they don't smoosh my ears.

I haven't had them long enough to really put them through the paces on charging or audio hours, but I will come back to add to this review a little later to comment on that.

So far, so amazingly good! Super pleased with the quality for the price I paid.

reviews.author: Pippa
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.content:
For an inexpensive pair of headphones, I’d say it was a good investment. The sound quality is very clear, although songs like Golden Hour the instruments could sound more crisp.

They do not fully cancel noise, however, but do block out some. They can also reach high volumes without becoming tinny, although I have never used at full blast to avoid bursting an eardrum 😂

They are very comfortable and light weight. I just wish they fit on my neck better because they are very right.

I also love the color- it’s so bright and fun! That is partially why I went with this brand, too. They connect very well to my iPhone, too.

While there are better headphones, I would recommend if you don’t want to spend a bunch of money. They will work and don’t break in a month and you’ll overall have a fairly good sound.

reviews.author: Shyanne
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.content: First use and pretty nice. I have had these on for my full 10 hour shift, and still have plenty of power. Light weight and comfortable. No issues so far. Easy to connect to my kindle. Outside noise is still heard. I work in a warehouse and I can hear my coworkers conversation still. The sound quality is clear and clean.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 7, 2024

reviews.author: Becca
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2026
reviews.content: I love these headphones! They work well even just for keeping out excess noise when I'm out in public but I don't feel like listening to any music. The audio quality is surrealistic that I have been watching a TV show, walked away from my phone and had to stop and take the headphones off to see if they were actually connected or if I was hearing this sound come out of my phone because I could hear in surround sound and wasn't expecting that. The pads are very comfortable and the adjustment hinges are very smooth and don't slide around or readjust on you with wear. I have fallen asleep with them on by accident and did not wake up with sore ears or a sore head. I love the colour and as a person with sensory issues, all of the parts were smooth to the touch and did not bother me. I also sincerely appreciate having the inner fabric marked as left and right because it saves me from having to look for a tiny marking on the outside like most headphones. I accidentally ordered two pairs and had to send one back but I am really impressed with the quality for the price. As a person who predominantly uses earbuds, they are a little bulky to fit in my purse, but they still fold up really nicely. I kind of wish I could buy them in every colour.

reviews.author: W. Arce
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 27, 2026
reviews.content: The BERIBES Bluetooth Headphones are a standout choice for anyone seeking high-quality audio without the premium price tag. The most impressive feature is undoubtedly the massive 65-hour playtime, which allows for over a week of casual listening on a single charge—perfect for long work weeks or travel. Sound enthusiasts will appreciate the 6 EQ modes (ranging from Rock and Pop to Vocal and Deep Bass), which let you customize the profile to match your genre of choice at the touch of a button. Despite their durable build, they are incredibly lightweight and feature soft memory-protein ear cushions, making them comfortable for all-day wear in a home office or during a commute. With a reliable Bluetooth 5.3 connection and a clear built-in microphone for calls, these headphones offer a level of versatility and performance that truly punches above their weight class. Highly recommended!!!

reviews.author: Meredith Miller
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.content:
5.0 out of 5 stars – Incredible Value! Almost gives my Beats a run for their money!

I can't gate keep any longer! I am absolutely blown away by these BERIBES headphones! I’ll be honest: I used a pair of Beats Pro for two years, so my standards for audio are pretty high. While these aren't 100% identical to the $200+ flagship brands, for this price point, they are definitely in the race!

Here is why I love them:

Insane Battery Life: The 65-hour playtime is no joke. I feel like I rarely have to charge them, which is a huge upgrade from my previous sets. When I do charge them, they charge fairly quickly (about 1 hour to full charge).

Custom Sound: Having 6 EQ modes is a game-changer. Whether I’m listening to a podcast or bass-heavy music, I can actually tune the headphones to match the vibe from Rock, Vocal, Jazz, Bass, Classic, or Pop. As a graduate student, I listen to a lot of binaural sounds and music as I study. These headphones can handle to different frequencies in each ear that binaural requires. They also cancel out pretty much all background noise, even though they do not advertise that

Comfort: They are super lightweight and comfortable for long sessions. The deep cup fits over my ear so well, there is never fabric touching my actual skin. My ears do not get irritated or sweaty during long-term use, and they are comfortable enough for falling asleep while wearing! The also swivel, and fold up for travel. They actually fit in my old Beats case!

Connectivity and ease of set up: Honestly, this is where they blow Beats out of the water. The initial pair was an instant piece of cake. They connect by bluetooth and I never have problems with them being dropped from my phone. If I need to switch between devices for some reason, they never give me a hard time.

The Price Point: You honestly cannot beat the quality you get for what you pay. If you want high-end sound and superior functionality without the "luxury" price tag, these are the ones to get. I had someone tell me that on a call, they sound better than my Beats and picked up less background sound!

If you’re looking for a reliable, great-sounding pair of headphones and don't want to break the bank, look no further. Highly highly HIGHLY recommended!

reviews.author: Lee T.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 26, 2025
reviews.content:
Beribes WH202a headphones.
1 star docked because the 3.5mm audio cable had bent tips. On both ends. But to be honest, I tossed the cable. 3.5mm audio on a lot of phones has an automatic hearing protection function that lowers the volume when you plug a set of these in. I don't want to deal with that. The cable was a good length though, and the coating around the jacks was not too thick, and fit well into all 3 of my cased devices.
Battery is really good. I'm more of an earBUDS kind of guy. So I'm not wearing/using these all day long. But lasts all week before needing a recharge.
Weight- very light.
Comfort- The light weight, and very soft padded areas make these earphones quite comfortable to wear. Being somebody with long hair, they aren't getting tangled in my hair as I take them off or put them on.
Construction- here is where I docked another star. The joints at the ear pieces seems a weak spot. And other reviews show broken joints. The consensus seems to be that they break around a year after purchase. But, at 20 dollars, I can see where some of the costs had to be reduced to get them into this price range.
Noise reduction- Minimal. At this price point, you are not buying ANC. So the only sound reduction is the padding covering your ears. Though, if you are buying these for your kids, that's a good thing, so you don't have to yell at them, as loud.
Connectivity- It takes them a second or two to connect. This, after holding the button down for a couple of seconds. There is a generic vocal notification that they've been powered on, that they connect, and when they power off.
Controls- I don't like the button. My thumb hits it when I'm putting on or taking off the headset. The buttons are good size, and easy to use. BUT- The way these volume controls are set up is just Bad. Press and Hold to raise/lower the volume is a terrible idea. Once that kicks in, your volume either skyrockets, or plummets. Leaving you using the volume controls on your phone, tablet, or whatever device primarily.

Sound- Now we get to why you're buying these. How do they sound? Short answer: pretty good, as long as you are not comparing them to other devices. I have 6 pairs of ear buds, and 2 other pairs of headphones. I really could compare.
On their own, these are pretty decent budget headphones. They will satisfy the needs of the young listener, and the general listener. As my son put it "they don't make my music sound like..." (I don't think Amazon would let me finish that quote, LOL.
These come with a pretty well tuned flat base soundstage. Flat is a good thing here, more later. The base soundstage will appeal to the average listener, who is not a bass head, or audiophile.
For those that do want more, the way this soundstage is set up, it offers a wide range of tuning through an equalizer. The built in equalizer presets are garbage, don't use them. And you have to power off the set, to reset the built in presets. Annoying.
Basically they start out good, and are made so that you can tune them to your own liking.
For their price point, they do a really good job of keeping the highs, mids, and low ranges in their own lane. The separation of the sounds was really good, until I started using some of my serious bass heavy presets. (I only have 2 sets that do that presets well, so don't hold it against these.)
Bass- these are not bass headsets. They can deliver, but not hard. More of a heartbeat kind of thump. And hardly any reverb.
For the average person who wants "work free" listening, just put them on, and enjoy, these are wonderful. And to those, I recommend. Aside from the weak joints.
To those that really want the most they can get, yeah, not so much.

reviews.author: Charles J.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2026
reviews.content: This brand was like 90% off marked down from $290 to like $30. So I figured I'd give them a shot and so far they are very nice.

reviews.author: Nancy Snider
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2026
reviews.content: Good so far. Ordered these because the earbuds hurt my ears after a while.

reviews.author: Hamady elhabib
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content: Very nice quality

reviews.author: MH
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 25, 2026
reviews.content:
ANC works on this one. I had previously bought pairs that were advertised ANC but did NOTHING <:(

I would recommend this for people to buy.

reviews.author: Naomi Mazek
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026
reviews.content: Worth the price for $33 and some notes id like to put is that the noise cancellation is really good, their easy to connect woth the bluetooth 6.0 ofc, and the battery life is really good but the sound quality is not that, unless you have software like Boom 3d or other simple ones the aound qualities terrible, it sounds like someone’s blowing a blow dryer to your ear and then the audio from your tabs just playing in the background. My take away is that its alright for 32 but DEFINITELY not that $300 original pricing quality it says it has!

reviews.author: goodtimesroll
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content:
These are thoroughly mediocre budget Chinese bluetooth headphones. You will get your $29 worth of very poor non-critical listening quality and both immediate and lasting discomfort from wearing them.

You will not feel in any way lucky to have found them. You will ask yourself if you would have been more satisfied spending $30 on lunch (and the answer will be yes).

The notion that they are offered at a 90% discount under a true MSRP value of $299 would be laughable if it was not so deceitful, and so fraudulent (shame on you, Amazon, for amplifying the deceit).

Please don't be fooled into thinking you have stumbled on to some blow-out discount on anything of even moderate quality. These truly suck every bit as badly as you'd expect for an under $30 product in the age of tariffs.

Avoid.

reviews.author: Herik
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content: This headphone are not worth $300, $29.99 is not a bad price for this. Sound is decent

reviews.author: colette Rodriguez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content: The sound quality is good however they heavy and are too big.

reviews.author: Jeremy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content:
I recently purchased the Bose QuietComfort headphones and they are exceptional. They arrived with a high quality protective case, which was a great first impression.

The setup and connectivity process was simple and seamless. Downloading the Bose app and pairing the headphones took only a few minutes. Everything worked exactly as expected, which is the level of quality I associate with Bose products.

The sound quality is far superior to anything I currently own. I use them both wirelessly with my phone and wired to my computer, and the audio clarity in both setups is outstanding. Music sounds rich and balanced, audiobooks are crisp, and dialogue in videos is incredibly clear.

Comfort is another major strength. The size and style are perfect! I can wear these for extended periods without discomfort. The noise canceling is extremely effective, especially in environments with a lot of background noise. For anyone who can feel overwhelmed by constant sound, these make a noticeable difference.

With the newer version recently released, these are currently priced at nearly half of what they were before, which makes them an even better value. For music, audiobooks, YouTube, or streaming on your phone, they are well worth the investment.

There is honestly nothing about these headphones that I do not like. I would absolutely recommend them.

reviews.author: Kaity Kimball
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 27, 2026
reviews.content:
The photos on this review are of the headphones after a year of use. I think they're still in great shape.

I work in a loud machine shop. I have worn these 40 hours+ a week every week for the past year, and they are without a doubt the best option for quality sound, and long term listening. I had a pair of Sony XM5s before this. Not only did those get uncomfortable after about 5 hours, they also did not last long and broke after a couple of months.

These Bose headphones are as perfect of a pair of headphones you can get these days. Battery life is great, they're lightweight and fit well, so they stay comfortable for 10 hours of use during my shifts, and they're durable enough to still be in great shape after the past year. Volume and noise cancelling control on the ear is convenient. BT range is great. I can walk all the way to the other end of the shop (100ft+) without losing connection.

Yes, these are expensive, but they are the same, if not cheaper than others and last much longer.

reviews.author: Dweezy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 25, 2026
reviews.content:
These Bose QuietComfort headphones are absolutely worth it. The noise cancellation is top-tier — it genuinely blocks out background noise and lets you focus or relax without distractions. The sound quality is crisp, balanced, and powerful without being overwhelming, whether I’m listening to music, podcasts, or audiobooks.

They’re also extremely comfortable. I can wear them for hours without any pressure or ear fatigue, which is huge for long listening sessions, travel, or workdays. Battery life has been solid, and Bluetooth connection is quick and reliable.

If you’re looking for premium sound, unbeatable noise cancellation, and all-day comfort, these are it. Once you try them, it’s hard to go back to anything else.

reviews.author: Val
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content: I love these headphones. The over ear component is super comfortable the battery lasts a long time, enough to last me the entirety of study days. The noise canceling feature really works. Able to hear audio clearly. The lilac color is a nice touch.

reviews.author: VM Express
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 15, 2024
reviews.content:
I've had these Bose QuietComfort NC Headphones for about a month now, and I really like how they feel and sound. For context, I have tried out about 5 different pairs in the last 6 years, starting with some Plantronics PRO's back in 2017, which i still use occasionally for travel. Then I moved to some Sony ones which I did not like. All of these other headphones were wireless and noise cancelling in the $100 to $150 range. They each had some shortfall that made them hard to use. Honestly out of all my previous headphoen the Plantronics were the best in quality and durability and Battery life. Sony always had connectivity issues and would crash randomly. Even the mid range model WHCH710N which had a lot of issues. They would randomly crash during zoom calls or listening to music. Happened about 3-4 times a week, it was getting really annoying.

I decided to tier up my headphones because I use them for work daily. I thought it was a worth while investment. I decided to go with Bose because of the name recognition and because I have seen many of my peers use them (Im a Software Engineer).

My first impressions of the Bose headphones were :
- Light
- Easy to setup - reliable bluetooth connection (something Sony was lacking)
- Excellent sound quality (Best sound quality - on par and slightly better than my Plantronics)
- Comfortable (The most comfortable out of all the other headphones I've used)

To elaborate further. A common pain point I've experienced is difficulty pairing and dealing with unstable bluetooth connections with my other headphones. I rightfully assumed that this was a price point issue, because I have not had that problem with the Bose headphones. The Bose headphones were easy to setup, they also have apps to help with it. But you can use them without any apps.

They are very comfortable and fairly light, comparing to others i've used. I sometimes wear these for 6-10 hours a day and I never have that weird ear pain that you can develop from wearing headphones for long. I have very big ears so that is saying something.

Sound quality is excellent. Deep bass with excellent sound range, very satisfying to listen to. They also have a great volume range. This is what Bose is known for so they definitely get this right.

CON 1:
They do feel rather flimsy. For this price point I expected more metal parts. The materials look well made and have excellent form factor. The headphones however are completely plastic from what I can tell. I feel like one good tug would snap the hinges on the ear pieces. This is a serious concern when you're wearing lose clothing or a hoodie. The headphone could get caught in the folds of your clothing and if were to pull hard or fast while going through the motions you might break them. I have no data to back this up obviously because I am not going to throw away perfectly good headphones to test that theory but you can sort of tell by holding them and pulling on them a little.

CON 2:
Battery life isn't great. If I forget to turn them off at the end of my work day they somehow continue to deplete the battery. The following morning they are already at 50% battery. So that is 50% a day with only 6-8 hours of actual work (active use). For reference, my old Plantronics headphones last for several days without needing a recharge. I have gone on to use them for 5+ days without having to charge them. Even the mid-range Sony headphones perform "slightly" better than this. I know it's good practice to turn them off after you're done but one forgets to do that far more often than one would think. There is room for improvement here.

Overall i'd say these are excellent headphones with great sound quality and form factor. Do take a little extra care because they are not the most durable headphones out there. I am happy with. my

Summary:
PROS: Sound Quality, Comfort, Weight, Form Factor, Easy Setup, Reliable Bluetooth Connection
CONS: Battery Life, Durability

reviews.author: Anna
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 23, 2024
reviews.content:
I’m a layperson, so let me first say I purchased these after much research but based on a “general audience’s” needs. What I mean by that, is my boyfriend mixes music, and while these are high quality this review isn’t for those types of people. This is for more layperson type of needs.
1. I actually used chat gpt to compare and contrast numerous headphones based on my desires so if you’re needing help choosing that was very helpful to me.
2. My needs were as follows: light, comfortable, noise cancelling, color, Bluetooth across devices, and ease of app use.
3. I discovered that these headphones outdo their Sony competition specifically because they’re lighter and they don’t drop Bluetooth when you move to your computer from your phone for example. I found Sony has issues trying to switch back to your phone when on computer.
Bluetooth is very important to me and a top need. I switch between devices enough for that to be a serious issue for me.
4. I have a tiny head and am a 5’2 woman. I don’t have large proportions. For example my boyfriend is 6 foot with a large head and headphone weight won’t affect him the same way. For me headphones can literally be too heavy and fatiguing so my other main qualifier was that these are lighter than all but 1 of the top 7 brands out there.
5. The cushion on these is shockingly luxurious and an added bonus.
6. I get a mean case of sensitive ears, I always have. When I run my ears ache, in the cold my ears ache, if I am in a plane they ache. I am part of a certain population percentage (I’ve googled it’s around 30% but fact check me- this is for you though if you are like me) who have ear canals that actually can be very painful with the wrong pressure. For this reason I’m fairly picky about headphones which apply a significant amount of pressure. These headphones have not triggered this issue for me.
7. I wanted good plane headphones and these will be great if I lean my head on the window area. The band doesn’t dig.
8. They look really nice.
9. The color options are cute
10. The case is nice
11. I heard for Sony there was popping on phone calls and it therefore became between these and Apple however apples are very very heavy. I ended up going with these for that reason. I have used them for numerous phone calls and haven’t had the issues, and people hear me easily and have had no issues hearing me. I did have popping once but I think it’s on the end of the person you’re on the phone with. My cousin was in his car shouting into his Bluetooth and I feel if he had been quieter it wouldn’t have happened. The popping has never occurred with music and is specific to a loud shout from someone on their own CarPlay so no biggie. Otherwise zero pop
12. The noise cancelling is exceptional, I put them on and don’t play anything, but can no longer hear my AC at all. You understand generally the sound of constant AC. Like vrrrrrrrrr. It’s completely gone.
13. Soothing without music to just make things less loud. Suitable for autistics who want a quieting incognito headphone that changes, also more comfy than alternative ear covers. (I am AuDHD.)
14. Music can get really loud and nice lots of depth (layerperson opinion but my boyfriend said the sound quality is great and the next step up is just studio mixing headphones)
15. If you wait for prime day or other sales they’re 150$ off usually (better than every other competitors prime or Black Friday deals)
16. They seem durable and collapse in many ways so you can’t easily break them.
17. The app has had no issues for me. Online there’s some app complaints and I have not run into them at all. The app seems to me very easy to navigate.

reviews.author: Dee
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026
reviews.content: I LOVE these headphones! They charge quickly so I have them when I need them. The quality of sound is great. The audio is crisp and the stereo is very satisfying. The last set of earphones I had hurt the top of my head but Bose has taken care of that problem with ample padding. They have also engineered noise blocking that can be switched on and off with the flick of a switch on the right earphone. These are “around the ear”, they do not put any pressure “on” one’s ears. I forget I have them on they are so comfortable. I hope I covered everything you wanted to know, they are worth the money!

reviews.author: Russell England
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026
reviews.content: These bad boys are amazing. Noise cancelling and sound quality are superb. I was debating between these and the Sony ones and I couldn’t pass up the Bose. I wish the battery life was a little longer, but that’s the only real negative I have. These are light weight and comfortable, can wear them all day with no issues.

reviews.author: Katie B
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 3, 2026
reviews.content:
The JBL Tune 510BT headphones are fantastic for the price. The sound is clear, balanced, and punchy, with deep bass that really makes music enjoyable. Bluetooth pairing is quick and reliable, and the 40-hour battery life means I can go days without charging. The built-in microphone works well for calls too.
The only minor downside is that after long listening sessions, they can leave a slight dent on your head. It’s not painful—just something to be aware of. Overall, they’re comfortable, high-quality, and a great value for anyone looking for wireless headphones.

reviews.author: Landyn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026
reviews.content: love these. the headphones have a amazing battery life, they are worth a little less than $50. the sound quality is amazing, it’s very lightweight, the bluetooth is amazing and it’s very easy to use and immediately connect to my phone as soon as they turn on

reviews.author: Pamela
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026
reviews.content: These are great Bluetooth headphones. I use them at my gym, and they pick up the signal from my phone in its locker at the far end of the gym. Excellent sound quality and they do a good job of blocking outside noise. I'm not technically savvy, but I found them easy to sync with my phone. The battery lasts a long time. They're a very good value.

reviews.author: Brandie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 4, 2026
reviews.content:
Pros: The sound quality is great, they block out a decent amount of outside sound, the battery life surpasses my expectations and needs, they're pretty comfortable over all.

Cons: They started out too tight then within a couple of months became too loose. Then after less than 6 months of use, they broke. The sound still worked, the battery life was still amazing but the left headphone broke and was just dangling there. They didn't hold up as long as I had hoped. I do use headphones daily but I'm not rough with them and I was the only user so it was disappointing for them to break so quickly.

Overall I would still recommend these and use these. I especially recommend these to people who use headphones daily as the battery life is incredible! In fact I replaced them with a similar JBL model because I love them so much. I would just caution you to be gentle with them.

reviews.author: John B
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
reviews.content: I purchased these instead of less expensive headphones because I like JBL products. First timing using these and just had to write a review. EXCELLENT quality sound and comfort. I'm hearing some of my tunes better than ever!

reviews.author: Brittany McCue
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 31, 2026
reviews.content:
I bought the JBL Tune 510BT headphones as an affordable, everyday pair for music, videos, and calls, and they’ve been a really solid purchase.

Setup was super easy—paired quickly with my phone and reconnects fast after that. The sound is what you expect from JBL: punchy bass, clear enough vocals, and plenty of volume. I’m not an audiophile, but for the price these sound way better than I expected.

They’re also lightweight and comfortable for longer wear. The on-ear style doesn’t feel heavy on my head, and the buttons are simple to use for skipping tracks and adjusting volume. Battery life has been excellent too—I’m not constantly charging them, which is a huge plus.

Only minor downside: because they’re on-ear, if you’re very sensitive to pressure you might need a quick break after a long session. But overall, they’re durable, convenient, and a great value.

If you want dependable Bluetooth headphones with good sound and battery life without spending a lot, the JBL Tune 510BT is an easy recommendation.

reviews.author: Jen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content: Wow, these really give you the most bang for your buck!! The sound quality is great, and they block out almost too much outside noise. My daughter got these for Christmas and hasn’t put them down since. I almost wish I hadn’t bought them — she loves them that much! Haha.

reviews.author: Asiyah
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2026
reviews.content:
The JBL Tune 510BT Bluetooth Headphones are exactly the kind of wireless audio you actually use — comfortable, reliable, and sounding way better than the price suggests.

Sound Quality: These deliver that classic JBL audio vibe — punchy bass, clear mids, and crisp highs. Music sounds fun and lively, not flat or tinny like some budget Bluetooth cans. Whether I’m jamming playlists, podcasts, or calls, they stay detailed and smooth.

Bluetooth Performance: Pairing is quick and painless. Once connected, they stay locked in without constant dropouts or that annoying “reconnect already” issue. Great for commuting, walking, or chilling at the house.

Comfort: Lightweight with cushy ear pads that don’t turn your ears into a pressure cooker. I can wear them for hours without the “why is my head tight” feeling. The adjustable headband actually fits my giant human head and doesn’t pinch.

Battery Life: Long enough to forget you charged them. They last through multiple days of use without begging for a plug, which is always delightful.

Build & Controls: Simple buttons that actually work without mystery combos or accidental presses. They feel sturdy, not like they’re going to break if you look at them wrong.

Overall this is a well-rounded, user-friendly pair of headphones that hits all the essential notes: good sound, comfy fit, reliable Bluetooth, and long battery life. Five stars because they make listening easy and enjoyable.

reviews.author: Brent Hinson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 16, 2025
reviews.content:
I can honestly say that these headphones are worth the investment.

There are times when I still need to plug in my headphones with a 3.5mm cable, so I like that I can use them wired or wirelessly.

When it comes to using them wirelessly with Bluetooth, they are amazing. You can connect the headphones to a single dedicated device or to two devices simultaneously. For me, that is such a great feature. I use the headphones to connect to my computer for work purposes, but they're also connected to my phone as well.

The sound quality is the best I've ever gotten from a set of headphones. I've never experienced noise-cancelling audio as precise as this. And for the times when you don't need to totally cancel everything out, the ambient sound mode provides audio that is just as good.

The Sony Sound Connect app you use with these headphones is designed to deliver the best possible sound. The equalizer function lets you control how you experience whatever you're listening to, offering options like bright, relaxed, vocal, speech, and bass boost.

I use these headphones all day while I work, and they hold a consistent charge. I still have plenty of battery life left at the end of the day, but I usually plug them up to charge when I'm done with them.

The volume control buttons on the headphones are easy to use. The only thing that can be frustrating at times is that the button to stop/start a song or move to the next track is right in the middle of the volume buttons, so I end up hitting it when I want to raise the volume. However, it is nice that they've also included a button on the headphones to toggle between noise-cancellation and ambient sound.

Again, I use these for work, so I wear them for the majority of the day. They fit well and are comfortable to wear. As the day wears on, my ears do get a little warm from the cushions around the headset, but compared to how much I like using the headphones, it's a minor issue.

The only other item of note is that, given the investment, it would be nice to include a storage box or bag.

If you're on the fence about buying these headphones, I highly recommend them. They are worth the investment, and you'll be pleased with both the sound and design.

reviews.author: Audrey Prather
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content: I ordered these (used) in 2023 and it is now 2026. I’ve used them pretty much every day since they arrived and they are AMAZING. They finally broke today so sadly I have to get new headphones. But the noise cancellation and sound quality is amazing, and it’s easy to connect. They’re little and easy to put in a backpack or just carry around, they also don’t hurt your ears. I recommend these a trillion times over.

reviews.author: V
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content: Light wieght massive battery volume control and NC top quality I would easily put these up against beats. So comfortable and they have a 3.5mm cable input. A design that doesnt crack with time. If youre looking for good headphones look no further. There cameras are incredible and so are their headphones

reviews.author: Choko Davis
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 2, 2026
reviews.content: Nice and lightweight. Good compatibility with devices and easy to use for pairing. Comfortable around the ear. Sony Connect app helps ease of use as well. Loud sound with noice cancellation options.

reviews.author: Brian
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 17, 2023
reviews.content:
SECOND UPDATE - 11/9/2023

OK, so I have been living with these for a couple of months now and I feel I can say some things with more confidence.

1. Comfort is still a bit hit or miss. I will say the choice to put those microphones or whatever those things that stick out on the insides of the earcups was not great. I don't always feel them pressing on my ears but the earpads are not very thick and if you don't have them on in exactly the right way (assuming you can find a way that works for you) then the pads will flatten a bit and you'll feel them on your ears. It's not exactly painful. Just unpleasant.

2. And I feel the need to come back to the ear cushions themselves. Again, they just barely fit over my ears. And I wear glasses so this is a big issue with most headphones. I can sometimes get the cushions just right so they don't smash the tips of my ears against the arms of my glasses too much but it sometimes takes a bit of extra fiddling around with them that can be annoying when I'm not in the mood for it. Again, not too much of a knock against Sony since this is a widespread issue with headphones in general. Manufacturers just seem to think if they make the cushions soft, they will be comfortable. Nope. If it smashes your ears, it will start to hurt after a while no matter how soft it is.

3. The battery life is decent but I do find myself having to charge them more often than I expected. Prior to these, I had an inexpensive pair of Jlab headphones and although their sound quality and features were lacking, the battery life was outstanding and I was just used to not charging for days and days. I pretty much wear headphones all day every day and I generally find that these will be at 50% after a couple of days. Of course that's with noise cancelling on. But also, these are noise cancelling headphones so they were designed to be used with it on for long periods. Or should have been. I don't know what the capacity of the battery is in these but the battery packs they use in wireless headphones are not expensive items anymore. You can buy a set of $30 wireless headphones now with a huge capacity battery. So, yes. These are lower end Sony but they're still well above the price you pay for really basic wireless headphones. They shouldn't have skimped on the battery capacity.

That said, though I haven't actually kept track of how many hours, it probably does meet the stated runtime. I had just hoped they were being conservative with the numbers. But also, you can't use them and charge them at the same time so if you use your headphones constantly, that can be annoying. I don't like to run lithium batteries much below 50%. I don't know that it's bad for them exactly but all of my lithium batteries have lasted me for many years using that policy so that's what I do. When I see 40-50% I charge the battery. I know they have the quick charge feature but I like to charge and forget for many hours. It would be more useful if I could plug into a power bank for 15 minutes and still use the headphones while it's charging. But nope. You plug into a charger and the headphones turn off.

Everything said, I'm reasonably happy with them but I paid the $150 asking price they were selling for when I bought them. Knowing all I know now, I would not pay that price again for these. These are $70 headphones. Tops. They're not that comfortable. The battery life is easily matched (or surpassed) by the least expensive wireless headphones on Amazon. The stock sound quality is not impressive at all. (I will say they sound pretty good if you tweak the EQ). The only things I have been really happy about is once I got them paired with my PC, they always connect right away when I turn them on. That's something that is hit or miss with most bluetooth devices. And can be incredibly annoying when you have to fight to get something connected every single time. I've also been pretty satisfied with the noise cancelling. Which they're the first NC headphones I've ever owned so I have nothing to compare them to.

I'm not really understanding what Sony was trying to do with these. Their top of the line ANC headphones might be some of the best ANC but are they really $400 good? Well, if they're anything like these, they are good but significantly overpriced.

UPDATE - 9/25/2023

I realize my original review is a bit long and probably most people don't want to read all that so after living with these for a week or so, here are some key points that I think people will want to know.

1. Comfort is just OK for people with small or medium ears that don't stick out too far. Beyond that, the comfort might be anything from uncomfortable to painful.

2. The ANC is good enough for me. I don't fly. I don't use them on a bus or train or anything. I use them at home. They block out things like computer fans and voices in adjacent rooms. I have a fan right by my head and although I can still hear it, the ANC really tames it to a very gentle hiss. If voices or TV are in the same room with you, it will cause them to seem to recede quite a bit but you will still probably hear them (especially TV). The ANC works better on some frequencies than others. It almost eliminates some noises but (for example) the sound of water running nearby is something you will definitely hear.

3. I use these 90% with my Windows 10 PC and I have not experienced any of the disconnection issues that many have reported with them as long as I am only using them with my PC. However, when I do connect them to my Android phone, if I leave them connected for a while, things can get glitchy and if I then turn off the bluetooth on my phone, it will sometimes make weird things happen. Like if I have the ANC turned on on the headphones, it will turn off and forget the EQ settings I have chosen. Also, sometimes when I try to disconnect them from the phone by simply turning off bluetooth, they don't make the disconnect sound. I don't know. I'm glad I didn't buy them to use exclusively with the phone. I have a different set of headphones for that. I just wanted to include this in my review to note that I did indeed notice some issues when I left them connected to my phone for a while. I will be keeping them because PC is all I care about.

END UPDATE
---------------------

ORIGINAL REVIEW

Things that mattered to me in choosing these headphones...

1. I'm not going to try and sugar coat this too much. I don't have money to burn. But a set of headphones that check all my boxes without doing anything I hate for a price I can handle is a tall order. Good headphones have never been cheap. ANC is still a premium feature (though this is changing fast). These headphones were just barely in my price range at $150. And frankly, although I like everything about them (so far) I think $100 would have been closer to the price these should be selling for. This segment of the market is crowded and if I was willing to gamble even more than I did, I could have probably gotten something I was happy with for significantly less than that $150. I went with Sony because I'm an old guy and I know that you usually don't pay more for a Sony product for no reason.

2. Comfort. Of course, we're talking perceived comfort. Comfort involves a lot of guesswork. For these, it appears that I mostly guessed right. These just fit my ears with a little pressure here and there. My ears are not small but also not that large. If you have large ears, you should probably go somewhere where you can try the headphones on before you buy. I probably should have done this myself because comfort is always my top priority (other than price). Most likely the pads on these will not be difficult to replace should the need arise. And it usually does with headphones. Sooner or later, the pads will always wear out.

3. ANC - Of course. This is my first pair of ANC headphones and it's everything I want. The transparency mode is slightly quieter than I would like but it's acceptable. I've seen some people complaining about the ANC not being effective on these. Not sure what they're talking about but maybe they're coming from much higher end ANC. For me, the first day I had them, I had some people come to see me and they were banging on my door. I heard absolutely nothing. They were freaked out because they thought something was wrong. So....my verdict on this is if you want perfect ANC, maybe shop around. I'm not here to tell you it's the best. I don't have anything to compare it to. I can only say it leaves me wanting nothing and I'm happy with it.

4. Sound quality - Out of the box, I found the factory sound to be lacking. But that usually happens with me when I have new headphones. I can't say for sure if the burn in effect is psychological or real but all I can say is a new pair of headphones takes some getting used to for me. At least the EQ is sufficiently adjustable in the app and I managed to get them to sound very nice. It's nice that the settings you choose in the app will be stored in the headphones so the same settings will apply on other devices. I mostly use Bluetooth headphones on my Windows 10 PC so I'm not going to be fiddling around with the app much.

These can go pretty loud and have decent bass. The drivers can provide good clarity for mids and highs if you fiddle with the EQ. The stock EQ sounds muffled. But that also might be a result of them not having been burned in.

CONS - It's really nitpicking for me to find many cons. These are great headphones (at least initially).

1. The buttons could be a bit more tactile. In particular, I find the play/pause button a bit difficult to find and always find myself hunting for it. It is right between the Vol +/- buttons and should stick out a little more than it does to make it obvious. Also, the ANC button is nearly flush with the earcup and still makes me hunt for it a bit more than I like. I think I'll get used to these buttons eventually but I don't love them.

2. The shortest timer for the auto off that's available in the app is 15 minutes. I was really kind of surprised that there was no option for a shorter time.

3. The headband is adjustable by clicks. So if the size you need is between two clicks, you might have to have them slightly too tight or slightly too loose. The higher end Sony headbands with no clicks seem like they would be better in this way.

4. Although (as I said) the comfort is pretty good, it's not great. But this is not terribly unusual for lower end headphones. I strongly prefer over ear (earcups that surround the ears rather than sit on them and smash them) and I'm very picky about it.
