I don't like it if my ears are not completely inside the earcups and I don't think it's asking much since my ears are not enormous. I find that these do kind of smash the tops of my ears. It's not something I notice right away but after about half an hour or so, it starts to become something I'm almost constantly aware of. It's not exactly painful but it isn't really pleasant either.

To make matters worse, there is a seam in the fake leather that runs all the way around the inside where your ear is probably gonna be if your ears are just slightly larger than the ears of a child. I find that many of these types of earpads have that seam and I don't know it's purpose but I know that ones that don't have it tend to be easier on your ears even if your ears don't fit perfectly. So all I'm saying is this isn't some upstart company. This is Sony. These are low end Sony headphones but they're still not really cheap and they're still made by an enormous company that's been around for many decades. I don't know what it is. Almost every company that makes headphones of this sort makes the earcups too small for even slightly larger ears. I guess they think people with larger ears don't use headphones. Of course my ears could be really large. In which case, I probably wouldn't be able to wear them even if they were a little larger.

5. Also, I have to kind of agree with all the other reviews that mentioned the little plastic part that sticks out from the area where the driver is on the inside of the earcup. I am not sure if it actually pushes on my ears or not but it seems like it might. I kind of feel something and it does stick out far enough to where I can see how it could rub your ears.

I think these pieces are the place where the microphones are mounted so if that's the case, they kind of have to be there but they probably don't have to be as poorly designed as they are. I feel like I might be able to trim some of the plastic off if I really wanted to but I'm not going to start working on a brand new set of $150 headphones. It doesn't bother me (yet) so maybe I'll change my mind later on. I think this could have been avoided if the padding were a bit thicker on the earpads.

reviews.author: Amy Macs 757
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 3, 2025
reviews.content:
​Sony WH-CH720N

​I am absolutely thrilled and genuinely impressed with my new Sony WH-CH720N wireless headphones! Knowing I was buying a Sony product instilled a high level of confidence from the start, and these headphones have completely exceeded my expectations. They deliver the premium quality the brand is known for, but at an incredibly accessible price point.
​The sound quality is nothing short of phenomenal. The audio is rich, vibrant, and remarkably well-balanced, providing a deeply immersive listening experience whether I'm enjoying complex classical scores, bass-heavy electronic tracks, or simply listening to a podcast. What takes the audio a step further is the brilliant ability to customize the equalizer settings directly on the companion app. Being able to fine-tune the acoustics to perfectly match my personal listening preference is a massive feature that truly makes a difference.
​Beyond the magnificent sound, the Noise Cancellation (NC) feature is a real game-changer. It creates a serene bubble, effortlessly muting the distractions of the outside world—whether it's the chatter of a busy office, the low hum of an airplane, or street traffic. This powerful noise-blocking capability, combined with the stellar audio, allows me to fully immerse myself in the music.
​If you are seeking headphones that blend top-tier sound performance, effective noise cancellation, and Sony's renowned quality, the WH-CH720N is an absolute must-buy. I couldn't be happier with this purchase!

reviews.author: Zachary Woodman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 22, 2023
reviews.content:
I have spent years fighting to find a decent pair of bluetooth headphones since no phone manufacturer wants to make a decent phone with a jack anymore (which is endlessly stupid, but I digress). I have tried everything form fancy expensive Galaxy Buds to a myriad of budget options, and all have the same problems. Flawed connectivity, noticeably glitchy, slightly reduced sound quality.

These finally fix it. The inclusion of an option to use a headphone jack on devices that let you is a great feature. Along with multipoint connect. They fit perfectly around my skinny neck (your mileage may very on that), so travel with them is a breeze. And for the sound quality, these are absolutely the best headphones for your money.

I was loathe to go to overears full time because I like being able to take one out to hear my surroundings, and every noise passthrough I used before was sucky. (usually the outside noise obnoxiously trebley). Not with these. I actually go to concerts to listen with my headphones on because it actually makes the concerts sound better than with no headphones. Extremely convincing, near perfect noise passthrough.

The app is easy to use and has a great selection of features: from custom EQs, to adjusting the noise pass through, to other various quality of life settings. The 3D adaptive sound feature is great, though a little tricky to set up at first, and noise cancellation is very good, though not the best in the world. The battery life is also almost unbelievable. I use these headphones a lot--sometimes all day--and I still only have to charge them once or twice a week. Multipoint connection is also a very nice plus if, say, I am working at my computer and still want to be able to answer phone calls hands-free.

There are some minor flaws with them, though. First sometimes very low bassey notes from the outside world can trigger the speakers in them and give you very loud, deep popping noises. (E.g., this occasionally happens when I am riding in the car and hitting bumps in the road). Occasional outside sounds in passthrough through will sound muddled. Also the noise cancellation--while quite good--isn't perfect for every situation. Specifically, it has more trouble than, say, Bose headphones with loud, sudden sounds like fireworks. But for the money, it is still the best noise cancellation and passthrough system you can buy.

Another (minor) complaint, though, is that the adaptive settings aren't always very great. It often thinks I am sitting and automatically turns on noise cancellation when I am very much driving or walking around town or once even ice skating--all situations where passthrough is crucial. And even if I am sitting, sometimes I really want passthrough on (like if I'm in a cafe), but it automatically tries to turn noise cancelling on annoyingly. Thankfully, it can be easily overridden in the app. These days, I usually just keep the adaptive sound setting off and adjust the settings to fit the situation but sometimes it will randomly go back to automatic/adaptive mode.

My final and complaint is with the microphone for phone calls. Sometimes it works perfectly and the other line can hear my fine, sometimes all they can hear is background noise, sometimes they really can't hear me at all. I am not sure if it is some sort of firmware issue or if the microphone placement is just too far from my mouth, but for phone calls it can be a tad unreliable. When it works, which is around 75% of the time, it's perfectly good even though I have to talk a little louder than I normally would sometimes.

But all those flaws aside, these are probably the best all-around headphones I have owned and for the $120 I paid for them it's a steal. They may be more expensive as I got them on sale, but I have used headphones multiple times the price that are poorer quality and less functional than these. I'd have paid upwards of twice the price for what I got.

reviews.author: jenjaine
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2023
reviews.content:
Update 3/9……so first I’m going to say that I bought these with my own money before any reviews were out. Didn’t realize they were a vine product. After a few weeks of use the padding has relaxed enough so that I can feel the drivers poking my ears. I wear my headphones around 4 hours a day on weekdays and 8-10 hours on each weekend day. As stated earlier I have a smaller than average head, and my ears do not stick out. I can usually adjust the cups to be lower, but I don’t like them riding my jawbone.

I would’ve have overlooked that because they do sound good, however they are now randomly disconnecting. I use them for YouTube and I’m literally 18 inches away and they disconnect. I check through the app and it shows a connection failure. If I pause a video, again, they show connected, but the sound comes out of my iPad. When I check the app it tells me there is a connection failure and to restart. While the sound is good I don’t think I care to restart my headphones every 10 minutes.

Maybe they don’t like Apple devices? I’m not sure….

I originally bought the xm5’s. I finally got them last week only to find they were quite oversized for my smaller than average head. The weight of them made matters worse as every time I looked down they flopped forward. I had to return them.

I am not one of those fancy audio people but I know what I like. My previous headphone were the solo pro 3’s and I do enjoy them. What I don’t like is the pain my ears experience after about an hour of having them on.

I wanted over the ear headphones and everything I read and all the videos I watched pointed me to the xm5’s which ended sadly. I searched Amazon and saw these were a new model. Looked up what little I could find online about them and the consensus was these were the cheap version of the xm5’s. Yeah, I’m sold so I got them.

Build:
They are cheaper feeling, not as bad as the 510’s, but nowhere near the xm5’s workmanship. But they are also not breaking the bank (as bad anyways, it still shocks me to see what headphones are going for nowadays). The sliding part is plastic reinforced by metal. I’ll be honest….I am wondering how they will hold up.

Comfort:
Excellent. These are super light. I don’t feel them resting on the crown of my head at all. There is no clamping on or around my ears. But the are secure on my head. The padding around the ear cups is decent and the cups are deep so most will not have their ears touching the speakers.

Bells and whistles: these are old school, they have buttons…no swipe features at all. I like that because in my week of using the xm5’s I discovered quickly that volume adjustment was hit or miss with response time. It might be that my hands are always cold, but in 6 hours of wearing these I have already mastered the buttons. They are very well placed and very responsive.

App: They are part of the Sony Headphone app. You get an equalizer and other customizable features including the ability to connect to two devices at the same time and it works great as I use the headphones for watching music videos on YouTube and Netflix. I use my phone to adjust the equalizer for the music playing on my iPad.

Another feature on the app is you can set the headphones to shut off when not in use. I was excited about the auto shutoff feature. Not so much now. It is not sensor based nor is it based on when you device’s screen shuts down. It is based on when you turn of Bluetooth. This makes it worthless to me and maybe a lot of us who do not ever turn Bluetooth off. I think it could have been done better.

Noise cancelling:
I’m going to call this adequate. With my Beats Solo pro with ANC I can’t hear anything. Not even someone yelling. These will drown out the hum of a fan pretty well, but they let in a lot of ambient noise (like my husband coughing in the next room right now!). Even the xm5’s were not as good as the Beats. But the on ear style of the Beats may have something to do with that. So I’ll stick to the xm5’s comparison. I also want to say that there are supposedly 3 modes…noise cancelling, ambient and off. I have not noticed any difference between ambient and noise cancelling. There is no voice prompt to let you know which setting you are in. I will play some more to see if I’m missing a setting in the app and if something changes I’ll edit my review.

Sound quality: (of course this is subjective to each person) Excellent out of the box and I could not say that about the xm5’s. My first mission was to find the equalizer when I took them out of the box and tried them. Not so with these. The sound was nice. I still tweaked it but if I wasn’t able to change it a bit, I would still be happy.

Other:
They do not come with a case. They do not fold. They come with an aux cord and charging cable, no charging block.

Final thoughts: This is my fourth pair of headphones in a month. I was willing to drop over 300 bucks on a pair. And I didn’t like any of them. Most important to me is fit and sound. Most were severely lacking in sound quality. These are not. And they were cheaper.

I am most disappointed in the build quality. I think they should’ve done better. To me $150 is alot of money to spend on headphones that feel this plasticy. And if they crack after 6 months I’ll be upset. I’m not expecting $350 quality, but better. These are not too much better than the 510’s when they should’ve been a little less than the 910’s. They are far less quality than the 910’s. Maybe I’m asking too much after coming from the Solo 3 pros and xm5’s which both have a superb build.

BUT…..they make up for it in having great sound that you can totally adjust to your liking. They are comparable to the xm5’s in sound. And far superior I feel to the 910’s which just sound overly undefined bass. And the comfort!! It’s spot on.

These are the best out there in terms of sound and app capabilities.

reviews.author: Jeremy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content:
I recently purchased the Bose QuietComfort headphones and they are exceptional. They arrived with a high quality protective case, which was a great first impression.

The setup and connectivity process was simple and seamless. Downloading the Bose app and pairing the headphones took only a few minutes. Everything worked exactly as expected, which is the level of quality I associate with Bose products.

The sound quality is far superior to anything I currently own. I use them both wirelessly with my phone and wired to my computer, and the audio clarity in both setups is outstanding. Music sounds rich and balanced, audiobooks are crisp, and dialogue in videos is incredibly clear.

Comfort is another major strength. The size and style are perfect! I can wear these for extended periods without discomfort. The noise canceling is extremely effective, especially in environments with a lot of background noise. For anyone who can feel overwhelmed by constant sound, these make a noticeable difference.

With the newer version recently released, these are currently priced at nearly half of what they were before, which makes them an even better value. For music, audiobooks, YouTube, or streaming on your phone, they are well worth the investment.

There is honestly nothing about these headphones that I do not like. I would absolutely recommend them.

reviews.author: Kaity Kimball
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 27, 2026
reviews.content:
The photos on this review are of the headphones after a year of use. I think they're still in great shape.

I work in a loud machine shop. I have worn these 40 hours+ a week every week for the past year, and they are without a doubt the best option for quality sound, and long term listening. I had a pair of Sony XM5s before this. Not only did those get uncomfortable after about 5 hours, they also did not last long and broke after a couple of months.

These Bose headphones are as perfect of a pair of headphones you can get these days. Battery life is great, they're lightweight and fit well, so they stay comfortable for 10 hours of use during my shifts, and they're durable enough to still be in great shape after the past year. Volume and noise cancelling control on the ear is convenient. BT range is great. I can walk all the way to the other end of the shop (100ft+) without losing connection.

Yes, these are expensive, but they are the same, if not cheaper than others and last much longer.

reviews.author: Dweezy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 25, 2026
reviews.content:
These Bose QuietComfort headphones are absolutely worth it. The noise cancellation is top-tier — it genuinely blocks out background noise and lets you focus or relax without distractions. The sound quality is crisp, balanced, and powerful without being overwhelming, whether I’m listening to music, podcasts, or audiobooks.

They’re also extremely comfortable. I can wear them for hours without any pressure or ear fatigue, which is huge for long listening sessions, travel, or workdays. Battery life has been solid, and Bluetooth connection is quick and reliable.

If you’re looking for premium sound, unbeatable noise cancellation, and all-day comfort, these are it. Once you try them, it’s hard to go back to anything else.

reviews.author: Val
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content: I love these headphones. The over ear component is super comfortable the battery lasts a long time, enough to last me the entirety of study days. The noise canceling feature really works. Able to hear audio clearly. The lilac color is a nice touch.

reviews.author: VM Express
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 15, 2024
reviews.content:
I've had these Bose QuietComfort NC Headphones for about a month now, and I really like how they feel and sound. For context, I have tried out about 5 different pairs in the last 6 years, starting with some Plantronics PRO's back in 2017, which i still use occasionally for travel. Then I moved to some Sony ones which I did not like. All of these other headphones were wireless and noise cancelling in the $100 to $150 range. They each had some shortfall that made them hard to use. Honestly out of all my previous headphoen the Plantronics were the best in quality and durability and Battery life. Sony always had connectivity issues and would crash randomly. Even the mid range model WHCH710N which had a lot of issues. They would randomly crash during zoom calls or listening to music. Happened about 3-4 times a week, it was getting really annoying.

I decided to tier up my headphones because I use them for work daily. I thought it was a worth while investment. I decided to go with Bose because of the name recognition and because I have seen many of my peers use them (Im a Software Engineer).

My first impressions of the Bose headphones were :
- Light
- Easy to setup - reliable bluetooth connection (something Sony was lacking)
- Excellent sound quality (Best sound quality - on par and slightly better than my Plantronics)
- Comfortable (The most comfortable out of all the other headphones I've used)

To elaborate further. A common pain point I've experienced is difficulty pairing and dealing with unstable bluetooth connections with my other headphones. I rightfully assumed that this was a price point issue, because I have not had that problem with the Bose headphones. The Bose headphones were easy to setup, they also have apps to help with it. But you can use them without any apps.

They are very comfortable and fairly light, comparing to others i've used. I sometimes wear these for 6-10 hours a day and I never have that weird ear pain that you can develop from wearing headphones for long. I have very big ears so that is saying something.

Sound quality is excellent. Deep bass with excellent sound range, very satisfying to listen to. They also have a great volume range. This is what Bose is known for so they definitely get this right.

CON 1:
They do feel rather flimsy. For this price point I expected more metal parts. The materials look well made and have excellent form factor. The headphones however are completely plastic from what I can tell. I feel like one good tug would snap the hinges on the ear pieces. This is a serious concern when you're wearing lose clothing or a hoodie. The headphone could get caught in the folds of your clothing and if were to pull hard or fast while going through the motions you might break them. I have no data to back this up obviously because I am not going to throw away perfectly good headphones to test that theory but you can sort of tell by holding them and pulling on them a little.

CON 2:
Battery life isn't great. If I forget to turn them off at the end of my work day they somehow continue to deplete the battery. The following morning they are already at 50% battery. So that is 50% a day with only 6-8 hours of actual work (active use). For reference, my old Plantronics headphones last for several days without needing a recharge. I have gone on to use them for 5+ days without having to charge them. Even the mid-range Sony headphones perform "slightly" better than this. I know it's good practice to turn them off after you're done but one forgets to do that far more often than one would think. There is room for improvement here.

Overall i'd say these are excellent headphones with great sound quality and form factor. Do take a little extra care because they are not the most durable headphones out there. I am happy with. my

Summary:
PROS: Sound Quality, Comfort, Weight, Form Factor, Easy Setup, Reliable Bluetooth Connection
CONS: Battery Life, Durability

reviews.author: Anna
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 23, 2024
reviews.content:
I’m a layperson, so let me first say I purchased these after much research but based on a “general audience’s” needs. What I mean by that, is my boyfriend mixes music, and while these are high quality this review isn’t for those types of people. This is for more layperson type of needs.
1. I actually used chat gpt to compare and contrast numerous headphones based on my desires so if you’re needing help choosing that was very helpful to me.
2. My needs were as follows: light, comfortable, noise cancelling, color, Bluetooth across devices, and ease of app use.
3. I discovered that these headphones outdo their Sony competition specifically because they’re lighter and they don’t drop Bluetooth when you move to your computer from your phone for example. I found Sony has issues trying to switch back to your phone when on computer.
Bluetooth is very important to me and a top need. I switch between devices enough for that to be a serious issue for me.
4. I have a tiny head and am a 5’2 woman. I don’t have large proportions. For example my boyfriend is 6 foot with a large head and headphone weight won’t affect him the same way. For me headphones can literally be too heavy and fatiguing so my other main qualifier was that these are lighter than all but 1 of the top 7 brands out there.
5. The cushion on these is shockingly luxurious and an added bonus.
6. I get a mean case of sensitive ears, I always have. When I run my ears ache, in the cold my ears ache, if I am in a plane they ache. I am part of a certain population percentage (I’ve googled it’s around 30% but fact check me- this is for you though if you are like me) who have ear canals that actually can be very painful with the wrong pressure. For this reason I’m fairly picky about headphones which apply a significant amount of pressure. These headphones have not triggered this issue for me.
7. I wanted good plane headphones and these will be great if I lean my head on the window area. The band doesn’t dig.
8. They look really nice.
9. The color options are cute
10. The case is nice
11. I heard for Sony there was popping on phone calls and it therefore became between these and Apple however apples are very very heavy. I ended up going with these for that reason. I have used them for numerous phone calls and haven’t had the issues, and people hear me easily and have had no issues hearing me. I did have popping once but I think it’s on the end of the person you’re on the phone with. My cousin was in his car shouting into his Bluetooth and I feel if he had been quieter it wouldn’t have happened. The popping has never occurred with music and is specific to a loud shout from someone on their own CarPlay so no biggie. Otherwise zero pop
12. The noise cancelling is exceptional, I put them on and don’t play anything, but can no longer hear my AC at all. You understand generally the sound of constant AC. Like vrrrrrrrrr. It’s completely gone.
13. Soothing without music to just make things less loud. Suitable for autistics who want a quieting incognito headphone that changes, also more comfy than alternative ear covers. (I am AuDHD.)
14. Music can get really loud and nice lots of depth (layerperson opinion but my boyfriend said the sound quality is great and the next step up is just studio mixing headphones)
15. If you wait for prime day or other sales they’re 150$ off usually (better than every other competitors prime or Black Friday deals)
16. They seem durable and collapse in many ways so you can’t easily break them.
17. The app has had no issues for me. Online there’s some app complaints and I have not run into them at all. The app seems to me very easy to navigate.

reviews.author: Dee
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026
reviews.content: I LOVE these headphones! They charge quickly so I have them when I need them. The quality of sound is great. The audio is crisp and the stereo is very satisfying. The last set of earphones I had hurt the top of my head but Bose has taken care of that problem with ample padding. They have also engineered noise blocking that can be switched on and off with the flick of a switch on the right earphone. These are “around the ear”, they do not put any pressure “on” one’s ears. I forget I have them on they are so comfortable. I hope I covered everything you wanted to know, they are worth the money!

reviews.author: Russell England
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026
reviews.content: These bad boys are amazing. Noise cancelling and sound quality are superb. I was debating between these and the Sony ones and I couldn’t pass up the Bose. I wish the battery life was a little longer, but that’s the only real negative I have. These are light weight and comfortable, can wear them all day with no issues.

reviews.author: SS
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content: I went through a half dozen pair of other options that were less pricey before settling on these. Other versions, I tried lasted, at most, a couple of months, before they broke. One pair broke while I was removing it from my head. Then I found these Sony headphones, which were a total game changer! I still have the first pair I purchased, which have lasted me for more than a year; and I recently purchased a second pair in another color for the office. They are such good quality, very comfortable, easy to adjust to fit children and adults, the sound quality is phenomenal, and they are easy to pair with your devices! I have used them for everything from Zoom calls, to music, listening, and beyond, without any issues. Several people at the office have ordered themselves a pair because they’re so great, and they have loved them as well! If you’re struggling to find headphones that last and/or can survive your kids’ wear and tear, look no further! These are well worth the money and they last, and look great! So far, I’ve ordered these in the blue color and the beige/cream color, and both are lovely, and do not look cheap at all. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Nitenday
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2026
reviews.content: These Bluetooth on‑ear headphones deliver excellent everyday performance at an affordable price. They’re comfortable, lightweight, and have impressive battery life — up to ~50 hours on a single charge so you can go days without recharging. The wireless connection is stable, the built‑in mic works well for calls, and the sound is clear and balanced for casual listening. A great pick if you want reliable daily headphones without breaking the bank.

reviews.author: Aline B
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 8, 2026
reviews.content:
I bought these Sony wireless headphones for my son, and they’ve been great. The sound quality is clear and balanced, the Bluetooth is easy to pair and connection is reliable, it has easy touch volume buttons, and they’re very comfortable for long use.

They hold a good charge and feel well made, look as sleek as a big brand expensive one. They fit very well kid or adult the head adjustment is great. Overall, a great value and a solid choice for everyday listening.

reviews.author: Brett Summers
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 12, 2025
reviews.content:
I did not read the description carefully. It states what devices these headphones are compatible with and "desktop computer" is not one of them. I called Sony and they told me they will work with almost any modern device that has Bluetooth connectivity. I easily wirelessly connected them to my desktop computer and they work great. Strangely the user guide gives instructions on how to use them with Mac and Windows computers, so read the user guide/manual that is linked in the description. If you want to use any of the functions of the Sony Headphones Connect app you will need a compatible phone. If you do not use the app (not compatible with computers) they function as a basic pair of wireless headphones. All rather confusing and the Amazon description does nothing to clear it up.

With that being said I connected it easily to my desktop computer. I am no audiophile and do not need a $400 pair of headphones. To me they sound great especially for the inexpensive price. They are NOT noise cancelling headphones and I did not want noise cancelling. They are "on the ear" headphones meaning they are about the size of your ear and press your ears against your head creating a bit of a seal keeping some exterior noise out. Works fine, but if you wear eyeglasses and wear the glasses and the headphones at the same time after a few hours it can become uncomfortable as the headphones press your ear against the arm of the glasses and the arm of the glasses into the side of your head.

The charging port on the headphones is USB C and they come with a USB A to USB C cable but no charging brick. I connected mine to my computer with a USB C to USB C cable and it charged fine. 50 hours of playtime on one charge is great.

One star for a confusing and unclear Amazon description. 4 stars for a good basic pair of headphones that works with almost any modern Bluetooth device, albeit without the functions of the Sony app (unless you are using it with a compatible phone).

reviews.author: Alan F. Jordano
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 31, 2024
reviews.content:
The product arrived well packaged. There was a delivery delay but it was not horrendous and to be expected given that it was ordered during Parime Days,

The headphones arrived almost fully charged, Set up was relatively easy, but you really do need to use their app to complete the set up process. The app also allows you to change some settings and select equalizer settings that work for you.

The switches have a nice feel to them. They do have raised areas designed to help you find the correct switch by feel. There are times however when I find that it is easier to adjust the volume using the phone or I remove the headphone to make a change. This is a very subjective items so you may not have the same issue. No stars were deducted for that reason.

These are an on ear device. It was not uncomfortable to me, but it did take some getting used to and here again...Personal preference comes into play. On a warm and humid day you may experience some sweating where the ear cup rides on your ear. That may allow for some movement / shifting of the headphone depending on what you are doing. They may thus not work out well for use doing exercising as an example.

The sound quality is great at this price point. I have Bose ear buds. The sound from this device is not Bose quality and you should not expect it to be Bose quality. It is good to very good however. The equalizer settings allow you to control all five typical equalizer settings PLUS a separate adjustment for th4 bass.

Paring was easy and fast. The connection remains stable most of the time. There is a selection that allows you to select either top quality sound or a more stable connection. I chose sound quality an the vast majority of the time the connection was stable with no noise present. There may be times however, that due to local conditions / interference you may have a little popping or very brief drop out. I have worn these headphones a LOT since they arrived and while I have experienced that, it was very limited. Now I also have a lot of electric / electronic devices in use on my property and that will may explain what I experienced. I did have some interference from some household devices when I wass very close to them.

The battery life seems to be very good. I have worn m for several hours and the voice system still indicates 100% at startup. Unlike my Bose products the battery monitoring system is not linear where it will call out 100%, 90%, 80%b etc... It may call out 100% until the battery hits 50% (As an example) and then te voice system will drop from stating 100% to 50%. I do not see that as an issue or negative given the very long stated by the manufacturer battery life, and I have no disagreement with their claims about battery life aty this point in time,

So again...Thus far I am very pleased with the fit, quality, and sound quality of these headphones. Do bed aware that tey are NOT noise cancelling. There will be some outside noise reduction dur to the ear cups of course.

reviews.author: William G.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 4, 2026
reviews.content:
These Sony headphones are sublime. I first purchased a pair for my father when he wanted to watch television while Mom was sleeping, and he liked them so much that I picked up a pair for myself.

First off, they are incredibly comfortable. They hug my ears, and were it not for the sound coming from them, I’d forget I had them on. I’ll have these on for hours at a time, and my ears never get sore. The padding on the plastic is comfortable.

The battery life is quite good, and upon turning them on, the headphones audibly tell me how much of a charge is left.

“Battery fully charged.”
“Battery is at about 70%.”

They also charge up pretty quickly via a USB connection, which is included. Mine rarely go below 70%, and a quick connection to my laptop charges them to full in less than 20 minutes.

I can’t comment as to how well they work for hands free telephone usage, as I only use these to watch television, but for their appointed task, they do a great job.

I have these setup on my Roku via Bluetooth, and when I hold down the power to turn them on, my Roku automatically picks them up, and switches from the tv speakers to the headphones. Sound quality is good, and I find that the dialogue from the movies I watch comes through clearly. Really good clarity for spoken word and ambient noise. Connectivity is a breeze.

My headphones are a real workhorse for me, as I watch a lot of movies. At about $55, I think they provide great value overall.

reviews.author: Mister Jake
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.content: These Sony headphones are absolutely incredible. They have amazing battery life, some of the best I have ever seen on any device. You can adjust settings and change the equalizer on your phone. The bass is amazing. The noise cancelation is also very good. Once again Sony puts out a spectacular product. Always worth every penny. I only buy Sony electronics. #MagicPlantProductions

reviews.author: V
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 21, 2025
reviews.content:
The Sony WH-CH520 Wireless Headphones are an absolute champion in the budget headphone category, offering an unbelievable set of features for their price point.
The Good:
• Marathon Battery Life: The standout feature is the claimed 50-hour battery life, which is truly excellent and means you can go days, even a week, without needing to charge. The quick-charge feature is also a lifesaver in a pinch.
• Great Sound for the Price: The audio quality is surprisingly well-balanced and clear, especially once you use the Sony Headphones Connect App to customize the EQ settings to your liking. They handle all genres well and even feature Sony's DSEE technology to enhance compressed music files.
• Excellent Features: Features like Multipoint Connection (allowing you to connect to your phone and laptop simultaneously) and clear hands-free calling make them perfect for multitasking and daily use.
The Drawback:
• On-Ear Design: The main point of contention is the on-ear design. While they are very lightweight and comfortable for short listening sessions, the earcups sit directly on your ear, and for some users (myself included), this can lead to discomfort or a "pinch" after wearing them for more than an hour or two straight.
Verdict:
If you are looking for an extremely affordable, dependable pair of headphones with battery life that won't quit, and you mostly use them for short commutes, workouts, or calls, these are a solid 5-star purchase. If you need to wear them for 8+ hours a day, you might want to look for an over-ear model instead. They offer superb value overall.

reviews.author: Obie Cintron
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 7, 2026
reviews.content:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
The Sony WH-CH520 Wireless Headphones are fantastic! The sound quality is clear and balanced with great bass for an on-ear headset. They’re lightweight and comfortable enough to wear for hours without discomfort.
Bluetooth pairing was quick and easy, and the connection stays strong. The battery life is impressive and lasts for days on a single charge. I also love the built-in microphone—it makes calls clear and works perfectly for meetings and everyday use.
Overall, these headphones deliver excellent performance, comfort, and value. If you’re looking for reliable wireless headphones from a trusted brand, I highly recommend the Sony WH-CH520! 🎧✨

reviews.author: bruce
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 14, 2025
reviews.content:
A Huge Upgrade — The Quietest, Most Comfortable He
I just upgraded to the Bose QuietComfort Ultra (2nd Gen) after four years with my previous Bose headset—which still works great—and this new version feels like a major step forward.

What stands out immediately is the noise cancellation. My old Bose did a great job, but the Ultra model takes it to a completely new level. When I put these on, the world goes silent. On airplanes, cabin noise all but disappears. I use mine with an AirFly Pro to watch movies wirelessly, and the experience is outstanding. The sound quality is richer and more immersive. Bose’s new Spatialized Audio really does make the audio feel like it’s happening in front of you, not inside your head. Comfort is another big improvement. The materials feel more luxurious and solid. These headphones have a premium feel. I can wear them for long flights without any discomfort. The battery life is excellent, easily lasting through a full travel day.

Unlike my previous model, when you remove these headphones, they go into a low-power sleep mode. It used to be frustrating forgetting to turn off my old headphones and returning the next day with zero battery. The Bluetooth connection is smoother than my previous Bose. Pairing is almost instant, and the connection stays solid. Calls are also clearer thanks to the noise-rejecting microphones that filter out background noise.

Overall, this is the best pair of headphones I’ve ever owned. Bose kept everything I loved about my old set and improved every part of it—sound, comfort, noise cancellation, battery life, and connectivity. If you travel often or just want true peace and quiet, these are absolutely worth the upgrade. If you love hearing crisp and clear sound, this Bose headset improves the great sound I was used to and takes it to a new level.

reviews.author: Zavion
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.content:
Simply put, these are the best pair of headphones I’ve ever owned. The sound quality is phenomenal, and the noise cancellation is excellent. It can even be adjusted through a slider in the app, which is a really nice touch. The app also includes its own equalizer, with multiple sound settings and customization options depending on how you want your audio to be heard.

The touch controls on the right ear cup feel pretty futuristic, and I’m all for cool technological features like that. On top of that, they’re extremely comfortable. I can honestly see myself sleeping with them on during travel because they fit so well for long periods of time.

Battery life is fantastic too — they truly last a long time from 100% down to 0%. They also pair smoothly with multiple devices. I’ve had no issues using them with both my desktop and my phone at the same time. You’ll only get audio from one device at once, of course, but switching between them is seamless.

Overall, this headset completely exceeded my expectations, and I highly recommend it to anyone looking for high-quality headphones. And just to be clear, I’m not a robot or a paid reviewer — just a hermit who loves listening to music while putting in heavy gaming hours.

reviews.author: Kassey
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 30, 2025
reviews.content:
Aptly named Quiet Comfort. These are very light weight and super comfortable! The sound quality and noise cancelation exceeds my expectations. I put these on with nothing playing and suddenly even the most faint nuisance noises are gone - like a vacation for my ears and mind. I really love the rich look of the White Smoke and chrome version and the quality feel of the whole device.

I learned a lot about how to maximize these amazing headphones (your sound system is only as good as its weakest link).

1. iPhones and iPads don’t put out the highest quality music through their Bluetooth connections. So you can connect via the included usb-c cable OR you get this little wizard of a sound upgrade to AptX Adaptive high bit-rate music for these incredible cans.
- Questyle QCC Dongle Pro [MFi Certified] USB-C Bluetooth Audio Adapter with LDAC,aptX Lossless&aptX-Adaptive,Low Latency Bluetooth 5.4 Dongle.

2. I like waring these for hours but almost all of the over ear headphones use some sort of unbreathable material (partly for ware and partly for ANC quality), so I added these and now I can ware the Bose QC Ultra 2s for hours: Easy installation and look fantastic on the QCs (very little impact on ANC).
- WC Freeze QC Ultra – Cooling Gel Replacement Earpads

3. If you have some legacy audio gear like I do (6-cd changer, USB turntable, cassette player) that you run through a nice amplifier, you might want to add this to allow output from those to your headphones anywhere in the home. This allows for AptX AD (adaptive) for high quality audio transmission over a big range.
- 1Mii B06TX+ Long Range Bluetooth 5.2 Transmitter for TV/PC/Projector to 2 Wireless Headphones/Speakers

4. Lastly, these are beautiful headphones and I want to both have them handy and visible. This works and make connecting a charging cable pretty simple.
- New bee Headphone Stand Headset Holder Earphone Stand with Aluminum Supporting Bar.

reviews.author: Estalyn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 28, 2025
reviews.content:
I love these Bose QuietComfort Ultra headphones. I used these on an international trip and the active noise canceling (ANC) was amazing. It is truly the best. I also liked that I could toggle between ANC and "Aware Mode" with just one tap of a button so I could hear announcements or quickly speak to people at the airport when needed. The ANC is the best I've had on any headphones. It was so quiet on the plan with these headphones. It created a quiet sanctuary and blocked out any jet engine noise or chatter going on around me. This allowed me to really enjoy watching movies with them because it made the spatial surround sound really stand out. This allows you to hear the sounds the way the filmmakers intended. This Cinema Mode allows you to hear sounds spatially (sounds that are far off in the distance sound far out in the distance; sounds that are behind you sound like they are coming from behind you). The surround sound is so well executed and it's hard to believe this quality of sound is coming from headphones.

The comfort of these (and all Bose headphones) are the best I've ever worn. They are light and the cups are soft and comfortable. The band does not feel tight even on my husband's ridiculously large head. The battery lasted my entire 30 hour flight (15 hours each way) without charging. Charging is fast with USB-C. It paired seamlessly with my phone and laptop. It has its own app if you want to refine the sound but works perfectly without any adjustments. Volume control is simple: just slide your finger along the raised groove on the right cup. When you turn your headphones on, it tells you the status of the battery so you'l always know how full it is. The ear cups are replaceable. I love that it comes with its own zippered, hard case (truly a nice touch given that so many products are cutting corners and making once included accessories an option to purchase). And can we talk about how luxe these headphones look? They will definitely elevate your style. The gold and black are classic and timeless.

reviews.author: JMC
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 26, 2025
reviews.content:
This is my third Bose headphones and have yet to be disappointed. I only received this last pair, the New QuietComfort Ultra Bluetooth Headphones (2nd Gen) a few days ago but using them have been an experience. The New QuietComfort Ultra Bluetooth Headphones (2nd Gen) model, are truly a work of science and art. The sound quality is unmatched and the comfort while using them is equally impeccable.

I predominantly use the headphones while on travel and I can see that using these on a long flight are going to feel comfortable all through the flight. The few moments I was able to use I was genuinely impressed. The headphones are light weight which greatly helps the comfort. The packaging is vastly improved, and the carry case is awesome. In my previous model the carry case had no placement molding, and I constantly fumbled with loose foam pieces to keep them in place. This time Bose did a great job and the headphones just fit in the carry case without having to fumble with multiple pieces.

The active cancellation is where the science melds in with the art of the design. Putting the headphones on put me in a different place - a quiet place - ready to be able to focus and avoid unnecessary distractions. Pairing the headphones to my phone was a breeze, worked first time. One would say you should expect that, well, I have struggled before with other pairings, but not with this latest New QuietComfort Ultra Bluetooth Headphones (2nd Gen) model. The phones easily pair with multiple devices making it possible to answer the phone while at the same time listen to music or watch a movie from another device.

The only part to mention which feels like a work in progress is the app, needs work! Overall, great job Bose on the phones, keep the good work, cannot think where to suggest improvements. I certainly recommend these phones to all tech enthusiast out there and all audiophiles.

You just have to try them.

reviews.author: MxM
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content:
I owned quite a few headphones in my life in the $300–$600 price range. These are the best by far. The sound quality is superb. The bass is very deep and “even frequency response” without being overwhelming, and the built-in equalizer lets you fine-tune the response. I reduce the bass by 2 dB (or whatever the units are) and keep the rest flat. They look great, the ear pads and headband leather are very soft and comfortable, and they’re light.

The mids and highs are very natural and clean, and the soundstage and imaging are excellent. They sound good at both loud and quiet volumes. I listen to a variety of music from instrumental to dubstep to classical, and they’re great for everything. No need for a good headphone amplifier like with some of my other headphones – they sound great as-is.

The only tiny minus for me is the volume control – it’s not as precise as the rotating knob on the Apple AirPods Max. I think even buttons would be better, but it’s a very minor complaint.

Noise canceling is also great – I keep it on even at home so nothing distracts me from the music. Super happy with this purchase. I’d give it 6 stars if I could – not a lot of products impress me this much!

reviews.author: Kiarae Toki
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 22, 2025
reviews.content: So I’ve been a beats girl for years.. I use my headphones specifically for my workouts.. my review today is somewhat in comparison to my beats because I loved my beats. The only reason I made the switch to try out Bose was because the plastic piece where the ears close inward broke & exposed the wires on that entire side. My man is a Bose guy & he insisted I get Bose because they’re more durable & I’m not the gentlest when it comes to my headphones. So far.. the Bose headphones are great, app allows me to set up the sound to my liking & it was very easy to connect to my phone. The headphones fit to my head very comfortably & snug.. beats are bulky & plastic so it’s not as comfy & more stiff (but truly I didn’t mind that). I’m still getting used to the touch control, I’m so used to my beats, but I will say I love the volume swipe.. SO COOL & I all the touch controls really are simple to use & easy to learn. Little features such as the pausing of my music when I take off an ear to try & hear what someone is trying to say to me is both great & not so great. Beats doesn’t care what people are trying to say to me, lol.. no pause feature on beats. I had 4 stars for the fact that these headphones slide off your head so much & so easily! Again.. I wear these solely for my workouts.. sometimes I’m upright, sometimes I’m bending forward, sometimes I’m laying down & every time.. the headphones will fall. I’m used to my beats falling too, but not nearly as much as these. The beats are more stiff & the ears don’t turn in/out so I feel like that feature is what makes the Bose fall off all the time. My beats were more controllable, I was able to tilt them a specific way prior to my positioning & it’d stay fine, these Bose ain’t doing that. Because these fall off a lot, that initiates the “pause music” feature & that’s where that feature can be ‘not so great’ at times because then sometimes putting my headphones back on after them falling doesn’t resume music & I have to go into my phone to press play.. I’m still getting used to it though & I’m sure I’ll figure out a way for all my problems with these headphones like I did with my beats. Overall I’m still really happy with my Bose. I wish there was a “lock” feature on the ear parts so maybe that will help with how much they fall off. The sounds on these definitely beat my beats. The comfort as well! The color I got is gorgeous too!

reviews.author: J. Public
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 14, 2025
reviews.content: After many years of owning noise-cancelling headphones and earbuds from several other brands, when I saw the new QuietComfort Ultra Bluetooth Headphones (2nd Gen) were coming out, I knew I had to try them. I am so glad that I did. Packaged in a stylish, color-matched storage case, the White Smoke color is truly beautiful. There is sort of a satin sheen to the headband and ear cups, accented by a bold splash of chrome that connects everything together. The color-matched buttons are intuitively positioned, and easy to find whether in your hand, or already on your head. The headband and ear cups have a solid, yet comfortably-plush quality to them, allowing for extended use periods. It seems these should fit nearly anyone from small children to sumo wrestlers. The headphones charge quickly via USB-C, and paired easily to my iOS device, as well as my desktop PC. The inclusion of multipoint bluetooth support means I can be listening to music while connected to my desktop PC, but if a phone call comes in via my iOS device, the call will be instantly routed to the headphones. This was a much-needed feature that I am so glad to see Bose has added to the Gen 2 Ultras. Sound quality is excellent, whether listening to music, gaming, watching a movie, or taking a live phone call. I was told during several test calls that the microphones were picking up my voice very well, and that it sounded completely natural to the listeners. When you need some quiet time, the Noise Cancellation mode does an incredible job of keeping the world out. This is after all what the line is known for, and the Bose reputation for excellence has been well-earned. I have about 10 hours of use so far, and according to Bose, the headphones can last up to 30 hours before needing to be charged again. I look forward to many years of quiet enjoyment. Thanks, Bose!

reviews.author: Ari Finkelman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.content:
Honestly, I was not expecting these to be this good. The price is ridiculously low, even for cheap Chinese tech. I genuinely expected these to be complete garbage and I would end up returning them. However, I am completely shocked at their quality. No, they dont have top of the line audio, the sound quality it decent, but a little thin. The bass is a little weak, but it comes through clearly. It is easy to pair and easy to operate. They sit in my ears comfortably, as do the arms.. They also do not interfere with my glasses. Most importantly, they stay in place. Yes, I shook my head back and forth pretty quickly and they didnt feel like they moved an inch.

I will give it one minor con. While absolutely functional, the case is terribly designed. Its made of cheap plastic. I would expect it to crack if it was dropped onto a hard floor from a medium height. What makes it worse is the plastic is super smooth and slippery. I think someone realized that too late and just added some ridges in the top. This is dumb, as you dont hold it by the top. Its also molded into a shape that just feels awkward to hold in one hand. The one good thing about the case, is that it stays closed with magnets which are strong enough to hold it shut - even with some strong shaking.

reviews.author: Nayari espina
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 5, 2026
reviews.content:
I tried these wireless headphones and they really surprised me a lot. 🎧

The sound is super clear, the bass sounds amazing and the best thing is that you don't have to deal with cables. The connection is fast and stable, so you can listen to music, watch videos or answer calls without interruptions.

They are also very comfortable, perfect to use while working, exercising or simply relaxing listening to your favorite playlist.

reviews.author: Wendell doe
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026
reviews.content: i have tiny ear canals, so i couldn't buy the ones that go into the ear. these don't and they work great. i have not had any problems at all. i can hear great with these and they change quick. and they fit perfect

reviews.author: Luis Ramón Brito González
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 25, 2026
reviews.content:
Very comfortable and with excellent battery life.

These headphones pleasantly surprised me. They connect quickly, the sound is clear with good bass, and the noise cancellation is very effective in noisy environments like traffic or the gym. They are very comfortable and stay securely in place while running. The battery lasts for many hours, and the charging case with a battery level display is very convenient. For the price, they are definitely worth it.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.content: Great product! This sound is really good for this price point, the fit and comfort is also above average and it stay put even with hard work or vibrations like I do on the construction. The only downside is the range of Bluetooth is not that great, I think here you can see were you paid for or were they cut corners. But for this price point is almost impossible to get any better!

reviews.author: Heidy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 25, 2026
reviews.content: Very good sound quality and stable connection with Bluetooth 5.4. They are comfortable, do not fall out when training and the noise cancellation works quite well. The battery lasts a long time and fast charging is a plus. In addition, the case with digital indicator is very practical. Excellent value for money.

reviews.author: Edith Vazquez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content: Super...good sounds, durable charge, sturdy,, by mistake I forgot one in a jacket and washed it...after all that water I thought it had ruined but nop still working like nothing happened..

reviews.author: manlycow
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 22, 2025
reviews.content:
I needed to replace my Baseus Bowie 30 earbuds (https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0CZ8FT6TD). They have excellent ANC and the sound is good, but they didn’t seem to work as well for calls, particularly Zoom calls. Lately I noticed that one of the earbuds distorts with higher volumes, so I was looking for a replacement.

One thing I don’t like about the Bowie 30s is that they will sometimes fall out of my ears if I’m doing anything active. To resolve that, I like the design that wraps around my ears.

These are the earbuds I tried:
Beniduck I25: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0G1MHWM4H
Sajawass BX31: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FSSJRYL3
Qecnato T12: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FM3Y2HFV
Trausi I27: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FX56V8L1
Jaddsa TP01: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FTHPS8LZ
Occiam T9: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FVLWBRRD
Occiam T19: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FPFL1HJZ
Golrex T59: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FC2G85QR

Fit/seal: Without a good seal, sound quality suffers, particularly bass. If you don’t have a good seal, try the different size tips. Let’s start off by saying that due to the design, I could not get a good seal on any of the earbuds except for the Golrex T59 and the two Occiam models T9 & T19. The difference I saw was that the part of the earbud that you put in your ear extended further on these 3 models compared to the others. The Golrex T59 had the best seal for my ears. Since I couldn’t get a good seal on the others, the comments on them will be limited from here on.

Connectivity: All earbuds connected without issues, so I never had an issue with my limited time with each of them.

Sound quality: I couldn’t tell a difference between those that I couldn’t get a good seal on. If you can get a good seal with them, it would drastically change the way they sound. The Occiam T9 had better bass than the Occiam T19 but otherwise they had similar sound. The Golrex T59 had the best bass and overall tone to me, but they might even be a little too bass heavy.

Noise cancellation: I can only comment on the 3 that I could get to seal. I’m not sure that the T19 has ANC. Their website doesn’t mention it for the T19 and it didn’t seem like it from my point of view. The T19. The T9’s ANC made a little bit of difference, but not much. The Golrex was slightly better but not as good as my Baseus Bowie 30s.

Calls/Zoom meetings: I only tried this on the Occiam and Golrex. They all seemed to work well and better than my Baseus Bowie 30s.

Conclusion: If Baseus had something that wrapped around my ears and made a good seal, I would have tried it but all the earbuds with this design were open air design, which I didn’t want. The Golrex T59 was the best choice for me since the seal, sound quality and ANC were the best. I’m happy that I will also be able to use them in my Zoom meetings. I wish they had the app that my Baseus Bowie 30s have to enable tone adjustment with an equalizer and to turn ANC on/off, but I think I can manage.

reviews.author: Patrick
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 23, 2019
reviews.content:
I have a HP laptop with speakers that are less than desirable. Compound this with a lifetime of audio problems, (hard of hearing), and the speakers are pretty much useless. A set of cheap computer/desktop speakers were purchased with good results as they had a separate volume control plus a jack for headphones/earbuds. Having a small desk, It was getting crowded between the laptop, the speakers and the desk light, compounded by the fact that the whole set up is against a wall that goes out to a common hallway. This restricts my volume as I don't need to have anyone hearing what I'm doing while I am online. Thus the purchase of these headphones.
I chose headphones over earbuds as my ears easily get irritated when something is inserted. My main concern was two fold. The first is I have a large head, (size 7 5/8) and past ear protection devices have been pretty much uncomfortable both against the ears and overall. The second is perspiration as sweat build up occurs after extended wear. Being quite a while since I wore headphones, (2012) I did note an improvement but was still a bit gun shy.
To be honest, these were not my first choice. I initially saw a set of DJ headphones with a 9' cord which appealed to me for freedom of movement. For whatever reason, these were ordered instead.
These headphones came 4 days after ordering through standard, free shipping. Arriving in a soft pack with a bubble lining, the headphones were in a box enclosed within a sleeve. This made unwrapping a bit of a challenge as I wanted to save the box in case of return. Finally gaining entry, the headphones were in a bubble wrapped bag and two sets of wiring were enclosed within another. It was then I realized my error. Throwing caution to the wind, I took them out and read the enclosed the multi language manual. This was helpful as it was discovered that the ear pieces work somewhat like a person's neck. One side rotating 90 degrees counter clockwise and the other 90 degrees clockwise. Both tilted both up and down and both have extenders to adjust to the wearer. Also noted was the padding. Both ear pieces had generous padding as well as the top of the head set. It was nice to note that both sides of the ear pieces had outlets to accomodate the speaker wires. This makes it easier to plug the side in from the source thus avoiding crossover.
Two sets of wires came. The first was a red, stretchable cord with a 6.5mm plug for items like guitars and speakers on one end and a 3mm plug for the head set. The cord is 2m long but can be stretched to 3m. The second cord is a 1.2m standard cord with 3mm plugs on both ends. There is also a "control" button should the wearer have the headphones plugged into a phone.
Assembling the pieces, I plugged the 3mm into the cheap speakers and ran a gauntlet of various apps from music to gaming. WOW! WOW! WOW! A full, rich, clear sound was experienced through both earpieces despite the plug being on the right side. Every sound was heard, no matter how subtle. I did note, however, it was a bit "bassy" for my preference, but I was completely overwhelmed by the sound quality. Wondering if maybe the speakers may have some influence, I plugged the headset directly into the laptop. NOPE! No change in quality and I gained a little more cord in the process.
After playing for about two to three hours, It was pleasantly noted that the only time it was noted the headset was on was when I reached up to my head to scratch. Very lightweight, snug, yet comfortable even with glasses. No sweat experienced. No discomfort against the ears or atop the head. The cheap speakers were then moved and attached to another device that also had less than desirable speakers. It is unknown about the noise cancellation as the residence is pretty quiet with the exception of others going up and downstairs as well as the upstairs resident. Time will tell.
These are definately a keeper. Even though I have owned these less than a day, I am totally impressed with the looks, feel, and performance of these headphones. Definately a keeper and highly recommended!!!!
04/29/2019 update. These have almost constantly on my head and I have made a couple observations. 1. Despite being "full" ear headphones, they are not sound blocking. On a couple of occasions, I have been hearing knocking on my door as well as the phone ringing and someone talking, even though I couldn't understand what was spoken. Both have originated about 20 feet away. 2. the head band has a tendency to slide forward towards the forehead. I haven't figured out why yet and will post when I do. Right now I am testing a couple of theories.

reviews.author: R. Nuttall
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content:
If you’re looking for solid wired headphones without dropping a stupid amount of money, the OneOdio Hi-Res Studio Monitors are actually kind of a sleeper pick.

First off, sound quality: for the price, these hit way above their weight. The bass is punchy but not muddy, mids are clear (vocals come through clean), and the highs are crisp without being harsh. They’re not $300 studio cans, but for casual mixing, recording, gaming, or just vibing to music, they absolutely get the job done.

They get loud too like, really loud without instantly distorting. If you’re plugging into an audio interface, guitar amp, or even just your laptop/phone (with a dongle), they drive easily and sound full.

Comfort-wise, they’re surprisingly decent. The ear cups are big and padded, and the headband doesn’t feel like it’s trying to crush your skull. You can wear them for a few hours without hating your life. They do get a little warm over long sessions, but that’s pretty normal for over-ears.

Build quality feels better than you’d expect at this price point. Mostly plastic, yeah, but not cheap-feeling plastic. They fold up, the ear cups swivel (nice for one-ear monitoring), and they usually come with multiple cables — which is honestly clutch. The detachable cable setup is a big win.

Downsides? They’re wired only. No Bluetooth. And the sound profile is slightly bass-boosted, so if you’re doing super critical professional mixing, you might want something flatter. But for beginners, home studios, content creators, or anyone who just wants good sound without overthinking it — these are solid.

Bottom line:
Affordable, loud, comfortable, and way better than you’d expect for the price. Not elite-tier studio gear, but an easy recommend for everyday use or entry-level production.

reviews.author: Tiffany Shaw
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.content: I was hesitant. But these are comfortable to wear and plugs into all my devices and my drum set no problem. The audio quality is on point and perfect for my needs.

reviews.author: Jonathan C.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content:
User-friendly, light weight.

*I found the sound quality decent, but lacking on bass.

I primarily use these for hearing protection while playing the drums over pre-recorded tracks.

They work for that purpose, quite well. No automatic noise cancellation. I wanted wired headphones, as the two other pairs I have are Wireless Bluetooth 5.1 & 5.2 pairs, and they can be a pain to frequently unpair & pair, once again.

To sum up, for Wired, over-the-ear phones, these are akin to AKG (lower) headphone sound quality. Yet, they fit tighter, and thus, include natural sound cancellation ability.

reviews.author: Omar
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content: I use these all the time for playback while recording vocals and guitar, and they work perfectly. The dual-duty cable is a lifesaver—being able to switch between the 1/4" amp jack and the 3.5mm computer jack without hunting for an adapter is so convenient. The sound is clear enough for monitoring, and they are comfortable for long sessions. Definitely recommend these for anyone building a home studio!

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content: These mama jama work GREAT and the sound is amazing for such non professional headphones. I'm a music producer and have $400 headphones for the studio. and when i got these i was AMAZED at the clarity and crisp stereo sound. These live up to their reputation.... and worth a try...TRUST WHAT I SAY. I have Studio monitors that cost thousands....and these are a joy to use when listening to music or anything that you want to hear. The highs are right on, the bass is a bit much, but none the less balanced & the mids are perfect...! again i was amazed & still use to this day! Can't ask for more...! I even convenienced someone else to get a pair for relax listening! LASTLY...,ALWAYS USE WIRED HEADPHONES....STAY AWAY FROM BLUETOOTH & save your brain stem from radiation...!

reviews.author: DudeintheDesert
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026
reviews.content: A little round for my head-shape, and a tad stiff. But for the price- pretty stellar! Sound quality is really good and they look great. It's feels like 4 stars, BUT they give you a bag, two cables- one with quarter inch- which is very convenient, and two plug in options. Good stuff. Worth the money.

reviews.author: Vohn A.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 12, 2025
reviews.content:
These headphones are new to me so I haven't used them for their intended purposes, yet; I hooked them up and gave a short listen though, sounds impressive.
The included storage bags are nice as new, but they don't seem very durable. Everything fits well in the bags and the drawstrings works well.
The included cables that are provided seem destined to fail. If they had been wired with that quality of wires (too flimsy to be considered cable by me) I wouldn't have considered them. The good news is the wire provided for the use of the headphones only can easily be replaced at a modest cost for a much more durable cable if required or desired. The headphones microphone-headphone wires look, in my opinion, like they were never meant to last very long, and I have no knowledge about a replacement if it fails. I didn't buy them to use in that way so it's of no real concern to me.
5 stars for sound quality and the construction of the headphones. I doubt that you could beat the price for what they are.
If they wanted more than 4 stars from me overall they shouldn't have been kidding with the "cables"

reviews.author: Lindsey Murphy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 28, 2026
reviews.content: I had these headphones for over a year and when I tell you, these headphones are good and the battery does not drain fast at all. They are very loud and you can hear the bass they’re comfortable and they’re big. They’re a big head suddenly and they charge up pretty quickly. I recommend you get these. The design is beautiful. They are very easy to use and they are very strong. I didn’t drop these down the stairs and dropped them so many times I haven’t had no cracks or anything to connect them to your phone is very easy and you don’t have to keep connecting them because once you turn them on, it will automatically connect to your phone.

reviews.author: Marcy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviews.content: Easy to use and very comfortable my husband loves them. Especially the flexibility and how great the music sounds. Excellent price and product.

reviews.author: Ezra
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content: easy to use, good for the price, great battery life, it’s okay at noise cancellation but your music will leak out of the headphones a bit if it’s loud and other ppl will hear it. sounds good though, i recommended these to a friend and they liked it

reviews.author: Wendy Ferrie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content: I am absolutely blown away by these KVIDIO headphones and honestly cannot believe the quality for the price. The first thing I noticed was the comfort; the memory foam ear cups are incredibly soft and I can wear them for hours during work or travel without any of that annoying pressure on my ears. The sound quality is remarkably crisp with deep, punchy bass that makes my music feel immersive, and the Bluetooth connection is rock solid with no lag. Perhaps the most impressive feature is the battery life, which seems to last forever on a single charge. They are lightweight, fold up easily for my bag, and look much more expensive than they actually are. If you are looking for a reliable, high-performing pair of headphones without breaking the bank, these are an absolute home run.

reviews.author: Harold F
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content: These are great headphones once you get them set up and paired with your phone and your computer or whatever? they worked very well They're nice and snug on your head so they don't keep falling off and there's no wire helps when your moving around, I actually took a walk down the hallway from where my room is and I would say probably 100 yards and the sound stayed with me the whole time Had no trouble with reception! I would recommend these highly to anybody who would like a wireless headset These are very good! I highly recommend these!

reviews.author: myah
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 6, 2026
reviews.content: they work perfectly fine. when I turn up the volume I can't hear anything beyond the music which is what I bought them for. very comfy, connecting is super easy and the sound quality is great

reviews.author: Robert Mandeville
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.content: I love these. They work really well and for a long time between charges. I use them every morning and every evening 7 days a week. They are comfortable and I can run videos on my laptop without disturbing my wifes TV viewing, and I don't have to hear the garbage she watches on TV. At a bargain price too.

reviews.author: Just Jill
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content:
I’ve had the exact ones in black for YEARS and the ear covers finally peeled and it became messy. But as I looked to replace the covers, these were so cheap I decided to get new ones. I am keeping the black ones as back us as they still work but I keep forgetting to look for new ear covers.

IMPORTANT: They never hurt my ears because they cover them fully. This is critical because I wear these all day every weekday, 5 to 14 hours. The battery lasts the whole day.

Honestly, I’m not picky about this. I want a clean sound, not muffled or cracking and that sound just as good at high volume and these are great. I listen to music, watch movies and you tube stuff. Nothing fancy, so they’re fabulous to me.

Also, they’re super light weight and I LOVE that they fold.

Bonus! By sheer coincidence, this green was the only in stock color I liked and turns out it perfectly matches my iPad cover. I love the color in person.

reviews.author: JenLyn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 27, 2025
reviews.content: I am very happy with my JBL TUNE headphones! Once charged they have incredible battery life and they are comfortable to wear more so than my ear buds. In my opinion. As far as the quality of sound you can get better but will spend more money doing so. I am satisfied with the audio quality and the different settings like noise canceling and ambient aware. Worth the money I spent on them. The app you download on your phone is disappointing. Not much there. I am happy with my purchase. Good balance of affordability and quality, easy connectivity, and good value for the money I spent.

reviews.author: Devin Willis
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 3, 2026
reviews.content: This is my first pair of noise canceling headphones. I really like these so far. Used them at work all day and barely used 20% of the battery. The sound quality is great. Haven't used them for calls yet so I can't say on the mic quality. They are pretty comfortable, but I do wish the ear cups were slightly larger. The quality is good and the controls on the headphones work great. It also has a companion app to download to customize the EQ and voice assist functions. Overall I would def recommend these as a great entry level noise canceling headphone. For the price they are a great buy for under 100 dollars. I will definitely recommend them to all my friends and family looking for a good pair of headphones on a budget.

reviews.author: NICKY
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 18, 2024
reviews.content:
As an Apple user, I wanted great over the ear headphones with noise cancellation, but there was Absolutely No Way I was going to drop the fat stacks on the Apple ones. I started looking around and considering my options, originally deciding on some SRhythm off-brand ones here on Amazon. When I came back with the money to buy, the SRhythms were sold out so it was back to browsing. At this point, I decided it would be a better idea to go with a trusted brand that didn’t have the possibility of disappearing from Amazon within the next few years.

The ideal headphone, for me, was the Sony XM5s - after reading reviews it sounded absolutely godly. But it also costs godly amounts of dollars, which was the original problem I was trying to avoid.

Enter JBL, I realized the speaker brand that went from “a few of your buddies ride or die by this brand” to a sound-junkie staple was IDEAL. I shopped around, looked at similar Skullcandy models, other brands similar in price but also not offering anything better than passive noise cancellation.

The comparable Skullcandy model (price wise, at least) has worse battery life, primarily because it uses (imo) a ridiculous number of microphones for Active Noise Cancellation. At the time of my purchase, the Skullcandy model also costed slightly more which made even less sense.

I find the noise cancellation on these to be easily comparable to the AirPod Pros I have used before. Especially with music playing, I cannot hear a single thing outside of my little bubble. I’ve used these at work for two days straight without ever having to plug them in. They connect to my phone with ease and the buttons on the side are very useful. The audio quality is crystal clear, and I often use these in the car instead of my stereo. These things perform beautifully.
