I mean what is there to say about these? They're not BOSE quality but the audio is fine to me. They go over the ear which is my favorite style of these kinds of earbuds as they're the least likely to fall off or get lost as a result, and if they get caught on something you FEEL IT.

Also: THESE HAVE AN ACTUAL BUTTON ON THEM? I had a pair before that tried to use a "touch" button on it aka not a button but like a touch screen on a 1 inch earbud? It might have been the worst design choice I've ever seen in my life. It would say tap to pause hold to skip hold LONGER to change volume hold 0.1 seconds less long for Google Assistant, and now for the OTHER ONE...no thank you. Give me REAL PHYSICAL BUTTONS LIKE THIS ONE.

The reason they're not 5 stars is every single pair of headphones like these I have had? Eventually they stop charging. Eventually the metal charging contact points either no longer make contact or just stop being able to transmit any power. That's actually how at least 2 pairs of headphones died on me. Most are me losing just 1 headphone though.

I mean what can I say they're cheap bluetooth headphones that work really well until they either suddenly stop charging or until you (I) lose them!

The comfort of these is middle of the road. I've had much more comfortable over the ear earbuds and much less comfortable ones. These are middle of the road.

Buy them if you need a pair headphones and don't like spending hundreds of dollars for something that's very likely to get lost (at least in my experience: they're tiny pieces of plastic you put in your ear its extremely impressive to me if you are someone who has had the same earbud headphones for MULTIPLE years and congrats to you. The rest of us though?

Buy these. They're usually on sale and also have a coupon on top of that. Buy them just to have a second set.

reviews.author: elizabeth mitchell
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content: Used them for a month now. No complaints! They hold charge for a long time! Stays put in my ear doing full movement exercises at the gym. The case is sturdy and functional. Good sound quality. Great for the price.

reviews.author: David
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.content:
Way better quality than I though they would be. I bought these around two years ago I think for basically nothing and never regretted it. The sound quality is above average but what these things really have going for them is their durability.

I have dropped, thrown, stepped on, and more and there has been no noticeable dip in quality.

At one point I actually forgot to take them out and went swimming with one in, it fell out of my ear, sank to the bottom of the pool, and when I finally realized what happened an hour later and retrieved it it was still playing music!

After drying it off and there was no long term damage aside from being slightly lower in volume, which is fine because I typically only have one in at a time anyways.

Combine that with the insane battery life and quick recharge speeds and you have a very high quality product.

My only complaint is that the lid to the needs to be secured better, as it is when you drop the case it opens and the earbuds themselves go flying.

reviews.author: Carinn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2026
reviews.content:
Comfortable fit and the adjustable ear pads come
In handy to find the perfect one for your ears. Easy to use, have good battery life, and the case is nice to keep track of them. We have 3 pairs in our house so the multiple color options have really come in handy!

reviews.author: Rachel Elliott
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2026
reviews.content:
So, I do have a few grievances, but the pros outweigh the cons by a lot.

Pros:
Literally lasts all day(no seriously. They do)
Charges fast.
Case holds two full charges by my estimate, perfect for listening to audiobooks all day.
Pretty color options, rose gold is really pretty
Nice case, and battery display.
Good sound
Really good base
Comfy if you wear them right.
Cons:
when someone calls you the ringtone goes up to maximum. I wish they would fix that.
Base is actually too strong. Turn on base reduction, or leave it on and play phantom of the opera full blast.
Setup was hard but I was also being an idiot about it.

Literally only lost the fifth star because of the calling volume issue when someone tries to call me. Heavily recommend these for people trying to get away from glitchy AirPods.

reviews.author: Christopher Pfaff
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content: Very comfortable. I wear glasses and these do not interfere with them over the ears. No one even see’s Im wearing them. Very light weight, sound is impeccable, highly recommend. The battery last an 8+ hour shift and has a great range. Works great with my iphone.

reviews.author: Joe Engineer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.content:
At first I thought these were too expensive for what they were so I went through many pairs of cheap Bluetooth earbuds and headphones thinking I would find an equal. All of them either had terrible battery life, Bluetooth reception, or the tiny spring loaded charging contacts would fail.

After throwing out many pairs mid run in frustration I decided to pick these up on a sale and never looked back. Awesome battery life, great Bluetooth reception that doesn't cut out if I turn my head, decent sound while still allowing me to hear around me, and super comfortable. These are unbeatable on long runs. Highly recommend.

reviews.author: Shae
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 16, 2025
reviews.content:
Quick summary:
- Comfortable to wear all day.
- Feasible to wear all day in all situations since it does not block your hearing and is waterproofed.
- Has no problem staying in position in nearly all situations.
- Battery lasts all day (I regularly end the day around ~80% battery left).
- Bluetooth multipoint is supported. Check the Shokz website for a video showing how to enable it. You must hold the on and multifunction button at the same time to enable multipoint.
- Use their sizing guide video to find the correct size before purchase. I bought the normal size, then had to buy the mini instead. If you need the mini but buy the normal size then it will push on the back of your ears uncomfortably.
- Standard USB-C charging means one fewer charger. I usually charge it in the morning while starting my day.
- Quick charging.
- Multifunction button is a physical button, so it's hard to activate it by accident.
- Sound is reasonable for listening to music and making calls. Microphone is reasonable. Look into how bone conduction sounds to get an idea of what to expect. I'm comfortable using these for music, games, and calls unless I'm wanting exquisite audio quality.

My story:
I purchased these because the idea of having a pair of headphones I could wear all day was appealing to me. I am the kind of person that prefers to never play videos etc. out loud, which usually results in me never seeing any content with sound. Wearing other earbuds or headphones all day isn't feasible because other headphones block out surrounding sounds and fall out/off easily. Unfortunately, I'm also the kind of person who is significantly motivated by music. What a bad combination! With these I'm able to put them on in the morning and only take them off at night. Bluetooth multipoint is a nice bonus, as it allows me to seamlessly switch between my phone and my computer.

Generally, these sound as if a speaker in the room is playing whatever you're listening to. Just like a real speaker in the room, you can turn it up to drown out surrounding sound or turn it down so surrounding sound takes over. I can comfortably play music during a conversation and still hear the conversation. I can also comfortably play music while shopping or while driving and still be able to hear my surroundings i.e. it's no different from a speaker in the store or car playing music.

I have used these for personal and work calls and they function well for that purpose. I have had no complaints about mic quality. With long hair, people often don't realize I'm even wearing these unless my hair is up. I'm able to wear these fine with both glasses and a mask. These usually comfortably rest slightly above the ear outside of my glasses. This position reduces the strain on my ears.

All in all, if you need an all-day wear pair of headphones for general listening and calls, these are an excellent choice.

reviews.author: F. Thies
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.content:
I had a pair of the larger Shokz but they didn’t fit as well as the mini. The band is closer to my head and more comfortable. They sound great and I forget I have them on. They work great for phone conversations which is the primary reason I purchased them. They are a super light and sound amazing. No clunky cover your ear type headphones. I will buy them again!

Highly recommend. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

reviews.author: Damion
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 13, 2025
reviews.content:
Needed a low temp replacement for the AirPods which become increasingly unreliable and quarrelsome as the temp drops and hats, hoods, and gloves join the party. The OpenRuns have been happy at -10° F (so far), but moreso have reminded me how nice life can be without dealing with independently needy nano-battery wireless devices and additional charging case.

The interesting surprise has been how much I enjoy them inside and at the computer. Not having your ears full is helpful outdoors, but indoors I appreciate the off-the-ear comfort, ambient awareness and zero fiddling required for short communications. Turns out I prefer working alongside its "speaker in the room" vibe vs the more attention-grabbing intimacy of traditional headphones. Sound & calls are plenty good, and the unfussy device exceeded expectations.

-1 for a brand new device not to work with the Shokz App and the non-UI button dance for a connected device. Manual off/on and device switching has been less than effortless, but I'm still learning and expect I'll adapt fine.

reviews.author: me
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 14, 2026
reviews.content:
Huge fan of these. Good battery life, sound is good, stay on my head just fine, no nasty headphones anymore.

I use these for the gym and outdoor walks mainly but have used them for car rides and around the house activities as well which is nice because I can listen to a podcast, do dishes, and not be dead/deaf to the world

I do notice sometimes with higher volume there can be a little vibration but the pros FAR outweigh and cons.

I’ve never spent more than $20 on headphones so this was big for me. Well worth it!

I don’t think I’ll ever use a different style.

reviews.author: Theo L
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content: I had an older version of these, but I'm really impressed with this latest generation. The sound quality is really great, and the control buttons are simple and easy to use. I also appreciate how they don't move around as I'm running; they fit very nicely. They feel well-made and sturdy and I don't have to worry about them breaking.

reviews.author: Beach Catz
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content: 2nd pair only because I misplace mine in my home and always use these. Now I have 2 for easy access. I love the weight of these, light, comfortable over my ears, nice fit, and easy to hear sound around me while I’m listening to something on my phone when I’m taking a walk or bike ride. The charge lasts quite long. Several of my friends have tried mine and threw out their old “in the ear” style earphones. Now they use these, too.

reviews.author: david
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2026
reviews.content:
Made me say wow. jbl customer for life, because as you see in reviews they do their job. I still have two old ones beat up but still work. Long battery life, perfect connection, durable. Same sound quality and everything else from day one till the end which I am not even sure what will break them, they withstand lots of abuse.

I have a cheap pair 30$ that were pretty good, just a cheap plastic design that broke when they fell. They still were usable though. The sound quality was cheaper but still ok, I didnt think to get new headphones right away. These were over the head style but on your ear pressed.

I also had a more expensive $300 one that was cup style more comfy but the sound wasnt much better. Its the functionality that sold me over time. I cant imagine dealing with simple issues like connection or breaking with just careful use, poor sound, buzzing and list goes on. JBL doesnt ever have that.

I did some research before getting these to find I love sub bass that shakes your ears which they delivered. It made me say wow. Ok so I tried listening to all music like rock, electric dance music and pop. Its more clear and better quality still but interestingly, if it doesnt have sub bass it doesnt bring out the best of these headphones 770nc.

I got it just for the sub bass though so if you are into that, its worth the hype. Its in a lot of genres of rap. It performs beyond expectation with the functionaity and durability of jbl. It really does make me say wow. I doubt that skull candy or even dr dre beats sound that good, you should really try these out.

reviews.author: Irish Kesterson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 6, 2025
reviews.content:
These are amazing headphones. I had tried out competitor headphones and was thoroughly not impressed with their low performance sound. I had read multiple reviews on these talking about how they had muddy sounding bass tones, and I do not find that factual at all. As a matter of fact, these things have such a smart application that comes with them. It will test your hearing and then set up the headphones to give you the best sound your ears can hear and up all the different frequencies so that whatever you’re hearing range is you hear the sounds clearly and it doesn’t drown them out. They still hit with solid bass and I can clearly hear every guitar note and the snare drum as if I’m playing my own instruments. The beautiful part is my wife can stand 5 feet in front of me and nag away and I can’t hear a word she says even at low volume because of the active noise cancellation they do and they also have another setting that allows some ambient sound to be heard so if you do need to hear things around you you still can. And within the application you can increase or reduce how much is blocked out on both settings. It’s also got a talk through setting so if you are in an environment where you will be speaking back to someone it lets you hear your voice through the headphones so you aren’t yelling in a quiet office setting because someone asked you a question or you attempt to sing along with Queen horrifying your close neighbors. They will block out all outside noise if I want them to block them out then it’s beautiful blissful listening time finally happens. I can write or music play music. I can hear every tone every instrument everything I want to hear is crystal clear through them.

I highly recommend these headphones over the beats Three or fours. They are not uncomfortable for me to wear. They don’t squish my head like the beats did. Which the beats lasted all of two weeks and I returned them for these because I was getting a headache from the pressure of wearing the beats. the absolute only thing that these lack is a pretty carrying case. And quite honestly whoop-de-do. I’ll take the much higher quality sound over a little carry case that I can go buy somewhere separately. they charge fast and so far I haven’t even noticed a dip in their battery after listening for an entire day. I am hooked on these headphones. And quite honestly, I am an audio junkie so if that carries any weight with any of you, I am as picky as it comes for sound and these are absolutely amazing. They make my son‘s AirPods look like something he got at Walmart for 15 bucks..

reviews.author: SLH
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026
reviews.content: The sound and fit are great. Seamlessly works with different devices. Easy to personalize experience and easy to set up. Noise cancelling feature works well. Can set up sound for video or audio. Well made

reviews.author: Brad's Mom
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 8, 2026
reviews.content:
The quality of sound is great - they are JBL!
Great price too! They are very comfortable and the charge lasts a long time. The headphones also seem very durable.

I normally )l0
have problems with things on my head (headaches) - can't wear hats, and have never been able to use headphones. Ear buds don't fit properly neither, always falling out of my ear. But I can wear these headphones for long periods of time with no issues. They are comfortable over my head and on my ears as well.

I would definitely buy these again.

reviews.author: Heather Lynn Hawley
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 23, 2025
reviews.content: I bought these for their positive noise cancelling reviews, over the ear fit, and price point. I really love these, and they weren’t too tight for me. I tried them out in the store and loved them! I had purchased the 670NC Lives before these, but they were too tight on my ears and hurt after about an hour of use. I do feel that the on-ear 670NCs were better in their noise cancelling abilities, but these are not far behind. The earphones sat perfectly on my ears on the smallest setting, and weren’t too tight. The sound quality and noise cancellation are really nice, and the build quality seems sturdy as well! They look sharp and are compact. Easy to use buttons and gestures. Would highly recommend!

reviews.author: Emily brown
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content: I've bought a lot of other headphones cheaper ones and I finally decided to buy higher quality ones these are by far the best headphones on the market the sound quality the battery life and the noise cancelling 10 out of 10 highly highly recommend they don't feel heavy on your head very clear sound easy to connect to your phone tv etc and the design is perfect very durable feeling headphones they will last a long time so if your looking for a good set of headphones these are the ones to get highly recommend

reviews.author: Andrew
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 13, 2026
reviews.content: Absolutely fantastic!!! Quality headphones for the price you pay. I went with the Live 770Nc due to others reviews and I’m quite happy with my purchase. They connect very easily to my iPhone 16 Pro max. The build quality of the headset is nice too, they don’t feel heavy on my head which is ideal for longer listening times. The ear cups are soft and aren’t uncomfortable while wearing glasses or sunglasses. After a little EQ work this headset has crisp highs, densely compressed lows, and a very solid mid range. The app to pair with the set is also quite convenient to use and very user friendly. If you’re on the fence of the LIVE vs TUNE……….spend a little extra and go with the Live 770’s.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026
reviews.content: These JBL headphones are amazing! They fit snug enough that they won’t fall off if you go for a jog but not too tight that long usage will hurt your ears. The sound quality is what you would expect. The bass is mind blowing but the highs are super crisp as well. Super easy to connect to your phone, it took me about 5 seconds. I purchased the classic all black ones but you can’t go wrong with any other color. There is an app that you can download too which has different settings you can mess with that make these headphones even more dynamic. The price is definitely worth the purchase.

reviews.author: Harbor S
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 18, 2019
reviews.content:
The Skull Candy Crusher Wireless is a great set of cans for multiple uses. Let me say, first, we have bought 3 pairs of these in less than a year. First pair was for our 15 yo son, second pair was for my husband, and the third pair I finally bought for myself (early Xmas gift). The fact we have 3 pairs of these should say it all! All 3 pairs we bought are in black. Too many reviews had the white pair breaking, so with this last purchase, I felt there were not enough reviews with enough time on them for the new colors to make me feel confident they, too, would not break in the same spot as the white ones. So, black it was. One can never go wrong with black in headphones!

The first pair was bought in early May, for a 15 yo who is not always super careful with his stuff. They've been dropped, and used every single day by him and are still working perfectly, still charge just as fast and the battery still lasts forever. They have held up to his clumsiness and look as if we bought them yesterday, though they've been dropped and dinged here and there. My husband got his the 3rd week in May and he uses his constantly. He works from home and is on the phone a LOT. Conference calls, broadcasts (radio/YouTube for interviews), and phone calls/video calls with friends both here in the States and globally. As for my husband's use - they DO work with Skype on a cell - we have Note 9s, and they DO work with Skype via the computer (for reference, he has an Asus Rog). He had to go into Skype and tell it to recognize these particular cans. They didn't work at first, but he did finally get them to. They work on all of our other call apps, WhatsApp, Wire, Signal, VSee Etc. They work with our regular phone calls beautifully too. They also connect to our Bluetooth unit we have hooked into our TV. They both connect even though we have the same headphones. We had some trouble getting 2 identical headphones connecting at first. One would automatically pick up, then we had to activate pairing mode on the headphones then press our bluetooth button on the tv unit and then it found them. Took a day to figure it out, but we did it lol! They work AWESOME with that! If you turn the bass all the way up or 3/4 of the way up, I swear, it seriously feels like you are sitting in a movie theater. The sound quality is awesome for watching tv. Often, one of us will fall asleep and to avoid waking the other, tv gets muted and headphones go on. Now, we use them even when we are both awake because they make for a great experience with the upgraded sound for the tv! Our son just keeps them connected to his phone or he uses the auxiliary cord and plugs them into his switch when he plays it. Sure beats ear buds. Plus, our son says earbuds are super uncomfortable for him and refuses to use them, so that's why we initially bought these for him.

The battery life is CRAZY GOOD! This is one of the reasons we went with these 3 times. They charge up from fully drained fairly quick (a few hours) and the battery lasts FOREVER. This battery, by far, is the best out there. Nothing else to say - battery life is AMAZING.

Side note - they sound even better when you go in to your cell and tweak the audio settings to your liking. On our Notes, we both had to download a good EQ/audio app and we tweaked from there. He did the same thing on his laptop, and again, amazing sound. Hes a bit of an audiophile, and while no - these aren't the top cans for an audiophile, but he is very impressed with the sound (music/tv) for the price. He actually loves these and prefers these cans for calls, watching TV, and listening to music over his top dollar Sony Noise Cancelling headphones and over his even higher priced Sennheisers. He got these because he needed a good pair with a good mic and sound for calls (as people were complaining over hearing him with the Sonys), and true cans for audiophiles, well, you can't really use them for phone calls lol. He has an expensive pair of Sennheiser cans, and once tweaked, honestly, these sounded better to me than the $400 ones. But - my ears are super sensitive to treble and it hurts my ears. So the highs not being very pronounced with these is perfect for me. The mids are definitely recognizable and neither of us have any issue with sound nor do we miss anything. When watching tv you pick up on all of the little background things you never notice. With music, you can actually hear the singer take the smallest of breaths where you couldn't before. Again, ours are tweaked for our phones and computers, BUT they are NOT tweaked for our tv, it's impossible there and we still catch every little thing.

So far, the hinges are holding up. Before we purchased these for our son, we actually went to Best Buy and looked at all of the models and different brands. Skull Candy, overall, were the best for the money and comfort out of all their in-store models. When we compared these to the Venues, we went with these because they feel far more substantial than the Venues. These are heavier and feel/seem to be made sturdier than the Venues. It seems to me the only differences in the two models are - ear pieces twist out on the Venue not on the Crusher, Venue has noise cancelling and Crushers do not, Crusher has the added bass adjustment & Venues don't, and finally, the Crusher seems far strudier/a bit heavier/better made than the Venues and are cheaper than the Venues.

I dont think these would be ideal for jogging or running...but I dont run anymore unless it's for my life - so, I dont run. Lol. If you put them on and tap the outside of the can, you can hear it "reverberating". And I've seen other reviews about hearing that while running due to the jolt of the "bouncing" of the body while jogging. That would irritate me. They are a little heavy, but you get used to that quickly and they are super comfy so the weight never was a bother to to me nor my husband or son. We all wear glasses/contacts. They do not hurt my ears where my earpieces are. The cups are super cushy and they only get softer/better over time. Keep in mind, these are NOT noise cancelling. But, my husband often listens to loud music 2 feet away from me and I can't say I can readily identify what he's listening to unless I got closer, so again, it's not a noticeable issue for me. When our toddler comes and crawls into bed with us, if we are watching tv, we have them on pretty loud, and it doesn't bother her a bit, even when she's got her head on my arm. Noises bother her if she's trying to go to sleep. She would, very loudly, have said something by now if she heard any sound from the headphones, or heard it loud enough. She has not.

I have paid 3 different prices for these each time I purchased them. 120 for our son, 108 for hubs, and I picked them up on Cyber Monday for 79.99. Even at 120, I would buy them again and would've happily paid it for the 2nd and 3rd pairs. They are holding up really well, have great sound, and fold up for smaller storage. They come with a nice little bag that holds them, but if you want any kind of real protection so you can throw them in a bag and go, I would invest in a hard shell case for them. The bag won't protect them other than keeping them put away when not in use at home. The charge cord is good and seems to be good quality and the aux cord it comes with is really nice! The part under the jack is like a box and causes less stress on the jack and plug so it wont short out as easy. I really love the design of the auxillary cord.

If you aren't too terribly rough on headphones and plan on using these for a range of things - like calls, music, & hooking into the tv - these are DEFINITELY the cans for you! They are WELL worth their price, whatever that may be. I would just invest in a hard shell case and take care of them. Most of the breakage issues I saw in reviews were with the white ones. Other colors, in my opinion, dont have enough reviews with enough time in use to be confident that they wont do the same thing the white ones seems to do. We have had zero issues with our 3 sets in black, with one son who isnt always as careful as he should be with the more expensive stuff he wants lol, and they've held up to him perfectly. These are definitely worth your hard earned money! Skull Candy did good with these!!! I would give them my money again if I needed a 4th pair for any reason. Just go on and buy them!!!!

reviews.author: Frequent buyer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 19, 2017
reviews.content:
I just got these headphones today and am loving them! They are definitely for people who love bass which is the main reason i got them. Hey anybody who knows skullcandy knows when they started they were bass heavy. Ive always had there wired earbuds but i tested the wired crushers and the hesh and they were good but nothing like these. The bass is like being in a car with a hard hitting system no joke its awesome but i cant imagine anyone who would listen to these with the bass all the way up it wouldnt be good for your hearing. I can stand max volume on these but not volume and bass there's no need. I just went through a set of sony bt850 extra bass and while id say the max volume is close maybe even a tiny bit louder on the sonys, the overall experience goes to the crushers with that bass not to mention the sonys will break at the hinges in time unless maybe your super gentle with them and not use them everyday but me, i need something more reliable and pivoting plastic hinges are bound to break (sony).. plus if you read reviews on the sonys they break on alot of people and i havnt read one for skullcandy breaking. I hope not becuz i do lawn maintenance and i need something a little more sturdy and i think these will serve the purpose for a while. These are a nice fit on my head, kinda tight but that means they'll stay put better. I did notice discomfort after wearing for a couple hours but not as much as the sonys plus the sonys didnt stay put so good during workouts or quick movement. Another thing about these thats great is the battery. 40hrs? Omg thats great cuz i hate charging! You get a verbal notification when bettery is low about 10mins i would guess before i ran out and i can say listening to these maxed out i got a good 20 or 25hrs on one charge over 3 days. Button placement is nice and easy. Design and looks are nice and sleek. Nothing cheap(quality) about these at a price of 140 the only reason i didnt buy them last year is because they were more expensive and 80bucks for the refurb sonys were more in my budget. By the way i got 8mo out of the sonys. These i got a replacement plan because accidents happen!

Update day 4 with headphones...
So first i want to add a couple things i didnt mention because when i 1st used them i was so hyped up from listening to that bass.. ok so these are 140$$ headphones and while i don't expect them to be studio quality at this price nor have active noise cancellation. Anyway to retouch on the issue of the max volume.. yea these crushers deffinetly are not as loud as i thought they would be. Im just guessing here but to paint a picture lets say the crushers put out 100db of loudness compared with the lg tone 1st gen i think 830's or my sony xb850bt would put out 130db of loudness. Now i know those arnt the actual rating but im just setting a example and alot if people probly wont use them at max levels but i do because the majority of my listening time is at work with lawnmowers and such so i can deffinetly tell the lack of volume but i will say if im not using heavy equipment then there's no need for max volume they are plenty load at 75% Another thing is people talking about memory foam ear pads. ???? Where? Not on my crushers. Id say quality foam but not memory foam. Memory foam is very dense even when warm or hot theres no mistaking it even if its cheap memory foam still very dense but itll feel more like foam to where quality memory foam feels more like a sponge that takes a while to decompress. But thats ok im fine with it just wanted to add my opinion because i read alot of reviews people saying memory foam. Still very comfy anyway. So thats just the cons that i have. Overall i wouldnt change my mind on buying these because they just hit so hard and sound great. Its not muddled with to much bass, you can hear every word perfectly its just the bass can be enhanced more then mids and highs which i love. Build design feels solid.. so thats my update! Ill keep u guys informed if something else happens or i forgot something

reviews.author: Fawaz
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 15, 2021
reviews.content:
I am a student, and as you know I will be in a budget constraint of 100 dollars when it comes to headphones (ik its a lot but the only thing I spend is in headphones so let me off lmao). I am your average customer who is into music than anything else. I too watch YouTube and many review websites to buy a product so I think my review might be of help to you. Firstly, hats off to Skullcandy for this masterpiece. I love this headphone so much since it arrived and the music is insanely good in this. If ur into bass and songs like hip hop and rap, don't think twice this is for you!! I may not be an audiophile, but you are not gonna be a music producer to look into frequencies and stuff (if you are then sorry this is not for you XD), and all you need is a headphone that can play your favorite songs. I cycle daily and this isolates the external sounds without any NOICE CANCELLATION. Moving forward, Let's discuss the pros and cons:

Pros:-
1. The ear cups are of good quality and can keep your ears comfortable for hours (the maximum I listened to was about six hours and have not felt any irritation or pain).
2. My ears are kinda big and the ear cups in this is big enough to fit all of my ear.
3. The bass is good and not disturbing the music in any way. I keep my bass slider in mid level, the higher you go the more you feel like your experiencing an earthquake. (Caution: At higher bass levels, the bass would interfere with your music a bit)
3. The overall build quality is durable and DOES NOT GIVE THE PLASTICKY FEEL.
4. The buttons are placed in a good position, I can comfortably change the volume without straining my hand (the other headphones that I tried was terrible in button positioning).
5. Battery is just FREAKING AMAZING! I charge it like once a month so, the tension of carrying a charger is dropped.

Cons:-
1. For prolonged uses, the ears might start to feel uncomfortable and you might experience a little pain (prolonged uses meaning, 6-7 hours)
2. The wires are exposed so, MIGHT get cut easily by any sharp object. Not necessarily a con but if you are a person who does not take care of their items, sorry this might be of a concerning issue (I take good care of mine and the wires are not touching your head in any way, so you can drop the thought of it irritating you while listening).
3. I am still confused about the microphone in this, like whether it works or not, or is it even there in the first place, no idea at all. (I will update you all after I find about the microphone).
4. Thats it, what ya looking for?!

Lastly, environments may differ from person to person. Any product, please do research well and then proceed forward to buy it. Hope this helps!

reviews.author: John D
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 23, 2018
reviews.content:
Whenever I got these almost 2 years ago, I was lucky enough to catch a deal at 150 dollars down from the original 199.

Fast forward to now, I was curious to see what black friday deals were going for these. 99 dollars is a steal just for the sound quality and battery life alone. I'm going on 2 years of fairly heavy use (I have these on all the time in my house except sleeping) and I often use them as a hairband doing stuff around the house if we're being honest. The range is exceptional. I had very low expectations whenever it came to the range, but after investing in one of the higher grade usb dongles (only 12 bucks or so) I can get signal through 3-4 walls 40 feet away consistently. Other bluetooth devices I had issues going through a single wall and resyncing properly.

That being said, while these look rugged they will develop hairline cracks around the housing that holds the cans even if you baby them. It's fully plastic from the cans to the steel band in the headband. While I was first irritated with the build quality, I began to learn just how to combat this common issue and currently feel much better with everything from the fit to the durability of them since. You will learn very quickly upon learning of an issue with a skullcandy product that the warranty has a lot of loopholes............so anything other than a complete technical failure of a feature is not covered whatsoever. Moreso, I believe there's even shipping that a customer would have to pay for pending a replacement or fix of an above mentioned technical failure.

I tried superglue, I wasn't concerned with the cosmetic look, it didn't work. I tried using sparingly amounts of jbweld (5 dollars for 2x1oz tubes of steel reinforced epoxy) that would work temporarily until eventually I had a complete separation as seen in many of those pictures on this page. That's when I just unapologetically gooped the jbweld all over the joint. It worked so well that a week later I intentional broke the other side (that I was having to baby for fear of breakage) just to goop it up with jbweld. Going for a rough break I was able to completely fuse all of those problem parts that tend to break, and I wish I would have done it sooner. It took time and a lot of nerves to get through, but it was well worth it.

I ended up completely caking the joint and letting it cure carefully (because it's pretty rigid after using it) two different times on each side to ensure it was able to fully cure and have enough support to keep the joint straight. It's been 6 months or so since I "fixed" them and I'm not worried in the least about them breaking again.

Another issue that could be unique to my headphones but I wanted to mention is the haptic bass switch. I've seen other reports of the switch being loose feeling and finicky. I've had the same issue from early on with my pair. I believe it's fairly common and remedied with patience, but I try my best to keep the switch from fully engaged or fully disengaged as there seems to be some disconnect of the wires if you're not careful. Also, it's worth mentioning to use the enhancements and "bass boost" in options if you feel like you can't find a good balance of haptic bass using the switch. Personally I have it set to boost 80hz and below by 3 db, and that lets the switch cover all the bases (puns).

I'm glad I have these crushers rather than the hesh 2 bluetooth design for sound quality and comfort, but if you wanted to have something to use in the gym and toss in a bag or to keep in your car without worrying about breaking them, I would definitely go with the hesh 2 variety. The bluetooth hadn't come out yet whenever I got my hesh 2s but I've had them for 4 years and they're still a functional semi-comfortable wired headphones with decent sound. If you're on the go I wouldn't want to have the crushers on anyways because they do block so much ambient sound.

In closing, if I felt like my current pair wouldn't stand the test of time I'd buy 2 at this price today just for the love of the sound quality, ear comfort, passive noise canceling, and powerful-and-versatile haptic bass. If you're a fan of chillstep, futurebass, or trance music these are a godsend. Other than the general build quality the only other thing I could see skullcandy on doing to improve these headphones is selling an accessory that turned the headset into a pillow you could sleep in, they're that comfortable.

Instead of some "insurance plan" that you'll never qualify for or want to mess with, I'd instead buy some jbweld that matches the finish you buy and remember to check for cracks before they get out of hand or hard to manage everytime you plug in to charge them. The battery lasts for a LONG time so that's only like once every 2-4 weeks.

reviews.author: Phil Kappe
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 3, 2018
reviews.content:
I would like to preface this by saying I don't write many reviews... I know I should, but this one deserved it.

I have gone through my fair share of headphones. When it came to "in-ear" headphones, I couldn't find anything that compared with Skullcandy Ink'd and have gone through many pairs of those (mostly had to buy new ones because of my own stupidity). Back in high school and a little after that time I would always use Koss UR/20's since they were reasonably priced and sounded great.

My current job has me listening to my headphones 8 hours a day, so I wanted a good pair of quality over-the-ear headphones. I looked at tons of different brands: Beats, Sony, Koss, Bose, etc. I had some criteria these headphones needed to meet:

- Comfortable wearing for long periods of time.
- Bluetooth with decent battery life.
- Able to plug in wired in case I needed to (battery dies, better quality, whatever it might be).
- Adjustable (I have a big head).
- Durable.
- Excellent range between highs, mids, and lows.
- Foldable, or easily transportable.

I can say without a doubt the SkullCandy Crusher's nailed all of them!

I have had them for a week now and listened to them well over 40 hours and I am in absolute love with them! Not to mention I haven't charged the battery yet, yes that's right, I have listened to these bad boys for over 40 hours on a single charge!

Now let me talk about the sound quality, I have never owned a pair of headphones with this much depth, especially when it comes to bass. The fact that I can adjust the bass by using a slider on the headphones is phenomenal! Whether I am listening to gaming music, trap, dubstep, rap, country, classical, rock, or even watching a movie, these things let me feel so immersed in what I am listening to, that most of the time I forget about those around me. I have heard others compare the bass to being something like a sub in the trunk of your car, that is honestly the best comparison I could come up with.. Maybe the bass you experience in a high quality movie theatre, but damn close either way. You would think the mids and highs would get drowned out with that much bass, well you would be right, but it's barely noticeable. I play all music right between 1/4 - 1/2 bass and I really don't need any more.. If you want to feel your head vibrate, crank these things up and you will get an ear massage... literally, your ears will tingle if you do. Beats have nothing on these in my opinion, and cost half as much.

If I had two heads, I would buy two pairs of these. I left these overnight at work, and yearned to have them on my head again. I have an 8 hour plane trip next week, and I can't wait to have these things with me the whole time.

If you are looking for a quality set of headphones that won't fail on being amazing in every aspect, you need not look any further. Buy these bad boys and you won't want to listen to

reviews.author: Saul Torres Gonzalez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.content: Great sound and quality

reviews.author: Adrian Pedraza
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 1, 2018
reviews.content:
I am usually one to write either a really good review or a really bad review for anything if it is EARNED either way. These headphones have EARNED my VERY GOOD review!

First off, as most people will say, they are an excellent investment for the price. Overall, the look, quality of build, sound, battery, comfort, etc all met or far exceeded my expectations.

The battery is probably the best feature. I got mine delivered at the end of April 2018 and JUST NOW (September 1, 2018) needed to charge them. AMAZING! Granted, I only use them when I am doing something art related or playing video games but that can be an average of about an hour or two every night. So the battery is 5 stars without question.

The comfort level of the ear pads are 5 stars as well. They are not too bulky and feel super soft and retain their original shape after taking them off. I have had BEATS in the past as well and I am not really one for Apple products as a whole. Definitely one-up on BEATS on-ear models. I would wear my beats and my ears would hurt after 30 minutes. Not a problem here.

The construction is very nice. I love the weight and the matte finish. The reviews that you read about these headphones being cheaply made are of no concern. I think you'll find that most people who are always complaining that things are cheaply made or always breaking on them are the types of people who don't know how to take basic care of nice things. Just sayin'. If you don't want them to break or snap or short out when they get wet, then don't give them to your 2-year-old to play with, don't wear them in the rain, and basically don't handle them like an newborn ape! Or at least explain why they broke rather than say "THAY CRACKED AN THAY SUK!"

The sound quality is great. These are a nice compromise to BEATS and definitely win over them in a lot of aspects. I will say thought that I can understand how people say the BASS switch makes them seem like the loud cars that pass your house late at night with their stereos rumbling the windows of every house on the block! These guys really rumble. BUT...I will say this. I listen to many different types of music (metal, rap, jazz, NOT country, etc.) They really affect different types of music different ways, WHICH IS COOL. "The power is YOURS" -Captain Planet. I was expecting to listen to Metallica's album "...And Justice For All" and hear a clear bass that has been missing. That was not the case. I was a little disappointed there. But when I listened to Mastodon's album Leviathan, specifically the song "Blood and Thunder" it really helped bring some beautiful color to the drums. LOVED IT. If you are not a metal fan, listen to something you like. Mess with the volume and the bass. You'll find that doesn't really do anything for classical music but the drums, or beats, on something drum or beat driven can really be manipulated alot better. That is definitely something that BEATS DO NOT HAVE! Plus, if you like gaming (ie. Battlefield, COD, etc) the gunshots and sounds or war really sound real!

I hope you have learned alot and enjoyed my guided tour of the Skullcandy Crusher Headphones from a standard consumer's viewpoint. All in all, return them if you think I'm wrong. Do you, dude! but you won't be disappointed if you have heeded my words! Go for it! Buy 'em! Do it! DO IT NOW!

reviews.author: ZeeTek
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 19, 2019
reviews.content:
First and foremost; I do electronics reviews for numerous different sellers here on amazon, but this is not one of them.

I simply love these headphones and occasionally actually decide to chose them over my $350 Sony WH-1000XM3 flagships. These headphones have the very unique feature on the left ear cup where you can control the bass output of any sound. This feature is very cool but it is NOT why I decided to buy these head phones and try them out.

The primary reason I decided to give these headphones a shot was for 3 reasons:
1.) I wanted a wireless pair of headphones that could handle bass without distorting or having the drivers physically "click" against the ear cup itself. This has been the case with a lot of high end headphones I have reviewed and, as a bass head, it is very disappointing to say the least. I wanted a head phone that was designed around the idea of a tremendous amount of bass so that I could rely on them not having this issue.

2.) I did not care about the true sound quality of the mids and highs (although they are semi-important and cannot be completely lacking). Referring to point 1, I use a god-like equalizer on my mac book which can make the dollar store impulse earbuds sound better than some of the 20-30 dollar earbuds I have reviewed. I cannot recommend this equalizer enough if you are on mac... it has literally changed how I perceive the fundamentals of sound and audio forever. Basically, you can control any frequency from lows to highs with very custom detail and get sound EXACTLY how you enjoy; then save it as a preset and use it whenever you're on mac!

3.) Price was the final factor. I simply didn't want to spend over 200 on this pair of cans seeing as they are all plastic. The build construction is pretty good though!

Final Words:

Pros:
-Bass variability and capabilities
-Battery Life
-Comfortable
-Dependable Bluetooth connectivity
-Sound is nearly perfect with a subjective 9/10 with my equalizer. The deep bass on these cans simply cannot be reproduced on any other pair of headphones for this price

Cons:

-Little ugly for commuters (headband and wires are a little odd)
-Ears get warm after a couple hours
-Any bass setting over 1/3 of the slider is purely for fun and does not sound good at all... it just takes over the sound
-Construction is all plastic leaving it prone to scratches and breaking
-Not too much noise cancellation (No ANC) but there is passive noise isolation that is alright
-Big ears might be cramped in the little ear cups.

OVERALL: 5/5 DUE TO THE CAPABILITIES THESE HAVE WITH AN EQUALIZER. IT WILL MAKE YOU WANT TO REVISIT YOUR ENTIRE SONG LIBRARY.

reviews.author: Sheri Lynn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 20, 2026
reviews.content:
The Glynzak Wireless Bluetooth Headphones offer an impressive blend of comfort, sound quality, and long battery life, making them a great choice for anyone who enjoys music on the go. Designed as over-ear headphones, they provide a snug fit that helps isolate external noise, perfect for immersive listening whether at home, commuting, or working out.

One of the standout features is the 65-hour playtime, which means you can go days without worrying about recharging. The HiFi stereo sound delivers clear, balanced audio with rich bass and crisp highs, catering well to a variety of music genres. The Bluetooth connection is stable and easy to pair with different devices, adding to the convenience.

Overall, these headphones strike a solid balance between functionality and comfort, making them ideal for daily use. If you’re looking for reliable wireless headphones with great battery life and good sound quality without breaking the bank, the Glynzak Wireless Bluetooth Headphones are definitely worth considering.

reviews.author: Toni Mascari
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content: Very nice, comfortable. Sound is great.

reviews.author: Jennifer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 24, 2023
reviews.content:
I've been using it just over a month now.

Connectivity/Sound:
~My main purpose in buying these was the multipoint connectivity. I need to quickly switch between my phone and computer, and these make it very convenient since it can connect to two devices at the same time. If you start playing media on one device and then start playing media on a second device, playback on device 1 will automatically pause while device 2 plays. My only gripe here is that if you turn the headphones off and back on, it only auto-connects to one device, and you need to manually connect to the 2nd device again. It would be nice if it could reconnect to both devices when powered on so I'm ready to go. Overall, I'm really happy with this feature.
~The range is really good; I've walked out to my car forgetting that I have them on and it surprisingly remained connected. None of my other headsets reach that far.
~I'm no audio engineer, but the sound quality is great. There is a function to change the equalizer settings if you want. As you press the button, robot voice says where in the cycle you are.
~The set comes with an 18 inch charging cable and a 47 inch 3.5mm audio cable. Of course, you can use the audio cable while the headphones are powered off. There is no noticeable difference in the sound quality vs using Bluetooth.

Battery:
This is definitely the 2nd best part about these. It is charged out of the box, but I charged it ~10 min to get it to full. They last FOREVER. I started using them on 11/21 at 10:45am on a full charge and used it nearly all day, every day after. I didn't get a "battery low" warning until 11/28 at 2:39pm. That's some serious juice. On that note, the only con is that said "battery low" warning does NOT mean your battery is that low. I wanted to see just how long I'd have before it died. That dayum robot continued to warn me every 3 minutes (literally--I tracked it) until after 1am the next day when I just couldn't take it anymore and needed to go to bed. Almost 12 hrs of "battery low"....really? I suppose it's going off some percentage of battery remaining, but it should have been set to a much more reasonable threshold, like 1-2 hours remaining (considering the very long battery life, as most devices would shut off within like 20 min of the warning). Every time it said the warning, my microphone/audit would cut out as I couldn't hear people on video calls and they couldn't hear me. This is a long time to deal with that. Thank you for reading my rant.

Fit:
I wear them for several hours a day, sometimes 10+, and have no complaints about comfort. If you wear them while doing heavy physical activity, they do trap heat, just a heads up.

Misc.:
~It takes unusually long to power off, enough time to make me wonder if something is wrong. Not a problem, just seems unintuitive to have to hold the power button for like 6 seconds (seriously, count that out and see how awkward it is just to shut a device off). It powers on more quickly.
~I'm grateful that there is no obnoxiously loud chiming with the controls. Quick beep or robot voice, nothing crazy.
~Press +/- button once to go to next/prev track. Press & hold for volume up/down. If anyone was wondering.
~I need a mute function for these conference calls where I walk away until I'm needed. Mfr dropped the ball on that one.

Conclusion:
For about ~$16 on sale, these are absolutely worth the purchase. I bought a backup for if/when these ultimately break down as anything cheap will do eventually (some other reviews confirm this). You will need to get a case separately. Until this starts giving me problems, I'd recommend it.

reviews.author: M.R.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content: There was nothing wrong with these headphones, but I returned them due to personal preference. They have a nice crisp sound to them and numerous settings. I personally prefer a more richer sound with deep base, so unfortunately, these didn’t fit my need. Definitely worth a try if you need something simple and affordable! They were also super cute!

reviews.author: Suzi
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026
reviews.content: Although these are too big for my head I found a way to wear them comfortably. I was going to return them and changed my mind. I really love the EQ for different needs like watching videos or music, and genres of music. The sound quality is excellent. Tunes out the outside noice. But can hear if someone is talking to me. Lightweight. Easy to clean. The hardest part I have is shutting it off. Or knowing when to charge without it just dying on me. I’ll learn yet.

reviews.author: Ana
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2026
reviews.content: I love my new pair of headphones, easy to connect to Bluetooth, battery lasts a very long time. I received them on January 16th and they were already charged, today is January 24th and still haven’t charged them. I love the color, and they are so comfortable and soft. Quality is good, I recommend this headphones, for you children, your mom, grandma whoever!

reviews.author: BUNNY CHASE
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 11, 2026
reviews.content:
I bought 3 including Tozo but kept this one. For one thing I like the rose and white like my previous Mpows. (The Tozo were nice but just too soft like the JBL I have. ) I think this is the best sound that is like Mpow/Sony sound I prefer. I like more treble to be available, not all bass. The EQ options which conveniently has its own button are actually pretty good, I like Pop. It has about the size and fit of the Mpow H-7 Pro as well. Fully fits around my big ears and comfortable to lean my head back. These have slightly higher rating than the $2 more version of same brand? If anything a little bassy and just slightly muddy with the field focused on the center, as cheap phones often are. Similar to the Mpow H-7 (not Pro). More importantly the volume is loud enough which is hard to find at this price point.
Glad I found them.

Okay, there's a V shaped recess in the frequency range, so the crunch of a rhythm guitar is subdued beneath the bass. So sound quality a bit short of Mpow/Sony sound, but it's still the best I've heard with current availability.

reviews.author: Tina
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviews.content: Ive used them a couple times since I've had them. They are really nice. I can connect them to my phone through Bluetooth and walk away without getting caught on something as if I was wearing wired headphones. They are comfortable, range is good, definitely noise cancelling and easy to use. Great purchase :)

reviews.author: Porter L
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content:
I come from a background of audio production and general audiophile interest. I've gone through a number of amps, DACs, and a fair variety of headphones/IEMs. I'm well acquainted with EQs, tuning, sound stage, types of drivers, and all the jazz that comes with it. I'm not necessarily obsessive, I just really like good and clean sound with a bit of customization. These are the most impressive headphones I've ever used. Let me tell you why:
- Out of the box these have neutral tuning with a slight preference for Sennheiser's tuning where they excel in the mids. This is likely due to a built in DSP helping achieve this performance on closed back headphones.
- Wireless with NO compromises. You have all the codecs are your disposal, and frankly it will be enough for most people. Fairly low latency (for Bluetooth that is) and decent bit rate. For those earnest in getting higher quality (including better than MP3 providers can give you) then the USB-C wireless dongle has you covered. It may not be playing true FLAC quality directly into your ears, mind you we're still entirely wireless here, but within the law of diminishing returns you will be VERY happy with that quality you can get out of these. Rest assured.
- Support for aux, airplane adapter, USB (wired), USB wireless dongle, and many bluetooth codecs
- Compatibility: works on all devices that support Bluetooth or USB-C from my testing. Steam Deck, Android, MacOS, Windows (including Equalizer APO), and Linux (with Easy Effects *chef kiss*). I haven't tried iOS, but it'll work there fine too.
- Battery life. Superb. I can't kill this thing, and when the battery inevitably degrades over time it'll still be more than plenty.
- Mobile app. Sennheiser withheld no expense. Let's cut straight to the chase: Parametric EQ, and you can share your settings with other people. Fully customizable, but they still offer Graphic EQ if you prefer that. There's even a simple "Bass boost" toggle if you can't be bothered to change the other settings. Only downside: you are limited to + or - 6db in either direction. So if you want to max out the sub-bass shelf then you'll have about 12db to work with (+6 on sub, -6 on everything else). You can combine this with software side EQ in addition to this if you wish, like EQ APO or Easy Effects as I mentioned before.
- Controls (swipes and taps on right cup) are fully customizable.
- Comfort. It's good, no complaints. I'll talk about clamp force later.
- Carrying case. It has room for the headphones, the wires, and adapters, plus room for more. It's also a flat case so it's easy to slip in to a bag.
- Sub bass rumble. You won't get this out of the box. You can get a small lift out of the box with the app. If you use other software you can get a great sub-bass rumble that are not muddy. Sennheiser has always done a good job with this. I also appreciate that their "Bass boost" toggle in the app does NOT equate to "louder low mids". Thank goodness for that. We don't play with v-shapes here.

Now that I've sang praises for its strengths, here are things that range from "passable" to "okay"
- ANC (you saw this coming). To be perfectly clear, it's good. Decent, even. But it's not at Sony's or Bose's levels. They excel in that industry. That said, these work passably and will enhance your music experience. It'll get you the most of the way there. If ANC is more important to you than sound presentation, you have options elsewhere.
- Transparency mode. It's good. Not the best I've used, but it's fine.
- Clamp force. Yeah, they are slightly on the tight side on my head. Did you know you can fix this without leaving a zero star rating? Yeah, literally just grab some books and stack them side by side slightly beyond the width of your head and put your headphone cups on either side. Go to bed. In the morning the clamp will have relaxed and your problem is solved. You could even use a pot. A tight clamp for one person is a weak clamp for another. So of course they err on the side of tight, and yes you can address it.
- Cost. You see the price. It's clear you're paying for R&D more than anything else. This wasn't intended to be the value option.
- Swipe controls (right cup only). They work but are a little finicky. Not my favorite to use.

TL;DR
If you want the best sound you can possibly get in wireless over-ear closed back headphones, these are it. Senheisser has knocked it out of the park. Full stop.

If you want the best sound there is but don't care about wireless or closed back, you have cheaper options that perform the same or better.
If you don't care about the v-shape signatures you get from the competitors in this space, or if you want better ANC, you also have other options.

reviews.author: Meganesan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 11, 2026
reviews.content: Best consumer ANC bluetooth Audiophile headphones you can buy. If you want those 3 qualities exactly (ANC, Audiophile and bluetooth) there is nothing quite like this. The only thing that would make these absolutely plerfect would be an improvement on the ANC, but I if the cost of that is sound quality I can definitely live with the current level of ANC these provide. I believe that after certain amount of cancellation there is diminishing return. The comfort is also excellent. While I've have wear more comfortable headphones these are some of the best for the ANC category, much better than the Sony XM6 for example. The latest QC 2 Bose does beat them out on this category tho. Also, the fact that these bring a dongle for connectivity is a blessing allowing you to get 92k on any USB device is actually pretty good. I can't believe other headphones don't do this.

reviews.author: Vince Rahal
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content:
The sound quality is impressive. And the app allows you to really tweak the sound they you like it. I didn’t think the Bluetooth dongle would make a difference in sound quality but it does on my iPhone. Not a Big difference but certainly a change in sound quality and staging. I still prefer my Mark Levinson 5909s but not by much.

ANC is good i suppose (don’t really care that much about ANC)

The comfort is good if your ears aren’t too big. Controls are as expected: swipe and tap. Build quality is a little flimsy. The leather-like material seems thin and could easily crack so good idea to gently clean them regularly.

Overall it’s the sound that sells these.

reviews.author: Critique R
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 2, 2026
reviews.content:
When you listen to hi-res lossless music through the BTD 700 dongle to the headphones, you can finally have a truly magnificent audiophile-level experience on an Apple device. Here's the thing though - these are not a connect and go solution, like the AirPods or Air Maxes, they require some adjustments to enjoy the hi-res codec. Most notable of these adjustments it to make sure you have "Hi-Res Lossless" enabled in Apple Music; without that feature enabled, there's no lossless source in Apple Music to send to the Sennheisers. Secondly, you must ensure that you're playing music through the BTD 700 dongle to the HDB 630 headphones. In Apple Music this is an easy thing to ensure...once you know where to look. When playing music in the app, select the specific track in the album you're playing, and locate the icon in the bottom-middle of the screen, which typically displays a headphones icon. Tap that icon, and ensure you select the BTD 700 - again, not selecting the dongle will allow the Apple Music app to play directly to the HDB 630 headphones, which bypasses the hi-res lossless features offered by the dongle and not available by just using the headphones. Once you get these settings mastered, as well as those organic to the Sennheiser Smart Control Plus app, the listening experience surpasses that offered by any other in-ear or over-the-ear headphones currently on the market - hands down, without a doubt. In fact, once the settings were dialed in, which was admittedly frustrating to learn and it's easy to miss an important step, the headphone/dongle combo rival my more expensive open-backed planar magnetic headphones that I listen to on my Mac Mini through a FIIO desktop DAC, using Apple Music as the source. Hard for me to believe it, but it's true. There was a time that there was no way in the universe to get such high-quality music from an Apple mobile device through wireless headphones, but no longer.

So, why the four stars? As I mentioned, setup was a bit frustrating and took longer than it should've. I don't assign blame to solely Sennheiser, though more detailed instructions would be incredibly helpful; Apple is at fault here too. It always feels like you have to pry high-res music out of an Apple device, it never seems to come easy or straightforward. In a post-hi res Apple Music world, I feel that this should no longer be the case, but sadly it is.

As for the fit/comfort, they're top-notch. I've always felt that my Air Maxes were way too heavy for long-duration listening. Add to that, the Apple approach of not angling the ear cups, and using a very slippery head band, makes the Maxes, for me, incredibly uncomfortable to use. In fact, I ended up selling them because I just got tired of trying to ignore their flaws. Although I've had the Sennheisers for only a few days, I've worn them extensively and can attest to their comfort for long-duration listening sessions. Battery life is fantastic as well, even when taking advantage of hi-res lossless listening, which due to the size of the data stream, always uses more battery than a lossy data stream/pair of headphones.

So, all that written, if you pay attention to the things I mention here, for the first time ever, you can have simply amazing wireless sound from an Apple iPhone and/or iPad.

reviews.author: zapman2000
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 3, 2026
reviews.content: great sound but they are truly let down by the amount of pressure they put on both the head with the head band and the squeezing of the ears, not worth the cost at all its like they spent all the money on sound and nothing on comfort for long listening sessions, I can last about 30 mins before I have to take them off due to extreme pressure on head and ears. 1 star for the cost being so much but only having 1 positive thing about them.

reviews.author: JW
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content: It sounds okay—smooth, but not especially detailed or punchy, and that ends up feeling a bit boring to me. The most frustrating part is that I still can’t get it as loud as I’d like, even after trying both a dongle and Bluetooth.

reviews.author: Rodolfo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2026
reviews.content:
Great sound quality, i had the momentums 4 and the sound its better, but its not that loud as the momentums.

They are more comfortable to use for extended period of time vs the momentums 4 ( which are already good at).

They feel lighter and the fit is good.

Overall Quality is excellent, i would purchase them again.

reviews.author: Kyle
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 15, 2025
reviews.content:
It is probably one of the best sounding wireless headphone you can buy if the objective is audio first. It is relatively light and the clamping force is right.

However, the app is far from being good in 2025 standard. It has all the feature you might ever need for audio, but basic touch customization is lacking. You can only use the default control or disable it. A basic feature to switch between normal, ANC, and transparent mode is not even an option at of writing.

Connecting to the hi-res dongle is not intuitive even with the paper quick start guide. The quick start guide is definitely not tested by someone not in the team, as the BTD700 section is so bad that even after I successfully pair the headphone to the dongle, I am still confused on what it is trying to tell me. The video on Sennheiser's channel is much better. If you cannot think of a way to teach the user using only pictures, you can at least provide a QR code to your channel for the better illustrations.

$500 headphone minus the cost of the adapter is $450, which is competitive with Sony's offering. If you're looking for the best sounding WIRELESS headphone around this price point, this is the one to go. However, the current UX is still a bit questionable for me to recommend it to the general users.

Hopefully the app becomes much more UX friendly in the future, but as of now, this is only for you if you're an audio first person.

reviews.author: Nextdoorneigh
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content: Great option if you're on a budget. I've owned expensive brand headphones in the past and have tried tons of cheaper brands but these are the best unexpensive headphones I've found. They last me about 1 year which is pretty good for the price. Highly recommend. Also, as least for my standards, they charge fast and last a descent amount of time and they're very easy to sync. Very good sound quality whether I sync them to my smart phone (Android), laptop (Windows) or iPad Pro (Apple).

reviews.author: Christopher S
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.content: Pretty rapid shipping. This is my second set. First pair has taken a lot of abuse. I mean a lot. Anyway wanted another pair because I didn’t know how long they would offer them so looking ahead incase first pair fails. They work great with good sound. I most like how they stay in place during various activities. Worth it!

reviews.author: Daniel M.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 7, 2026
reviews.content: This product gets the job done, is not universal fitting, but still does what it needs to. Quick connectivity to my phone which I appreciate, charges quickly and overall enjoyable to use. Ear size is very good. Longevity lasts like 12 hours of constant use. The clarity is superb. Color is good too. My only gripe is they do not fit snug in the ear to prevent falling out if you are active.

reviews.author: Cartoon Head
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 31, 2025
reviews.content:
I continue to love this brand and particular model. The tech used by this product has been copied hundreds of times and you will find multiple variations available for sale. I've bought several, and this one is the best. The main differences are the way the bud stays secured in your ear, the type of USB connector to plug into the charging cord, and (oddly) whether the British woman's voice says "YOUR HEADPHONES ARE CONNECTED" or just "CONNECTED". (They all say "BATTERY LOW" in the same manner, so that's just a strange difference.)

What I like #1 is the connector. This uses a USB-C connector, so (as the older type require) you don't have to try inserting it, realize it's upside down, turn it around, try again.

What I like #2 is the way it secures. Another design had a soft plastic mesh "finger" that would lock it into place on your ear, but that thing always seemed to slip out of place. This model has the securing element built-in to the design of the earbud. So with an easy twist forward, it's locked in.

And #3, it's the full version of "YOUR HEADPHONES ARE CONNECTED". Not that it's any big deal, but it seems almost like evidence that they didn't skimp, and paid the lady for 3 extra words. (LOL, probably not)

They do seem to make improvements with each updated model number. There are a couple of tactile elements to the design that make it easier to identify where the button is (for pause/play, etc.).

I don't actually use these sitting up or running, however. My use is predominantly for bedtime white noise. (I have a particular gentle-thunderstorm track that plays on repeat all night long.) I'm a side sleeper, so any time I get up to shift sides, I swap one bud in, the other out, and just leave the other bud loose at the edge of the pillow. It works well for this purpose.

But I'm replacing a prior model where I must have squashed the free bud, and after about a year, something got sensitive and started shorting out the sound. (no visible cord or bud damage, so it could have been ordinary device failure) But for the price, it didn't upset me. We'll see how this holds up over the year, but so far, it's all good.

Sound quality is good, going plenty loud enough (for my purposes anyway). I tend to dislike heavy bass (which causes permanent hearing loss anyway), and this doesn't really have that, so it's a good match for ME. If you wanted that low-end inner-ear-damaging bass, this won't really deliver. (I already pay the price for my youth with constant tinnitus -- kids, take care of your hearing! The loud ringing 24/7 for the rest of your life seriously sucks. -- And no, the tinnitus is not because I listen to quiet rain sounds all night long, they are on low volume, and I had it long before starting to do this - the rain sounds are just to calm my thoughts.)

Speaking of which, it has a good seal on your ear that really does help block noise. (Sometimes I'm so tired that I forget to start the rain track, and it does a good job blocking those insects and highway noises.)

reviews.author: AJ
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 1, 2025
reviews.content:
I've bought these headphones three times now, I think. They are cheap earbuds, not the loudest, and the cord looks kinda lame to some people.

However, if you are like me and you are constantly losing one earbud, the cord is a godsend. These earbuds generally last me longer than almost any other pair I've owned. I go through earbuds pretty quickly, even when I get nicer ones, so I try to stick to the cheap yet durable ones. The sound is loud enough to hear at the gym, wish it could be louder, but it's good enough to get/keep me in the zone. I don't store them in the case, I just wear them around my shoulders when I'm not using them. They are easy and quick to recharge. Great if u just want something reliable and hard to lose

reviews.author: KenM KAAO
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 12, 2025
reviews.content:
POSITIVES:
Superb battery life, to date my use time record is approximately 17 hours.
Good ear fit with loop for security
Charge time is about 2 hours
Very quick Bluetooth acquisition
Sound quality is 4 out of 5 with very good bass provided the ear plug is fitted properly.
Price point is very very very attractive.
Volume is intuitive.
On-0ff switch design is such that it is highly unlikely for inadvertent actuation and low volume is the first switch you feel so conversation is possible without turning off power.
This is NOT going to fall off unless you use in a contact sport. I use use these much more than my other set.

NEGATIVES:
No indication of existing battery charge.
No power or Bluetooth status light indicator.
Not as comfortable as my $120 Bose but almost.
Sound quality is not perfect but close to my Bose.
Putting them on quickly takes practice.

reviews.author: Jennifer Pavlovic
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 18, 2025
reviews.content:
UPDATE: After reading the review, the company offered me a replacement or a refund. I chose replacement. I will update after I know if these will hold up. But Kudos to the company for standing behind the product.

When I first got these, they were amazing. They sound great. I have hearing loss, and I find I can hear on the phone pretty well with these. They have about an 18-hour charge on battery, and I could walk through most of my house without my phone and without interruption. Now, after six months, the battery charge is more like 8 hours, and if I get more than 6 feet away, it disconnects and shortly after turns off. If I walk to the bathroom off my bedroom and the phone is in the bedroom, it will disconnect. So if you only use them a little, you may do well for longer, but if you use them a lot or answer your phone with them, be aware that they wear down fairly quickly. Since you are paying so little, maybe it's worth it for the intermittent users. I have two of them for rotation, and they both started it around the same time, so not a fluke with just one of them. Outside of the short use time without issues i love them. The sound is good, the fit is good, and it seems to work with anything Bluetooth... for a while.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 22, 2026
reviews.content: These worked well and were relatively comfortable. Fancier earphones had noticeably better sound but these are great for $20 for music or speech. Reduced to 3 stars because they totally died just under one year after purchase for no reason. I didn’t drop them. No water incidence. Customer service sent me a replacement very quickly so I'm giving them back the 5 stars.

reviews.author: Pasquale 61
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 26, 2024
reviews.content:
I was a little skeptical about these at first, but after talking to others and reading reviews, I decided to get these. I have to say, I really love these things! I have no problem wearing them with my bike helmet on, and they feel like I'm not wearing anything on my ears. The sound quality is much better than I expected. They are not as good as the best in-ear buds, but they are good enough and the benefits far outweigh the cons. I used to only wear one ear bud, loosely in one ear so that I could be aware of my surroundings while riding.
