reviews.content: These ear buds have great sound quality. Even at higher volumes I don’t feel like my ear drums are splitting. They have a really nice, even sound. They are fairly comfortable- I have smaller ears and have a tough time keeping ear buds in, but these work pretty well. They keep their charge for a decent amount of time. Overall a great product.

reviews.author: thirst4music
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2023
reviews.content:
Sometimes I'm amazed by the advancements in wireless and micro technology, especially when it comes to audio. These popped up in an email from Amazon a few days ago. Though I usually ignore those Deal notices this one intrigued me. I have a JBL Everest over-ear headset that I love for indoor phone calls and they're pretty good for music too (though I don't use them that way). I often like to go outside when I'm on the phone and the JBLs aren't convenient to use with a stocking cap, so I'd thought briefly about getting something in-ear to wear under my hat. These were $35 and the reviews were good. I liked that the case wirelessly charges the ear buds, and the case can be charged wirelessly too. My Samsung phone has wireless charging so I already have two wireless chargers and they're very convenient (especially given that the USB port on my phone was getting sketchy). So I figured I'd give them a try. I played a variety of my favorite music. I first asked for Jeff Buckley live music and the first song that started playing as I stepped outside was "Hallelujah." I walked around the property listening to my favorite music and before I knew it I had been listening to music for about three hours. I had really only cared about the sound quality for phone calls and whether they would stay in my ears (which is usually a problem for me). They never came close to falling out and they are comfortable. For phone calls the sound quality is very good on both ends. And though they're rated as having a 33' range I walked all around the house and got considerably farther than that from my phone with two walls separating the devices and I had no dropouts. Overall I am so happy with them that after a week of using them I ordered a backup pair.

I have three VERY minor niggles but even combined I wouldn't consider them significant enough to knock off even ½ star. First, I had trouble gripping them well enough to get them out of the case quickly when I got an unexpected phone call. I started leaving them on a paper towel on the table next to me, but it now seems I've discovered the right way to grip them. Second, though they have a voice announcement of which side is which when first removed from the case, I was concerned that I might confuse L and R as they don't appear to have any visible indicators. My solution was to use my fine-point permanent marker to write an L on the left one. Third, one of my wireless phone chargers is a tilted stand that holds my phone at a convenient angle. I was unable to get the Ear buds case to wirelessly charge on that one until last night when I accidentally discovered that the problem was that the case was too small to hit the sweet spot on the charger. I solved the problem by boosting the case up with a pad of mini sticky notes (as shown in the photo).

EDIT (I apologize if I forget to come back to delete my previous review):

None of the above has changed but I forgot to mention that these are NOT good for noise isolation (at least not the way they fit me) but I prefer it that way. I'm a musician and music sounds less natural to me when the surrounding environment is completely eliminated, and I like being able to have a conversation or hear a knock at the door without removing them. Even without a tight seal I never have to reseat them and the sound quality stays consistent (a first for me after trying several other brands).

UPDATE: I've been using these ear buds for five weeks now. I use them for hours every day and they continue to impress me. On three occasions only the right channel had a signal when I initially put them in to listen to music, but each time I put them back in the case and took them out again (which turned them off and on) and they worked normally. I've only had this happen with music and only when I first take them out of the case (i.e. it hasn't happened while on a call or after I've been listening to music with both working).

reviews.author: Cody Jackson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.content: Overall very good product for a good price. Great sound and functionality. Volume is bit weird, as at 2nd lowest setting on my galaxy 23, it still sounds loud then I lower one level and i cant hear anymore but typically i only have one in at a time so not a deal breaker. Really good battery life, lasts all week at work listening at least 50% of the day each day. I have a pair of beats studio buds and it kept dropping connection at work. I got these to have at work to augment the beats and I have really liked them. I usually end up taking them home and using instead of the beats now. I just miss the beats function of noise canceling then going to transparency mode.

reviews.author: Steve
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 19, 2025
reviews.content:
This is actually my *third* pair of WI-C100s- the first pair lasted a good several months (if not over a year) but the cable from the battery to the earbud came loose on one side; the second I lost on the way to work after months of use, probably while having to run for a bus or snagged on my backpack strap and fell off (was...not a good morning hah)

So....yes, since they're so light they are easy to slip off accidentally if you don't have one or both ears in-use to keep it from running off your neck (mostly when running or leaning/laying back, basic day-to-day activities don't usually knock em loose ime) that's really the only "big" downside I've found so far across these- and I've been using this same model for a few years now. And worst case scenario, if you do lose em they're cheap and easy to replace, widely available- sometimes even at local stores (though my nearby places didn't this time around)

Because they're tethered together, I don't have to worry about losing one ear because it fell out mid-use or I forgot to put it back into the case when not in use. Sometimes you may need to be careful using these when wearing any "cross-chest strap" bags or otherwise (messenger/gym/duffel bags, purses, arm-slings, tools with a neck/shoulder strap, etc) to make sure the earbuds don't get caught under the strap- but for the tradeoff of reliability and peace-of-mind of having them semi-wired, I don't mind that occasional snag too much. Even when they do lightly catch on my backpack strap, they haven't seemed to wear down or come loose around the wire at either end (the pair I had that *did* break in that way came loose at the battery end, and wasn't caused by a shoulder strap- was also after several months of heavy use, even while sleeping)

So build quality is reasonable, how do they sound? Honestly, for a $20-30 pair of *wireless* earbuds, pretty decent ^-^ sure it may not satisfy any audiophiles, but again....don't forget the price- even for that, they still sound great to me (of course, that could just be Sony and their reasonable consistency/quality with even budget headphones over the years heh) and the extra features from the Sound Connect app are a nice bonus. Of course it's not going to have extra-thumpy bass (try their "XB" line of products for that) or Sennheiser/Bose/what-have-you quality- but the equalizer and DSEE do give you some extra control over how they sound. Oh, and you can also enable/disable the voice msgs from the app, so it'll only speak up when turning on/off the headset, and when the battery is low

The connection consistency and quality have been pretty good across all 3 pairs I've had. For reference, I not only use them with my phone daily (close proximity, but also connecting to Android over BT often works better than with Windows) but also with my PC just as often- and I've never had a problem with initial pairing or swapping between the two. When in use with my PC, even when in another room several ft away they still maintain a good connection, only *rarely* drops out from far away if I move my arm in the way of the line-of-sight (granted, that may also be another advantage of the "pair on the same wire" style of headset over individual or TWS [true wireless sound, or "airpod style" with the separate batteries/bt connections per ear] buds- ymmv with your device(s) of choice)

The battery life....hoo boy, they're not kidding when they say upwards of 20-25 hrs. Not only do these have a larger battery thanks to the type of headset- but also since the battery is along the band instead of the buds themselves, the earbuds are much lower profile and work great for wearing during sleep without having to worry about them falling out or losing charge too soon (as long as you don't mind the wires- but even then, they're pretty lightweight and unobtrusive) especially with the right eartips to help keep em snug and secure. Personally, with my living situation having something playing in my ears to drown out outside noise while sleeping was....a necessity- with some memory foam eartips (the included ones would normally be ok, but my inner ear is a bit wide even for the included large tips to work ._.) you almost don't need active noise-cancelling with these ^-^ speaking of eartips, the posts on these are also decently long unlike most TWS buds, so they have a bit more flexibility with options for those (one of my major problems with several TWS pairs I've tried)

Between the long battery life (and decently fast charging speed, even over a PC USB instead of 2A wall adapter), comfortable wear, decent sound quality for price, and extra features in the companion app, I'm pretty happy with these as a daily driver

(As for the provided eartips, there's nothing wrong with em and will likely work for most, I'm just a bit of an odd case with ear shape hah)

reviews.author: wali h
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 15, 2025
reviews.content:
I've used its cousin, the WI-C130, for the past two years and felt like it was about time to replace them given their waning battery life. I've used those out and about, listening to music and, most importantly, while lying in bed listening to audiobooks (including side-sleeping). Most earphones aren't comfortable for that; either they poke out too much, are generally uncomfortable, or too loose/unsecure. But these (both C100 and C130) are amazingly comfortable for that use-case.

The WI-C100 is comparable to WI-C130. Given Sony's notoriously confusing naming scheme, its not clear which of the models is meant to be a step-up over the other.

- In minor aspects of build quality, the WI-C130 seems better; slightly better quality plastic with magnets (at least weak ones) on the eartips for convenient attaching when not in use.
- But otherwise, the WI-C100 seems better; the sound quality (from initial impressions) and the availability of software preferences. E.g., after initial connection with an android smartphone, it prompts you to install the Sony SoundConnect app which has a nice equalizer with a "clear bass" option. It also includes helpful guides on the button functionality and choosing the right sized eartips for your ears. It also includes (what seems like) a gimmick feature: a "360 live sound" app which simulates surround sound and adapts it to a model of your ear shape. I'm not sure whether this applies globally, or only in the small collection of paid bloatware apps it offers to install. The app could've been more polished, and if the 360 live sound thingy was better implemented, it would definitely outcompete other such branded apps.

Two important shortcomings:

- These should've had multipoint bluetooth connections so that at least two devices could be connected at a time. It seems they could've implemented that in this package, esp. at this price-point.
- These don't automatically turn off after a timeout period when there's no sound. I suspect this needlessly shortens their battery health overtime, like it does for the WI-C130, especially when used during sleep time. Its a shame because an automatic-switch-off feature would've made these even more robust and long-lasting.

TL;DR: In the relatively overlooked niche of wireless neckband earphones, this is a very decent, economical option, providing Sony-level sound quality and reliability.

(Having said that, the WI-C130s were $18 two years ago, now these comparable WI-C100s are almost double the price. That's regrettable especially since the cheap plastic build quality is nothing to write home about, also with the two shortcomings above. There are other companies providing more features at this price-point (albeit without the Sony-level comfort). I hope Sony limits price-hikes for these and related models, or at least justifies them with tangible improvements in build quality, sound, IP rating, battery life, QoL features, etc).

Bonus tip: cross the earphones around the neck for a more secure fit. This advice goes for most wireless earphones with neckbands.

reviews.author: SandyGee
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 11, 2025
reviews.content: I can't believe my luck with these amazing (and affordable) headphones. They work wonderfully. They are in-ear, attached to a flexible neckband worn around the neck - impossible for it to slip off and disappear. They run a long time on a single charge. You need to provide your own USB-C cable which is no problem. It was easy to set up and connect to the Bluetooth on the phone. The ear tips are soft, secure, and feel comfortable in the ears (a couple of extra tips are included, I assume various sizes). I primarily listen to language learning apps and these in-ear headphones are great for this purpose - sound is crisp and clear. They aren't noise cancelling, which is what I wanted (walking on city streets). You can adjust the settings on the headphone cable, but I prefer to do this on the phone. Either way, easy. I turn them off after each use. Not sure how they compare to fancier and more expensive versions, but these are simple, well-made, nothing fancy, they do the job well.

reviews.author: Sandy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 21, 2025
reviews.content:
Quality listening is nice.
Battery is great.
Fit is ok. Color is ok.
Regarding functionality I believe this item should have the magnet to keep both sides together in neck when you are not using them and that detects and pauses what you are listening when in break. That helps out to not loose them, feel more comfortable and have more battery durability. They function well but to carry is harder.

reviews.author: Winter
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026
reviews.content:
I like these so much I got two as a gift. I use these every day, and they last *all* day if I charge them fully. I can even forget to charge them one night and get them for a pretty good chunk of the next day. High quality sound, super comfy, and keeps outside noise out. The controls are super convenient without being too much either. Pretty easy to pair too, switching between your phone or PC or whatever is not hard.

The microphone quality is nothing to write home about, it's actually not that great but this is common for ear buds. It's about on par with any other ear buds microphone.

The latency is alright but it is a noticable delay. It usually feels around 150 ms but that's just how it feels to me not a real measurement. It does not negatively impact games at all for me, but it is slightly noticable in rhythm games - but still good enough to be used for them if you're not trying to be super competitive.

If you're looking for something to use every day I suggest these a lot, I don't see myself wanting anything else. I just also suggest a separate dedicated microphone if you're getting it for something like PC gaming or communication.

reviews.author: DrJones
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 14, 2023
reviews.content:
I really wanted to like these more...in fact, initially I thought they were great, but the more I've used them, the more I've considered returning them.

These replaced a few-years-old LG Tone headset/headphones so I compare these to those. That was my preferred style/pair (a plastic band worn across the back of the neck, with the earbuds coming out the ends but easily connected to the band ends by magnets. I first got LG Tone as a gift about 10 years ago, for my first Bluetooth headphones, and liked them so much I would just get the same (on eBay) every few years when one of the earbuds would lose its sound (cord wearing down), but now they are not available. I don't like the airpods style for several reasons -- I don't like the hard plastic in my ear, don't think they fit well/stay in place well, are too easy to lose since they aren't conjoined, and the controls are too difficult to use effectively for my big fingers (e.g. adjusting volume/track).

So for this Sony pair, pros and cons:
PROS
1) battery life is EXCELLENT, this is one aspect far superior to the former LG pair; the box says 25 hours and that might be right--i use at the gym for roughly 3-8 hours a week, and I've charged them just TWICE in the two months I've had them
2) value is good as they are under $20 for a name-brand
3) sound quality is solid, especially for the price - there is supposed to be some special sound processing tech, not sure if it's any better or worse than the "aptX" the LG's had
4) charging is easy with a USB-C connection (no a cable isn't included, but I have like 10 extra cords lying around, they are pretty ubiquitous these days and I'm sure you can get one for like a dollar if you didn't happen to have one)

CONS:
1) connection stability is POOR - they drop the connection generally once a listening session, which gets really old fast. The LGs I previously had would only do this roughly once every 50 uses or so
2) they can take forever to pair (sometimes up to two minutes), and it only does it automatically when turning on roughly half the time without going into the phone's menu to connect them
3) design does not necessarily work well when "active," e.g. at the gym -- draping them across the back of the neck, with the "L" on the left and the "R" on the right, they constantly shift around, making them frustrating to wear -- another reviewer (must be a short/small person?) suggested the cord length in between the L and R control "boxes" should be SHORTER?? but for me they need to be significantly longer. For reference, I am 6'2" (1.87 m). With them worn this way, the controls are also not intuitive to use (and it's also annoying you have to press and hold the + or - buttons for like three seconds to go to previous or next track)
**[As I write this review, the connection just dropped! Then clicking connect on the phone, it only connected for phone audio, then dropped again entirely]**
4) when they initially connect, or re-connect when it invariably drops, often there is a long delay for the audio, like 10-20 seconds after you hit play and they actually start playing through the buds
5) trying to re-connect them as I write this review, they refuse to re-connect despite three attempts (oh wait, now it finally did on the 3rd attempt and after turning off and on, and after taking around 45 seconds to connect)

**I discovered they are better to wear with the ears flipped -- drape across the back of your neck but with the L on the right and the R on the left, and then place into the corresponding ear, so they crisscross across the bottom of the front of your neck/upper chest. This way the controls are in easy reach of your right hand, and they stay in place better (mostly).

reviews.author: Az Jack 520 in Tucson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 9, 2026
reviews.content: The Sony WI-C100 Wireless In-Ear Headphones deliver a nice, crisp sound and get good life on each charge. The cord length is just right to make the controls easy to reach. I wanted this style after losing one of a wireless pair, and the fact that they're wired to each other keeps that from happening. They're very comfortable and callers tell me the voice clarity is exceptional.

reviews.author: MurphysLaw
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 16, 2024
reviews.content:
I was in need of a new headset and bought 2 different sets - this and the Tonemac N8s, both were in the under $30 range. There is a more detailed review for the Tonemacs on that product page but I will touch on a few points for both in case others are comparison shopping.

The Good:
1) The Sony headset has an exceptional sound at this price point. Solid bass, clear vocals and treble, no muddled sound. You can distinctively hear individual instruments and vocal tracks, sounds are crisp and clear. They sound higher end than the price point and significantly better than the Tonemacs.

2) The Sony buds connected to my Bluetooth immediately. I had no issues with the connection dropping which was not the case with the Tonemacs. The range is decent as well. There is a very clear female voice that advises the power status and battery level, connecting, etc.

3) The Sony earbuds are very comfortable in the ear. There is a left and right side. They come with 3 pair of tips, small, medium and large. They aren't noise canceling but do block most outside sounds.

4) The volume range is quite good and clear at even low levels. This is a significant improvement over the Tonemacs, that had to be at nearly full volume but still had muddled sound.

5) Battery life appears to be quite good and 25 hours seems to be spot on. The Tonemacs boast about 40 hours.

The Bad:
1) I'm not a fan of the multi-function buttons on just about every electronic device these days. I prefer separate track select buttons but these are combined with the volume up/down buttons however they do work well. You hold the volume up or down button about 2 seconds to move to the next or previous track. It chimes when it changes the track, which is a nice feature. The Tonemacs were a hot mess, flipping through multiple tracks, etc.

2) The flexible neckband is a pain. It slides and flops around. If you are wearing these while exercising, they will eventually fall so far to one side that it pulls the earbud out. I have found putting the controller on the right side and then crossing the earbuds in the front of your neck (so the bud on the right side is in the left ear, etc.) is really the only way to keep them in place if you are moving around. They should have included magnetic tips but apparently Sony had to save a few pennies.

3) I was shocked that a charge cord isn't included. I mean, come on. It isn't a huge deal to me because I have several C cords but that may not be true of everyone. I don't think people should have to purchase an additional item just to get the product to function. These cords are inexpensive, which is all the more reason it should be included. Ridiculous and so petty and cheap on Sony's part. Just an FYI in case anyone is wondering - you can't listen and charge simultaneously but that is the case for most headsets.

4) The build quality is decent but very basic, no frills. The absence of magnetic tips is annoying because they could help keep them from falling off. The plastic cover over the charge port feels extremely fragile but the cords seem to be decent enough. The headset is lightweight, but doesn't feel cheap or flimsy. Build quality is on par with the cost.

Overall, the sound of this headset is what makes it stand out. Everything else is really quite basic. To me though, the sound is what matters and these sound WAY better than the Tonemacs. Just make sure you have a C charging cable or you won't be able to use them.

reviews.author: Severina
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content:
These earbuds get a true 5 stars for multiple reasons.

They are comfortable, sound quality is so so so good, they hook up to my phone and laptop quickly without problems every time, they have crisp clear sound and the app has customizable sound options that are very intuitive, I can wear them in the shower bc they have a wonderful water resistance rating ( one of my earbuds has went through the washer not once but TWICE and took a ride in the dryer and STILL FUNCTIONS EXACTLY THE SAME), they charge quick and have a loooooooong battery life. I know there are more pros that I could list here but I think you get the gist.

Cons- genuinely none that I can think of, I am not paid for this review and this was not a free product given to me either.

These bad boys were under 25 dollars, sound great, and can take a serious beating. If you are on the fence, just get them! I promise you won't regret it! I lost one of the earbuds about a month ago and will be ordering these again. They are my ride or die. THANK YOU ANKER FOR THESE AMAZING AFFORDABLE EARBUDS ❤️

There is no reason to spend a lot of money on earbuds when anker is right here with these affordable gems 💎

reviews.author: Amy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026
reviews.content:
These Soundcore P20i earbuds by Anker have seriously impressed me. I’ve tried several wireless earbuds over the years, and these are easily my favorite so far.

The sound quality is excellent — the bass is deep and punchy without overpowering everything else, and audio stays clear for music, podcasts, and calls. They connect quickly, stay connected, and are very comfortable to wear for long periods.

What I love:
Big, rich bass with clear sound
Long battery life — I rarely worry about charging
Comfortable fit that stays put
Call quality is clear on both ends
App customization with EQ presets is a great bonus

Overall, these have exceeded my expectations, especially for the price. If you’re looking for reliable, great-sounding true wireless earbuds, I highly recommend these. I’d absolutely buy them again.

reviews.author: Jason Wanzel
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.content: Had these for just over a month now and just had to charge again. Left in my pocket and put in the washer, let fill up and was going to start washer in the AM. 2 hours later I realized I couldn’t find them!! Fished them out of the washer after their 2 hour soak and thought they were ruined. Ordered another set. Shook out as much water as I could and left on the countertop till next day. Did not anticipate they would work. Put back in case and light came on so I tried them and THEY WORKED!!! Keeping the replacement pair as a back up now. Great product, very impressed with these buds especially for the price point.

reviews.author: Whoknowes
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 8, 2026
reviews.content: Nice ear buds, ordered these on a whim and have been pleased with them, they seem to be well built and of good quality material. They fit in the ear well and have 3 different size adapters if you need larger or smaller tips. They charge quickly and the case has a battery to recharge them after use too. Super easy to use with the app, you can adjust EQ settings and top options. I was wondering about the sound from these and have not been disappointed. You can really adjust the sound to your preference to get just the right sound for the audio you are listening to.

reviews.author: Doug L.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.content:
I normally use Apple Airpods but found that they can slip out of my ear easily if I’m too active or laying down.

I decided to pick up a pair of cheap earbuds that looked like they would stay in place better and that I wouldn’t be too upset if I lost or damaged.

I’ve been happy with quality of Anker power and cable products I’ve purchased in the past so I bought these to try out.

The sound isn’t quite as good as my Air Pods but they seem to have more bass. Because of the rubber ear-plug like design, I found that these were easier to hear while on an airplane than my Air Pods are.

These aren’t quite as good as my Air Pods at being detected by my phone when I remove them from the case to use and I will occasionally need to open the Bluetooth settings on my iPhone and hit the “connect” button. I was actually expecting this to be more of an issues than it turned out to be.

The Air Pods are more comfortable when I am wearing them for
long periods of time and will be relatively stationary so I favor them when I’m working and am on calls all day. Other than that, I find that these Ankers are the ear buds I grab when I’m leaving the house.

These are a great value at $20.

reviews.author: Malissa
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2026
reviews.content: I just got these and am pleasantly shocked!! I spent $80 last week on the Samgsung 3 basic buds expecting those to be as good as the Samgsung 2nd Gen ones I recently lost, but was so disappointed with them. The Samgsungs don't stay in my ear and the sound quality is crap compared to the 2s. I got these after reading the reviews and thought it's only$20, why not? I'm absolutely SHOCKED at how they sound! They're perfectly clear, bass is great on these, actually better than the samgsungs I recently purchased. I changed the rubber part with a smaller one it comes with and they fit perfectly in my ear, not loose or falling out. It's weird because the case is very light weight, feels kinda cheap but the buds are incredible! The case also has a small strap on the side you can hook to your keys or maybe put through the strap of a purse or backpack. I have no idea how the battery life is as I just got them and am listening to them right now. They came charged which was really nice! So far, I'm loving these!

reviews.author: Fiona Ferguson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2026
reviews.content:
I’m a person that has had a million different kinds of earbuds/headphones. Anker is by FAR the best brand out there!
I use earbuds no less than 8 hours a day and they endure sweat, heavy dirt, being dropped, etc. No other brand has lasted as long as Anker has for me. My first pair lasted 2+ years, I had a set of Onn that lasted a little over a year, and a set of AirPods that didn’t even last a year. The AirPods were so difficult to get to connect, yet would connect to every device you didn’t want it to. They would fall out, had no noise cancellation and had surprisingly crummy sound quality. They were super uncomfortable after 2+ hours with them in and would fall out of my pocket constantly because the case was so slick. Then after 10 months, the right ear quit working for good. Incredibly disappointing product.

Every pair of Ankers I’ve had now are durable, comfortable, have outstanding sound quality, great battery life, and not to mention, are so cute! They have excellent noise cancellation - you don’t have to your music high at all to be able hear well, even in very loud environments. They’re great for volume sensitive ears and ear protection for this reason. As fore mentioned, they last a long time for their price point. My last ones only died because my infant brother repeatedly (and I mean like at least 7-8 times, I know for) put the speaker heads in his mouth and basically drown them, slobbery baby style.

They fit my ears so ergonomically well and I’ve never had them fall out due to movements such as walking, running, bending over, etc. The case also feels much more sturdy and has just the ever so slightest bit of texture (think like a matte finish as opposed to smooth and glossy) so it doesn’t constantly slip out of your pocket as you bend down and move around. Finally, the color is so cute! Perfect addition to my other everyday care items, they look so cute with my keychain and Yeti cup! I’m going to be purchasing 3 more for siblings here soon.

Overall, this is definitely a case where price DOES NOT mean quality. You can get a far superior product for less than a quarter of the price in some cases. If you’re on the fence about these earbuds, this is your sign to get them! I promise you will not be disappointed!

reviews.author: Jon H
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content:
For $20 you're buying the advertised 10 hr usage on a charge as well as the name brand of Anker. These $20 earbuds are inferior to competitors like CMF. You can get the CMF earbuds on Amazon for the same price, sometimes less depending on dynamic pricing, and here's a list of what the Anker P20i earbuds can't do vs. what the CMF can do:

1. Adjust volume. You can't do this with touch with the P20i. The CMF allows you to do this. So if adjusting the earbud volume by touching the earbuds matters to you the P20i fall short here
2. Awareness mode: You don't get this with the P20i. With the CMF earbuds you can tap to activate it and hear the outside noise. From what I can tell with the P20i this doesn't exist with these earbuds
3. Active Noise Cancelling (ANC): You don't get that with these. They rely on ear seal to block out sound. So if the ear seal isn't good with your ears you'll hear outside noise. The fit may vary for you but this could be a deal breaker if they don't fit your ears well
4. Sound quality: It's just okay. It's not horrible but it's not the best I've heard. You can try and play around in the app but after 5 mins I decided it wasn't worth it. The quality wasn't improving to a noticeable point. I thought it had but then I went back to the default and realized I wasted 5 mins convincing myself I was making things better when there wasn't a noticeable difference

Would I buy these again? For $10 sure but honestly if the CMF earbuds are on sale for $15 I'd take those in a heart beat over these.

reviews.author: Mehran
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content:
I’ve been using the SteelSeries Arctis Nova 1 Multi System headset and honestly it surprised me in a good way. The first thing I noticed was how comfortable it is. The ear cushions are soft and it doesn’t clamp too tight, so even after a few hours of gaming or watching videos, my head doesn’t feel sore.

Sound quality is clear and balanced. Footsteps and small in game details are easy to hear, which actually gives you an advantage in competitive games. Music sounds clean too, not overly bass heavy but not flat either. It feels well tuned for everyday use, not just gaming.

The microphone is another strong point. My voice comes through clearly and teammates have not complained about background noise or distortion. For a headset in this price range, the mic quality is better than expected.

What I also like is that it works with different systems without any hassle. I can plug it into my PC or console and it just works. No complicated setup.

Overall, it feels like a smart buy if you want something comfortable, reliable, and good sounding without spending a crazy amount of money.

reviews.author: Alexa
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.content:
Extremely comfy and amazing audio quality! It was everything I've been looking for: Built-in microphone that can retract, comfortable on the ear and head (doesn't cause the "gamer headphone dent"), noise cancelling, simplistic design and affordable! It brings the headphones (can fit all head sizes simply by moving the band) and has its cable to connect.

I love how it also came with some stickers which is always a plus. Would definitely recommend to my friends and any other gamers

reviews.author: Hiskoto
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 11, 2023
reviews.content:
Theres some pros and cons to this.
Comparing to my old headset these were much louder and the noise cancellation were much better. But the mic quality in discord specifically was a bit meh other people i was with said it sounded like i was underwater. I would still recommend this headset as its pretty good for budget, the audio quality was nice and for your ear it was really comfortable. Also the wire was a bit short to my liking.
UPDATE AFTER 2 YEARS OF USE :
I daily this thing as my main headset and yes there's a few problem already. First of all the headset like leather material keep falling off and currently all of it is gone. Secondly the jack insert bent (this one is my fault but yeah still a problem). Third issue I'm running into is the mic barely working as it sometimes not detecting anything and i mean anything. Another issue is the headset ear puff almost falling out. I mean it has been treated a bit rough but since it lasted 2 years I can say its pretty good

reviews.author: LionMachine
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content:
Works well for the price.

I recommend downloading their software to adjust the base because it’s loud. Aside from the base the product is able to be used for games like R6, Apex, or just for music. Works a lot better than you would expect honestly.

The only issue I would say is the MIC isn’t too good and detecting your voice. Still works great and I still use it to chat with my family and friends during games.

5/5 recommend if you’re seriously in a budget and just need a headset for detecting footsteps and detailed sound in game.

Other things you might wonder, is it comfortable? Yes, I wear glasses and can play for hours without ear fatigue.

Does the quality feel good? Yes, quality isn’t bad and it hold up well. The headband is especially nice and comfortable.

reviews.author: Sabrina
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
reviews.content: The sound quality of these is really nice. They have worked great on my phone and laptop. Noise cancellation is noticeable. However, there is one tiny screw that holds the earphone to the headband and it comes unscrewed easily. Once it falls out, it's very difficult to put back into place. You're cooked if you lose that little screw. Poor engineering on these.

reviews.author: KnightDelta
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 10, 2023
reviews.content:
(note: I wear glasses. I feel like this is important to mention with headsets >.>)

Honestly, I never expected headphones to make such a big difference in experience. My first headset was a 25$-ish headset that broke, so I decided well, lets try something a bit better from a reputable brand, and ended up buying this one for 40$, and it is worth every. single. cent. spent.

Audio is amazing, and it comes with an application you can download that allows you to tweak the sound depending on your use. It did seem a bit overwhelming at first, but its actually fairly simple to use once you get to understand it a bit.

The headset does feel a little tight on the head, but so far its not uncomfortable to use for long periods of time, as long as you put it on right. Even while using glasses, this feels ok on the head. If you put it on wrong however, like I did when I first got it, it would be very uncomfortable and even painful.

My only complaints would be that, because its all plastic and feels a bit hard, I get worried about breaking it and try to use it as gently as I can. However, I have seen a Youtuber do a review of this headset and he stretches it beyond what my heart can handle without breaking, so there's that little bit of relief found there xD. The 2nd complaint is the short wire length. Would have loved having it be a bit longer, but its not bad.

Overall, my experience with it has been pretty well. So well that, despite the 2 complaints I listed above, I'd still be willing to give this a 5/5. (edit: 4.5 now, due to below issue)

Edit: after using it for a couple months, this headset is amazing and is no longer as tight on the head. However, now I'm facing a new issue, and that is the mic wont fully retract into the headset and would get stuck like there is something blocking it from being pushed into its slot. This comes and goes and I have to use some level of force here and there to get it fully in, but out of fear of breaking it, I stopped doing that. Still, because I only use it for the pc, its not really much of an issue except when I'm eating. For that inconvenience, id drop the review to 4.5. Would still buy a steelseries though because its a fantastic audio experience.

reviews.author: elizabeth colwell
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 4, 2025
reviews.content:
The SteelSeries Arctis Nova 1 is the perfect headset for gamers who want premium quality without breaking the bank. The sound is crisp and immersive, making every game feel more alive — footsteps, dialogue, and music all come through crystal clear. The microphone is clear and reliable, great for chatting with teammates, and the lightweight design with breathable ear cushions makes it super comfortable for long gaming sessions.
✨ What I love:
• Multi-System Compatibility — works flawlessly on PC, PlayStation, Xbox, and even mobile.
• Amazing Audio Quality — balanced sound that’s perfect for both gaming and music.
• Comfortable Fit — lightweight build and soft cushions for hours of wear.
• Sleek Design — looks modern and stylish without being bulky.
Whether I’m gaming online, streaming, or just listening to music, this headset delivers every time. A must-have for anyone looking for comfort and performance in one package!

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 13, 2026
reviews.content: the quality of the headphones are amazing. use them for playing call of duty, I can hear everything. the only thing is the mic on the headphones do work but people can barley hear you speak. not worth $55, being as though the mic barely works and they are not wireless, but they are very comfortable and sound amazing.

reviews.author: Levi Ely
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 4, 2025
reviews.content:
I've had a pair of these headphones since 2020.

They've lasted me for a long, long time. I've come back 5 years later to get me another pair, because only recently, the leather ear pads have started to peel off, and the mic got kinda worn out. Of course, these are only cosmetic problems. The mic works excellently, and my friends have even randomly just been like "Wow, how is your voice so clear". It's great. The sound quality is just as good as new. I've put these headphones through the ringer and they still work just fine. So if any of you particularly rage-prone gamers out there get you a pair of these headphones, I'm pretty confident they'll survive an intimate high speed collision with your drywall. In all seriousness though, if you treat them well, I can see 'em lasting years longer than they lasted for me. Along with that, these headphones are a great budget option, especially if you don't like rolling the dice on those unknown Chinese manufacturers that plague Amazon. For $40, I can't think of a better option for a set of gaming headphones.

If on the off chance something goes wrong, I'll update this post (doubt I'll need to lol).

reviews.author: Shannon C.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 24, 2022
reviews.content:
I have had these for over two years now and really can't complain about much. I play video games and stream with them and have gotten comments on how nice and clear my mic is.

Audio: I think the audio is pretty standard. Not the best you'll ever hear in life but in this price range it's amazing. I've been jump scared a few times not being able to hear someone come up to my desk so the noise cancelling is pretty decent.

Mic: I thought that maybe I needed to upgrade my mic but I haven't found anything aside upgrading to an external mic that tops this. I love the flip to mute ability and use it frequently.

Comfort: I usually start to feel the tiniest discomfort after about 5-6 hours which is plenty time for me most of the time. I have worn them longer and it's not painful but just a little sore when I take them off. They are on the lighter side and adjust nicely.

Longevity: There's some slight wear on the headband part but that just started after all this time and they do rest on a holder in that spot. I did have some issues with sound stopping out of one earphone at one point, I think something became loose and so I just have to keep the volume scroll wheel on the actual headset in a certain spot now but it's not a problem because I never used that to control the volume anyway. It hasn't stopped working in a few months now. I haven't seen anyone else have this issue when it started so could just be a rare occasion and of course eventually they'll need to be replaced. For this price though I'd buy the same ones again and be set for a few more years at least.

Comparison: I saw some HyperX Cloud Alpha S on sale and bought them thinking I was getting an upgrade and I was so wrong. The HyperX mic quality was so quiet I thought maybe it was just me but saw others posting online having the same issues. So far I haven't been able to fix it enough and in order to stream and not annoy talking with friends in Discord I'll be returning to using my Logitech headset for the foreseeable future. The HyperX was also much heavier and started to cause discomfort much sooner, maybe about two hours.

Overall: Despite some minor issues these headphones have done really well for me and are better than other headsets I've tried that may be similarly priced. I would definitely recommend these to anyone looking for a new gaming headset.

reviews.author: Bryson C.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.content:
Plug and play with zero issues. Comes with headphone jack as well as use connection for easy use

Pros-
comfortable headset that doesn’t press down on your ears
Sound quality and loudness is great
Lightweight and easy to manipulate volume knob while wearing

Cons-

Will start to short out at the headset connection after approx 2 years of use. Have had 2 pairs with both the same issues
Cord will develop memory and start to self twist and coil, that’s the process to pay while running wired headsets

Overall I give this 4/5 and would recommend

reviews.author: Trell Williams
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 20, 2022
reviews.content:
The headphones I ordered had some damage but I received another pair as a gift for my birthday last week so I can write my review! I really like these. So far, they are more sturdy than other headphones I've tried, like the Corsair HS35, HS 50, and some lower end Turtle Beach headsets. They are a decent set of wired headphones and are reasonably priced. They have great noise cancellation and feel very comfortable over the ears. I've worn them for work as well as while gaming on a livestream and again, they are comfortable enough to be work for several hours.

The mic is nice. I've had headphones with a removeable mic as well as others similar to these where the mic is attached but can be flipped up. I don't have a preference one way or the other but if you prefer to be able to completely remove the mic from the headset, these aren't for you. What is missing from this headset is a mute button. I have a Plantronics Blackwire C3210 Headset and even that has a mute button. It DOES have volume controls though, which I feel is pretty standard but I thought it worth mentioning.

Some units come with a velcro wrap for cable management when the headset is not in use. I notice that the damaged unit I received had one but this newer one does not.

Overall, I really like this headset, it works well, good sound quality and noise cancellation, and is pretty reasonably priced.

reviews.author: bcal
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2026
reviews.content: I am very happy with these, just please note that the actual connector on the end is a 3.5mm audio jack, and it comes with an adapter for usb and another adapter for 2x 3.5mm. Also, there is a volume control wheel on the back of ear cup, which I did not notice for the first week that I had these. Otherwise, they are really great sound quality. And I'm very happy so far.

reviews.author: DamonRa
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 7, 2023
reviews.content:
Pros:
1) It's $10 cheaper than where I originally found it online.
2) It's wired, so I don't have to worry about batteries needing to be recharged.
3) It's comfortable to wear. I'm a big guy, so having a headset that doesn't squeeze my head while using it helps.

Cons:
1) To use this properly, you need to download the, "Logitech G Hub," software from the Logitech website. I was having trouble getting the headset to work properly, and thought it just needed a driver update. That's what I thought I was downloading from their website, and got the app instead. I don't need something taking up more hard drive (or SSD) space.

2) Testing the sound, it doesn't work as it should. Sound comes across as 5.0 or 5.1 instead of the 7.1 surround sound it's supposed to be. The G Hub app has a surround sound test I was able to use. The, "left," "left rear surround," and "left surround," channels were all the same speaker. The same with the, "right," "right surround," and "right rear surround," channels. The way the test is designed, a slight difference on the right side was less difficult to detect. By slight, I mean close to zero difference between the three channels on each side.

I tried it with several games. Destiny 2, Call of Duty, Resident Evil Village (aka Resident Evil 8, and RE8), and A Plague Tale: Requiem, are the first games I tried. The first 2, sound was lackluster and had some spatial distortion. RE8 needed some extra tweaking in the game settings. With some research, Requiem required a sound setting in the operating system modified. The headset went from not plug-n-play, to less-than plug-n-play. If I have to do technical acrobatics to get moderate surround sound and sub-par sound for other games – not to mention movies and other forms of entertainment – then this will be the last Logitech headset I get.

3) I was talking to Logitech customer service online, and she confirmed the 3.5mm-to-USB converter (she called it a USB DAC) is needed for the device to work as a true 7.1 surround sound. Even while using it and the Logitech G Hub, it still wasn't giving me 7.1 surround sound.

4) The headphones do NOT work with every game. I tried it with Resident Evil VII.I.age, and sound was almost none existent, and choppy extremely low when it did exist.

5) The sound was off. I have a Bluetooth headset by another company that sounds much better. Recording games, then playing them back, the sound is even worse.

I would NOT recommend this headset. It's a shame, because I've never had problems with other Logitech equipment I've used, and would like to stick with them. I guess their name on headsets (or at least this one) doesn't mean much.

(edited)

(Further edit) 11 months, and a few short drops later, one of the sides broke off and was unrepairable. That makes this the shortest time I've had to replace any headphone set.

reviews.author: Kindle Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 29, 2025
reviews.content: I would like to say that I'm happy with my gaming headset. It hugs the ears comfortably, the sound pick up for the mic and the earphones are good. Unfortunately, my set has a caustic chemical (I suppose) impregnated in the ear pads. As heat and moisture from my ears interact with the earpad, my ears start getting itchy and uncomfortable. I had been hoping that this was "just the way things are." and had not worn them for a week. But today while I was in a middling long game, it came back and I thought back to other headphones that I've worn. Yes, the pressure of the pads would impact my ears, but relieving the stress fixed that. But this? Well, my ears are itchy as if they've been exposed to acid (or base, I'm not a chemist). I did the first aid thing and washed my ears off and put neosporin on them. I also washed the head phone pads with soap and water. and I'll also use a thick fabric (like a washcloth), for further testing of the problem. In the meantime, my ears are inflamed and there's a possibility that they will leak fluid again (as happened the first time, but I didn't wash my ears off then). I know this all sounds very scary - but it's my review. You can mitigate this by protecting your ears, and washing off the head phones first. Not what I've ever had to do before - but I guess that's what we have to expect from our consumer products nowadays.

reviews.author: CP
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 5, 2026
reviews.content: Works well, I'm not sure if any software was downloaded or not. My son accidentally tripped over his cord and broke the headset plug after having it for 8 mo. He has since used my husband's bose noise cancelling set and I'm not comfortable with that, he should be able to hear a little. So he raved about this when we got it, so I'm buying again.

reviews.author: Doug Haggard
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 25, 2026
reviews.content:
Ordered these around November 10th, delivered as indicated on 12th, packed well & arrived in good shape. I immediately put them on a charger as indicated prior to use. I usually am up 2 to 3 hours prior to my wife. The 13th I paired these with the Dish 3 Hopper.

Most days since, I used these an hour or two to watch recordings of shows. Today is February 25th & I got the 1st notice of low power. I have put them back on the charger for 1st time since the original arrival charge. I rate these 5 Stars for living up to time between charges. Note, using several hours at a time, versus 1 or 2 may increase rate of discharge.

Performance listening to music is also excellent. I was also pleased with quality on listening to YouTube Music in 8D & greater tunes. I lost a lot of hearing in left ear in Vietnam. I swear in those songs, my left ear appears to be working again. Again, 5 Stars for audio performance for Music & TV Shows/Movies.

I find these headphones great for comfort & they fit over my ears fine. However, one having larger ears than I may find a tighter fit. Other reviewers have noted this. For me, 5 Stars for comfort!

Only nit I'd pick, is this model does not support showing remaining power level in the App. However, headphones do have a verbal low power warning. I received this today with about 40 minutes left in a TV recording. I was able to finish watching it with a couple more warning. I turned headphones off at end & put them on the charger. Despite App not showing power left, the verbal warning is sufficient. I would like to see JBL indicate in specifications that this model does not support sending App Power Level Remaining. Still, would rate 5 Stars overall with this one caveat.

reviews.author: John R. Hansen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026
reviews.content: Work great! Battery life is amazing! Days and days worth of play time on a short charge. Sound quality is great too. These things pair easily to everything! Sound system. Television. Phone. Highly recommend.

reviews.author: Mike Jigande
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 11, 2026
reviews.content:
I recently got my hands on the JBL Tune 720BT wireless over-ear headphones, and overall, they deliver a surprisingly solid experience for the price.

Here’s a breakdown:

Sound Quality: 🔊
The bass is punchy without being overwhelming, mids are clear, and highs are crisp. For casual listening—whether it’s hip-hop, EDM, or podcasts—these headphones hit the sweet spot. Audiophiles might notice some slight compression at high volumes, but for everyday use, it’s more than sufficient.

Comfort & Design: 😃
The over-ear design is comfortable for long listening sessions, and the ear cups are soft enough to avoid fatigue. They’re lightweight, and the foldable design makes them easy to pack for travel.

Battery Life: 🔋
JBL claims up to 40 hours of battery life, and from my experience, that seems accurate. A quick 5-minute charge gives you enough juice for a couple of hours, which is super convenient if you’re in a rush.

Connectivity: 📶
Pairing via Bluetooth is quick and seamless. The connection is stable even when moving around the house, and switching between devices is easy.

Extras: 🌎
The built-in controls let you manage music and calls without reaching for your phone. While there’s no active noise canceling, the passive isolation from the over-ear design does a decent job at reducing background noise.

Verdict: ✅
The JBL Tune 720BT is a great pick if you want solid wireless sound, long battery life, and comfort without breaking the bank. Perfect for students, commuters, and casual listeners.

Rating: 5/5 ⭐

reviews.author: Ed
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 2, 2026
reviews.content:
These headphones are an impressive upgrade, especially coming from someone who only recently started using Bluetooth headphones. I was initially concerned about sound fidelity, but those worries were quickly put to rest.

I previously owned the JBL on-ear version, which sounded good but eventually failed at the pivot point near the ears. By comparison, these feel more solid and thoughtfully designed.

The sound quality is excellent, with clear, well-balanced audio, and pairing with both a Mac and a PC is quick and effortless. They fully cover my ears and provide a comfortable, secure fit. They are slightly snug for me, but that’s more a matter of my generously sized head than a flaw in the design. Overall, they deliver both comfort and performance in a way that exceeded my expectations.

reviews.author: silvio pereira
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content: The sound from these headphones is INCREDIBLE! The noise cancellation is also really good and the battery life too. Just make sure not to leave your device connected to them when not in use because they won’t turn off alone. You definitely get your moneys worth with these though because unfortunately after about two years they have started to fall apart but I will be purchasing another one soon!

reviews.author: Kalley
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 5, 2026
reviews.content:
If you're looking for affordable, reliable, and stylish wireless headphones, the JBL Tune 720BT checks all the boxes—especially for college students who need great sound on the go.

JBL is known for its legendary bass, and the Tune 720BT delivers exactly that. With JBL Pure Bass Sound, the audio is deep, punchy, and immersive. Whether you're listening to music while studying, watching lectures, or taking a break with your favorite playlist, the sound quality is clear and energetic without being overwhelming.

Equipped with Bluetooth 5.3, these headphones provide fast pairing and a stable connection. Switching between your laptop and phone is smooth—perfect for students juggling online classes, Zoom meetings, and music streaming throughout the day.

College life can be busy, and these headphones are made to keep up. The build feels sturdy yet lightweight, making them easy to toss into a backpack without worry. The cushioned ear cups are comfortable for long study sessions at the library or long commutes between classes.

reviews.author: Kelly
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026
reviews.content: Love these headphones. They sound great and are comfortable to wear for multiple hours. I wanted beats but they were too expensive, so this was a nice alterative. I love the color and design. The charge last for multiple days and you can use any type c charger which is great. I love how they included a wire for devices that need a headphone jack. I have never seen that before.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content: Fast charging, good sound and are very easy to connect via Bluetooth technology! I couldn't find a good pair of wired headphones to use on a plane to connect to my phone to watch movies. This is a perfect solution for both my phone and my Ipod. They fit over the ears and are comfortable to wear for extended periods. I would recommend these to anyone seeking some good bass sounding headphones!

reviews.author: James Klarkowski
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 11, 2025
reviews.content:
It's getting a 5 star. Not because it's 5 star quality, like you'd expect from Sennheiser or Bose, but because it's surprisingly good quality relative to the price point.
For $10, you expect some hollow, crackling, gas station-level trash earbuds, but what you get with these is somewhere in the range of early skull candies that you would get for $15-20 over 10 years ago.

These are a good set of very basic headphones. The sound is good. They're comfy and they fit well (with extra tips for different ear sizes.)

If you're bargain shopping for headphones, I don't expect that you're looking for crazy features or amazing quality, and as such, I suspect that these will meet your expectations without issue.

reviews.author: Frank Kroto IV
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content: As of today, these are fantastic for the price. Probably the best cheap earbuds you can buy at 10$ price range. Sizes were good for me. They are mostly comfortable. Block sounds well. Clear audio. Had to click “headphones” prompt when plugged into iPhone. Will update In a month if issues occur.

reviews.author: Jessie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 15, 2023
reviews.content:
Honestly, when I bought these I wasn't expecting much. I just needed a pair for a brief period of time while I worked on getting a genuine headset. But I've had these things for about a year now and I'm happy (and mostly surprised) to say that they still work.
I'm no audio connoisseur or anything- but based on my knowledge of having had a great many wired earbuds over the span of my life, I can say that these are actually pretty decent. The sound quality is good and has remained such. The microphone quality is not the greatest but you'll definitely be audible (and it has also continued to work). And most important of all in my opinion; the earbud jack, and the part of the cable that connects to the earbud/mic itself, has remained strong and intact.
Which, if you've ever had earbuds, chances are you know the feeling of having to practically crack the DaVinci Code moving the wire every time you want to get audio in both ears.
But with these, I've never had that problem in the entirety of owning them.

So TDLR: if you're looking for pretty cheap headphones, want them to last a long time, and don't really care about audio quality either way; these are a great pair to purchase.

But also a bit of a heads-up; the wire itself is a bit flimsy, so do be careful with it. Otherwise you should be good!

EDIT: Just my luck, a few weeks after I wrote this review, the headphones finally gave out. Left side only works if I hold the wire at a very specific angle, and right side gave out completely. Overall though, they lasted a little over a year total (with my heavy use and terrible treatment of the things), so really still worth getting if you need a decent pair of earbuds for a low cost.

reviews.author: Gabby
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 1, 2025
reviews.content: Great quality and super comfortable! They’re amazing for the price. I bought some from a different brand that were more expensive than these. The sound was horrible and the hard plastic hurt my eardrums. Within 6 days they were glitching and didn’t work anymore. That made me appreciate the sound quality and comfort of these earbuds even more. You don’t have to wiggle the cable to fix the sound or anything. I’m very happy with this purchase, and they work great for zoom calls because they have a microphone included.

reviews.author: V. Sofitel
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 17, 2025
reviews.content:
I rarely use earbuds, but there are definitely times when they come in handy, and there is NO WAY I am putting bluetooth inside my head. In upgrading my phone, I am forced to get a USB-C set of ear buds and these ones worked fine and until they didn't. So with minimal use in 10months they crapped out during a call.

The value for the money is I guess reasonable, since it essentially cost me about a $1/mo to use and they did work well when they worked. The ear buds were comfortable and came with three different sized bud covers. The sound quality was great until they stopped working.

reviews.author: Austen Waldron
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content: In this world of wireless accessories, some folks just want a traditional wired headphone. They can be hard to find in store because they’re not as in high demand, and phones don’t even have a headphone jack anymore. My dad has a habit of watching videos on his phone at a volume my mom deems unacceptable, so these were a perfect gift for him. He likes the a lot and says the sound quality is great. They work with his iPhone and fit perfectly comfortable in his ears. For the price it was well worth my mom’s peace of mind and dad is happily watching his videos on whatever volume he desires. Much easier to keep up with for him than a tiny case and two tiny ear buds. He can wrap these around his phone and find them easily. A win win!

reviews.author: Vincent M.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 23, 2025
reviews.content:
These are genuinely good sounding earbuds. They're deeper and punchier and grungier sounding than my other buds and headphones. Obviously not gonna be amazing sound quality, but really surprisingly good for $10. Especially when I bought them just to sit at my desk at work.

Can't recommend them for exercise. Can't say they'll be durable and I doubt theyre sweat resistant, and I don't think they'll stay in the ear great when in motion, but just trying them out sitting on the couch in my quiet living room,

actually really awesome. Do recommend.

reviews.author: KJ
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviews.content: I wanted something cheap for meetings, this does a great job!

reviews.author: William Taylor
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 31, 2025
reviews.content: These were bought for replacement headphones for an assisted listening system in a church. We bought two of these and two low price Sony headsets which were three times the cost and put all four in service. I gave these 5-stars because at around $5.00 per headset they work just fine for what they are intended and could be affordably replaced if they get dirty or damaged. They do not sound bad but are not audiophile quality by any means. They work fine for the assisted listening receivers. The ear cushions are foam so you cannot clean them and if multiple people are using them you need to keep an eye on them and replace when needed. We hand out the two Sony's (which can be wiped down) first then these so with the minimal usage, they should last a very long time making them a good long term investment and at the low cost we are not going to feel bad if we have to replace one.

reviews.author: JeLyBr
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 26, 2013
reviews.content:
Our teenage son always seems to somehow 'confiscate' and then proceed to break and/or lose every set of earbud-type earphones in our house, including mine that came with my iPod. So, he has now been relegated to only owning the least expensive kind I can find for him (even if that means pink ones, lol). So, after perusing Amazon, I bought these headphones for him because 1) they were so inexpensive, 2) the reviews were pretty good, and 3) did I mention they were so inexpensive?

Well, when they arrived, I opened them up to check them out and make sure they worked and such. I was impressed and I really liked them. But when I gave them to my son, I quickly learned that, according to my son, over-the-head type "headphones" (as opposed to the in-ear or ear bud type) are so "80's" and "uncool looking". Was I angry at him for basically calling me "old and uncool" for liking these headphones? No way!! This was my perfect solution to keep him from "borrowing" and then losing/breaking MY headphones. Besides, I never really liked the ones that go into the ears anyhow.

So now these are mine! For what it's worth, I feel neither "old" nor "uncool" while wearing them. I promise!!

*They fit great, they are snug but not TOO snug. If I was into jogging and/or some other type of strenuous exercise, I don't think I would have any trouble keeping them in place on my ears/head (I swim for exercise so obviously I can't wear them for that, lol).

*The quality of sound is fantastic to me - although I'm not really an "audio snob".

*They are extremely adjustable on both sides although I happen to use them on the smallest setting without needing to make them any bigger.

*They are lightweight, as stated in the description, but not in a 'flimsy' kind of way. I don't think they are any more or less breakable than any other kind of similar headphones (yes, I did go through many sets of headphones like these during the 1980's).

Since I don't expect to be rough with them at all, I am hoping they last me quite awhile. If not, and they do somehow break, at least I wouldn't feel *too* bad only being out a few dollars. I would ABSOLUTELY order these again and highly recommend them to anyone looking for inexpensive, but nice, headphones. They are perfect for this "uncool" mom. :-)

***UPDATE*** June, 2015: Well it's been nearly two years and I've gone through about three pairs of these headphones (and I'm going to order a few more after I write this - now that they have pretty color options). Not one pair has broken yet, they just seem to get lost somehow (in our house, we joke that when things go missing it's because unicorns come and eat them). I'm glad they have color options now and I would still absolutely recommend these inexpensive, and nice, headphones.

reviews.author: Reid Family
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 3, 2025
reviews.content: I use these as PC headphones. For podcasts and a little bit of music listening, these are plenty good enough. For the price, you can easily buy several pairs and have backups. The sound quality is okay, but there isn't much bass response which is expected at this price point. I have a large head and these fit snug, but you can bend the plastic headband a bit and that works to get comfortable. The cord length is short, but good for a phone or sitting close to a laptop. It is a bit difficult to see which headphone is Left and Right, but there is an L and R if you look really closely. Be aware, there is no mic or volume button controls.

reviews.author: Toya Jackson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.content: A great value, even if the ear covers never stay on

reviews.author: jmr
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026
reviews.content:
Why didn't I think of this a long time ago??? Years of trying to hear dialogue from the muffled sound coming out of the speakers *behind* the TV, giving up and keeping subtitles on always. Kept vaguely trying to figure out where I could put a sound bar, and thinking how so many sound bars are apparently just *bad* (unless you spend hundreds of $ more - ?).
These lightweight headphones are great for the price, I no longer need subtitles, and with a 25' extension cord, I can even let the dog out without missing anything.

reviews.author: K C
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 29, 2025
reviews.content: Good enough. The top part that goes over the head seems to be made of plastic. It's very tight when placed over the head. I want to stretch it out to make it more comfortable, but am afraid it will crack. I just turn up the volume and wear around my neck. Headphones were cheap, so it's good enough.

reviews.author: Adam
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviews.content: I'd like to start this review by stating I am not an audiophile by any means, I just dabble in it. I needed a cheap, lightweight headset for listening to YouTube and other platforms when I grow tired of my wireless earbuds; or they die and I'm frankly too lazy to charge them. This fits that need. The cord length is fine albeit a bit thin my cats did chew through one pair (bought two). Comfort wise is as expected for plastic speakers covered with a soft foam, if you had a Walkman back in the day, you're already familiar. The sound quality surprised me for the price, but they're Koss I shouldn't be surprised that they're good.

reviews.author: Cataptra
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 21, 2025
reviews.content:
Horrible headphones, not worth more around $1.00. Bought 2 sets, 1 for myself and 1 for my wife.

Within just an hour or two if my wife using hers one of the earpiece pads just fell off and wasn't able to be reattached, it just split apart, rendering it useless.

The audio from them is the worst I've ever heard, as a matter of fact I bought a set that was much nicer and cost less than these at my local Dollar General, and those were even better made and far superior sound quality too.

I can't even describe how horrid the sound is on these, but I sure can't see why anyone, especially a school would recommend these for young students, they're not a great quality in build or audio for student use. Absolutely not.

I only bought these because my local D.G. Store was closed currently out of stock and no idea when they'll get the ones that lasted me over 7 years for a smaller price, folded up, had noise cancellation, and the ear coverings were like very soft earmuffs covered in plastic, comfortable.

These terrible headphones had none of this, but I did expect a better quality audio, but I believe the audio is diminished by using 32-ohm speakers instead of the standard 8-ohm type which gives far superior sound quality(my opinion) but that's also what my Dollar General headphones were rated 8-ohm and until I accidentally sat on them and broke one side off, I'd still be using those.

I absolutely DO NOT RECOMMEND THESE HEADPHONES! LOOK FOR A BETTER QUALITY PAIR. These are junk.

reviews.author: Coco
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 21, 2025
reviews.content:
You can hardly ever find me without these on. There are super portable and I like the cord between the earpieces so I can walk around without them in and they never get lost. I also like that they go over the ear because I can pull them out a little in an office and they stay on, but also I find it comfortable as someone with small ears and ear holes. They come with different sized buds so you can switch those out for comfort. Good sounds and clarity for the average user. I listen to multiple hours of podcasts everyday (and music for workouts) and have been satisfied with the performance and battery life. Great value for the money.
I try to review all the products I love so I hope this is helpful!

reviews.author: Claire B
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content: These are my favorites. For reference, I have a problem woth breaking ear buds and these will break(or get lost) if you're as careless as me.... So over the years I've purchased 3 different pairs of these between trying out other more expensive, wireless headphones. (Also borrowed my kids' when in need.) I swear there is something about the sound quality of these that I love more than any others. Perhaps bc they are actually loud and still maintain their sound. I also just like how they fit on my ear. Not my inner ear canal, but how they actually fit over my ear. I can go to the gym and they stay put and the little wire in between is never a nuisance. After ordering some Raycons a month ago, I was disappointed and I have since decided I will no longer keep trying more expensive name brands. These are it.

reviews.author: Michael Biggerstaff
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 16, 2025
reviews.content:
It works and the price point isn't bad.

Pros:
- audio quality seems pretty good
- (new) battery life is still pretty good
- control buttons are easy to use
- purchased for exercise as well as office work, they fit snugly
- support connecting to 2 Bluetooth sources at once (with slightly lowered volume level)

Cons:
- automated voice for "power on", "disconnected" and "power off" is low/damaged quality, lending quality concerns
- Bluetooth distance seems to be a 1-1.5 meters shorter than my prior unit
- doesn't seem to consistent provide correctly configured connection (IE volume up/down, media connection, audio ... fail 1 of 20 times; not sure the reason yet.)

3 June 2025: customer support reached out and replaced the prior unit with a new one which is appropriately improved. Customer Support was very good.

reviews.author: Nana Nana
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 6, 2026
reviews.content:
The ear buds are excellent quality for the price!

The seller reached out to me and handled the issue..

Very pleased!

reviews.author: Art
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 9, 2026
reviews.content: I like this type older style earphones, they way better than any airpods I tried this particular brand I am impressed, very nice bass and pretty good sound, for the price is good enough for me, if you are looking for cheap but good working earphones Bluetooth than I recommend those, I like good sound for my techno music and 90s rap music with strong bass and those earphones worked great, sure there are more expensive earphones and airpods but I am 52 years old and ain't spending over 200$ on headphones I learned my lesson just because it's expensive doesn't mean quality is there.

reviews.author: Amyss
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 18, 2025
reviews.content: I buy these headphones all the time ( family constantly “borrows” and doesn’t take care of them like I do). Recently another brand I was starting to enjoy got knocked to the floor, and in the dark when I got on I hear a plastic *crack*. I sighed, and immediately ordered Joywise and I am so happy- the noise cancellation is unbeatable, the comfort is 100%, after charging i can go 1-2 days without recharging depending on usage. The size for my ears is perfect and the carrier holds a charger, extra earplugs and headphones perfectly and slips right into my small purse. It’s perfect- please always have the available!!!

reviews.author: mw
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 30, 2025
reviews.content:
I've had these for a full year now and I use them daily. Sadly, I have to report that these have NEVER gotten anywhere near 16 hours of battery life. Closer to 10 hours on very low volume for the first month or so. Since then the battery life has gotten continually worse. At this point a full charge lasts, at best, 3 hours.

All of that said, they're very inexpensive and work well for what they are.

UPDATE: After posting this review the seller reached out to me and, much to my surprise, even after having had the product for a full year offered to send me a replacement.
