The new set definitely has tremendous battery life and I haven't had any problems with them at all. Upgrading to 5 stars for the amazing customer service .

reviews.author: Ross Mantia
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 19, 2025
reviews.content: We loved these bluetooth wireless headphones so much, we bought more of them. They stay in the ears until you take them off and the sound quality is perfect. They're comfy when wearing eye glasses. I use them an hour a day and don't have to recharge them for probably five days. Recharging only takes 30 minutes, or so. Will buy again.

reviews.author: Charles E. Brown
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 25, 2023
reviews.content:
From the moment I unboxed the Razer BlackShark V2 HyperSpeed Wireless Gaming Headset, I knew I was in for an extraordinary gaming journey. This headset is not just a piece of technology; it's a game-changer in the truest sense.

**1. Feather-Light Comfort:**
Firstly, the 280g lightweight design is nothing short of revolutionary. Long gaming sessions can often lead to discomfort, but with the BlackShark V2, it almost feels like wearing air. The headset sits comfortably on your head, ensuring that your focus is entirely on the game, not on adjusting your headset.

**2. Immersive Audio Experience:**
The THX Spatial Audio is a marvel. It's like having a high-end surround sound system right in your ears. The audio quality is crisp, clear, and balanced, allowing you to hear every detail in your game. From the subtle footsteps of an enemy sneaking up behind you to the roaring engines in a high-speed chase, the sound is so lifelike, it completely immerses you in your gaming world.

**3. Crystal Clear Communication:**
The bendable mic is another standout feature. It's not just flexible; it's also incredibly clear. Communication with teammates is flawless, with no background noise or interference. The mic picks up your voice with such clarity, it feels like you're in the same room with your team.

**4. Powerful Drivers for Dynamic Sound:**
The 50mm drivers in this headset are a powerhouse. They deliver sound that's not just loud but rich and nuanced. Whether it's the subtle guitar strumming in a game's soundtrack or the booming explosions in an action-packed scene, the audio quality remains top-notch.

**5. Versatile Connectivity:**
The options of 2.4GHz, Bluetooth, or USB connectivity make this headset incredibly versatile. Whether you're gaming on a PC, console, or mobile device, the BlackShark V2 has got you covered. The seamless switching between devices is a huge plus, making it a great all-in-one headset for all your gaming needs.

**6. Marathon Battery Life:**
The 70-hour battery life is simply astounding. Knowing that your headset can last for days on a single charge is incredibly reassuring. And when you do need to power up, the USB Type-C charging is quick and convenient.

**7. Sleek Aesthetic:**
Lastly, the design and aesthetic of the headset deserve a mention. The sleek black look is stylish and professional, making it a great accessory for gaming setups and workstations.

In conclusion, the Razer BlackShark V2 HyperSpeed Wireless Gaming Headset is a masterpiece of gaming technology. It's comfortable, offers exceptional sound quality, has a fantastic mic, versatile connectivity options, and a battery life that keeps you gaming for days. It's not just a headset; it's an indispensable tool for any serious gamer looking to elevate their gaming experience. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Marina Cabral
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 25, 2025
reviews.content:
I’ve been a Razer fan for over 10 years and once again, they nailed it. I bought this to use with my PS5 and it was super easy to connect — totally plug and play. The wireless experience is smooth and reliable.

The sound quality is absolutely insane. It’s super immersive and also blocks outside noise really well. The mic picks up my voice clean and clear, and the headset is surprisingly very comfortable, even after long gaming sessions.

What I love the most is the battery — I’ve been using it for 5 days straight and still haven’t had to charge it. Switching from PS5 to PC is also super quick and easy.

Honestly, I don’t have a single complaint. If you’re thinking about it… just go for it. Totally worth the investment!

reviews.author: Chris
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content:
DONT WASTE THE MONEY!!! This device will constantly have connection issues!

Ive purchased this Twice. First one, wouldnt connect to its own USB fob. Refusing to connect to its Non-Bluetooth Wireless option. I could connect via Bluetooth and get a worse sound quality (for both Inbound and outbound), or could connect Via Wired option. I bought this for a great gaming experience and to allow wireless due to needing to constantly get up for Children or other tasks and not wanting to be leashed.

After running into that issue of it not connecting, I contacted Razor support, They basicly made sure I wasnt stupid and connected everything correctly, and found that after I followed all the steps on the instruction book, that they couldnt do anything more to troubleshoot and found it faulty and told me to return through Amazon. So return submitted and I figured, it seemed like a good deal and a sale going on, I bought a 2nd one.

I got the 2nd one in the mail, and this time, following the same steps, YAY it connected! Worked just fine for the last 2ish months since I bought it. Then a couple days ago, I disconnected the cable/fob from my computer to take with me out of town with my laptop, Ended up not usinging. Came back, Pluged it back into my computer, and SAME issue as before... Now nolonger wanting to connect to the USB Fob. Ive done a handful of troubleshooting, and Its refusing to connect now.

Dont waste your money, this thing refuses to connect and there are much better options that will for the price with less issues. Sure you can use it wired for great sound, and has a decent cable with it, but dont do it for wireless unless you dont care about the poor sound quality of a Bluetooth Connectiln.

reviews.author: SEEK
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 25, 2026
reviews.content:
So far i am extremely happy with this headset.
I don't often come back to write reviews, but felt compelled on this one.
The headset is super light and the memory foam is great. Easy to forget you have the headset on.
Sound from them is fantastic in my opinion.
Using on Linux Mint if anyone is interested to know.

reviews.author: Jeffery Jelinski
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 18, 2024
reviews.content:
I did not buy these as my primary headset. I already have a dedicated audio stack in my computer room with a professional mic and many audiophile headphones. I do however have a living room PC hooked to a home theater, where I enjoy all of my emulators and controller friendly PC titles. For that setup these work well for relatively cheap, and allow me to voice chat with my friends while playing together or just enjoy a movie without waking the wife. They are massively convenient.

Build quality is... okay. They're very light but also feel plastic and cheap. There are clearly some build weaknesses as shown in review photos here on Amazon. I reinforced mine with a little electrical tape wrapped around the weak spots on the headband where the yolks attach. For the light use and gentle handling these get they should be okay. The original OG Blackshark was built from professional grade parts and was super rugged so this is kinda disappointing but hey you pay a premium for wireless that eats into other aspects of the build. Be gentle with them and pay $10 for a cheap headphone stand. The earpads are a kind of hybrid design and are fairly nice. Since the headphones are so light and don't clamp hard comfort is good. That said Razer is known for cheap earpads that blow out over time so be prepared to replace them eventually.

Wireless connectivity is great with the dongle. Syncs instantly and flips the Windows setting automatically when I turn them on, and flips back when I turn them off. Range seems good as I can move around the room or even walk over to the fridge and get a drink while still talking/listening without dropping out. Synapse is known to be a little buggy but I seem to have good luck with it. It offers good control over the headset with all the basic features we've come to expect. It can even do firmware updates over USB.

Sound is fine. These aren't audiophile headpones by any means but they sound just fine. Neutral with a little warmth and a little bass bump. They are a bit quiet out of the box but with Synapse 3 installed you can turn the gain up and then they get pretty loud. They aren't really special at 3D positioning but they're more than adequate to play Ghost Recon Wildlands or Helldivers 2 with my brother. They work well enough for movies and music and don't ever sound bad in anything. Just average. Lots of headphones in this range have problems with their sound so for me this is a win. Others have mentioned bad mic quality or low mic volume on older models of this headset but the HyperSpeed seems to have fixed that issue. The mic isn't amazing quality but has clean sound and is easily loud enough for voice chat via Discord.

Overall these aren't a bad choice. If you need something that just works, doesn't sound awful, and won't make you poor, these are the ones. It's a lot more money for appreciably better build quality, and at this level sound quality is kind of low priority so you take what you can get. I think long term I will probably be skipping "gamer" brands and looking at Audeze Maxwell. My other Audeze headphones are fantastic. In the meantime this is a solid entry level headset that ticks all the boxes.

reviews.author: Raymond
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 28, 2023
reviews.content:
Well, at least, my 5+ year headset(I just need the wireless for exercise, I can plug it in to the PC if I need to)...

You can hear that they took the Razer Blackshark V2 Pro's drivers and put them in these.
The sound quality is superb, I can't really knock it because while it feels a little weak on bass, my Blackshark V2 X's were heavy on bass, so that is probably it. Plus, Razer Synapse makes it easy to EQ them to have a bit of a bass bump. Funny thing, EQ's work as long as you follow the 'zero' rule. Set the highest band on the EQ to 0DB and go from there. Then just turn up the headsest itself. The Blackshark V2 X and Hyperspeed are both loud enough that this is fine.

Install the firmware!!! It's pretty straightforward and easy as long as you have a PC(why would you buy these otherwise?)... just do the usual firmware rules, make sure it's plugged in and staying that way etc...
It's worth the trouble.
When you connect it to bluetooth, you can double-tap the right button to switch between the PC wireless and the bluetooth. Both preserve the superb sound quality. I guess I'm too deaf to notice bluetooth compression!

And finally, use the included USB cable if at all possible. It acted finnicky when I tried to use my own. Might try it again, as my own cord is 10 feet while the Hyperspeed's is 6 feet.
But with a 70 hour battery, I suspect I'll never need to use it wired as long as I charge it every night.

That's the only downside of this headset: There's no way to keep the charge between 30% and 80%, so they will die in, what, 5 years or so? Ouch. But I don't think any headset displays exact enough battery information to regulate the charge like this, so is it really a downside if it applies to everything?

The microphone is a huge improvement over the Blackshark V2 X... but it's a much more expensive headset so that's to be expected to some degree. But it's really good. It's not gonna be a Snowball or Yeti, but I think that's obvious.

Razer earned their 5 stars on this one. I made a spreadsheet of a bunch of headsets and their downsides before buying. This headset had the most bearable downsides(on paper before I bought it) so it's the one I chose. I chose wisely.

reviews.author: TylerOLifts
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.content: 10/10. Hugs nicely on my ears which I love while gaming. The noise cancellation is the best part but my girlfriend hates that I can’t hear her talk to me while i’m gaming lol. Got it on sale and definitely the best headset I ever had. I use it to play Counter Strike (Csgo/Cs2). The built in mic is not removable which is okay because if i don’t use it i just added velcro pads to move it out of the way. The quality of the mic is amazing though, is on the same level as my hyperx quad cast.

reviews.author: Cheyanne
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 3, 2026
reviews.content: These were bought for my husband as a Christmas gift. He states that they're extremely comfortable on his head and ears, not being too tight and fitting over his ears. The sound quality is great when he is gaming and he likes that they're wireless. The battery life is great, with the headphones only needing to be charged every few days.

reviews.author: John Brooklyn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Music, Travel, Gym, Sport
reviews.content: I rarely leave reviews, but how was this product? It was so good, after about a month, I bought a second pair! For the low price of $15.98, you will not find anything better! No matter how long I use them (and I use them a lot listening to podcasts all day), they never seem to fall below 90% charged. They are comfortable to wear, and while music quality-wise, I'm sure you could do better for a lot more money, the quality is just fine, with different EQs to choose from, though I usually just leave it on Pop or Rock. It's a great buy for the money IMHO.

reviews.author: Taleisha
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Music, Travel, Gym, Sport
reviews.content: Affordable quality, sound is great Bluetooth connectivity range is amazing. EQ settings to fit just what you’re looking for. Budget friendly. Noise cancellation is amazing perfect for travel and working out must have will definitely add to my linktree easy to use volume control is excellent sleek design size appropriate for all heads easy setup comes with charging cord and aux cord to connect to your device cord is ideal length

reviews.author: My Trendy Vida With Marigold. 🌼
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 13, 2026
reviews.format: Color: PurpleSize: StandardStyle: Music, Travel, Gym, Sport
reviews.content:
I absolutely love these headphones. They are extremely comfortable, snug and secure without ever feeling tight. I can wear them for long periods with no discomfort at all. Turning them on and off is simple, the volume controls are easy to use, and the sound quality is fantastic.

What really blew me away is the price. I snagged these on sale for $20, and honestly, they feel like headphones that should cost much more. The value is unreal. I ordered the purple color, and it is absolutely beautiful, soft, rich, and eye catching.

I’m beyond happy with this purchase and would 100% buy these again as a gift for just about any occasion. Comfortable, great sound, gorgeous color, and an amazing deal, this product is a win all around.

reviews.author: Yostina Ibrahim
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2026
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Music, Travel, Gym, Sport
reviews.content:
Pretty good headphones
Pros: The headphones hit all the beats and there are no issues I noticed with the sound. They last for a pretty good time. I enjoyed how they were easy to connect to my phone and laptop. With my other earbuds, I had to disconnect them on my phone in order to connect them to my laptop but this isn’t the case here. It’s lightweight and easy to carry.
Cons: If I’m on my phone, scrolling, I felt the sound was too loud, even on the lowest level. It could just be a sensory thing for me or the fact that I’m just getting used to them. It could be a stupid thing but some people may find it important: I don’t like the position of the pause button. It’s on the back and it’s a little bit of a hassle to simply pause my music. If you’ll be using these in an environment where you’re constantly pausing your music (school, work, studying with friends, etc) then consider this factor. I will say, this may not be a product for someone with very sophisticated taste in technology. My sister hated them but I got the opinions of others and they thought it was fine. They aren’t noise canceling so that’s either a pro or a con depending on your situation.
Overall, for the price, it’s pretty decent quality. If your someone who isn’t too picky about every detail of your headphones, this isn’t a bad pick.

reviews.author: Dannyboy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Music, Travel, Gym, Sport
reviews.content: Great build quality and excellent sound. I cannot believe the price of $16.00. I can understand why these headphones are sold in great numbers. The sound is warm and airy with a detailed bass. There is no electronic noise cancellation but they seem to seal out the noise very well. They are extremely comfortable and do not overheat my ears. The headphones pair well with my Fiio Snowsky Disc which was my reason to try them. My Apple AirPods Pro and my SoundCore Q35s would not pair with the Snowsky Disc which seems to have Bluetooth issues.

reviews.author: Lokiamus
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2025
reviews.format: Color: Rose GoldSize: StandardStyle: Music, Travel, Gym, Sport

reviews.author: Amanda H.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 17, 2026
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Music, Travel, Gym, Sport
reviews.content: I've had these for 2 years. (Black headphones). They still work perfectly. They stay charged for 2-3 days without having to be recharged. They fit over your ears. The sound is really good. Pretty durable and very comfortable to wear. They help cancel out outside noise, not completely, but it's acceptable. These are really nice and I would buy again for myself and others.

reviews.author: Kevin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Music, Travel, Gym, Sport
reviews.content: Bought these from my Grandson and he loves them. Easy to connect and then stay connect up to 30 feet away. Sound quality is great too. Great Product for the price.

reviews.author: Zachary King
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content:
This is hands down the best wireless headset I've tried so far. There's practically zero noticable latency and the sound quality is great, not to mention the mic quality is amazing. I love that it has on-board audio and voice backend controls and a mic mute button on the back. The battery lasts a very long time (but I still instinctuay plug it in when I'm not using it). And I love that you can seamlessly swap between the dongle and bluetooth, so I could swap between my PC and my phone if I so desired. They're also really comfortable on my big head, one of the most comfy headsets I've owned even. They aren't even heavy either. I've only had it for a few weeks now and I already love it.

My one and only complaint is that the mic is removable instead of being attached and having a twist-up feature to hide it from view like some headsets do, and I am scared of removing it from the jack because I don't want to wear down the connection over time. But this is such a nothing complaint that I can't help but give it 5 stars regardless. And yes, I bought the cat ears for it haha

If you're thinking about buying it, then I say you should go for it. You're NOT gonna find a better headset within this price range

reviews.author: Nathan D. Wilder
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 22, 2025
reviews.content: Fantastic, the way the ears extend is clunky, but also super comfy and takes the stress out of the angle, reducing pressure compared to regular gaming headsets I have used. Spacial awareness by 3d sound works well in Fortnite and Borderlands 4. Mic noise cancelation and focus are amazing compared to my last headset of another brand. Also, the wireless signal from the dangle when inserted into the front port of my PS5, not hidden behind the TV, carries clear audio across the house, wheres prior headset plugged into the controller, audio would break. I was concerned I would have the poor experience shared by many of another brand that underperformed, but this came highly recommended by people I trust who game hard. Money well spent, sits well with glasses, after hours no idea it's there. Local voice level (hear yourself) being on the right ear can is odd, but once known is cool. Mute is hard to find at first, but once you know, you know. Super nice charge cable and port extender cables, antigrip, and flatter. Very smooth use and design, took a second to adjust to the mic position, but works well for me now. Can also be used plugged in, not just wireless! My PS5 detected it when connected and on from charging. Dig it.

reviews.author: Jayden
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.content: Good quality, razer software allows plenty of options for adjustments, great headset of you're not trying to break the bank

reviews.author: Darkside83807
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 15, 2025
reviews.content: Excellent battery life, Mic and sound quality, and exceptional range, works with dongle or via Bluetooth, highly recommend for the low price.

reviews.author: SAYED MOHAMMED JAWAD
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 1, 2025
reviews.content:
Here’s a review for the Razer BlackShark V3 X HyperSpeed, hitting all your points!

Razer BlackShark V3 X HyperSpeed: A Wireless Contender with Jaw-Dropping Battery Life

**Rating: ★★★★☆ (4 out of 5 stars)**

As someone who spends countless hours gaming, communication and comfort are non-negotiables. I recently got my hands on the Razer BlackShark V3 X HyperSpeed, and after putting it through its paces, I have some solid thoughts to share. It truly shines in several key areas, though there are a couple of points I hope Razer addresses in future iterations.

The High Points (What I Absolutely Loved):

1. Microphone Quality - Simply Stellar!
Seriously, the mic on this headset is a showstopper. My teammates noticed the upgrade immediately. My voice comes through crystal clear, without any noticeable compression or background noise. For competitive gaming or even just casual chats on Discord, this mic ensures every call-out and joke lands perfectly. It's a huge step up from many other gaming headsets I've tried.

2. Unbelievable 70-Hour Battery Life
This is where the BlackShark V3 X HyperSpeed truly blows the competition out of the water. The advertised 70 hours of battery life* isn't just marketing fluff – it's real! I can go for days, sometimes even a week or more, of regular gaming sessions without needing to plug it in. This freedom from constantly worrying about charging is incredibly liberating and a massive win for marathon gaming sessions.

3. Seamless Communication
Beyond the excellent mic, the overall communication experience is rock-solid. The HyperSpeed wireless connection is incredibly stable, providing a lag-free and consistent connection. I've experienced no dropouts or stutters, which is crucial for intense gaming moments where every second counts.

4. Comfort if Your head is circle
Razer has done a fantastic job with the comfort aspect. The headset is surprisingly lightweight, and the plush earcups are incredibly soft and breathable. I can wear these for hours on end – during long gaming sessions, work calls, or watching movies – without any discomfort or ear fatigue. It truly feels like it disappears on your head.

Areas for Improvement (Where It Could Be Better):

1. Gaming EQ Can Be Detrimental
This was a bit of a head-scratcher for me. While I appreciate the inclusion of gaming EQ profiles, I found some of them to be overly aggressive or poorly tuned, often making the audio sound harsh, tinny, or even muddy, rather than enhancing it. Instead of giving me a competitive edge, it sometimes felt detrimental to the overall sound quality. I often found myself reverting to a flatter profile or manually tweaking things to get a more balanced sound.

2. Lack of Active Noise Cancellation (ANC)
In a bustling household or a noisy gaming environment, the absence of ANC is noticeable. While the earcups offer decent passive noise isolation, a proper active noise cancellation feature would have really elevated the immersion. I occasionally found myself distracted by ambient sounds, wishing I could completely tune out the outside world.

3. Lack of Firmware Updates
This is probably my biggest frustration. Since its launch, there seems to have been a noticeable absence of firmware updates. Ongoing firmware support is crucial for bug fixes, performance improvements, and potentially even refining those EQ profiles. It feels a bit like a "set it and forget it" situation from Razer, and I hope they address this with more consistent updates in the future.

Final Verdict:

The Razer BlackShark V3 X HyperSpeed is a truly compelling wireless gaming headset, especially if **microphone clarity, unparalleled battery life, and long-session comfort** are your top priorities. It's a fantastic daily driver for communicating with your squad and enjoying uninterrupted gaming. However, if you're an audiophile who expects finely tuned EQ presets out of the box or if active noise cancellation is a must-have for your environment, you might find yourself wishing for a bit more. Despite these minor drawbacks, it offers excellent value for its core strengths.

reviews.author: DrBucky
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026
reviews.content: Everything is great so far. I have yet to do anything on computer with these MULTI-TASK beauties! I use for my PS5 (awesome) and then switch over to my telephone effortlessly. Music is clear and as deep as ya want it!

reviews.author: Cohen Holder
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 10, 2026
reviews.content: Before saying anything I wanna say that I was "upgrading" to this headset from the Razer Blackshark V2 Pro (2020), which obviously at the time was a much more expensive headset than the V3 X Hyperspeed. But I thought I'd give these a try, assuming that 6 years of "innovation and development" would mean that these headphones, even though cheaper, may be better sounding. Nope, I was very wrong. I got these out of the box, super excited to try them out, and I was shocked when I first put them on. Not only were they much worse in audio quality than my V2 Pros, they may have been the worse audio quality I have heard on a headphone in probably a decade. I've purchased $10 headsets from the dollar store for my siblings, they sounded 10x better than these. Only good thing I'm able to say about these is that the wireless capability is pretty amazing, they are able to be much farther away from my pc than my V2 Pros while still staying connected, could even go outside if I wanted.

reviews.author: Nathan M Davalos
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 11, 2025
reviews.content:
The quality is meh, not terribly impressive. I guess it's worth the money when it's on sale.

VERY IMPORTANT INFORMATION FOR BUYERS
1. You need to get the Synapse software from Razer in order to really use the headset and get GOOD quality sound out of it

2. YOU HAVE TO PLUG IT IN TO YOUR PC WITH THE PROVIDED USB CABLE FOR THE SYNAPSE SOFTWARE TO DETECT YOUR HEADSET - THIS IS A MUST WHEN YOU GO TO CONFIGURE IT

3. It appears whatever configurations you do in the Synapse software get copied to the headset maybe? It appears once you configure it the configuration still applies when you are using it over the wifi dongle or bluetooth

4. Bluetooth works just as well as the wifi dongle, so if your PC has bluetooth (and a newer version like 6) you're fine not using the wifi dongle or USB cable

5. In order to use the headset with the 7.1 surround sound you need Synapse and you need to install the 7.1 Surround software for Synapse - DO NOT INSTALL THE THX SPATIAL AUDIO SOFTWARE FROM RAZER, IT IS NOT THE RIGHT MODULE

6. You MUST register the headset on the Razer website and wait maybe 20-30 minutes for the confirmation email. You should also get a product key for the 7.1 Surround software module for Synapse around the same time. Wait for the emails before you try to do anything with the headset

The most frustrating part of the whole setup and purchase is trying to navigate the Razer site and figure out what the hell to do with this stuff. The above notes should get you through setup and activation with little frustration.

reviews.author: Mandi Mills
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 10, 2026
reviews.format: Color: Classic BlackSize: StandardStyle: Barracuda XPattern: Headset
reviews.content:
I’ve been using this headset for gaming and mobile, and it’s been amazing. The sound is clear and immersive, whether I’m playing on my PC or my Switch, and the 40mm drivers really make a difference in quality. The detachable mic is handy, and I love that it’s lightweight enough to wear for hours without discomfort.

The battery life is impressive too—50 hours is more than enough for long gaming sessions or travel. Connecting via wireless or Bluetooth is easy, and it switches between devices smoothly. Overall, it feels premium, works reliably, and has quickly become my go-to headset.

reviews.author: Michael Orr
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 21, 2025
reviews.format: Color: Classic BlackSize: StandardStyle: BarracudaPattern: Headset
reviews.content:
I'm looking at buying another gaming headset after owning this one for 3 years! I immediately already know I'm purchasing the same thing.

Honestly it still works great, but the plastics get a bit brittle after this long. They're very comfortable still, even with glasses! That was the biggest hurdle I experienced with finding a headset. Something with soft ear pieces that would push my glasses into the sides of my head. 3 years and honestly they're still going strong. They can connect to both computer via usb dongle or any Bluetooth device. (computer, phone, etc) The mic is very clear to all my friends online. I actually have an audio interface with a phantom power suspended mic (very high dollar), and you can tell when I switch to the other mic, but my friends have told me it's only a slight difference. They're lightweight, and cordless which is always a win/win. Range on them seem pretty good as well. I game in the basement, and can walk out into the garage upstairs or even into the driveway of my house before experiencing any issues. Definitely reaches the bathroom without any issues and that's upstairs on the other side of the house. I did buy a 12ft long USB cord, plug the dongle into it, and put it on the other side of my gaming room to help extend the range a bit though. (put it at the bottom of the stairs, behind my couch). I've touched base on one "con" already with the plastics being brittle, which I've been just using "Mighty Putty" to fix and then letting it sit all night, then it's fine again, and honestly there's only one more small thing. The volume control on it seems a bit weird after about a year, if I had to guess on the timing. I try turning it down with the headset slider, and it'll turn it down then back up even with me only turning it down. It's really not a huge deal for me though, because when I turn it down a slider pops up on my computer screen and I can click on that to turn it down just as easily and with my phone obviously you can just use your side buttons to adjust the volume. Overall though these have definitely stood the test of time as far as the speakers go. Both right, and left still work just fine. Plenty of sound for someone looking to listen to music, audiobooks, video calls, or even trying to hear footsteps in your favorite game. The noise deadening works great, but not TOO much. If your house is on fire, dogs barking, or wife/husband/significant other is yelling at you, you'll know it. 😉😉 Another quick note is that the adjustment for size on it works as it should. I have a bit of a small head and it fits me just fine. Wife wears it fine too. I don't have to adjust it at all for it to fit me and in fact, at the largest size, it would probably fit a giant.. Just sayin. They get to be very large if that's what you're trying to find.

Like I said, after 3 years, and I'm now looking for a new headset, just strickly for anesthetics. (Tired of looking at the Mighty Putty haha) But tbh I'm seriously considering just buying the same headset again.

reviews.author: Lincoln F.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2026
reviews.format: Color: WhiteSize: StandardStyle: Barracuda XPattern: Headset
reviews.content: Some reviews talk bad about these things, but they're great. I love them, I got them at retail ($100), but Black Friday was like 2 weeks after I bought them and I didn't want to deal with all the hassle of returning them to save $30. They use a USB-C for the dongle (it was so hard to figure that out) which is GREAT for a gaming laptop that only has 2 USB-A ports that are currently in use for a wireless mouse and wireless keyboard. They're also Bluetooth, and I use them for my iPhone alongside my Airpod Pros, depending on my outfit. You can remove the mic so you don't look like a geek in public, and the razer logo blends nicely and doesn't stand out AT ALL, again saving you from looking like a geek in public. These things are loud too, with a great sound quality. Using the razer app on your phone you can boost and change the equalizer and make them even louder. They'd fit a lot of ears. The only issue I have is that when I'm shredding my eardrums, a lot of sound bleeds. I walked into my college English class like 5 minutes late and EVERYONE could hear my music. I mean everyone. It wasn't an auditorium classroom thank god, but it was your normal I guess high-school sized classroom. These things are great and sturdy too, I fell off my longboard and ran to stop myself from falling and these things shot off my head like a rifle and only slightly scratched the plastic. Not bad, would recommend one thousand percet.

reviews.author: Kindle Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 17, 2025
reviews.format: Color: PinkSize: StandardStyle: Barracuda XPattern: Headset
reviews.content:
Okay so... so many of these reviews talk about this headset having great audio. People who LOVE audio even say its so good...

I put these things on to set them up for my girl, played a youtube video and it literally sounded like a pair of airpods playing from across the room inside of a tin can. I was insulted at the audio quality. What the hell? I've owned several Razer headsets in my time and I've never had a single one sound so utterly trash. Immediate return.

The device itself? It's cute AF, she loved it, and it feels lightweight and sturdy. Though I'm sure with a non-careful hand one might snap something if they set it down to hard or yank to much to pull it off their head. Use TWO hands people. Cmon. I've seen your reviews...

Everything about this item is great, except the god awful sound quality when using bluetooth. I refuse to let her experience video games and music at this poor level of quality.

THAT being said, when plugging in the audio jack, the quality was slightly improved, but unfortunately only coming out clear through 1 ear cup, and half as loud in the other one, and not so improved as to be worth wearing these over some standard issue call center headset... Hmm. Literally a pair of 2005 ipod earphones sounds better.

I'm returning these ones and getting the exact same pair in hopes that perhaps it is just a defective model. I will update the review to let you all know.

I'll also look into whether or not it is the software drivers, which, low key hard to update as these headphones do not work with Synapse.

To be fair, I didn't try all that hard to update them due to the distaste I had from hearing the initial BT quality. I work with music and audio on hobby projects so yes I know about EQ apps and the like, but I wasn't even going to bother with this particular pair after hearing the audio jack quality.

Frankly you shouldn't need a 3rd party EQ app to make things sound better. They should sound good on their own, and the EQ apps can serve to enhance them if you want. Hard to enhance garbage sound quality though..

Really confusing when looking at all the glowing reviews for this product, so I'm holding out that there is hope for this purchase. We will see tonight when they show up. Razer is usually a good product.

Will keep you appraised.

*EDIT*

2nd pair sound more like it did with the audio jack plugged in. which was just OK. By no means amazing or great. My air pods have better quality than these. Like I said though, they are super cute and lightweight, and they earcups are comfy. I'm just disappointed in the sound. First pair was certainly defective.

reviews.author: Valentina M.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 27, 2025
reviews.format: Color: WhiteSize: StandardStyle: Barracuda XPattern: Headset
reviews.content:
I have been using this headset for a month and the sound is nice, not amazing, but nice. Considering how expensive gaming headsets are, this one is fine overall for the price.

Pros:

-Nice design, I fell in love with it since I was watching reviews, even if for some people the plastic material is not the best thing on market.

-As mention, the sound is really nice. Not amazing, but still works for what I was personally expecting.

-Mic is really good for a wireless gaming headset at this price (but still kinda high price even that, in my opinion).

-Really comfys, haven't had problems with that until now.

-Bluetooth connection works fine until now, and the dongle as well. I was a little scared of that because of the reviews. However I am totally disappointed on how the mic lose quality with the Bluetooth mode and the dongle on pc (it works better with the jack). Honestly is REALLY annoying, but the quality of the sound stays always the same and haven't had problems with connectivity until now. And even with the inconvenience, sounds clear, not perfect but clear.

Cons:

-Doesn't cancel noise, at least, not completely. For me is fine but there are maybe people who look for that.

-I was expecting to be able to use it with the usb cable but it only works with the dongle, and as I mentioned, I think is awful that the mic quality is affected by using it with the dongle or bluetooth on pc, it works perfectly with the jack. And considering they marketed this as "versatile" yeah, no. BUT again, with mobile devices both Bluetooth and dongle work perfectly fine. The sound quality for me is the same in all devices, but the mic no.

Conclusion: For the price, considering is a gaming headset, is fine. Even if it's 100 dollars (almost) at regular price, it's fine, and for a beginner headset, I would say it's worth it.

reviews.author: GR
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 27, 2024
reviews.format: Color: Classic BlackSize: StandardStyle: Barracuda XPattern: Headset
reviews.content:
I was looking for essentially the best bluetooth mic I could get for work. A boom mic would be the minimum requirement for me, because you need the mic physically close to your mouth, so it had to be some sort of gaming headset. After that, I looked for mic sound tests and all that.

So, you'll watch reviews and hear soundtests for the mic, and you'll think it sounds kinda bad on bluetooth. Plus some of the negative reviews on Amazon would make you concerned on the build quality. Even using the USB C dongle, other headsets like the Razer BlackShark V2 Pro or the Hyperspeed are significantly better.

Well, take this from an audiophile: I tried these first hand and they are great. From the build quality, the mic quality and ability to not pick up background noise, all the way to the sound quality on bluetooth. I was under the impression that I was going to settle for a mediocre mic, and thankfully that's not the case at all. This mic is up there with a desktop boom mic, and sounds better than my $150 Logi Pro X. Your voice comes across clear and crisp without background noise, even if you speak at a low volume.

The default EQ preset is garbage, but download the EQ app and set the preset to music or do your own custom preset, and it definitely surpassed my expectations. They also feel super solid though I'll have to break them in for a bit. So far, I love them. I'm glad I didn't go for the more expensive BlackShark hyperspeed. If these ever break on me, I'm more likely to buy them again than not. Sound clarity / definition is definitely good, comparable to quality open-backs like Sennheiser's. Sadly Razer uses a trash EQ by default, but nothing that can't be fixed by the user.

These can get "bust-your-eardrums" loud, if necessary. The thing itself is not uncomfortable and does not put a ton of pressure on your skull, but with time and use it gets gradually more comfortable.

I love how well-built they are. They are lightweight, but not in a cheap way. Razer is slowly earning me as a loyal customer, this is my third device from them and it's a hit like the others. First two being the Kiyo Pro webcam and the Siren V2 X mic.

One tip for Discord regardless of device or headset: Disable echo cancellation, It only causes problems.

The auto call switching feature is a nice add-on, and the USB-C dongle works on your phone if you want, even with a case on, as long as it's not a very thick case.

10/10, I hope Razer doesn't abandon this product line (at this price) and they continue to make newer / better iterations years down the line.

EDIT: I ended up downloading Wavelet on my phone and using an AutoEQ profile specific to the 2022 Barracuda X. I couldn't be happier, sound clarity and quality improved a lot. (If you wanna do this and previously used the Razer app to set an EQ preset, go back to that app, connect the headset, and change it back to default. Otherwise the AutoEQ won't work properly).

reviews.author: Emily Virgen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 28, 2022
reviews.format: Color: Classic BlackSize: StandardStyle: Barracuda XPattern: Headset
reviews.content:
Razer is renowned for being among some of the great gaming tech companies, and it shows in these headphones.

Quality:
First up, quality. The headphones have a sleek design. They’re not the most compact headphones you could get, but they are still slim enough to store or travel with.
They have a comfortable fit, and enclose my ear within the soft foam muff and padded headband.
The materials overall seem really sturdy, as in they should hold up for a great deal of time — you definitely get what you pay for, in a good way.

Noise Cancellation:
The headphones have pretty good noise cancellation in my opinion. With the volume up to 30-50%, it’s nearly impossible for me to hear a level speaking tone beyond them. When they’re off/ sound is muted, I can hear beyond them a bit; albeit the outside world is still fairly muffled. May not be the top of the line in noise cancellation, but they still do a mighty fine job.

Sound Quality:
As aforementioned I keep my audio about 30-50% of the way depending on the game/ who I am playing with. These headphones have excellent sound performance that, to an everyday user and a connoisseur by no means, would argue are a close second on sound quality to any expensive headset (noise cancellation aside).

Microphone (mic):
The mic itself is (as far as I’m aware) a good mic. It doesn’t pick up the hum of background noise, or sound fuzzy — thanks to the padding around the mic itself. Though, because it doesn’t pick up background noise it does need to be within about an inch or two of your mouth if you’re more soft spoken like myself (maybe not so much if you’re raging on it).
One minor concern I do have would be the foam the microphone cover is made of. It’s a very basic foam, so over a significant deal of time I do imagine it would take some wear and tear.
One thing I do like though is that the mic is detachable. It fits into the headset pretty snug (though it took a moment to learn how to make sure I had it in all the way properly), so taking it out and replacing it shouldn’t be a huge concern in terms of it fitting back in place in accordance to wear and tear. That said, you could easily use the headset to listen to music or watch tv in your day to day as well. Alternatively of course you could still leave the mic in and bend it out of the way.

Battery Life:
In terms of battery life, the headset holds a pretty decent charge. I believe I’ve gone like 3-4 days of using it for about 4-5 hours on average and still not needed a charge. I haven’t tested it’s limits, but it seem to have a pretty decent battery life for those who like to have extended gaming sessions/ usage.
That said, these headphones are wireless so if wireless & having to recharge headphones is not for you I wouldn’t recommend them.

Overall/ TLDR:
The headphones are great quality, and rival more expensive headsets at a great price for the everyday user. They were certainly worth the buy :)

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 21, 2023
reviews.format: Color: WhiteSize: StandardStyle: Barracuda XPattern: Headset
reviews.content:
This a review for both the Barracuda and Barracuda X

I started 2 years ago with the Barracuda X on sale. It was a good value. Comfortable, sounded good enough, excellent battery life. It had a 2 year warranty, but the headband broke 2 years to the DAY I ordered it from Amazon.

I literally ordered both the X and Barracuda on the same date, 2 years apart.

Well done, Razer.

The Barracudas were on sale, so I just decided to upgrade. I would never buy them at regular price. I like the aesthetics of not having a boom mic, as I can jump on a conference call and it doesn't look as gamy.

Yes, they sound a little better, definitely have a lot more bass, and they work with the Synergy software - which, honestly, should be available for the X as well.

The Synergy software was hell to get working, as it was clear my previously having another Razer headset installed screwed it up. It refused to identify the Barracudas as being set to default device, which blocked me from using the software. I saw other people with this issue on the forums and no one from Razer answered it - in fact they locked the thread. Inexcusable.

IMHO, these headphone companies probably contract developers to make or modify the software, then cut them loose after launch, and I suspect they have no maintenance team to speak of until they absolutely need to do a new version. That's how support feels. As a senior dev for major mobile apps, this level of bug would not last 30 days without a hotfix.

I did a dozen different tricks suggested in the forums but it was ChatGPT that bailed me out - evidently Razer has a cleanup tool to remove traces of previous installs, which allowed me to install clean and it worked. No thanks to Razer support.

As for comfort, they are a smidge heavier and band a bit tighter than the X, but they are less janky and feel very secure, like part of your head. So its a tradeoff. Not having a boom mic is great for drinking and eating and in general too, although the mic isnt quite as good as the X's boom.

If you are on a budget and not an audiophile, don't hesitate to get the X and save a few bucks. Its a great value. Just handle them carefully.

If you can get the Barracudas on sale and have a little more scratch its your call. I would even suggest staying with these two and ignoring other brands in the under two hundred level.

reviews.author: Abigail
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 11, 2025
reviews.content: A little bit on the bassy side of sound, but it's otherwise very high-quality. Music, games, movies, and even calls sound wonderful in it. I can't say the same for the microphone that comes in with one of the cables, as it tended to have a lot of white noise. Otherwise, the microphone was clear and my calls were audible with some noise cancellation, albeit it sucks to need that. The headset's comfortable enough to wear all day, as the cups are soft and the headband is quality. The cable length is great, but I wish it was a tad little longer for my case as my PC is under my desk and the cable barely reaches the headset on my head when seated a tad too far away from my desk. One of the cables also didn't last very long, as I had to switch to the other after half a year(?) of using it. Speaking of which, props to Corsair for bringing a mic cable and a non-mic cable for the headset. I love the simplicity of the headset - pure 3.5mm analog connection and no volume slider to worry about, like most other headphones. The VIRTUOSO was a solid buy.

reviews.author: Richard
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
reviews.content: The sound quality is fantastic and great for gaming and music production via Ableton. Listening too music extremely enjoyable...BUT after only owning them for 4 months of light use, the plastic casing on the headband that holds the adjustable slide broke making them fall apart on my head. Very disappointed with the build quality for the price. My Hyperx Cloud 2s lasted for 3 years before I ran into wear and tear problems.

reviews.author: Lindsey
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content:
Pros:
- Headset feels solid when you first get it.
- Mic and sound quality are decent.
- One of the few options for an open back headset.

Cons:
- Design flaw. The cushion cover just falls out. It happened to one headset and I had to pay $25 to ship it to Corsair before they would sent another one. The second one lasted 2 months before the same thing happened.
- The audio cable is awful quality and regularly makes audio go in and out unless you twist it just right.

reviews.author: Theo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 14, 2024
reviews.content:
Pros:
Excellent build quality
Extremely comfortable
Phenomenal soundstage, punchy and tight, accurate
Broadcast quality microphone
Can be used with or without mic
Ease of use

Cons:
Braided cables allow movement and abrasive noise to pass through into sound and mic
Mic Mute toggle is not easy to get to
On the heavier side for open back headsets
Top padding could have a bit more padding
Price is not attractive

Needed an(other) open back design headphone for gaming/work. Decided on the Virtuoso Pro because supposedly these were one of the best reviewed open back headset next and similarly compared to the pc38x (which I had). Virtuoso Pros are extremely comfortable, build quality is excellent, and its soundstage is phenomenal. The mic quality is great, they like to say “broadcast quality”, but I will say that heavy breathers and plosives emphasizers, beware because mic is sensitive, but it’s a good thing for sound quality. Speaking of mic sound quality, the braided design of the wires allow more sounds to be picked up, and I thought these sounds from my wires rubbing against my shirt or me touching it with my arm or controller were just passing through into the headphones, but it also passed through the mic and you could wear the annoyances in microphone playback recordings. It doesn’t bother me too much because I have the Virtuoso Pro plugged into my waveXLR which in turn allows me to use my Rode Mic as a dedicated microphone. And VSTs allow me to set thresholds so that sound pickup is limited. I love and need open back designed headsets/headphones. Yes, they allow external noise to leak in and out, but with two young toddlers in the house, I am not afforded the luxury to “block out noise” so it’s good that I can still hear them cry or yell if they need something. I just wished that they were priced significantly lower. $200 is knocking at audiophile territory, but $150, which they are currently at as of writing this review is much more attractive. $100-150 is the sweet spot, only because that’s where the pc38x usually sits at and these two are relatively speaking the best open back gaming headsets.

reviews.author: Efrain Acuña Miranda
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 27, 2025
reviews.content:
The left side input jack was faulty right from the start, I took the headset out of the box and connected to my PC, you need to wiggle the cable around to get sound, the headset comes with 2 sets of cables, the same issue happens with both sets.

I decided to reach out to Amazon support and they redirected me to Corsair support, they told me that they could replace the headset after further inspection but I needed to send it back, unfortunately I am from outside the United States and it was going to be costly to send them back then wait some weeks until they get to HQ to be reviewed and decide if they would send new ones back to me, so I decided to live with it and just wiggle the cable.

I was not going to leave any negative review because ultimately they were willing to review the situation, but about 2 weeks ago I noticed the ear pad getting detached from the headset and the fabric that covers the earpad, I decided to glue it and just live with it, unfortunately today the other earpad started to do the same, I always keep the headset on its case when not using it and I am careful to store them gently but even so thing is just falling apart.

Bad quality for the amount you paid.

reviews.author: Jbro808
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 3, 2023
reviews.content:
I've had this headset for a few months now and I can say I it's a great headset and worth the cost.

The good stuff:

*It's a great looking headset first off, it has a elegant look and feel to it, it's very well built, nothing feels flimsy or cheap.
*The sound quality is balanced and detailed, although it is a bit strong with the highs. The bass is solid and the open back aspect make it great for a bit of real ambient sound while gaming, it's hard to explain but games are kinda more immersive because of the open back.
*The detachable boom mic is great for gaming and streaming, but I wouldn't use it to record quality audio. It gets the job done like most gaming mics but there is room for improvement. The mic is detachable, which should be standard but it's nice that Cosair built it this way.
*The Cosair Virtuoso Pro comes with a really nice travel case, it's sturdy and looks good, I'd feel comfortable throwing it in my backpack while traveling for sure.
*Comfortable during long gaming sessions, the clamping force is solid but not to tight, even while wearing a hat and glasses. I have worn these for hours during gaming sessions and no discomfort at all, the ear cups are deep enough for most ears and are really soft and comfortable.

The not so good stuff:
*I'd say the price is the first con, although this is a great headset it's slightly overpriced. I'm going to keep them because I like the look and sound quality but if your a audiophile you could probably find a better headset for a bit less. But if your a gamer who wants a premium sounding headphones with a non rgb look then you can't go wrong with these.
*The only other con to me is the cables, the ones that attach to the headset (both mic and non mic ones) have a texture to them that kinda rubs on what ever it touches and makes a dragging sound that is distracting. It's not a big deal if your not moving your head around a lot, but if you move your head a lot while gaming this can be distracting.

All in all I really like this headset, I like that it's a gamer headset without the crazy gamer rgb look that almost everything has now. The sound is great and they are comfortable.

reviews.author: rosanne notabartolo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 8, 2025
reviews.content:
I've been using the Corsair Virtuoso Pro headset for months now, and I can honestly say it's one of the best headsets I've ever owned. The sound quality is truly phenomenal the audio is so crisp and clear, making every detail pop. As someone who has always used closed-back headphones, this is my first experience with an open-back headset, and I absolutely love it. The design allows me to hear my surroundings more easily, which is great for staying aware of my environment, while the audio itself has a natural, expansive quality that's incredibly immersive. Despite being an open-back design, I've found that it barely leaks any sound, which is a huge plus.
The versatility of this headset is fantastic, as it works amazingly with both my PC and my Xbox Series X. It comes with a mic attachment, which is a great bonus. The mic sounds pretty good for what it is, but for my primary use, I stick with my dedicated PC microphone.
The build quality is top-notch, and the headset feels very premium and durable. Comfort is another major highlight. I've spent countless hours gaming and studying while listening to music, and the generous padding on the earcups and headband makes it one of the most comfortable headsets I've ever worn. I can wear it for extended periods without any fatigue. Seriously, every aspect of this headset is five stars in my book. I would 100% recommend it to anyone looking for a high-quality, comfortable, and versatile audio experience.

reviews.author: Christian
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2025
reviews.content: The sound quality is pretty good! You can hear a majority of the things around you when you have it on. However, there's one glaring issue with this product. The quality of how it's built. I've only had this headset for about two months and am only able to use it on the weekends, but part of the headset that held the foam on top broke when I was putting it on, and the cover for the foam came undone and is slowly peeling off. There's also an issue with the right headphones jack where it has to sit just right on it, otherwise it won't have any sound coming out of it. I don't know if I just got a unit that wasn't built well or if this is for all of them. If you're looking for great sound, this is your headset. Just don't expect it to last too long if you plan on using it a lot

reviews.author: Alicia Brizzi
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 26, 2025
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Kraken V3 X
reviews.content:
These are wired over-ear headphones that focus more on passive noise isolation than active noise canceling. The ear cups fit snugly around the ears, which helps reduce outside noise without any electronics involved. This makes them useful in situations where you don’t want to worry about batteries or charging.

Sound quality is solid for everyday listening. Bass is present but not overpowering, mids are clear, and vocals come through cleanly. They perform well for watching videos, casual music listening, and computer use. The wired connection is stable with no lag, which is helpful for video editing or streaming.

Comfort is good for extended use. Padding is soft enough that it doesn’t cause pressure quickly, though long sessions may still require short breaks. Build quality feels decent for the price, with adjustable sizing that accommodates different head shapes.

Overall, these are dependable wired headphones that do what they’re supposed to do without unnecessary features.

Theres no need to worry about Battery life becuase its wired.

Its fully compatible with ANY device, HOWEVER you need to make a razer ID in order to unlock the free 7.1 surround sound, whitch is a complicated process.

reviews.author: John Schuler
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Kraken V3 X
reviews.content: Works amazing! Sounds clear, the buttons feel great, amazing steal for the price, they feel sturdy

reviews.author: AmazonUser
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 21, 2022
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Kraken V3 HyperSense
reviews.content:
Backstory, I was looking for a new gaming headset, and I went with the very highly reviewed hyperx cloud alpha S, and hated it. I decided to try this one out because I’ve always enjoyed razer products, even if there are a few bad reviews.

This headset blows the HyperX out of the water. Better sound, way better comfort, clearer mic, and the hyper sense haptic thing is game changing.

Pros:
-excellent sound quality
-Very comfortable. Ear cups don’t press on jaw like the HyperX, padding is thicker and more resilient feeling.
-Noise cancelling is just right. Little things don’t break immersion, but if someone falls down the stairs or the house catches fire, you’ll hear it.
-mic is very clear. The synapse tests and peer reviews tell me this is the clearest mic I’ve used, plus it has separate adjustable thresholds for volume, background noise filtering, and voice clarification
-Hyper Sense Haptics…. I think this was mostly for People playing FPS games, makes gunfire and such “feel” more real, but as someone who always has Spotify up while gaming, I Love the simulated base this adds in on low/medium settings. You could never get base like that from speakers that strap to your head, but the haptics can emulate having a nice sub nearby. Very nice touch.
-RGB is a tasteful accent, not garish. I also like that I can set which colors I want in my spectrum for various patterns. Setting this to just the shades of blue and green is very nice, and matches the Corsair RGB on my desk/tower

Cons:
-No mute light on mic. This is a nice feature most brands have and I’ve gotten used to. Nice visible indicator that I have my mic muted to prevent embarrassing over sharing.
-Thx surround feature sounds bad. I’m sure it’s great for FPS players that are willing to sacrifice sound quality to have better directional sense, but I’m not a FPS player so it’s not worth the drop in quality. This one isn’t really a detriment, it’s just a feature I don’t use. Sound quality with THX left off is phenomenal.

Overall I’m super excited about this one. This is the best headset I’ve had in my 15+ years of gaming headset usage. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Annie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 22, 2025
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Kraken V3 X
reviews.content:
Comfortable Headset with Good Sound (Best Without 7.1 Enabled)
The Razer Kraken V3 X Wired USB Gaming Headset is a solid option overall. The sound quality is clear and immersive, and it works well for gaming, videos, and voice chat. It’s also very comfortable, even during long sessions, thanks to the lightweight design and soft ear cushions.

One thing to note: the 7.1 surround sound didn’t work well with certain programs for me and would cut out randomly. I ended up turning the surround sound off, and I actually like it better that way. The audio is more consistent and still sounds great in standard mode.

Overall, it’s a good headset for the price, especially if you value comfort and clear sound. Just be aware that the surround sound feature may not be ideal depending on what programs you’re using.

reviews.author: Gypsy Gies
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Kraken V3 X
reviews.content: Very crisp clear sounds, microphone delivers your voice clearly. Superb quality.

reviews.author: J. Bell
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 31, 2024
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Kraken V3 HyperSense
reviews.content:
I got this upgrade since my V2's cable mostly died after a few years. This model for some reason has earcups nearly HALF the size of the V2s. Check out the comparison photo. I'm a petite woman and they're just too small even for me, I have no idea who they would fit comfortably. The V2 ear cups encompassed the whole ear without any pressure points, but these tiny ear cups are very uncomfortable even if you rotate them to an angle that matches your ear shape. I had a headache come on about 5 minutes into wearing them. I've never had to try to painfully "break in" a $120 headset before.

And if you think you can swap out the earcups with the bigger V2 ones - think again. They completely redesigned the earcup attachment mechanism to somehow be worse, so they're incompatible and there are no other V3 ear cup sizes on the market as of writing this review. So good luck if you have big ears which I supposedly have because somehow all these 5 stars reviews say these things are comfortable.

From my tests of the audio, the sound is great to me. I like that the boom mic cushion is removable. I miss the retractable mic of the V2 though. And I miss the mute button being on the mic with a red light indicator when muted. The volume scroll wheel on the headset is sort of annoying since it's easy to accidentally nudge it removing and putting them on and then have to readjust the volume back to where it was every time.

I'm going to keep these for now though, and if they don't "break in" soon I will consider returning them. I'm disappointed.

EDIT: After directly comparing the audio to the 7.1 Kraken V2s, the sound quality on these is a lot worse actually. The haptic feedback is nice when playing music with heavy bass, but I had to do a lot of manual editing of the EQ levels in the Synapse software to get more "punch" out of the audio in the mid-high ranges listening to music, and put the headphones in Stereo instead of the THX mode which just sounds muddy. The default mic quality was trash to I had to disable all of the "mic enhancements" in Synapse as well to get it sounding good, but the mic quality is noticeably worse than the V2s. The V2s had such amazing sound quality I absolutely loved them for music, but these do not compare. I'm just overall pretty disappointed with these. I may just keep them for the LED coolness factor but I'm on the fence about returning them as they're worse in almost every way.

EDIT 2: I returned them and knocked the rating down to 1 star. I spent a long time trying to troubleshoot the audio. When I looked the issue up online I discovered 3 months ago since writing this review, Razer released a Synapse update that worsened the sound quality across their whole headset line for tons of customers and then doubled down on the update so there's no hope of improving the inferior sound quality of this headset now. Plus I started noticing cable nose and crackling/popping in the left ear which was the last straw. Avoid at all costs.

reviews.author: Jared Lee
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Kraken V3 X
reviews.content: showed up really fast within a day. really pleased with how well it was packaged. it's functions great, sound quality is great. it only had an amazon label on the product box which isn't a problem in my neighborhood but for other neighborhoods could pose a problem could've been a bit more discreet but they notified me as soon as it arrived so not an issue all in all great product

reviews.author: Christian Cepeda
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 1, 2021
reviews.format: Color: BlackSize: StandardStyle: Kraken V3 HyperSense
reviews.content:
Thought I'd let you guys before you read my review. Make sure to leave the format at 16 bit, 48000Hz. If you set it to 16 bit, 96000Hz you will hear a ton of crackling and static noises. I tried multiple of the same headsets and they all had the same issue.

Pros

• Great sounding
• Very comfortable. I wore them for 8 hours straight and felt no discomfort the entire time
• HyperSense is awesome (high settings is too much for me, low-medium is best)
• Nice RGB lighting on the sides
• Long USB cable
• Actually decent mic

Cons

• HyperSense might cause tension headaches if set to high
• Replacing ear cups seems like a pain. Hard to take off and harder to put on new ones. The V2 headset earcups were very easy to take off and replace. They had a snap in mechanic.

Overall, very solid headsets with a decent mic. Also, HyperSense technology to make gaming and listening to music more immersive.

EDIT: I am changing my 5 star review to a 1 start review until Razer fixes this issue

I power off my computer every night for bedtime. When I power on my PC , the headphones are not powered on at all. I have to disconnect and reconnect everyday. Why don't they power on with the rest of the PC? I've tried this on 4 different machines and same issue.

This is annoying. I do a lot of work on my PC that requires at least 30 reboots throughout the day and to get up and unplug and plug back in for these headsets to work, makes me upset. Please fix this.

EDIT # 2 - Got new ones and they worked great. Seems mine was defective. Changing back to 5 star. Issue was resolved in 1 day.

EDIT #3 - After some time with these headsets, there has been this annoying issue of just random static crackling noise. It happens at the most random times and it's really annoying. As of January 20, 2022 - Razer released a huge Synapse 3 update. I was hoping they'd do some work on the headsets, but nothing. Here are the patch notes I had to ask an agent for.

reviews.author: Thong Nguyen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 12, 2025
reviews.content:
The ASUS ROG Pelta Wireless nails it with a strong mix of performance, comfort, and design. The build quality feels premium, and the headset looks great with sleek lines and tasteful RGB lighting. Wireless connectivity is rock-solid—SpeedNova 2.4 GHz delivers ultra-low latency for serious gaming, while Bluetooth and USB-C options make it super versatile across PC, PS5, Switch, and even mobile. The 10mm super-wideband mic is a standout feature—my voice comes through crisp and natural, a big upgrade from the muffled sound you get on most wireless headsets. Everything is easy to control with intuitive buttons, and the battery life is a beast—lasting up to 70 hours on a single charge.

What really impressed me was the comfort. At just 309g, it’s lightweight enough for long sessions without fatigue, and the memory foam ear cushions are soft and breathable. The fit feels secure without being tight, and I’ve had zero issues with overheating or pressure points. Sound quality is excellent across the board—powerful bass, clear mids, and sparkling highs make both games and music enjoyable. For the price, the value is absolutely there. If you’re looking for a wireless gaming headset that performs well, fits comfortably, and works flawlessly across multiple devices, the ROG Pelta is an easy recommendation.

reviews.author: Shellz
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 6, 2026
reviews.content: This headset is amazing I mean the sound is just amazing the microphone is super clear according to my discord friends they said it sounded amazing .. I love this head set and for the price point it's a steal .. and I've tried every head set I can and this one is great and you'll forget your even wearing it with how comfortable it is ... My only complaints is the the treble is a lil too high but that's of no issue with the eq setting and a huge complaint I have is that it feels so cheap it literally feels like it was made at the dollar store but despite that it seems like it's a very strong headset ... Good job to Asus making a good gaming headset

reviews.author: CWFisk
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content: Great sound, comfortable feel, but probably not suitable for small heads. Battery life is very good. The mic is functional, but as expected not as high quality as a standalone mic.

reviews.author: Kenji
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.content: I love everything Asus makes so I knew these headphones would be no different. They're so good! The sound quality is perfect for listening to music. I didn't buy them for playing games but I'll eventually use them for that too. The connectivity is perfect and simple and the volume control is effortless. Also, they are super comfy they fit just right! Not too big not too small. I also did not purchase it for the mic capability so I don't have any comment on that part.

reviews.author: Robin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 27, 2026
reviews.content: sounds great, the mic has a hard time picking up your voice all the time but it sounds good otherwise

reviews.author: Norman F.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content: Awesome best headset in my opinion easy to used easy to connect, sounds amazing, material is great and is super comfortable

reviews.author: Simon E
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 20, 2025
reviews.content:
The headphone’s overall performance is just okay compared to the Razer V3 Pro. In CS2, the sound positioning isn’t very accurate, which makes it harder to locate enemies by audio cues. Sometimes there’s also an annoying static/electric current noise.

The microphone quality is disappointing—it disconnected twice during my PC gaming sessions, which was really frustrating.

The headband design is another downside. It only offers three fixed adjustment options, and none of them fit me well. There’s no way to make fine-tuned adjustments, so comfort is a big issue.

reviews.author: Christopher
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2026
reviews.content: Amazing headset. The sound quality is great. Great volume. Very sturdy and very comfortable. The price I couldn't beat. The mic sounds great. Syncs with my aura sync. Has a ton of gaming presets, and an equalizer. Very happy with this purchase.

reviews.author: Michael Bierman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviews.content: Can't beat it for the price. 6.3 adapter is simple and nice bonus for my turn table system. Sounds great on PC and games. There is a little more bass than I want for games but I adjust that with an equalizer. They sound amazing. No volume adjuster. Just good quality wired headphones.

reviews.author: Ryan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content: Initially feeling these headphones in your hands they feel cheap. Then you realize they’re purposefully light for the comfortable fit they provide. Sound quality is wonderful, though keep in mind that there is no active noise cancellation. When the volume is turned to the max others around you can hear. Great entry level headset if you’re looking for a comfortable wireless headset.

reviews.author: JDA
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 22, 2023
reviews.content:
I do music production and also general listening. After using this BT headset I have to say I am very impressed by the battery life as it can hold an entire session of production (few hours, 6 or more and for numerous days) without getting a low battery notification - After couple weeks I got my first low battery so you probably dont need to have this parked on the usb charger for many times in a month. That is the mark of a true BT studio headphone for me and reason why I'd recommend it for just that purpose.

The bass quality is actually very noticeable but not overbearing where mid and highs would be flattened out. However it is very punchy bass without "breaking in" out of the box. Any low-lows will be noticeable if you need to work on a bassline sequence in Ableton or an alternate program.

The charging time itself from low to full, is at least a couple of hours which is acceptable. Quality build is very high. Fit-wise it is very snug to the ears and buttons are on the left side, which some people may find to have a learning curve (not a high one). Easy to press rectangle style buttons. I haven't used it to gym or cardio with but they aren't heavy enough to where they would fall, I think the JBL TUNE 500s are made for that, because they are feather weight "light" and do a great job cancelling treadmill noise. Im sure these would do the job fine at the gym, they aren't bulky.

They do come with a cord in case you want to use without the internal battery, so that's a neat feature. Overall latest BT specs and long battery Im sure these would do the job for a long time, they are my go to, audio feels twice that for the price so it almost feels like a bargain but they probably sound like $150 phones. Ive had the Monster Inspirations which have a similar price tag but they are 4x as heavy and audio signature is similar but wireless and way more utility. I highly recommend them.

reviews.author: Zaki
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 17, 2025
reviews.content: After wearing these headphones for a couple of weeks every day, here’s what I found. I would say that it is truthful. They do give flat audio so that is very good for music creation, and mixing. But the durability of these headphones suffers pretty bad. Only after three days of use. The headphones have started cutting out audio when plugged into my computers audio jack and I would either have to reposition the cord or plug it in and plug it out. These headphones are not Bluetooth they’re wired so there’s no other option but wired is a good thing so you don’t have to deal with latency. They’re pretty comfortable to wear nothing crazy but they wouldn’t get uncomfortable if you use them for more than five hours at a time I would say, and then you would need to take a break.. I have an average ear size so they fit snuggly around my ears and they can be resized for bigger and smaller heads. Overall, these headphones are a four out of five with the main cons being the connectivity in the cord is very weak. The material is very fragile and the bass and volume isn’t as loud as I would want to get.. the pros being it does give flat audio, it is a wired connection with no latency. The sound quality is pretty good and is very simple and easy to use.. for a budget option I would recommend

reviews.author: Bill_6400
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 31, 2026
reviews.content: Good sound for the price. I bought these to replace an old in the ear set to listen to books. The sound surprised me for both books and music. Being over the ear they dint smash my ears like some I tried and therefor I'm able to wear much longer. Listening to books or music while I'm walking around doing things I dint pay attention to how long I use them. So all I can say for battery life is I haven't drained them yet. Of course when I'm not using them I out them on a charger.

reviews.author: Darrell Arrington Jr
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 9, 2026
reviews.content: Was shopping for Bluetooth over the ear headphones between Audio-Technica and several other brands. For the price, these are exceptional. I've worn these daily for the last 3 years.
