Exceptional sound, materials on ear surrounds and headband tend to flake with extreme use. Overall worth the money!

reviews.author: Matt Oliver
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 24, 2024
reviews.content:
For the cost, you'd be hard pressed to find better sounding headphones. These are close to being studio monitors, with the convenience of wireless features.

These headphones do a good job of separating instruments in tracks and provide a better soundstage than I expected for this price. They are a touch, and I mean barely, warm. Meaning, they are mostly neutral as you would expect from a studio headphone, with the slightest bump in the lower end. Sub bass isn't really present, but I appreciate the modest punch in the low end.

Keep in mind, these are not noise cancelling headphones. I find the ear pads fit my average sized head, and smaller ears really well. The seal they create helps block out some ambient sounds on their own. When I'm doing chores around the house and pumping some tunes, I really don't hear anything but the music. I even took these out in public once to get my tires changed. I could not hear the sounds of the storefront or the shop, even as I sat writing at the bar by the shop window. I was able to lose myself in the music and my writing without any problems. I don't find there is much sound leakage as these are closed back, but I also don't listen to my music at excessive levels (~75% at most).

The one problem I have with them so far is the fact that the earcups do not swivel much, and thus cannot fold completely flat for travel. I would have also liked to see a cheap case or bag come with this product to help in transporting them. I have purchased the WS990 BT in the past which came with a leather bag that I still use when traveling. I will likely continue to use that pair for work travel, rather than the M20X.

One concern I have for the long run is their build quality. They are mostly plastic with an aluminum band for extending the earcups. Being constructed as such, they are very lightweight. On one hand, this adds to the comfort of the headphones as I forget I'm wearing them sometimes due to their featherweight and light clamping force. However, I worry that these could be broken if I decide to toss them into a backpack and take them out more often. With the light clamping force I find that I can shake them loose if I shake my head side to side rapidly. While I don't do that often, I think that anyone looking for a headphone to wear while exercising should look elsewhere, or consider finding a different ear pad that provides more grip than the provided pleather set.

For me, these will likely be relegated to around the house wear, when I don't want to be strapped to my computer desk by my R70Xs. I'm perfectly okay with that as I do really enjoy the sound these produce for the money and the comfort they provide. I think they punch above their price in the audio department and are an excellent option for someone looking for a good, reliable headphone for casual wear.

reviews.author: USArmyMPVet
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 9, 2025
reviews.content:
First let me say I am a Vine reviewer but I bought these as a lightweight, long lasting, battery powered headset.
There are 2 sets of reviews on this page. 1 for Bluetooth and 1 for wired. I will say right now don't buy these for wireless, they are literally horrible sounding.
When these are used on Bluetooth the sound goes down the drain. As wired they are fantastic sounding, but the second you hook them up to Bluetooth the quality drops from from studio quality, to tape recorder quality. I have no idea why. I tried them on 3 different PC's and all did the same thing. I tried them on a Apple iPhone and on my Samsung Galaxy and it was the same issue.
I gave these 3 stars, but only as a wireless headset. When you wire these to a 3.5mm jack they rival my Sennheiser HD's, but I get tired of a wire at times, so I wanted to give these a try. As a wired headset everything is great, but when on Bluetooth, the sound has cracks you can hear, the volume is reduced to about 60%, just a bad experience and sound. Bass is barely there, and treble is more monotone then vibrant. Even using a EQ would not fix this. If your wanting a wired headset, hey these sound great and lightweight, A bit more clamping pressure than I would like, but that can be stretched into any headset. The build quality? They were obviously going for lightweight in that area, but if you don't toss these about I would say it wouldn't be an issue. Pretty comfortable and light for an adult head. Design is good, but that is where it stops. Save some money and buy the wired versions not the BT ones. These will have to be returned and I hate returning anything.

reviews.author: Anonymous buyer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 28, 2024
reviews.content:
I been hearing lots of praise for the mdr-z1r and now that I have my own I believe it definitely deserves it.

The mdr-z1r are extremely well constructed and designed with absolutely no creaking and at least for my head the headband is just the right tension that works together with the deep molded earpads that soft in a way that It seals around your ear but it's firm enough to maintain its shape keeping my ears from making contact with the drivers, making these the most comfortable headphones I worn yet.

For this section I may not know the correct technical audiophile terms to describe the mdr-z1r's performance and you can get a better description from other reviewers here, but I can probably describe the z1r better by comparison with other headphones I have, for example:

Mdr-1am2, good build quality and light as a feather but earcups are shallow and sometimes it creaks a bit, sound profile is v shaped with more emphasis on the highs compared to the z1r but somewhat less subbass and very narrow soundstage which is expected for a typical close back of its price range.

Mdr-z7m2, this one is very comparable in build quality and comfort and having that wide soundstage but the sound profile while still v shaped and detailed it's kinda dull across the board especially when compared to the 1am2. It's like if the z1r was to split into two separate headphones with the z7m2 having the build quality and the soundstage while the 1am2 keeps the exciting sound profile.

That concludes my review of the mdr-z1r and I hope this gives you a better idea if you happen to own either of those headphones in the comparison.

reviews.author: Panagiotaropoulos
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026
reviews.content: I use a cabled connection from my iPhone 17 to a Chord Hugo2 D/A and headphone amplifier. My previous top of the line headphones were the Focal Stellia. The Sonys are excellent, more layering with significantly more bass. I guess there is a reason they have remained unchanged for many years now. It’s not like Sony lacks the means to come with a “new” model every couple years. The fit is a little tight and I have to see if it will improve with time.

reviews.author: ARVIN CASTRO
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 21, 2021
reviews.content:
Headphones...like all audio devices/equipment...have their own unique sound signature. Some will find the Sony MDR-Z1R to their linking, few won’t. What’s undeniable is that from the presentation, unboxing and feeling in your hands and on your head, it’s easy to see that this is truly a flagship product. You can tell Sony held nothing back in terms of giving its designers and engineers free reign to create their dream headphone...and in my eyes and hands, they’ve truly delivered.

But what about sound? Here is where you will find the never ending debate come to a head (pardon the pun): Closed-back vs. Open-Back designs. If you prefer an open-back sound, there probably isn’t anything the Sony can do to sway you from the absolute best open-back cans. However, the Z1R’s have the widest, deepest soundstage I’ve ever heard from a closed design as well as presenting well recorded music with all the air and layering you would only expect from a really good open-back. And, due to the closed-back, you get an amazing amount of bass that’s always in control, with a full sense of depth and tightness, never boomy or muddy. They also have a great amount of isolation from the outside world. FWIW, I mostly use my Z1R’s with my Sony NW-ZX2 Hi-Res player and on my laptops & iPad Air with the incredible THX Onyx DAC/headphone amplifier. Material is Tidal (CD and MQA quality) streaming and stored high-res FLAC and DSD files.

All in, these are undoubtedly the best closed-back headphones in the world, with reference-levels of sound quality and amazing build quality to boot. If you value the sonic signature and other qualities that a closed-back headphone brings...bass presence and authority, tempo, control and isolation are areas the Sony excels in...then you probably can’t go wrong with the Z1R’s. They are hugely expensive, yes, but the best is rarely ever cheap.

reviews.author: Steve
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 12, 2025
reviews.content: They are very good headphones. I replaced the headphone cables with better aftermarket cables and noticed a substantial improvement in audio quality. I recommend doing so.

reviews.author: Matthew T. Weflen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2021
reviews.content:
This is a hard review to write. In part it's difficult because trying to describe the subjective experience of appreciating sound and music is hard, but also in part it is a difficult exercise in taming things like "confirmation bias" and trying to justify a huge purchase.

I didn't think I would become a guy who would drop 26 Benjamins on a pair of headphones, a cable, and a digital audio player. Yet, here I am. How did this happen?

MY HEADPHONE HISTORY

I decided to do a deep dive into classical music about 3 years ago. I was down about the state of the country and the world, and I wanted an escape valve. I bought a big box set of Herbert von Karajan albums, and then I needed something to play them on. I started with Sony's MDR-7506 "Studio" headphones because they were praised for their "flatness" and were relatively inexpensive. I needed something that could hold the entire box set ripped as FLAC files, so I got a Sony NW-A45 Walkman. And this was fine... for a while.

I felt like I could get more. More bass, smoother sound, more comfort. So I bought a pair of Sony MDR-1000X noise canceling headphones. This was a nice purchase because they paired with the Walkman easily and transmitted audio over LDAC, a higher resolution codec than standard Bluetooth and AptX. And things were fine... for a while.

The MDR-1000X are plastic headphones with pleather ear pads. They are comfortable for about an hour at most. Great for a bus ride or for some listening at work, but not great for a 3 hour listen in the late evening.

So I started to look into wired cans with more premium materials. I settled on Sony's MDR-Z7 headphones. These were Sony's flagship for 2014, praised for their huge 70mm drivers and seismic bass, but criticized for being a little over-emphasized in the bass region and a little recessed for vocals. But with wired headphones, you start to hear about better standards. "Balanced" cable provides more power and less "noise" than standard 3.5mm mini cable. OK, fine. I plumped for a balanced cable and Sony's NW-ZX300 Walkman, which has a balanced output. And it was fine... for a while.

I will say this - there were improvements with each step. Bass was deeper and more controlled, less "boomy" while still making it feel like you were surrounded by cellos or double basses (or, for rock, by bass guitars and kickdrums). I was hearing more from my music. Was it a linear progression in audio improvement that matched the increase in price? No. "Diminishing Returns" is something you'll hear a lot about in this hobby - and it's the real deal.

So why did I eventually go for the WM1A Walkman and the MDR-Z1R headphones? Well, in addition to chasing the dragon so to speak, I wanted durability and comfort. The MDR-Z7 are very comfortable, but the earpads are pleather, and thus prone to wearing out, and are difficult to source replacements for. The WM1A has a much larger screen, which is key for album art, something I enjoy.

So I saved up my Amazon points for the Z1R. I found a WM1A on eBay for about 75% of MSRP. I purchased a used Sony Kimber balanced cable from a Head-Fi user. And I took the plunge on the headphones at the beginning of this month. I purchased a pair of Dekoni leather replacement pads as a backup for the stock pads.

I am done. Done! I will not be buying any more gear. What have I learned? What wisdom can I pass on to you?

EVALUATING THE Z1R

The MDR-Z1R sells for almost thrice what the next Sony step-down model, the MDR-Z7M2 does. Does it provide thrice the performance? No way. No how. It is better, don't get me wrong. You'll hear more detail in the bass region especially - but it's not night and day. I would wager that you get 90% of the performance for 40% of the price.

So why buy these things?

Well, the build quality is supreme. There are no creaks or squeaks. The frame is entirely metal, covered by real leather. The pads twist off easily, as opposed to the screw that holds in the Z7 pads. The comfort on the head is about even with the Z7. The headphones come in a beautiful wood and leather storage case, though I think for the price Sony should have included a travel case, too (there are plenty of options from companies like Geekria, though). The case makes a great storage area for my Walkman and cables, as well.

I can tell you from A/B testing that there is not much difference between the stock cables (both 3.5mm and 4.4mm balanced are included) and the Kimber upgrade cable. There may be a small difference, but I would have to do double blind testing to try and determine it (something I can't do with 1 Walkman and 1 pair of headphones).

On first listen, the headphones seemed a bit too forward in the bass region and some vocals seemed a bit recessed. I lightly adjusted the EQ on the WM1A (pictured) to compensate, very successfully in my opinion.

After settling in and tuning/listening for a good 40 hours, I find the sound quality to be excellent, and yes, a solid upgrade over the Z7. I listen primarily to classical, and in this genre the Z1R provides a deep, enveloping experience, very much like being in a concert hall, with perhaps slightly elevated bass (though it can be tuned down). Particularly notable is the way in which deep, resonant bass tones (such as those from contrabasses and cellos) retain all of their detail and do not crowd out other parts of the mix in the middle tones. Digitally recorded concert music especially gives this sense of "being there" detail and richness. The digital Karajan/BPO Brahms Symphony 1 is apocalyptically huge. For rock and pop, drums have a real slam to them, while vocals are crystal clear and can compete (Especially when tuned up slightly). Vocals in Haim's "Women In Music Pt. III" are close and intimate. The Strokes' "The New Abnormal" sounds amazing, from bass guitar to vocals to percussion. Well-recorded jazz makes you feel like you're in the studio, easily sensing where the instruments are in the room. John Coltrane's "A Love Supreme" sounds like it is surrounding your head. Well-mastered electronic dance music, such as Lady Gaga's "Chromatica," sounds pretty incredible, too, with very impactful beats and no sonic muddiness. Basically, the Z1R handles all of these genres with aplomb, as long as you tune the sound to your preferences (as pictured, I lowered the bass levels slightly and boosted the range where vocals reside slightly).

There is some controversy on the question of whether these headphones require a separate amplifier to sound their best. Reviewers seem split, as is the enthusiast community. Personally, I do not own or use an amplifier, and I can't say I feel like anything is "missing." I use the WM1A Walkman with balanced cables, and generally keep the volume at a setting of about 48/120 for orchestral music, 38-40/120 for pop and rock. Much louder gets uncomfortable. I also ran these over standard 3.5mm cable from my Surface Book 2 laptop. Again, I couldn't push them past about the 40% mark on Windows 10's volume control without getting really uncomfortable (25% was better). I have seen various jargon-laden explanations of how sound would theoretically improve with an amp, but I remain unconvinced. It sounds incredible directly from the Walkman over balanced cable, and very good from the laptop over 3.5mm.

CONCLUSION

The overall selling point for this headphone is "richness." This is not a thin, bright sound that tires your ears out after an hour. It's smooth, rich, deep. In headphone circles, this is termed a "dark" headphone while certain brands, like Sennheiser or Beyer Dynamic headphones, are "bright." While brightness is prized by some audiophiles for its detail in the treble region of the signal, it can really fatigue the listener after a while. The Z1R can be listened to very comfortably for hours on end. It sounds more balanced to my ears, at least. I certainly feel as though I can hear all the high instruments, even when the bass is pronounced.

The other thing you're paying for is build quality. It feels like a finely tuned luxury car with leather seats and perfect handling. Like a BMW or a Porsche. The leather pads are extremely comfortable, and their shape conforms to the head very nicely. I would say these are the best pads I've experienced. It's very, very comfortable. Overall, the Z1R seems like it should last nearly forever if cared for well.

They are not a good value, in the same way a BMW or Porsche are not a good value. The improvement over a pair that costs half as much simply isn't parallel and linear. There are definite diminishing returns. For most people, a good pair of noise-cancelers by Sony or Bose will more than meet their needs, and they will costs about a quarter as much. For almost everyone else who is looking at wired cans, the Sony MDR-Z7M2 will do nicely for a closed headphone, or say a Hifiman Sundara if you're looking for an open-back headphone (closed means that the earcups seal sound in, which gives you more privacy; open means that the earcups let sound out through an open grille, which might disturb others in the room/office but also might make the headphones sound more spacious and airy).

The Z1R is a terrible value but a great headphone. It's for dragon-chasers. I am certainly enjoying my music quite a bit with them. I don't feel like I need to go any further. I admire them as a physical object every time I take them out and fire them up. I do wonder what I might have spent that extra G on instead, though. That value proposition is why I give it 4 stars instead of 5 (which it more than earns on performance and build quality).

I hope my "journey" gives you, the reader, some ideas to chew on as you mull over your purchase plans.

UPDATE: I have now logged a good 300 hours on these cans, driven by my WM1A Walkman. I cannot say to what degree the sound has changed because I was listening the whole time - but I can say that my appreciation for the sound has deepened. Recordings that I thought were a little subpar on my previous headphones reveal themselves to be deep and precise with the Z1R. Most notably, I have switched to the stock balanced cable, which turns out to be fabulous (the Sony Kimber cable's gold-plated connector wore out, and I recommend against purchasing it). There is no need to buy aftermarket cables here! After 300 hours there are no parts that seem in danger of wearing out. The stock pads may be just ever so slightly compressed, but this does not present any issues in the actual wear and listening of the cans. After 7 months my initial verdict stands - 5-star headphones at a 4-star price.

reviews.author: Joshua Thomas
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 8, 2020
reviews.content: I received the product from Amazon well packaged with no serious damage to the outside of the delivery box. The Sony box on the inside of said package was even cleaner indicating it was packed and shipped well. I got the headphones out of the well-built leather wrapped wood(?) box and the left ear cup falls off. Upon further inspection and removing 2 tiny phillips head screws with a jeweler’s screwdriver, I was able to see the source of the problem. The headband was missing a tiny clip that keeps the metal post from pulling through. I inspected it on the other side and this clip was properly installed. No clip was found in the box, no clip inside the headband cavity, no signs of damage. I reached out to Sony customer support immediately providing proof of purchase from Amazon and pictures of the "damage". In my direct ticket to Sony and my correspondence on the phone, I stated multiple times that these headphones were not broken and I just required a small tiny clip. From the beginning I told them I wanted to keep the headphones, just wanted to repair them properly. I was told by customer support my proof of purchase + storefront was approved for warranty but I needed to wait till their "technical team" took a look at it and determine a solution. This ticket was submitted on a Friday so I had to wait till Monday to receive a final response. I crafted a tiny clip myself out of a piece of metal and installed it on the headphone which worked perfectly (clip on my finger in one of the attached photos). I mean, "its just a tiny piece of metal, not a huge deal right? I'll get the perfect fit clip and install it on the headphone and they will be working just fine!" or so I told myself... Monday I received a phone call from Sony customer support and was told that the headphone will be considered as damaged and that they would not cover it because it was NOT a manufacturers defect (more on this later). I was told that I would have to take it up with amazon. Further questions of just receiving a little clip got me nowhere with the cold human on the other side. There was no sympathy from customer support in this call, just a big FU and click of the receiver. Now for those who don't know, these headphones are hand assembled by Sony Artificers in Japan. Part of the magic is that these are hand assembled. Its obvious that whomever assembled this forgot the clip. My disdain isn't with him/her since it was just a simple mistake with NO DAMAGE. I make approximately 450 mistakes a second so its understandable that someone may have forgot this, or it fell out. Most of that clip is held in by gravity with the pressure between the headphone earcup and headband. If the headphones are flat on a surface with that clip exposed, it could very well fall out. But the engineering design wants this to be loose so that the earcups swivel easily yet feel solid. This clip cannot fall out once installed into the headband since it leans tightly on the sides of the aluminum frame. The other fact that infuriated me was that they were putting this as damage on Amazon... I may have been born yesterday but this clip is hidden behind a solid aluminum shroud called the headband, then inside a Fort Knox foam packed leather box which is tightly crafted to hold the headphones in place, which then that box goes inside another box, which then that box goes inside an Amazon shipping box with some packing material. These headphones are so well protected that I would have to take a sledgehammer to the box just to attempt to damage the cargo on the inside. All of the materials packed in the box had all their original wrapping material and did not appear to be tampered with so its not like someone opened this box to remove this clip for their own gain. They make so little of these headphones I find it highly unlikely that would be the case since its not a known issue that I can find anywhere. In all, I returned this product to Amazon and indicated it was "damaged". Luckily Amazon LLC has a great return policy because I bought through Amazon LLC to get official support by Sony for their "warranty". If you buy from other places that aren't an official storefront you don't get the "warranty" so I guess they just don't pick up the phone? I would have maybe preferred that at this point. I know that some of you will buy this product anyways but the only way that these deaf greedy corporations learn anything is to speak with your wallet. Buy from someone who has compassion in their product from top down: from the engineers to the salespersons.

reviews.author: John Lavette
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2021
reviews.content: The Z1Rs are my first high end pair of headphones. I bought them after I spent a few months with my first DAC, and realized how good music, movies, and games could actually sound. The Z1Rs take every bit of sound quality my DAC provides and clearly has even greater potential. So I ordered a ScHiit Magnius and Modius combo in order to experience balanced audio. These headphones are beautiful to look at, and in the elegant complexity of their construction. They are the most comfortable headphones I have ever worn, despite a greater than normal size. That extra size provides a really amazing sense of space and depth to the sound. They are very easy to drive, requiring very little power from an amp in order to sound their best. I am not an Audiophile. The best recommendation I can offer is to tell you that I spent $1800.00 + on this pair of headphones, and I consider the purchase one of the very best that I have ever made!

reviews.author: aTucsonDrummer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 20, 2020
reviews.content: These Sony MDR-Z1R headphones are simply incredible! I am using them with the Sony NW-WM1A Walkman. These are more than what one would call “detailed”. These make music come alive to the point that the materials the instruments are made of can be heard and felt....Wooden instruments sound like wood, metal strings sound like metal, the friction of the hairs of a violin bow against the metal strings can be felt, synthetic drum heads can be distinguished from animal skin ones, vocals sound human. These go beyond accuracy, instrument separation, imaging, sound stage, etc. and actually “create” the music. As a musician I can say I have not yet heard any headphones as good as these or like these at any price. These are a conduit to the musicians on the other end. They also achieve this even while listening at very low volumes which is pretty amazing and are also outstandingly comfortable and light weight too. These headphones should be considered musical instruments in themselves. Bravo, Sony!

reviews.author: Mmmichelle
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.content: Awesome earbuds! The sound quality is amazing! They're very comfortable! Great value! Quality product! They're a "must buy," even if only to have an extra pair. Bought a pair for my spouse as well who has issues with earbuds hurting her ears and falling out. She loves them and was surprised by the sound quality, fit, and refuses to believe how inexpensive they were. Buy them! You're welcome.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content: Awesome buds nice sound and durable

reviews.author: Robert Tena
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content:
I'm old school , I still enjoy the wire uses in earphones. Sure I got a pair of Bluetooth earphones. Skullcandy as well they also come with a wire. I just don't like the earbuds . Said yes satisfaction completely satisfied with Skullcandy.
What sound is Crisp clear. Very dependable , prices are reasonable. You will not break the bank with Skullcandy earphones. Easy use and comfortable convenient use.

reviews.author: Mds4710
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2026
reviews.content: Function as they should. Great pair for the gym. Sound is clear. Don't use for the microphone so can't comment on that. Have only had them for a week and a half so can't say about longevity. Overall im satisfied with my purchase.

reviews.author: A V
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026
reviews.content: These are my go-to earbuds for my iPod classic. Great sound. Package comes with various-sized silicone bits for a good ear fit. The noise isolation is good; you can still hear the world around you, but you can also hear the music well. If you want a great pair of everyday earbuds, look no further. They are also not that scary to lose or break, since they don't cost all that much

reviews.author: Avallac
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content: Have been working well for me, I wear these to bed actually. Seem reasonably durable so far, although I haven't used every single day, only a 2-3 times per week. Comfortable enough to have in your ear while sleeping, even on the side, at least for me. They also come with a one year warranty.

reviews.author: birdy84
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 16, 2026
reviews.content: I've used these Skullcandy earbuds for years. Anytime I have a pair die after a few years of harsh use, I order a few more. My absolute go-to earbuds. I have wireless Beats and old school Sony earbuds - and Skullcandy is just as good as the others... if not better! And the price is always right! Amazing sound, fits my little ears, and lasts a long time even if you aren't very kind and gentle with your earbuds! And I love the built-in mic and how you can hang up/answer calls by pressing the mic button. It also controls your multimedia when playing on your phone! VERY impressive for the price!

reviews.author: C man
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 31, 2026
reviews.content: Keep in mind the price. Could be a four star rating except that these wired headphones don't have a volume up and volume down button. Also the click to play and pause does not work on the device I'm using.

reviews.author: Dylan beaudry
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 13, 2025
reviews.content:
Have the same pair for 9 years. I was 16, and getting into music and gaming around the time. Anyone my age would've said to get a pair of Razer whatever's with the lights and the green, but my grandpa had been an audio nerd since the dinosaurs roamed and Buzz Armstrong was still in Korea. And he was in charge of my birthday gift.

And when i said i wanted headphones this is what i ended up with. However the were $68 then.

These headphones are durable, light, and very comfortable. The original velvet pads are holding up well and the cables still mostly have their gold plating.

Thanks to grandpa, i got into audio much more than i would've otherwise and i can say that wven among some of my more expensive headphones these hang right along with them. The sound is crisp and detailed with just enough bass to let you know what's supposed to have it. Id say they were well worth the price they were then, but a bit of a stretch with the current price being about double. But i wouldn't be disappointed even with the price. Because there are headphones twice this price that still sound bad and wont last half as long. They look really sleek, and conform to your head well. Inwtill have all my cords, the coil is nice to have it i want to listen from the couch into my amplifier and need a longer cable.

reviews.author: Heavyarms55
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 25, 2021
reviews.content:
Let me start by saying I'm not an audiophile or an audio expert of any sort. This is the first time I've really splurged on audio equipment at all. In fact, it's 3 times the price of my previous headphone purchase, and even 50 bucks more than I spent on my speakers for my gaming PC.

So was it worth it? In short, YES. Yes it very much was!

The audio quality is in extreme excess, FAR better than anything else I own to the point that comparing them directly just feels unfair. I have MP3s that I've had on my phone and hard drive for almost 20 years, and I listen to them with these headphones and realize I've been missing details of my absolute favorite music for all this time.

The noise cancelling is almost too good too. When I'm listening to music with these, the rest of the world is shut off to me. Much to my roommate's annoyance often times. However the noise cancelling does seem to be tied to being listening to something actively. When the audio is paused, the noise cancelling is a lot less effective. Maybe that's normal, maybe not, like I said, I am a total amateur. But I love the way it works. If I want to tune everything else out, these absolutely work fantastically!

Finally, I did choose these because I wanted closed backs that don't bleed much audio, so I can listen to whatever I wish without concern about bothering those around me - and because I like my privacy. These work well for that too.

And lastly, I think they are immensely comfortable. Certainly better than any cheaper pairs I've owned. The padded headband is nice, and the headphones themselves are adjustable enough that they fit over my ears and I can wear the headset for hours at a time without complaint. Even with my glasses on, and the headphones pressing my frames on my head.

These do feel like a luxury item. I wouldn't say anyone really needs these. But I would say that they feel like a luxury item, considerably nicer than more average purchases in the same area.

Honestly, I'm ruined for cheaper headphones now. Going back to my old no-name pair any time that I have - just feels so glaringly inferior now - despite having used them for years without issue. The audio, comfort and noise cancelling are just THAT much nicer.

reviews.author: C Welsh
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content: Astounding sound and comfortable. May need and adaptor plug. It does come with the old style and a smaller plug.

reviews.author: Bubba
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026
reviews.content:
sound great, good isolation from outside noise, the cable is removable though I think the connection to the headset is proprietary, the small connector is 4 conductor rather than the plain headphoe 3 conductor jack ( has a mic on cable)
Very tight fit - I can't wear them for very long

reviews.author: WDeVercelly
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 31, 2020
reviews.content:
I've seen a few complaints about these cans here concerning headband and ear cushion wear issues. I cannot comment on that yet, but I have to say that these are the best headphones I've ever worn and I've been around for 65 years.

Comfort A+

These are closed-back and other than the closed in sensation, which is to be expected, they are a dream so far. That's saying something because I have a huge melon and I haven't even stretched them out yet! I own the HD 58x Jubilees and they took some time to break in and it took some "gorillaing" to stretch them out to help that. These felt great out of the box.

Weight - what weight?

Sound:

Keep in mind that I'm coming at this from an audiophile perspective. I won't grade them on a scale because everyone is looking for something different. These are no dubstep or hip-hop phones unless you are mixing with them. These are more like real professional monitors than entertainment cans. They could use maybe 3dB more bass, but I'd rather have a little less than too much. Compared to the Jubilees, they are a little thin, but I feel that the 58x's can be a bit too much and the bass seems to overload (distort) too soon. The mids are perfect and vocals are clean and accurate. I can't speak for the highs because I'm old and lost the highs long ago. I do wear hearing aids and they seem fine, but I don't even remember what natural high frequencies sound like. These artificial (Oticon) highs do sound great thru these though. I'll leave it at that. Put it this way: I currently have a pair of Buchardt s300s (traded in KEF LS50s.) and these are a lot closer to what those sound like than anything else I've heard. All cans seem bass-heavy to me lately (other than Apple Airpods and some on-ear sets - honorable mention to the Cooler Master 751, which I game with). These are a relief from the usual mud.

Gaming:

No mic, but if you just want to hear them coming, these are great. Sharp with detail and no artificial boom or slop. If you are familiar with the Cooler Master 751, these are balanced like those, but have much better detail and similar comfort. If you crave bass, I'd recommend the Jubilees. They have that and more. They will need breaking in on the comfort end though.

Efficiency:

Easy to drive! On an iPhone, I'm about about 35% through the Apple DAC dongle. You don't have to waste money on an amp. I would recommend a good DAC on a PC though. A Dragonfly Black would suffice. It sure beats an onboard chip.

In short, I wouldn't recommend these for someone buying their first set. They are for the "mature" listener. If you want something that can use a small bass boost to get you there, these are great. If you hate boosted bass, these are perfect. You won't have to change a thing.

reviews.author: Eric
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 26, 2020
reviews.content:
On a technical level - it's terrific. Perhaps the best sounding headphones I've ever had. I walk by the noisy subway here in New York, and I can barely hear it. The music comes out loudly, clearly, and beautifully. I have a small head and wear glasses, and they're superbly comfortable.

However, it works when it wants to. When connected to my refurbished iPhone 6S, and usually when I'm walking around, It fast forwards the songs, or it skips the songs, or it just randomly pauses. When I press the button on the cable to pause the music, it's a crap shoot as to whether it will work or not. It randomly goes to voice control - even though I have it completely turned off. Again, the iPhone issues only happen when I'm walking around.

I used a $10 headphones for a little while on my iPhone 6S and it worked without fault.

When I connected it to 2 different Xbox One controllers, it rarely works. However, it works just fine when playing or watching movies on my Windows desktop, PS4 controller, or a laptop. I just plug and play on the latter devices.

I used the same $10 headphones for the Xbox One controller - and again it worked without fault.

A couple of weeks ago, I returned a pair of HD 569s that were giving me the same problems. I requested a replacement thinking I had just received a faulty unit. Still, the same problems arise.

It's not a pair of headphones that work consistently. Have no idea what's wrong, or how to fix it. Have tried several solutions - from going back to factory settings on the iPhone, to checking if the liquid contact indicators had gone red, to making sure all devices were up to date - nothing has helped with these pair of headphones.

Mixed feelings overall on this headphones. Still undecided as to if I want to keep it or not. Comes with a 2 year warranty from purchase date, at least. If I'm missing something - I'm all ears. Thanks for reading.

reviews.author: Astromike
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2024
reviews.content:
Updating after almost 2 years of daily use. Purchased here on Amazon. These are the best headphones I have ever owned. They truly have withstood the test of time. I have abused these headphones so much. Tossed into a backpack, plugged into phones, laptops, external DACs, you name it. They work as great now as the day I opened the box. Both the cables included have held up well and this is the amazing. I have accidentally ripped them out of my phone and laptop so many times. The smaller cable is starting to separate at the plig but still works fine. No it isn't a quality issue, that is from me accidentally dropping the headphones and them getting ripped out of the devices. Still works. These headphones are tough and sound great. I listen to metal and rap. Everything sounds great. For best results you will need an amp/dac. These aren't Walmart headphones. They thrive on power. I pair mine with a Soundblaster G6 external soun card. I can use the software to increase the base to ear shattering levels. These headphones punch way above their price point.

I see amazon has a warning that these get returned frequently. I honestly have no idea why. I would buy these again without question but I don't need to as mine are still working loke new after 2 years. 2 cables means even if 1 ends up breaking, you have a longer bigger backup. You just need a 3.5m adapter for it. Both cables have 0 issues with background noise when moved. They are good cables.

If you are on a budget and you want audiophile grade headphones that don't break the bank, These are the best closed back headphones you will find. Big fan if Senheiser. I have owned many over the years. These ones are an excellent option in a long line of quality headphones.

reviews.author: Valerie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 24, 2024
reviews.content: I felt like Goldilocks trying to find mispriced headphones. Too light, too heavy, too muddy, too tinny, too bassy and so on. I wanted them for cancelling noise on a long flight. BUT, these sound so good are so comfortable that I know I will wear them often during my work from home days and photo editing nights- so much better sound than speakers. The sound is clear and crisp, but not soulless. The bass could be a little fuller but it is acceptable. I am unwilling to pay $300 for headphones. I “feel” the beauty of the music with these and they have handled any kind of music I throw at them -everything from light folk and jazz to r&b and rock. All the sounds and voices are separate but still blended into one. I can comfortably wear my glasses with them, too. These are wired, which may not meet everyone’s need but wireless make my head feel like I have constant little electrical shocks going through my head - I can’t wear them. They are light enough (but not overly so) and sturdy.

reviews.author: Nils
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 24, 2025
reviews.content:
When it comes to audio gear, few brands evoke the kind of admiration that Bang & Olufsen does. The H100 earbuds are no exception. Just a day after ordering them from Amazon, I found myself delighting in the rich sound quality that these little wonders provide.

From the moment I unboxed them, I was struck by the sleek design, a hallmark of Bang & Olufsen's commitment to aesthetics as much as performance. But it’s the sound that truly steals the show. The clarity is remarkable, delivering crisp highs and deep bass that make every note come alive. Whether I'm indulging in classical symphonies or the latest pop hits, the audio experience is nothing short of immersive.

The H100 is not just about sound, though. They fit comfortably in the ear, allowing me to wear them for hours without any discomfort. The intuitive controls make it easy to manage playback without fumbling around, which is a huge plus for someone who appreciates convenience.

In a world where technology can sometimes feel overwhelming, the H100 offers a delightful simplicity combined with exceptional quality. It’s easy to forget about the outside noise when you're lost in your favorite tunes, and that’s a satisfaction that’s hard to put into words. Overall, my experience with the Bang & Olufsen H100 has been nothing short of delightful, and I can confidently say they’ve made my listening time all the more enjoyable.

reviews.author: Tri-Dent Raider
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 8, 2024
reviews.content:
I originally bought these because I thought the H-95 didn’t have enough power and volume. I must say that not only do the H-100 have more power, but the sound quality for a wireless pair of headphones is unbelievable. I don’t have the words to describe properly or to give these headphones justice. But the clarity, separation of instruments, the bass must be heard to understand and appreciate. I know they are heinously expensive but if you have the money and are an audiophile you need to check them out. I told myself well if they don’t perform up to the price tag I’ll send them back. I put on LED Zeppelin’s Stairway to Heaven and within 30 seconds I had decided to keep them.

The materials are of course top notch. They are very comfortable and the ANC is very much improved over the H-95’s. They are still not as good as Sony, Bose, or Apple’s headphones for ANC, but they are very close. The microphones are good and phone calls are great and sound natural. They have 30-40 hours of playtime and the coolest thing is the auto shutoff and turn on is simply taking them off or putting them on. It’s a really cool feature and better than any other headphones I have owned. But above everything else is the sound quality. Nothing and I mean nothing even comes close to them in this department.

reviews.author: M&M
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 29, 2025
reviews.content:
This is the most amazing headset you’ll ever get, IF, 1. you have the budget 2. very picky for sound quality 3. Like the light weight headset. Then these are great choice, you simply can’t get any better. The quality is off the chart. It offers all ranges plus more. The immersive experience is super impressive, I’ll never listen Tennessee Whiskey the same way after using this, and I love the metal dial on each side. It’s super duper easy to use it. The design overall does give luxury vibe to it. Kind of like a pair loro piana shoes - looks plain but expensive. And the ANC is top notch, you really can’t hear anything from outside.

However, every time I walk by the mirror, saw the look of me wearing these, I look like a teletuby! The shape is so round, no human head will fit into that perfectly, especially if you have a small head, not sure if it looks good on girls. Because it leaves the huge gap on each side, I can put my hand thru the gap, looks SO weird, to the point, I can’t stand looking at myself, let alone walking in public. And I was planing to use this while working out and traveling.
Also the metal dials are super sensitive, if you accidentally touch it, it stops. Because the headset covers your ears completely without gap, you do feel hot, I had to take off and let my ears breathe and cool down.
And the big bag is cheaply made imho. I’d think they ought to look like the stitches from the Porsche cars or the Hermès bags. And I’m not sure if it’s made in real leather but the material is very hard and my bag came in dinted already. Just fyi, The bag shuts the headset off, so maybe it has to be made in hard than soft material.

Overall, not sure about >$2000, half of that, i’d think it’s fair.

reviews.author: Leandro Camaraza
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 24, 2025
reviews.content: Great quality in both the sound and the equipment. They’re super comfortable and have excellent noise cancellation. It’s true they’re a bit expensive, but definitely a solid choice if you enjoy listening to high-quality music.

reviews.author: Whalers
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 26, 2025
reviews.content: I own the H95’s as well the newest Bose Gen 2. Took a chance and decided to spend the extra money after trying out the newest Sony’s (returned due to sound issues) and the Bowers & Wilkins Px8 and PX8-S2’s (gave PX8’s to my son) to see what the hype about the H100’s was all about. Needless to say, I get it and love the sound quality they produce which is slightly louder, crisper and more spacial. Are they worth the extra money over the H95’s? That’s still debatable, but there is definitely a sound difference. Also the personal prestige of knowing you wear one of the best wireless headsets on the market clinched it. For me, these are definite keepers, but if I had to do it over again I would try and wait until they go on sale to offset the sting of paying $2200.

reviews.author: iowadave
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 10, 2025
reviews.content: They sound very good but are far less comfortable than the AirPods Max. Even worse, if you have an iPhone 17 series running iOS 26 (or an iPad running PadOS 26) they will not connect with the Beoplay app. The company has acknowledged this but good luck getting an ETA on a fix (or an ETA on adding the hi-res Codecs). The H100s will still work with just a Bluetooth connection, but you lose out on all the customization functions. Under the circumstances, no way these are worth the money.

reviews.author: Omar Shahadat
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 24, 2025
reviews.content:
Okay these headphones are really incredible. They sound so so good, the battery life is probably my favorite part about it. It lasts forever and charges super quick.
The EQ is very simple it's a circular design and you select what mode you prefer (bass,treble,etc). The bass is super rich and deep, rap and hip hop tracks sound incredible on these. Rock also sounds amazing. Instrumental heavy music is incredible the separation of the instruments sound insane.

The left ear cup rotates and adjusts the amount of ANC you want, while the right adjusts volume. Both sides of the headphones are soft touch glass with swipe gestures. You also have multifunctional buttons on both of them where you can set in the app.

The app, pretty much straight forward app, adjusts the sound, buttons, pretty much everything on the headphones. They are continuously updating the app, they recently just added better Bluetooth connection from the update.

Now things I don't like. I think for the price they are solidy built but I think they are overpriced for sure. I have been using the PX8 from BW and I have to say sound quality wise they are similar, the BO being more clearer and the BW having more deep bass. The build quality of the BW is also very good and it has 5.3 Bluetooth and imo the app looks much cleaner. I'm bringing these up because when you are shopping for headphones in this level of quality you will definitely bump into the bowers and Wilkins.
Not to mention they are a fraction of the price.

Lastly the Bluetooth on the h100s is okay, multi connection is alright, and the anc is alright. Bose and Sony definitely have it beat at that. I think if you are in the market for these wait for them to get on sale and then purchase them. I picked mine up at about $1700. So if you can find it around this price then I'd say definitely worth trying it out. But anything more I'd pass on it especially at its $2200 full price.

reviews.author: Daniel Podgurski
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 16, 2025
reviews.content:
the ability to field strip to clean made the deal. no more smelly headset

with eu privacy rights, you can give the middle finger to data collection

sound: great
build quality: bombproof
value: diminishing returns depending. yours. they are eye wateringly expensive, but worth it to me.

reviews.author: T. J.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 10, 2024
reviews.content:
I purchased the SkullCandy Crusher ANC-2 after reading many reviews....starting with the 1- & 2-Star reviews. I don't feel the need to read good reviews if I can be content with the bad reviews. Many of the bad and/or negative reviews I read were either about:
- Bass overpowering/ inadequate ability to adjust
- Noise Cancelling didn't work well enough
- Comfort
- The 'matte' finish got blemished too easily

So to quickly touch on the BEST points about the SkullCandy Crusher ANC-2.
- ***** BLUETOOTH Distance WELL OVER 175+ FEET AWAY. Tested this over a 10-minute period in open, unobstructed line-of-sight........and over 60+ feet within a house, many rooms away.
- ***** SOUND QUALITY is Clear, Crisp, and seems to auto-adjust (not sure if it does or not) for the music I am listening to....and I listen to pretty much all of the major genres...i.e. Rock, Rap, Country, and even Electronic Dance Music (EDM)....sounds great with all!
- ***** COMFORT fit and holds to the head whether you are working out or working in the yard, the SkullCandy Crusher ANC-2 maintains comfortability!
- ***** BATERY LIFE IS PHENOMENAL!!!! After my initial charge, I wore the SkullCandy Crusher ANC-2 for a full day (8-hours of play time) and only used 20% of the battery....so the following day, I wore for about another full day again and used 20%....CRUSHER told me I had 60% battery remaining after 2-days of use.
- ***** HANDS-FREE Voice control and SK app makes connecting to music easy once set-up properly!

As for what most reviews "complained" about....I didn't see an issue with any of them really!

- The intensity of the "Crusher" Bass 'can' be turned up/down, however it should be noted that the dial that rolls, also needs to be PUSHED in to make adjustments to bass output....but this is NOT like the "BASS KNOB" on your car radio....meaning, you can not adjust the bass the near-0...or even to a "4-level", to be honest. The headphones are made to put a bit more bass-bump into your ears. PERFECT for gym time and those audiophiles!

- I mentioned my thoughts about comfort above.

- The MATTE FINISH IS THE BEST!!!!....(for me!)
I have a pretty active lifestyle that includes wearing around the house....doing yard work....driving tractors and backhoes...gardening...etc. In the short time (2-1/2 weeks) I have had the SkullCandy Crusher ANC-2, I have dropped (or otherwise) them numerous times. The matte finish has never scratched, as I fear a "glossy finish" would have, making imperfections more obvious. I just take a disinfectant wipe to clean the headphones.

- I feel MANY PEOPLE who "complained" about the ANC "noise cancelling" feature not working are CONFUSED as to what this is exactly! This DOES NOT mean that these headphones completely "soundproof" your ears!!! AND THIS ESPECIALLY doesn't work if you have no sound coming through the headphones at all!!! If you want those headphones, you're better off looking in an aviation-pilot store somewhere. BUT the ANC feature does do it's job in cancelling outside noise while listeninng to tunes at an adequate level.

----

BTW...I'm not one to ever write reviews for products, but I have been legitimately blown away by the SkullCandy Crusher ANC 2, that I felt a need to counter some of the negative reviews that made me ponder my decision to buy. It wasn't until a good friend of mine told me she loves her SkullCandy products, that I took a chance and purchased.

I'm glad I did!

TJ

reviews.author: Alexa
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 10, 2025
reviews.content:
One day I asked my dad what headphones he thinks were the best to purchase ( I was originally thinking of giving him a red and black beats that literally suited his rockstar personality for Father’s Day) I was expecting him to say beats headphones but for my surprise he said skull candy. I started looking multiple reviews on different skull candy headphones models, I found on sale the skull candy crusher in light greenish second hand well taken care off and on sale, when it first arrived I was SO SURPRISED, when I first used them to make sure they were working perfectly fine I got jealous because you can adjust the bass with one bottom, which is something that my beats headphone don’t have and thought it was awesome that some pair of headphones could have that. Fast forward I took the decision of buying another pair for me the same I gave to my dad. I ended up buying the crusher ANC 2 just because of the good reviews and stars and let me tell…. It has been the best decision!. The crushers ANC2 it’s so easy to pair. One thing I first tried is voice recognition something that I used to struggle with my Siri when I owned my beats headphones.
I could never get Siri work or hear me
Properly , always took so many tries. With the crusher ANC2 is was immediately the voice recognition, super fast that I put a face like : 😱,another cool thing that crusher ANC2 has is that has like a little wheel to adjust how much bass you want (it tells you the percentage 20%,40%, 60% ,etc) , something that the regular crushers don’t really have, and definitely something that beats don’t even have!. It also has a noise cancellation option and awareness. I thought the ANC 2 were going to be HEAVY, but comparing it with my beats IT’S SO LIGHTWEIGHT, like hello? How!!?. My beats were so heavy , always ripping my hair off hurting my Elix piercing so bad, falling out of my head hurting my hears , this ANC2 are so comfy and cover all your ears and around it, It covers your ears but it gives it a little bit more extra space outside your ears, which makes it comfy for piercings! ( finally 🥹) it has a cushion on the top
Of your head and feels like a little pillow super soft , beats does have a cushion on top of the head which is stiff and regular crushers Evo do have one but it’s the same material has the beats but bigger than beats. It comes with two types of chargers , a type C and normal and one type USB and a cable to connect it directly to any computer( can be use wired or wireless )the only thing I don’t really like it’s the case, beats headphones come with a hard case and
A clip.this crushers come with a case that looks like a bag. But other than that I’m obsessed with them it is worth every penny. I also tested out while I working out and it stayed in placed, the beats headphones always slipped out of my head
No Matter how tight I try to adjusted to my head. My dad have tested out both headphones and he said : “approved by a rock fan ( evo crusher and ANC2) , this is NUTS, can’t wait to listen my favorite rock songs 😃” has a person that has owned JBL headphones, BEATS ,RODE, regular AirPods , etc this ones are the best so far , it won’t disappoint… trust me.I’m a big fan of headphones always looking for quality sound
Forgot to mention that on the app for this headphones has different modes of listening such as music mode , movie, podcast , same for audio and change function of bottoms

reviews.author: John Mackerell
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2026
reviews.content:
The sound quality and the bass are an AMAZING experience!!! Spent hundreds on other high-quality headphones, and these are what I've been wanting. Complete control of bass while maintaining clarity of vocals, mids, and highs, and my first experience with ANC (wow, just wow). I no longer need an external amp for my computer to enjoy full Dolby Atmos for headphones. The ANC alone made the Dolby Atmos just come to life.

The bass on these is crazy! This is coming from my background of qty:8,12"subs in the back of a hatchback (161db's from sealed enclosures in a 1996 Hyundai Accent lol). I have the bass turned up to 75%, and that's enough. 100% turned up, and I love it, but it fatigues me quickly and it's so not the same as a full-body SPL bass hit, but it does the job without pissing off your neighbors in a several block radius. One great thing about these is that having the bass at 100%, I can still hear vocals and shake the crap out of my head lol. I listen to a lot of EDM/trance, and some lyricical rap (not mumble crap), some hip/hop, and pop and these are beautiful with bass at 50%. EDM/trance at 50-75% is like being the club! If I could describe the overall tone of the headset, I'd say a very warm tone. The 1Khz band on up is not strong, but might be handled by the "IQ" app (have not played with it as I'm extremely happy stock). Not sure if this is a bad thing, as I hated my ears being beatup but high freq tweeters. High crisp freqs are like being attacked by 100's of hissing snakes on your ear drums lol

I got the real tree white set. The build quality and the skin are freaking amazing! If these end up being $20-40 more than the solid color, still get them! Never seen headphones with this pattern, and it's really something to see. Even the storage bag has the same pattern. So worth every penny.

Bluetooth connection was super easy. My computer and phone literally have it as "crusher ANC 2" so there is no trying to figure out what your headphones are. The Bluetooth distance is insane, too. I have a 1800sq/ft cement-walled house, and there are ZERO deadspots.

reviews.author: Trevor
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 16, 2023
reviews.content:
I'm a mix between a basshead and an audiophile. I installed car stereos for 15 years so I've heard a lot of different types of setups. I had a pair of Sony XB950BT's that I really liked, but they finally broke, so I needed some replacement cans. I loved the Sony's low end and they didn't sound too bad everywhere else either, so I wanted something comparable. I bought a pair of beats solo3's and was very disappointed with the lack of low end, volume, and overall sound quality. So like any intelligent idiot, I did my research after impulse buying something that didn't fit the bill and came across these ANC 2's. They deliver. I wasn't really sure what the sound quality would be like, but it's way better than I expected. These beat the Sony's in every way, better sound quality, more volume by quite a bit, and bass like a couple of 12's in the trunk with 6-700 watts. It's an impressive amount of bass. The only thing missing is feeling it in your chest when the bassline drops. You'd probably have to spend 2k on a setup in your car to replicate the way these sound as a whole package.

You do need to tweak them some with the equalizer in the skull IQ app. I've found that turning the the low and the low mid about 3 lines down from the top and then adjusting the bass through the crusher wheel gets you the cleanest sound. If you turn the crusher bass all the way off, it loses a lot below about 40Hz. But adjusted like that with the wheel at about 10 or 20% gets you a nice full sound with some good thump on the bottom end but it doesn't get muddy. That way also keeps it fairly linear through the volume range so you don't have way too much bass at lower levels until you turn it up like some other ways I had tried tuning it before. Some songs don't sound great above that, but some you can go all the way to the max on the crusher wheel and still sound clean. These seem to have the highest pressure levels around 40Hz and drop off a fair amount under 30Hz. You can still hear them down to and below 20Hz though, but not nearly at the pressure levels you do at 40Hz. The mids and highs are way cleaner than I expected and these handle rock and classic rock very well. Like I said before, these aren't audiophile quality, but from being in the sound industry for a lot of years, I got picky about sound quality, but still love more than average low end. These marry the two perfectly if tuned right and I don't have any problem with using these as my daily set.

They're also very comfortable. They don't clamp too tight on your ears, and the ear cushions go all the way around my ears. No hotspots. I can wear these for hours and be comfortable. The ANC has a hiss when it's on, but I didn't buy these for noise cancelling so I just turn it off. I can't comment on the durability because I just bought them, but if these hold up long term, they're worth every penny and then some if you're like me and want a ton of bass at times, but also want a nice rich/full sounding set of cans when the mood strikes I love that I can change them from one to the other with the roll of the crusher wheel and not have to do anything else.

reviews.author: Milagra Norman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.content: These headphones are exceptional quality 👌 the color is vibrant, it covers the ear comfortably, it connects to your phone easily and adjust the volume it it's too high or to low , you can adjust on your head, It won't slide off. The battery life is amazing, The BASS is on point. YOU CAN NOT GO WRONG WITH THESE

reviews.author: Tyman Hayashi
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 28, 2025
reviews.content: I was very surprised to have Skullcandy enter the fold of Boise cancelling. I never really liked beats in my ears because all them never really fit. Bose were good, but weren’t that good looking. When I bought these, I wanted to try them out and I was surprised to actually get great noise cancelling out of them. They are more for leisure use, so when I am on the go with airplanes and buses, I can’t hear anything outside much. It fold up well so easy to carry out. It has a roll and clip bag that is easy to stick on a bag. I have more rounder ears, but these surprisingly fit around my whole ear covering it. I like how comfortable it is on my head. The crusher is basically another word for bass. It amplifies the bass on your earphones and I have to say, the bass goes hard on these things. Easy to use.

reviews.author: Harrison Knight
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 1, 2026
reviews.content: Just a great pair of headphones, good battery life, and comfortable fit. They don’t feel too bulky, but laying on your side with them is a big no. You see, every time I apply pressure on the side of the headphones while the ANC is activated, it makes this painful sharp noise that hurts my ear for a second. That’s really the only bad thing about these headphones, overall they’re awesome!

reviews.author: Keith Cannon
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content: After owning 5 pair of crushers I can say I will be happy with these (until they improve the ANC...then I will buy the new model). These have great bass. The app is good. They are louder in the mids and highs than the standard crushers and evo's. The anc isn't great...I usually have it turned off. These are not for audiophiles. Just for bass heads!

reviews.author: Devall
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 10, 2025
reviews.content: Wonderful sound, blocks out a lot of noise. Steelseries always comes correct with even their cheaper products! I use this for my ps5, switch 2, and sometimes my MacBook and it works flawlessly. The mic is very good and being retract it in and out is a great feature. They are also so comfortable! The headband makes it feel like there is a barely anything on your head. Super light! Truly plug and play. Volume control is a little touchy but works just fine.

reviews.author: Leoray
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 26, 2025
reviews.content:
I am a hardcore gamer, so when I need equipment like keyboards, mice, and or headsets I always go with "Steel Series". While it is name brand and can be expensive, you get your moneys worth. This headset is amazing and very comfortable. It has high-quality sound that can let you hear things you normally couldnt hear through a speaker. The microphone sound is clear and everyone listening to you can hear clearly without any inconvenient noises thay come with some mics.
I also find it convenient that the mic is fully retractable as that when I am not using it I can simply tuck it away. Another thing I like is that the mic arm is flexible and can be moved closer to you or further out most others. As for the head frame, there is a cloth piece that spans the width of the headset thaf is adjustable and or removable. This is very convenient and comfortable as wearing headsets (particularly the hard plastic that sits on the head), for long periods of time can hurt or be uncomfortable. Also the ear pieces are made from very soft breathable cloth and they are big enough to fit around the ears instead of sitting on them or the edges. I have had multiple headsets and all of them sat on the edges of my ears and would eventually become uncomfortable but not with this set. The ears on this set literally fits AROUND the ENTIRE ear and instead of MOST of the ear. Other sets you can feel your ears under the edge of the cover but with this one you can not feel them at all. As far as long gaming sessions I have sat in on a four hour session with this set and the comfort was so great that I nearly forgot I was wearing a headset. It also has a large built in on / off button for the microphone as well as a big volume roll button for volume control.

reviews.author: Maximiliano Abramovich
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026
reviews.content:
I’ve been using the SteelSeries Arctis Nova 1P for a few weeks now, and overall it’s a solid choice if you want a wired gaming headset that’s comfortable, versatile, and sounds great for the price.

🎮 Sound & Performance:
The audio is clear and well-balanced, making it easy to hear footsteps and directional cues in shooters as well as enjoy movies or music. It supports 360° spatial audio and delivers good positional awareness for gaming.

🎧 Comfort:
These are lightweight (around ~236 g) and comfy for long sessions thanks to adjustable headband and breathable ear pads. They sit nicely even with glasses.

🎤 Microphone & Controls:
The ClearCast Gen 2 noise-canceling mic does a good job keeping background noise down and makes your voice easy to hear in game chat. On-ear controls for volume and mute are handy, and the universal 3.5 mm connection works with PS5, Xbox, PC, Switch and mobile devices without adapters.

⚠️ A Few Things to Know:

The cable can feel a bit short on PC setups and sometimes requires a Y-adapter.

Some users note that customizing EQ via software can take a bit of learning.

There are occasional reports online of mic detection or durability quirks, but these seem to be edge cases rather than the norm.

Bottom Line: If you’re after reliable wired performance with good comfort and clear audio for gaming and voice chat, the Arctis Nova 1P is definitely worth considering — especially if you plan to use it across multiple platforms.

reviews.author: Katalina Eunice
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 17, 2025
reviews.content: Each SteelSeries I’ve bought has been raved about. My brothers, boyfriend and I all love them. They sell themselves. Comfort, quality sound and quality made AND affordable. Even their base models hit all the marks, noise cancellation and volume adjustability and having a working mic. Never have anything bad to say about a SteelSeries. My brother doesn’t treat his stuff well but loved his set so much in comparison to other headsets that he used his SteelSeries for years after breaking part of the band, rather than using a perfectly new TurtleBeach. If it’s not SteelSeries, it’s not for us.

reviews.author: Benson Smith
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026
reviews.content:
Decent sound. Haven’t used them during gaming. Gonna have to see what gunfire and explosions sound like. They do work better with the PS5’s 3D audio though. I’ve only ever owned Turtle beach’s. On another note, delivery people need to follow delivery instructions. There’s a monopoly on Amazon packages and there’s a reason why I have “please ring doorbell “ in the delivery instructions. Seller did a great job though. Packaging was superb.

Update: Love them. I thought Turtle beach’s were the best and those are all I’ve ever owned other than the platinum PlayStation. These Steele series are phenomenal in my opinion.

reviews.author: Helena
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 4, 2024
reviews.content: I bought the Arctis Nova 1P to replace a headset that has a terribly uncomfortable head-band. What this product didn't make obvious or even suggest from the outside was that 1, it is NOT wireless, which is a major requirement for me, 2, does NOT come with instructions for what to do with it, 3, comes with ONE short cable that is NOT used to charge the device, leading me to wonder what the Hell it's even for, not to mention, 4, repeating the fact that it is a VERY short cable, and neither of my 2 PCs have jacks close enough to put it in. This singular cable's got male audio jacks on both ends, and I have no clue what I'm supposed to do with it. The one tutorial/set-up video I could find had the barest subtitle in the midst of the footage that said a separate dual-ended cable isn't even included on the Nova 1P, and NOTHING is telling me what I'm supposed to do with the cable it DID give me. Is there supposed to be a USB dongle??? A USB-C cable??? Why isn't it included??? What the HELL do I do with this damn thing!? I've never been so flummoxed in my life over a simple bloody headset. I'm so angry and irritated. I wish I could take this listing off Amazon for being a fraud product. Now I have to go through the hassle of returning it just so I can be paranoid about the next headset I buy being misleading, too.

reviews.author: Brandon
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 22, 2021
reviews.content:
(Xbox user) I went thru 3 different brand headphones and it was either durability or the mic that was a issue. I was extremely skeptical on what to get next and kept going back and forth trying to decide. Finally I just said F it bought the Arctis 3 console edition .
Pros-
-Very lite and and ear pads are very soft feels great for long gaming sessions and wearing glasses with.
- placement of buttons are very convenient
- love the hanging band idea making it feel like the headset isn’t even on and no memory foam to worry about coming out from bad stitching or glue like my last pair on the headband part.
- so far strong wire connection into headset , doesn’t just pull out easy if I accidentally snag it or pull on it.
- mic is awesome and friends can hear me great and it’s not picking up gaming noise from the headphones when I’m not talking which was a big issue picking up sounds from my headphones to the mic when i wasn’t speaking with my other brands.
Cons
- they aren’t very snug so sometimes they move very little while playing but that can be a pro for some folks giving not everyone likes the snug fit.
- wish it had noise cancellation. It deft cuts out a lot of noise but if complete noise canceling is what u want these will let some sound in . Not a lot but some and it’s noticeable compared to true noise cancellation.
- could be a tad bit louder. It sounds great and I can hear everything in the game very well but I feel they can just be alittle louder compared to my last set of headsets.

All in all definitely a 5 star for me !! Great sounding and very comfortable . The cons for me even tho they are downsides they are very minuscule and don’t take away from any gaming session at all . If your skeptical just go for it with these you won’t be disappointed!

reviews.author: Justin M.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 15, 2024
reviews.content: I bought 2 pairs of these for my kids for Christmas. When we opened them the overall quality of the headset was really nice for the price. My kids even commented of how comfortable they were to wear. Within days of having them, one of the earpieces detached from the headset portion. It was just a screw that came loose and my husband was able to easily put it back together. Unfortunately this kept happening over and over again, only to realize the screw that hold the 2 pieces together isn’t quite long enough and with the Earpiece swiveling, I guess it kept coming loose. After many attempts to fix, we now have had to super glue the earpiece to the headpiece and of course has no ability to swivel. On a side note, the 2nd headset I bought is still intact and hasn’t broken yet, so make was a fluke with just one. I would give 5 stars if one of them hadn’t broke.

reviews.author: Kristian
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content:
Good audio , clear mic and decent battery.
For my needs these are 5/5

reviews.author: Cal Lim
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 23, 2024
reviews.content:
I just bought the Xbox Wireless Headset for a myself for Christmas and it exceeded my expectations in every way. The sound quality is phenomenal, with deep bass and clear highs that make gaming incredibly immersive. The noise-isolating design allows me to focus entirely on my games, and the microphone is crystal clear for voice chats.

What stood out the most was the comfort—the lightweight design and adjustable fit make it perfect for extended gaming sessions without any discomfort. The seamless connectivity with my Xbox Series X was a huge plus, and I also appreciated the Bluetooth feature for pairing with my phone.

There’s honestly nothing I dislike about this headset. It’s become my go-to for everything from gaming marathons to casual music listening. For the price, the build quality and features are unbeatable. Highly recommend it to any gamer looking for a reliable and versatile headset!

reviews.author: Jennifer Schultz
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 10, 2026
reviews.content: Quality product. Has two volume controls that make it easy to hear soft spoken players without raising the volume of the game.

reviews.author: robert
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content: Love the simultaneous Bluetooth, the quality is fine and i like the bass. I've never had an issue with a headset feeling so uncomfortable. I guess i have a large head, i havent had discomfort with any other. I dont like wearing the headset for more than 30 minutes.

reviews.author: Rebecca Nauglebaugh
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2026
reviews.content: My son lives these A said they were great quality especially for the price. I don’t know anything about this kind of product but he approved so we give five stars.

reviews.author: Juan Lozano
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviews.content: Al principio son incómodos, toca usarlos para que se asienten, luego de eso ni se notan que los llevas puestos. La calidad del micrófono es excelente y ni hablar de la calidad del sonido. Son excelentes en todo sentido. La función de que se conecta al celular para poder recibir llamadas mientras juegas me pareció super interesante

reviews.author: William Allen
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.content: For me, they are absilutely awesome. I have never used headphones with the Dolby app. I am. very happy with this purchase.

reviews.author: Shane Councill
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 17, 2025
reviews.content: Good Xbox gaming headset at a very affordable price. Very impressive to me that it can simultaneously connect to the Xbox and a smartphone. The dials on each side of the headset are amazing and such an intuitive feature. Microphone also functions fantastically and is easily used for phone calls on your phone or Xbox parties. Only complaints I have, I have to max out the size adjustment on the headset, because I have a huge head. Also, it deals with some connectivity issues but I think that's only because my girlfriend also has an Xbox nearby and it might be interfering with the signal. Overall, it's a great headset at a great price.

reviews.author: Andrew D
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 4, 2021
reviews.content:
Short of plugging these into a HyperX Quadcast mic, I doubt you'll find a much better setup for gaming....especially at this price point.
