The sound quality is amazing for headphones in any context and the earcups are very very comfy. The built-in mic sounds very good for a headset mic, but I digress...

I started off with the Cloud I's first and absolutely loved them, but the volume pot started getting scratchy and I worried about damage to the attached cable becoming an issue. So I figured I'd stick with HyperX and give these Cloud Alpha's a try, since the cable does detach. I love the Cloud I's, so I'll just stick to comparing the two of those as they are both phenomenal headsets and would both be a good buy.

-Build and Comfort-
HyperX is just KILLING it on the comfort front IMO. The earcups are basically like tempurpedic memory foam and are a joy to wear. Where the Alpha differs from the Cloud I's is how they feel out of the box. The Alphas still super comfy, but firmer than the Cloud I's. After some wear-in, they soften up, but I can imagine the Alphas lasting longer because of this. Still comfy, just not quite as squishy. Some people may prefer a firmer feel, YMMV.

Both headphones feel very solid and have a good weight to them. Could use some more padding in the headband, but I don't really feel much discomfort after wearing them. This is about the only comfort area I could see them needing improvement on. Not sure if it really amounts to build quality, but the previously mentioned attached cable on the Cloud I's has become an issue for me (the volume pot is really scratchy), but the Alphas have a removable cord and the volume pot feels much more sturdy as well.

-Sound Quality-
Holy crap, I have literally returned $500 Shure headphones in favor of these. And to all assuming I have unrefined, plebian ears...16 years as a guitarist/bassist, audio engineering work for 6 of those years, running PA boards at a few venues, and a gig as a backing bassist for a solo artist (using in-ears) would put me a cut above the typical dude listening to Spotify on his iPhone. I know a good pair when I hear it.

Now the Shures were not flat response for mixing or monitoring, they were listening headphones, but while there is a slight EQ curve and some "signature" enhancements on the Alphas, they are surprisingly flat in a good way...albeit with some extended bass and very smooth highs. The difference is the Cloud I's seemed to have more bass due to an EQ bump in the low-end response along with the seal being tighter on your ears with their squishier earcups. The Alphas are firmer and so the seal isn't quite as tight on my head, making for less perceived bass at first, but if you squish them on your ears you catch a bit more of the low-end.

I will also say the low-end on the Alphas is much cleaner than on the Cloud I's, which may not be caught by some listeners. When you hear explosions, rumbling, or are listening to bassy music you won't hear these buzzing or bottoming out like the Cloud I's can. I use an external Sound Blaster card with one of my PCs and my custom EQ curve for music listening is handled beautifully by these Alphas. This by no means is an insult to the Cloud I's, they are easily contenders with most $100-$250 listening headsets out there, but in comparison to the Alphas, these are the differences you'll experience.

-Noise Isolation-
The Alphas are, again, not as tight a seal as the Cloud I's but that could change in time. FWIW, these cut out a noticeable amount of noise...with only really rumbly or booming sounds getting through. A neighbor firing up a giant truck or thunder outside will catch my attention, but high pitched sounds get drowned out when you are listening to music or playing a game. I'm sure with wear they'll get even better, but for a passive headset they do a fine job.

-Mic Quality-
The mic is perfectly fine and right in line with most $100+ gaming headset mics. Don't expect something on-par with a dedicated mic like the HyperX Quadcast or a Razer Siren, this is a totally different league of mic, but does a respectable job. Most people use these for Discord or Zoom calls, or want to cut down on background noise as opposed to using an external mic.

-Connectors-
The core cable has the headphone/mic conductors built into one 3.5mm plug, the same as old iPhone headsets and many other gaming headsets do. There is an extender cable that splits it out to a headphone and mic plug separately. One thing I found weird, is my Cloud I's were the same, but when I plugged the single 3.5mm combo jack into my HyperX Quadcast they flat out would not work. HOWEVER, the Alphas DO work for some reason. Not sure why, seems like basically the same cable. Either way, the cable is long enough for most folks' rigs so no worries on that.

-Overall-
If you want a gaming headset, or even just a headset for listening to music at your computer comfortably, this is an amazing headset. If you're fine with over-ear headphones for listening on the go, there's no reason not to use these IMO. If you're a streamer and want to save money and still get great performance...BUY THESE!!

Some people might scoff at these and not consider them good since they aren't "Pro Level" gear, but honestly for monitoring and even music listening I would put these up against nearly any sub-$600 headset. I don't abuse the hell out of my headphones, but they definitely get put to use for hours on end every day and I've still had no issues. The Cloud I's (non-USB) are great too, but for a bit more the Alphas are just plain amazing. The only thing you need to worry about these is if they discontinue them!

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026
reviews.content:
I grabbed this as an inexpensive upgrade from basic earbuds, and for the price it’s surprisingly decent. Build quality is mostly plastic but feels sturdy enough. The ear cushions are soft and comfy even after a couple hours of gaming, and the headband doesn’t dig in.

Audio is balanced — games sound clear, voices are crisp in Discord/party chat, and lows are decent for the price range. Don’t expect deep bass like higher-end headsets, but it’s good enough for shooters, RPGs, and voice chat without fatigue.

Mic quality is okay — not studio grade, but picks up voice clearly and cancels a bit of background noise. Definitely fine for gaming sessions with friends.

reviews.author: Consumer Extraordinaire
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 6, 2021
reviews.content:
Let me just start this off by saying I never leave reviews. I'm not very easily impressed. I usually buy something, receive it, and carry on with my day. However, I've had these for over two years now, and it only just recently dawned on me how much of an anomaly this headset has been compared to others I've had in the past. In the hundreds, if not thousands of hours I've used this headset for, not once has it ever consciously occurred to me that they're being worn while in use. You slap them on out of the box, adjust the earcups to your liking, and then forget they're there. These things are so damn comfortable that I regularly forget I'm wearing them when I leave my apartment to go do other activities. I can't sing its praises enough in terms of comfort, since it feels like wearing nothing at all once properly adjusted.

As for the sound quality, it's pretty damn good. I've never really been much of an audio snob, but this is more than sufficient if you're just looking for something with quality sound. I've used them for everything from games to podcasts, and there has never been an instance where I was underwhelmed in any capacity by the sound quality here. Absolutely zero complaints from me in this department. The noise cancellation is pretty good, too. I mean, it's not gonna completely tune out anything and everything, but background noise is definitely reduced. My apartment's AC is only about ten feet from my desk, and it gets to be decently loud at times. Though the noise from it is still noticeable when wearing them, it still becomes maybe 30% quieter; even at that close a range. Get sucked into a game, movie, podcast, or album wearing these, and you'll soon forget about any white noise going on in your immediate vicinity anyway. You can also buy HyperX's 7.1 surround sound USB adapter to beef up the sound a bit should you so choose, but I mainly use it for additional volume control and ease of use instead of the surround sound. Maybe I'm using it wrong, but it's just never worked for me.

If you're sometimes rough with your peripherals like me, you probably are looking for something that can take a bit more punishment than most. Rest assured you won't be disappointed here, because this thing's rock solid. Just off the top of my head, my Cloud Alpha has survived:

-Being slammed in the heavy wooden door leading to my apartment
-Being slammed in a car door
-Being accidentally rolled over at least two dozen times by sixty pound steel office chair
-Being tossed onto hardwood flooring when I missed my intended target of the couch more times than I can count
-Being dropped six feet onto solid concrete
-Being stepped on at least two hundred different times
-Being vomited on on more than one occasion, though that can probably be attributed to luck more than anything else

Seriously, there are times when throw this guy around and have the headband pretzel in a concerning fashion, only to pick them up again, put them back on, and continue where I left off none the worse for wear. Every time I'd get a little alarmed at the aggressive way that I'd just manhandled my headset, it'd be a completely irrational fear. Barring smashing these things with a hammer or actually trying to bend the headband 180 degrees in the opposite direction, there's no conceivable way to me you could break these in day to day use. I've included a few pictures of the wear and tear they've accumulated over the years, as well as the lengths to which they regularly bend just to give you an idea of the punishment they can take. Believe me when I say the build quality is second to none.

Now, at this point you know the headset itself is durable, but you might still be wondering about the cable used to connect said headset to your preferred device. Wonder no more. The included cable is 48 inches of (what might as well be) braided steel, and in my two years of usage, it's accumulated nary a scratch save for a small, nearly nonexistent kink below the volume control. It's detachable too, so you never have to worry about the annoyance and potential stress point that comes from having a cable permanently anchored to your headset. I've coiled this thing up all sorts of ways, tossed it into my pockets and backpack in a ball, and it's none the worse for wear considering. Even if you DO somehow manage to break the cable or render it otherwise unusable, don't worry; it can be replaced with any other 3.5mm audio cable with zero hit to sound quality. Trust me. I've checked.

My only real gripe about this product is that my microphone has never worked; even brand new out of the box. I've since bought a replacement mic that also hasn't worked either, so my guess is that it's a manufacturing defect rather than something that broke during use over the years. This is really only something that you should think about if you don't already have a mic and/or will be using this mainly for games. Even if yours also turns out to be broken, I'm sure the folks in customer support would be more than willing to exchange your defective set for a functioning unit.

But yeah. This has got to be the best headset I've ever purchased in the ten odd years I've been using them. The price is a bit steep compared your standard headset fare, but the price of admission is well worth it. I've used these things so much that the pleather on the earcups is starting to peel off, and I'll continue to use them until I literally can't anymore, for whatever reason that may be. Can't recommend them enough.

reviews.author: Cody Morgan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 2, 2025
reviews.content:
The HyperX Cloud Alpha is a solid headset, especially for the price point. The sound quality is excellent — crisp highs, solid mids, and the kind of bass you’d expect from something in this tier. The mic quality is also surprisingly good for a non-premium headset — my friends on voice chat have never had any complaints.

That said, I’ll be honest — I’m a bit spoiled by Astro headsets, and in direct comparison, the Cloud Alpha just doesn’t hit the same in terms of comfort. I usually game in 3+ hour sessions, and with these, I find myself constantly adjusting them on my head. They’re not unbearable by any means, but they’re definitely not as comfortable as I’d like for extended use.

Build quality overall is very solid. The headset feels durable and well-made — nothing about it feels cheap. If you’re looking for a budget-friendly headset with great audio and a decent mic, this is a strong choice. But if comfort during long gaming sessions is a priority for you, you may want to spend a little more.

4 out of 5 stars from me — mostly for the sound, mic, and build quality.

reviews.author: Michael Kehoe
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2018
reviews.content:
This is nothing but a downgrade over my original HyperX Cloud headset that I bought these to replace. Nothing wrong with my current headset, it was just several years old and showing some wear and tear. These are actually worse than those original Clouds. The sound isolation is far, far worse, to the point of offering almost no sound isolation at all. Best guess that lack of sound isolation is due to the different ear-pad material and the fact that there are several small hole at the top of each ear cup. What purpose those holes serve I have no idea, but I do know they allow sound to leak out of the ear cups, so that means they are allowing sound to leak into the ear cups as well.

The clamping force is almost non existent as well. This adds even more to the lack of sound isolation. The ear-pads are not maintain a tight seal and I can hear the difference if I physically press them tighter against my head.

The removable cable creates the same issue that every headset with a removable cable has. It transfers the vibrations of the cable rubbing against your shirt into the ear cup. A hardwired connection doesn't transfer those vibrations as much to the point where they are as noticeable. But when you have a removable cable, the socket and metal connection amplify those vibrations. Not a huge deal, and I knew this before I made my purchase, but its important to point out as I am comparing how they are worse than my original Clouds.

I have not been able to test the mic quality with a third party yet but testing the mic for picking up that sound leakage I mentioned earlier, its really bad. Running a recorder and playing a song in the background, the song is clearly picked up through the microphone and that's why my volume at only 48%, which is where I run it at all times under normal conditions. I did not have this issue with my original Cloud headset.

I am finding no benefit to the advertised "dual chamber" design. They sound no better than my original Cloud headset, but at least they sound no worse. A final parting thought, while I appreciate that the inline control is smaller than the original, the new one is obvious garbage. I guarantee the volume wheel will be the first point of failure on this headset. Especially since they put it so close to the ear cup that its basically designed to get bumped around by the user. I can't tuck it away safely on my desk like I could with my original Cloud.

Edit:
Lowered to 1 star as I've determined what the holes at the top of the ear cups are for. They lie and bill this headset as a closed back design, but as I mentioned before it doesn't have the sound isolation like it should as a closed back headset. Well its because of those holes. Compounding that issue is the drivers used in this headset sound terrible if you convert it into a closed back design by plugging those holes. I put a piece of electrical tape over each row of holes on the ear cups. It helps with the sound isolation, but made them sound terrible. The original clouds are fully closed back and sound great.

Like I thought, the whole dual chamber design is a gimmick. The drivers they're using are simply garbage compared to what they used previously when they were really just slapping their branding on a headset made by someone else. You can test what I'm talking about without using tape. I have a fan running in my room, so its easy for me to notice the sound isolation. With these Alphas on, if I run my fingers along the top to block those holes, the sound from my fan drops significantly. You can run that same test if you don't want to stick tape on them. But doing so reduces the sound quality. I can hear the bass drop in the song I was listening to.

Edit 2: There terrible placement of the in line controls means it smacks into my desk the second every time I take the headset off. Its at that perfect, terrible, height to ensure that the second it comes off your head it will hit whatever is around you. Its at a terrible height when you're wearing it too, you have to lift your hand up off your desk and bend your wrist to reach near your left nipple. The original cloud had the inline controls far enough away from the headset that it could rest on your desk. Which was perfect because it was all of 2 inches from your hand if you needed to adjust something.

While some people may not like that, if they wanted to keep it up off the desk it would better if it was up closer to your collarbone. Its a more natural position to reach for. If you simply bend your arm at the elbow, like doing a curl, your hand comes to rest near your collarbone. It would be more comfortable to reach and wouldn't smack on your desk the second you take the headset off.

reviews.author: JohnB
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2026
reviews.content: I have only had these for a couple of weeks but they have been a tremendous asset. I am able to enjoy games sounds and music with the same fidelity I had from my THX 2.1 PC speakers. Base is strong and midrange is very distinct. The only drawback is that they are a little heavy. The padding on the head band as well as ear pieces are very thick and comfortable and go a long way to making the headset comfortable. I was able to get these at 30% off and they are well worth every penny!

reviews.author: Phillip who cares
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content: For price quality overall usage including but not limited to the sound the materials the overall comfort level for extended usage and so forth avid game don’t mind spending for quality this for price is better then most 250$ headsets

reviews.author: The Avenues Exchange
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 22, 2026
reviews.content:
Here's my personal take on the HyperX Cloud Alpha headset, on my actual experience over the past 3 years.

Long-Term Durability
Honestly, the main selling point for me after all this time is how solid the HyperX Cloud Alpha is. The aluminum frame is genuinely tough and has survived a lot of daily abuse. I've been using it pretty much non-stop since I bought it back in 2023 for use at work at a Call Center and PC and console gaming, plus music and movies in my off time...
The only real sign of age is the material covering the cushions. After about three years, the leatherette on the top of the headband and the earcups started to peel and rip a bit. It looks a bit rough now, but the headset itself is still completely functional. It's a common issue with the material, but overall the build quality is excellent for the price, and replacement ear pads are a thing.
Comfort
When I first got it, comfort was amazing. The memory foam earcups felt plush and made a great seal, which helped with noise isolation. I could wear it for hours without any issues. Now that the earcups are worn down, though, my ears get kinda squished against the inside, and it gets uncomfortable during super long sessions. A simple fix is just to buy some new, sturdier earcups, which should solve the problem.
Sound & Features
I wouldn't call myself an audio snob, but the sound is seriously good for gaming. The dual-chamber drivers do a great job of separating sounds, so it's easy to hear subtle things like footsteps in games, which is a huge advantage. It also handles music surprisingly well, with a clean and dynamic response.
The features are simple and straightforward, which I like. The in-line volume control and mute on the braided cable is also super convenient for quick adjustments.
Final Verdict
All in all, I'm a big fan of the HyperX Cloud Alpha - Gaming Headset. It's not perfect—the earpads wear out and the mic is just okay—but its exceptional durability and great sound quality make it an incredible value and a reliable workhorse.

reviews.author: Oliver
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 4, 2026
reviews.content: I'm not a first rate person to ask about audio specifics, but these are miles better than the headphones I used before. The sound is so crisp and clear. The suspension headband is very comfortable. And the mic works pretty well too. I would most definitely recommend someone buy these if they're looking for some thing high quality and budget friendly.

reviews.author: Bob M.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 9, 2025
reviews.content: The over-the-ear feature is exactly what I was looking. The earpiece material is made of cloth, unlike faux leather which eventually peels off. That's a plus for me. I don't really use the microphone, except for one occasion, and there were no issues. The sound quality is very good for gaming, music, even a TV/Cable sound system. The volume is fine enough, as I usually put the headphones on max volume and then do additional adjustments through the device it's connected to. It's bendable and twistable, lightweight and adjustable for any size/shaped head, and I even accidentally sat on them once, with no damage at all. They were so comfortable that I even bought one for my wife.

reviews.author: Nick
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 11, 2025
reviews.content:
The sound is nearly indistinguishable from my AirPods Pro 2, which have great sound. These are missing some clarity, but if you can get these on sale they’re a great value. Also, they are very comfortable. Most comfortable headset/headphones I’ve ever used. The mesh over the drivers touches my ears, but it’s soft and I stopped noticing very quickly. I don’t use the mic so I can’t speak on that. The cable is nice. Thin and flexible.

So these aren’t the best of the best, but terrific value on sale. I recommend these if you want the most bang for your buck.

reviews.author: Obviousman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 20, 2026
reviews.content: I bought these on sale as a backup headset for my Sennheiser PC38X headphones. Well, the cable on my Sennheiser's stopped working, so while waiting on a new cable, I popped the Logitech's on. As a backup they work great. Sound is clear and you can hear what direction shots and enemies are coming from. Only issues I have is that they are a little tinny and bass lacking when compared to the Sennheiser's. The mic is very average too. Definitely wouldn't use these for listening to music or movies. If you can pick these up when they go on sale, you can't beat the value, making them an excellent backup option.

reviews.author: Alex
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.content: I bought these for my husband who is a gamer, and he really enjoys them. They’re affordable, and most sizes fit all. They work with most PC gaming units. They’re long lasting. They are soft and comfortable to use. They have decent cord length and great noise cancellation capabilities. The volume is good as well. They don’t store easily though. I think they are long lasting. My husband had this same pair before I bought this one for him and he said they lasted longer than 3 year.

reviews.author: carmen hall
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.content: I purchased these for my daughter to use for work. She is a Speech Pathologist and teaches children remotely. She said the sound quality and microphone are great and the headphones are comfortable and not too heavy.

reviews.author: Emanuel Jacques
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 23, 2025
reviews.content:
1 star because the audio cable and mic should be detachable. And the audio cable should at least be braided. They made it of a rubbery material that snags on damn near anything that bends it. Which causes stress on the connection on both ends and increases tears.

If every section was modular, down to the earpads, It would be a stellar five star. But otherwise, its really just a disposable headset with cotton earpads. Which is easy on the ears compared to yucky leathery plastic but doesnt seal air off to create noise cancellation. So the audio is just as cheap as it was fifty years ago.

At the price, this is basically something they want consumers replacing every 9 months. Because the cable and earpads will rip. And the mic will become flimsy and annoying. They clearly made the wireless version with a detachable mic. Which is very inexpensive and ridiculous to not include that in the wired one as well. Mind you, it was released the year before.

reviews.author: Chaly
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 21, 2024
reviews.content:
I write the bad things first so if you want to see the good things about these headphones you have to scroll down.

Ok first thing first, that was my biggest letdown about this headset it was too small for my head I can still use it but it doesn't really fit me. Second the microphone is too short with no way to adjust whenever I tried to talk with friends they would say they have a hard time hearing me or say i sound like I'm too far away from the Mic. Third I would've liked if the headset labeled which wire was which for the microphone and the headphones so I don't have to search up what color goes into which port. Lastly the aux port quality is kind of bad it's like the old school aux ports where you have to kind of fidget with it or else you won't be able to hear clearly with it. Other than that there isn't much other issues I have with this head set when it works it's actually pretty good compared to some other headphones it's lightweight I could literally wear it for 5-7 hours without my head hurting. The sound quality isn't that bad when you get it working (honestly think it might be better as a speaker than a headset) the microphone quality isn't too bad as when I'm hearing myself whenever I'm testing it or my voice goes through, my friends say I sound clear. Other than that it's not terrible, it's okay, not decent, for it's price tag I would get something else but on sale it's an okay pick.

reviews.author: Real person :3
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.content: great for the price, amazing sound quality on pc, but kinda mid on mobile via usbc(unless you fine tune the eq), it's usable with no software but to extract the best out of it you'll need the 7.1 surround software, my only gripe is it does fill kinda flimsy, but not the point where it'll break with normal use or anything, overall great product

reviews.author: Marianne
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 7, 2025
reviews.content:
Easy to use : yes
Functional: good
Connectivity good
Quality good
Clarity sound good
Value of money is here
Work well for pc and PS5
My husband is happy player PS5
Comfortable but I do not like the cushion headband too soft for adjusting on my ears , I have small head 😔 you need extra foam protector....

reviews.author: Jeff Chambers
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 3, 2026
reviews.content: Feels premium and sturdy, everything you need comes in the box. The mic and headphones work perfectly! Just plug in and you'll be able to use it, no hassle. Love the volume roller on the side and the mute button. Also love the way the mic can slide up into the headphones. Good job

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 10, 2026
reviews.content: Upgraded from an older razer headset and the sound quality improvement was quite noticeable. I like the retractable microphone is a nice extra feature. Visable light on the end of the microphone to show you if it is muted is a nice extra function.

reviews.author: Nathan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 29, 2025
reviews.content: It's a good headset, don't download the software or bother with it unless you want to change the RGB. The surround 7.1 isn't great it actively makes sound feel choppy and blocky if they makes any sense. It fit well and the connections are great. It also has a comfortable weight.

reviews.author: Critters
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 8, 2025
reviews.content:
This might sound unusual, but I didn’t actually buy these for gaming. I work in an office where we rely heavily on headsets for phone calls and virtual meetings. The standard-issue Plantronics headset never felt comfortable on my head. While the audio and mic quality were solid, I constantly got the cords caught on things around my desk—and ended up breaking three pairs because of it.

I needed something more comfortable and durable. I tried a few alternatives, including other Plantronics models and some from Logitech, but nothing really worked for me. Eventually, I decided to give these a shot. They looked big, sturdy, and well-built—and I figured if they’re good enough for gaming communication, why not try them in the office?

As it turns out, they’re perfect. Call quality is excellent—clients and coworkers hear me clearly—and the audio is so good I use them to listen to music and videos when I’m not on calls. I also appreciate the thick, durable cable. While I still occasionally snag the cord around my desk, this headset has held up without any issues so far.

If you’re looking for a comfortable, reliable headset for office use, I definitely recommend giving this one a try—even if it’s marketed for gamers.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.content: Worked great for about 3 days then just seemed to brick itself - no power to device so no sound or microphone. Tested on 4 devices with same affect of not working on any of them.

reviews.author: Lisa M. McMahon
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 26, 2025
reviews.content: This is my second pair of Razer Kraken headphones. On my first pair the covering on the ears completely dissolved and I couldn't find a suitable solution to fix this even though the headphones still worked. It looks like they have different material covering the ears and I hope this stands up better. The last pair did last for a long time so I expect these to last as long, if not longer. They are very comfortable and the sound is excellent. They use a USB connection, which I prefer over Bluetooth. No batteries required. The color changing on them is a nice touch even though I don't really need this.

reviews.author: Br35
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 8, 2025
reviews.content: Amazing detail and addictive sound that is so real it makes you feel you've upgraded your ears. I'm hearing sounds in songs I've never heard before. It makes everything else feel like a step down.

reviews.author: Alex W
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 29, 2025
reviews.content:
I love my Naim Uniti Atom HE + Focal Utopia (2022) almost as much as my children.

They enable you to experience music at an entirely new depth, with world-class dynamics and exceptional resolution and soundstage.

reviews.author: Keith S
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 17, 2026
reviews.content: Amazing sounding headphone but the build quality of the headband is not good for such a flagship headphone. It's okay when it's brand new but creaks very badly with age.

reviews.author: ED
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 30, 2024
reviews.content: You will not be sorry.

reviews.author: Matt H
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 27, 2025
reviews.content:
Pros: Definitely premium, eye-catching, maybe with the right amplifiers they would sound great.. The emphasis here is clarity: you'll hear things you never heard before in your music. They're powerful, capable, top-of-the-line headphones. This is the pinnacle of what headphones can do right now.

Cons: They're heavy, I often can't seem to get an adjustment that where they feel level and stable, they're extremely expensive, they're open-back, the there's something so harsh and piercing about the mid highs and treble that I suspected that they were damaged. If you're looking for "musical" headphones with thumping bass, good noise isolation, headphones that will greatly increase your enjoyment of music, these aren't it. These are more about absolute clarity. I made the mistake of equating audiophile with musical enjoyment and now I'm out $5k. I kind of knew it had a focus on clarity to the exclusion of other factors but I didn't truly appreciate what that meant. I don't like the look of these either. It's sort of an industrial, futuristic, alienware type vibe.

I'll keep trying them from time to time and tweaking settings on my equipment. Possibly with the right amplifier they're as amazing as people say. Everyone says they're very source-revealing. I wish I had gone with Meze, Diana, or Focal's own Stellia, Azurys, or Bathys.

reviews.author: Timothy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 17, 2025
reviews.content: I have the 2020 model , I have to say this is the finest headphone I have EVER listened to. BUT keep in mind the purchase of this headphone NEEDS a high quality DAC, and a really good Amp. If you can't afford the premium equipment to this headphone buy a cheaper unit. When you pair this headphone with a good amp/Dac and push up the impedance you are rewarded with sonic magic .

reviews.author: María Hidalgo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 14, 2025
reviews.content: I bought those like couple weeks ago and unfortunately I had to return them today because at the beginning I didn't have any problems but after two weeks I started hearing statics and a very bad noise .. I usually wear them in the gym and wasn't able to heard my music ... I'm very disappointed because with sonic headphones after wearing the for 4 years did not have any problems... I guess after getting my money back I'm going to buy another sonic headphones...

reviews.author: ShanG....
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 23, 2023
reviews.content:
I use this for my work zoom meetings through out the day and also simultaneously connect to my cell phone as well. You domt need two different bluetooth as this connects to 2 devices simultaneously.

Noise cancellation works like a charm. The other side hear only your voice and not the back ground sounds. Its very comfortable on your ears for day long wearing.

Battery is very decent and i get little over 8.5 hours when i use all day.

Yoiu get excellent quality for a fraction of popular models cost.

I recommend this and its a great buy.

reviews.author: yogagal
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 2, 2023
reviews.content: This headset is a tremendous all-around asset to my everyday routine for working remotely, workouts, errands, and etc. I keep it around my neck so it is always handy (a bonus of this style) and use it frequently throughout the day. I have struggled to find a headset that works just as well for listening to music and audiobooks and making hands-free calls, and this one is perfect for both. After trying the G9, which is also a great headset, the seller offered the G3 to test. The headset arrived well-packaged with a thorough instructional booklet. I am equally as happy with this G3, which does have a couple of distinctions, depending on one's preferences. The G9 has a detachable mic and is a bit more flexible to easily stuff into a pocket or small phone purse, if those are your needs. The G3 is also light though a bit more substantial, feels very solid with an attached, rubberized mic. This headset has a very nice fit around the ears and a roomy wire band, with sizeable power, volume, and mic on/off buttons. I really appreciate the button and control features being on the larger side, as I can easily feel what I am pressing and push the right ones. No more accidentally hanging up on callers or trying to figure out how to pause audio or increase/decrease volume as I usually experience with ear pods, as the layout of these features is also quite logical and spaced well around the headset. Sound is great and the speakers sit comfortably on my ears, with the rubberized finish providing a nice, soft feel. Love this headset - highly recommend!

reviews.author: K. Albright
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 18, 2023
reviews.content:
I originally bought these for my jobs, both of which are primarily phone or telehealth-focused. I got the G3 it was very good: people told me they could hear me clearly with the microphone and it was a discreet enough model that it wasn't a big concern. It's comfortable, easy to use. I'd say it's a little bulky behind the ears (some people have compared it to Shokz in that way) but not uncomfortable, at least for me.

My two biggest cons are the fit (which I'm finding has much more to do with ME than the model; I've gone through a couple of headphones lately so it's a me problem) and the sound. While the sound is clear, the volume needs to be higher than I would like. I have a hearing problem in one of my ears and so I am trying to use headphones that will ensure I am not blasting sound into my ears constantly. So to have this require 30-60 on the volume meter on my computer isn't great...BUT because of the way the model is, it's sturdy enough for me to take off my bad ear to protect it, and that's very helpful.

It was definitely worth the purchase and so I use it primarily for quieter environments (like home) when the volume doesn't need to be so high. It's a great model, does the job well, and is inexpensive.

reviews.author: Art
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 29, 2023
reviews.content:
I am very impressed with the sound quality and clarity of the recording. Originally, I purchased this product for work and could hear the call perfectly without any distortion. In terms of comfort, the product sits well on my ears without any discomfort, but I had expected the product to wrap around my head. While that assumption was my fault, it did not have a significant impact. The headset's battery life is good, and I was able to use it during my entire work shift without any problems. Controls are easy to use and the headset itself is soft.

Sadly, I had to return the product since it was unable to connect to my iPhone 13 and Samsung G S22 via Bluetooth. Customer service says it's compatible, but I just couldn't get it to work after some troubleshooting. Although the product connected to my Samsung Tablet and work laptop, it wasn't enough.

reviews.author: Jay Honsey
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 20, 2025
reviews.content: Fragile, broke just behind the right ear. Weak point at the connection between the bow and the electronics.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 27, 2023
reviews.content:
I really like these headphones. They are different in that they sit in front of the ear, leaving an open ear to hear noise around you, so, obviously not meant for noise cancelling. They sound great! And are extremely affordable. The microphone sounds great (you have to be able to select the microphone input in whatever recording app you're using, so these didn't work with my simple camera/video app on my phone. But using a recording app that you can select the input was great! They do connect for calls with no problem) They seem to cancel out ambient noise from recording and phone/video calls. the microphone is very clear. These are my go to for video calls and recordings for my business (I record meditations and hypno therapy sessions)

Support is fantastic. I reached out because I couldn't connect the mic to my video app and they explained how to use them. They also offered to let me try another style for free. This was actually the second style which I loved more than the G9 (also great, but not as) and G2 (Ok but my least favorite).

reviews.author: Kyle S. Ioea
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 9, 2025
reviews.content: Sound quality is amazing... then the left ear piece broke off. No response from Asurion about replacement, so I glued it back together and limped onward... THEN, last night the right ear piece cracked apart and a wire pulled off. SO, I contacted Asurion through the link at Amazon site... They said contact Amazon vendor for replacement. So, I am angry and out a headset. They Crack easy, so buyer beware. Superglue saved me the first time, but I don't know where the orange wire goes.... sad panda.

reviews.author: old_school_hiphop73
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 4, 2024
reviews.content:
The G3 is a good headset for phone calls, virtual meetings, and other voice applications. I bought it along with the G2 model when they were being sold together at a discount. Again I am happy with both. The incoming and outgoing voice quality of the G3 is good and clear and even louder than the G2. Unlike earbuds which are unreliable and will randomly fall out of the ear, these style headsets have a band that fits behind the neck area and hooks over both ear lobes making them stay in place reliably and comfortably. The speakers and mic rest right below the wearers temples, allowing them to hear both the headset audio and while still being able to hear sound clearly from outside surroundings. Someone in person nearby will still be able to get your attention without tapping on your shoulder or having to excessively raise their voice. And with the G3 you will get even more talk time between charges due to it having a larger battery capacity. Again a good product for voice applications. I would also like to add that both devices both come well packaged and have very good full color manuals included.

Edit Update 8-1-24. I did not notice until today that the G3 model also has a tiny slider switch on the end of the mic boom. This switch allows the user to turn the mic off completely which is a bonus for the privacy minded user. The ability to physically turn the mic off with the slide switch is great when dealing with questionable apps such as Microsoft Teams which is notoriously known to un-mute Bluetooth mics at random during teams meetings despite not being granted permission. Great product.

reviews.author: Matthew Sandoval
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.content: They are incredibly lightweight, considering the price, but surprisingly offer excellent audio clarity and comfort. The USB stick works well. I've worn them to sleep, and they fit and work well. The mic quality is good, but it might cut out when talking to others on Discord. They are easy to use, and the color is just like the image shown.

reviews.author: Chris
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 8, 2025
reviews.content:
Why did you pick this product vs others?:
Price, quality, comfort. I think it's on sale now but I snagged a set a few years back for a similar price. I wear these every day, and am currently on my second pair. I work from home and play PC games after work so these bad boys are in constant use. They are great. I don't use the bluetooth but stick with the usb lightning connection- the range between the headset and USB is pretty impressive (maybe like 30-40 ft with some walls between me and the USB dock) which makes getting up for a glass of water during work calls or a bathroom break while gaming pretty easy and convienient. I recommend the black pair, as my first pair was the white and blue and the top piece got pretty dirty and isn't easy to clean. I will say, and keep in mind this was probably after 1000+ hours of use, the microphone changes somehow... It's hard to describe. It's almost like your surroundings get picked up by the microphone and some output comes through the speakers. This doesn't happen until after a long use, and only noticeable when the microphone is in use (i.e. Discord call, Xbox chat, etc.). The sound quality is pretty good and mic is alright too. I wear these things basically 60/70 hours a week, and they hold up still after 2.5 years. I think the battery's starting to go on the ones I have currently-- seems like it doesn't hold as much of a charge as it used to, but that and the aforementioned thing that happens with the mic are the only things I've had issues with.
I think these are probably the best headphones someone can get for everyday use-- be it video games, online browsing/watching, or for telecommuting-- in this price range. Honestly I think this even beats some of the head phones in the lower $100s price range. I rated this 4/5 stars before some retrospection while writing this review made me think 5/5. I think I'd give it a 4.8/5 if I could, but Amazon won't let me.

reviews.author: Triforce86
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 19, 2024
reviews.content:
Very light weight compared to other brands. It also supports use of the USB dongle and bluetooth which is convenient if you want to use it with two devices and not be disconnecting the receiver all the time. Battery life is decent too, but not sure if they last quite as long as advertised. You barely feel them as someone who can go a few hours on my PS4. The controls on the unit seem easier to memorize by feel compared to other brands. The mic also sounds good.

HOWEVER, I did find that they started to cut out at times. For example, playing Horizon Zero Dawn, they’d cut out when it started raining on the game as if it couldn’t handle it. I have also noticed that during dialogue with background sounds, it was hard to hear the talking. Lastly, the volume does not get super high. These issues could also be the PS4 usb audio too.

Another issue is using with the Nintendo Switch. The USB receiver did not work when the system was docked. I could only use it in bluetooth mode. Not a big deal but it’s kind of a disappointment that the best quality can’t be used on the system and it doesn’t support multiple pairings.

Overall, a decent headset in the price range, but I’d wait for a sale.

reviews.author: Isaiah
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 26, 2025
reviews.content:
I wasn’t sure what to expect since these are on the more affordable side for gaming headsets, but the Logitech G435 honestly surprised me in all the right ways. The first thing I noticed was how light they are. You can wear them for hours without feeling that usual heavy “headset fatigue,” which is a huge win for long gaming sessions.

Sound quality is really solid for the price. I use them for FPS games and I can hear footsteps and directional audio clearly.

The built-in mics were another pleasant surprise. They’re not studio-quality obviously, but my friends said I sound clear and natural, not muffled like some budget headsets. Plus, not having a big mic boom sticking out is a nice touch.

Battery life has been right around what Logitech claims. I get a couple of full days of use before needing to charge. The Lightspeed dongle connection is super responsive; no lag, no dropouts. Bluetooth works well too, so I can switch between my console, phone, and laptop without hassle.

If I had one complaint, it’s that the ear cushions aren’t the softest in the world. They’re fine, just not ultra-plush. But considering the comfort, weight, and overall performance, it’s a minor trade-off.

Overall, fantastic headset for the price. Lightweight, sounds great, and is aesthetically pleasing. Definitely worth grabbing if you want something reliable without spending a fortune.

reviews.author: George A.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 25, 2025
reviews.content: It's probably one of the lightest headsets you can get so you can wear this for hours theoretically depending on your physical characteristics. My head is a little larger and it's comfortable for at least an hour no problem. I usually take a break at that point so I don't really know more than that. The sound is great it's not something that will blow you away but compared to Google Pixel Buds it's just a slightly a step down, for 99% of people it's gonna sound fantastic I appreciate the bass as well. It's plastic so hopefully these will last for 5 years or more depends on usage and how you take care of them, The charge is pretty long lasting but not as long as some other headsets that I've used in the past It's USB-C so you could use any type of charger 5 Volt charger and you're good. I have a multiport charger that I charge my phone, my iPad so I plug this into it as well next to my desk and you could even have it plugged in charging and use it at the same time. I had no issues in Windows 11 it's Bluetooth, it connected once I searched for it, just read the manual on how to do it and you're good to go.

reviews.author: JR
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 10, 2026
reviews.content: Excellent headphones if you use Lightspeed! They will not sound great to you and you won't sound great to others if you use Bluetooth. For the price, or even a much larger price tag, they are really solid. Super lightweight, great range, good battery life.

reviews.author: niesie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 29, 2025
reviews.content: I have loved these headphones. I bought them in January 2025, and they were perfect- comfortable, great sound, didn't cut off anything spoken but does cut out background noise. They have been so useful. I don't use them every week; but I can use them several times in a week, then might not for a couple weeks. I use them for meetings, for recording video for my graduate-level school work, in interviews... Great... Until November 2025 when they quit working without explanation. The dongle makes connection- it will say it is connected, but no sound comes over. For the price, I guess it is worth getting, but have a backup on hand. I just think it should have lasted longer.

reviews.author: Dingus
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 30, 2022
reviews.content:
First use. Just cracked these bad boys open today after losing my beloved second pair of Logitech H600s. Haven't really gamed on them yet, but I watched a show and that was okay. The noise cancelling wasn't that great, because it had trouble with voices. I got a lot of white noise garbage 'slurry' sounds when a lot of sounds overlap with dialogue.

Then it's on to some music. For that, I of course listened to music I'm used to hearing (Earl Sweatshirt, the best rapper ever), so I know how it's *supposed* to sound (my other pair of headphones are flat/neutral studio monitors) These Logitechs exaggerate the bass, but it doesn't sound like it's "struggling" like a lot of cheaper headphones do. But a lot of the subtle nuance I expect is still there. The bass isn't muddy, either. It basically gives you the hint of what the studio probably sounded like, but without the intense resonance you'd get from a set of reference monitors that pick up everything.

Keep in mind, as I have, that these are marketed towards gaming. Not music, or movies. I'm personally going to use them for work. This is the BEST headset I can find for the money. Old Logitech H600's now retail for $70 and they are NOT worth that. They are going on some sites for $100. The reasons I compare them to these is because they are the only BUDGET headset on the market that is WIFI (not bluetooth), have TWO earbuds and not just one like a telemarketer's headset, cover the entire ear (the H600's don't but that's kinda a bonus, since in an office setting you need to be able to hear your surroundings - Boss might be coming!), and have a MUTE button, which for me is vital since I'm NEVER at my desk.

So many bad headsets out there without physical mute buttons and are bluetooth only and leave you beholden to whatever chip was soldered on your device's motherboard. Usually it will be a cheap, bottom-of-the-barrel chip that splits the audio so you can't even hear others talking while you are talking, so any noise that hits your mic will prevent you from hearing a conversation. It's really annoying.

Dedicated USB is usually found only on high-end sets and will prevent this. When I go in to work, I will test how it works in a conference. It can't be worse than using a bluetooth set, even a "high-end" one. I will try these with bluetooth and the dongle and see how it holds up, then update this review.

So far, this one is okay, but it does a little too much for my needs. I expect gaming to be good with them though, judging by the music tests so far.

Edit:
Editing this to add the things learned so far (~1 month in)
I've used these daily for the past month or so and they are worth every bit. The old H600s were prefect, and these are near that, if a big more complicated.

The Pros:
I sleep with these things and so far they've held up remarkably.

The bluetooth still sucks, but it's better than all other bluetooth headsets I've used, including $100+ units. These were half that much and sound way better than those other "under water" headsets. They are totally usable in a meeting. In fact, I forgot my USB dongle and had to use the bluetooth one day. It was fine. Other headsets were almost unusable.

Gaming was great. Basically work like headphones, and the sounds are as good as any other headset if not better.

The cons:
It will shut off/disconnect after a certain period of inactivity. I have to check my connectivity periodically so I don't miss calls.

There is an issue with them being too quiet, due to some "feature" that sets the sound output to 85%. I haven't quite explored this too much, but I think it is simple to "fix," an annoyance at worst.

Over-all I'm happy with my purchase. Not ideal, but then again, this market doesn't cater to people like me. Until something better comes along at this price point. I would recommend them for people who want to work, game and listen on one device and don't want to spend a bunch for features they don't need/want.

FYI - 2 years later, still sleeping with them on, still working with them, still gaming and watching with them. They don't stop. Logitech makes a great product.

reviews.author: Mario
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.format: Style: Without Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+
reviews.content:
I bought these AirPods and I am very satisfied with the purchase. The sound is clear and with very good quality, both for music and for calls. The connection is fast and stable, they easily link to the device and do not disconnect.

The battery lasts a long time and the charging case is very practical to take them anywhere there are times that it lasts up to two weeks and I use it for all my calls. In addition, they are comfortable to use even for several hours and do not fall easily. They are an excellent product for their quality, design and ease of use. I totally recommend them.

reviews.author: Dylan Manzanares
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 21, 2024
reviews.format: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+
reviews.content:
I never take the time to write reviews because why spend so much time on something that will ultimately have no effect on my life? Well in this case, these Airpods have met and exceeded every single one of my expectations and I figured that was worth writing about.

Important disclaimers:
- OF COURSE this is a subjective opinion, because that is all a review is. Take it with a grain of salt.
- Different products work better or worse for different people, it's just that simple.
- When talking about noise cancelling headphones, it's important to consider them for what they are, not what we think they SHOULD be.

THE FIT:
OKAY. I am someone who has very small ear cannals. I have spent years trying to find earbuds that fit my ears comfortably. Silicone in-ear tips have always been extremely uncomfortable for me. That being said, I did own the first gen Airpods Pro for a couple of years before buying these Airpods 4. I wanted earbuds that I could exercise with, use for commuting, and use for blocking out sound when I'm at school or on an airplane. The airpods pro fit the bill, but I could only wear them for short periods of time before they would start to seriously hurt. On top of that, they would always slowly come out of my ears. It didn't matter what size of eartips I used. My ears are just NOT made for in-ear silicone tips. BUT THESE AIRPODS 4!!! Holy smokes they are so comfortable. They never fall out. I can wear them for hours at a time and not even notice them. I haven't worn a pair of Airpods this comfortable since the gen 2 Airpods. The 3's didn't work for me either, but somehow apple really cooked up the ideal fit for these Airpods 4.

THE NOISE CANCELLATION:
Okay. Obviously, these are NOT going to have the same level of noise reduction as in-ear earbuds because there is no seal created by any silicone tips. If Airpods Pros and silicone tips have always worked for you, just buy those. However, considering that the noise cancellation in these Airpods is ALL driven by software, I have been extremely impressed with the results. It reduces the white noise of annoying sounds like public trains, airplanes, etc. very effectively. While voices and other louder sounds aren't cancelled completely, when music is playing through the earbuds these noises are quite enough to hardly notice. For me, that's enough. I don't need complete silence, I have my over-ear noise cancelling headphones for that.

THE SOUND QUALITY:
When I say that I was blown away the first time I listened to music on these Airpods, I was NOT kidding. The amount of bass that they managed to pack in to such small, open-eared earbuds is frankly impressive. The sound profile allows for an incredible listening experience no matter what genre of music you listen to (I listen to a wide range of pop, folk, indie, and rap primarily).

THE OVERALL VERDICT:
These Airpods 4 with Active Noise Cancellation are exactly as I expected them to be. They fit extremely well, the ANC is very acceptable for what they are, and they sound amazing. That's not to mention the TINY case that they come in, making them so easy to slip anywhere. I have been looking for a solid pair of earbuds with ANC that work with my ears for the longest time now. I'm happy to say that these are everything I've ever wanted. If you have had issues with silicone ear-tip earbuds fitting you properly, then these are definitely worth a shot!

reviews.author: Jeff Stokes
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.format: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+
reviews.content:
Picked these up for my wife, and they’ve been a hit. Setup was seamless with her Apple devices, and they paired quickly without any hassle. The sound quality is clear and balanced, with strong voice clarity for calls and good overall depth for music and podcasts.

She especially appreciates the Active Noise Cancellation, which noticeably reduces background noise without making everything feel unnatural. They’re comfortable enough for extended wear and stay in place well during normal daily activity. Battery life has also been solid for everyday use.

There haven’t been any complaints so far. For someone already in the Apple ecosystem who wants reliable performance and effective noise control, these have been an excellent choice.

reviews.author: Ruslan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.format: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+
reviews.content: These AirPods 4 with Active Noise Cancellation are absolutely incredible! The sound quality is outstanding with rich bass and crystal clear highs. The ANC works phenomenally well - it blocks out almost all ambient noise. The Adaptive Audio feature automatically adjusts based on your environment which is brilliant. Battery life easily lasts all day. The fit is very comfortable for extended wear. Pairing with my iPhone was instant and seamless. Call quality is excellent with clear voice transmission. The spatial audio feature is mind-blowing for movies and music. These are the best wireless earbuds I've ever used. Worth every penny!

reviews.author: JBradford
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 10, 2026
reviews.format: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+
reviews.content: The AirPods 4 are very good but not great. The options including noise cancellation is terrific. The sound is very good but you know these are not full on cover the ears headphones, so keep that in its proper perspective. I would have rated them higher but I mainly use AirPods for running and walking. I found out fairly quickly while in the gym on treadmill that, in my case, the earbuds cannot hold in place while running. One fell out and popped out of view. Fortunately, I found it lying between a couple of treadmills that were a little further down the line. So, no harm and it survived, but that is the last time I will use them when I run, bike, or work in the yard. A dog walk and they should be fine. As they say, your mileage may vary. If you mainly want them for music, podcasts, or watching a movie on a laptop or AppleTV they would be a solid choice, especially with the noise cancellation. No great reason to buy these if you not in the Apple environment.

reviews.author: Brandon
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 26, 2025
reviews.format: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+
reviews.content:
Not noticeably louder than cheap earbuds, but sound quality is much fuller and clearer. Noise cancelling makes a big difference by stopping background noise from competing with music or podcasts. Audio is easier to focus on, especially for spoken content.

Pairing note (important for Android users):
• iPhones pair automatically
• For Android, tap and hold the front of the AirPods case until the light turns white
• White light means pairing mode
• This is not obvious and there is no visible button

Listening modes explained:
• Off mode lets outside sound in naturally, similar to basic earbuds
• Noise Cancellation blocks steady background noise very well, sometimes almost too quiet
• Transparency lets outside sounds and voices come through clearly with no noise reduction
• Adaptive mode balances both by reducing constant noise while still allowing important sounds through

Best feature:
• Adaptive mode works extremely well for treadmill walking and everyday use
• Cuts most fan and machine noise but still feels natural

Overall:
• These are not about volume, they are about clarity and control
• Podcasts and music sound cleaner and more focused
• Once you understand pairing and modes, they are easy to use
• Worth it on sale, especially for noise cancelling and Adaptive mode

reviews.author: Mary
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.format: Style: Without Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+
reviews.content: Small but great quality! Can be well hidden under your hair, so good for sneaking your airpods into class or work lol. Battery span in one use is very good too! My old airpod pros would last maybe 6 hours out of my 7 hour shift and then completely drain my fully charged case. The call quality sounded so dog poop as well. You'd think these would've worse since they're cheaper, but with a fully charged case, these lasted me all 7 hours with battery still left! The sound quality for calls is also very good! I definitely recommend if you're looking for an 'affordable' pair of name brands. You could definitely get something way better for cheaper, but if you care about name brands or stuff like that, these are a good fit.

reviews.author: Renesha
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.format: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+
reviews.content: Amazing sound. I do not have to worry about turning down the volume. The sound is clear enough to recognize if someone is talking directly to you or just to others around you. Has multiple choices for how you want to hear your surroundings. Lightweight. It doesn't fall out of my ear when I am talking or eating. Have not dropped them dont plan on it. It connects so well to all my Apple devices. Biggest bang for your buck. So far sturdy and durable for a mom of 3.

reviews.author: Happy Reader!
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 11, 2023
reviews.content:
For the price these are excellent headphones and a worthy candidate for "daily driver." For me, these are computer/desk headphones. That is my use and that is probably how you should plan on using them as well. The cushions are very soft but the fit itself is on the firm side. Because the cushions are soft, they are nice and comfortable. Because the cushions are soft I'd be hesitant about getting too much sweat in them at the gym. They won't fall off-- that's not the issue-- but I'm guessing you don't want to get the cushions wet. Are they durable? They sit at my desk. I thought about stuffing them in a "getting too full" backpack for a flight recently and I declined to do so. If you are really going to be putting them into very full bags maybe see if you can handle a pair in the store before making a decision. On the desk? As long as you aren't yanking these things off you should be fine.

Sound review? This is hard because there is so much that goes into it. I'll give it a shot. I can't talk about leakage because I'm not usually around people when these are on. There is no noise cancelling but the fit and the pads do an okay job of getting rid of a lot of background noise. These replaced some Koss Porta-Pros and some old Sol headphones as my main laptop headphones. (That's where I do most of my work.) So yes, I'm running off a laptop. No fancy power sources. They can get nice and loud and not bring in any distortion. That's good. I guess these are probably "somewhat warm" sounding headphones. I listen to some vaporwave and a lot of metal (black/thrash and such) so I think I cover the need to hear all levels and ranges of sound and don't have a need to focus on one aspect over the other but I guess the elephant in the room is always bass? It handles bass pretty well. It isn't an overly aggressive bass sound but you can hear it nicely and distinctly. There is some punch to the bass but, to be sure, it isn't at the level of beats headphones (and I like beats, but they are also a lot more money). Listening to some of the raw metal recordings, the bass is nice and it handles the distortion and rawness of metal tracks very well. So, the bass is good to the extent that the headphones generally maintain a consistent and clear sound-profile. And there is clearly more bass than more clear or neutral sounding headphones. It isn't necessarily a bass heavy headphone but it is definitely bass capable. I can make the volume louder or softer without introducing any more distortion and I don't have to listen to it loudly to "hear" the music.

These are very good headphones for the price. The four stars is because the headphone market is brutal and these are four star headphones, especially for the price. They are not magic value headphones that you would/could pay +$150 for, or something like that. That is, in my mind, going in to five star territory. For the price you will probably pay for these on this site, you are getting a really great pair of daily drivers and they are easy to recommend. I'm considering getting the (a) blue-tooth version of these.

reviews.author: Mom of 4
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 19, 2025
reviews.content:
I originally bought a pair of these 7 or 8 years ago and loved that pair, then replaced it when the ear covers were worrn and the replacement was also great. Now, I bought another to replace the one that now has worn out ear covers.
The sound quality is excellent and I don't worry about pairing since it is corded. The headset is lightweight and very comfortable. I use this when travelling on planes and clearly can hear music without being annoyed by other sounds. I wouldn't call it noise cancelling as much as noise buffering. It is much less and you don't focus on external noises, but you can still hear them very softly.

The ear covers will wear over 3 years, but the sound quality is still great! Excellent headphones for the price.

reviews.author: Beccaboo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 14, 2025
reviews.content: These are seriously the best on ear headphones. They are so flipping comfortable. I also like that they don’t completely surround my eat(that gets hot hot) and I can still hear(slightly) the world around me. They are light weight, love the color options and the price can’t be beat. Plus the sound gets super loud and doesn’t crack out. Tbh they break before they go bad but that’s because I use them constantly. And I keep buying them because I love the sound quality.

reviews.author: Phyra
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 15, 2025
reviews.content:
I purchased the Skullcandy Riff On-Ear Wired Headphones for my 10-year-old son, who needed a reliable pair of headphones to help him focus on game days. These have been a fantastic solution for cutting through the noise and distractions from teammates and the competition.

Pros:
- Good Sound Quality: The audio is crisp and clear, making it easy for my son to immerse himself in his pre-game routine.

- Comfortable for Kids: Despite being on-ear, my son hasn’t complained about discomfort, even after wearing them for long periods of time.

- Durable Build: The headphones feel sturdy and have held up well under regular use by an active 10-year-old.

- Weight and Stylish: They’re lightweight and easy to carry, plus the design is sleek enough that my son loves wearing them.

Con:
- Flat Over-Ear Fit: The on-ear design sits flat against the ears. While my son hasn’t had any issues with discomfort, this could potentially bother users who are more sensitive to pressure over long periods.

Overall, the Skullcandy Riff headphones deliver excellent quality at a reasonable price. They’ve been a hit with my son, helping him stay focused and confident on game days. If you’re looking for a reliable and stylish pair of headphones for kids, these are a great choice.

reviews.author: Ayana Dickson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 6, 2026
reviews.content: Comfortable, love the sound, noise canceling. Second pair I've ordered only because we accidentally broke the first pair in half.

reviews.author: Terry Seifman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 24, 2025
reviews.content:
Here’s a review of Skullcandy **wired headphones** (over-ear or on-ear style), focusing on durability (lasting 3+ years), excellent sound profile, and best bang for your buck under \$20:

---

I got a pair of Skullcandy wired headphones a little over three years ago, paid less than \$20, and they’re still going strong. For something so cheap, they’ve held up way better than I expected. The headband spring is still tight, the ear pads haven’t completely flattened (though they softened a bit), the hinges haven’t broken, and the cable jack still works in both ears. If you treat them right—don’t wrap the cord too tight, avoid yanking the plug, keep them clean—they can easily go beyond three years of regular use.

Sound wise, they punch way above their price. The bass is warm and solid—not overpowering, but giving songs a fullness you don’t expect at this cost. Mids (vocals and guitars) are decent: you lose some polish compared to high-end models, but voices come through clear enough, even in busy tracks. The highs are honest—if not super crisp—but they add enough brightness that things don’t sound dull. They isolate reasonably well thanks to the ear pads; outside noise gets reduced a lot, so you can enjoy music or podcasts in noisy places.

What makes them an excellent bang for the buck: durability + sound + price. For under \$20, you get decent build materials, components that don’t fail quickly, and a sound profile that’s fun and usable. You’re not getting luxury leather or Bluetooth, but you do get actual wired reliability, no batteries to die, and fewer parts to fail. Compared with ultra-cheap models I’ve tried, these Skullcandy headphones still sound better and last longer.

If you want wired headphones that won’t break down after a few months, and still give you good sound quality without spending much, these Skullcandy wired headphones are a smart pick. Not perfect, but you get way more than you pay for.

reviews.author: sofia bracho
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.content: Best headphones. So comfy and good sound

reviews.author: B Randall
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 14, 2025
reviews.content: Honestly, I thought for a name brand, I would get something sturdier than I did. The fabric inside the headphones that hid the foam from view immediately started to displace and bunch up after a month. When one of the foam pieces just popped off entirely. i took out the bunched up material and was dealing with just having to look at the ugly foam all the time. But once that earpiece came off, it started coming off all the time. And it was so frustrating to have to stop and fix it before a run or a work session. Over time (mind you, less than a year), the volume buttons stopped working correctly, the wire on the top started to work its way out, and then today, 10 months after purchase, I dropped them from about 3 feet onto carpet and they snapped in half. Disappointed in the lack of durability in a big way. Won't be buying Skull Candy again.

reviews.author: Michael McNabb
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.content: These headphones work really well. These headphones are light, comfortable, and fitting. I'm not used to open back, however, it was quite refreshing hearing my own, and my wife's voice while gaming. But if you don't desire to hear any outside noise, just turn up the volume. The sound is extremely balanced. I would suggest running the headphones through an audio interface hub for that flat balanced crisp quality audio technica is known for. Note, I'm a pc gamer. I can't speak for console gaming. But very good headphones.

reviews.author: AHeins
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2024
reviews.content:
Was looking to replace my old headset (a Razer kraken tournament edition) as the fake leather had started shedding everywhere and it never really sounded good to begin with. When I bought my previous set I specifically needed both PS4 and PC compatibility for my job at the time and the kraken was a cheap option. My needs have evolved and I got a different job so now I spend more time at my desk at home. I wanted something that was comfortable to wear all day, built to last, had a microphone for meetings, and provided an interesting listening experience for my music which I usually play in the background while cranking through projects for work. This Audio-Technicha headset has more or less met the needs while surprising me in a few ways.

Comfort:
This headset is unbelievably lightweight so there is almost no fatigue from wearing it. The open back design improved the airflow and makes my ears not feel so sweaty or greasy after wearing it all day. They block almost no ambient sound, which is perfect because I wanted to be able to hear the fam. The cloth covered foam pads are pretty cozy and work with my glasses, but I personally miss the cooling gel from my old headset. Only real complaint is where the cable connects to the headphones, the connector is fairly long and stiff which makes it brush my shoulder and jostle the phones when I turn my head. I think most people will find it very comfortable, to the point you will forget you are wearing it after a while.
Material quality:
No fake leather to eventually crumble is a big plus, materials mostly seem like they will last. I think the pads, cable, and head strap are replaceable with parts from the company. The connector on the cable that goes into the headphones is a unique size (bigger than standard 3.5mm and smaller than a 1/4" instrument cable) so you probably have to buy replacement cords from them, limiting options. The connection initially seemed loose and the mic was cutting out frequently, but I eventually realized you really have to jam the cable into the headphones until there is a solid snap to seat the connector fully. Now the connection seems solid and unlikely to accidentally unplug. The plastic is a little cheap feeling, but the important bits are metal so I think it should hold up as long as I continue using it gently in the home office. I wouldn't take it outside or put it in a laptop bag. I think the trade off for the incredible lightness is that it feels fairly delicate.
Microphone:
After doing a few test calls and meetings, the mic works great for what I need. I have a low voice so I can sound distorted or unintelligible on some microphones, this one managed to mostly preserve the low timbre while also making my words understandable. It was directed well enough that the automatic noise filtering on Discord and Teams were able to cut any background noise without cutting my speech. It detaches, but is also light enough you don't have to and you can generally bend it out of your way when you don't want it. I dislike the cheap feel of the mute switch, but it works and I wasn't planning on using it. One note: It is a MUTE on/off switch so it needs to be set to off for the microphone to work which seemed backwards to me initially. The open back design is generally a really big plus in calls, as it makes my voice sound more natural to myself as I speak so I don't feel as awkward or the need to shout. On the flip side, I can hear people across the house so sometimes it can be distracting.
Music:
I enjoy deep listening to music, and these provide an interesting and different sound than anything I had owned previously. This is my first time trying an open back set of headphones and it is definitely something I found myself preferring. It really makes instruments feel more spaced out on tracks so it is easier to pick out the different parts. Most frequencies sound great, but I had to use EQ to lower the high end a bit. Before the adjustment, cymbals and sometimes higher vocals had a noticeably harsh hiss.
