Naturally they sound okay, since I wasn't expecting much, but they're being used almost exclusively for non-critical, background listening to music on my tablet while I'm doing other things (almost anything instead of watching the idiot box while my wife is in the same room, online and talking with her coworkers and clients and trying to concentrate on her new full-time, off-site job). So, since I haven't really been thinking about anything more than whether or not they reproduce music, they're okay. Not awful, not great, but okay. That's what I expected so that's pretty much what I got. I should have been more careful.

A couple of days ago my wife was out exercising before starting her work shift, so I took advantage of the opportunity to test-drive some new music I was thinking about purchasing. I needed to be more discerning in my listening and I needed to be online, so I moved over to my desk so I could use my souped-up PC and Klipsch Promedia 2.1 speaker system, a combination that reproduces music pretty well for me. I realized from the outset I couldn't expect to favorably compare my inexpensive Sony 'phones to that, but I was completely surprised even so. I'd never checked, so I never realized just how much of the music I'd been missing with the new headphones.

Now I started thinking about my headphones a bit more critically, the old and new. Yes, the old set was a bit long in the tooth; after all, it was quite a few years old and had seen a lot of use. Even so, I'd replaced it not because of a problem with the sound reproduction, but because the connector cable was wearing out and getting a bit ratty. Worse, it had developed a somewhat quirky connector that often required reseating in the jack to work. A pain in the butt, but when they worked, they still sounded quite good. I then remembered listening to music and thinking about just how great it sounded on a brand new laptop I used recently (with the old headset). At the time, I attributed the great sound pretty much entirely to the new sound chip in the costly new Dell, but maybe it had been more than just the laptop.

Time and past time for action! I pulled open the junk drawer in my desk and started digging around, movin' aside all the old serial cables, card readers, cabled mouses (mice?) and such until I found the cloth bag I'd stored my old headphones in when I got the new set. I disconnected the Sony's, set 'em aside and plugged in the old ones. Rats: same old problem. I reach up and jiggle the connector around this way and that, push it in a bit further and pull it out a skosh until it's just right and finally, sound! Wow. I noticed immediately that the music was much more detailed than it had been with the new headphones. I was clearly hearing vocals and instruments as distinct individual elements, instead of, or rather, in addition to the one musical whole produced by the entire band I heard with the Sony headphones. A very noticeable difference. So I listened carefully for awhile, unplugged the old set and tried the newer Sony set again for a couple of songs, then unplugged them again and reconnected the old headphones. There was absolutely no doubt about it -- the old no-name headset (only about $20 more expensive than the new Sony's) absolutely reproduced the music with more clarity and even a deeper low end.

Now what to do? Well, when I'm not paying close attention to the music, the Sony headphones and my Samsung tablet are fine, so I have those in the living room beside my easy chair (to use while my wife is working). If I want better sound while she's at work, now I have the option to move over to my desk and use my old headphones on my desktop. Not ideal maybe, but it works for me. I still have the problem with the connector on the old set every now and again, but unless and until it breaks down completely, it works.

Bottom line? These inexpensive Sony headphones work, they're not uncomfortable, they're not even close to being awful, so they may be perfect for listening to music in the background or other such offhand use where not too much attention will be paid to the details. Folks that aren't too picky about music or really don't care about all the subtle little nuances that better, more expensive headphones reproduce accurately should be happy with this inexpensive choice. Again, they're not awful, they're not great, but they are okay. On the other hand, for some being merely okay really might not be all that okay, so if that's you, or music in all its multifaceted, heavily nuanced glory is more your thing, it might be better to think about something better.

reviews.author: ronan johnston
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2023
reviews.content:
overall - 4.5 stars
my main complaint is the noise cancellation but looking past that these headphones are honestly amazing for only $10. they're great budget headphones for average use, but don't expect them to be anything fancy (i see a lot of people complaining about the lack of bass. the bass sound comes through perfectly and is still there, it's just not boosted.) they're definitely not crap quality headphones, these are probably the best headphones in their price range.

sound quality - 5 stars
i think a lot of personal preference goes into sound quality but as someone who's never had a super expensive pair of headphones i LOVE the sound quality in these. the bass comes through well (it's not anything boosted though, but i don't mind as i didn't expect it), plus it really does sound like a small stereo next to your ear. the quality still holds strong at loud volumes as well. in songs where they split parts of the audio between two ears i can make out both parts clearly. vocals also come through beautifully!

noise cancellation - 3 stars
i listen to music pretty loud so this may be mostly personal preference but the noise cancellation is pretty bad lol, though they're not over-the-ear so i somewhat expected this. when i take off my headphones in a quiet room with my computer volume halfway up, i can still hear my music clearly. despite this i don't think it'd bother another person across the room, maybe someone closer to you though. when i have the volume all the way up in a quiet room and i take my headphones off, the music is perfectly clear and sounds like its coming from a phone at a low volume.

fit - 5 stars
I am a 14 year old boy, these headphones fit my head perfectly with no discomfort. when i turn or shake my head lightly they don't slide around, but when i lean my head forward they start to fall. they surprisingly fit my ears very well though! i can wear them for an extended period of time without my ears starting to hurt. the foam on both speakers is comfortable as well.

quality - 4 stars
they seem kinda flimsy but that's what i'd expect with $10 headphones, especially the part that connects the two speakers. the foam over the speakers looks well made, and it's firmly attached to the plastic on the speakers. the wire also looks sturdy, it's a third the thickness of my pinky (again, 14 year old boy). the headphones fold up so that they're flat, and you can also wrap up the wire well for storage as it doesn't get tangled easily. the headphone jack looks sturdy and not easy to damage.

look and feel - 5 STARS!!! 10 IF I COULD
by far the most aesthetically appealing headphones I've worn or owned. they don't look clunky on my head or around my neck. they are light and don't weigh down my head. plus the speakers are flat so they're perfect for decorating with stickers and paint and such (something i plan to do) they are made with soft foam and a matte feeling plastic, apart from the outside of the speakers which is a more smooth and slightly reflective plastic (perfect for stickers lol)

headphone jack/plugin - 5 stars
its a standard 3.5mm jack, so this means it's not directly compatible with most newer phones (anything above an iPhone 6, anything above a Pixel XL, and most new androids made past 2018). despite this it works with pretty much all computers and gaming consoles (works with my nintendo switch fine). and even if these headphones don't plug directly into your phone, you can buy headphone adapters for your specific phone model.

lifetime - unrated
i have yet to use these headphones for a while, but from what i see from other reviews these can last 2 years if you're careful with them which is great. they also seem like they'd survive a while from the quality, but I've seen mixed opinions about how long each speaker lasts. despite all that i'm pretty sure these should last a while if you're mindful of how you handle them.

reviews.author: Udo Schuermann
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 8, 2023
reviews.content:
This review may be a bit lengthy, but I shall strive to give as comprehensive and hopefully useful a review as I can:

I bought a pair of these on impulse in early 2021 at a store, when I began an 80-day "workation" trip around the country. My first impression, after unpacking, was that they were flimsy and would likely break within days or weeks, but treating them gently, I have traveled with them literally for months in a pocket of my backpack, taken them out and put them back on a daily basis, and they are still in perfect shape after practically daily use for the past 2.5 years. Fearing recently that they might break some day no matter what, and I would never find another pair, I bought two more when I saw them here on Amazon. I like them that much!

They are light-weight and the ear muffs (not cups, they won't go around your ear, but sit lightly against) will fold inward into the bow by which they rest atop your head. This makes them compact for travel. If you do take them on the road, you might consider keeping the box in which they came, as it will protect them better against crushing, but as I said, I traveled with them many times, and they have held up admirably.

The headphones will adjust for your head size. From the top of the bow to the center line between the ear muffs, their height can be adjusted from about 5 inches (13cm) to 6.5 inches (17cm), which I imagine should fit just about anyone.

They are marked (L)eft and (R)ight, but also the ear muffs are very slightly angled so that you feel immediately if you have left and right reversed.

I will not pretend to be an audiophile, or to have investigated audio range or such, but from a purely objective standpoint, the audio quality seems well-balanced and makes my music enjoyable: I have another pair of head phones which sound a bit "tinny" by comparison. These Sony headphones sound full and reproduce audio quality which I find pleasing and eminently enjoyable.

I also use them for meetings: I listen to people talking, and rely on the laptop's built-in microphone to pick up my voice. This works well for me. Audio quality from people talking is excellent.

I've worn these headphones for near-three hour meetings, barely noticed that they were on my head, and not felt the need afterwards to rub my ears, meaning that I've found them quite comfortable for long-term wear.

The audio cable is permanently affixed (not removable), sturdy, and about 3.5 feet (1 meter) in length. It's enough for me to stand up from the laptop into which they're plugged, to stretch or reach for something on the bookshelf behind me, but it doesn't provide a great deal of range. If I ever felt this to be an actual limitation (and couldn't afford to remove the headphones for a moment), I might consider an audio extension cable.

All that said, these are extremely basic head phones, as reflected in the price. They have no volume control, no noise cancellation, no microphone, nor any "advanced" features at all. Plug them in, listen to music, and enjoy.

I feel that for this price especially, there is simply no better bang for the buck.

reviews.author: Xmetal
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 9, 2017
reviews.content:
June 2023: Well another pair ... one of the pair I bought in 2022... has failed completely on one side at least. This is the second pair of these "110's" (look at the end of the model number) that has done this in say a year to a year 1/2 at best. I may have gotten lucky but I had a pair of the "100's" (sound basically the same, discontinued, and they have a thicker insolation around the wire .. aka a thicker cable) that lasted me for about 10 YEARS. Under the same condition these last two pair that broke on me were subjected to. I am on the borderline of saying "I cant recommend these" anymore ... but I admit for the right price I may get another pair and just KNOW these will not last and if i get even a single year out of them, that will be great. It stinks and I am NOT happy but, the other options I see do not sound as good in this price range or sound as good but are made even cheaper. I want a pair that sonud decent enough when eq'ed (I do like my music) but aren't 100's of dollars since alot of times i am in the hot Florida heat and humidity. That pair that lasted 10 years at least I am sure I even got them in a unintential rain storm or two over that time. These 110;s dont seem to last. Even the pair of Sony "300/310s" I have in red have an EVEN THINNER cable.

Feb 2023: just noticed this yesterday but between that pair that seemed like it was tuned funny and another pair i use at my laptop (main computer i use alot) ... the earpads that NORMALLY flake to pieces in no time ... well in both pair they are a-ok and not even starting to flake off ... from an old pair that broke i still have some memory foam earpads that i can swap on anytime i want (they are more comfortable though the stock pads are decent enough too comfort wise) ... but credit where it's due on the not flaking to pieces issue

Jan 2023 Update for "Muddy" pair vs others EQ'd the same way ... well i have to go back and read my review ... I think i adjusted the mids somehow but i have been using this pair constantly and not only does the pair bought in Jan 2022 still work fine, the earpads which i expected to go by now, are still ok and also the sound issue ... EQ'ed thanks to Rockbox ... I dont notice any "issue" (granted my expectations of these NOT being audiophile quality sound but a good clear V-shape with decent mids .. not too back and not too forward) ... yeah i am ok with even that pair, now

Feb 2022 update to the newest Jan 2022 pair i mentioned sounding muddy. I have used them a few days and now i think I have the fix ... they need EQ help but they sound decent now, even if not "great". My fix was in Rockbox (Sansa mp3 players running Rockbox was always my main use-case) I left "Treble" and "base" alone and kept all of my prior sound settings I had for prior models sounding good. I kept the i want to say around the 1k and 2k EQ ranges that were at zero and i pulled them down a bit ... THEN I also (based off a youtube video suggestion on muddy sound) i pulled down the low end in the advanced EQ part, STILL leaving a boosted "base" in the individial "base" part .... hard to explain for those that dont know Rockbox but it's like an android EQ where you pull the low end down in the slider portion but also ENABLE a "base boost" toggle or something that an EQ app may have ... that sort of thing only you have more control over how much the "base boost" is "boosted".

Not all songs sound great but that could be the source file ....even the one i am thinking of doesn't make me go "wow .. that is not the way i am use to these headphones sounding on that track" .... I still would prefer not having to do this and having them tuned the way they use to be tuned (maybe i got a bad pair) ... but they are usuable now and i like them

Jan 2022 Update: I recent bought a "backup pair" this past December and just tested them today ... I do like vs the 2016/2017 versions of the "110's" the cable from the split down to the connect it seems thicker (like the "100's use to be") ... though what I dont like is ... the tuning ... I still have those old 110's and I 100% notice a clarity difference ... turns out I cant return these if i wanted to (not saying I would ... ) but doing an A-vs-B comparsion I totally hear a clarity difference ... these new ones sound balanced, do not get me wrong, but they sound a tad muddy vs the old pair ... and I totally admit i do a combo of a U or a V shaped EQ when using the Sony 100's or 110's ... these dont sound great un-EQ'ed .... .but ... this exact pair while they work sound muddier than any 100's or 110's I have had before ... if this was my own experience with the MDRXZ 100's or 110's ... I'd be considering other brands in the same price range
--------------------------------------------------
This review is based on my first impressions with the "110's" on a number of mp3 player, and a number of "100's" (the previous model https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B004WODP20/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1 ) that I have had and used over the years.

Pro - Sound Quality
So far I DO notice more bass in these new (my first pair of the "110's") pair of headphones and unlike prior experience with Sony (and other companies too ...) earbuds where the "new great model" .. didn't sound as good as the prior ones did ...

These 110's just add enough to the bass and possibly treble too, that makes it definitely sound better then the 100's though it doesn't fall into the trap I described above, when sometimes they add "more bass" and it turns out the bass is so strong, that it drowns everything else out. I have to say that this is not really a knock on the 100's .. I do still like them but for the same price on either ... I'd go with the 110's but if there is a "good deal" on the 100's and I was getting them again ... I am not against getting another pair of the 100's either.

Yes these are "cheap" headphones and probably not as good quality wise as a really expensive pair though I do recall some reviews saying they tried these along side a pair of "$200 Beats" and while the did say their friend's Beats did sound better ... they personally would rather get maybe two or three pair of these and still have say $150 left over ... meaning "they do not sound price-vs-quality of the sound" .. 'that much better'

Pro/Con - Build quality
These ARE plastic headphones of course, though I do not agree with those that say "oh its like they are from the Dollar store ... the plastic is horrible ..etc etc"... just if your going to buy a much more expensive pair of headphones ... your will probably get better quality too

The one downside that ... I admit I see in these, and are a problem in the pair of "the 100's" that was 100% caused by me ... AND EVEN THEN that exact pair, needed fixing but still sounds great ... I am using THEM while typing this review, is the part where the 100's and the 110's ear-cups move. I am not sure "how it could be made better" but that was the exact point of failure for my long-used pair (again 100% my fault for HOW the break,happened) .. note that if it wasn't for me they would still be fine and that is after 3+ years of HEAVY use and they still work ... so maybe it's fine for being able to fold (in both models I mean)

Pro - Fit
I am not sure if i can say one way or the other "I have a big head" (100% not a small head) but they fit fine .. even out of the box. Heck after that DIY type of repair I did on my broke pair .. .I had to flip them backwards and .. they for the most part still fit great ... these 110's are no different.

By the way I have glasses (regular and sunglasses used when wearing these) and just like the 100's, these are nice and comfy ... not too tight or too lose, if you ask me

??? - The cord
The cord looks different from the 100's I have and I like the cord ... basically I think some of the cords on others are way way too small and that always leads to the problem of one of the two sides going out for good ... The cord on the 110's looks a tad smaller diameter wise but really if that doesn't mean that issue I just described DOESN'T happen .. I do not really care... I just want the headphones to work for a long time ... if the cord is different but there are no issues ... this change is fine by me.

Plug - I will keep this review updated IF there is a change but I have no had these for long enough to comment on some of the reviews who keep having issues with the plug breaking ... NOT on this pair but my other pair of the 100's get plugged into and unplugged from stuff for over three years now and often too ... never had a problem .. but then again I cant comment on these. for now.

Summary

I would recommend these no question ... though if your expecting a super high end sound (aka are an "audiophile") you MAY BE disappointed though as someone who loves his music ... I think the sound with the right equalizer settings, on the device or program your using... I do LOVE the sound of these.

Update - After using a new pair of the "100's" ... aka the prior model

Really ... the 110's do have the slightly higher bass and that is not a bad thing either .... the new pair of the 100's I am using I actually thought were the 110's (I just forgot what headphones I was using)... THE POINT? ... they both have great bass and I think the pair that I use to wear daily (the broken pair that still works great.... just doesn't fit perfectly anymore)'s fit actually effects the bass ... that old pair I hold the earcups closer to my ears than how they "sit" now .. .and I hear alot more bass.

Between the 100s and 110's ... if the way each one of them fold doesn't matter to you for say fitting them into a caring case .... I recommend both 1000% (no typo) ... find the best deal ... if you can get one a little cheaper than the other ...cool ... there is more bass by a little bit on the 110's but do not think I am saying a new pair of the 100's are weak in that department.

reviews.author: Sunny Reader
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 29, 2025
reviews.content:
I noticed the sound quality is not the best, esp. the bass.
My pc has an equalizer so after adjusting it, it sounds decent.
Another thing is the headset does not fit snug against your ears, but good enough for the price.
As someone pointed out, just because it's Sony doesn't mean you're always getting the best quality product.
They have their own low end products and this is one of them to capture this segment of the consumer like me (cheap).
Of course their mid/higher end products are probably very good.

reviews.author: C. Norton
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 5, 2025
reviews.content: Nice, comfortable an a lot easier to use in that regard. Background noise largely stifled. Great for the elderly who do avoid having earplug-style aids, as with most modern headphones. Affordable too. Good money value.

reviews.author: Rose
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 12, 2025
reviews.content:
Comfortable and easy to wear for hours without my ears hurting. Sony has nailed the design for long term wear for all wireless Bluetooth earbuds I've owned. Does not fall out easily and fits ergonomically. I am confident I won't lose these easily. I love the large button pressed to control music, calls, etc. I enjoy that it's not touch-sensitive like Samsung earbuds, which have been falsely set off by my hair or beanie. My complaints: these buttons are not programmable: what type of madman want people to press 4 times to change volume up/down? I would also love a "Find Earbuds" function if they are connected to a device, but the owner isn't sure where they placed them.

Noise cancellation is non-existent, but you have ambient sound (listen to your surroundings) if you wish to have both music and some situational awareness. The case is *slim*, much slimmer than Sony's previous cases. Sound is very decent for the price. With the latest iterations of Bluetooth, these earbuds can be connected to 2 devices at a time, but they cannot play sound from both sources at once. All in all, I recommend if you need a decent and comfortable pair of earbuds with which to listen to music and audio for hours. Sometimes, I use them for more than 6 hours and I'm surprised the battery still goes strong. I've been using them for 5 months now, and like other Sony earbuds I've owned in the past, I expect their longevity to last years.

reviews.author: Carter Davis
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content: Bought these to replace my favorite earbuds of the past few years, the KZ SKS and was a little disappointed at first. That was until I tweaked the EQ in the SONY app, which these responded wonderfully to. The bass is very lacking out of the box, but it makes for balanced tone. I like a lot of bass and bright highs, so I moved the slider to 7 on bass and adjusted the others accordingly, with no loss in sound quality. The biggest change though, was the exceptional battery life and amazing passthrough mode that I did not expect to like as much as I do. It allows conversations to be carried out while on the phone or listening to music. Even though these are roughly double the price of my $30 KZs, I would actually say they're an even better value with the features and battery life. They're also super light and relatively small, so they won't fall out of your ears. For a 6mm driver (which is supposedly shared with some of their more expensive models), these have an excellent soundstage and really can thump. The ONLY drawback (if you want to call it that), is the buttons are not capacitive, and must be physically clicked. This can be helpful in preventing accidental presses though, which is a plus. Build quality is what you'd expect from Sony. Very good. Will update if these have any drawbacks in the future.

reviews.author: Hap
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2026
reviews.content: Very good earbuds! Long battery life and unparalleled sound. Comfortable fit and lightweight. Great price! Get a pair.

reviews.author: idontknowme
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 11, 2025
reviews.content:
I woke-up on a Friday and purchased these C510's (Sony uses terrible nomenclature) two minutes later. The discounted price seemed enticing to me. I never thought I would be a person that has seven sets of earbuds. But three years later, I've used nine or ten pairs (others broke or sent to RMA). Now, I'm somewhat consistently searching for my next daily pair(s). Considering my diverse set of earbuds, I like to think I have good reference points for users.

I've used 3 Sony WF-xM4's, 3 Sennheiser Momentum 3's, Nothing Ear Wireless Earbuds, Earfun Air Pro 4, OnePlus Buds 3. Finally, I now have the Sony C510's. The silicone tips worked as some of the best I've ever used. However, I changes to my OEM foam tips (revised version) from my XM4's. Everything improved! The sound isolation on these C510's is so good that for a day or two, I thought they had active isolation - especially since using the foam tips. But. that's actually for the next version ( I think the 710's).

I was able to do some comparison with every pair of my stock and except for my two-hundred dollars pair (or more when new), I prefer these for their all-around sound, wear and feature ability. The physical device is good to interface with and only ousted by Sennheiser and my XM4's. As for how they sound, I think the overall profile is definitely a more narrow sound stage of the XM4's. Voice and bass are most narrow, but can be somewhat compensated for in EQ. The EQ barley needs modification(s). I've attached my EQ setting, for example. The Clear Bass profile does a decent job of balancing the tone at lower frequencies. I had the 510's at full-volume and I had my standard assumptions confirmed. The distortion was appropriate, for the price. The quality of the sound is good. Not passing sixty-isn percent of volume will general have you safe from from the cost-cutting, dostortion-allowing components that give these earbuds a very fair price.

I have worn these for ten hours per day, three days now - including in bed - and have zero soreness in my ear canals. I have fully drained the batteries and can confirm eleven hours use with a few percent to spare.

I have one major gripe and if you have battery anxiety; you're not going to like this. The BMS seems to only update the capacity three or four times in the entire10+ hours during use. The app and my phone will show one-hundred percent for several hours and then drop to fifty percent capacity. There is literally three or four increments to the device updating remaining capacity. Getting three-ish hours on a twenty percent, "low battery" chime is certainly a confusing feeling. Here is an example of update capacity: 100% (four or five hours), fifty percent (three of four hours later), twenty percent (three or four hours later), twenty percent (to zero) for two hours. This is not exact, but a general replication of the insanity of the BMS (battery management system).

reviews.author: Dragon
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content: Good music sound quality. Listen to music and made a 45 minute phone call in 6.5 hours shift with one earbud and it only use 50 percent battery. Volume was at half. Was back to 100 percent by 20 minutes. The call quality wasn’t that great and apparently let in a lot of outside noise. But it got the job done. On iPhone I had to download an app to connect and to see battery life. The app does let you use an eq for the headphones. Annoyed with the fact I have to have an app but everything else is good. Didn’t irritate my ear at all. Didn’t have to adjust it during the shift. It’s a button press rather than touch which I like. I know with all electronics the battery life will dwindle. Only used it once and was very happy with the purchase.

reviews.author: Lori Whitty
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2026
reviews.content: These earbuds are the most comfortable in earbuds I've ever owned and are small enough to sleep in comfortably and yet they don't fall out. I like them so much that while they're on sale I bought a second pair and the case that they show that you can get to put this case into is a great accessory to consider! But the sound with these is amazing, there are several sound profiles for presets, but there's a way to go in and customize your own equalizer settings which I loved! And the color was perfect for me! I figured they'd be good for being a Sony brand but I didn't know I'd like them as much as I do. And wearing them 11 hours at a shot means sleeping with them overnight is not a problem without worrying about them running out of battery. The noise cancellation option is very good too. Would highly recommend these earbuds!

reviews.author: Todd Tran
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2025
reviews.content:
I purchased this set when there was a price drop (down to $59, at the time of purchase). It is now $48, at the time of this review. I am still within my return window, but it's still OK, and I have too much going on right now, I can't be bothered.

Off the top, there are 3 things I like about these buds.
- they are SUPER comfortable, the best I have tried. In the last 2 years, I have been on a mission to find a super pair. I have tried about 10-15 different products at this point, some expensive like Bose and some cheap like Reccon. These are by far the most comfortable. Even after an entire day of work (audio on and off through the day), they disappear and I forget they are there.
- the 'ambient mode' is also very good. I thinkntheybcall it aggmented reality where you can amplify some sounds (I don't known the criteria at the moment) and suppression mode. The suppression is very good also, as good as my Beats over-the-ear. Imagine, an in-ear bud that suppresses as well as a good over-the-ear headphone.
- the battery-life seems great. However, battery-life tends to degrade after some time, so we'll see how it goes.

Now for the bad
- there are only 2 buttons, one per side. You can't control the volume. I can't find how, even going through the instruction 'sheet'
- I think ear buds or ear phones that require an app is ridiculous. They should Just-work. I get it, you can customize it. I've had several buds that 'require an app', but I don't download the apps for any reason, but they still 'just work' properly with no app.
- related to the above points, you cannot customers these buds for volume control. There are 2 buttons. Ibuse them the way they came, which is left-button (ambient/mode), right button (what they call media control, which is only a 'play button'. No volume, no skip, nothing.
- out of all the buds I have tried, the volume on these buds are really low. You can max the volume on the phone, not on the bud, but the volume is super low. I am currently using 3 sets of buds and a set of headphones: SoundSourrce A1, a Chinese pair I liked with wireless Qi charging, this Sony bud and my Beats headphones (the oldest one I have).

Why? I use the Soundsource while sleeping. They are the second most comfortable and you cannot accidentally press the buttons. And most mornings, they are still in my ears, and I'm a tosser. I also use them in the shower. They seem to be the mist waterproof. They do eventually fall out whennwet.
The Chinese ones are the loudest, and I use them while driving. I use them for GPS notifications, not much phone calls or music. I wore them in the shower by accident once and one side cut out. It cane back after a 5 minute drip-dry. They have the 'ear hook' which work well, but they also get caught on the pillows and blankets so I consider myself lucky when I wake up with even one still in my ear. They are also 'touch sensitive', so you can change things, just by readjusting them or sleeping.
This set of Sony buds, I'm kind of just evaluating them as they are my newest pair. So far, they are not the ultimate buds, so I'm using multiple buds.
I use the Beats at work because they sound the best, good controls, but the cushions are too small, they hurt after an hour or so.

In the end, I am disappointed because for $59 (the price I paid), is outperformed by a Chinese brand (Sound source A1, AKA Anker), which I believe I paid about $50 for. I've had 2 of these. The first pair was actually better than the new pair, but the old pair's battery-life diminished. Unused them for about 2-3 years before in replaced them with a new pair of A1 (current pair, which I've had about 6 months.)

reviews.author: Andrea Starrett
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 1, 2026
reviews.content: I have really small ears and these don't hurt or annoy me at all. The sounds is good. The pairing is easy and fast. The charge last long. The price is right. No noice cancelling but who cares.

reviews.author: wowaddict
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.content: Exactly what i needed for watching tv. it cuts down on the noise and are snug but comfortable

reviews.author: KSmith
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 3, 2026
reviews.content:
More than 15 years ago, I bought a set of small over-the-ear Sennheiser headphones because I'm sensitive to having something on my head and hoped their flexible ear pads and head band would be comfortable; also, reviewers said they delivered great sound. Not only did they deliver all that, they proved their durability and they fold up small for travel.

When I wanted larger, ear-surrounding headphones, I went back to Sennheiser and bought these HD 280 PROs because I trusted that they'd also have great sound and be comfortable on my head. Again, all true. I love the padding on the top of the head band and the way the cups swivel to adjust naturally to my head so they sit comfortably over my ears. The first song I played through them was "Chain Chain Chains" sung by Aretha Franklin, and the sound quality was excellent, including the base.

I have a pacemaker so I can't use wireless devices close to my chest and needed wired headphones, but long, straight cords next to my head and hands do annoy me so I appreciate the curly cord. It looks quite thick and sturdy.

In response to the Nov. 2025 review titled "description wrong and misleading in referring to a "1/4" Jack Adaptor [sic]", there is actually a 1/8" jack on the headphones cord, but mine was shipped with the 1/4" jack adapter screwed on over it so it wasn't immediately visible. So yes, the 1/4" jack is indeed an adapter and the headphones come with the 1/8" jack that plugs into smart phones.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 15, 2025
reviews.content:
I'm writing this before being able to try the headphones. Seems to me that the description is at best faulty: It says "1/4" Jack Adaptor [sic] Included" - well, to me that means there is an adapter, and I assumed (bad assumption, I will admit) that it would be an adapter for adapting a 1/8" plug to a 1/4" jack as is common in proper audio equipment, if that is what the buyer is using. Turns out there is no "adapter" - only a 1/4" plug - which is why I can't use the headphones yet. (The adapter I have since ordered will be here soon, and I'll revisit this then.)

In defense of my understanding, I quote Google AI: In answer to my question "in audio equpment, can a plug be considered a "jack adapter" ", it replied "AI Overview No, a plug cannot be a "jack adapter" on its own; a plug is the male connector that inserts into a jack, which is the female connector. An adapter, however, is a separate device that can convert a plug from one size or type to another (e.g., a 3.5mm plug adapter for a 1/4-inch jack), but a single plug is not an adapter itself."

There 'tis! my understanding exactly.

Hope this description will get changed to read "1/4" plug"... ??? :-)

reviews.author: Antonio Merced Pabon
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 15, 2025
reviews.content: I have using sennhheiser head phones for 20 years! They have incredible sound

reviews.author: Zpheriel
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026
reviews.content: Accuracy and sound quality are strong a great pick if you’re looking for some simple studio headphones

reviews.author: Joy Agboola
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 28, 2025
reviews.content: Incredible, great sound and quality

reviews.author: Santiago Riaño
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content: Excelente sonido para mezcla y master

reviews.author: Nerio Fernandez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 14, 2026
reviews.content: tremendos audifonos, super profesionales

reviews.author: Thomas Lindsey
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 22, 2020
reviews.content:
While I do have wireless earbuds, I usually only use them for exercising just because always worrying about a charge is a task and I tend to overlook that. That said, I just wanted something that was wired that I could use at a moment's notice and have great sound with a very good price. These 100% do the trick. I'm not some kind of cork-sniffing obsessive compulsive audiophile, but I like to have great sound with a good balance of lows, highs, and mids when I'm using the audio player on my phone without having to EQ anything drastic. With these, I can run the EQ flat and still get reliably superior sound. If the audio is not good, it's more than likely coming from the sound file and not these (garbage in, garbage out).

These earbuds are extremely sturdy and well built, and the wiring is a bit more heavy duty than most wiring that you'd see on any other earbuds. The controls work well and I have had zero issues or complaints. I really like the mesh bag and tin (like an Altoids can) that they came in. I keep them in this, as I tend to baby the stuff that I have to keep up their lifespans, so this is very handy. I did change out the cushion earbuds themselves and put on triple flange ones that I had ordered for another pair a while back for other (now deceased) ear buds. I just favor those.

I'm leaving this review as my son came to me and said he needs new wired earbuds as he destroyed his last (crappy) pair. If he ruins these, there are not other earbuds on earth that might be suitable for him. If that's not enough of an endorsement that I'm buying a second pair with the success of my first, then I don't know what would be.

UPDATE (MAY 12, 2021)
I've bought 4 sets of these so far. The first pair for myself well over a year ago that I use every single day for meetings (yay, telework!) and education stuff and leisure phone time. They get plugged in and unplugged multiple times a day. They are still 100% good (I baby them, clean them, and keep them in the tin). My son has ruined 2 pairs (he ruins EVERYTHING, this is no surprise). My daughter has a pair one month old and uses them every day. She loves them!!

UPDATE (JULY 29, 2022)
I still have mine, and they still work as good as the day that I bough them. My kids have gone through theirs, as well as a few other pairs, but they don't take care of them, so........ The replacement ear buds that I use are here: https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B07R8RMY2C/ref=ppx_yo_dt_b_asin_title_o00_s02?ie=UTF8&psc=1
I still use them pretty much every day while at work for meetings and listening to streaming music and taking classes (and communicating through various webinar/teleworking platforms). Zero issues. I do take the ear buds pieces off daily to clean them under water and wipe down the wired earbuds. I also wrap them up and put them back in the little case the same way I found them. My son ended up getting Bluetooth earbuds, which suits him as there is less to destroy overall. My things last a VERY long time, due to how I treat them. If you are careless or a big slob, then it won't matter what you buy. Definitely got my money out of these!

reviews.author: John Doe
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2021
reviews.content:
Update 6/21/21: OK, after 4+ months of owning these they have started to fail. After several cord yanks, the headphones are causing my phone to do random things like skip forward or backward a song or two, stop the song or even close the app. Occasionally there is some crackling in the sound also. The wiring is obviously damaged and they are annoying enough now that I will be buying a new set of a different brand very soon. As I pointed out below in my original review, the previous pair I had took a beating with far more abuse than these for over 3 years before replacement and actually cost less. These sound great while they last but the build quality now is suspect. I don't actually see any physical damage on them and yet they are failing rapidly.

Original Review: I haven't owned this long but so far I can say it's a great value. The last ear bud set I bought on Amazon was a Symphonized wooden constructed pair with great reviews that was excellent also for the price. I thought that pair was going to be the best I could find for the price range but I decided to branch out and try another brand for comparison purpose. Well, I'm not disappointed. I feel like for the price, this pair is slightly better. The sound quality is great. This pair is not as bass heavy as the Symphonized pair but that's not a bad thing. All the sounds that come through sound more accurate to the music and more natural. The music sounds very vibrant with a good mix of volume and clarity in all the sound ranges including bass. So far the build quality seems good too. The cord is a nice mesh style that is far less prone to getting tangled than the last pair I owned. And it's also easier to un-tangle when it does get a little looped up on itself. So far the cord has been yanked a couple times with no negative effect. Still fully functional, but I realize it hasn't been long enough to really judge fully. The Symphonized pair was VERY sturdy and took a heavy beating for 3.5 years before finally failing so there's quite a lot to live up to there. Overall this pair is nice looking, sounds great, is easy to work with and seems to have good build quality. Also I have made a few calls on my phone with it, and that has worked flawlessly. The mic seems good. The sound quality while in a call is excellent and the other person on the phone seems to have no issue hearing me clearly. The buttons on the cord are easy to use and work as advertised so far. The one thing I wish these had that the previous pair had, is a clip for clipping the cord in place on a shirt collar or other clothing. But I was able to remove the clip from my Symphonized pair and put it on these and it works great. No issue there. So to sum up, if you prefer buds with heavy bass that is maybe a little over emphasized (which some people do and it's totally legitimate as a preference and still sounds great) I would recommend another great quality set (like the Symphonized ones) but if you prefer more natural sound with excellent clarity that is balanced, you can't go wrong with these.

reviews.author: namco
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2023
reviews.content:
Update March 28 2023: the foam eartips have apparently sucked up my earwax, deformed, and are now rock hard. The foam started off super squishy and comfy and now its just a mess. They need to find another foam material to use that wont deform or harden from absorbing ear wax. Reduced by 1 star for this.

So I have been going through earbuds like crazy. I mean a lot of them. USUALLY when I am not trying out new buds, I will opt for the cheap sony earbuds knowing that I can have them in while sleeping and not hurt my left ear (laying my head on a pillow with buds in).

Opening the box, small, compact, nothing crazy. Had a had written note inside thanking me for supporting a "small business" which okay. Glad I could help? lmao. Anyway. I bought the black with orange accents. Feeling the cable, I like the rubberized coating. It feels good in your hands. I would assume if you were someone into sports, this rubber coating will be great considering if you get it sweaty you will probably still have grip thanks to it being rubber and not cloth. I wont be testing that because I am not capable (handicapped).

Anyway. The sound quality isn't BAD per say, but its obviously not perfect neither. When I sleep I use Binaural/Isochronic frequencies. Which generally means one frequency in one ear and another frequency in the other for Binaural, and both frequencies in both ears for Isochronic. SO FAR, I have been focused mainly on Isochronic lately. AT LOW VOLUMES like when studying, the headphones don't like it. The tones used at 70hz and 72hz which gives you a 2hz Isochronic effect. Or I will do 63hz and 70hz for 7hz Iso which is great for imagination and visualization. Anyway, at low volumes these headphones don't like having pure frequencies playing. You can hear something inside clapping like how a speaker cone will flap. Otherwise for music/tv/anime you don't have any issues at any volume.

Now I just so happened to buy the Logitech G333 at the same time. Similar pricing. I will say you can tell the difference in sound profile. The G333 has more bass as it has a dual driver design. So the low end sounds WAY better in everything compared to these. However, the LUDOS here have way better upper end in terms of treble. So its sharper and thus slightly more clear sounding. The LUDOS have this weird special effect. Generally for typical "over the ear" headphones, you would have closed back and open back. Open back give you more special sound while closed back generally feel more enclosed making the sound feel closer to you. Most people don't care about this but figured I would mention it. The LUDOS somehow make you feel like it has special sound even though they are ear buds, but at the same time they are feel close. So you get this weird close but far sound from them. Its not bad, its just, unique. This is generally personal preference anyway. I don't mind it, some "audio snob" might care. Granted I don't think audio snobs are buying cheap headphones! LOL.

So far, I am happy with these. I ESPECIALLY love the colored foamy tips they come with. I haven't even tried to crack open the extra pack of tips because I just LOVE how comfy the foamy tips are. I personally think all brand should go with the foam like tips as LUDOS have. Honestly the BEST feature in my opinion.... Again I am happy with these, mainly because I can wear them when I sleep. Which is the main reason for my buying earbuds in the first place. When I am up and about, i generally where my snobby Sony MDR-Z7M2 cans which are just, next level..... Sadly the G333 cannot be worn when sleeping, they hurt my ears.... So LUDOS won my nighttime. NOW the question is, how long do they last. My sony earbuds generally last 6 months which isn't great. Granted sleeping with headphones is considered "abuse" at least in my mind. My last pair of earbuds were JVC memory foam earbuds which sadly only lasted from DEC 2022 when I bought them until January 2023 when I got these LUDOS to replace them. So yeah the JVC couldn't handle the stress of my nighttime routine (mainly the drivers failing due to pure frequency being played all night long and not actual content like music/etc).

I hope LUDOS get some frequency reading gear for output, so they can test their earbuds and find the frequency curve. Everything used to make a headphone will effect its sound in some way. Sometimes minor, sometimes major. The housing material (plastic, metal, composite, etc) to the shape of said housing and even the shape inside where the sound bounces around before it gets to your ear, to the driver being used, the cable thickness and length, can all effect the sound. Going off my own ears and knowing what neutral headphones should sound like, these are definitely not a flat response. However, I will mention again the upper end, the treble range, sounds extremely good. Very clear and sharp. For those that prefer that kind of sound profile. If you want more bass and less top end, the Logitech G333 would be a better alternative (1:1 comparisons without using an equalizer, obviously you could in theory use an equalizer to add bass to the LUDOS and make them sound more full on the bottom end). OTHERWISE, these have been great SO FAR for me.

reviews.author: Quinniece Wilson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 22, 2020
reviews.content:
EDIT 2 [OCT. 8, 2020]
After getting in contact with customer service, they were very amicable and understanding, offering to send a replacement for the defective product. The replacement pair was back on par with the standard I came to expect from Ludos, and works properly! I can go back to recommending them in good faith knowing that customer service is reasonable when it comes to these kinds of issues.

___________________________
EDIT:
So this brand had pretty much become my go to. Bought the headphones a couple times, and they were great! Lasted a decent time, good sound, the whole shebang. However, my most recent order was a total dud. The volume controls didn't work properly as they should. The sound would sometimes fade out in one ear if you moved the cord a certain way. Just a big let down. Which sucks, because I really liked this brand. Can't really keep recommending them with a glowing review if they send out duds, though. EDIT 2: It's even worse than I thought. Just tried to take a class with these bad boys...the mic isn't even functional. I'm gonna have to drop it another star. I sincerely hope this isn't indicative of a drop in quality. I really used to see this as a reliable brand...but a pair of headphones with this many problems on day one? I'm just...so disappointed.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
These are definitely a solid pair of earbuds! It's always a struggle finding a good pair based on reviews, and the old brand that I used to buy from has steadily declined in quality. However, these are absolutely amazing! Nice clear sound, no tinny sound--they get nice and loud and even do a good job at noise cancellation. I definitely haven't found anything that can best these at this price. Nice to have found a new go to.

reviews.author: redfuddman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 26, 2025
reviews.content:
Good sound, multiple sized buds included. Much better than the crap wired earbuds you get on some flights. Way more comfortable than apple earbuds to me. I bought an adaptor to use with iPhone. Haven’t had them very long so I won’t talk about longevity. Much healthier than wireless from what I have been reading. Also keeps your phone away from your brain. Great value so far.
Update: lost my first pair and just repurchased same thing. The additional ear buds are round instead of the elongated ones shown on the product. I find them more comfortable, more external sound blocking(sizing) but strangely enough they make the earphones sound better to me. More bass and less tinny. I really like these. I hope whoever found my other ones enjoys them as well. 😆

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.content:
They're solid all round but not amazing.

Only issues I have.
There's a slight buzzing when there's no sound, and no mute button.
Honestly other than that not half bad for $20 I use them with the series x
Hope this helps someone. 👍

Edit: They lasted a month and right earphones went out and people started echoing through the earphones somehow... They were nice enough to reach out to me and give me a refund.

reviews.author: Destin P.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 27, 2026
reviews.content: Lovely so far! Much better than i thought they would be. Fits perfectly. didn't have to change out earbuds, sound is loud and bass is great as well. couldn't have asked for anything better! Got this after receiving the Hyperx Earbuds 2 that were sent broken and earbuds didn't fit well as well. If you are planning or looking for great earbuds, don't hesitate to get these you guys.

reviews.author: Zoë
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 28, 2025
reviews.content: I originally bought these so I could use them during online school. I liked the little storage container they came with. They sound good, people can understand me pretty well if using the mic. The only complaints I have is that they're not really soundproof, I can hear a faucet dripping from across the room or people talking in the background even with music playing. I can also hear a quiet hhhssshhhhh-ing sound while I'm using them? I'm not sure what that is or how to adjust my settings so I don't hear that. And then the silicone covers aren't working for me because they both really irritate my ears(I've never had an allergy to silicone?) and they slip out when my ears start to develop moisture. I picked the covers that fit my ears best, which were the smallest ones, the others were too big and hurt my ears.

reviews.author: Kiki
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content: These are actually really nice headphones, especially for being 30$. They’re super soft and comfy, and the old pair I had lasted two years of being thrown at a wall, so they’re definitely durable. Great sound quality too! They don’t actively noise cancel, but they naturally block out most noise! A full battery charge lasts more than five hours straight!

reviews.author: JAM
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content:
Super easy to pair to phone
Higher quality than I expected for the price
I only use in the gym for an hour twice a week and so far zero complaints. Note: I’m not a sound snob I just don’t want to have to listen to the music in the gym. I think the sound quality is decent.

reviews.author: Peedy Pie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 21, 2025
reviews.content:
I've had these for several months and I have no complaints about the quality, the sound, or the comfort. Sometimes there's an issue with connecting to my phone, but I'm not sure if this is a phone issue or headphone issue. I simply flip it back off and then back on and they connect at that point.

I did have a few issues with audio sync when I used them to watch videos though, but I read that's somewhat common with Bluetooth in general so I can't blame these headphones specifically. For the money I paid, I get everything I need out of having a wireless headset and I love how small they fold down so I can throw them into my bag.

There are also buttons on the side for picking up radio and other functions but I've never used those so I can't speak to them. I just use these for streaming music and they're comfortable to wear for a long time.

The battery life is also top notch. I don't charge these more than every other week, and I use them almost every day.

reviews.author: Cyrielle
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026
reviews.content: Very comfortable and easy to use. Sounds good when listening to music. I also like that they can be wired as well as wireless. Battery life lasts a long time. It also comes with a case which I really like that about it.

reviews.author: Verified Amazon Gold Member
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 3, 2026
reviews.content:
These are great and last a very long time. My first purchase of these headphones was 4 and 1/2 years ago so I purchased them again. On my old headphones, the left one recently lost about 75% of sound.

I have been using them with my TV, and they connect flawlessly every time. They are incredibly comfortable to wear for very long periods of time. The cushioned ear phones feel weightless. I've forgotten to turn them off a few times, and they're still on and working well over 24 hours later.

I've tried a lot of expensive headphones in the past, and I was surprised at the quality of sound and the bass of these headphones. Worth every penny and more. Should these need to be replaced in another 4 and 1/2 years, and they're still available for sale, I'll buy them again. :)

reviews.author: IzzysMom
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026
reviews.content: These are wonderful I bought these a year ago and they still work perfectly! The sound quality is good and I love the fact that I can put a sd card into it. It connects to my phone everytime too! For thirty dollar headphones I couldn’t be more happy with them. Oh and the battery life always surprises me. I was on a ten hour flight and they barely went down! Definitely recommend

reviews.author: JW
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content:
I guess there must be a huge price difference going from wired to wireless headphones, because despite being cheaper, my wired set feels very sturdy and comfortable--much more so than this Bluetooth headset.

The ear pads/cups are smaller, and the foam edge harder, than expected. Adjusting to the longest headband length helped to get them surrounding my ears instead of pressing directly onto them. The plastic feels lightweight/cheap, like I will break them if they bend too much in any direction. However, they do stay on, and I appreciate that they fold inward for storage. I also like the zippered case and the fact that I can choose to use them as wired or wireless.

Pairing them to my PC was easy, and the audio is clear. The biggest recommendation I can give for these is that so far, they work across the house. My previous wireless headphones couldn't handle it if the fridge was between me and my PC, and they would stutter if I walked into various rooms, putting more walls in between. These don't seem to have any problem with me walking all over the house, although if I crouch and put a fridge or freezer directly in between me and my computer, they do get choppy. But mostly they give consistent audio while I'm doing chores (so far, of course it's early days). This is exactly what I wanted in terms of functionality, so I will keep these earphones despite them feeling cheap and being a little less comfortable to wear than I hoped.

reviews.author: Rachel Ladensack
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026
reviews.content:
These headphones are great for tuning out distractions and really focusing on your music. The sound is clear and immersive, even at lower volumes, and the memory-foam earmuffs stay comfortable for long listening sessions.

I like that they work both wirelessly and with a cord, so you’re never stuck if the battery runs low. Bluetooth pairing is quick, the fit is flexible, and they fold up nicely for travel. Overall, they’re a comfortable, reliable option for everyday listening, commuting, or long trips.

reviews.author: OV Amazon's customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.content: I used these for work as I could not relay on bluetooth headphones as these get connection issues. Good sound and overall quality is okay.

reviews.author: Karen B
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.content: great quality especially for the price ! really comfortable and great sound and blocks out noise - perfect

reviews.author: Scott
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 4, 2025
reviews.content:
**DO NOT BUY!** Not only are the headphones terrible, but JBL's customer service is too. I purchased these headphones as a gift for my daughter, and within the first five minutes of using them, the cushioning fell off the headset. I tried to reattach it, but it was nearly impossible. Since I was outside the return window, I contacted JBL, and they sent me replacement cushions in a different color than the headset.

During that time, I was under the impression that I would receive a new headset, so I disposed of the broken ones. When just the cushions arrived, I emailed JBL to ask for the replacement headset, but they informed me that I had to return the broken unit, which I had already thrown away. Overall, this is poor customer service. Who would want replacement cushions in a different color from the rest of the headphones?

reviews.author: MD
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content: EXCELLENT SOUND

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 24, 2025
reviews.content: They fit well and are a great color. They could be a little more snug so the sound is louder, but its probably better They aren't. Great choice for people who don't want to do Bluetooth anymore.

reviews.author: Brian
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
reviews.content: Headphones work well. No issues.

reviews.author: Eric
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.content: excellent bluetooth headphones the price.

reviews.author: BlocParty
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2026
reviews.content:
Sounds good but they squeeze too tight. Never experienced that before. Then one day, maybe two months after I purchased them the cushion just came right off and couldn’t be reattached.
I wouldn’t recommend these because of these two issues. But the sound quality is quite good

reviews.author: Michael R.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026
reviews.content: Stay in the ear very well. The sound quality is what you’d expect from Skullcandy. They are super easy and fast so setup. The battery life is one of the best I’ve ever seen. I can listen to music nonstop on a 8 hour shift on a full charge and still listen more the next day. High quality and very durable, I’ve dropped them a couple of times.

reviews.author: Ellie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 2, 2024
reviews.content:
At this price point, I expect to be making tradeoffs on earbuds. The Dime 3's aren't perfect earbuds, but they're almost perfect for me. I have other earbuds that I really like, including ones that cost significantly more. But these are now my go-to everyday buds.

First off, they're extremely comfortable. This is a personal thing, of course, but these are the most comfortable earbuds that I own. I have narrow ear canals and the design of these is perfect. I think what makes the difference for me is that the nozzle where the silicone tips attach is oval rather than round and that works better for me. The buds are very small and lightweight and they stay securely in my ears. I can basically wear these forever without them becoming uncomfortable.

Related to that, they seal really well and I don't actually miss ANC much at all. I'm sure there are situations where I would want to go for my ANC buds, but in everyday use, these are sufficient. I spent a while a walking around the Mall of America on a very busy day with these earbuds and could listen to my music just fine without any ANC. Related to that, transparency mode also works well. When I turned it on at the Mall of America, I could carry on a conversation just fine and it really didn't feel like I was wearing earbuds, but it still took the edge off the noise. As someone with some noise sensitivity issues, this matters to me.

The big selling point for me is that the case attaches to my keychain. I always have earbuds in my bag, but sometimes I want to just grab my keys and my phone and go. With these earbuds, I can do that, and have them handy if I noise is getting to be too much for me, or I just want some tunes. The case is about the same footprint as my car fob and about twice as thick. It's a little bulky in my jeans pocket, but fine in a jacket pocket. On first inspecting things, I was a little worried about the lid popping open, but it hasn't happened. I've had these on my keychain for several weeks now with zero problems.

Of course, these are earbuds, so it matters how they sound, and they're good! They don't have the bells and whistles of more expensive earbuds, like custom EQ, but the sound is good enough right out of the box that I don't really care. I have better-sounding earbuds, but these still punch above their price point.

The Tile functionality was a selling point for me (because they're on my keychain with my house and car keys!) and I think it's not worth it. First, the sound the make is pretty quiet, so it's not useless, but only useful if you're in the same room. More importantly, using the Tile functionality drains the batteries. The battery capacity is fine, but not stellar, but if the batteries are drained after just a few days of non-use, it defeats the point of having the these earbuds on my keychain. As a result, I deleted the Tile app from my phone and reset the earbuds, so hopefully the battery will last longer.

Overall, the pros far outweigh the cons for me and I really love these earbuds. Not perfect for everybody, but I do recommend them if your needs are similar to mine.

reviews.author: Rakesh Shankar
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 17, 2025
reviews.content:
The Skullcandy Dime 3 Wireless Earbuds deliver surprisingly crisp audio for their size, earning praise from customers who feel they offer excellent sound quality at a fraction of the price of premium competitors. Many users highlight the earbuds’ noise-isolating fit, which comfortably adapts to smaller ears, making them ideal for extended listening without discomfort. The integrated lanyard and compact design add convenience, making these earbuds easy to carry anywhere.

However, experiences with battery life and overall functionality are mixed. Some users report impressive battery longevity and fast charging, enabling hours of uninterrupted playback. Conversely, others note that the battery drains quickly or that the earbuds unexpectedly stop working, which can be frustrating. Connection issues also appear sporadically, though most users find pairing with iPhone or Android devices straightforward.

Build quality feels solid for the price, but it may not satisfy those seeking ultra-durable earbuds. Overall, the Skullcandy Dime 3 earbuds are a strong choice for budget-conscious buyers prioritizing sound quality and comfort. Just be aware that battery performance may vary, and occasional connection hiccups could occur.

reviews.author: John D
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 24, 2024
reviews.content:
I've been using the Skullcandy Dime 3 In-Ear Wireless Earbuds for a few months now, and they have quickly become my go-to earbuds for both work and leisure. These earbuds offer an impressive combination of sound quality, convenience, and affordability that is hard to beat.

First and foremost, the sound quality of these earbuds is excellent. Despite their small size, they deliver a rich, balanced audio experience with clear highs, mids, and surprisingly punchy bass. Whether I'm listening to music, podcasts, or taking calls, the audio is crisp and immersive, making my listening experience enjoyable and engaging. Although I'll add, utilizing the right size silicone earbuds is an essential part of this experience.

The 20-hour battery life is another standout feature. With a full charge, these earbuds easily last me through a full day of use, including my commute, work hours, and evening workouts. The charging case is compact and portable, making it easy to recharge the earbuds on the go. This extended battery life means I don't have to worry about constantly recharging them, which is a huge convenience.

One of the things I love most about the Skullcandy Dime 3 earbuds is their compact design. They fit snugly and comfortably in my ears, and the lightweight design makes them perfect for long listening sessions without any discomfort. The included ear tips in different sizes ensure a secure fit, which is especially important during workouts or while running errands.

The built-in microphone works seamlessly with both my iPhone and Android devices, providing clear and reliable call quality. Bluetooth connectivity is quick and hassle-free, and the earbuds maintain a stable connection without any dropouts, even when I'm moving around. This makes them perfect for hands-free calls and voice commands, adding to their overall convenience.

Durability is another area where these earbuds excel. They feel sturdy and well-built, capable of withstanding daily wear and tear. The IPX4 rating adds an extra layer of protection against sweat and light rain, making them a reliable choice for workouts and outdoor activities.

In conclusion, the Skullcandy Dime 3 In-Ear Wireless Earbuds offer outstanding value for their price. With their excellent sound quality, long battery life, compact design, and reliable performance, they have quickly become an essential part of my daily routine. Whether you're looking for a budget-friendly option or a reliable pair of earbuds for everyday use, I highly recommend giving these a try. You won't be disappointed!

reviews.author: Fly
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 15, 2025
reviews.content:
The sound quality is insanely crisp with deep, punchy bass that makes music, podcasts, and calls sound incredible. The noise-isolating fit blocks out distractions so I can stay fully locked in whether I’m at the gym, working, or just chilling.
The 20-hour battery life is no joke — I’ve gone days without recharging, and they still keep pumping out crystal-clear audio. The Bluetooth connection is rock solid with zero dropouts, and the built-in mic makes calls sound clean and professional.
The compact design with the integrated lanyard is genius — I can clip them to my bag or wear them without worrying about losing them. Comfortable, stylish, and built for everyday life.
If you want wireless earbuds that deliver big performance in a small, affordable package… the Dime 3 is straight-up 🔥. Skullcandy nailed it again! 💯👏

reviews.author: AP
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 20, 2025
reviews.content:
Average headphones with quality that matches but does not exceed the $30 price tag.

The sound frequencies are very balanced. The bass is not over emphasized like most modern headphones, which might might sound flat to people accustomed to stronger bass. I see some reviews complaining that the bass is flat. I recommend looking up sound frequencies before purchasing headphones. It's like complaining about buying a PC with low RAM, then complaining it doesn't have enough RAM.

Fit is actually better than most in-ear headphones, and goes in just deep enough to be stable but not uncomfortable. They are very lightweight.

Volume is fine.

Battery is pretty bad. It will only last half of a work day, even at medium volume. When there's around 30 or 40 min of battery left, it will beep about every 10 minutes.

Connectivity range is pretty mediocre (not short, but not long either), however it matches the cheap price tag. It has 5.3 version Bluetooth. You can travel to an adjacent room and the connectivity will be fine, but might break up any farther.

The color is nice (I purchased the white version), it has not faded or yellowed at least after a few weeks of usage.

Overall, I see these as a direct upgrade one level above headphones you'd buy at a drug store. I use these as backup headphones and not main ones. However if you really want multi-device pairing headphones, Dime 3 can connect to 2 devices at once which may more than justify the price tag for you.

reviews.author: M
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2026
reviews.content: I've been a Skullcandy loyalist ever since I was a teenager. Listen, I know they're going to stop working in six months to a year because all Skullcandy earbuds do, but this brand is the most comfortable earphone brand I've ever worn. They're small and weigh basically nothing. They also hold a very long battery. All other brands have been very disappointing in both of these aspects. Also, they look cool as hell. 20/30$ is a fair price for these (depending on the sale). But again, be warned, I've been buying from Skullcandy for a while (both expensive and inexpensive earphones) and I can't speak highly about the longevity of their products, specifically wireless earbuds (wired will last you decades). So buy these if battery life, comfort, and style are your priority, but don't if you want something that'll last you years.

reviews.author: Clint I
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content:
Not bad for a budget type of earphones! These are small, durable, and portable to attach to keys or a bag. The Bluetooth connectivity is easy to use when pairing with a phone/computer. The sound quality is quite similar amongst any other pair of earphones but this has that extra bass. As for comfort and fitment, I would like to give it a solid 7/10. If you're using it while laying on the bed and watching reels/videos on the internet, these can be a burdensome and will hurt your ears.

I wish it had more colors but so far so good. I would like to rate these earphones a 9/10. Only one point off due to the comfortability of using them for an extended period. I would recommend these if you are going on a travel since the battery life on these are long lasting with 20 hours. If you got that airplane trip overseas, these will help your trip go by quicker.

reviews.author: James G. Riley
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 21, 2025
reviews.content:
Unbelievable value for under $20. These headphones sound excellent, connect easily, and even deliver effective noise cancelling. Durable, comfortable, and inexpensive enough to buy more than one pair - truly astounding for the price.

This is the third pair of these headphones I’ve bought - not as replacements, but so I can keep one in my office and avoid carrying the same pair back and forth from home. I also got a pair for my fiancée. The quality of these headphones is astonishing for the price - under $20 (this pair was even less, about $19).

The noise-cancelling feature is nearly unbelievable, but it works beautifully. The frequency range does seem to reach the advertised 20Hz–20,000Hz. The sound leans a bit colored in the midrange, but with a little EQ dip around 1k, they balance out nicely.

Although a 'generic' no-name brand, in Bluetooth setups they appear as “Pioneer 32,” and they connect quickly and reliably every time. Despite the low price, the build feels strong and durable. Honestly, I don’t know how this company makes a profit.

Bottom line: buy these headphones now. They sound excellent, they’re inexpensive enough to grab multiple pairs, and they save you a ton of cash compared to big-name brands. Absolutely astounding for the price.

reviews.author: Scorpgal
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content:
These have incredible active noise cancelling, they're comfortable, they have a long battery life, and they never fail to pair to my devices. The ANC works even when the headphones aren't paired with a device, which is essential if you're looking for QUIET, rather than just replacing your neighbor's noise with other noise that you've chosen.

However, they're huge - making them impossible to sleep with, unless you lay on your back & don't move around in your sleep. They also mess up my hair! I'd love to have earbuds that perform in a similar manner, but no such thing exists. Cheap earbuds from various brands don't have the promised ANC functionality, and the expensive pair of Bose QuietComfort earbuds I bought directly from Bose were defective & had to be returned.

reviews.author: Eric the K
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 8, 2025
reviews.content:
Although a decidedly generic product, I find the construction, comfort and battery life comparable to my Soundcore Q20 headphones. Since I use headphones only when flying and to watch TV to avoid disturbing my wife when she's sleeping or working, I can't give a very realistic assessment of the sound quality but I am satisfied with most other aspects of performance.

Bluetooth pairing (with TV, Roku, Fire Stick and laptop)
was a snap and the headphones reconnect quickly and consistently (unlike with the Soundcore which sometimes takes two or three tries). The earpieces fit snugly over my ears and provide very good noise isolation. The Active Noise Cancelling (ANC) feature suppresses outside sounds somewhat more but I can't say that it's strikingly greater than the passive effect of the ear cushions: Motor (vacuum) or engine (airplane --- jet and prop) sounds are diminished (30-60%?) but not eliminated.
I'm less satisfied with the "transparency" mode which allows some ambient sounds to pass through (particularly in the mid-range) but seems to actually amplify some of them. Wind noise is particularly apparent, as is the rustling of my own clothing as I move, to the point of annoyance.

I haven't determined the actual battery life yet but I've been using the headphones for an hour or two almost daily and haven't had to recharge in nearly three weeks.

I will note that the headphones came with a protective insert in just one ear cup (photo). Whether that was by design or omission, I can't tell but, overall, I'm quite satisfied with the purchase.

reviews.author: HappyBerry
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 17, 2025
reviews.content:
For about 20 bucks these are more than expected.
