reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 30, 2025
reviews.content:
These headphones are a solid buy for the price. The volume is impressively loud—much louder than the company-provided headset I was using. They were super easy to connect, and the range is great. I was able to walk around freely while on hold without losing connection.

I work 10-hour shifts, and the battery life held up the entire time without needing a charge, which really impressed me. The only downside is that they feel a bit snug on the ears and caused some discomfort after extended use. I ended up adding extra padding from an old headset, which helped a lot. I do wish the ear covers were a bit softer out of the box.

Overall, these are great for work calls and daily use—just be prepared to tweak them a little for comfort if you wear them all day.

reviews.author: Kach22
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 7, 2022
reviews.content:
How it works: Four buttons on right side control everything. On/Off, Volume Up/Down, Pause/Play etc.

Pros:
- These TeckNet TK-HS006 BT headphones are comfortable with memory foam softly padded ear muffs and headband.
- The padded muffs seal well and provide a fair amount of passive noise cancellation (this is not “active” noise cancellation circuitry)
- They have surprisingly good speaker fidelity with 3 EQ modes for Bass / Vocal / Pop.
- The current Bluetooth 5.0 has good range and connected quickly.
- The headphones can pair with two smart phones concurrently.
- Switching BT input signal is easy as stopping music playing on 1st device, & starting music on 2nd device.
- If using the Aux cord to connect to a device like a PC, the headphones act like a “dead” device, drawing power from the PC to work & turning BT and internal power “off” to save the battery.
- The multi-purpose buttons for volume Up/Down and Rewind/Fwd Pause/Play, All Phone Call activities worked without error.
- The Li-Ion 600 mAh / 3.7V battery arrived about 90% charged, topped off quickly and appears to honor the 36 hour of play-time suggested in the User Guide.
- The 10” USB-C charging cord is the new standard that’s smaller, carries higher voltage or data speed, and best of all is reversible (there’s no wrong way to insert it).
- The built-in microphone works well for accepting phone calls without removing the headset.
- Mine were carefully packaged, suggesting quality control was important.
- Came with a 3.5 mm audio/aux cable, and small User Guide
- Amazon had a $2 off coupon, making the price for quality an even better deal! (And as I write this, Amazon is currently offering 12% off plus a $6 coupon, making them an amazing bargain at $17 at the moment!)

Cons: None! Very nice quality for a low price.

Overall Evaluation: Comfortable, Good Sound, Good Price!

reviews.author: Fae Knight
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 13, 2025
reviews.content:
To start with, if you're looking at this to use with a Playstation 5, don't. PS5 doesn't support bluetooth audio devices, as I discovered today. First, the pros. It is a breeze to pair, turn on, and turn off. A voice clip plays when turned on, pairing, or turning off, so you know what's happening. It's comfortable, and from my limited testing the microphone works rather well. As for range, it was able to connect to my PC from the other side of the house, on a different floor.

Now for the cons, and they are many. The store page says it can connect to two different devices, but from my testing that's a lie. It can pair with one device at a time, and if that device is on then all attempts to pair will default to it. In addition the audio quality is not very good. It struggles at times to maintain a signal even when right next to the source device, frequently dropping sound for a second. The volume buttons adjust things in +/- 7% increments, which is fine I suppose. But anything less then 50% and you may have trouble making out what you're hearing due to the low quality speaker. Any higher then 50% is far too loud, but anything less then that is too quiet and tinny.

Would I recommend this headset? No, not really. If you need a cheap headset for your computer or phone, it'll work fine enough for that. And the price is rather good. But with the poor audio quality I have trouble recommending this product.

UPDATE: After using this headset for a bit over four months, I can honestly say you should not buy this. $30 was far too much for the product, and in truth it's only worth maybe half that. WHile the headset can CONNECT to a device from across the house, audio starts cutting out after stepping outside of the room, call it 4 or 5 feet away from the device. When trying to record, there is a constant ticking sound. And then today, March 1st 2026) the ticking became audible in the headset, followed by a high pitched screech before the headset died. It was not used very much during the 4 and a half months of owning the headset. Maybe a total of 30 hours of use per month.

Do Not Buy.

reviews.author: I bought this to use a usb plug and play microphone and headphones to use with garage band and the headphones play perfectly but the microphone audio sounds skipy and crackleing on my iPhone 11.. works not not the best at all for what I need it for
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 11, 2026
reviews.content:
Great product!! I use it all the time for calls, podcast, music and anytime I need to be hands free. Perfect for the long trips
I would definitely recommend for all CDL drivers for sure and for everyone in that matter

reviews.author: Mike Alexander
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 25, 2024
reviews.content: I bought these to replace a slightly more expensive headset that no longer worked. The previous set unfortunately had a micro-usb charge port and inevitably the pins got bent because it’s so frequently reconnected. This one has usb-c and I don’t have to deal with that micro horribleness. But the ear pad is small and not as comfortable. This one also has a long lag to power on and mate with bluetooth. The announcement voice is loud compared to the speaking volume and a little jarring. But it works and it’s cheap. The sound quality is acceptable if kind of “dry”. If you have an extra 10 or 15 bucks to spend, you can get something that feels nicer on your ear and works more smoothly.

reviews.author: E. Kraemer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 4, 2024
reviews.content: An inexpensive ["cheap"] product does not always mean a piece of junk. This Headset is simple to "pair" to your Android smart phone - I don't know about iPhones. The sound, what you hear and what the person on the other end hears, is very clear. The noise canceling, while not perfect, is acceptable. There is no futzing with the headset to activate it to answer calls or start one. I tested the Bluetooth range to well over 20 feet with zero problems in sound quality. The headset can be worn either right or left. Time on a single charge is impressive - my own test was several sessions lasting over six hours in total in cold weather [they say about 16 hours talk and 200 standby]. Using this in an indoor office environment should not be an issue. The headset is light, less than 5oz, and reasonably comfortable, but a noticeable presence after an hour or so - everything takes getting used to, and I was wearing a winter hat over the ear piece at the time, sooo. In short, definitely a keeper and not a waste of money for the use I needed it for.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 2, 2026
reviews.content: Great little headsets! I ordered two for our boys, for our travel, as they grew out of their little kid ones and they worked great! Long lasting charge and good sound! They had no issues while watching movies on the plane!

reviews.author: Anne Holmes
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.content: The best kids headphones I’ve purchased yet! Love that they are wireless and they hold a charge for a long time, we are talking days! Will buy more as my other kids headphones break!

reviews.author: Jess
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 23, 2026
reviews.content: These are the absolute best headphones I have gotten for my now 7 year old son. He's on the spectrum so headphones are his thing. We've tried sooo many and they either break, don't work well, or don't last. I just recently checked my purchase history and realized these headphones have lasted a year between 2 other purchases!! Now I'm here to purchase a 3rd. I also will add the replacements weren't because of them naturally breaking down but by being broken accidentally! Well worth it. Love these and so does he!

reviews.author: Reta Thatcher
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 28, 2025
reviews.content: It's very comfortable and you can hear the sounds very clearly no matter if you are watching something on your tablet or iPad or listening to music. It doesn't matter if it's for your kids but adults can wear them also.

reviews.author: Tiffany Huffman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026
reviews.content: These are great. They hold a charge well, the lights are cool (if a bit bright especially when dying, a little annoying), the sound is good and it keeps the noise volume down and I dont get too overstimulated for the volume of noise around me. My son loves these headphones and thinks they're awesome. The band is coming apart after about a year and hanging. My son is 6 but I keep them put up when they're not in use. The ear pieces have stayed in good shape and nothing else even looks worn on them except the one part where it extends and is coming apart.

reviews.author: #1 Ranked Top Reviewer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 24, 2025
reviews.content:
Going wireless for my kids headsets has been a game changer. No more tangled cords. No more replacing headphones because my kids somehow damaged the tip that plugs into their device.

My kids love not having to deal with a dangling cord. Makes it easier when we’re in the car or just around the house.

Yes, these are rechargeable but they last a long time. Also, the lighting feature is totally hokey and useless. Seems like a waste, so we just turn that off.

Bottom line - we should have made the switch a long time ago.

reviews.author: April Mckenzie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2026
reviews.content: Fits great for my 4 yr old grandbaby. He loves them.

reviews.author: Cody
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 6, 2026
reviews.content: My son is autistic and he loves these. The battery latest for 2-3 days with no charge needed.

reviews.author: Yuliana
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.format: Color: True BlackSize: One SizeStyle: Bundle
reviews.content:
I’m really impressed with these Skullcandy Hesh Evo headphones. The sound quality is clear with strong bass but still balanced enough for music, podcasts, and calls. They get loud without sounding distorted.

They’re very comfortable, even after wearing them for a few hours. The ear cushions are soft and the headband doesn’t feel tight. Pairing with Bluetooth was quick and easy, and the connection stays stable.

Battery life is excellent — I don’t have to charge them constantly, which is a big plus. For the price, the quality is definitely worth it.

I would recommend them if you’re looking for stylish, reliable, and affordable wireless headphones.

reviews.author: 4th Musketeer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 16, 2025
reviews.format: Color: True BlackSize: One SizeStyle: Bundle
reviews.content: I originally found these at Walmart a few years ago when I really needed a Bluetooth set of headphones. L-O-V-E-D them. Went back for another spare. Then went back to get one for my mom who tried mine out and loved them. By the time my cousin tried them out and decided that she wanted a pair for herself, Walmart was no longer carrying them. No prob - found on Amazon. Ended up buying a pair for another cousin + my dad for Christmas. I'm telling you - loooooooove these headphones! No cons - just pros!!!! Light weight, clear great sound quality, durable and can handle a few drops on different types of floors, survived a dog, soft and comfortable, fit over ears and head - great size. Connect well and easily to my phone, great battery life, plug is standard - ie. don't need specialized connecting cable to recharge. Also - recharge is pretty fast. Easy to use and ALL ages are giving thumbs up on these bad boys!!!

reviews.author: Elle
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2026
reviews.format: Color: True BlackSize: One SizeStyle: Bundle
reviews.content: Great sound. Over the ear is very comfortable. Bluetooth connect is super quick. Compatible to all my Bluetooth devices. Color is black.

reviews.author: Rin Hively
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 23, 2024
reviews.format: Color: True BlackSize: One SizeStyle: Bundle
reviews.content: These aren’t bad if you’re not paying full price for them. I got them on discount & I’m glad I did because they are not worth their full price. Absolutely not noise cancelling unless you have them turned all the way up. I would call them noise dampening, at best. I also received the incorrect charging cord. The headphones have a type c port & I received a micro usb cord. Which unfortunately is completely useless. Removing a star for those issues. That aside, these do have great sound. As others have said, they don’t get SUPER loud as other headphones I’ve had, but they get loud enough for me. I don’t need them turned up all the way. They’re extremely comfortable to wear and don’t hurt my head. Not super bulky with a sleek, low profile look and feel. Great battery life. I haven’t had any issues with the Bluetooth connectivity on my phone. Range has been fine so far. I’m able to walk all around my apartment without any issues. All in all, I would recommend these to someone looking for a decent pair of wireless headphones.

reviews.author: Steven V.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 20, 2026
reviews.format: Color: True BlackSize: One SizeStyle: Bundle
reviews.content: Just got them today, they sound great and are comfortable. Just so the information is straight... These do not have an external bass adjustment. I read ALOT of reviews on these prior to buying and ALOT of them referred to the bass control slider. That is a different model. I knew this when I bought them because I did some research on them 1st. It's rather irritating that the reviews get mixed together Amazon NEEDS to change this ! Also there is no app for these so any EQ adjustment made have to be done from whatever you connect them to. So far they sound great on my phone, tv & PS5. if you want something with more EQ control I'd look elsewhere. As far as the noise isolation, I'd compare them to wearing ear muffs and definitely block out background noise when your listening to something through them. They are comfortable I wore them for about 6 hours straight today and only had to readjust them a couple times. But that's due to me never using over the ear headphones before and honestly after a while it got a little uncomfortable. I'm sure I'll get used to wearing them. I love the 3.5 mm jack as my PS5 does not support Bluetooth headphones so I need to plug them in to the controller. I can't comment on the mic as I haven't used that feature yet. All and all is definitely recommend them for the price. I personally didn't want to pay more then double for the EQ adjustment feature.

reviews.author: Antonio

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 19, 2024
reviews.content:
Pros
- Looks cute.
- Easily folded and height adjusted.
- Lightweight construction.
- Works for general audio usage.

Cons
- Tight on a slightly larger head (Smaller heads won't have this issue.)
- Smaller than expected earmuffs (I have bigger ears along with my larger head, so, not super surprised.)
- The material isn't sensitive skin friendly (Sticks easily, especially in US summer heat.)
- The sound is slightly muffled, there's not a great amount of clarity. Not the best for games that require directional, clear audio like Overwatch and other PvP games.
- Not sure this product is worth the price, would place between $15 - $25 tops.

Potential Issues
- Didn't connect to my PC properly like any other Bluetooth headset. It would stay connected for a very short time, didn't produce any sound on any application and no matter the setting changes would not function until using the AUX cable.
- The lighting doesn't seem to function unless Bluetooth is active? This is a really odd issue that I am not sure if it's just my setup or if it's the product. So take the Bluetooth notes with a grain of salt, as everyone has a different setup.

Overall
I feel this is a decent headset for general use but not great for gamers and people who want an ease of use from a supposedly wireless headset with a potentially faulty Bluetooth connection. It has a specific look to it but honestly, I've seen better at and slightly higher price ranges from companies that tend to have a more known and respected name/business practices.

reviews.author: Alisher Damirbekov
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.content: Very cute and comfortable headphones! The LED cat ears look amazing, sound quality is good, and Bluetooth connects easily. Great value for the price. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Cuqui
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.content: I recently bought these headphones from my daughter and was impressed with the quality of it. The audio is better than any hearing aid I've had before I was surprised by how much I didn't want to take them off. The design is super nice

reviews.author: Joel M.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026
reviews.content:
Um, what? GTFO here. Kick your BOSE, your ROGs and your SONYs to the side. I'll address the mic later but woof, the 7.1 experience. I almost put 7.1 in quotes, but I think you get a true 7.1 out of these. With little equalizer interaction. I like to melt into my music. It's advertised as a gaming headset, but, the mic is a tertiary function of the headset. It really is about the sound quality.

I went straight dongle mode and it fleshed out perfectly. But of course you can BT with any device that supports Bluetooth. You can even connect Aux if you'd like. They even provide a threaded aux cord if you want to go straight into a monitor.

All the buttons are located on the left ear muff besides one. The right button controls the toggle between standard studio and 7.1 and your headphone RGBs. I won't give you the full directions that's what the pamphlet is for. If you are not using the mic you only need to know about 4 buttons/hard presses. Takes about 10 minutes of wear to figure out.

If you want to hear those footsteps before anyone else. Or the the heavy breath of someone rushing your spot...the rustle of trash being brushed aside...the door directly the your southwest. If you want pinpoint accuracy of that baddie, this ain't a bad option. Just remember to turn that 7.1 on before the match for maximum impact.

They are incredibly comfortable, I've been sick and tired of headphones that are leather or pleather oriented. They crack or peel without constant attention. This headset is lightweight; the cushioned thin cloth material is of very high quality. The bevels in the band are very sensitive and contour fantastically. You can tell a lot of attention to detail was paid to head comfortability. I wore these one night for 6 hours straight without even remembering to take them off because I was listening to a concert. I FORGET they were on.

The mic is very simplistic, which honestly is all it needs to be. It picked up my voice fine and my group heard me fine during my matches and my discord group had no trouble either (crisp and clean). It only extends to about corner lip, at least on the size of my head. There is also no worry about stupid wired mics in the way. it RETRACTS into the muff. How it does this without compromising sound quality is impressive.

Battery life is also very impressive. I don't stream so I see no point in having the lights active on the headphones, so those get turned off every time I turn the headphones on (one annoyance). So far I've actively run them for about 17 hours with no warning of low battery. Given that's subjective usage, mainly music but some just video game stuff which can have a lot of minimal workload.

Overall, very much recommend. Amazing sound quality buckled with comfort and affordability. These should easily cost triple what they are going for. Get them at this price while you can.

Edit: Sign up for their warranty. It is legit and they will address ALL your concerns. If a muff comes off, or your mic spring spikes out, that's a faulty pair. They got you. There is a card in the box, just sign up for their lifetime guarantee customer service. All it takes is an email. I did it myself, it's real.

reviews.author: Justin Slate
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 25, 2026
reviews.content:
Got to give this 5 stars. Very high quality feel/build. The sound is great. Microphone is fantastic (pair with nvidia broadcast app). I love that the microphone retracts.
I've used with PC. Just tried the wired Xbox mode and worked perfectly as well. This is a great product and possibly best bang for your buck. Comfortable and can hear very clearly.

reviews.author: Ron Xmas-Shopping
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 28, 2024
reviews.content:
Lets just say I searched the entirety of the internet trying to find a DECENT headset for a REASONABLE price...

I have purchased many types of JUST Headphones over the decades... I spent countless dollars on them and learned about how each "technical specification" relates to the overall sound....which to me is the most important factor for headphones... So.... to say the very least... I knew what I was looking for...

This was my very 1st official "GAMING Headset" That I have purchased.... Yeah I have purchased other cheap regular headsets which were ok for what I needed them for.... but this time... I wanted to get something really nice...

After searching far and wide... comparing price... to performance... to technical specifications... other reviews (both written and video)... I came accross these bad boyz!!!

Most of the high end headsets that cost in the HUNDREDS of dollars just seamed waaaay to unfeasible to justify spending that kind of money on something that I wasn't sure If I would like... and besides... there are much less expensive head sets that have even better technical specifications and virtually identical functionality....

So.... after narrowing my search to about 10 different medium priced headsets and tons of reviews from Amazon, YouTube, even asking some advice of real life people who have purchased many "GAMING Headsets" of their own... THERE WAS BY FAR O N L Y O N E CLEAR WINNER....

The "VALORISE UG-08S"

The reviews alone were astonishing how many people loved these head sets (NOW JUST LIKE ME)

The price was JUST RIGHT... The quality of the manufacturing is VERY impressive.... it feels and looks like its put together with an incredible amount of precision and they are feel very durable.

Some headsets in the past....(some not much less expensive than these)...... just always felt cheap....shotty....flimsy... like they could snap in half at any moment....terrible ear phone material (BY THE WAY.... NEVER EVER GET THOSE CRAPPY PLEATHER EAR CUPS...THEY SHRED VERY QUICKLY AND MOST YOU CAN'T EVEN REPLACE....)

The VALORISE UG-08S has NONE OF THAT.... (not that i recommend doing this next thing....but you "could" throw these across the room at a wall and they will damage your wall before they damage the headset....lol) Build VERY durable

The Ear cups are a VERY NICE breathable cloth like fabric that cover some ABSOLUTLEY AWESOME foam material underneath.... It feels great!!! Its thick enough to block out almost ANYTHING around you..... so they are PERFECT if you want isolated immersion into your games..... (AND ALSO....aside from just games.... THEY ARE EXCEPTION HEADPHONES if you just want to use them for just that...which I often do, when im just doing stuff around the house, like cleaning, etc.) BECAUSE OF ANOTHER AWESOME FEATURE.... which ive NEVER seen on any headset before.... The Microphone kinds "slides/tucks/rolls" up into the Headset itself....(kinda like a snail...i suppose) THIS IS SUCH A GREAT FEATURE ! ! ! so when I want the microphone out of the way and tucked in its like its not even there.... which works GREAT FOR ME cause I use these every day for listening to music, or just Bluetoothing them to ANYTHING AT ALL... (like my phone, smart TV, stereo,etc.).....BY THE WAY....THE SOUND IS FLAWLESS EVEN WITH BLUETOOTH CONNECTION (which natoriously for most every headphone/headset ive ever used....you lose a lot of quality of the sound....not with these things....they sound identical no matter how you connect them) ....oh forgot to mention the microphone.....sound quality is flawless... the flexible rubber sleaved wire where the mouth peice is attached is flexible, but sturdy so its conveint to be able to position the mic anywhere your need (cause everyone has a different preference where they like their mice) .......WHICH ALSO HAS A SEPERATE small spring loaded mute button on the side of the Ear muff which makes it easy to tell if your muted or live just by the feel of the button....(plus the mic has a small green light on the mouse peice itself so you can VISIBLY see just out of the corner of your eye whether or not your mic is also muted or live.....I SWEAR THEY THOUGHT OF FRIKKIN EVERYTHING WITH THIS HEADSET...

.....and the distance on the Bluetooth is VERY VERY good... I can walk all the way accross my very large apartment into another room even on the opposite end....AND IT STILL WORKS!!! probably the longest bluetooth connection Ive ever seen on any device to be honest....AND THATS NOT EVEN THE BEST PART!!!

You can connect the VALORISE UG-08S in 3 different ways...

(1) STRAIGHT BLUETOOTH ....no special dongle required (WHICH BTW.... IT DOES COME WITH A DONGLE IF YOU PREFER THAT....

(2) WIRELESS CONNECTION ....which DOES obviously require the use of the dongle for WIRELESS MODE... which is no big deal...thats how all wireless devices work....BUT HERE'S THE THING.... (I love this part....) The wireless dongle comes as about a little over an inch from end to the beginning of where the USB Male part is.....BUT IT ALSO CAN SPLIT off and inside it has a USB-C Male end....so no matter what kinda device you need to plug it into.... its capable of BOTH.... I didnt even know about that until after I received the headset....which i thought was VERY COOL....EXTREMLY VERSATILE!!!

(3) HARD WIRED 3.5mm (standard headphone jack) IT DOES COME WITH IT'S OWN 3.5mm cable

but ABSOLUTLEY incredible.... I never new any headset or headphones had all 3 of these connection methods to use with ANYTHING YOU CAN IMAGINE!!! You can even use it to take phone calls with on your standard network phone, (plus VOIP calls obviously as well...like using Google Voice on your pc or any smart device) I dont use it for that....but YOU TOTALLY CAN ! ! !.

The battery life last quite a long time considering how the VALORISE UG-08S functions at very high levels ALL ACCROSS THE BOARD.... The Charging plug is USB-C (THANK GAWD!!!!) EVERYTHING SHOULD BE USB-C now adays.... I CANNOT STAND "mini USB" plugs and cables....

BTW.... Even though it comes with its own charging "cable" you need to provide your own charging "adapter" which honestly.....is no big deal.... EVERYONE HAS ABOUT 20 DIFFERENT EXTRA PHONE OR DEVICE CHARGERS THEY ARENT EVEN USING.... BUT MAKE SURE YOU READ THE SPECS OF WHATEVER CHARGING ADAPTER YOUR PLANNING TO USE ! ! ! ! !

* * * ONLY USE a "5v 1,000ma/1.0a" or a "5v 2,000ma/2.0a" --- AT THE VERY MAX.... DO NOT GO ABOVE 2,000ma/2.0a * * * I ONLY MENTION THIS BECAUSE I FIGURED OUT IN A VERY TRAGIC WAY YEARS AGO ABOUT USING "EXACT POWER ADAPTER SPECIFICATIONS" (before i learned about how important that stuff is.... I blew up a $150 Guitar Multi FX pedal many years ago by using a plug that fit....but it was about 3,000ma/3.0a OVER the MAX tolerance for my poor BOSS Guitar FX Pedal...now buried very tragically in the backyard of a house i used to live back in the day....(MAN I MISS THAT PEDAL....is was soooo awesome) (*dries his tears*) .....ANYWAY * * * ALWAYS USE EXACT ELECTRIC POWER SPECIFICATIONS ON ANY ELECTRONIC DEVICE!!!

It's got 7.1 surround sound....which sounds AMAZING!

....but the best feature has got to be the sound quality.... THE AUDIO FIDELITY IS ABSOLUTLEY A #1 CHEIF PRIORITY FOR ME WHEN USING ANY HEADSET OR HEADPHONES.... I'm such a snob when it comes to headphones/headset when it comes to that...

My philosophy: "IT HAS TO PERFORM TO MY ***EXACT*** AUDIO NEEDS ON....EVERY LEVEL...." (otherwise it's just straight garbage...... as my man NEGAN........DID SAY......." N O E X C E P T I O N S ! ! ! ! "

if it doesn't sound CLEAN, CLEAR, and is capable of handling the highest of the "highs" (trebble) and the lowest of the "lows" (bass) without sounded "muffled" or in any way distorted.....
I JUST WONT USE IT! PERIOD!

This headset performs with 100% approval as far as MY PERSONAL BAR is......and I gotta tell you.... IF ITS GOOD ENOUGH FOR ME....ITS DEFINATLEY GOOD ENOUGH FOR ANYONE!!!

I've only been using these for a very short time....and as long as they keep holding up the way they do right out of the box..... IM DEFINATLEY GETTING MORE THAN ONE PAIR OF THESE....

(speaking of the box....) the presentation is fantastic.... the box is VERY NICE.... its not just some flimsy cheap standard cardboard box (thats the thickness of a box of cereal) the box is very robust and well made....and when you open it up it set in there VERY NICELY and you can tell they put VERY SERIOUS EFFORT to make everything about this headset and even the packaging....which speaks volumes when you purchase an item from a company for the first time.... it tells you THEY ARE SERIOUS ABOUT MAKING A QUALITY PRODUCT....AND THEY TAKE PRIDE IN THEIR PRODUCTS.....respect!!!!

IM VERY HAPPY WITH MY PURCHASE!

I HIGHLY RECOMMEND THE VALORISE UG-08S TO ANYONE AND EVERYONE WHO WANTS A EXTREMLY WELL MADE AND HIGH PERFORMANCE HEADSET OR EVEN IF YOU JUST WANT TO USE IT FOR HEADPHONES TO LISTEN TO MUSIC OR WHATEVER....

*** UPDATE *** STILL WORKING PERFECTLY JUST LIKE IT WAS FRESH OUT THE BOX
I have ordered a 2nd one of these....for someone....and i plan to order YET ANOTHER ONE (OR MORE) So I can keep extras at different places (work, one in my travel bag or backpack, one to keep as a backup in case something happens to my main one.....2 words......"WOMEN's SCORN".....I think you get the picture....lol : ) Ive been telling and showing a lot of people this headset....and some of them have already purchased their own and are just as happy with the headset as I am....and apparently like many other reviewers as well

reviews.author: Shawn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026
reviews.content:
Pros:
Sound quality - I'm big on bright headphones. Most fall short in both deep bass and high frequencies. This excels at both and sounds surprisingly good. It even sounds better than my Sennheiser HD212 Pro headphones.
Battery life: I still haven't discharged them, even after a few days--a total of multiple house (with the LED lighting off, mix of bluetooth and dongle mode)
Ergonomics: The over-the-ear design is comfortable and the headphones are light weight, good for longer sessions.

The cons (some of these are minor)
Connectivity is awkward with multiple devices. I have two tablets, two phones, and a Steam Deck, and it will only remember the pairing with one bluetooth device at a time, meaning I'll have to disable bluetooth on the device it's paired with to use them with another device. It is kind of awkward.
The LED is on by default, and does not remember the setting from session to session, meaning I have to turn it off every single time.
The mic is pretty quiet in dongle mode--it could stand to be more sensitive
It would be nice if the 4-pole cable came with a y-adapter to two 3-pole plugs for separate headphone and mic connections. I had to buy this separately.

reviews.author: Cole Presley
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 17, 2025
reviews.content:
Update:
After speaking with customer service, they made things right. I have a new headset coming my way and I am sure the battery will live up to the advertised claims. I am hard on companies that sell products and don't stand behind them or the product doesn't perform as advertised. This is not one of those instances. This company definitely stood behind their product! I originally rated 2 stars due to my headset and the battery life (I think I just got a bad battery or something). With the communication provided by their customer service team and the determination to make it right, I am raising my rating to 5 stars. This is not 5 stars for the product itself. I would rate these headphones at a solid 4, but the fact that customer service cares about their customers, they have my loyalty for life.

Bluetooth mode update: I originally had these in Dongle mode, not Bluetooth. The Bluetooth mode worked great but I did notice my mute button did not work in Bluetooth mode. Again, I may have just got a headphone set that was destined to fail. Hopefully my new set will work flawlessly. Based on other reviewers, I feel good about it.

Original:
Everything about this headset is good, not great, but good except the battery life. This thing dies fast! Maybe I got a faulty battery or something, but there is no way this thing lasts 40 hours with the light effect turned off, much less the 100 hours they advertise. I could charge it every day, or every other day, but I shouldn't have to. This should last a minimum of 4.5 days of use based on the manufacture's specs.

I purchased this to use as my headset for work. I run a call center and use the headset and mic for 8.5 hours a day. I turn it on at 8am and turn it off at 4:30pm. (I usually turn the headset off for my 30-minute lunch.) I make sure the light effect is turned off to conserve power as I didn't really need this anyway. The battery only lasts about 3.5 days. That is only 29ish give or take hours of run time before the headset says, "Battery Low". Maybe the seller will send me a new one without a bad battery, but for now, the 2-star rating stands as I can't make it through the whole week using this set.

The microphone is nice and clear with no echo. This was a huge thing for me since I am on calls all day. The only downside to the microphone is there is no noise canceling. The microphone will pick up every noise in your room. I have a foot massager at my desk, and I am told it sounds like I am in a machine factory when I have it on during a call.

The audio is clear and sounds great for my calls. When listening to music, I would give it a 6.5 on a scale of 10. The volume is a bit limited. I feel like for what these are, they could be louder. The sound is crisp and does not distort, even when turned all the way up. I find the bass to be lacking though. You can hear it, but it should be much deeper considering they call this product 7.1 surround sound headphones. I would be slightly disappointed in the bass quality if I purchased these for FPS games. I do feel like you may be able to hear the enemy directionally with these though, given they claim 7.1.

I have no doubt this would work just fine for gaming, but that is not what I purchased it for.

Comfort level is obviously a big deal when you wear these for extended periods of time. The cushion on the top of the headset that rests on your head could be better if filled with some type of gel. There is a bit of foam, but after a few hours, it starts to hurt and I have to adjust it and move it to a new spot on my head. The earmuffs are absolutely awesome though! I can't even tell they are on. They do not block out any outside noise though, so don't expect a quiet experience if you have background noise in the room. It muffles it, but only due to covering your ears.

The Bluetooth works great for what I need! I needed to be able to get up from my desk and walk around it without being attached with a short cord. It does not work at far ranges though. I tried to walk into my kitchen and the audio and mic cut out at about 10-15 feet. I does not disconnect until I reach my bedroom which is about 50-70 feet but then reconnects when I get closer to the computer. Again, there is no audio after about 10-15 feet though, so I'm not sure what the use of it being connect further away is.

One last thing to mention is these are BIG! I mean that in the sense of the earmuffs are really quite large and heavy. That probably leads to the discomfort after extended periods or use.

I will update this rating if the manufacturer issues a new headset with a better battery life as that is the whole reason I rated this 2-stars. It is very frustrating to purchase something and it not work for the specific purpose you needed. In my case a battery that would last all week.

reviews.author: TRISH
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 10, 2026
reviews.content:
This was a gift for my
Son in law. I gave it a good rating based on how much he like it....The wireless connection is his favorite part, he moves constantly while he's playing and it stays connected. Lightweight & Comfortable, great sound & the noise cancellation feature is 2 thumbs up! These headphones were on sale and very reasonable...Best bang for your buck

reviews.author: GAD
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 16, 2025
reviews.content:
I use a separate mic and haven't tested the mic but the headset audio sounds great and there's a ton of very well thought out design to this device:

* Audio is fantastic, with excellent response range and clarity, smooth and sensitive drivers. The 7.1 simulated surround is great, enough to pacify me who is *incredibly* picky and won't usually deign to use a headset without literally 4 to 5 physical speakers in each cup. (i.e. ASUS ROG Centurion 7.1 et. al.). I recommend using Equalizer APO on windows for preferential tuning.
* Incredible battery life, can charge (usb-c) while using, lasts probably at least 50 music-on hours in 7.1 mode using dongle with lights off and mic off, but I usually remember to plug it in every 2-3 days... takes less than 2 hours to charge from 0-100% though anyway... bluetooth mode with no lights or mic probably lasts over 100 hours. I rarely hear the low battery notice unless I've been especially neglectful.
* Comprehensive connection options (wireless-dongle that works as usb-c or even usb-a with the built-in adapter, highly compatible bluetooth, even a 3.5mm jack!!)
* Build quality is comfortable and simple, and quite durable. Cups rotate backward 90° and tilt just barely maybe <10°-- enough to conform to head. Cushions are comfortable and don't really give me tightness even on my fat head, while still being quite secure. My glasses arm digs into behind my ear a bit but that's just from using headphones in general, and less so with this headset.
* While I don't use it, the microphone is a fishing-reel-gummy-arm style that retracts into the cup or pulls out and the arm is made of a material that holds to any position you adjust it to, so it can be right where you need it. Max pull-out is just barely past the corner of my mouth. As far as I tested the audio quality is more than acceptable, I just have a nice desktop mic I use.
* The lighting is very low-key, just enough to be meaningful without being obnoxious; most importantly, can be toggled completely off with zero effort. The only running lights while I'm using it are the tiny power-on blue dot and tiny 7.1-on white dot, both at the bottom so essentially invisible from anywhere in the room.
* It boggles my mind that these are sold at $50, that's mind-bogglingly cheap compared to competitors at this quality range. Frequently they're on sale for $35~ even, which is insane. When I ordered these because my last headset gave out, I was NOT expecting these to be more than "usable" until I found something up to my usual snuff, but I was very pleasantly surprised.

As someone who's used many, many, many, *many* expensive headsets (I'm talking $200-$600 range) and quite a few cheap ones, this is hands down the best stereo headset I've come across that isn't some absurdly expensive audiophile device-- and it's so far above the average you would think it's similar to a $150-$200 sound. Except it also has better and more intelligent array of features than those tend to offer.

Are they what I want to use? No. I want to use REAL 7.1 headsets... problem is that kind of thing isn't made anymore.
I am incredibly picky about what I'm willing to use, and for me to have come to terms with companies not making what I want anymore, and effectively "settling" for 2.0 and me deciding that this is all I'm going to use from now on (short of some companies coming back with luxury REAL 7.1 products),
I'm so confident in it that I've ordered 2 more of these headsets even though there's nothing wrong with mine and I don't estimate there ever will be; just in case *this* company stops making these great products and I somehow destroy my current ones. I can't imagine I would go use any other headset in the future unless some absolutely ridiculous and amazing thing comes out, but I don't expect that to happen so it's these for as long as they're made.

There are only three misgivings I have, none of which really matter and are just some pondering:
1. My volume scroll wheel, possibly from laying on a pillow with it frequently, has an issue that cuts out the right cup audio if it's turned to exactly 100%... solution: turn to 99% and all is fine. Easy workaround, no stress; muscle memory fixes it in 1 second. The wheel doesn't easily adjust inadvertently, it's not difficult to turn or anything but unless you brush it pretty deliberately it's not gonna randomly change.
2. This has two buttons (and a physical mic toggle clicker): Power to play/pause/answer/hangup/reject and toggle dongle/bt. M to toggle 7.1 and lights. I don't really wish it had an extra button or two or feature for media forward/back but I could see it being a useful feature for some and I would probably use it if it had.
3. It'd be nice if the cups rotated vertically slightly more but honestly completely unnecessary and is just a personal preference thing; they rotate enough to conform to the head at any band-extension as-is so it's a non-issue.

reviews.author: ContentCreatorDan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.content:
I bought 2 pairs of these headphones. I'll start out with why it only got 3 stars. One pair works flawlessly, one pair causes interference with the game controllers on a ps5 and the audio from the mic sounds like you are constantly rubbing the mic with something (very staticy).
I've used them on both a ps4 and ps5, as well as Bluetooth to my phone. So compatibility is wide! Using them with the ps5 is definitely a better experience than the ps4 due to the audio output from the console itself.
They have a good neutral audio, not too much highs or lows. For the size of the driver you'd expect distortion from the variety of audio coming out, but it's clear clean audio!
The wife loves the pink and I enjoy the black and red! The light on the mic is nice, as it is only lit when it isn't muted.
I like the scroll wheel audio adjustment. The 7.1 surround button makes quite a difference! Not quite dolby atmos, but simulates well!
The wires and plugs are all nice quality and look like they'll hold up well.
They aren't super heavy and the ear pads are comfy.
The battery life seems good so far, only time will tell how fast the battery degrades.
I would suggest these if you are on a budget and need a good headset. If you can spend more, don't cheap out and get yourself a turtle beach or something.

reviews.author: James
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 10, 2024
reviews.content:
I've owned these Bluetooth over-ear hearing protection earmuffs for about 9 months. I've used these earphones countless times now and love them. I use them mowing the lawn, with power tools, such as mitre saw, power washer, leaf blower, etc. and I can listen to my podcasts or music without issue. I don't know how these compare with expensive models, but for the price I'm extremely happy with the comfort, use and durability. I've had them covered in sawdust, have gotten them wet from the power washer and they continue to work perfectly. They charge nicely, I don't know how long they last on one charge, but I've used them multiple days, left them not charging, used them again for multiple days and only had them die on me once. I charge them periodically from time-to-time and they always work well. I'm happy with the audio quality. I used to have just regular noise cancelling earmuffs, which were only earmuffs, no speakers. If I don't have to, I'll never go back to regular earmuffs, I love the Bluetooth feature and listening while I work. See below paragraphs for how they fit, charging and speakers.

- The over-ear fit - I'm a 6'2" male, 22.5" head size (7 1/8 hat size). These earmuffs are adjustable and fit me just fine. The wire over-earmuff is extendable and comes completely retracted. I thought there was something wrong with them originally because they were so short, and I don't remember seeing instructions how to extend them. I just had to put them on my head and pull down pretty forcefully to begin extending the wire arms to fit my head right. They stay in place nicely, so the wires don't move easily, which is a good thing, they adjust and stay where you put them.

- Charging - I charged the Bluetooth earmuff headphones before using for the first time. Comes with a USB plug-in with USB-C port. No cover over the port on the earmuff, I haven't had a problem with debris getting in the port while using outside (ie: lawn clippings, sawdust, etc.) After charging they paired easily to my phone. I used them for many hours over several days and they still had a charge. The manual indicates you can use them for many hours without a charge and it seems to be the case for me. A week or two later I used them again without charging, they only worked for about half an hour and then died. So I speculate they do drain while not in use, so I would say it's imperative to charge before each use or charge regularly to keep them charged to a sufficient level.

- Speakers - I'm happy with the speakers, the sound is good. I've been listening to talk shows while doing loud, outdoor work. Both speakers put out the same sound, clear and crisp. They are loud enough to hear the entire conversation fine without busting your eardrum. No cracking or breaking up, no Bluetooth disconnection issues. Overall very happy with this purchase and would definitely buy again!

reviews.author: Muddiman family
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 28, 2025
reviews.content:
These are, by far, the best muffs Ive ever owned...both for price and quality.

They last a long time on a single charge; have awesome sound and connection quality to phone for music and calls; and noise reduction even wearing glasses is excellent (at least for the gel pads!)

I wear them operating many different highway equipment/machines including a volvo open cab road roller and 3/4 drive pneumatic tools in a closed garage, and I can listen to an audio book easily.

These make 3M eartunes feel and look like overpriced junk (which they are imo)

reviews.author: Gbucks
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 16, 2024
reviews.content:
After comparing to more expensive namebrand options, I decided to give this one a try. The sound quality and noise reduction are both good. The pairing was easy and seems to work pretty seamlessly. The controls are fairly intuitive and easy to operate one-handed. The battery life seems solid - I generally use it for 1-3 hours at a time, and can go for 8-10 hours of use before needing to recharge.

Mostly I use this for mowing the lawn or medium-intensity carpentry and woodworking projects around the house. Primarily I listen to podcasts, and it works great for these use cases.

My only real critique is comfort. As other reviewers have noted, they are a little tight on the ears. I have a larger-than-average head and with the earmuffs in full extension, they exert a slight upward pressure on my ears. It's fine for a couple of hours, but start to get uncomfortable after that. For all day worksite use, they wouldn't work for me and my big head. I think if the earmuffs extended another 10-20 mm further down on each side this issue would likely be mostly resolved (though I think I might still find the inward compression a little tight for my taste).

Overall a good product for me though, and since my days of all-day worksite headphone use are behind me for now, I'll be keeping them and using them happily for shorter stints.

reviews.author: Tom Adams
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026
reviews.content: Received it in about 2 days! Looks good and works perfectly! Very comfortable! The sound quality and the sound-deadening is incredible! The Bluetooth works great! I would buy these again in a heart beat!

reviews.author: Greyson English
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.content:
On a diffrent account I have bought these same headphones two other times.

They each lasted 1 year while being subjected to continuous use. (10 hrs a day for 365 days at mid volume)

The headband broke long before the audio. I am still able to use my mangled pair but I'm happy to get another.

They are AMAZING. The sound muffling is decent and the bluetooth range is phenomenal.

I can leave my phone in my room and walk at least a quarter mile into my yard without any static cutting out.

I'm not an audiobuff by any means but I can hear every instrument evenly and make out vocals cleanly.

The cushions are very comfortable, even during long listening sessions.

To top it off, the support staff are understanding and well trained.

100% reccomend!

reviews.author: C Cross
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.content:
ProHear/Amazon wont seem to post this review. But Im not deleting it. I have written many a great review about great products on Amazon, and of course, this isnt exactly one of them. But if we, the consumer, are the new quality control, the rating results shouldnt only take in to account good reviews to hold companies to a better standard.
I recently purchased a set of ProHear shooting muffs with the walkie talkie feature. I have another set of shooting muffs with a walkie walkie attachment from a popular brand. And was going to order another set for my uncle for when we go out plinking. But I saw the price of the ProHear set and after some research determined that they were as good, if not better, than the traditional brand set. After testing those, and being VERY impressed, especially per the pricepoint that I paid for them, I was convinced that I also needed a set of jobsite muffs from ProHear.
I'm a bit neutral on my feelings of these, hoqever, per the pricepoint.
Ive determined that these are definitely not a replacement for my jobsite muffs.
I dont have a dB reader, so I'd have to assume the dB protection is accurate (maybe I'll ask the safety guys to test the levels for me, at some point). But I have had less expensive jobsite muffs, that dont have bluetooth that noticeably cut the dB levels down more.
Understanding that adding the Bluetooth tech (for music) into them adds to the manufactured cost, I cant gripe on it. And, the sound quality is decent, for the price. Are they high end music muffs? No. Thats not what they are trying to be. They are PPE that allows you to not ALSO have to use wireless ear buds underneath for music.
But my take on them:
I got them for the sale price of $36.
For that price, they suffice. If I had paid the full price, as advertised, I would have been pretty disappointed with the dB protection quality at that price.
In my opinion, these are $30-$35 muffs.
And considering I bought these for home shop use, they will be sufficient enough to not use my actual jobsite muffs/wireless earbuds combo at home anymore.
And if the battery life stacks up to the reviews, I imagine they will work fine for home shop use.
*Also worth mentioning: They also have a 3.5mm jack. So, if the battery dies and you have a music device that has 3.5mm you can still listen to music with a dead battery. That was a thoughtfully added feature in my opinion.
*Update on battery: I have been using these all day, every day, while running saws and sanders, for the past two weeks. I plug them into the charger over night every night and turn them on every morning. I am consistently getting 4.5 hours of music playback before they power off. I just put in my in-ear buds (under these) to listen to music after these die every afternoon. Ive tried them at various low and mid levels of volume, and I have my (6 month old model) phone (music source) plugged in to a charger about 5-10 feet away 90% of the day. So, I'd definitely say the advertised battery life is WAY, WAY over fluffed.
Again, they are hearing protection. And for the most part, they do that job well enough to keep using them. And they save me 4.5 hours of battery use of my in-ear music buds. But, the buds still get daily use for 50%+ of the day.

reviews.author: Jackie's alphin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.content: Comfortable and work well. Would recommend. Much better at blocking out the outside noise than normal headphones like the beats and that type of headphone. I use them at work running machines and they are great at blocking out the sound and I can hear my podcast without having to turn it all the way up.

reviews.author: Adam Koontz
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 11, 2025
reviews.content: My puppy decided to eat my other big name brand BT hearing protection so I tried this more affordable alternative. If the big brand are a 10/10, these are 8.5/10 at a fraction of the cost. Slightly less comfortable, slightly less sound quality, but still very good and I’m happy with them. No issues connecting to any devices I’ve tried to pair. Battery lasts a good long time. Db reduction seems to be same as others as far as I can tell. I think these are in the sweet spot for me for quality/ features vs price and I wouldn’t hesitate to buy them again if needed.

reviews.author: Manne
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content: Just wow! For the compact size and comfort they offer, these are so well tuned for genres such as rock and classical. Some would say that these lack a punch in the bass - and they are not wrong. I personally have never liked bass heavy headphones anyway, esp. the ones that don't leave enough space for the mids and highs. These I believe use 32mm drivers - and I find the bass sufficient for my taste. These are amongst the most sturdy yet lightest headphones I have owned. The way Marshall has done their soundstage feature is also fantastic - I find it way better than the implementation from other competitors under $400. Noise cancellation is 80% as good as that of Bose's or Sony's: They might not cut out highs in some environments. But Marshall's NC implementation does not have the same 'cabin pressure' type of feeling - which is another aspect I love about them. Multipoint bluetooth connectivity is seamless. The auto play/pause feature has a lag of about 3 seconds when you take them off (they are fine when you put them on). Its not a deal breaker - but hopefully something Marshall fix through a s/w update?

reviews.author: Jeff
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 14, 2026
reviews.content: Super comfortable and lightweight, super durable and they sound excellent. Overall functionality and fit is top notch. The app is nice and features a 5 band EQ to help dial in your preferred sound... I go with “V” shape and it sounds perfect. Case is cool, kinda awkward to grab onto but it’s protective and has great styling, headphones fold up nicely into it. All in all these are an excellent value… super premium and versatile headphones that will last many years. Marshall really did an exceptional job with these, I really dig em.

reviews.author: ash
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 25, 2026
reviews.content:
I wanted a good quality pair of headphones so I could really enjoy my music. I did my research and finally landed on this beautiful pair.

Good headphones are expensive, but they are in the price range of comparable brands, so no complaints there. They are also the most comfortable pair I've ever used. The inner ear cups feel like clouds.

They connect to my phone easily. I use the wire for my console and PC. The wire is a 3.5mm on one end and USB-C on the other end. Hopefully they sell replacements in case it breaks though no issues so far.

The battery life is good. I often forget to turn them off and it kills the battery. Make sure you set it up to have auto turn off in the app.

There are 3 modes of noise cancelling: on, off, pass-through. Pass-through is nice if I'm playing a game but someone wants to talk to me. Off, I can still kind of hear them but not clearly. On, I can't hear anything people say. The noise cancelling isn't 100%. You don't go into a silent bubble and this is actually why I got these over other ones. I don't want 100% noise cancellation. However they are still pretty good at blocking out noises enough that once your music or game is playing, you won't hear anything but what you're focused on.

The headphones function great. The sound is beautiful, the sound stage is an interesting mode. One thing I love about these is the intuitive physical buttons. Left ear cup has a button to control noise cancelling. Right ear cup has a button to control the soundstage on or off. And another button on that ear cup to control power, volume, and next song.

There's no voice saying "power on" or whatever. Instead it's a pleasant little sound effect for each thing you're trying to do.

There's an app for these headphones and I recommend getting it. It contains an equalizer, a way to fine-tune Soundstage, battery life, and a bunch of other things.

I would buy these again and again.

reviews.author: D. K. Corbett
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 28, 2025
reviews.content: Pleasantly surprised at all the upgrades. Love the overall construction, and overall design. Definitely an iPod chaser. A great value for the money. Never was a fan of touchscreens so these cans don't disappoint. The brass knob and buttons on the brackets are easy to operate. Sturdy, yet lightweight. Removable earcups, which, by the way are soft and comfortable. The app is easy to install. The sound is clear and crisp with just enough bass. Also like the silicone headband strip. Charging is quick. The usb cord though, is somewhat short and it could have come with a usb o 3.5 cable

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 11, 2025
reviews.content:
These Marshall Monitor III have quickly become my favorite headphones, after extensive listening sessions with: Bose QuietComfort Ultra (V2), Bowers & Wilkins Px7 S2e and S3, Cambridge P100 SE, and JBL Tour One M3.

Summary:
Love the Marshall’s sound quality for all my listening. The “Marshall” baseline EQ is very good, and I’m really happy with a single Custom EQ that resolves personal sensitivities (more below) better than most of those competitors above. I hear a lovely balance with just-right bass, mids and treble, appropriately placed vocals, lots of detail and layering. Soundstage is plenty wide enough without spatial augmentation (not a priority).

Great, plush comfort for long listening sessions, although more earcup depth would be nice for my big ears. My apparently large head doesn’t cause any clamping pressure and the headband is unnoticeable. The Marshall controls are fantastic - love that joystick on the right shell! They’re easy to learn and use intuitively, and the App allows some customization of the two hinge buttons. Build quality is confidence inspiring, battery life is incredible and the compact fold is handy, in or out of the beautiful case. Connectivity is easy including source switching.

While I couldn’t be considered an audiophile, whatever that is, my musical preferences have become more engaging with good headphones. These ears are over seven decades old, aided by Sennheiser-branded hearing aids to combat high frequency hearing loss. EQ adjustments have become a big decision factor among those candidates to deal with my HA-boosted sensitivity to sibilance and stridency that distracts from the music.

Top decision factors are sound quality and comfort. Then there’s build quality, easy connectivity and switching between sources, battery life since fewer charging cycles translates to longer overall battery life. Controls have turned out to be important.

My sources are iPhone, iPad, and Bluetooth connection to TV, with no requirement for higher quality codecs that I probably wouldn’t fully appreciate at this age. As a retiree with no commuting and few Zoom calls, noise cancelling (ANC) and call quality aren’t important in my decision.

My musical tastes run strongly toward classic rock and modern variations, with playlists dominated by guitarists including Mark Knopfler, Pink Floyd/David Gilmour, Zeppelin/Plant, Jackson Browne, Bonnie Raitt (her voice can slip into sibilance) and similar. I’ve recently discovered Joe Bonamassa - really enjoy his live acoustic concerts, and at other ends of the guitarist spectrum, Tommy Emmanuel, Jake Shimabukuro (amazing ukulele!) and mellower albums from Buckethead!

Way too much about me! The headphone decisions:

The P100 SE and Px7 S3 are both fantastic but their EQ’s couldn’t sufficiently tame the sibilance/stridency that those guitarists and vocalists can create. They both also had an uncomfortable clamping force on my big head. The Cambridge App was quite buggy, frequently not being able to “find” the P100 SE’s. The Px7 S3 was surprisingly uncomfortable on my large ears, unlike the Px7 S2e which was comfortable except for clamping pressure. That one had a usually delightful SQ despite essentially no EQ, to deal with sibilance, and there was always a slight veil over the often recessed vocals.

The Marshall, Bose and JBL were all very comfortable on head and ears. But Bose controls and connectivity switching were really frustrating and its minimal EQ made my music less satisfying than the Marshall or JBL. The Bose and JBL App have many options, but Bose’ was difficult to decipher. The Marshall App feels like just enough choices.

It came down to a close call between Marshall and JBL. The excellent JBL met all my criteria, but there’s a sense that they won’t hold up over time, and there are no user-replaceable pads or anything else. I used JBL’s fantastic EQ to create several Custom curves that worked for sibilance sensitivity, with variations in mids and bass. Realized that I prefer to stick with just one EQ curve for everything. Interesting that I modeled the JBL curves from my Marshall Custom curve with a notch on its 2.5kHz slider that nicely deals with sibilance.

JBL didn’t meet their battery life spec despite minimal ANC use, and I disliked that ANC defaults to ON. The separate JBL SmartTX seems like a good idea, but just another thing to charge and not lose. I always have my phone anyway, so not much use.

The Marshall Monitor III’s allow me to be fully engaged with music for hours without ever thinking about the device. Nearly perfect!

reviews.author: webzulu
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 24, 2025
reviews.content:
I love, love this headphone! The sound quality is great and the build quality is top-notch. The Marshall Monitor III came across my feed and I was immediately taken with its style. So I poured over many positive reviews and made the purchase. I couldn't be more pleased!

Upon first opening the package I was immediately impressed with the presentation. Inside a high quality box rested an impossibly small leather-like headphone case with a pebble finish that housed the headphones. The MM3 is very light. The ear cushions are plush, the softest i have experienced and very comfortable. The rest of the headphone is covered with a leather-like material that feels very premium.

The sound quality is fantastic. The MM3 has bass but not as thumping. They have a bright clear sound, I am no audiophile so that's the best I can describe it. I like them so much that they are the first headphones I pick up when I wanna listen to music, news or podcasts. I recently took them on a weekend trip to my son's basketball tournament with no regrets. Noise Cancelling was decent, not as good as the Momentum 4 but still good. I really like the button placement. The single button for vol +- and prev/next was really easy to navigate as well as the unique positioning of the ANC button was extremely convenient. I never made he mistake of pushing he wrong button as I often do with my other headphones.

Finally, these headphones come at a premium price and I am price conscious but I in no way regret this purchase. They check all the boxes of what's important to me: comfortable, portable and stylish...along with excellent sound quality (I was initially concerned that they don't support hi-res audio, Apt-x or Ldac but they sound great to me ...go figure!)...and long battery like...I could go on and on about these things...LOVE Them!!

Update: 3 Months Later
These are still my favorite headphones. One thing that I like a lot about the headphones is how compact they are when they are on your head and when they are folded up. I wish ANC was a little better but still pretty good. Still very very comfortable to wear and sound great.

reviews.author: Marine Petrosyan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 24, 2025
reviews.content:
First Impressions - Absolutely Love it! How it feels, how it sounds, how it weighs.
1. Comfortable: No pressure on the head, light, feels very comfortable on ears.
2. Sound: Sounds great so far, amazing Dolby sound.
3. Noise cancellation: Have not tested all scenarios yet, but so far, so good. I have had JBL, Sony and Beats before this: definitely better than JBL and Beats. I think as good as Sony, probably.
4. No touch buttons, everything is real: I adore that all buttons are real, you feel what you press. I may sound a little more classic or old-school, but I really like it. You don't have to worry about accident touches.
5. Materials feel great, built differently, very flexible, cool to own this piece.
6. Love the feature, that it automatically turns the playing material off, when you put it off.
Let's see how it behaves on the long run.

reviews.author: MARCELO INOSTROZA
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 2, 2026
reviews.content: Great fit, great comfort, real high definition listening. Great connectivity. Very good quality materials. Volume not as loud as I would like thou.

reviews.author: Quin Rider
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 6, 2025
reviews.content:
I’ve tried so many headaches homes to find the perfect ones and these are it.

NOISE CANCELLATION: Their noise cancellation feature is actual magic I can barely hear the noise of the world, which is crucial as an autistic person. There’s a button on the left side of the headphones that switches from noice canceling to normal mode so you can easily switch between modes without using an app.

BASE: The base is great and editable through the app to suit your individual preferences.

COMFORT: Since I use these every day for hours at a time I needed to invest in comfort. These have a soft padded head band that’s thin and stretches easily over larger heads. The ear cushion is made out of the same squishy soft material and comfortably fits over my ears. ( the material also smells really good, kinda like a faint cologne smell that’s not overpowering but just feels expensive and high quality.) About every 4 hours my head starts to hurt a little and I have to slightly adjust the headphone band to a different spot, but that’s a super simple fix that is still worth it for me.

VOICE: The female voice of the headphones that says things like “power on” and “low battery” is a super soothing low octave that doesn’t startle me like other headphones usually do. You can tell that they put money and thought into developing that feature.

VOICE AWARE FEATURE: If you are like me and you get frustrated when your headphones pause every time you make a noice you can turn off the voice aware feature with the app so that your headphones don’t pick up on you speaking and pause what you are listening to.

TOUCH FEATURES: I thought that the touch features would be really annoying at first because I tend to forget about them and accidentally mess with what I’m listening to but they aren’t as sensitive as other headphones and it’s only on one side so I don’t do it on accident as often. The other side is buttons which is what I prefer.

reviews.author: Edward Adams
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2026
reviews.content:
Newer model replacing my older Sony’s. Haven’t used this one yet but I’m sure it’ll be fine. Third pair owned since stepping up on headsets.
UPDATE: Using them now (Feb 6). REALLY NICE!
Cool features (like swiping up or down on the right ear cup to change volume! Great sound as expected!👍🏾

reviews.author: AC
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 15, 2025
reviews.content: I bought the Sony WH-1000XM5 for my son, and he absolutely loves them. The build quality is excellent, very sleek and comfortable for long listening sessions. The sound is superb — crystal clear with deep bass and amazing noise cancellation that really blocks out distractions. They feel premium in every way and are worth every penny. Easily one of the best headphones we’ve owned. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Zoi kouroupi
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 14, 2025
reviews.content: Bought because my studio beats kept breaking. These are amazing , the sound is way better than any headphones I’ve used and they’re so comfy on my ears, especially since I have alot of piercings and usually beats hurt my ears a lot! Highly recommend

reviews.author: Jellybeanbonza
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 5, 2024
reviews.content:
The sound and battery life are great. I love being connected to my laptop and phone at the same time, but sometimes it can act glitchy, such as when playing Spotify on my phone, I have to remember to go into the Headphones app and tell it to prioritize my phone over my laptop. There are other glitches I've had trouble figuring out, such as how to disable voice-activated conversation mode after it's turned itself on.

The main issue I have is with comfort: The headband is basically a rounded plastic bar instead of a wide flat surface. It's padded, but if you're bald like me, the entire weight of the headphones is resting on a pencil-wide stripe across my scalp, which gets uncomfortable long before my workday is over.

But the noise cancelling is SPOOKY good. I wouldn't hesitate to wear these on a 3–4 hour flight—with a baseball cap so they don't leave a dent across the top of my head.

reviews.author: Ben Sayles
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 7, 2025
reviews.content:
Didn’t even last a year sitting from extremely light use. Cheaply made plastic.

Update:
Contacted Sony support and went up the chain. They really struggled to explain how a product that never traveled or was dropped failed from standard use. Ultimately this is a manufacturing issue and they will not honor the warranty. Class action lawsuit incoming.

reviews.author: Pola
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.content: Batery last so long, and they are so comfortable, they have become part of my cozy rutine

reviews.author: Sam R
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 6, 2025
reviews.content: I was always told that they can be connected to multiple devices at once. Honestly, the sound quality is great, but for some reason, they sound much better when connected to ancompared to Apple devices. I don’t know why. The headset is relatively comfortable for about one hour or less; Iend using it for more than that.adset is very light, so if you have an Android device get

reviews.author: Hailey
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 28, 2026
reviews.content: These sport wireless earbuds booth work so great I bought these for my son and absolutely loves these. He literally uses them everyday and the ease of use is easy for him and the comfort level is good. He loves the sound quality of these and the connectivity as well. The size is great for him they sit on his ears really nicely and he can't be any more happier than he is with this product I feel like the value of this product is well worth my money. I am a happy customer all together.

reviews.author: S.Bernadac
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.content:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ (5/5)
I bought these earbuds back in November and I’m honestly very impressed. The battery life is amazing—I only have to charge the case occasionally, even with regular use. The sound quality is clear with strong bass, and they get loud enough without distortion.

I really like the earhook design because they stay in place during workouts, running, and walking—no slipping at all. The Bluetooth connection is fast and stable, and the mic works well for calls; people can hear me clearly.

reviews.author: Placeholder
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.content: These hit the target on every metric: sound quality, battery life, speed of Bluetooth connectivity, overall quality, comfort, secure ear connection, and charging speed. These are superior to my big name brand ear buds that I once used before I went in search of quality at a better price. The plane has finally landed. Thank you.

reviews.author: Roxana Martinez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 26, 2025
reviews.content:
I’m really impressed with these wireless earbuds. The sound quality is clear with good bass, and they get loud enough without distortion. They fit comfortably in my ears and stay in place even during workouts or long walks, which is a big plus for me.

The Bluetooth connection is stable and pairs quickly with my phone every time. Battery life is also very good—I can use them for several sessions before needing to recharge. The charging case is compact and easy to carry.

For the price, these earbuds offer excellent performance and reliability. I would definitely recommend them to anyone looking for affordable, sporty wireless headphones with solid sound quality.

reviews.author: KoaMan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content:
Although I have a pair of wireless earbuds, I wanted a separate pair for use with my MacBook & my iMac and not have to pair and unpair it to my iPhone each time I use them.

I considered getting wired headphones for by dynamic sound but they would take up more room in my backpack when I travel/fly and I travel light so decided on these earbuds instead.

Clarity is very good as is its range. I can go to another room while paired to my iMac 25+ feet away and still have a strong connection without any drop out. This is handy to be able to listen while cooking or doing other tasks while away from my Mac.

Lowering volume is done by pressing on the the left earbud control. Raising volume is done by pressing on the right earbud control.

Because their shape at the top where the ear piece loop connects to the volume controls, it's not a gentle curve so is not as comfortable as my other pair. (see photo) The ear piece loop is soft but the controls are in a hard plastic.

When returned to their charger/holder it has a digital display showing the percentage of charge, a real time bar-type display and tiny red lights can be seen on the controls indicating they're being charged. (See photo)

Price point is reasonable and competitive and the quality of construction is fine.

reviews.author: Richard D. Galbreath
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content: These are well made, attractive holder, pair easily with my phone. Music and podcasts sound good through them. I have been using these alot at the gym and my wood shop. A good buy for sure.

reviews.author: Linda Jessica Marquez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 7, 2026
reviews.content: I’ve been using these Occiam wireless sport earbuds for my daily workouts, and they perform great. The hook design feels secure and stays in place while running or moving around. Sound quality is clear with noticeably deep bass, and the noise-cancelling mic works well for quick calls. I like the water resistance because I don’t worry about sweat. The charging case is practical, and the battery lasts several days for me. For the price, they are comfortable, reliable, and ideal for exercise. I recommend them.

reviews.author: Reader's Edyn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 7, 2026
reviews.content: These earbuds sound great, fit well, and stay secure during use. Battery life is excellent, and they work very well overall. The only downside is the volume control: volume up works, but volume down does nothing on my Galaxy Fold 5. Not sure if it’s a compatibility issue or a defect. Still a solid set, but full volume control from the earbuds would make them perfect.

reviews.author: Emma Alanis
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 31, 2025
reviews.content: For its price it doesn’t sound half bad! I have gotten compliments on how much clearer I sound when I play on my consoles with friends. One of my best purchases ever since I can play with comfort and at ease. I like how it has the option to light up different colors, and it’s worth the purchase. Great quality and hasn’t given me any trouble with its battery life. Inly thing is it might sound quiet with its audio at first but you can just adjust that in your settings to your device.

reviews.author: Kyle Hoaks
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 17, 2026
reviews.content: The headphones sound great! Mic is super clear for my friends to hear me. Noise cancelation works as well. And I just love the L.E.D lights on it. These headphones are super awesome

reviews.author: kendra medina
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
