reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 28, 2018
reviews.content:
These headphones are fantastic. I got the 500x model. This might be kinda long so buckle up..

First off,

Sound Quality:
The one thing I heard the most in nearly every single review and unboxing etc etc I could find about these (which was a lot, I researched for a few days) is that the bass is very low so "if you're a bass head they're not for you". This is simply not true. The bass is just fine. It is very slightly light on the lowest end of the spectrum but that's only because of the open back letting out some of the rumble. I have an equalizer program (DFX) where I even set the high mids and full high ends up for FPS games (which is why I got these) and the bass is still very present. So yeah myth busted I guess.

Next thing to note is how crystal clear the sound is. It's a beautiful thing. Music sounds so crisp and all the layers are presented very very well. The highs can be a little too high if there are any shrill noises in what you're listening to. But they never distort in any of the tests I've given them.

Next, positional audio or the sound stage is absolutely legendary. Like I said I bought these primarily to use for combat games (CS:GO, Fortnite, Paladins, etc) where hearing the enemy is very important. These headphones are practically cheating. The direction of sound is so clear that I get confused by all the clearly separate directions the sounds come from. It will take getting used to hearing the in game sounds as if my ears are actually in the game. In fact it's so realistic that I have turned my head to look at the sound multiple times now because I thought someone was actually behind me. It's fantastic.

As for sound leakage, it's not too bad. If whatever you're listening to is very loud it can be heard from a few feet away. But any medium volume stuff can only be heard if somone is directly next to you. High tones tend to be louder in general for these and those always carry more than low tones so usually that is all people will hear if they're nearby. And then for sound coming into the headphones, when nothing is playing you can hear your surroundings just as well as you could wearing no headphones at all. When something is playing though it almost entirely blocks outside noise. It would have to be pretty dang loud around you to be able to drown out whatever you're listening to. But still I probably wouldn't recommend these as ideal for public transportation, classrooms, libraries, or other quiet environments where it might bother people.

Build Quality/Comfort:

These are extremely comfortable. The cloth covered memory foam pads are great. They still get a little warm even though they are so breathable but my head runs pretty hot so I don't think the pads are to blame.
The clamping force is very minimal and fairly evenly distributed around the pads. Slightly more pressure on the bottom under the ear which isn't exactly pleasant but it's not bad enough to care. Just worth noting. Also people said that their ears pressed against the drivers inside the cups and they must have huge ears because mine are pretty big and there's plenty of space in there for me. I have a fairly large head so the pads create a good seal around my ears. I dunno if a smaller noggin would have any issues with the cups laying flush since they don't move at all. I know one of the reviewers I saw had a complaint about that.
Oh and the wings or head flap adjuster things. I was worried a bit that I might hate them and it almost prevented me from even trying them out. But they're awesome. Not having pressure on the crown of my head feels so nice. These things are easy to almost forget about.

As for the build quality, they seem very sturdy. Not like super rugged but they could take a few drops and some bending and twisting and still be perfectly fine. The top bars creak a little if you squeeze them together but never during normal use. The metal mesh looks cool to me and feels nice and sturdy. The cord is thick and feels like it would take a few years worth of normal bending to put a kink in it, and it doesn't feel like normal rubber wire wraps that will fray. It does want to stay coiled a bit like it comes in the package but stretching it into a straight line for a few hours has already made a difference.

The only part that feels moderately weak are the flaps. But they're not flimsy. Just weaker than the rest of the headphones.

So, yeah. I think that's all the stuff.

TL:DR they're effin amazing. Buy them!

reviews.author: Ashley Hinson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 19, 2026
reviews.content: OMG what can I not say. These hand bands are so comfortable, and the battery lasts a very long time. It works over 8 or 9 hours. Sound quality is amazing also. Definitely worth the money.

reviews.author: Laurie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 9, 2025
reviews.content: These bluetooth speaker headbands are incredibly comfortable. I sleep in mine, can't even feel the speakers on my ears. Sound quality is EXCELLENT! They were super easy to pair with my phone. I use mine to help me go back to sleep when I wake up with my nightly bouts of insomnia. I slip on my headband, turn on a deep sleep frequency music channel, and I'm able to drift back to sleep in less than 10 minutes. This is a first for me after years of every other intervention imaginable. These fit my almost-child-sized head without slipping off, and bonus (!) stay exactly where I put them on my ears. The battery is USB-C rechargeable, and lasts me a week of nightly use (4-6 hours per night) before I need to recharge. I have zero complaints about these headbands. They work exactly like I wanted, are easy to set up, sound great, are affordable, are comfortable, and are useful to either entertain me or help me go to sleep without bothering my husband happily snoring away next to me.

reviews.author: Vic
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 6, 2025
reviews.content:
While you need to handle gently for slipping the control module around to line up with the slit in the band to charge, and to softly slide the speakers to be positioned over your ears (gently to protect the speaker wires); it certainly works as advertised. Pairing was simple; just like any other bluetooth device. Both headbands paired instantly.
I slept with them on last night and it was very comfortable; I put the narrow part of the headband on my forehead.
For sleep comfort. these are better than the "in the ear" buds like Mpow30 or Apple AirPods pro 2. Sound is decent. Have my fingers crossed that this will turn out to be durable.

reviews.author: Pat Louden, PCC
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 16, 2025
reviews.content:
Why did you pick this product vs others?:
Not going to lie.  I chose this because it was the only sleep headphone band that had same day delivery. I needed it for a trip I'm leaving on in the morning after realizing I had misplaced my more expensive sleep headband.  I tried this on and it had a partial charge, and I'm really impressed about the fit and sound for the price.  I also love that the charging unit is sewn into place with a small hole for charging so you don't have to remove it from the headband. It's super soft and feels quite comfortable.  The only possible negative is that the headband is not adjustable so it could be too small or too big for some heads.  However, it fits me, and I'm very happy with it.

reviews.author: HonestMom
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 4, 2026
reviews.content: I listen to audiobooks at night to fall asleep, and I leave them on. I cannot have earbuds in my ears. Prior to this wonderful find, I used to keep the phone under my pillow—but different pillows (like at a hotel) let sound through differently, and my husband didn’t like the noise so we had a lot of challenges with volume / snoring! These bands are awesome. They are loose enough to be very comfortable and silent to others. The earphones can be slid around in the headband and the controls are easy. I am so thrilled with these! I have not tried for exercise, but imagine they’d work for that, too!!

reviews.author: Kenneth Thompson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026
reviews.content:
Not a very good first impression. There is virtually no instruction for setup. No explanation as to what the buttons do. No word for even the most basic need: how to turn it off or on. Or how to tell if it’s already off or on.
The speakers are locked in and cannot be moved or adjusted. They are “almost right” but do not quite sit properly over my ears.
The band does fit my rather large head snuggly but comfortably and I do think it will remain in place while sleeping.
It paired quickly and easily. I have no plans for anything other than white noise, or similar, fed from an app through iPhone.
The sound is good, and plenty loud but not painfully so.
I’m a side sleeper and tried them out lying down on my very firm orthopedic pillow. The speaker on my ear gave no discomfort at all from pressure on my ear or the rather loud white noise emitting from it.

I’m unsure if I will keep the bands, mainly due to the simple fact that the vendor couldn’t be bothered with including basic instructions, even the most basic on how to turn it off or on.

I wonder what the chances are of getting a response from the vendor.

reviews.author: Rocio Diaz

reviews.content: These AOC Bluetooth over-ear headphones exceeded my expectations. The battery life is outstanding—I can use them for days without recharging. I also love the option to switch between Bluetooth and wired mode with both 3.5mm and 6.35mm jacks, which makes them very versatile. They are comfortable to wear for long sessions and feel solid and well-built. Highly recommended for the price!

They have a robust build, very decent noise isolation, and powerful bass, making them ideal for DJing or simply enjoying high-energy music. I loved that they included a high-quality coiled cable and a carrying case—details you usually only see with much more expensive brands. They're comfortable for long sessions, and the battery life lives up to expectations. A highly recommended purchase if you're looking for all-around headphones that won't let you down, whether you're on the street or in the DJ booth.

reviews.content: Great quality and value I've come to expect from Bose. The color is a little different in person than online. The challenge is likely my own as I have color vision issues. I have deuteranopia and the 'blue' device looks a bit lavender to me in person.

reviews.author: No name given
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content: Comfortable and delivers quality audio

reviews.author: Kindle Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 26, 2025
reviews.content: Wonderful!

reviews.author: R. Dowling
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 5, 2025
reviews.content: These are good headphones. I bought them this year (2025) to replace the wired equivalent of this very same model that I purchase in 2012. They fit snuggly and are super comfortable and effective with noise reduction. They are easy to use. However, there is one thing missing: the ability to turn off the noise cancelling factor. It has the two levels, 'Quiet' and 'Aware'. But there is no way to use them as headphones without the noise cancelling--meaning to have 3 levels, including 'Off'. I am one of those people who is sensitive to the noise-cancelling "sound". If I have it on too long, it makes my brain and ears feel "tight", for lack of a better description. So for short periods, both levels are great. But even keeping them on the 'Aware' setting (which is lesser intensity of noise cancelling) gives me a headache and brain fog. So I opted instead for Bowers & Wilkins Px7 S2, which are comparable but allow me to still use them as my audio source but with the noise cancelling off. They also have the two levels of when noise cancelling is on. The only thing I don't like about the Bowers & Wilkins headphones by comparison to the Bose headphones is that the B&W case is about 1.5x bigger, so less convenient for travel. (however, I do like that the B&W case is white to match my headphone color instead of black for white headphones)

reviews.author: Lord Fingy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2025
reviews.content: Good pair of earbuds. Extremely long battery life. Once worked a 14 hour shift with them in my ears almost the entire time and they were fine. Would definitely recommend. Sound quality is pretty good for the price and they fit in your ear to stay. I had some connection issues but I'm not sure if that was my phone or the earbuds. They aren't waterproof or resistant so keep that in mind. Ended up falling out while doing dishes. Pulled them out immediately after and they were still irrecoverably damaged.

reviews.author: William Edstrom
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 26, 2025
reviews.content: These sound great, much better than the cheapo ones

reviews.author: cody andrew
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 23, 2026
reviews.content: Sony did it again. Relative to more expensive brands like Jabra, Sony holds its own and has better battery life.

reviews.author: Jorge
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 11, 2025
reviews.content:
I bought these almost two years ago and have been using them consistently. Overall these are pretty good, especially for the price.

A couple of pros: The buds are good quality and they fit in my ears well. Pairing it to my phone is usually quick and easy, and Sony has their own app to configure a couple of settings. Sound quality is great, pretty punchy bass, but it won't blow you away if you're comparing it to other higher end earbuds or headphones. Battery life is good. The buds lately have lasted me around 5-6 hours each, and the case can charge them like 2-3 times before it's dead. USB-C is a plus. They're also very reliable with very few connectivity issues and the batteries have outlasted the ones in my old Airpod Pros.

Cons: The buds have a high center of gravity, and after a while they try to tilt out of my ear. They rarely have fallen out though, but I don't have this issue with other earbuds. The contacts in the case and the buds are a little annoying too. If you don't consistently keep them clean the buds will struggle to connect to the case, so I'm usually wiggling them into place until the orange charge indicator comes on. Frustrating when you leave them in another room and you realize they're still paired to your phone because they didn't detect the case. Also, for whatever reason the buttons on the buds are not configurable, so the right but will always be used to pause/skip songs and the left will be used to change the volume. So if you just have the left bud in, you're going to have to take your phone out or skip the song with an accessory like a smartwatch. Also despite repetitive cleaning, the buttons on the buds are getting harder to push and don't "click" like they used to.

Still, very good value for the money. I still daily these and will until they fail. 4 stars, there's just a few things holding these back.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 15, 2024
reviews.content:
I've purchased cheaper headphones that have an on-par sound quality and noise cancellation AND allow you to pair to multiple devices. I walk places a lot and want to talk on the phone with my headphones, but switching from laptop to phone is an incredibly annoying process. I wish I would have repurchased a cheaper brand. Additionally, they don't FEEL like they last a long time to me. If you're someone who is constantly using your earbuds then this isn't a long-lasting pair like other reviews make you think. It feels like it lasts maybe less than a day and you have to essentially keep the container charged constantly. Annoying.

Overall they're alright in terms of sound quality, but not the best pair of earbuds I've ever owned. They give an annoying buzzing static sound if you have them in your ear without audio playing but if you're older or have not as great hearing then that probably won't be an issue. The inability to easily switch devices is really killing me it's so annoying and the audio quality isn't like some stellar amount that makes me feel like the usability quirks are worth it. Once these die out I'm going to go for a different brand.

These earbuds are just entirely annoying to deal with for me.

UPDATE
I haven't even owned them for a full year and now the right ear bud has died and just flickers in and out but overall won't play audio. I've moved my review to one star, these are really pathetic earbuds, save yourself the money.

reviews.author: Paul D
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 9, 2025
reviews.content: I love these earbuds. Very versatile, good battery, life, and a fairly decent app to help augment sound. I originally bought a pair for my wife. She uses while exercising. I tried them and liked them enough to buy my self ASAP that I use for work. Highly recommend.

reviews.author: Nichole E.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 15, 2023
reviews.content:
These are ok. I thought the Sont name would make them great but not the case. The right one usually says its charged at about 70% even after being in the charger case all day and the left one says its at 100%. Just hope they last. Otherwise they are ok. 3 stars for uncertainty. People on ther end can hear me well when making calls so that is a plus. Just that charging issue is my only complaint.

So now under 3 months of using these and i dont use them alot so not alot of use...these now dont work at all. They will connect but dont play anything or cant use for calls. Unable to contact anyone so i recommend NOT buying these. The Sony same doesnt work here. These seem like knock offs and not real Sony products. If they would replace or refund id change my review.

reviews.author: Chuck
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 14, 2024
reviews.content: I purchased these headphones to replace an identical set that I had been using for several years. Why...you ask? Because I didn't follow the manufacturer's advice and left them in a pair of jeans that went through a washing machine cycle AND a dryer cycle before they were discovered by my wife banging around in the dryer. They were still in their charging case and believe it or not, they still worked perfectly when I turned them on. The only reason I had to order a new set was that the charging case they were in didn't survive...and a replacement case was no longer available from Sony.

reviews.author: Frabbit
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 24, 2025
reviews.content:
Its pretty amazing what they are selling for this price now, other than true audiophiles this is more than satisfactory sound for most people. Its comfortable, generous very soft padding and space for ears so no pressure points, oval ear cup, very deep for ear clearance, but less initially wide than the Tozo HT3 which has a wider less oval oval, but less padding with shallower depth, the Jbuds Lux also has thinner padding. That being said they are all comfortable to wear for all but the biggest ears.

Sound is on the bass emphasized side on the default EQ, the bass is a bit artificially boosted like a bass boost button, unlike the Tozo HT3 which is balanced, the Jbuds Lux just sounds a bit thinner while being balanced, they all sound good don't get me wrong, these are quibbles between very good units all good for their price.

Range is above average, from a long range 1 watt class 1 transmitter(most usb nubs are class 2 so less so) I got to around 70 feet, the Lux was about the same, the Tozo basically doubled it.

It lacks a 3.5 headphone jack, useful for things like electronic keyboards, only the Tozo HT3 includes this. All three act as usb C sound cards though so you can use them wired on the computer through the USB jack.

Build quality is impressive, matte plastic looks high end, very smooth joints, the padding is plush and generous, the pads look very soft, makes the other two look like cheap plastic in comparison. Its mid bulk, the Tozo is more bulbous, and the jlabs is most compact. All wear comfortably for hours.

All 3 have excellent ANC, silencing the air purifier/PC fan noise, only higher frequency wind/road noise/keyboard clacks slightly gets through.

All three have fully featured apps, the Tozo has things like voice control, but I personally haven't bothered with it. None force a sign on to use the app, Baseaus lets you use a guest mode if you don't want to login.

Battery life is exceptional like the BH1, the LUX has slightly less run time.

They seem durable enough, but they are plastic, I wouldn't sleep in them.

All are multi-point, making controlling via the phone app easy while still connected to PC

These are all great values for the price, a few years ago this would easily be double the price if not more.

reviews.author: Tech Guy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 13, 2026
reviews.content:
I didn't expect much at this price point so I was truly surprised how good the sound quality was. I really can’t tell a big difference between this and my Sony WH-1000XM5, which is pretty impressive considering Sony costs $200. Excellent customer service. I reached out to them with a question and they responded quickly

Pros – Sound quality. Long battery charge - I had it now for couple of months and only had to charge it once even with frequent use. Very comfortable to wear. Good button placement. Easy to on/off take to change NC mode. Good nose cancellation (not great but good enough for the price point). Five stars all the way!

Cons – LDAC only possible with the App. (No, I’m not loading a Chinese app on my phone). With WH-1000XM5 the LDAC can be selected from Bluetooth settings without using any App. I’m not too concerned about this considering how good the sound quality is even without that.

reviews.author: Schulzy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 8, 2025
reviews.content:
I own over 40 pairs of ANC headphones ranging from $12-$100.00 that I have either purchased or received from the Amazon Vine program. I am always on the lookout for new pairs. When the Baseus BH1 NC appeared I ordered them for comparison. At that time they were on an introductory sale of $19.99. They arrived quickly and easily paired to my Motorola G 5G (2023) and Nvidia Shield Pro.

Basic Specs: Bluetooth 6.0. USB-C. 55-80HR Playtime. 40mm Drivers. Multipoint Connection. SBC/AAC/LDAC Codecs. App support.

Notes: They have a very sleek design that is understated. The headband has a textured fabric underside similar to the JLab Lux ANC. They are a tight fit on my 23" head at full extension and the headband ripples across the top. The ear cushions are comfortable. The buttons are small but spaced well and easy to locate by touch. There are voice prompts for Power/Connect/ANC. I tested the sound with my usual three songs: Sleep Token - "The Summoning", Lauren Daigle - "Kaleidescope Jesus", and Chris Botti - "My Funny Valentine". They offer a clean/balanced sound. With the Super Bass enabled they have excellent punch that rivals my Skullcandy Hesh ANC. The ANC works well in reducing air conditioner, dishwasher, and background TV noise. There is a Transparency mode. The app has a very clean layout and is easy to navigate with options for ANC, EQ, LDAC, and Multipoint. My other deduction is for lack of a storage bag/case.

Final Ratings: Style 4/5. Build 4/5. Battery 4/5. Comfort 4/5. Controls 4/5. Sound 5/5. ANC 4/5. App 5/5. Value 4/5.

reviews.author: Edgardo Bustamante
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 11, 2025
reviews.content:
I got mine yesterday and I used them this morning for my morning commute from Brooklyn to Jersey on public tranportation: I am not an audiophile but these sounds pretty darn good. They have bass boost on by default. I downloaded the app and set it up after pairing to both my phones. Everything below are settings found and configured within the app:

There are 3 different ANC modes: Normal, Transparency, and Noise Reduction. I left it on normal as I didn't like the other two, and later I turned it off completely because the snug clamping pressure of the headphones and they way it covers my ears completely pretty much sealed off losts of ambient noise. They had 3 Spacial Acoustics: Normal, Music, Cinema. I left it on Normal as the other two sounded weird. EQ mode has lots of presets but I left it on default because I listen to mostly hip-hop so the other EQ profiles didn't work for me. There is an option to create a custom EQ (but because I'm not an audiophile, I didn't mess with it). I connected to both my phones; personal iPhone 14, and work iPhone 13. Follow the instructions and do one first, then turn of bluetooth on the first one, then connect to the second one, then turn bluetooth on the first one again and both will be connected at the same time. There is a Low Latency Mode which I did not toggle on because I don't really watch videos on my phone during my commute as I tend to get motion sickness while reading or watching something while in a moving vehicle. LDAC is an option as well, but all my audio is streaming from my self-hosted music library at 320kbps max. If you enable LDAC mode, you lose out on dual-device connection, spatial audio, and Low-Latency mode. There is a sleep mode, which looks like it's white noise or something to put on while taking a nap or something, I gotta try it out later.

I will note as well: Right when I was arriving to work on my last bus, I noticed a odd sound like a wobble. I was leaning up against the window and I think the bus's vibrations was messing with the ANC so I turned it off and it went away. There is also a particular spot at Port Authority on 40th and 8th Ave. in front of Starbucks where my Google Pixel Buds Pro would spazz out and get staticky when crossing the street. It happened too with AirPods Pro. I think there are a lot of devices in that area that cause interference but I did not get that weirdness with these headphones on.

reviews.author: Marc Windheuser
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 5, 2026
reviews.content: Very good sound. Battery lasts a long time. Noise canceling works well. Just as good as more expensive brands in my opinion. I ended getting Everyone in my family a set.

reviews.author: EP4444
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 29, 2025
reviews.content: They sound OK. Decent for this price point but not a sound that impressed me at all. The ear cups are soft and comfortable, design is a bit bulky. Connects very easily, app is user friendly. If I did it over, I’d probably invest a little more money and buy a tier or two above these in brand/quality for better sound.

reviews.author: Amazonian Flier
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 16, 2025
reviews.content:
I picked up the Baseus BH1 Bluetooth headphones after seeing the Prime coupon drop the price to about thirty dollars, and honestly, the experience has been phenomenal from the moment they arrived next-day. The build quality feels far more premium than the price suggests, the compact size makes them easy to carry, and they slipped right into my daily routine with zero fuss.

The biggest surprise is the sound quality. These easily compete with headphones in the $250–$300 range. With a bit of EQ tuning in the Baseus app, the BH1 delivers clarity, presence, and bass impact on par with my Sony WH-XB910N - but with noticeably higher maximum volume. The bass stays deep and satisfying, only edging into distortion at the absolute top end, and even that can be avoided with the hearing-protection feature or simply dialing back one volume notch. Connectivity is also great - very easy to pair it up and configure via the Baseus app.

The noise cancelling itself shocked me. It’s genuinely right there with my Sony. It cuts out low-frequency rumble extremely well, and the adaptive system adjusts in real time exactly as you’d expect from a much higher-end model. Transparency mode is also excellent and sounds natural, making quick conversations or situational awareness easy and seamless. Everything just works the way it should.

One of the standout features is the hearing-protection option in the Baseus app. It lets you set safe decibel limits in a way I’ve never seen on Sony’s or Anker’s apps. Add in a five-band EQ, bass boost, latency adjustments, and enhanced Bluetooth audio options on Android (not available with iOS), and the level of customization is genuinely rare at this price point.

Battery life is fantastic. You can get up to 80 hours with ANC off and around 55 with ANC on. A quick 10-minute charge gives you roughly 10 hours of playback, which is perfect for travel or long days.

Comfort is solid, especially considering the more compact design. Adults will need to extend the sliders, but once adjusted, the earcups seal comfortably and fully around the ear. The protein-leather cushions are soft and skin-friendly, making them easy to wear for long listening sessions.

Packaging is simple but attractive - clearly designed to keep costs down while still giving you a pleasant unboxing. The only thing missing is a basic drawstring carrying pouch, which would have enhanced the overall experience without adding much cost.

Key specs for my fellow Amazon shoppers:

Real-Time Adaptive Hybrid ANC (up to –45 dB reduction)

Transparency mode

Hi-Res & Hi-Res Wireless certified

LDAC support for high-bitrate audio (Android only)

SuperBass 2.0 dynamic bass enhancement

Bio-cellulose dynamic drivers

Bluetooth 6.0

80 hours playtime (ANC off) / 55 hours (ANC on)

5-mic AI ENC system for clear calls

Fast charging: 10 minutes = 10 hours playback

I love discovering and sharing great products, and this is genuinely one of my best purchases of 2025. I bought these with my own money, and they absolutely earned my stamp of approval.

If you want great sound, strong ANC, deep customization, and unbeatable value, you can buy these with confidence.

Highly recommended - cheers!

reviews.author: ray9279
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 19, 2025
reviews.content:
I am honestly blown away by the value of these Baseus Bass BH1 Lite headphones. If you are a "basshead" on a budget, look no further—these punch way above their weight class.

Why They’re a 5-Star Win:
The 80-Hour Battery is Real: I’ve been using these for over a week for several hours a day, and the battery is still going strong. The fact that you can get 10+ hours of playback from just a 10-minute charge is a lifesaver when you're heading out the door.

Thumping Bass: The "SuperBass" claim isn't just marketing. The 40mm drivers deliver a deep, vibrating low-end that makes EDM and Hip-Hop sound incredible. If the bass is too much, you can easily tweak it in the Baseus App.

Cloud-Like Comfort: These are surprisingly lightweight (only about 210g). The memory foam earcups are super soft and don't pinch your ears, which makes them great for long study sessions or flights.

Reliable Connection: With Bluetooth 6.0, the pairing is instant and I haven't experienced a single dropout. The Low Latency mode is also a huge plus—no "lip-sync" delay when watching movies or gaming.

Crystal Clear Calls: The AI noise reduction for the mic works wonders. I’ve taken calls on a windy street and the person on the other end could hear me perfectly.

Verdict:
For a "Lite" version, these feel anything but cheap. They are sturdy, stylish, and the battery life is industry-leading at this price point.

reviews.author: Roger
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2026
reviews.content: Took a while to figure out how to connect it to my Samsung smart TV but once I got that the performance has been flawless. AND, the wife isn't constantly complaining I have the TV too loud!

reviews.author: W. Smith
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 23, 2023
reviews.content: I ordered these highly-rated headphones on Amazon early in December, from a third-party vendor which was to have shipped them directly via USPS, but alas -- I never received them. A week after I ordered them, my Amazon tracking window reported this: “An exceptional incident has occurred.” The exceptional incident was not explained, but a subsequent chat with Amazon concluded that my headphones had been “lost” in transit. Translation: they were undoubtedly shipped in their original branded packaging and were stolen somewhere in transit. Subsequently and fortunately, I found them available at a local Best Buy for the same price as advertised on Amazon, and having just connected them to my new tv, I can vouch for their quality, which is top-notch. I bought them to pair with a brand-new Samsung QLED 4K Q80C Smart TV and a Samsung HW-Q800C 5.1.2ch Soundbar (both purchased from Amazon), and the Main Purpose of this review is to help other users sort the intricacies of PAIRING and using these headphones successfully with this Samsung tv and soundbar combo. I was initially advised by Samsung to connect the headphone docking station to the tv (not the soundbar), and that sound from the tv-soundbar would need to be manually turned off when using the headphones. I had purchased a high-quality optical cable for this connection but was initially frustrated in my attempts to silence the tv-soundbar output while using the headphones. The simplistic black remote control sticks which came with the tv and the soundbar were useless in figuring out this trick. Their sound settings were of no help whatsoever. But fortunately, I had also purchased a universal remote (the Vtinvan BN59-01315A) also available here on Amazon, which has an “Expert Settings” feature. That solved my problem. What you will likely need to do is go into this settings menu and (1) select the PCM digital audio output, (2) turn the “simultaneous optical output” to “on”, and then use either remote control to turn the tv set sound to its lowest setting in order to mute the sound coming from the tv-soundbar while listening to the very high quality sound from the Insignia headphones. BUT, Do NOT apply the Mute button on the remote to cancel sound from the tv. That will turn off ALL of the sound output. The volume control on the Insignia headphones then works perfectly to adjust the output from the headphones. It’s a bit ironic that you need all this gadgetry to make RF earphones work properly with a “smart” tv, but then, what else is new? But as for the headphones themselves, they certainly live up to their online reviews as one of the best headphones available for comfort, simplicity and pure clarity of sound. For me, the sound from these headphones is even superior to the surround sound generated by the tv-soundbar combo alone. I can highly recommend them. But be careful how and where you order them.

reviews.author: PARISS
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 16, 2024
reviews.content:
When they work, the sound quality is excellent.
However, I have difficulty believing that whatever fault they had originally was, in fact, cured.
The sound cuts in and out and could be corrected by restarting (sometimes more than once).
Because of the limited warranty, getting a replacement was not an option.

Now, the headset will only stay on for a matter of moments before cutting out completely.
I have now written off the expense as a lesson learned and ordered a brand-new headset.

Let the buyer beware: the refurbished items from this vendor may be a risk that is not worth taking!

reviews.author: tbone
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 3, 2022
reviews.content: These are great headphones for TV listening. I have a TCL Roku TV in a guest room that has no bluetooth. I tried using an aptx low latency bluetooth transmitter/receiver with like headphones and every combination ended up with a significant audio lag time. Part of this problem is there are many bluetooth headphones and ear buds that say they are low latency but are actually not. My transmitter verifies this and after buying multiple ear buds that didn’t work I decided to try these RF types instead. Perfect right out of the box. Hooked them up in minutes and no audio lag at all. They are very comfortable with very good sound, do a decent job of cancelling external sound, and volume can be controlled with your TV remote. There are several hook up up options and I used the 3.5m jack on the TV. I did get an open box item for $20 that was like new which makes this a truly great deal. These go for up to $120 on some sites. Prices on Amazon fluctuate between $20 for open box to $95 new. Open box on eBay routinely go for under $25. It does appear the battery cannot be replaced but with the amount I plan to use them I estimate they will last for 3-5 years.

reviews.author: Christine
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 16, 2025
reviews.content: Felt it wasn’t clearly advised it was a refurbished item. Taking a star off for sending me an email to make sure I was ok with it being used and will hold order. Then several hours later shipped item without hearing from me. I did appreciate the optical cable included, even though it said not included. The headphones were easy to set up with optical cable. I just needed to buy an optical adapter with 2 connections for headphones and sound bar. This is for my elderly mom and she loves them. Just getting used to the buttons, since you can’t really see them. She has neuropathy in finger tips and can’t feel the volume buttons. But in the end we are happy with them.

reviews.author: Vanessa Vasquez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026
reviews.content: My 84 year old Dad loves this item! He loves it so much that he doesn’t have to have his hearing aids on when he’s watching TV . Grateful for the quietness in the house.👍🙏🏼

reviews.author: G. Gunn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 10, 2021
reviews.content: We have used Insignia headphones for years, and they are usually easy to install and work just fine ... except that one has to purchase an optical cable not included. I have purchased a box that changes from analog to digital on previous headphones I have purchased. C'mon folks. Who uses analog sound these days? Yet that is what comes in the box, and you have to purchase the optical cable in order to plug them into any smart TV. Recently, I purchased a set from Best Buy (the last ones on the shelf), but returned them because the sound faded in and out over the headset. I purchased a set from Amazon, and the exact same thing happened. Both boxes were not new, and had obviously been returned. Amazon sent another pair and the same thing happened. Finally, I tried them on another TV in another part of the house. They work fine. The old pair I was replacing worked fine on the TV in the living room, but I haven't found a pair that will work on that TV. I will look for a brand other than Insignia. Although it had previously been a great brand, I have not been happy that they won't work on the living room TV. And both had been returned. Why would you put headphones that were returned back on the shelf to send to another customer?

reviews.author: Cathy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 6, 2025
reviews.content: I am very pleased with these TV headphones! I bought them for my 94-year-old mom, who's nearly deaf, and they've been a game-changer. The sound quality is crystal clear, and she can finally hear dialogue from her favorite TV shows without straining. The setup was a breeze, and the headphones are super comfortable to wear. I'm also impressed with the battery life - it's exceeded my expectations. These headphones have brought so much joy back to my mom's TV-watching experience, and I'm grateful for the peace of mind they bring me. Highly recommend!"

reviews.author: Alberto Florez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.content: Easy to pair and the ways to use it is pretty unique and useful (volume control). Sound wise they're good. I love this color, like chocolate but the the skull more visible compared to white and black. This don't feel cheap. The fit is good, but ears don't feel as comfortable after a while. I wish you could fold them flat to wear it on neck easier for a stylish look if intended to use as accesory.

reviews.author: Andres A.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 20, 2025
reviews.content:
I’ve been using these Skullcandy Icon ANC headphones daily, and honestly — they deliver way more than I expected. The sound is clean, with punchy bass and clear highs, perfect for music, podcasts, or even calls.

The Active Noise Cancelling (ANC) works really well. It blocks out background noise without making you feel completely isolated. Great for staying focused when I’m working or at the gym.

The battery life is a beast — I’ve gone several days without charging, and they just keep going. Skullcandy says 60 hours, and I believe it.

They’re super comfortable for long sessions, and the on-ear design is surprisingly light. Plus, they fold up easily and feel really durable.

I also appreciate the sweat and water resistance — I’ve used them during workouts with no issues at all.

If you’re looking for wireless headphones that are reliable, sound great, and don’t die on you midweek, these are totally worth it.

Battery life is solid and the Bluetooth connection is fast and stable. For the price, you really can’t go wrong. Totally recommended.

reviews.author: Camilo Vargas
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 4, 2025
reviews.content: In my opinion, good product according to the price , (I bought them in offer), good design. Small, good quality sound, noise cancellation is acceptable , long battery use. I use them for gym and are awesome , also the microphones are good for making and receiving phone calls, they have a “joystick” that helps to control volume and songs

reviews.author: Aaron
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 22, 2025
reviews.content:
Sound is clear and clean but I wish it had a bit better bass. Controls are easy to use and feel. Tactical feedback is nice. It can pair to multiple devices at a time which is nice but you have to stop using one in order to get sound from the other but honestly I could live with that. Unless youre trying to multitask on multiple devices it shouldn't really be a deal breaker. The fit is a little smaller than what id prefer so if you have a smaller head, these are perfect. The design is actually pretty nice and the material is well made, honestly better looking and well made than most headphones at this price. Its also nice that it gives you the percentage of the battery when turned on so that way you know how much life youre getting per use. 7-8/10 in my opinion.

Update: found out that the skullcandy app allows for EQ adjusting so that immediately made it better in terms of sound quality.

reviews.author: Anonymous
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 30, 2025
reviews.content: Excellent mat black with yellowish highlights and shiny black Skullcandy logo. Sounds are vibrant and clear. Volume and media controls are quick and effortless with the adaption of a joystick with internal buttons, you won't have stick drift and all you need is to tilt the stick in one direction a lil. The noise cancelling without audio blocks out almost all noises, and with audio playing, completely blocks out all noise. Stay aware provides clear audio of the outside. Both modes work excellently. The ear pads and headband are cozy enough to fall asleep in.

reviews.author: Heidi W.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 8, 2026
reviews.content: I bought these for my 19 year old son. He had the black ones as well. He loves them and the high quality they offer. He is also a big music guy and knows a lot about sound quality.

reviews.author: Morenaa
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 24, 2025
reviews.content: After about 4 years, and officially losing one of my Beats fit pro earbuds- I decided to just venture out and try something new all together. I’ve used Skullcandy earbuds back in my high school days, and they were always one of the best imo (back then, idk about now lol). Now I knew this item in particular wasn’t even going to compare with beats brand, but I wanted something cheaper and decent to have, until I have enough spending money to get better ones. This was my first headphone purchase with them. I decided to go with Skullcandy, since it’s a familiar brand that I’ve trusted. Although, I’ve only had their earbuds, and it’s been years. Sad to say, I am highly disappointed. LISTEN TO THE REVIEWS! The product is super overpriced for the performance, and quality of said product. The ANC is a joke! Literally a hair of a difference between awareness mode and ANC. The sound overall is just HORRID! Even with fooling around with the equalizer and trying to customize the sound, it was beyond underwhelming/frustrating. The bass was terrible. I had to turn it damn near all the way down to not get annoyed. The louder you turn it up, the worse it sounds. Listening to music with these was not a pleasant experience. Every minute or few seconds, it sounds static-y. I’ve had grocery store headphones better than these ones. I love skull candy, but this one is a flop. I cannot speak for any of their other models of headphones, but this one is just not even up to par with the cheap ones I’ve had. I wanted to like these so bad, but they just didn’t work for me. I usually will just take the L and make the best of it, but I couldn’t justify that with the price in these. I ended up returning it. I thought about trying their other models, but I just coughed up the money for the AirPods Max, and they’ve been apart of me ever since lol.

reviews.author: yakob
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 5, 2026
reviews.content: Seriously love these headphones. They stay on during my workouts really well, not too tight but comfortable. Really good sound quality too. Really good value for money.

reviews.content: Very nice illustrations with characters of all shades, and both male and female.

reviews.author: The Kevster
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2025
reviews.content:
So this is another stop on my journey to review random wired earbuds. I reviewed the less expensive philips and was impressed. At twice the price, are these an improvement? Absolutely. These use the same 1 button remote and mic which function but other headsets in this range have better functionality with their remote/mic. But the sound quality is where these shine. If you want full, non-fatiguing sound and a very comfortable earbud, these are for you.

Construction:
These feel durable enough. The cable is thick enough that I think it could take some abuse. The buds are light and look attractive with the shiny metallic-y backs. They are on par with other earbuds in this price range.

Features:
These have a functioning remote and mic, but their use is limited. The remote is a single button that will play/pause music and answer/hang up phone calls. You can't change the volume or change tracks like other sets around this price. The mic works but your caller won't be able to hear you super well. They include extra eartip sizes.

Comfort:
These are very comfortable for in-ear IEMs. I used the largest tips provided for my big ears and I got a tight seal and then they disappeared. It's also a very secure fit so I could imagine using these in the gym or while on a jog 10 years ago before true-wireless.

Sound Quality:
If this is your price range and you seek the most balanced sound in this price range, then these are my top recommendation. These sound great and I legitimately prefer them to sets that are twice the price or even more. The bass does have a subtle bump but I wouldn't consider these bass forward. The bass is full and rhythmic and will have you bobbing your head. The mids are very clear and natural. Instruments and voices sound more realistic with these compared to other sets at this price point. Highs are a little soft compared to the bass and mids but this makes them super easy to listen to. There is no harshness or sharpness that will hurt your ears and you can listen for hours.

Even with the subpar remote and mic, these are my favorite earbuds out of 5 that I've tested in the $10-$40 range. These are winners.

reviews.author: 1Dub79
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 31, 2019
reviews.content:
I was looking for a pair of earbuds with a mic. I stumbled upon the Performance PRO1605. I was surprised to see some small earbuds were advertised, as Hi-Res Audio. Not to mention, I was shocked by the price tag. Therefore, I was willing to take a chance on Philips, as I already own their superb, SHP9500.

DESIGN: The PRO6105 has a nice and shiny, metallic design on the outside of the earphones. On the inside, is a plastic, navy blue surface with the L and R on their corresponding sides. This is located beneath the ear tips. In addition, the PRO6105 comes with three (S,M,L) interchangeable ear tips. The large ear tips on the earbuds by default. The ear tips fit snug inside my ears. No worry about them slipping out. The cord is like any other cord for headphones. However, on the left side of the headphones is a microphone. On the right side is a rubber clip, which organizes the cords in a neat fashion.

SOUND: The PRO6105 has 8.6mm neodymium drivers, which is above average for most earbuds. The frequency response is 7-40,000hz, which is Hi-Res Audio. The SHP9500 is not, as they are 12-35,000hz The sensitivity is 109 db, compared to the SHP9500’s 101 db. Of course, you cannot compare earphones to headphones, but in the case of the PRO6105 in comparison to the SHP9500, that is impressive. The impedance is 16 ohms. The maximum input power is only 20mW. For some earbuds, they have superb, crystal clear and clean sound. The highs and mids are great. The lows are pretty good, as well. No distortion. The bass is deep, clean, clear but not overdone. Of course, most Hi-Res audio is not bass-heavy. With that said, if you are a bass-head, these headphones are not for you. Beats by Dre might be, however, In addition to everything, the noise-cancellation is great on these earbuds. I played some music, while the TV was up and could only hear the music.

The music sounds excellent. Of course, these earbuds are made, primarily for that. Words sound crystal clear. The detail of background sounds i.e. instruments, drums, etc. of the music are heard, crystal clear. The treble is nice. Good bass, as I mentioned before. Out of curiosity, I performed a test of some games. I played an old, saved video of Hitman 2 on PSN. There was a part, where a massive explosion takes place. The explosion was punchy, along with the sound of debris, hitting the floor, sounded loud and amazingly, clear. The sounds around the environment, sounded clear, as well. I heard birds chirping, conversations of NPCs around the map, footsteps, etc. I was impressed. With that said, if you choose to game on PS4 with these, the controller can drive these earbuds with ease. In fact, I had the controller volume set to max but had to set it to level seven. The sound was still, ambient enough, without feeling, as if my eardrums were going to burst.

MIC: On PSN, I did a test for the adjust mic settings. I could hear my voice, crystal clear. Also, for personal calls, I could hear their voice, crystal clear and they could hear mine. Plus, these earbuds have echo cancellation. Also, for personal calls, with the press of a button, music can be paused to accept phone calls.

VERDICT: Considering these are Hi-Res, along with the accessories, which come with them, they are a STEAL for only twenty bucks. Not to mention, they can compete with some top headphones out there. Great for music and surprisingly, good for gaming.

reviews.author: Jeremy Gorrell
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 30, 2025
reviews.content:
I would describe these as "typical" for wired earbuds. Perfectly acceptable ear fit, perfectly acceptable cord and plug, perfectly acceptable construction, and perfectly acceptable sound. The bass tones are a little thin and anemic but honestly, you can't expect much bass from lightweight in-ear buds like these so I don't count that against them.
Honestly, I got exactly what I expected based on the product description. No surprises, no disappointment, just perfectly average earbuds. I have no complaints whatsoever.

reviews.author: Bill Meyer - KMED
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 26, 2025
reviews.content: Absolutely fantastic set of earbuds. My challenge has been finding an affordable set of earbuds which sound clean and don't suffer from muddy rap-style bass "Thumpa Thumpa-itis". This set exceeds my expectations. Highs are clear and clean and bass is strong but DEFINED and clean, no mud These Phillip Pros are so good that I'm buying a couple more as backups just in case Phillips were to stop making them. Oh, easy to wear for long periods of time, mucho comfortable.

reviews.author: Herz aus Metal
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 9, 2019
reviews.content:
Ok, these muggers just arrived, so this is a first listen review - not a longevity assessment.

I'm using the earbud covers that the item arrived wearing. They seemed to fit fine. As humans, we have variances, so wear whichever is most comfortable - you have three choices.

Now, on to the music. I'm playing from a Sony mp3 player. Before these, I used the OEM earbuds for about 10 years, so I'm pretty good with the care of my electronics. When you see the pictures, you'll understand why I love this old mp3 player. The earbuds, the SOUND, is better than a pair of $100 Audio Technica that I tried about a week ago. They didn't impress me as being as good as the OEM Sonys. For that amount of money, they should blow me away. For $19.90, these Philips give me bass, more mid than I ever heard before, and I have listened to three different kinds of music so far, and all have been satisfying. (I only wish they made players LOUDER - I am the type that would be fine with dying if I could hear music for an eternity.)

So, Eisbrecher's Schlachtbank played GREAT. It is a fantastic production, so it would be hard not to play loud enough. Then, Jarvis' Left Overs. Very good! The Go-Go's Our Lips Are Sealed was also very good, except for the part where she sings kind of "dirty/rough" toward the beginning didn't give me that goosebump feeling.

Anyway, if you have a good amp, these will rock quite well for less than $20. I would probably even pay $40-45 for these. And I'm pretty cheap & critical. LOL These kick those Audio Technica's behind, and I am so glad I took a leap and got these. If you see no follow up review, it means there's no problems with their longevity. So far, I don't see any defects. HAPPY - H - yeah! (I bought these because I had an old Philips shelf system that I dearly loved and it kicked.)

OhMG, Siouxsie and the Banshee's Hong Kong Garden just came on, Kevin Shields' strings remix, and it is f-ng crystal!!!! Front 242's Geography II is also coming through clear and precise.

reviews.author: Dude
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 23, 2026
reviews.content: The sound was different volumes levels between the two sides. I had these in the past and they were decent. But when I bought again recently they were defective. Clearly they’re cutting corners and don’t do quality checks. Returning and will never buy this product again

reviews.author: Jeff R
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 5, 2024
reviews.content:
There are two things you need to know about these buds. First, they have very good sound quality. Good bass, not tinny and the highs are clear and not shrill , unlike some other brands I have tried. Secondly, I have to rave about the fit, these stay in place! The earbuds don't move around in my ears which means the sound stays consistent. I also find that they're great for using in bed. They don't stick out and you hardly even notice that you're wearing them.
I am also very impressed with their performance. I listen to mostly heavy metal. The first song I ever listened to with these was Aces High by Iron Maiden and I was blown away. Great for movies too.
They're definitely a good value for your money. So far no issues with durability.

reviews.author: Mantik-X
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 31, 2022
reviews.content:
Arrives in a 2.5" x 6.5" box - could've used less materials - but presentation counts

CONSTRUCTION:
They have the generic dark grey rubbery plastic buds out of the box and as replacements included - in case you lose one .

I like the smooth non-stick rubber shielding around the cables.

The cable connections to all the components look like pretty solid (except to the mic section). Other than that area, It doesn't appear that normal "caring" wear and tear will yank wires loose.

The ear bud , mic section and jack hardware are made of black plastic

PERFORMANCE:

Regardless of what music I like to listen to, I like using recordings of real physical musical instruments as a reference to judge audio quality of hardware - so I'm using a meditation sound track of crystal chimes being struck and quartz crystal bowls being rubbed for these ...

That high frequency "Tinggggg-NNNNNN-ingggggggg-NNNNNNN-inggggg reverberating strike sound (of the crystal chimes) and the quartz crystal bowls sound good - not spectacular - there's not as much mid to deep lows as I've experienced with some other brands. But, hey, what if these are more accurate and the others are over enhancing the original recording?

Noise Cancellation - not great but adequate

Volume - I've had other brands that can go higher than this one (need less battery power to drive the mini-speakers) - but it's still good.

reviews.author: RRA
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 23, 2026
reviews.content:
The best dj headphone I've ever owned in my life.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026
reviews.content: Budget friendly and works pretty good

reviews.author: william meyer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 23, 2023
reviews.content:
I bought obviously for dj usages and already did a small test and put it up against my old but still functioning customize sony mdr v700 and a sennheiser hd 25 . And this is my verdict, first all headphones are great.
- The cue1 sounds more bass punchy for my taste but tolerable, mids and highs sounds a little bit flat and in the lower end But not bad.
Great construction and looks like heavy duty gear. And they are incredibly comfortable and lightweight. Good on ear headphones

- against the sony mdr v700.
The sony has for me better mid-highs and the bass is not as punchy but sounds in a perfect sweet spot. But they are heavy and not as robust as some newer headphones still they are great over the ear headphones and a good classic earpiece also repairable.

- against the sennheiser hd 25
This are one of the most standards dj on ear headphones in the market right now. They sound great all across the board and all frequency are on point but are alot more expensive. but the cue1 doesn't have to Envy them on nothing with it's highly customizable parts and detachable cable are a good advantage agaist the Sennheiser. Although they sound quite different in there frequencies both are highly workable in my opinion

Hope this was a good review for those that are considering buying this headphones

Lets see how do they behave in the long run especially there durability

reviews.content: Great headphones! Fits perfect to my head. The over the ear piece isn’t too big which is was I was looking for. Something perfect to fit over my ears with out being too large

It's best if you looked into other pioneer headphones that go over ear! Overall these headphones are worth the price, but invest a few extra $$ on comfy ones

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content: Exelente.

reviews.author: Christian Pean
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 15, 2025
reviews.content: The quality of these were the best. The size and fit was perfect I could adjust it however needed. Play on one ear or the other. Sound quality was insanely good. I would use these normally lol. Super durable don’t try to break though. Definitely worth what I paid.

reviews.author: Michael Jordan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 6, 2025
reviews.content:
The materials used are what you'd expect from a very cheap set of over ear headphones. They're very light, usually more expensive gear is heavier, it's both a good and bad thing. The overall design is okay, it's just the materials holding it together aren't the best, so the way things stretch and bend feel like a low end product. The size is regular for a mid size over ear headphone. The sound quality isn't great, but it's decent, and it gets loud enough.

So you're probably asking yourself, then why the 5 star rating? I have been a DJ for over 20 years and was in the market for a new set of headphones. I could have spent the normal amount 180-220 price range I would normally spend on some headphones, but decided to give this a try. I usually buy Pioneer headphones, so I didn't really hesitate after seeing the price and description. It actually exceeded my expectations. Even though they feel really cheap (because they are), they very much do what I need them to do for DJing purposes. I don't expect them to last forever, as they never do. But if these last a while, they are a less expensive maintenence cost as a DJ. Futhermore, the people that complain about the cushion falling apart, that happens with all the brands I've seen. I've noticed it happens with really sweaty people.

reviews.author: Daniella
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 8, 2025
reviews.content:
I bought the ProCase Kids Noise Cancelling Headphones in Red for my 4-year-old daughter, who is autistic and sensitive to loud noises like monster truck shows or fireworks, and these have been the best solution for helping her stay calm and comfortable in noisy environments.

Design and Features: These earmuffs are designed for kids with a padded headband and soft ear cushions, offering a noise reduction rating suitable for sensory sensitivities. The bright red color is appealing to my daughter, and the adjustable headband fits her small head snugly without slipping. They're lightweight and fold compactly for storage in a backpack or drawer, making them easy to carry for outings.

Performance and Comfort: These headphones are a game-changer—my daughter wears them during loud events, and they significantly dampen sounds like truck roars or sudden bangs, reducing her sensory overload. She says they’re comfortable with no pain, even after hours of wear, and they don’t press too hard on her ears. The soft padding avoids irritation, and I love how easy they are for her to put on herself, supporting her independence.

Durability and Ease: The build is sturdy, holding up to her active movements without cracking, and they’re easy to wipe clean. Storing them away is simple due to the foldable design, fitting neatly in our home. They've been reliable for multiple uses without losing effectiveness.

Pros:
Effective noise reduction for autistic kids’ sensory needs.
Comfortable, no-pain fit for long wear.
Easy for toddlers to use and adjust.
Compact and durable for storage and travel.

Final Thoughts: These ProCase headphones are the best for my 4-year-old’s sensory challenges, making loud events manageable with their comfort and ease. I love how they help her enjoy outings without distress, and I’d highly recommend them to parents of autistic or noise-sensitive kids.

reviews.author: jessica lockavitch
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 12, 2025
reviews.content:
Bought these for a student who has sensory issues with loud noise. He loves them as they are his favorite color. These have worked great. They twist and pivot so he can fold them up for easy storage into a book bag.

Ear pieces are soft and a good thickness. My student said he likes the way they fit and feel. I liked it has a cushioned headband piece too.
Kids can place stickers on them to customize as well. Adjustable - just pull the metal pieces to make larger if needed.

Perfect for younger elementary aged kids- I would say 4-8 year old- older if they are tiny.

These have worked well to reduce loud noises in the cafeteria or gym or at assemblies. They Fit snug but still very comfortable. And my student can easily put them on and take off himself. awesome price point as well. I would buy again if needed.

reviews.author: Milad Mazouri
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 14, 2026
reviews.content: This was a perfect fit for our three year old son. We recently took him to a Monster Jam event and these headphones completely cancelled the high decibels. These monster trucks were probably the loudest things I’ve ever heard.

reviews.author: Victoria
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 22, 2025
reviews.content: These earmuffs are perfect! They’re soft, comfortable, and easy to adjust for a good fit. My child loves the Spider-Man design, and they really help with loud noises — great for fireworks, events, or travel. The quality feels solid, and they seem very durable. A must-have for sensitive ears!

reviews.author: James
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 18, 2025
reviews.content: Good quality and well built. They are comfortable enough to wear for extended periods of time. My son really enjoys the fun Spiderman design and colors. Works well for high pitch noise reduction, lacks in the area for low end bass and instead amplifies these sounds when worn. Easy to adjust.

reviews.author: Holly
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 30, 2025
reviews.content: I was worried these would be cheap quality since they were inexpensive but I was wrong, these surpassed my expectations they are great and so cute and make my toddler so happy to be around loud noises!!!!! They don't remove all noise so he can still hear us, they are strong and durable and weigh almost nothing!

reviews.author: Mioko
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026
reviews.content: I got these for a three-year-old child with autism. First of all, he absolutely loves the color and the fact that they're Spider-Man. He always goes to them and wants them when something is too loud or a certain noise is bothering him, and they actually work extremely well at calming him down and putting him at ease. I would definitely recommend these for anyone looking for affordable noise-canceling headphones for a toddler.

reviews.author: A.Johnson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 1, 2025
reviews.content: These cute Spidey headphones can in handy as we prepared to attend our first NFL game. I knew that my 4yo son would not be okay with loud sounds and needed to find something comfortable and cool for him. They worked so well...almost too well. He just kicked his feet back and forth and ate his snacks, all with his headphones on. The price and quality were perfect. They are just the right size for his head and is adjustable for his gallon sized head (jk). MY children are prone to breaking things and these headphones were not something easy to break.

reviews.author: B.Nicole
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 22, 2025
reviews.content:
I purchased these for my trucking husband and he absolutely loves them. He uses them daily on the road, and it gives me so much peace of mind knowing he doesn’t have to hold his phone while driving. He’s normally a name-brand guy, so he was skeptical at first, but these completely won him over.

The sound quality is excellent, both for calls and music, and he really likes that he can switch between single-ear and dual-ear depending on what he’s doing. The noise cancellation works well too—he says his calls come through clear even with road noise in the background.

He also mentioned how comfortable they are for long hours, and the build feels sturdy and durable, not cheap at all. The battery life has been amazing, and he loves having the option to connect multiple devices at once.

For the price, these are a fantastic value. They’ve held up incredibly well, and he even wears them outside the truck because the sound is that good. Highly recommend for anyone who needs reliable, hands-free communication without paying premium brand prices.

reviews.author: Pickles
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2024
reviews.content:
I've looked for a mono headset for years. All I could ever find was a headphone designed for tv and I got that twice but they were trash and broke quickly. So I've been using earbuds but my ears don't like having an earbud in all day. I listen to media on my PC all day as I work/do chores etc.

I had "Bone conduction" over the ear hook "earbuds" most recently that were good but are wearing out now so I tried a search for single ear headset again and was pleasantly surprised to see these.

Bonus points for it being convertible to a full headset if I want too. Most of the time I need to keep one ear open to my environment though and pushing a full headset off one ear is super uncomfortable. So far so good. (edit:I just saw there is a newer model and it's currently cheaper. The only difference is battery life 70 vs 50h, so buy which ever is cheaper at the time)

They feel comfortable enough. The padding of the cup goes around the ear and doesn't rest on it. Would be nicer if they had more velveteen type cover though to reduce heat retention against the skin but it's the same as most as is. I may look for replacement covers later but it's soft and feels fine.

The mic is reversible so you can do a single headset on either ear you like.

Range is great with dongle. Way better than bluetooth. I went all the way to the opposite corner of the home with an outside wall between me and the computer at the far corner from me and it sounded just as clear with no drops as right next to it. I even wore it to go get some firewood and it still got clear signal with a metal shed between the headset and the dongle way back in my room. I didn't bother to try the bluetooth but assume that would compare with the range my earbuds were getting.

Sound quality is good. There are definitely better headsets or headphones out there. If you get these you're most likely more concerned with what they do, convert to a mono headset. They sound good. They sound better than any of my earbuds. Much more range and I can hear things I couldn't with the buds. They are also MUCH louder as earbuds are required to limit how loud they can get to try and prevent hearing loss.

Want to add that I'm in a very noisy room as I have a wall heater right next to me with a VERY loud fan and this sounds good. I have the volume at 30% when I almost always had volume at 80%-100% with earbuds, and that sometimes still not being enough.

It's handy that the headset has pause/play and volume buttons on it. The volume button controls the devices volume rather than have it's own volume boost, but as I've said it's plenty loud. Slight down side is they are positioned to be blocked by the mic, when it's in the upright position.

They are very light. For me this is a big plus as a past headset I had was very heavy and was always falling off. This fits well. Pretty close to perfect for me at the smallest adjustment. Despite being light they do not seem to be flimsy. I'll find out with time.

The instruction book is nice and clear.

So over all great mono headset. Good sound. Not the best headset out there but I've for sure had worse sounding. These sound perfectly fine and if you're an audiophile you probably aren't looking for a mono headset anyway. Good base and highs, all you need for basic media listening.

Tip: if something doesn't work check on updating the device driver. Pause stopped working for me but that fixed it)

reviews.author: Catriona M.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content: I changed my previous review. I gave them one star because I thought they were no longer charging. The sound icon on the bottom of my computer screen keeps saying my headset is at 63%. But after I made the initial review I decided to explore sound settings. And in the actual settings it read that the head set was indeed fully charged. So I dont know what the sound icon was lying to me, but its not a headset issue. That being said I love this headset. The sound quality is good. I havent had any complaints about my voice pickup. I also love that I can use with both ears or have one disconnected. They are comfortable as well.

reviews.author: James L.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 2, 2025
reviews.content:
I (the husband) bought this headset for the ability to switch between one and two ear pieces, and the price. I prefer to use both speakers to hear better, but it is not practical in public. One car does not have an "Infotainment System", so it isn't safe to take a call while driving with both ears covered.
The pad that switches out the removable earpiece is a bit stiff for my head, but I usually wear a cap that takes car of that issue. It is only OK for music, with emphasis on the bass and treble that is artificial sounding to me. I really bought this for voice calls, which this Tecknet does well. The ear pads and speaker can assembly does not block out external sound as well as my previous headset. I was not able to successfully pair two sources with the headset via bluetooth, but using the USB dongle that comes with it allowed me to connect with my workstation. This is acceptable to me, since I can move it to my laptop when I travel.

If you are looking for a headset that sounds better with music while blocking out external sound, read on...
I had an Avantree Aria Pro headset with fixed stereo speakers and a removable microphone It sounded much better than this one to me. It was much better at blocking out exterior sound than the Tecknet. The microphone was a bit cumbersome to work with, often slipping out of the socket. It lasted just short of 3 years. At the time of this writing, it was selling for around $100 US dollars.

[2025-08-12 Update from the husband]:
. I had an issue back in May... The detachable earpiece started rattling, like the speaker element came loose. I emailed them to find out if I could open it up and fix it. After a few emails, I had a total replacement!
. I just took the ear piece and put it on the original headset, and again had stereo. I let wifey take the new one for her phone and just used the one speaker configuration. A month later, the removeable headset started sounding distorted. Testing between the two headsets, the old one had the issue. We swapped and were back in business.
. Today, the replacement headset started sounding distorted. I am afraid I need to look for another stereo headset with microphone. It still sounds great with one earpiece, but I want both.

reviews.author: Alias Etan Rowe
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 17, 2026
reviews.content:
This are by far the best over the ear Headset you can find for call center, listen to music, casual gaming, and Well it says is a trucker Headset, but I use it for call center work.
I have tried so far different brands; and expensive options like logitech,turtle beach, among others like Jabra which i had to charge daily ughh; but the sound on this Tecknet for under $50.00 it's great; the calls are crystal clear; whereas all others I've tried some calls were clear and others were just too much bassy or muffled. The only thing I wish the tecknet had a flip to mute boom mic, But I guess the mute button works fine and has a sound that tells you when you're in mute. The BATTERY LAST FOREVER; ITS BEEN A WEEK AND A HALF AND STILL GOING. I definitely would recommend this set of headset.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 27, 2025
reviews.content: Awesome product! Perfect fit. Have been using it for 3 years now At least 5 days a week while driving. Can hear clearly, but it only cuts out probably 85% of back ground noise.

reviews.author: J
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 22, 2025
reviews.content:
Heard video and other reviews about it so I bought one. Overall I liked but a few important issues were bad enough to make me stop using it and return it. There is almost no point of listing the pros at the risk that this device has. For one, the mute button for remote phone calls work but not how you would want it to. There is a loud beep that plays constantly to remind you it's on mute and you can't lower it, so it does hurt and annoys you after a while. Secondly, and probably the worst part of all... My computer began over heating and having it's fan run faster and for hours even when it wasn't being used. For 3 years the only time that problem happened was when I was running a heavy program but usually that would subside after a few minutes. My computer was reset and all its used for is work nothing else is on it. The timing of me getting this headphone plugging the dongle in the computer and the overheating and an excessively hard running fan, cannot be a coincidence. On top of this, the computer task manager was showing very high usage from that device even when it wasn't in use but still connected ! My computer fan would wake me up from how hard it was blowing. It never did that prior to me installing this headphone.

In short avoid for the sake of the safety of your data and devices. I should've know there was a reason they are willing to apply a $20 coupon , they have devices that are either trying to hack into your computer or are defective and dangerous for the life of your device.

reviews.author: T
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 22, 2023
reviews.content:
Pros:
1) one or two over the ear pieces
2) excellent noise cancellation on the microphone

Cons:
1) awkward button. Always pressing the wrong thing.
2) at MAXIMUM volume, it is impossible to hear, except in a very quiet room. Impossible on the road.
3) Technet's web site does not have the manual posted. (The manual that comes with it has impossibly tiny print.)

Only buy this if you are using it in a quiet room.

Additional feedback:

Originally, I had purchased a TK-HS005 headset. The target application was for single over the ear unit (both ears are illegal to drive with in my state) for use in a noisy Subaru Forester. The unit was well made with the exception that the volume was very dim. It could only be used in a quiet setting with blue tooth volume at maximum. I contacted Tecknet’s customer support to see if there was a workaround. There was not. Instead they had me return the unit (and refunded me) and shipped me a free TK-HS001 to try.
