Unfortunately, that unit also had the same issue. (Probably the same internal chipset.)

The noise canceling microphones on both units was extraordinary.

I count at least nine letters back and forth with their customer service. This is not your typical (not) work at home support that ignores you and ghosts you. These guys are EXTRAORDINARY. Possibly the best customer service I have received in my 66 year of life. I can not wait for the new design to hit the market. If their customer service has anything to say about it, the volume issue will be more than solved!

With the exception of these two units in a noisy environment, I would highly recommend their products. Their support WILL take good care of you.

So five stars for extraordinary customer service and one star for didn’t work right: average three stars

reviews.author: Chris
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content: Very lightweight and comfortable headphones, perfect for running or workouts without blocking surrounding sounds. Bluetooth connection is stable and battery life is good. They fit well and stay in place during exercise. Sound quality is decent for sports use, and the microphone works well. I definitely recommend them.

reviews.author: T. Luong
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026
reviews.content:
I’ve been really happy with these Ogogrs bone conduction headphones. The sound is clear and loud enough for music and calls, and the microphone works well for meetings. Obviously they’re not like heavy bass headphones, but for bone conduction, they sound great.

They’re super lightweight and comfortable. I can wear them on long runs without any ear pain, and they stay put even during tough workouts. The titanium frame feels flexible but sturdy.

Battery life is solid — I get close to the full 10 hours, and the Type-C charging is quick and convenient. I also love that I can still hear traffic and people around me while listening to music. It makes outdoor workouts feel much safer.

Overall, great value for the price and perfect if you want open-ear headphones for running, cycling, or just everyday use. Would definitely recommend!

reviews.author: Brittany
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2026
reviews.content:
I have been looking for a headset just like this, I came across this one and knew I had to give it a try! Fits very comfortably, and had no issues with pairing it to my phone through Bluetooth. Sound quality has been very clear, I use this a lot for phone calls. I have no problems hearing the other person, and they can hear me as well fine with no muffled sounds. It doesn’t completely cancel out background noises but that’s okay, I feel like it’s better being able to hear some background noises if you’re wearing these while you are out of the house and need to be aware of your surroundings.
They have been staying secure and in place when I use them. I’m always one to wear a hat, so having a headset like this makes it easy to wear a hat and still have my headset on! Battery has been lasting me just fine so far, I haven’t had to recharge it just yet so I can’t say how fast charging is. Honestly for the price you pay you really can’t beat it. They are comfortable, sturdy enough to stay in place during use, sound has been great, calling people with these it’s very clear to hear them, and battery life seems to last!

reviews.author: abrar
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026
reviews.content:
These bone conduction headphones are fantastic for running and workouts. The open-ear design lets me stay aware of my surroundings while still enjoying clear, crisp audio. They’re lightweight, comfortable, and don’t cause ear fatigue even during long sessions. Value for money.
Battery life is impressive, and the Type-C quick charging is very convenient. The waterproof feature adds confidence for sweaty workouts or light rain. Call quality with the built-in mic is also clear.
Overall, a great choice for anyone active, safe, comfortable, and reliable. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Soumya
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.content:
I’ve been using these Ogogrs bone conduction headphones for runs, walks, and bike rides, and they’ve quickly become my go-to pair. The open-ear design is exactly what I wanted: I can clearly hear my music and podcasts while still staying aware of traffic and people around me, which feels much safer than traditional in-ear buds.

The headphones are very lightweight and comfortable, and the fit stays secure even during longer, sweatier workouts. I really like the flexible frame and the fact that they don’t put pressure inside my ears, so there’s no soreness after wearing them for a long time.

Sound quality is surprisingly good for bone conduction — voices are clear for calls and podcasts, and music sounds crisp enough for everyday listening. Battery life is solid; I can get multiple workouts in on a single charge, and the Type‑C quick charging is super convenient when I forget to plug them in.

The Bluetooth connection has been stable with my phone, with no annoying dropouts, and the built-in mic works well for quick calls. The waterproof rating is a big plus too, since I don’t have to worry about sweat or a bit of rain. Overall, these are an excellent value if you want safe, comfortable headphones for running, cycling, or general everyday use.

reviews.author: Anthony and Erika
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.content: These are the best I've tried. I tried a few brands including Raycon that were more than double this price and they weren't nearly as good. I haven't tried Shokz but this price these are great. I've had them for weeks. They are comfortable, they sound as good as conduction headphones can I believe. They seem to be of good quality and the battery lasts a long time. I haven't worn then from full charge to dead but they've lasted many hours one day without dying. The volume control works well and very easy to pair up.

reviews.author: Niral
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.content:
Really impressed with these! I use them for runs and workouts, and the open-ear design makes a huge difference. I can hear my music clearly while still being aware of traffic, which feels much safer.

They’re lightweight, comfortable, and stay put even during tougher sessions. Battery life has been solid so far, and charging is quick.

Great value for the price. Definitely happy with this purchase.

reviews.author: Aries Luna
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.content: The sound quality is amazing, especially with the earplugs in. The only downside I found was that the microphone doesn't work properly. I sounded extremely muffled. They are very comfortable, and they stay in place for the most part. The size adjuster that was included (It looks like a little rubber string with two holes on opposite ends) was helpful.

reviews.author: vineandbranches
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2024
reviews.content:
If you are looking for a comfortable and versatile pair of over-ears with decent sound quality for as cheap as possible, this is a great choice. The ear cups are on the small side for over-ears but they fit comfortably, and the compactness is handy. The clamp strength is quite strong, which is good, other than it can squeeze a little too hard if you are wearing them for multiple hours at a time. Battery length is great, as advertised, and it is handy that it uses the same charger as an iPhone.

The audio quality is quite serviceable for the price range. They are obviously not going to stand up against headphones in higher price ranges, but there is nothing distractingly off about the audio of these. It is flat but pretty clear. I use these as my backup pair to my Sony WH-CH720n headphones (which are worth $150 and which have much more in depth EQ options), and the downgrade between them is not as drastic as you might expect.

The Bluetooth connection works pretty well overall, other than it can be a little wonky to switch between multiple devices. It's doable, but you might have to fiddle with the Bluetooth settings on every device to make it happen.

reviews.author: Dodge Casteel
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 9, 2021
reviews.content:
Sound Performance

So, they sound phenomenal. I am the superintendent at a golf course country club, and spend a ton of my day driving around on loud equipment (mowers/blowers/sanders/etc.) These things cancel that noise out,deliver powerful sound with great bass for headphones.

Quality vs. Cost

Couldn't be happier with the quality of these things either. I am rough on them. Sweat a lot in the sun, doesn't phase them. They get tossed around as I'm on and off equipment all day, doesn't phase them. For the price, I don't know if theres a better deal out there.

Comfortability

They aren't too hot on your head either when wearing them to do outdoor activities. Very comfortable. I wear these things 10-15 hours a day some days straight through. Can barely even tell your wearing them. If I'm not jamming on them, I forget they're on sometimes.

Battery Longevity

Battery lasts forever at full volume. I've never had them for on me while using them. There are clear indicators that let you know when the battery is getting low (female voice literally tells you), and when you turn them on, she will tell you if your battery is full, or what percent they are at.

Ease of Use

Very easy Bluetooth set up procedure. Very easy to understand controls. Very clear instructions come with it to explain controls. Surprising this day and age when everything comes from a country that does not use English as it's first language. They have a good translator/transcriber, or they at least sourced that work in the States.

Downsides

Comes with a charging cable that is 6" long. That's 6 inches, not feet. No charging box comes with it. There is also no microphone.

Overall

Highly recommend these headphones. They look good, feel good, and most importantly, they sound amazing!

reviews.author: Wizbang72
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 16, 2021
reviews.content:
I've been an audiophile freak for close to 40yrs. As such, I mock cheap gear, especially the end gear, that being speakers and headphones. I did not buy these for myself, but was really impressed with the bass and overall sound quality. They are comfy on the head with no ear fatigue, and the battery lasts an hours long listening session with plenty to spare. Bluetooth range was adequate in the 1200sq ft, single floor listening space.

Side note - I was not super familiar with JLab, and bought these only because I have a set of JL ear buds I use for podcast at work. While I cannot comment on sound quality of those based on podcasts, I have been impressed with the durability. Because podcast, I never spent much money on 'buds, and usually got 3-6mo out of a pair. The JL's have lasted 2yrs thus far. Based on that alone, I looked to JLab for a reasonably priced pair of full 'phones when one of my kids asked for a Bluetooth set. Again, I am pretty impressed.

A note on poor reviews. I have seen a few complaining of buttons wearing out an sticking. I will update this review if that happens (I update ALL my reviews). As far as the complaints of them not pairing, failing after first use, and poor sound quality: You have to keep in mind $40 (believe it or not) is NOT a lot of money for headphones. That said, you have to expect some quality control issues. Bad sets will make it in a box. Cheap 'phones, or most anything for that matter, or a gamble. I honestly think these are a good gamble. Buy with confidence. On the off chance you get a bum set, JLab claims a 24-48 response to all issues. I am certain they will make it right. For best response, I would suggest you contact them directly. Google JLab for their website….

reviews.author: Kelley
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 9, 2023
reviews.content:
Let me start by saying that for the price I do think these are great headphones for audio (as opposed to audio accompanying video; more on this at the end of the review). Studio Pros are durable, their battery life is fantastic, and most importantly they are super comfortable because each phone fits around your entire ear, and exerts no pressure on the ear itself, so they don't hurt after a long listening session (also with no synthetic fabric touching the ear, they're surprisingly a lot less sweaty than smaller headphones in warm weather).

There is one substantial problem with this model that I wish J-Labs would fix, however: Probably in an effort to compensate for wireless audio transmission lag, these actually have a kind of reverse or negative lag, where the sound feeds on the headphone BEFORE it occurs in a video file. So, for example, if you're watching a movie you'll hear a character say a line about 1/5 of a second before you see their mouth move. It's a very pronounced effect and annoying/distracting enough that I mostly avoid using these headphones for video. This is my third pair of JL Studio Pros so I know this problem is a function of the model itself, not a malfunction with my particular set, and I also know it's something JL could solve for if they wanted to, because I've also had several pairs of JL AirSports and they do not have this problem.

Keep in mind also that while the audio out is very decent for the price, audio in is extremely poor, all but non-existent; it's not a big deal as this technically isn't a headset, but worth noting if you intend to e.g. use them for work and go back and forth between listening to music/podcasts and attending meetings.

Despite the few shortcomings, I still highly recommend.

reviews.author: Ruben Getts
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 12, 2025
reviews.content: This is my second pair. Couldn’t pass up on the price! 1st pair I bought over 2 years ago was double the price and still running strong. Charge lasts all day and recharges quickly. I use one set for yard work and the other for travel they aren’t full noise cancelling but they do cancel well enough for me. They take a beating for sure and still clean up nicely from sweat and dirt. Small draw back is the foam pads after 2+ years is starting to show wear but it’s not terrible. See photo. Definitely got my moneys worth for great sound quality, light weight, and durability.

reviews.author: Sunshine Superman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 2, 2024
reviews.content: I had another pair of Jlab headphones. I forget the model number, I got them in 2022, maybe 2023, they served me well & I lost them. RIP. So I got this model. I was enjoying them out the box, they felt nice and snug. I am not an audiophile, but for my needs--mostly listening to audio from my laptop and phone, they sounded great. For a day. Then they just didn't want to pair anymore. I went through 101 hoops, reset, made sure my machine was updated blah blah.. I even watched a few tutorials (While I am not a religious person, I am certain there is a VIP section in heaven for the folks who make tutorials on mundane items). Nope. I contacted the company. They replied quickly.Then they said they would replace them if I "destroyed them in water" and filmed it. I get it, they want to make sure people are not being shady. I worked retail and I have heard and seen all sorts of elaborate stories to get free things, so I wasn't annoyed.But, I am old fashioned and I am disabled, so it went through the exchange with Amazon. Got my new pair. Certain they'd be fantastic. But nope. Same thing. This pair matches. Then discharges. Then I have to match them again to get it to stick. I read enough people with the same issue on various sites to come to the conclusion that this particular model is a lemon and why I am giving it one star. I debated about doing as such, but I continue to use the second pair as backups when my other pair by another brand that starts with "J" is charging and I am reminded every time I actually need to use them for something timely that they stink. I would have returned this pair but life happened, I missed the window and I am stuck. I am not throwing in the towel with Jlab, the other pair had was great and they have nice CS folks. I don't know who is leaving these fantastic reviews, but they must have a different batch or made the mistake of reviewing them within the first couple of days of owning them. But I can't in good concious recommend this model.

reviews.author: J's Opinion
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 2, 2025
reviews.content:
I find these very comfortable. More comfortable than the headphones I spent way more money on. The earpieces fit completely over my ears so my ears do not get irritated when I wear them. I haven't tried it yet, but I bet I could wear these on a long plane flight and be comfortable the entire time.

I am not an audiophile so my review of the sound quality is a bit meaningless. But for simple listening to podcasts or music needs, they work great for me. If you need better sound then you had best be looking at those high-end brands.

I like the three sound settings and they are easy to switch between while you are listening to music so you can hear which option you prefer. They seem to hold a charge for a long time.

They did not come with a travel case so when I do fly, I will have to figure out how to pack them safely without a case. But at this price, I wouldn't expect a case to come with them.

Because I have had them only for a month or two now, I really can't report on their long-term durability, but they don't feel cheaply made to me. If you care for them properly, I think they should last quite a while.

reviews.author: Jayson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 15, 2024
reviews.content:
This is my second time buying these headphones because I broke my first pair and thought they were okay enough to buy them again. I've been using these daily since at least 2022. Here are a few things you should know before you buy them:
Pros:
-They hold a charge for quite a while before you have to recharge them.
-The sound quality is nice and clear
-They fit nicely on my ears and are pretty good at keeping your music in, and the noise of life out
-They're pretty durable, and my first pair only broke because I accidentally stepped on them
Cons:
-When the battery gets too low, they will constantly interrupt your song with the words, "low battery" every 30 or so seconds until you finally give in and recharge them
-The volume adjustment button broke on both pairs I've owned
-If you try and call someone, the mic in the headphones is a huge joke
Considering all this, I'd give them a solid 3 out of 5 stars. They aren't the best headphones you'll find, but for $20-$40 (depending on if they're on sale or not), they're not bad at all.

reviews.author: aaron hilmer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 19, 2025
reviews.content: These headphones are very good especially with ldac and noise canceling, the build quality is also very good, and the price is reasonable, i would recommend these to anyone that wants a good sound quality experience for the price.

reviews.author: Holderman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 2, 2025
reviews.content: these are awesome. i love how they have multiple ways to use: blue tooth and plug in adaptor. they are very comfy and block out other noise so the sound quality is good. great price. wonderful for travel!!

reviews.author: Kaniki
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 30, 2025
reviews.content:
I tried this connected to my TV, and 2 different tablets, all via Bluetooth.. I had no problem with any of them.. I then tried to connect it to my one tablet via the cord and it was a bit glitchy.. I could not get it to connect very well.. But then, I turned the cord around.. The end that was in the tablet, I put in the headset, and vise versa, and it started working with no problems.. Not sure why.. Small glitch??

The sound was pretty good.. I saw some people complain about the sound, so it had me wondering.. I just want decent sound.. I am not an enthusiast, but I want something better than basic sound.. Maybe the quality of sound that was in most cars 25 years ago.. and this did that with no problem..

I have a large head, and this fit me with no problem.. In fact, the sides extend, and i did not even need to extend them to get them to fit me.. This was a first.. So fit was good..

These have a nice comfortable feel too them.. Enough pressure to hold them in place, but not so much that it will bother you using them over a long length of time.. Very comfortable..

I did find that while using them, they did seem to slip a bit, so they did have to be adjusted every once in a while.. Not a big deal.

As with anything that covers you whole ear, they will make you sweat over time.. So you will want to take a break every once in a while to let your ears cool off.. But that is to be expected.

I did see one person complain about how you can not use it while charging it, and it has to have power to run it, even with it plugged in via the 3.5mm cord.. Honestly, I am not even sure why they were complaining about this.., These are powered headphones.. 3.5mm jacks are used to send a signal for the sound.. They are not meant to send power to power anything other than sound.. So it is expected that something with circuitry will need power, which is why it will need to draw power off of the battery to work.. Don't want to do that, then get something with nothing but speakers.

The other thing I thought was kind of off about that comment.. Lets think about this.. These have a 75 hour run time.. That is the equivalent of about 3 days of non stop use.. They are complaining about having to charge something once every 3 or more days?? I am pretty sure that you can find the time to do that once every few days, even if it is while you are sleeping..

Overall, for $35, these are much better and nicer than what I expected for the price.. So far, that one cord glitch is the only problem that I have found, and all that I needed to do was flip the cord around to fix that problem.. So am I happy with them?? Definitely.. and I love the fact that I can use these on both, portable MP3 players through the cord, or through bluetooth for other devices.. Yes, I would definitely recommend them for the price.

reviews.author: Hawaiian
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.content: I have a Y7 and love it. This was for my wife. The noise cancellation works great while mowing the lawn.

reviews.author: Eng208
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 2, 2025
reviews.content: These sound great!! The ANC works good at blocking out ambient noise and they are very comfortable over the ears! I’ve had Sony the last two times and get about four years out of them so we will see how long these hold up with yard and tractor work.

reviews.author: Anita Terrill
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 12, 2025
reviews.content: Good price and good product. I would buy again.

reviews.author: Brian
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 23, 2026
reviews.content: This item just would not pair with my phone never seen anything like it

reviews.author: Mako G
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 26, 2025
reviews.content:
The sound is clear, the noise cancelling works, they're comfortable, seem to have good battery life, but people say they can hardly hear me through the mic. They could hear me fine on my last headset.

One big issue I have, and might make me return these, is the lag while using the cord. A little lag with bluetooth is normal, but not when using a cord. I got these to use while traveling, so I could use them on the plane and to record music, but the lag makes recording almost impossible. I suspect they're doing some kind of audio processing, but it's like this even when the noise cancelling is turned off. I wish you could turn the power off when using a cord, and just use them like normal headphones, but they automatically turn on, and don't turn off until you unplug them.

reviews.author: Wildflower
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 2, 2024
reviews.content:
There was absolutely no incentive involved with this review, it's coming purely from the genuine joy in my heart and undying love I have for these dang headphones. (I just ordered a 3rd pair if that tells you anything.) My first pair I used virtually non-stop daily for a year or so. Zero issues. I just bought myself an extra pair in a different color for home vs. travel and a pair for my dad.

This will be a long breakdown, but detailed and useful in your decision making, so please bear with me if you want the DL on these puppies...

PROS:

1) The sound quality for listening is INCREDIBLE! I'm honestly blown away by the supurb sound for the low price tag. It's rich, immersive and clear, and it offers fantastic deep, clear bass, but without overpowering the other ranges.

The sound quality of this pair easily beats out ALL of my others, hands down! This includes my double-the-price pair of $50 "Beats Flex" and my much more high-end $125 pair of "LG TONE Flex HBS-XL7."

The two other brands I mentioned above are both ok/good units. The LG has very good sound quality both incoming and outgoing, and they are much better for listening purposes than the Beats (the Beats are a bit heavy on treble and "tinny"), but the battery is much better on the Beats compared to the expensive LG pair, almost double the length in fact.

The sound quality is also clearer on the caller's end with both of the above units, but the sound quality for you, the listener, is not nearly as good as this much more affordable "Lecefty" pair (currently $26 before a 15% off coupon promotion).

If you mostly plan to use this Lecefty headset for listening and not phone calls, go with these! Don't hesitate! The sound is just as good as the premium LG, way better than the Beats, and the battery outshines both of those by miles!

I should also mention that the listening sound is equally fantastic with the Lecefty for all activities (music, video or audio). When I'm listening to music it's rich with that bass and treble balance that gets you moving to the beat.

When I'm watching a movie, I feel like I'm sitting in the theater with the rumbles, etc. you get during action scenes, plus the delicacy and detail needed for more quiet, suspenseful or conversational moments.

When listening to YouTube channels (my primary form of entertainment and what I use these for 95% of the time), it's also... you guessed it... excellent. Just friggin' EXCELLENT.

It seems like all of the sound elements are just balanced so perfectly that they each shine on their own, but without overpowering each other in any way. Emercive sound would be the best description that comes to mind.

2) THE BATTERY!!!! Oh my gosh people, the battery! When they claim 200 hours they aren't kidding. I have no idea how they even made a pair of earbuds, especially this lightweight, that can last as long as these do.

I haven't technically charted exactly how long they last, but I can tell you with certainty that even with nearly constant daily use for 12 hours or more, it's days, and days, and daaaaaays!

I'm betting you could easily take these on a week-long trip and as long as you turn them off when not in use they will make it through without any charge needed. I'm equally blown away by the battery life as I am by the quality of the sound.

3) It pairs with multiple devices at once and stays connected to both (at least 2 in my experience). You can seamlessly switch back and forth within a second. None of my other headsets have ever had this awesome feature, not even the $125 LG set.

I've also never had any issues pairing this headset with new devices and I've never encountered it acting moody when it comes to finding/connecting to devices it already knows. As soon as I turn it on, it will seamlessly auto-connect to both of the two devices I have it paired with and it stays that way unless I manually disconnect one of them.

4) The Bluetooth range on this Lecefty far surpasses both of my aforementioned name-brand units. I only lose range in the very, very far ends of the house. I can go to the basement to do laundry with it connected to my laptop upstairs or go outside to get some sun and the signal is just fine, even through a couple of rooms and a thick plaster wall with brick exterior. I can wander the entire house and get things done without issue. It's a noticable improvement in range verses other headset brands.

5) They look cool and offer a variety of gradient colors to choose from, so you can have more than one pair and easily assign them to specific users/uses/devices that are identifiable at a glance.

6) They're very lightweight (you forget you're wearing them).

7) So comfortable and light I fall asleep wearing them all the time, even with the earbuds in. Secondary bonus, the earbuds serve nicely as effective earplugs, even with no sound. Nice 2-in-1 feature for travelers on flights and staying in hotels where there are always unfamiliar noises.

8) The earbud cover size straight out of the box is a good fit for me, but like most products, there are two other size options to go up or down if you need a different fit.

9) They seem to charge quickly and the charging light is easily visible. Red when charging, off when full. They use a USB C which is the tech industry standard these days, so most of you will already have several of these charger cables on hand (and therefore you'll probably only need one charger for all your devices when traveling too).

10) They are flexible, as the name implies, so you can squeeze/fold/curl them into tight spaces and they can get jumbled around a decent bit in a purse or luggage without damage.

11) The earbuds are magnetic for when around your neck and not in use. Note: The magnets do not pause what you are playing/watching (this is one feature where the Beats Flex have an advantage).

CONS:

1) Quality of sound on the caller's end is not as high caliber. However, I've been told they can still hear me just fine, it's just not quite as crisp as those two other brands I own. I asked 2 people for their opinions on the phone while using all 3 headset brands I own. One of those individuals is older and terribly hard-of-hearing and he could still make out everything I was saying on this less expensive Lecefty unit, so I don't think it's a deal breaker provided you aren't using these *solely* for work calls all day long. It'll suffice for the occasional caller probably just fine.

2) The earbuds are not retractable. I wouldn't expect them to be for this price point though. Not a big deal for me personally.

3) It won't work with Android or iPhone virtual assistants like my expensive LG TONE Flex do, but again, I wouldn't mark this as a negative against them because I wouldn't expect that for this price range anyway. Just something to note though if your expectations are over the moon.

As you can tell, I really cannot recommend this headset highly enough! I own 3 pairs now and whenever someone needs a new headset, this is definitely what I'll recommend or gift to them. 💯

reviews.author: Sarah Muddiman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content:
I needed to give time before leaving a review. I won't pretend it's the greatest headset I've ever had, but for the affordable the price, I am quite satisfied after having used it for only a week now.
It doesn't have completel noise cancellation but it does offer better sound quality & noise reduction than I expected. It's comfortable around my neck, functions perfectly & the volume & other buttons are easy to use.
But the thing that impressed me the most is the battery life. The headset delivered January 31st, came with a 90% charge & I have been using it frequently nearly all day, everyday, between phone calls, streaming shows & listening to music.
It is now the 6th, just after 11pm as I'm typing this & it's barely down to 60% battery life. I have not charged it once yet. For such a low price, I was shocked at such a great battery life.
Is it the best headset? No. But it's definitely worth it, especially for anyone with a tight budget. I would definitely recommend.

reviews.author: Sheenashark
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content:
Pros-
Great sound quality
Charge lasts forever and a half
Good range(~50 feet at least)
Durable (I've had my set for about a year and a half and no noticeable degradation of sound quality or battery life. I've used it a lot over the time I have had it)

Cons -
Tends to try and slip off, especially if buds are not in your ears.

I also wish it was hold down to skip a track, and click to adjust volume TuT vice versa isn't super intuitive, and I usually accidentally turn the volume super high, or super low because I have to hold it down.

reviews.author: Sirius Gaspard
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 28, 2025
reviews.content:
As someone who just had these die on them after a full calendar year, I can say that they did the job. They lasted a full year (which is how long the warranty is) and they were good quality despite the price. They consistently had very long battery life even when I would leave them on for way too long. The only problem I ever encountered during the last 6 months of having them was a repeated disconnect and reconnect of the headphones. Sometimes, they wouldn't even disconnect. I would just have my phone call or my music interrupted by the automated voice saying, "Disconnected" followed by "Connected" after a few more seconds. Sometimes, it would disconnect and I would have to wait for it to randomly connect again. This was only toward the second half of its life span, however.

I didn't experience the slipperiness that others are talking about. I felt that the buttons on the side were incredibly easy to use and over all the size of the headphones as well as the earbuds themselves were perfect. I just wish it lasted longer than a year and I wish that it didn't have that disconnecting issue, but you get what you pay for. Considering those are small issues and it was a small price for a set of headphones that lasted a whole year, I'll probably order them again.

reviews.author: Heather Pauly
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2026
reviews.content:
These are amazing. I have gone through 3 pairs now. I keep rebuying because these last my whole work week without needing to be charged!
Now don't let the fact that I've already gone through 3 pairs scare you. I use mine hard, they don't leave my neck unless I'm showering or they fall off in my sleep. Yes, I sleep with these on.
For my ears they are comfortable and come with several different sized earbuds to help find a good fit for anyone. The sound is really good, not the best I've ever had. Still very good.

In my experience, left side always goes out first and it sounds like a connection issue until you try the other side and realize right side works just fine. There's no static, just a sudden cut off.
Mine last between 6-8 months but would easily last longer if I took better care of them. For $25-$26 bucks it's just easier to replace them.
I don't use the cord adjusters, they just annoy me to be honest.
Sometimes they do flatten out and start slipping to one side. I just take them off and loop the neckband around two of my fingers about 3 times and squeeze the loops for 30 seconds, and your good to go again. Again that's the neck band part, don't do that to the ear cords.

reviews.author: loveguitar
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 10, 2025
reviews.content: This is my 5th pair of this brand of Bluetooth headphones. I've been using one pair for the past 3 years, one pair completely failed after a couple of months, one pair after 3 years lost use in one earbud, and the other two have been going strong. I'm just happy to get a couple of years out them and some of these are still working after 3 years of hard use. Me and hubs use these every day so it's not like they sit unused for long periods of time. They connect well to whatever we want them too, the sound is excellent and battery life is absolutely awesome. I can go days before they're even halfway depleted. Though I have bought 5 pairs of them, 4 are still functional after a long time.

reviews.author: Joey Stocking
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 23, 2025
reviews.content:
For less than $30 this is a decent headset. Sounds quality is good. Battery life is excellent. The fact that the + and - buttons skip back and skip forward in my audio book is excellent and huge deal for me. However, the size of the is large and bulky. I do not like the way the wire and earbuds just dangle and the magnet to hold them together doesn't help. Lastly the buttons all in a row on one side make it hard to find the button you want to push. I have to start at the bottom and count them going up to find the pause/play button.

I have tried all of the following neckband headsets: LG HBS-730, LG HBS-750, LG HBS-770, LG HBS-780, LG HBS-800, LG HBS-810, LG HBS-820S, LG HBS-835, LG HBS-910, LG HBS-920, LG HBS-SL6S, Exfit (Fermata) BCS-700, Exfit 800, and now Lecefty-i35plus.

To neckband bluetooth headset engineers! Listen up! The perfect headset would be as follows:

-on/off button like the Exfit BCS-700 that slides on and off and is located on the inside toward the neck
-dedicated "Forward" and "Backward" buttons or rocker button on the left outside
-play/pause/answer button on the left outside (not on the topside like the HBS-770 or Exfit 800, because it is too easy to bump)
-dedicated google assistant/gemini button on the right outside
-dedicated volume up and down buttons or rocker button on the right outside
-bluetooth version 5.2 or higher
-slim size even at the sacrifice of battery life (I can charge every other day)
-Dual MEMS mic (hopefully design so the small holes for the mic don't get plugged with debris that make me sound muffled until I clean it out.)
-Neckband that is flexible that won't break or kink.

That is it. I know you can do. It has all been done before, but not together in one headset. I believe in you.

reviews.author: Hayley Thompson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 30, 2026
reviews.content: I went over two months without ever needing to charge these. I would say I used them for an average of an hour or so a day. So, I would say they at least have 70+ hours of playtime, which is more than I've ever gotten our of any headphones before. I think when I bought these, they were advertised as 1000H battery-life, which I thought was a crazy claim. They now claim to have 200H of battery-life, which I'm still not sure is completely accurate. Never-the-less, the charge on these lasted such an impressively long tme in my opinion that I have to give them 5 stars. Plus they are comfy, have great sound quality, and I love the colors!

reviews.author: Anne
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 11, 2022
reviews.content:
So far the K361 are my favorite headphones.
I used them every day at my computer since July, 2021.
They broke on me twice but customer care has been fantastic so far.
The first time the wire became frayed and eventually failed after around 6 months of use.
I called support and they sent me a new cord for free because it was under warranty.
Around the 11 month mark the plastic on the ears started to wear off and eventually the foam ear fell off making them unusable.
I sent them in for repair and I'm still waiting for them to be returned.
The support team has been in contact with me and is sending me a new set.
I will update when the replacement arrives but the customer service I have received so far has convinced me that the new headphones are going to be great.

I would highly recommend the K361.
Comfortable, great sound, folds easily, the only downside is that the 3.5mm cable is not interchangeable, it has a special plastic clip that keeps it in the phone so you can't just use any 3.5mm cable if it frays.
Have to buy the special one only they sell.

reviews.author: Oscar
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 31, 2023
reviews.content:
Good headphones. Received them in very good quality.

Headphones feel nice but do squeeze and feel uncomfortable after about an hour. Earcups are also fairly shallow resulting in your ears touching the driver which adds to the discomfort.

Sound is good. Very mid focused. Very recessed top end. Results in a similar sound to the HD650 but much more exaggerated in terms of the "veil" they display. Still a good sound. Mostly follow the Harman target and as a result can sound harsh to some but mostly laid back.

The headphones are also very sensitive and can take plenty of EQ without a lot of power as a result.

Very good for the price and nice as a closed back to accompany my openbacks.

reviews.author: Zac
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2020
reviews.content:
Material quality and comfort are great. It's very light weight.
I'm one to watch many reviews prior to purchasing, but if you're reading this trying to get clues about the sound quality then allow me to prepare you - the bass is pretty mild if you're looking for some heavy bass listening. The K371 has more bass to give you that low end punch. I for once prefer the cleaner and more balanced sound since I have a variety of cans.
The midrange and high frequencies are very subjective to taste, so I won't bother and all I'll say is it's rather balanced for most, no peaks or weird areas in the frequency range.

reviews.author: Lewis
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 9, 2025
reviews.content: Been using these for years now and still look brand new and sound fantastic. Great for anyone in the sound and music industry.

reviews.author: GuyinGrandy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 31, 2020
reviews.content:
They are even better than anticipated. Light weight. Has 2 Chords, a short and long. Adapter to use on the smaller jack sizes.
Colorless, Flat. Low Impedance - meaning it doesn’t tax your headphone amp, headphone out. Took my mixing to a new level. Also very quiet when nothing is playing, I noticed that as soon as I put them on. Lightweight, wearable for hrs.

reviews.author: WB
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 2, 2022
reviews.content:
This review is for the K361 which were $100 in Nov 2022.

Sound: 3/5. They sound like $100 headphones. They are relatively flat. Not too much treble, not too much bass, but no magic or wow factor. They sounded a bit claustrophobic to me. They will probably sound good if you are coming from $50 or less headphones. The HD6XX for $200 sounds waaaay better than these.

Comfort: 4.5/5 they are very light weight, compact, low clamp force, easy to forget you are wearing them. Low profile and comfortable when lying down. Nice oval ear shaped cups. Only complaint is the pads could be a bit thicker, my ears touched inside a little bit.

Quality: 2/5 Mine had a channel imbalance, one side was louder than the other. The cable is very cheap and stays kinked.

They are very sensitive and easy to drive with any headphone jack, no amp needed.

Overall these are fine for the price (if they don't have a channel imbalance) but if you want a more high end sound, look elsewhere and spend a bit more. I returned these.

reviews.author: Isaiah
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 22, 2025
reviews.content: Impressive sound quality for the price. I also like how you can fold them up and Bluetooth works well. However, long-term durability and comfort are lacking. The headband on my original snapped when I accidentally slammed them into my PC case. It was my fault, but support replaced them anyway and even upgraded me to the wireless version for free, so no complaints about customer service. That said, they get hot quickly, the ear cups fall apart over time, and they're hard to repair or replace, making build quality disappointing for the price range.

reviews.author: DA
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 12, 2024
reviews.content:
I love the seal on them. It helps me to eliminate outside noise and focus on the audio.

The headphones are very comfortable and the sound profile is great!

reviews.author: Parks N' Tech
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 20, 2026
reviews.content:
From the moment I plugged these JBL Tune 500s into my device, I knew I was in for a serious treat, but I wasn't prepared for just how deep the soundstage would actually be. The audio profile is genuinely robust, centered heavily around that signature JBL Pure Bass Sound which lives up to every bit of the hype. It isn't just loud; it is precise, thumping with a rich resonance that makes hip-hop and electronic tracks feel alive without drowning out the mids or piercing the highs. I have listened to everything from intricate classical pieces to heavy metal on these cans, and the clarity remains pristine across the board. It is refreshing to experience such high-fidelity audio without needing a massive amplifier or a complex setup; just a simple connection delivers a concert-like experience right to my ears.

Comfort is often the Achilles' heel of on-ear headphones, but the engineering here is spot on. The lightweight materials and soft ear cushions create a fit that feels almost weightless, allowing me to wear them for hours on end—whether I am grinding through a workday or getting lost in a gaming session—without any of that dreaded "clamp" pressure on my ears. The foldable design is incredibly convenient for tossing them into my bag when I am on the move, and I appreciate that the build feels sturdy despite being so light. I never feel like I have to baby them; they are built to handle real life, and the flat cable design is a stroke of genius. I have pulled these out of a cluttered backpack countless times, and not once have I had to fight a tangled mess of wires.

Speaking of that cable, the one-button remote and microphone are flawlessly integrated. I love the tactical click of the button; it is responsive and makes controlling music playback or answering calls effortless without needing to fumble with my phone. The microphone quality is surprisingly crisp for an inline unit, picking up my voice clearly even in busier environments, which makes switching from jamming out to taking a professional call seamless. There is also something incredibly liberating about the wired connection. I never have the anxiety of a dying battery cutting my playlist short, and I don't have to carry around extra charging cases. It is just pure, uninterrupted reliability that works every single time I need it.

Ultimately, these headphones remind me why wired audio is still king for consistent quality. The JBL Tune 500 manages to pack a premium audio punch into a package that is accessible, stylish, and incredibly user-friendly. I find myself reaching for these over much more expensive wireless pairs simply because I know exactly what I am going to get: immediate connection, zero latency, and bass that absolutely rocks. If you value a hassle-free listening experience that prioritizes sound quality above all else, these are an absolute triumph.

reviews.author: Martin Nerren
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 29, 2025
reviews.content: Love my headphones!! These JBL's sound great, phone Mike on the cord, wear them a lot and are comfy too. Highly recommend especially the quality and value!!

reviews.author: igomesf
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 31, 2025
reviews.content:
The JBL Tune 500 headphones have been a fantastic purchase! The JBL Pure Bass Sound delivers rich, deep bass while maintaining clear mids and highs, perfect for all types of music The on-ear design is lightweight and comfortable, even after long listening sessions

The 1-button remote/mic is super convenient, allowing me to easily control music and take calls without reaching for my phone, and I love being able to ask Siri or Google Now directly from the headphones The tangle-free flat cable is a bonus, keeping everything neat and easy to store

These headphones are also foldable, making them easy to carry and store when not in use The blue color is vibrant and stylish, adding a fun pop to my tech gear

This product offers great value for money, excellent functionality, and is easy to use If you're looking for comfortable, high-quality wired headphones with excellent sound, I highly recommend the JBL Tune 500! Highly recommend

reviews.author: Jonathan Gheorghin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 18, 2026
reviews.content: I’m happy, sounds nice . I’ve had better, these do the job though!

reviews.author: Mario
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 22, 2026
reviews.content: Works awesome. Using these headphones with my mustang micro on my guitar and they sound amazing.

reviews.author: Terrel Wooten
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content: Great Product

reviews.author: stacy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 21, 2026
reviews.content: Love these headphones!!

reviews.author: Sara
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.content: Liked very much the option to have bluetooth, I can walk around my room and even to my kitchen during meetings and still connected and well. The ear muffs are in the smaller than expected but that did not interrupt the listening or sound of the m. So far been very happy with them and no issues.

reviews.author: kgard
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 3, 2025
reviews.content: I’ve bought 3 sets of these headphones for my grandchildren last Christmas. Over 10 months later and they’re still going strong. They get used DAILY. My daughter told me BEST presents ever and she’s very impressed with their durability. The headphones are a must have with the player, especially if siblings have them. They can each listen to their own books and music for road trips or just each listening in the same room. The sound is quality and the volume control great. They must be comfortable because my grandkids, ages 3, 5 & 8 wear them for hours at a time on road trips and airplane travel.

reviews.author: Meghan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 2, 2025
reviews.content: My sons both have headphones for their Yoto and they both love them! These fit well on both my three and six year old! They are quite durable as my sons are pretty hard on them. One of my kiddos has the larger Yoto while the other got a mini. These work great with both. Each son has a different carrying case, and these fit well in each. These headphones have been great for very long car trips and I am pleased with my purchase.

reviews.author: Let the Good In
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 9, 2025
reviews.content: Good sounds, adjustable, comfortable, and we like that they're wired -- makes these headphones usable with other players too (and/or can be plugged in on an airplane).

reviews.author: betsy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 18, 2025
reviews.content: Love them. Work well. Great for car rides and quiet time.

reviews.author: Mason E.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 2, 2025
reviews.content: These headphones are perfect for my daughter. They fit her head nicely and she says they are very comfortable. The sound quality on them is better than I expected at the price point as well. The bag also is helpful in not losing track of them. Great value!

reviews.author: geakerson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 16, 2025
reviews.content: Perfect for young child

reviews.author: Lindsey Peterson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 17, 2025
reviews.content: Broke within 2 months. Unfortunate for expensive kid headphones.

reviews.author: JudiG
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 7, 2023
reviews.content: Thankfully my grandson doesn’t use often, as they are not the most durable. But they are good for travel and yoto makes a case for these.. they do have great volume control for little ears.

reviews.author: Kait O.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2026
reviews.content:
The only negative things I can say about these earbuds are: The way they plug in to the charging case is kind of weird and doesn't seem very secure, and the battery life is not the greatest, 6-8hrs at most with noise cancellation off.

Everything else is pretty much amazing across the board. The Noise Cancellation is VERY solid. The ability to connect to two devices at once & switch between them via the app is pretty great. The tap controls are easier to use compared to the older Bose Earbuds I have, these only require one tap to play/pause, the old ones it was double tap. Switching Noise modes is a simple long press, next track/restart track are also tap functions which you can find in the instructions. Volume control is what you would expect, swipe one way for up, the other way for down. The build quality of the Earbuds themselves is good. They fit pretty snug with the default rubber-pieces that came on them for me, which is good because that aides in the noise cancellation/sound dampening even when you don't have the Active feature turned on.
The sound quality on these things is AMAZING. This & the ANC is what you should be buying these for, superior sound & great noise cancelling tech. Are they worth the price? That's really subjective, as to how much of a music/audio snob you are. In my case, I have noticed that there are parts in several songs I've listened to for over a year, that I'm hearing notes or instrumentation clearly that was muddled or almost not even present on other sound devices. It really is that good.

reviews.author: Chris Davis
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 6, 2026
reviews.content: I went back and forth for about a week when considering these, researching and comparing everything I could think of. I was trying to justify spending three hundred dolars for some earbuds. Well, they offered a tomorrow delivery and I rolled the dice. SO glad I did. They were ordered after midnight on Tues/Wed am, and arrived mid day Thursday. In fact, UPS sent an update that the original by 5:30 delivery would be by 2:30. At 1:30 the package was on the porch. Now let me tell you why I needed these. It wasn't for great sounding music or videos (which they do sound amazing). It was for the ANC technology. And let me tell you IT WORKS (I had the idea for this like 17 years ago but the tech wasn'tavailable). Anyway, I HAD a major issue with noise campaigns at all times. Super loud modified welding trucks idling for up to 2hours at a time every morning from 5 to 7 am. Other loud ram trucks revving starting and stopping at all hours (DEALER) and crappy little cars missing exhaust caps or something, and inconsiderate TPT booming home and car stereos at all hours of the night an day randomly. Not to mention TWEAKERS mething around the yards and repetitively slamming car doors and trunks and yelling at each other in the wee hours. Earplugs didn't work. Sound machines didn't work. Asking didn't work, and the landlord would do very little or nothing about it. Considered professional soundproofing (like a studio room) which would have cost thousands of dollars to be effective, and also considered subject elimination. Not being keen to the idea of a life sentence, I went with $300 earbuds. I am hear to tell you I could not be happier. This technology is not from this world. Have you ever tried to eliminate low frequency noise pollution? It's a near impossible never win battle...UNTIL NOW. I can hear my surroundings like someone talking in the room, or my alarm, but no longer can I hear anything else. No traffic. No music. No banging. No slamming doors. NOTHING. Just peaceful quiet. Silent Lucidity of sorts. Complete rest in less time. Joy. Even the hum of road noise and traffic are oblitterated while driving. It's like I spent $1500 on quet EV tires! Phone calls sound like you're in a recording studio and the other person is talking to you through the soundproof glass on a microphone. Music sounds immersive without ANY ambient noise pollution. No people. No screaming kids. No dogs barking. No office buzz. Just QUIET. The best thing is it's not a complete shut which could be dangerous because I can choose what sounds I want to be aware of! And my cameras send immediatee alerts right to me if anyone gets close to my property or personal space. And if they get past that point, well Texas is a stand your ground and protect your person and property state, so Mr. SIG my personal body guard and security system is always on the ready. I call the system FAFO SECURITY. So, if you desire peace and quiet or need to make noise campaigns obsolete, then do not hesitate to get these earbuds. They are practically weightless, very snug/secure and comfortable. Even while running or working out (and sleeping). And glasses or sunglasses go on and off with ease and no interferance. It's like having a frequency equalizer/canceler right in your ears!!! BOSE nailed the name on these "QUIET COMFORT ULTRA". And don't fall for the 1/2 price trick that they will tempt you with in the algorithm to just get the Gen 1 buds. They're 1/2 price because they can't sell them and they're easily half the product and quality. There were too many bugs, issues, hissing, connectivity problems and inferior ANC technological issues in the first attempt. And I didn't find that information video in the search results until AFTER I received the superior product. GEN2 is the best $300 ever spent (well besides my personal security system). ZERO remorse or buyer's guilt. GET THEM!!! BOSE did not pay me for this review (and neither did the seller who was super fast with shipping...nor UPS, which was lightning speed with delivery), but perhaps they should all seriously consider it HAHAHA!

reviews.author: Hannah
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.content: These earbuds are so amazing! I came from Beats Solo 4s (and switched b/c they did not fit around my afro), but the sound quality is much better. The noise cancelling is perfect, and so much so I couldn't hear anything but my music in a loud building. The music is clear and not scratchy- there is a slight fuzz sound, when Immersion mode is on, and no music plays, but that is just the design of the noise cancelling mode. The earbuds fit in my ear perfectly, and they are well worth the money. The have lasted great since I bought then in December, and they connect between my laptop and iphone seamlessly. Get these! They are so amazing!

reviews.author: Cynthia MP
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026
reviews.content:
I have been a long term Bose user... Over the ear noise cancelling for many years; in-ear wired noise canceling buds; then the first round of the QC wireless earbuds. I loved them all. Sadly, I must have put the QC earbuds into the wrong type of charger and I fried my case after 5 years. Really a bummer. But, I like Bose a lot so I looked into what was new and came across these buds on sale.

First, I absolutely love the plum color! They are very fun, and since I have dark hair it often helps my coworkers realize I have them in. They are very light weight. You can really customize the fit, and I have different sized wings for each ear and haven't had any issues with them falling out. I actually like them a lot better than the original wings as they are more secure and more comfortable. The case is a little smaller in footprint than the original case, and I don't think I would want to drop it for fear of breaking the lid. The earbuds themselves seem durable and overall build and packaging seems great.

The sound quality of this pair is just amazing, especially in immersive mode. It's very fun to have spatial audio. It is hard to describe to people what this mode is like, but I never want to listen to music any other way again. These are not inexpensive, but I 100% feel they are a great value for what they accomplish! The app is also very helpful for adjusting key listening and hot button options. The case hold several rounds of charging for the earbuds, and this time I'm going to be a lot more careful with using the included cable and high quality chargers.

reviews.author: Alec H.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 4, 2025
reviews.content:
After a frustrating journey with other premium earbuds, I can confidently say the Bose QuietComfort Ultra Earbuds (2nd Gen) are the best I’ve ever owned.
First, some context: I tried the AirPods Pro 3 (latest model) and returned them the same week. They simply refused to stay in my ears no matter what tips I used, and honestly the sound was underwhelming – basically on par with $100–150 earbuds. Nothing wrong with them for Apple users, but the fit was a total deal-breaker for me.
Next I grabbed the Nothing Ear (3). On paper they’re incredible value, and for the first few days I thought I’d found the perfect pair – amazing bass, excellent fit, and a fun sound signature. Unfortunately, at higher volumes (even 70–80%) my unit started making an awful scratchy, distorted plastic-like noise that completely ruined the experience. I’m 99% sure it was a defective pair, but after already returning the AirPods I didn’t feel right doing another return loop on Amazon. Lesson learned: sometimes you just have to spend more to stop the cycle.
Enter the Bose QC Ultra Earbuds.
Wow. From the moment I put them in, everything clicked. The fit is perfect and secure (the wing tips + multiple ear tip sizes make it foolproof), the active noise cancellation is in another league (better than both AirPods Pro and Nothing), and the sound… it’s rich, detailed, spacious, and just musical. Bass is deep and controlled, mids are clear, highs are crisp without ever being harsh. I listen to everything from metal to jazz to electronic and they handle it all beautifully. Spatial audio with head-tracking is also genuinely impressive when watching movies.
One honest complaint: the charging case feels shockingly cheap for a $299 product. It’s super light plastic and feels like it belongs on $20 gas-station earbuds. It works fine and supports wireless charging, but come on Bose, you can do better.
That small gripe aside, the earbuds themselves are luxurious in hand and in-ear, battery life is excellent, the mics are clear for calls, and CustomTune + the new Immersive Audio features actually make a noticeable difference.
If you’ve been disappointed by AirPods Pro fit/sound or ran into issues with more “budget flagship” options like the Nothing Ear series, do yourself a favor and grab these. Yeah, they’re expensive, but after wasting time and money on returns, I’m finally happy and done shopping for earbuds. These are keepers.
Highly recommended. 5 stars (would be 4.5 if the case wasn’t so flimsy, but the performance more than makes up for it).

reviews.author: alexander berek
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2026
reviews.content:
Best ear buds I have ever owned!!! I am an audiophile and am VERY PICKY when it comes to my audio! I listen to audio ALL DAY LONG! I love BASS!! I was worried when I got these that 1. I wouldn’t get enough Bass and that 2. I wouldn’t be able to get enough volume out of these ear buds seeing that my previous ear buds wouldn’t go high enough even when they were at Max volume! So I gave these a try.
I am extremely happy to report that 1. These have wonderful bass!! Which you are able to adjust using the Bose app which is pretty easy to use. Just install after you link the ear buds to your phone or computer/laptop/tablet then install the app and adjust the bass in the EQ! EZ PZ! 2.these have more than enough volume! I am able to go to about 75% volume and leave it there depending on the app I am using and hear just fine and that is using only one earbud. I use one earbud at a time so that I am able to extend the length of time that I am able to use the earbuds throughout the day as I stay up ALL day and night but when I do use both they stay at about the same volume as well.
The battery life is GREAT!!! They last well throughout the day and night and I get about 3 charges from the case before I need to recharge the case. That is a charge of the right earbud the Left earbud and another full charge of the right ear bud and a partial charge of the Left ear bud before I have to recharge the case. You are able to charge the case by USB-C cable or by wireless charger.
When I first started wearing these earbuds they gave me some discomfort but I was able to adapt to that and now I am fine with them and have no discomfort and they feel GREAT!! They fit great in my ears and don’t move at ALL! They are snug and at times I forget that I have them in at all lol!
The case is nice and light. It’s tiny enough that it fits in my pocket and I know where it is at all times lol it has a light on the front so that when I am charging the earbuds the light radiates and when the charging case is done charging the light goes out. These are things the seller does not tell you!!
When you are using the earbuds you will know the earbuds are about to go out of battery because a nice lady will come into your ear and tell you that the battery is low two times and then u will get about 20-30 more minutes of power before they die so either change the earbud if u are using only one or charge them if u are using both!
They fit VERY snug in the case sometimes so snug you can’t get them out lol so no worries about them falling out of the case!! When u put them in your ear at first you will be greeted with the amount of battery life you have in the earbud.
Overall I am so happy with these earbuds and I don’t want to get a new pair EVER LOL! I keep these with me 24/7 and the case is in my pocket at all times. I would HIGHLY recommend these to anyone that is interested in a pair of BOSE earbuds! These are the best earbuds I have ever had and I will be buying a new pair if I ever need to again!!

reviews.author: Anime, Manga and Harlequin Romance Girl
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 11, 2025
reviews.content: I have an Android phone and I was using a different earbuds which unfortunately Im unable to use all the features it touted. So I needed an earbuds that aside from comfort I'm able to use all capabilities. I've had other Bose products and I was fairly confident that Bose wouldn't let me down. I'm happy to say that yay, I got my money's worth! The sound quality was impressive and the sample audio clip that Bose had during the setup phase really showed that off. I like that it had all these different features in there, Still Immersion, etc. So of course, I listed to my own songs and it was great. Noise cancelling was superb in my honest opinion. I heard the difference when it is on and off. I didn't hear my Mom when she was talking with the earbuds on and I'm like duh! i have the earbuds on lol. This was without any sound on so my Mom's voice was so distant and far away. I also tested the Bose with my work laptop as I conduct video meetings and again I'm very happy with it. My coworkers did not complain that they couldn't hear me so it looks like the mic placement in the Bose is great. I'm happy to no longer use my big headphones with the sticking out microphone. By the way, the Bose was Bluetooth connected to my two devices, my Android Phone and work laptop and there was no issue to start using the work laptop for the video meetings so that was very convenient. In regard to fit, I did not change the pads. It looks like medium is my size, and so far, I have not experienced any pain in my ear from wearing it too long. I've had it on for about an hour. I will have to continue to observe if I experience any pain if I wear it for more than an hour. The case itself is pretty portable, in my opinion, and it is not slippery — which I love because I don’t feel the need to purchase a case cover. This uses a USB-C and has wireless charging, and both so far have worked flawlessly. In conclusion, I totally recommend these Bose earbuds. I am using it for both work and personal reasons and so Im definitely getting the bang out of my buck.

reviews.author: ChocoT
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026
reviews.content:
I previously owned the 2020 Bose QC Earbuds. Those earbuds came with a very nice, premium charging case. The earbuds also had a different design for the rubber "wings" which helped keep the buds in the ear.

So when I took these new earbuds out of the box, I was slightly disappointed. The charging case feels pretty cheap and flimsy. The lid feels like it can be easily ripped off. These do technically have wings, they're just very small. So far I haven't had these fall out of my ears, so right now I would say it's a non-issue. What I have had problems with however, is the wing ring slipping and rotating. I discovered that when this happens, I don't really notice BUT they will prevent your earbuds from charging. This is because when they rotate, they lift the earbuds slightly when you put them back into the case which means the metal contacts don't touch with the contacts in the case.

Another disappointment was the Bose app for Android. If you have an older or low end Android device, it might not work. I'm guessing it's fine for newer Android devices. But luckily I also have an iPad, and that worked immediately. Normally this wouldn't matter but there are features I wanted to use that are only available if you use the Bose app. For example, EQ and noise cancellation profiles. It's a little annoying that if I want to change those things I have to switch to my iPad. If you have an older or lower end Android device this might be a risk for you.

On the bright side, the sound quality is decent. These won't give you an audiophile, perfectly flat frequency response. They're Bose earbuds, which means they're what I think of as fun earbuds. Plenty of bass and decently clear treble. These are earbuds you listen to if you want to have fun and jam out, *not* to appreciate the technical wonder of flawless sound reproduction. Another department these shine in is the noise cancellation. Right now, Bose and Apple are the best in the industry. I also have AirPods Pro 2, and the ANC in both are very very close. I would say the Bose QC Ultra are very slightly better in situations where you have varied noise, like someone's watching a movie in the same room or you're in a room with a lot of people talking. But again, to my ears they're almost the same - the best ANC on the market right now.

Another department these shine in is comfort. With the stock silicone ear tips, out of the box these feel great and I don't really have issues with them falling out. I absolutely do have issues with my AirPods Pro 2 falling out of my ears with the stock tips.

In conclusion...these are a fairly solid competitor against the AirPods Pro. I wish the case were of better quality, and that they would bring back the old wing design. The jury is out on how long these will last. My 2020 QC Earbuds literally died a month after the warranty expired. The left earbud stopped charging, which is apparently a common problem if you do an internet search. Hopefully they figured out how to manufacture these things. If they die right after the warranty ends I'll probably never buy another Bose product again lol.

reviews.author: Joseph
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.content: Love them. Low cost with quality. I am a tech support specialist, thus a lot of phone time. Very light, comfortable and quality sound. I like being able to hear what’s happening around me. These fit the bill on all levels

reviews.author: SkyGodSam
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.content: I'm a BIG believer in wired headsets vs blue tooth to keep the radiation on my brain down. Especially on something I'm using all day! I use it for phone calls, Zooms etc. Everyone says it sounds great on their end! Super comfy earpads, nice design.

reviews.author: Brian R.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 1, 2026
reviews.content: I’m not a subject matter expert on headsets, or even close. Truth be told, I dislike them intensely. Nevertheless I have to admit, this headset worked for me. Great sound and microphone. No fiddling around with bluetooth connection issues. Plug it in and good to go!

reviews.author: Joelayn
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 25, 2025
reviews.content: They work with Teams and that’s great. The sound quality is okay for meetings and web based training.

reviews.author: REMorgan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content: Excellent quality sound and very comfortable

reviews.author: Jennifer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 2, 2026
reviews.content: I use these for work. Great on conference calls

reviews.author: Kapil Singh
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 20, 2026
reviews.content: Works well, simple plug and play, zoom picks it up and switches over automatically. Not the fanciest of headsets out there but this is what my job recommended and it does the job quite fine.

reviews.author: Risa Quarantotto
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.content: I don’t care for the foam ear pieces and the sound quality of calls is really below par. For my preference.

reviews.author: Dwight L. Brown
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2026
reviews.content: By far the most comfortable headphones I have ever had. The self adjusting aspect is very good. They sound great and the micro fiber ear pads are wonderful. I am using them mostly for my ham radio station where they do everything I need them to do. The plug is a bit backwards in that the line has a 1/4" plug and the adapter takes that to 1/8" which is backwards from what I'm used to but it works fine. At this price they are a real bargain .

reviews.author: Belle Joe
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content: Bought them for the sound quality, online without a chance to listen or compare. I used to have a set of AKG 101 30 years ago and remembered them for incredible definition mid and high rangen, but spongy bass. These headphones are way better than those earlier ones which were already great for the money. Incredibly clear mids, highs and dynamic response. Many people will look for more bass but it is very clean and also well defined. I bought a headphone amp without which you don't get the full quality of the headphones because of their high impedance. With these headphones you really start looking for your best recorded music to enjoy it (and you discover what's not been well recorded). They wear well for a long time (usually my ear suffer with headphones, but not these). I take care not to throw them around or put things on them. They have a 3 meter cable which is nice and long. Gold connector, nice. They adjust in size easily, at least for me, just put them on without ratcheting around.

reviews.author: Will 123
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 29, 2016
reviews.content:
I upgraded from my Audio-Technica ATH-M30 (cost about 60$ to 80$) to a pair of AKG K701 headphones ($550 at launch, about 200$ to 250$ now) and so far, I'm not disappointed. A word of caution however: They are known for a potential (but mostly fixable) defect (more about that at the end of this review).

Overall sound quality: The K701's offer a well balanced and clear sound, with a very flat frequency response and a wide soundstage. The high frequencies are clear and the mids are well present. The basses are what you would expect from proper monitoring headphones: They are not "boosted" or colored at all. For some people, it might be an issue, especially if they are bass lovers. I would recommend to check out the bass mod on the Internet, it's just a matter of removing a piece of felt that is covering the bass port in each ear cups. This mod is reversible and makes the basses more "punchy", sort of like making the attack quicker on a ADSR envelope. My M30's sound kinda muffled when compared to the K701's, but they have more bass than the K701's.

Build quality: Made of plastic, with some metalic parts (mostly the decorative parts). I would not recommend these headphones if you plan to travel or go outdoor with them. The plastic is quite thin and glossy. My M30's are build tougher than the K701's in comparison.

Comfort: Clearly, the best headphones I ever had in term of comfort. They are super light and the ear pads are soft but not too soft, so they keep their shape and no plastic rests near your ears. I should mention too: These headphones use an elastic mechanism that automatically adjust the headband to the proper setting. I was not sure about this system at first, but it works very well ! In comparison, my M30's would hurt my ears after an hour or so of use because the ear pads were too soft and thin.

Now, the potential issue you might face with these headphones. AKG K701's are known for disconnected wire(s) in the ear cups. There are many reports about this issue over the Internet and a video on how to fix it. Basically, some solders are weak and tend to break, so you might lose the sound on one or both sides. Sometimes, wire might still make a contact when the ear cup is in a certain position and you will get sound, but you will lose it when moving the ear cup. This issue is usually fixable by taking apart the headphones and soldering the wire correctly. It's easy to do if you know how to follow instructions. Just be careful to not damage the little decorative grids, you should always turn counter-clockwise to remove them !

It doesn't seem to be an recent issue, since you can find reports dating as far as 2011. Recent K701's are made in China, the AKG logo on the headband is now simply "AKG" (no more "three hearts" logo) and the box is different from the original I think (it as a full-size window at the front instead of no window).

Overall, very satisfied by these headphones even with the (fixed) wire issue.

reviews.author: sdc30161
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 1, 2012
reviews.content:
I've been listening to my Grado Prestige Series SR325is Headphones for most of the year and have no complaints, other than the initial comfort factor. But I really wanted another mid-to-high end set to keep from moving the Grados from one room to another (I know, lazy, huh?). I almost went with the Sennheiser HD 650 Headphones, but I've already got a smaller pair of Sennheiser HD 238 Open Aire Stereo Headphones Optimized for iPod/iPhone/MP3/and Music Players and wanted to try the K701s because of the excellent reviews.
