On first listen, I wasn't impressed. The music didn't seem to have any depth to it, like it was all coming to you on a flat plane, if that makes any sense at all. I knew these headphones need some break-in time, so I kept listening. Mainly I plugged them into my home theater and played some Dead Space 2 on the PS3. Slowly but surely, they turned around, and now they're my go-to headphones (I still love you Grado, but you're going into a drawer for a little while).

The detail and clarity is spectacular; the bass is just right (I don't find it at all lacking as some others have noted); comfort level is fantastic. My only complaint: I really liked the thick cord on the Grados and wish the cord on the AKGs felt as sturdy. That's more of a comparison than a complaint, though.

As far as amplification goes, I don't know how these sound without it. I've only used them on a newer Pioneer 1120-K (late 2010) receiver and, in my bedroom, a Peachtree Audio Decco2, which is both a headphone amplifier and DAC in one (and a wonderful piece of equipment, I might add). I stream audio from MOG (supposedly 320 kpps, the highest rate available to my knowledge) to either an Apple TV MC572LL/A (NEWEST VERSION) or Apple Airport Express MB321LL/A [NEWEST VERSION] and then into, respectively, the Pioneer receiver or the Decco2. Both have DACs, so I feel like I'm getting a good source going into a good output, and that truly makes these headphones shine.

A quick word on DACs: I purchased a small portable DAC earlier this year and returned it. I could tell no difference in the sound. But the DAC in the Decco2 really fills the music out and makes it sound so rich and detailed. If you're going to invest good money into headphones, please consider the source going into them. I suspect if you simply plugged these headphones into your iPod with no amplification or conversion to analog, you might wonder why you spent so much money. But with the right equipment, you'll fall in love with your music all over again.

And one last thing! If you're playing video games using a home theater system, i.e. you have a console hooked up to a receiver and surround sound speakers, these headphones are truly fantastic for gaming. I've mainly been playing Dead Space 2, and the detail and directional effects are amazing. I can't imagine any of the Ear Force or Astro headphones besting these AKGs.

UPDATE JULY 2012: I've been back and forth between these cans and my Grados for the past few months, with most of my listening through Grado. But I m sitting here now listening to Scissor Sister's new album on my AKGs, and I often forget how great these sound, so detailed and full and lush. They're more impressive now than when I first purchased them. Highly recommended. If I had to chose between the AKG and the Grado 325, I'd probably still go with Grado, just because the build is so sturdy and they're Made in America. But there is nothing wrong with the AKG at all. Excellent stuff all around!

reviews.author: Barry J Maher
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content: Love the sound, love the comfort. I also like how they don’t artificially pump up the bass. I typically use them to listen to small group jazz - quartets, piano trios. They provide a detailed sound and I can wear them for hours without discomfort. I would have like to see a cord that can be detached and replaced, but I’ve had no issues.

reviews.author: ap
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 25, 2021
reviews.content:
These headphones are nothing short of incredible. I'd definitely recommend pairing with a DAC/amp to get the best quality and balance but these still sound great being driven by a Macbook audio port. Before buying, I saw some complaints about these being light in the bass range. Although it's not artificially compensated (like many Beats products, for example), these still deliver an appropriate and balanced amount of bass for the song. In terms of mids and highs, these are just as terrific here.

When it comes to comfort, these are incredibly lightweight and breathable, although I wish they used more metal instead of plastic (see below). Ear cups are soft but firm enough to stay in place.

Now that I talked about the good, I'm going to mention a couple less amazing points. Firstly, I wish these weren't made with as much plastic. For this price point, I'd like to see better materials. Still, this doesn't affect the audio quality. Another point I'd like to mention is how our music culture has become so accustomed to artificially bass-heavy music and how volume and bass response have become the main selling points of many mainstream audio products. These K701s sound natural....like the artist is giving me a personal concert in my own home. Switching back to other products such as Beats, AirPods, etc. after using these just further exposes the flaws in mainstream products. Although this isn't some life-changing cause, it's important to note that you likely won't enjoy cheaper headphones as much as you did before.

reviews.author: Entony
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026
reviews.content:
I was choosing a new pair of headphones and immediately ordered a dozen models. Including several from AKG, namely the K612, K712, K701 and K371.
Let's start with the K371. Although they are the cheapest, they are a very decent device. They don't press on the head, and the soft ear cushions allow you to wear the headphones for a long time. The device stays on the head very securely, you can easily take it outside for a run, a bike ride or just a walk.
The rather strange design raises some questions. It's unclear how fragile it is. Judging by many reviews here: very fragile. The headphones sound decent for their price, a little bassy.
The K612, K712 and K701 headphones are of a completely different type: they are open-back and significantly larger, you can't take them outside, and there's no point. A detachable cable is only available on the K712, and that's a shame. I believe that absolutely all headphones more expensive than ~$100 should have a detachable cable. But there are detailed photo instructions on the Internet on how to add and solder a connector yourself.
They all sit on the head perfectly, albeit with slight differences. The K712 is the "older brother" of the K612, so the ear cushions are slightly improved. They are a little thicker, softer and made of a different material (memory foam, it seems). So the K712 is a little more comfortable than the K612, but that doesn't mean that the K612 themselves are uncomfortable. There are practically no differences between the K612 and K701 in terms of comfort.
Sound. Personally, I liked the K612 the most for its clean, "unbiased" sound. In them, you can focus on all the instruments or vocals, and none of them "stick out" too much. The K712 are generally considered a more bass-heavy version of the K612, but I only partially agree with this: there is a difference, but it's very small. At the same time, it seemed to me that the K712 lost some clarity and purity compared to the K612. The K701 are a completely different pair. They have very strong high frequencies, and the low frequencies are, on the contrary, weakened. While the K612 and K712 are easily suitable for simple and enjoyable music listening, the K701 might seem "boring" due to the unexpressive bass, but they are very detailed! You can hear all the nuances of the composition, and that's really cool. I also think they would be well suited for FPS games.
Regarding reliability. Things are bad here. There are many bad reviews with photos of the K371 regarding the reliability of the construction. The K series headphones have known problems with the quality of soldering of the thin cables inside, which often leads to the sound disappearing in one of the headphones. I personally encountered a similar problem twice: the first pair of K701 I received had a non-working left earphone, and in the K712, during testing, the sound started to cut out.
Also, in the K series, the black headband is held by four thin elastic threads, two on each side. They constantly pull the headband down, allowing people with different head shapes to wear the headphones comfortably. However, over time, these threads degrade and deteriorate, like office rubber bands, which means the headband will always be in the highest position, making it difficult for people with smaller heads to wear the headphones. One of the pairs I received had exactly these worn-out elastic bands, and I could only wear the headphones because my head is quite large. In the end, I returned all the AKG headphones and kept a pair from a completely different brand, but if it weren't for the build quality issues, I would have kept the K612 for home listening, I really liked them. Or the K371 for the gym and cycling trips.
I hope this short review will help someone make a decision and find their favorite pair of headphones.

reviews.author: Zeljko M.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content:
I am enjoying these earphones. Sound is very recognisable AKG sound.
They need an amp in between iPhone and phones.

reviews.author: T. Malloy
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 19, 2025
reviews.content:
Fantastic lightweight, great audio single ear headset. The ear cup is lightweight, buttery soft material with breathable pinholes for airflow- genius. Most headsets give me an instant earache which turns into a headache but after doing my research to find one that is truly comfortable I am so happy with my decision. I've been wearing them for two months now and they're just as great as a day, I got them. My other favorite feature is just raising the microphone to mute. It's a very simple set up and it's never run out of a charge. I'm glad I got the charging stand, but I have used the headset for 10 hours at a time and never a problem.

UPDATE: I noticed some mild static initially when I got this headset, but it was manageable and I thought maybe it was just my connection but the static has gotten increasingly worse and there is no live support at YEAHLINK AND NOW I CAN'T RETURN THE ITEM BECAUSE IT'S BEEN TOO MUCH TIME SINCE JUNE. I SHOULD'VE RETURNED IT IMMEDIATELY. SUPER COMFORTABLE, BUT STATIC CONNECTION AND LACK OF CUSTOMER SUPPORT DOES NOT WORK FOR ME.

reviews.author: CLF
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 23, 2026
reviews.content: This is much more comfortable than my previous headset. I wear it for hours without a headache. The noise reduction keeps my co-workers from hearing my kids in the background. It connects easily and sound quality is just fine for meetings.

reviews.author: Jordan H.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 2, 2025
reviews.content:
Yea, it’s all you need.
Got these for my wife, we have a 2 year old boy.. enough said.
Plus a generator running outside.
It’s like she’s in a room on her own, no noise coming through at all and I was shouting while testing.
Easy setup and straightforward controls. Using the dongle but Bluetooth seems pretty easy.
Light and comfortable ear cushions.
Also does a good job of passive noise canceling.
Great built quality and looks sleek.

reviews.author: Dog Mom
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 24, 2025
reviews.content:
I really wanted to like this headset, but I will end up returning. I wear glasses and am on calls all day, easily spending up to 12-15 hours with my headset on. The headset simply places to much pressure on my head. After 4 hours, my head is killing me from the pressure. The ear piece is just to big and bulky and the headband grips my head to tightly. It’s very uncomfortable for all day wear.

I’ve been using a Jabra 9450 for 10 years. It works great still and I love it. It’s extremely comfortable. I wanted an upgrade to have on headset mute button so I can walk around and unmute myself without needed to run back to my computer. I also don’t need desk phone functionality anymore and was looking for a stand that took up less desk space. I’ll be going back to this until I can find a suitable headset.

Things I love:
- headset mute button + raising the mic to mute
- Simple charging base
- Busy light indicator
- Ability to raise my hand from the headset. I can fully participate in meetings no matter where I am! Plus, works with my cell phone, very easy to switch over.
- The audio indicators when you mute/unmute
- The software is just what you need, nothing less and nothing more.
- Worked straight out of the box
- Great quality, comes with a travel case and a dongle storage.
- General features are perfect, everything you need!
- Affordable! Some headset are $200+ which is way too much money for everyday people. This gives you everything you need for just the right price.

Things I hate:
- the biggest con: uncomfortable! very heavy/bulky, creates so much pressure on the head and ear after about 4 hours. The pain is to much.
- When you walk the mic jiggles, super annoying when you’re walking and talking. very distracting while I was trying to speak
- The headset must be placed on the charging station carefully or it doesn’t click to charge, causing it to be dead the next day. I’ve had this less than 30 days and twice I’ve had a dead headset from it not being on the charging base correctly.
- The battery could be better
- range is terrible, can’t even fill my water up in the kitchen without it cutting out.

The WH64 model could solve the range and battery. The jiggly mic and stand placement are annoying, but not a big deal. Sadly, as it’s similar in size and style, it is still just too much pressure and pain for long term daily wear. It’s a real shame because it’s a great headset otherwise. It checks all the boxes, except comfort.

reviews.author: Troy Light
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 5, 2026
reviews.content: Comfortable to wear all day. Works both for calls and as headphones for PC. Has a downloadable program to tweak setting just to your liking. I like it let's you answer by just pulling down the mic. Battery lasts at least 10 hours. Works a good distance from the base too.

reviews.author: Barb
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content: Good quality and sound easy to sync

reviews.author: Sol T
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 4, 2026
reviews.content: Bluetooth usb does not work, also, it keeps on connecting and disconnecting to Bluetooth by itself

reviews.author: William Brizuelas
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.content: I use this headset everyday for work! Works great and my customers can't hear any background noise just my voice

reviews.author: Brian Cortez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 1, 2025
reviews.content: These are amazing . I am a driver so I use only one earbud at a time , and one earbud last me 8-9 hrs it last all my shift . Good sound , compact , it connects to my phone instantly as soon as I take them off the case . The ear hook does seem to be a little flimsy asfter the 5 hrs , but I have to flame that on my sweat. They don’t fall off and they are for the most part comfortable

reviews.author: Deborah Ingram
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 4, 2026
reviews.content: Love Skull Candy products

reviews.author: jennifer
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 2, 2026
reviews.content: They look very nice, they stay in my ears while at the gym or everyday stuff. The battery last me a long time. Easy fit in my pocket or any bag and they have connected to everything I've tried easily.

reviews.author: Jo KFall
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.content: These blue ones weren't as comfy as the black ones, but they are still comfortable for my sensitive earholes. I don't know what it is about the shape of these Skullcandy ones, but all these other brands hurt my ears, and these just don't. I like the hook over the year for being on an elliptical, and that extra snug feel. Oh sound quality is good- I don't listen to music or audiobooks loudly so it works well for my needs.

reviews.author: Sasicin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 2, 2026
reviews.content: Purchased for a grandfather that always had trouble finding in ear wired earphones that fit and were comfortable. He loves these and says they are comfortable.

reviews.author: Dk0811
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 20, 2025
reviews.content:
Why did you pick this product vs others?:
I love the skull candy brand and have used it for years. These ear buds were perfect for what I wanted and really did stay in my ears through all kinds of activity. I also really like that they don’t hurt my ears and the little piece that goes over your ear is very flexible and comfortable. BUT I’ve only had them a little over a month and my left earbud stopped charging. Inconveniently for me, right after my return window closed. It’s dead now and refuses to charge which is so strange to me since I use the right one so much more and only use the left one when I’m working out. The right side works great, but it’s annoying now to workout with only one bud. 

Update: Now even my right one is having trouble charging. At this point I’m just waiting for it to stop working completely. I’ve only had them for around two months. And apparently I can’t return them after 30days even through their defective. If you’re going to buy these, get them from somewhere else with a better return policy.

reviews.author: GDWolf
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 30, 2025
reviews.content: Works okay but not the best sound quality for this one than I'm used to. Also a flaw to the charging design sometimes it's hard to get them to charge without using a cloth or isopropyl achohol, poor charging design. The arms on the ear piece also don't clean well and discolor.

reviews.author: Brian W. Goodman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 5, 2025
reviews.content: These ear buds have awesome sound and connect super fast and easy. I have a pair for my office at work and one for at home. They are well worth the money.

reviews.author: M W
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2015
reviews.content:
I want to start off by saying I'm not any kind of audiophile. I have no expertise on sound quality or the like, so keep that in mind when reading further. Any notes on sound quality should be taken with a grain of salt (though I won't dwell on that much).

I use these headphones on a daily basis for my job. I am an IT help-desk agent for my job, meaning I am constantly on the phone. This requires a headset with acceptable sound quality, a good microphone, and most importantly, sustained comfort. These headphones have provided exactly what I needed.

I mentioned before that I'm no audiophile, but the sound quality is great for any average user. I don't listen to a lot of music, but podcasts sounds nice and crisp. The output volume is great, and can reach levels above what I'm comfortable using when listening on an iPhone. My normal use at my job is all analog phone quality audio, so most of the time high quality audio isn't even an option. But, these allow me to hear my calls just fine (and are great when making calls on iPhone, too - Facetime audio sounds as crisp as anything).

There are issues with sound bleed. At mid-level volumes, people nearby will be able to hear some audio coming from the headphones. Also, as on-ear headphones, they have zero noise cancelling. I am not docking them any points for this, as that's sort of par for the course with on-ears. If you need noise cancelling and zero sound bleed, try another kind of headphone (over-ear or earbuds).

The microphone picks up fairly well. I've done some audio tests with the sound recorder on my iPhone, and it seems to have pretty good range. No replacement for a studio mic or anything, but it's more than acceptable for normal use. Siri/voice commands work with no issues. I also use these as a headset with my PS4, and I've had no complaints when playing with friends (though, the quality of voice chat over PSN is less than great). At work, I have to wrap the cable around the ear-muff to bring the microphone closer to my mouth for better clarity when talking to users on the other end. I don't think that is worth docking a star, as it doesn't affect the fit or feel when I do it, but I think it should be mentioned.

What I was most surprised by is the level of comfort with these. I have pretty sensitive ears, and they are pretty easily fatigued when wearing headphones. I've had many, many pairs of over-ear phones that give me ear pain after about an hour of use. These, however, are comfortable for the 5 to 7 hours I wear them at my job. The foam on the earpads is thick and will actually mold to my ear shape after a bit. The foam rounds back out overnight, so it doesn't pack in and lose padding. The earpads also have a decent range of vertical and horizontal rotation on its pivot point (which is a ball pivot). This should accommodate most ear shapes.

The headband is aluminum, which gives them a little rigidity, but is flexible enough to not clamp too hard on your head. It's also quite light, though maybe heavier than plastic would be. The band is also designed so the connectors on the earpads can adjust about 3/4 of the way to the top of the band before they slide out. This means the headphones have a wide range of fit, and can be disassembled for easy storage, as well as customization.

The headphones come with 3 pairs of earpad connectors - the part attaching the earpads to the headband. I purchased the black/black colorway, which came with black, red, and green connectors. These slide out of the headband by adjusting them towards the top, and pop off the earpads by pulling them off with a moderate tug.

Construction seems great. The aluminum band appears to be anodized, the earpads are a high quality foam, and the cups and connectors are sturdy plastic. They're constructed with individual cables for each earpad, and they connect at a large rubber joint, joining into a single cable (about a meter long from the joint), which ends at a 90-degree angled 8mm audio jack. The cabling is all rubber coated, and is a fairly thick gauge throughout.

If you have any questions, or if there's anything I may have left out, please leave a comment and I'll try to update quickly!

reviews.author: Chan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2025
reviews.content:
I have a lot of conference calls and have many headsets (lost counting, but all wired). I used typical video call headsets with a boom mic (Sennheiser headsets are good for work, but not great for music). My beloved Teenage Engineering headsets earpieces were worn after several years of abuse (my best travel companion) - unable to purchase ear covers separately (TE responded to buy a new one at a marginal discount (I think 10% off from the retail price). I don't want to have another one since my current one is working fine except messy looking. Hence, search for new kind of headset began (what a strange logic, but this justified my need - great sound for not only conference call and music, plus style!!)

Fast forward. I love my Aiaiai headphone (3.5mm style)!! Traditional and simple headband style (metal structure). I worried mic due to location (conventional cellphone earphones mic, couple inches below your chin), but I was wrong - it is crisp!! Not so much noise cancellation (well, there is none), but clear enough for discussion - not recommended for very loud environments. Since there is no boom mic, I don't need to flip it before sipping my coffee.

I am not an audiophile, but its sound quality is superb, not awesome as listening only headphones/IEM earphones - subtle boost on bass, but mostly flat (good for not only classical and jazz, but heck, also heavy metal!!). No need to crank the volume to hear what others are saying even though this is "on ear" headphones. Very comfortably sitting on my ears for long usage (4-5 hours of back-to-back zoom meetings every day) - this might be subject since some might say too tight (good for me).

Only minor thing I would like to address is pinching - if you have a long hair, be warned: this headset will bite your hair when you take it off. I don't mind this is 3.5mm version at all.

If you are currently searching for a new wired headset with mic, please give it a try!! You are going to love it!!!

reviews.author: AML
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 16, 2012
reviews.content:
Only had them a few days so these are early impressions. However: had the same delight receiving the package and opening these headphones you might have opening an Apple product. Packaging is high design, high quality - and so is the product.

The phones look great and have a simple, unfussy design. The ear pieces are comfortable and swivel to fit your head; the control buttons, with embedded mic, are tactile but not overly sensitive. You won't inadvertently cue forward to the next track.

But most importantly - to me, at least - they sound really great. The sound is balanced and rich. The bass is full and clear without boominess. Music is pleasurable to my ears, and I look forward to putting them on. Phone call audio is equally clear and balanced.

And they don't fall out or need to be drilled deep into my ear canal to hear the bass properly, like earbuds. I realized I can't stand earbuds, and these were my solution - couldn't be happier, so far.

Only con I am aware of so far - they are of course bulkier than simple earbuds, you can't just stuff them in your pocket. On the up side, there is less to get tangled up cables-wise (another reason I loathe earbuds).

They aren't dirt cheap, but $85 seems reasonable given the quality and design. They do seem like if you sat on them, they might not fare so well - the long term durability is unknown. But for the normal 2-4 year lifespan of a pair of headphones - I don't see why not...

Enjoy!

reviews.author: Stephanie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 31, 2015
reviews.content:
AIAIAI is a danish headphone company that is relatively unheard of and I admit I hadn't heard of them until coming across these headphones. The unique look to these intrigued me as did the positive reviews. They are a great pair of headphones for casual use and their design is reminiscent of vintage style headphones. I've had numerous people ask me where I found these. They come with a carrying bag and three sets of sliders (black, red, green). I interchanged the right side of the headphones slide (the right side has the mic) with a red slider so they would easily be differentiated (and red indicates right stereo).
The good:
-Sound quality is clear and unconstrained in the way people complain earbuds do. Very clear bass and not tinny at all. Balanced mids and highs provide a very nice listening experience. Sound as good or better than my portapros however the portapros are much cheaper but with these you are clearly paying partly for design and name.
-Unique design.
-Fairly comfortable, not a strong clamping force.
-Take up very little room.
-Powered easily by a portable device.

Possible caveats
-Made for small heads. I have a medium sized head and have the ear pieces extended almost as far as they will go, will not fit large heads.
-A lot A LOT of noise leakage. There is nothing preventing the sound from escaping and they are very open. At average listening volumes people around me could tell the song I was listening to. They are on-ear headphones in a sense but they do not hug your ears the way normal on ear heads do and instead just sort of sit on your ear.
-Do not fold up. While these aren't very large they are not collapsible and could easily break.
-Build quality. While there isn't anything particularly weak about the build quality there's an overall lack of materials that can be seen by the picture and I wouldn't recommend these for people who are rough on their headphones.

Overall an interesting pair of headphones that are convenient for casual use but if you are looking for a specifically small pair of headphones that fold up for travel check out Koss Portapros. For durable on ear headphones that can stand up to abuse you're going to have to go up in price and might want to check out v-moda.

reviews.author: graham a.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 25, 2025
reviews.content:
sounds quality is amazing, they look super cool, and i love the design.

problem is the cable started fragging after a few months - i have to disconnect and reconnect to my phone when audio cuts out. that's not the worst of it though - sometimes when audio goes bad it blasts an internse super super LOUD white noise that feels like it causes me ear damage.

1 month later.. the headphones are now unusable, they cut in and our every few seconds. staying w two stars cuz they were awesome - until they broke. $60 for 4 months of use just isn't enough

reviews.author: Stephen B.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 28, 2012
reviews.content:
I received my Tracks only a few days ago, but it's already clear that they are perfect (for me).

I do not want headphones that attempt to replace a stereo system. I have a stereo system, and like it very much.

I do not want ear buds. I have ear buds (good quality), but do not like the feel of ear buds.

I want headphones that have good clear sound, and are light weight and comfortable. Tracks are perfect (for me). I like their looks, but that is personal. I like their design because it seems simple, durable, and adjustable to fit anybody. The cord is about 55".

In a store demo, I tried Monster Beats Pro by Dr. Dre and Bose Quiet Comfort 15. I preferred the Beats Pro, and I think both of them have better sound than Tracks. However, Tracks have good sound, and the other two are not what I want. They're both heavier, less comfortable, bulkier, with joints that can break, and I don't want headphones that require a battery.

Before I bought my Tracks, I sent email to the manufacturer to ask the cord length. I received a prompt reply. Interestingly, it said the cord length is about 3.5 feet (i.e., 42"). My guess is that the answer measured the cord to the point where it divides rather than to the ear pads.

reviews.author: Michael Knoll
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 20, 2020
reviews.content:
Pros: Very good sound quality for music, and they also have a built in microphone without a boom for phone calls. Light weight. Compact. Fairly comfortable.

Cons: The earpieces keep falling off the headband and are exceedingly difficult or impossible to reattach. They're poorly attached with tiny plastic beads. Jostling them frequently knocks them off. It's too hard to reattach them. I've given up on these and am discarding them.

reviews.author: Parker Ponder
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 28, 2025
reviews.content: They are very aesthetically, pleasing and sound nice. Very good value.

reviews.author: Justin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 15, 2025
reviews.content: I love these headphones so much, I had another pair and they lasted me 5 years (they survived through highschool!) and finally broke on me when they fell in the sink while doing dishes. The sound quality is just impeccable, both bluetooth and wired, and there's not that annoying little delay like some bluetooth headphones have. they're super comfy too, the padding is soft and also cancels out outside noise! I adore them, I'll never buy another brand of headphones.

reviews.author: FireSoul LA
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 4, 2025
reviews.content: These wireless headphones have an amazing sound quality. The bass is deep, clear, and powerful. They look aesthetically pleasing as well. They are the best wireless headphones I’ve ever bought. You can completely block out all the noise. Don’t listen to anyone who says otherwise.

reviews.author: Deryck Trimble
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.content: I have had these headphones before but from walmart. Good quality and sound for the price. Love the future of either being wireless or if your batteries are dead you have the option to plug them in directly to any device with a audio port

reviews.author: Clara W
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 17, 2026
reviews.content: Headphones are great quality, very comfortable, and can be very loud if that’s what you want. Bluetooth functions great. The cord that comes with it isn’t that good, so I would recommend buying a different cord to go with it for something like a computer.

reviews.author: K S
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 20, 2025
reviews.content: I LOVE my Skullcandy headphones!!!!! I've owned several Skullcandy headphones/ earbuds! These headphones have an EXCELLENT battery. Takes about 60 minutes to fully charge and will go for several hours of use. If used just a little bit at a time one charge can last several days. The headphones covers the ears fully and are comfortable to wear. The buttons are easy to use. It connects easily once you figure out how to sync it. Black is my favorite color and the quality is great for the price!

reviews.author: V
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 19, 2025
reviews.content: My son loves these for making music. He tried the more expensive ones of this brand and did not like them, so these are a much better deal. After about a year they end up breaking, but we still rebuy because they're worth it.

reviews.author: Vince Mcknight
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 8, 2025
reviews.content: Great sound, good battery life, no complaints Recommend.

reviews.author: Kat
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 21, 2025
reviews.content: Skullcandy should really be putting Beats outta business. The price point to sound quality ratio for Cassette headphones in particular is literally insane. The music feels *inside* your head, the fit of the headphones ensures that no sound escapes. You get full bass and mids plus crisp percussion. Connecting and volume control is a breeze and the game changer is the back up aux cord. Nonstop listening. I am so *greatful* (left the typo because i feel great this morning because these headphones arrived into my life). These are going to give me plenty of great experiences. Thank you Skullcandy. You have a lifetime customer in me (over 15 years and still going with this brand).

reviews.author: Joe #1
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 9, 2025
reviews.content: These were a gift, and the recipient (90) is quite pleased. They are exclusively used for listening to TV. They're easy to use, connect reliably, sound decent enough, and attractively priced. I bought two pair so when #1 needs recharging, the user can switch to pair #2 while charging pair #1. They go for a few days before needing a charge. JVC builds a reliable product, unlike their competitors to the west whose products just do not measure up.

reviews.author: paul bernard
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 24, 2025
reviews.content: These are really good headphones. They make music sound really good. I just wish the headband was a little bit thinner

reviews.author: Stephen Paul Malott
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 19, 2026
reviews.content: 22rd pair. Pretty sturdy and adequate sound. folds flat to place under my pillow so it doesn’t bother others

reviews.author: rkb
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 4, 2025
reviews.content: 1 year later review. My husband uses his JVC several times a week for the past year. They have held up very well, the base is good and strong and the volume is true. Bluetooth is easy. The adjustable padded ear covers are so much more comfortable than earbuds!

reviews.author: MAX
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 21, 2025
reviews.content: Affordable, good sound quality and long Bluetooth range

reviews.author: Sarah Ehrman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 18, 2023
reviews.content:
I'll go for days or weeks without using it or charging it, connect it to my phone, and it'll still day it's at 90%. most other things will be under 50% or die within an hour's usage
i like jvc branded headphones for their affordability and quality of sound. it's the one brand i know what quality and longevity I'm getting when i buy any kind of headphones or earbuds from them. (i actually started out on the gumy, then tried some very cheap headphones of theirs, and I've had them last throughout a lot of daily handling as headphones to use out and about in daily life and taking in and out of bags. so i wasn't too afraid to invest in their own wireless one. so far it's been great, and I've owned the headset for a few months now!

reviews.author: Frank Camilo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 28, 2026
reviews.content: I bought these headphones to use while traveling by plane. On the first flight, I lost one of the ear tips. I don't know how it came off, but now I can't use them. Furthermore, they are on-ear, not over-ear, as stated in the sales data. How can I buy a replacement?

reviews.author: S. W.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 23, 2025
reviews.content: Really good sound and deep bass. Connects easily. Over the ear not great on hot days. Great quality for the price.

reviews.author: Patricia Miller
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 3, 2026
reviews.content:
Best headphones for my sanity! Father in law and husband
Both hard of hearing and the sound was driving me crazy! They both love them and my husband uses them on iPad too. Long battery life. The tv is on at least 14 hrs then put on charger at night. Haven’t ran down yet! Easy to hook up and very comfortable but my father in law takes his glasses off if not needed cause together they bother his ears . Perfect sound and quality!

reviews.author: DP
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 1, 2025
reviews.content:
While watching TV,
have you ever become frustratingly annoyed to hear your husband or wife demandingly inquire at the most pivotal and strategic moment of the movie
“What did they say? … what did they say?”

Of course you have, especially, if you are over 60 years old and one or both of you have experienced some level of hearing loss! This is so frustrating for both parties and neither are to blame, but neither will be able to enjoy the movie or TV Show.

I know and speak from experience with this exact problem. Even with a hearing aid, I sometimes could not hear the words coming from the TV speakers with clarity. So, I would find my self saying those four horribly frustrating words to my wife,
time and time again, to the point it drastically affected and negatively impacted our TV watching together.

Without thinking, I would instinctively increase the volume trying to hear the words without success, but doing a great job at blowing the doors off the neighbors house two blocks down the street with my volume.
Further realizing, that when I watched TV by myself, my inexpensive Amazon wireless headphones allowed me to hear each word substantially better and I could regulate the volume as needed without interfering or affecting anyone one else.

So, assuming I could find a pair of headphones that both my wife and I could wear that would allow us to watch and hear the same program at the same time, yet, still have independent controls, that could / would resolve this particular problem allowing us to enjoy watching TV and movies together again.

After several hours of Internet research, and the visiting of multiple electronic stores, therein, inquiring from their Tech Specialist as to the best possible makes and brands producing this type product, Eureka, I found it!

The Avantree HT41899 - Dual Bluetooth Wireless Headsets.

I ordered the headsets hoping they would provide the needed resolution to my four word problem as noted above …
and with great satisfaction,
I share with you today …
that it did exactly what I / we needed it to do!

We are now able to enjoy together whatever media comes through our TV, like Movies, TV Programs, music, DVD’s, etc.

It is simple to hook-up … there is a wire that you plug one end of into the earphone jack of your TV, and the other end into a small transmitter that will then, send a wireless signal to both headsets at the same time so you both can hear much more clearly, while each can still control your individual volume directly on the headset (with a volume control button). It simple, it works, it’s awesome!

For those of you whom have the newest top of the line integrated TV Systems, yes, the devise has several additional ways to attach and install the devise to your TV, Sound Bar and or Audio System. Most everything you will need for a standard set-up comes packed with the devise.

My wife and I have now been using and enjoying the Avantree Dual Wireless Headphone System for over a year with no problems, no hiccups, and most importantly, my wife enjoys watching TV with me again, because I do not have to constantly ask … those four frustrating words any longer!

Being able to hear is a wonderful thing …
Good Luck!

reviews.author: Bruce Ames
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 10, 2026
reviews.content: Easy installation... Comfortable Headphones to wear ...with Crisp Quality Sound. On/off switch and Volume Control on Headphones- Excellent. But other reviews are right -when they complain that the overall volume is NOT LOUD ENOUGH. These would be frustrating for anyone with a hearing impairment or hearing aids!

reviews.author: M. Gibson
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 8, 2025
reviews.content:
I started having problems with my Sennheiser HR 120 headsets with interference so I figured it was time for me to do a Bluetooth setup with my Smart TV. I wasn't expecting the sound quality to match the Sennheiser but boy was I wrong. It is better than the Sennheiser, and the headphones are more comfortable as well and don't fall off of my head when I bend over. Sound quality is actually better as well and it does not suffer from dropout like the other set did. One thing that was not advertised is that these headphones actually have two equalizer presets in them neutral and voice. The neutral setting is actually really good because the voice quality is there and the lows might be slightly emphasized but it doesn't sound excessively amplified.
I originally chose to try these because this is the only set that I could find that was Bluetooth and two headsets. I need two headsets because my wife and I like to watch TV in the evenings together but not keep our kids up with the TV volume on. Also, I have a moderate to severe hearing loss and these can get uncomfortably loud even for me and still not distort much.
I had not heard of this company before but I definitely will have no problems buying from them again in the future if I need headsets or if I know of someone looking for headsets to connect to their TV.

reviews.author: Still_Standin
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 25, 2025
reviews.content:
when we opened the box there were a few pictures but all the instructions were in other languages... In one place it said to scan QR code, so we did but it offered the some sort of AI that this old lady cannot understand...
I finally did find something to scan that downloaded instructions.. but.. there are 47 pages and it's confusing..
Update.... Slept on it.. ugh... Okay, we decided to given them a try... Since we already had headphones off another brand we just plugged out called into this transmitter.. turned on headphones and they paired automatically.. easy . The sound is great, crisp. We set the audio on fixed as we had better volume.. and they are really nice headphones. Lil black button on them that goes between 'balanced' & 'clear voice'.. bc they have performed so well I updated & upgraded to 5 stars.. but you do need to put English instructions in the printed materials with the other 8 or 9 languages...

reviews.author: M.R.
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 19, 2025
reviews.content: I've used headsets for years while listening to shows while I clean and cook. My old ones, different brand, would cut in and out and give static if you moved too much. These are wonderful!!! My dad had a set and I was amazed how clear they were, zero static. I had to get a set for my home and can move pretty much anywhere in my house and not lose clarity! The range has even gone as far as taking the trash out to side of house and not lose connection, depends I'm sure on location of setup. Very comfortable, lighter weight than others I've owned and the battery life has been excellent. After a few days of use, we just plug them into charger overnight and they are ready for another several days. I might add that I'm a homemaker and listen to TV shows, podcasts, court TV shows a lot throughout the day so the battery life seems to be really great so far. Another great thing is they aren't hard to install and we can disconnect and move to our RV when we are camping and want to watch a movie at night without irritating fellow campers! Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Melody Minor
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 22, 2021
reviews.content:
When we first received, everything worked fine. Both sets were easy to pair. The second set to turn on can sometimes take a few minutes/attempts to get connected, but then works fine. Unfortunately, just about a month after purchase, one set of headphones will no longer charge at all. I was hoping it was a cable issue, but such was not the case. The other set works fine, but my husband and I use these together to watch tv in the evening to keep the noise level down in the house while others are sleeping, so just having one of the headphones working is useless to us. Contacted the manufacturer. Will update, if results.
Update: The manufacturer responded and sent us a new headset. It charged, paired, and works. However, now the second headset, which was working previously, will no longer pair. Frustrated! Have reached out to the manufacturer again.
Second update: So they mixed up the two work orders, told me to send back the red pair after I told them the fixes for pairing didn't work, and then they closed the ticket. Now they claim they meant send the black pair back, which it was over a week later that they asked me, so I assumed they meant send the red tagged pair back in before they would replace it, since I just told them this and thought maybe they were suspicious at the second pair going out, so I sent the red-tagged pair back. Then, it took them so long to "check into this" after several emails, that now they say since it was over two weeks since the red tagged pair was received back, they threw them out, so they could not send back to me, so basically too bad. And why would I want back a pair that was not working? I tried their fixes before I even sent them back, and I had my son, who is an IT specialist and can certainly pair headphones, try for me, as well, which I told them. So, I paid $170 for one set of not very good headphones, which I have a great set of single user headphones, frankly, much better than these that I payed half as much for. Yes, I'm frustrated, as their customer service was slow, then lacked clarity, then due to their own slowness tossing out the set I sent back since over two weeks, even though I had initiated the conversation about the snafu inside that time frame, I'm the one out of luck.

reviews.author: Cataptra
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 6, 2023
reviews.content:
DISLIKES: I really loathe companies that DO NOT SUPPLY power adapters or chargers with their products, anything that requires power or recharging SHOULD ALWAYS come with the appropriate cables and power supplies to make them work. It is one of my BIGGEST COMPLAINTS when I purchase an item, and then I find out I have to purchase adapters or chargers or cables that SHOULD come with the device. This item came with 1 charging cable for TWO earphones, and 1 UBS cable to connect to the TV set for powering the transmitter to the headphones, NOT ALL TV's have USB ports or they only have ONE and it may already be in use. My soundbar has one, but it would NOT power the transmitter on, my TV USB port WOULD NOT power the transmitter either. So I had to go and try and find an adapter to be able to use these headphones. Not only that,I had to buy a 2nd cable so I could charge them both at the same time, since they only supply one charging cable for a set of 2 earphones, each requiring their own cable ti charge.

Because of these two things, that's why this review only has a rating of 3-Stars, had there been an adapter fr powering the transmitter, a 2nd adapter for recharging the headphones and 2 charging cables, instead of 1, then this would have in all probability been a 5-Star review, especially for the cost of this item, those additional items should have been included, otherwise the price is somewhat too high for what you get.

It's difficult to find the charging port flap on the earphones to open it to plug in the USB cable to charge the earphones. I would have chosen some other color other than a black rubberized covering on black based earphones. If the rubber covering had been white, red, yellow or any other lighter color, other than black or dark grey, it would have been easier to locate and open on the correct side.

Can't turn off the female voice prompts when the headphones are turned on or off, having to wait for that when deciding to use them or put them away can be quite annoying at times.

LIKES:
Independent volume control for each user of the headphones. Color coded, so you know which headphone goes with the correct number on the transmitter. Instructions say only headphones #1 can be used by itself, but headphones #2, can't without headphone one being in use. I found this is inaccurate, and yes, the red headphones will also work independently from headphones #1. So you can use either the black, #1 or the red #2 headphones by themselves or both together, just select which headphone you're using on the transmitter.

Sound quality is decent, however, if you're using a soundbar with your TV, you need to MUTE your soundbar, if you don't, you'll get an echo effect from the headphones, this was using the optical cables. Not sure how other non-optical audio cables would work.

Distance is unknown, as I haven't tested them throughout my house yet. But they work fine in bed, while my wife is sleeping, I can relax and watch TV at whatever volume level that is comfortable for me using the headphones.

I can recommend them, but with the caveat you'll need to find or buy some USB adapters to charge your headphones and one to power the transmitter to use them. Especially if you want to charge both earphones at the same time will require either their own adapter for each headphone and cable, so a total of 3 USB adapters are needed, 2 for recharging and 1 for powering the transmitter. Or for recharging the headphones, a powered USB [electrically powered] hub connected to a computer USB port, or a powered stand-alone USB "charging" hub with multiple charging ports to plug the headphones into for charging. Fortunately, I do have such a powered USB hub connected to my computer and has 4 high power charging ports that are powered, so I have a 4 port charging area set up near my computer for charging all my rechargeable items. Which does work very well for recharging these headphones, and other rechargeable items I use frequently.

reviews.author: chrising
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 18, 2024

reviews.author: SunBeautie
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 22, 2024
reviews.content: When it comes to School classrooms, it can be hard for teachers to have to supply things like headphones for testing as well as technology class. At the elementary school level, most of the time you really only need 8 to 10 at a time as students are rotating and sharing these devices (not in technology specials). For the teachers a pack like this is great for students. I personally like the padded circular earphones better than the just padded sleeve on some of the others that you see. This pack is small and compact which makes it easy for storing away when not in use. The headband portion is definitely on the Slimmer side and these are definitely size more for an elementary school use and not for adult. The sound quality is pretty good and I tested it myself. The headband is adjustable fairly easy although I would say that the portion that covers the ear is a tad smaller than you would prefer although my son says they are still comfortable. I feel like they are not much bigger than Oreo sized. But for a pack of 10 for a classroom the price is very reasonable.

reviews.author: Angelina M Stocker
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 21, 2025
reviews.content: Very disappointed. Bought for a classroom and they are falling apart in just a couple of weeks.

reviews.author: Jen Connell
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 20, 2024
reviews.content: Purchased for my classroom. Two pairs didnt work brand new from the bag. They are comfortable but fit a little loose on the ears. Ok for the money, but shame that already 2 dont work.

reviews.author: Nikki
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 31, 2024
reviews.content:
Loving this pack of 10 over the ear headphones. My son is in Kindergarten and the teacher requested jack port headphones. My son is not a fan a wired headphones and has been known to break the cord to make them “wireless” so I thought this order was perfect just in case he broke one or two during the school year he’ll have backups to pull from.

They’re comfortable and the head piece adjust to your head size easily. The sound quality is good and the earphones bend and twist easily so they should hold up overtime they look good to me and the ear cushion is not the best quality out there but is decent enough for school use. This set is a great value for the money especially for teachers, schools and or families with multiple kids in school that require over the ear headphones! Great find!!!

reviews.author: Lt Aldo Raine
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 22, 2024
reviews.content: This is a great product! I love finding such a big pack of headphones to get for my classroom to have on hand as extras. Each pair of headphones is also individually wrapped inside the big bag to help keep them protected. My students have used these for weeks and haven’t had any issues or complaints. They fit young students well and this pack of 10 is a great value for the money!

reviews.author: Thinker3
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 26, 2024
reviews.content:
This is definitely a quantity over quality purchase -- and one that value shoppers can appreciate. At the current price, these come out to just over a dollar and a half for a pair of usable headphones. And there are 10 of them.

I got them because my grandkids need headphones for school. They don't take good enough care of their stuff to get them quality stuff -- after all, these are in their backpack all year long. So there are several kids and they'll need several headphones a piece throughout the year.

This product satisfies my needs. And the headphones work well -- or, at least, well enough. They're plug and play. Individually wrapped.

However, I thought the earpads were a little bit undersized, even for kids. And the band was perhaps a tad awkward. So they are not perfect.

But good enough for lots of kids -- either at a home or in a classroom. I wouldn't rely on these for high sound quality, either.

But at this price point, I'd certainly recommend these for the right circumstances.

reviews.author: I. Zaman
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 21, 2024
reviews.content: These headphones are great for students! I bought them for my daughter's class and they are perfect! They are durable, well made and have lasted for half a year so far. The audio is very clear. They are comfortable and the kids love it!

reviews.author: Amanda Gray
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 12, 2025
reviews.content:
Peace of Mind for Parents, Comfort for Baby!
Get these now!

I purchased these baby headphones for my 3-month-old for the Fourth of July , and I couldn't be happier with the quality and performance. The noise was loud, but my baby stayed calm and content with the headphones on the entire time. They really do an excellent job at blocking out harsh sounds while still being comfortable enough for little ears. He even slept during the fireworks with these on!

The design is super soft and adjustable, which is great since my baby is on the smaller side. No red marks or discomfort even after wearing them for over an hour. The headband fits snugly without being too tight, and they stayed in place through naps and stroller rides.

We’ve also used them at a noisy restaurant and they’ve been a lifesaver every time. I love knowing that I’m protecting my baby’s hearing without sacrificing comfort.

If you're a parent looking for reliable hearing protection for your little one, these are definitely worth it. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Meranda
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 7, 2026
reviews.content: Worked great for my 6month old! Love that it was adjustable and seemed comfy for her (she took a nap during Monster Jam!). She didn’t pull at them once either.

reviews.author: B.Nicole
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 2, 2026
reviews.content:
These baby ear protectors are such a great purchase and extremely well made. The materials are soft and high quality, and the stretchy headband is gentle and comfortable for my child to wear without feeling tight or restrictive. I really love that there’s no pressure on the fontanel, which was very important to me when choosing hearing protection.

We purchased these specifically for Disney, and they came in so handy. Between rides, shows, crowds, and fireworks, the noise can get overwhelming, and these gave me so much peace of mind knowing my child’s ears were protected. They stayed in place well, didn’t slip around, and most importantly my child didn’t mind wearing them at all.

The noise reduction is noticeable without being too much, and the overall fit feels very thoughtfully designed. They’re lightweight, adjustable, durable, and clearly made with babies’ comfort and safety in mind. This is one of those items I’m really glad we had for a busy trip, and I would absolutely recommend them to other parents planning Disney or any loud outings.

reviews.author: KCSP20489
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 8, 2025
reviews.content: We bought these for our 7-month-old ahead of a family wedding, knowing the reception would get loud. I'm so glad we did! The Muffy Baby headphones worked like a charm. Our little one slept through a good portion of the speeches and the loud band music without a single flinch. The best part is the design. I was worried about pressure on his head, but the soft elastic band is a much better solution than a traditional hard-cased headband. He didn't seem to mind wearing them at all, and there were no marks on his skin when we took them off. The little carrying pouch is also a nice touch for keeping them clean in the diaper bag. My only small complaint is getting them on. Trying to hold a squirming baby while positioning both ear cups and securing the velcro band by myself was a bit of a challenge. It took a couple of tries to get the fit just right so they wouldn't slip. Once they were on securely, they stayed put, but it's definitely easier with two people. Overall, a fantastic product that gives us real peace of mind. Despite the slight learning curve with fitting them, I'd absolutely recommend them.

reviews.author: Mumbojumbo
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 23, 2026
reviews.content: Used these for loud venues with our 2 month old. They are easily adjustable and our baby fell asleep with them on because they blocked out the loud music.

reviews.author: Meghan
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.content: These are amazing. Went to an indoor monster truck show and my baby slept through the entire thing. Did not even stir, and it was LOUD. Absolutely 10/10 recommend. Fantastic price for value. Great design, easy to move ear pieces, and quality band. Light weight and good size for baby ears, not too big.

reviews.author: Jose Ramirez
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 2, 2026
reviews.content:
These Alpine Muffy Baby Ear Protection earmuffs do the job — they help reduce loud noises and fit babies and toddlers reasonably well. The headband is adjustable and soft, so my little one didn’t fuss too much while wearing them.

That said, they feel a bit bulky for tiny heads, and the noise reduction is okay but not super impressive. They work for everyday outings or moderately loud environments, but for very loud events, they might not block enough sound.

reviews.author: Courtney
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.content:
These were perfect for the basketball game that I attended with my 10-month-old daughter! I tested them out at hope first and they definitely muffle the sound. She didn't seem like the loud sound at the game bothered her at all and there was lots going on from the band playing, announcers, music, fireworks, etc.

Adjustable fit for her head and overall enjoyed the look of these as well. The ear padding and strap was soft for her head and didn't leave any indentations. I chose the black color because it was neutral.

I would recommend! Definitely worth the money, and I trust this brand!

reviews.author: kbs
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 22, 2023
reviews.content:
I bought these because I had a gift card to spend, and in hopes that I could completely separate from my Airpod Pro’s (1rst gen). I wanted to get rid of my Airpod pro’s because I have become so completely fed up with popping them in my ears and having only one earbud connect. That happens about 25% of the time I use them, and of course it’s always right when I’m in a hurry and need them now. I have to reset them every time it happens and frankly I’m fed up with it – unacceptable reliability.
So, it’s only been a little more than a week but I haven’t had this happen once. Nor has it ever happened on any of my other true wireless earbuds (Soundcore Liberty 4 NC’s and some cheap no-name sports hook-style earbuds). The Sony’s have had stellar reviews, but my one reservation was that I’m in a full Apple ecosphere, and I had read that if you’re in the Apple ecosphere, you’ll want the Airpod Pro series II. Using anything else would mean missing out on that typical Apple integration – things work great together. Well, I’ve never tried the Airpod Pro II’s, but I couldn’t get myself to reward Apple by ordering another pair of headphones from them after my dissatisfaction with the AirPod Pro I’s.
So, how are the Sony’s? Absolutely fantastic and boy, am I glad I went with them! First of all, in terms of integration/functionality with my Apple world, best I can tell so far its flawless. Transitions between my iPhone 15 and iPad Pro are just as smooth as my AirPods were. I can send/receive calls just as easily, and the Sony has one feature I don’t think AirPods have – you can just nod your head to answer calls! I also find the touch sensor vastly superior to Apple’s squeeze surface (or whatever it’s called). Much more intuitive to me, and it works every time on the first try.
Another big advantage the Sony’s have over the Airpod Pro I’s is battery life. One reason I picked the Sony’s was better sleep. My wife makes quite a racket sleeping at night and I had read that these were great for sleeping. My Airpod Pro’s were also quite good at that, but they wouldn’t last through the night – only about 4.5 hours (w/NC on). Not sure how long the Sony can last, but I’ve gone over 8 hours (w/NC on) – and they still had some battery left! As for the noise cancelling, I’d say it’s slightly disappointing. I also own the over-ear WH-1000XM5’s, and they have AWESOME noise cancelling! I’d say the WF-1000XM5’s are nowhere near that level – I’d say they are about the same as my Airpod Pro I’s. Good enough to attenuate my wife’s snoring (at least enough that I can sleep), but not completely block it (so I hear nothing at all) as on the WH-1000XM5’s. But of course, the WH-1000MX5’s are way too big to sleep with (full size over-ear headphones). Another minor disappointment is no “find my earbud” functionality, which makes me a little paranoid about losing them, but hopefully not a big deal.
Saving the best for last is the sound quality. I bought the big-brother WH-1000XM5’s a year ago and was quite surprised at their sound quality. Same thing here – these sound fantastic! I’m a fairly serious audiophile and have quite a collection of high-end headphones, including one pair of earbuds that are more than triple the cost of these Sony’s (JH Audio J13 v2’s). And all the electronics to go with it (DAP’s, DAC’s, amps, cables, etc.). They took a while to break in properly (maybe 30-50 hours) but they are really wonderful. They are among the most natural sounding earbuds I’ve heard and I’m a bit stumped how that could be possible since it uses crappy Bluetooth. My high-end headphones are fed almost exclusively high-rez files. Bluetooth AAC (can’t use LDAC on an iPhone) is the best I can run, which of course are only a small fraction the bitrate resolution of true high-rez files. But somehow these sound absolutely wonderful – just a natural “rightness” that’s hard to describe. BY FAR the best bass I’ve ever heard from an earbud. They have a confidence, an “assertiveness” (yes, that’s a new audio metaphor) – not aggressiveness - that I just love. I’m not saying that they are flat-out better than my high-end earbuds (my Shure M535’s are my favorite), but I will say they are at least in the same ballpark. I can pop these in and really enjoy listening for hours – and not feel like I’m missing out in not using my much more expensive earbuds. Very high compliment indeed! So, great job Sony! BTW, I read that Sony bought Audeze (high-end true audiophile headphone brand), which means I can’t wait to see what they come out with in the future – if these are as good as they are, I can’t wait to find out what the XM6’s or XM7’s will sound like!
Update 4/16/24: Just wanted to mention one more remarkable thing about these. I am an avid cyclist and I’ve been on a long search to find the best earbuds for cycling - which for me, is mostly about reducing WIND NOISE. That is by far my biggest goal for cycling headphones. These are downright spooky in their ability to hugely reduce wind noise - BY FAR the most effective I’ve ever tried, and I’ve tried quite a few (AirPod Pro’s, Soundcore Liberty 4 NC, etc…). So now I face an interesting dilemma - do i dare use a $300 pair of earbuds for cycling? The problem with using them for cycling is that cycling is of course brutal on earbuds (constant sweat). So I’ll have to decide if I dare to do that long term, but I can say that these are by far the best headphones I have ever used in terms of wind blocking. So these really are amazing - fantastic sound, powerful noise cancelling, long battery life and the best wind noise blocking I’ve ever heard…

reviews.author: Whyborg
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 31, 2023
reviews.content:
There’s a bit of possible Sony hate going on in some reviews, like others I got burned on a pair of WF-10004XM refurbished and one of the batteries started failing after a year. Wasn’t worth the cost of repair. Have others from QCY and Soundcore that were surprisingly good for less cost. Also had trouble with refurbished headphones WH-1000XM4, luckily had added insurance. Cut to buying a new pair that have been flawless.
Got the Sony WF-C700N a couple months ago to scratch my earbud itch. Very good for the price. If you’re not ready to throw down $300 for the XM5 they’re a great alternative. Not quite as musical, but no gripes anyway. You can get picky about the throwback buttons but oh well.
One comment before I get more specific on the XM5. I fully agree with another review that calls out Sony for how complicated their Sony Connect app has become. It could be simplified for people who don’t want to get into the tech so much.
Now, how do they sound? I’m impressed and I’m not using them to their full potential. Using with iPhone so Bluetooth SBC codec is the most basic and lowest quality solution (after digging into the codec question a bit more it does appear that the iPhone has the AAC codec also). However, with the help of Sony’s equalizer and DSEE function I’m very happy with the sound. I almost don’t want to try them with a better codec and then have to go back. (Still applies with AAC, DSE does improve sound)
I need treble boost for my old ears and having played bass no surprise I like a lot of bass. These don’t disappoint. I did an A-B comparison with the XM4 headphones and they didn’t have quite the low end but good and punchy with good definition between bass and drums.both can become nice and “growly” on the low end.
Noise cancellation is very good, not as good as the headphones but after only a couple days I have been quite satisfied so far.
Comfort level is better than the XM4 buds, but I’m so pissed I wasted the money now I don’t want to dig them out to compare audio! I think Sony really improved the fit and design to be lighter, more comfortable, and have impressive sound. Let’s just hope they found a better battery.
Used overnight and had battery left in the morning but playing audiobook on timer so i couldn’t say how long continuous playback.
I’ll update after I’ve had them longer.
(Update) battery life seems to be very similar to the XM4. Sometimes I have battery left in the morning sometimes it comes up short.
Another follow up, comes with 4 sets of tips but apparently I have very large ear canals as I’m unable to get a complete seal with the included buds. The large felt good at first but when I used the connect app to check for airtight seals I could not get them to seal. Fortunately I have many tips I’ve saved over the years and by mixing, matching, and testing I have a couple that seal. I even tried ordering extra large tips from a couple vendors and they didn’t work either. Nice that Sony is including extra small but what about us with extra large needs! I do have a possible solution on the way, find a video on YouTube that shows how to use Sugro glue rolled up and inserted in the mushroom part of the tip. Press in the ear to get a fit and then allow to cure over night. Hoping it works, I have a few tips I’d like to try this on. Was even investigating custom molded tips but for $150 plus I’ll try the Sugro hack…
8/5/23 tried the Sugro hack yesterday. Still have to work to get an airtight fit but it definitely helped.
My opinion on the sound has not diminished. Excellent with what I listen too. A lot of classic rock but also new material from Elle King and others. Listening to Lourdes “Royals” a very deep bass which presents low, solid, and clear. You can easily distinguish the bass and drums. The low rumbling bass of Pink Floyd “Welcome to the Machine” couldn’t be much better in any buds. Almost like I could feel it.
I’m now using the WF-C700n as my workout and dog walking buds. Sound signature is very similar but the bass is not quite as good. One thing they are lacking is the airtight fit check in the app. I had to use other tips and the Sugro mold hack to get a good consistent fit in both. The included tips are worthless to me and my big ears.
I see a lot of criticism of the noise cancellation for the XM5. I have no way to measure so it’s pretty subjective but I’m not disappointed. At least as good as the XM4. But with any ANC earbuds the airtight seal is critical.
If you’re not ready to throw down $300 the WF-C700n are a very good alternative, but are not touch controlled, a single button on each side. Not quite as good on the low end but not disappointing either. In direct comparison you can hear the difference.
I still think these are 5 star worthy. I’d take off half a star because of the somewhat complex app but if you like to tinker with the sound and features, give it back. People who are not “technical minded” may find it intimidating. I find it useful but jammed with some features I wouldn’t normally use, like automatically changing settings depending on location as an example. Some might find it useful but not me. The equalizer and clear bass functions are excellent. DSEE function makes a noticeable difference in sound quality. Happier with these than I was with the XM4. Just hope the batteries last, I would hope Sony changed from the $70 replacement cost batteries (not including labor) used in the XM4 as apparently so many people had problems with them dying out after a few months.
9/22/23
Used these today instead of the WF-C700n for my indoor cycling and to walk the dog. Usually I do those activities with the cheaper buds as for $300 I want to take every precaution to protect them. So…more feedback on the ANC. Definitely a difference on the bike with 2 pretty loud blower fans on. The WF-C700n does a very good job in the same situation but I could definitely tell a difference. Could hardly hear the fans at all and the WF-1000XM5 does have the superior sound performance. Note these blowers are loud enough that if I have my Apple Watch to close to them I get the over 90db warning. They’re not 90db from the bike but they are loud. These cancelled almost all the noise. With volume on my music I don’t hear the fans at all.

reviews.author: E7
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 2, 2023
reviews.content:
I have the Sony WF-1000XM4 and now the XM5. The XM4 battery life is terrible. They have issues with those batteries going bad too soon so I don't recommend those.

The XM5 noise cancellation is great and the music quality is good but I have a few complaints. The earbuds don't stay in my ear when using the original foam tips. It's annoying having your hands busy at work and feeling your earbud shifting about to fall out. I had to buy aftermarket foam tips. The sound could be better and it's probably due to the foam tips letting the sound out.

My 2nd complaint is they're not loud enough. Even with aftermarket foam tips, the earbuds are just not loud enough. I work in a noisy environment and it's hard to listen to podcasts with these. The noise cancellation only works if you have both earbuds in. It sounds like it wants to work when using one earbud but only when there's a really loud noise. It cuts the music momentarily and goes back to letting all the noise in. It would really help if it would work with only one earbud in because I wouldn't have any problems hearing whatever I'm listening to.

I hate the touch sensitive controls. I wish they had a physical button that gave me a clicky feedback every time I press it. There is a tone whenever you pause or skip but like I said, it's hard to hear in a noisy environment. Most of the time, I have to raise my volume to notice if it paused, skipped or didn't register me pressing the touch sensitive controls. I originally bought these to use while I'm at work but now I only use these at home. For work I use the Beats Studio Buds which are louder, stay in my ear (with comply foam tips), have great audio and have a clicky button for the controls. No more guessing if I pressed the button or not. With the Beats Studio Buds I can now listen to the same podcasts I couldn't hear with my XM5s in the same noisy environment. I just bought the Studio Buds + which have a better battery life than the 1st gen. The XM5s are overrated in my opinion.

1 YEAR UPDATE: I have the XM5s connected to only ONE device and they sometimes don't want to connect at all. I have to reconnect them like I'm adding a new device. It's so annoying. Even my cheap $40 earbuds don't do that. I'm starting to think these are a waste of money. I'm giving them 1 star...

UPDATE: The connectivity issues keep getting worse. I only have them paired to one device (my laptop) and I have to unpair and pair them way too often or else it keeps trying to connect but doesn't. The laptop isn't the issue because no other bluetooth device does that. Sony needs to concentrate on fixing the basic issues instead of trying to add all kinds of features. I need earbuds that turn on, pair properly, are loud enough, stay in my ear and have decent battery life. These earbuds don't cover my basic needs. These are the last Sony XM earbuds I'm buying.

reviews.author: Samuel Holland
reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 1, 2023
reviews.content:
Edit 01/19/24
Short and sweet-
I use buds just about everyday.
I liked the pixel buds more, and is why my xm4's will not accept a charge or turn on because after getting the pixel buds, I never used the XM4's again.
We're here for XM5 though, so please excuse the rant.1st Sony customer service, second to none including Google who used to be pretty good(IMO)(pre-'so-called'pandemic).
2nd -Stop right now and take a picture of bottom of XM5 case & package it came in(identifying Numbers). If u ever have issue you will need.
3rd & final what you read for---➡️❤️ My original review is below and still valid but after cleaning and adjusting tips, I'm happy to report I haven't had the 'connect from pocket problem'. Changing the tips made a big diff, but they're not near as secure and comfortable as pixel buds pro for my everyday use. That bring said, the pass through, pass thru blend, etc are the best hands down. Their competition isn't even close for this category(IMO_of_course).
The only thing I find myself having to adjust often. They want to sit halfway in my ear, instead of locked into place. Many times when I go to adjust, I hit pause, which could be disabled in all fairness. I still use these as my everyday buds though, and are about to try more tips when I can find the time. They are superior in sound and especially the noise cancellation/passthrough/blend feature. It's actually amazing how good they are in all honesty. (If you don't like foam tips, get some silicone because these only come with the foam style.) (The tips are the same though as any other set.)
I just wanted to come back and follow up.
They deserve five stars.⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐. I have to leave the one star off but if I could I'd rate them 4.7-4.8, so sorry it's not five stars for a pretty solid product.
I hope this can help someone, and give yourself a day or two once getting to be able to see the difference, since they have some pretty solid picks out there. Thanks, & may the good Lord bless.❤️

I had the 3rd & 4th Gen earbuds, so upon getting these, I was wondering did I make the right choice, coming from pixel buds pro?
